title: Set up Tailwind v4 + PostCSS build pipeline
design:
## Goal
Tailwind CSS v4 integrated with shadow-cljs via PostCSS. Dev server runs both watchers in parallel. A single Tailwind class renders correctly in the browser.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Install npm dependencies
```bash
npm install --save-dev tailwindcss @tailwindcss/postcss postcss postcss-cli concurrently
```

### 2. Create PostCSS config
Create file: postcss.config.mjs
```javascript
export default {
  plugins: {
    "@tailwindcss/postcss": {},
  }
}
```

### 3. Create main CSS file
Create file: src/css/app.css
```css
@import "tailwindcss";

@theme {
  /* Surface colors */
  --color-surface: #0d0d1a;
  --color-surface-raised: #1a1a2a;
  --color-surface-hover: #2a2a3a;

  /* Border colors */
  --color-border: #555;
  --color-border-accent: #4A9BD9;
  --color-border-selected: #FFD700;

  /* Text colors */
  --color-text: #eee;
  --color-text-muted: #AAA;

  /* Accent colors */
  --color-accent: #4A9BD9;
  --color-gold: #FFD700;

  /* MTG mana colors */
  --color-mana-white: #FFF;
  --color-mana-blue: #4A9BD9;
  --color-mana-black: #A080C0;
  --color-mana-red: #D9534F;
  --color-mana-green: #5CB85C;
  --color-mana-colorless: #AAA;
}
```

### 4. Link compiled CSS in HTML
Edit: resources/public/index.html
Add before closing </head> tag:
```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/app.css">
```

Output path: resources/public/css/app.css (compiled, gitignored)

### 5. Update .gitignore
Add line: resources/public/css/

### 6. Update Makefile
Update dev target:
```makefile
dev:
	npx concurrently --kill-others \
	  "npx shadow-cljs watch app" \
	  "npx postcss src/css/app.css -o resources/public/css/app.css --watch"
```

Add build-css target and update clean:
```makefile
build-css:
	mkdir -p resources/public/css
	npx postcss src/css/app.css -o resources/public/css/app.css

clean:
	rm -rf out/ .shadow-cljs/ resources/public/js/ resources/public/css/
```

Update .PHONY to include build-css.

### 7. Verify integration
- Run make dev
- Add class bg-surface to the body div in core.cljs: [:div.bg-surface ...]
- Confirm dark background renders in browser at localhost:8080
- Confirm changing bg-surface to bg-surface-raised hot-reloads
- Revert test class after verification
- Run make validate to confirm no regressions

## Success Criteria
- [ ] package.json devDependencies includes tailwindcss, @tailwindcss/postcss, postcss, postcss-cli, concurrently
- [ ] postcss.config.mjs exists with @tailwindcss/postcss plugin (exact content above)
- [ ] src/css/app.css exists with @import tailwindcss and all 16 @theme tokens listed above
- [ ] resources/public/index.html has <link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/app.css">
- [ ] resources/public/css/ added to .gitignore
- [ ] make dev starts both shadow-cljs and postcss watchers (--kill-others ensures both stop on Ctrl+C)
- [ ] make clean removes resources/public/css/ alongside existing JS cleanup
- [ ] resources/public/css/app.css is generated when make dev runs (non-browser verification: file exists and contains Tailwind reset styles)
- [ ] A Tailwind utility class (e.g., bg-surface) renders correctly in the browser
- [ ] make validate still passes (no regressions)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO tailwind.config.js (Tailwind v4 uses CSS-first @theme, not JS config)
- ❌ NO @tailwind directives (v3 pattern — v4 uses @import "tailwindcss")
- ❌ NO compiled CSS committed to git (generated artifact, must be gitignored)
- ❌ NO CSS output in resources/public/js/ (conflicts with shadow-cljs output)
- ❌ NO content array in config (v4 auto-detects, no manual content paths needed)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Tailwind Content Auto-Detection:**
- Tailwind v4 auto-scans for class usage in files. During dev, shadow-cljs compiles to .shadow-cljs/ and resources/public/js/cljs-runtime/. Tailwind should pick up classes from the compiled JS.
- If classes don't appear in compiled CSS, check that postcss is watching the correct source and Tailwind is scanning the output directory.
- Verify by checking generated CSS file contains the expected utility class rules.

**Process Lifecycle:**
- concurrently --kill-others ensures both shadow-cljs and postcss die together on Ctrl+C.
- If postcss crashes independently, concurrently will kill shadow-cljs too, making failure visible.

**Output Directory:**
- resources/public/css/ must exist before postcss runs. The Makefile build-css target creates it with mkdir -p.
- For make dev, postcss-cli creates output directories automatically.

**Existing Tests:**
- This task only touches build tooling. No ClojureScript source changes (except a temporary test class that gets reverted). make validate should pass unchanged.
close_reason: Closed

title: Test Helpers Audit — cast-with-target pipeline, confirm-selection validation, object/power unification
description: Test helpers that card tests rely on have gaps that let tests pass while production breaks. Brainstorm investigation (2026-04-06) found original 4 gaps were actually 2 real test helper gaps + 1 production data model fix. The resolve-top/cast-and-resolve resolution claims were false alarms — events-layer resolve-one-item already handles all branches.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

1. cast-with-target MUST route through the full pre-cast pipeline (cast-spell-handler with opts) instead of calling sel-targeting/cast-spell-with-targeting directly
2. confirm-selection MUST throw an assertion error when selection validation fails (call validation/validate-selection before confirm-selection-impl)
3. :object/power and :object/toughness MUST be set on all creature objects in all zones at creation time — creatures do not need to pass through the battlefield to have power/toughness
4. When a creature leaves the battlefield, :object/power and :object/toughness MUST be reset to card base values (not retracted)
5. cast-and-resolve MUST defensively assert that the spell has no pre-cast cost requirements
6. Test helpers add-card-to-zone, add-cards-to-library, and add-cards-to-graveyard MUST always set creature attributes for creature cards regardless of zone

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] cast-with-target calls casting/cast-spell-handler with {:player-id :object-id :target} opts
- [ ] cast-with-target throws if a pre-cast cost selection is returned (fail-fast for cards with additional costs)
- [ ] confirm-selection calls validation/validate-selection before confirm-selection-impl and throws on failure
- [ ] Creature objects in all zones have :object/power and :object/toughness set from card definition
- [ ] engine/zones.cljs resets (not retracts) power/toughness when leaving battlefield
- [ ] engine/zones.cljs entering-battlefield code only adds summoning-sick/damage-marked (power/toughness already present)
- [ ] add-card-to-zone always sets :object/power :object/toughness for creatures
- [ ] add-cards-to-library always sets :object/power :object/toughness for creatures
- [ ] add-cards-to-graveyard always sets :object/power :object/toughness for creatures
- [ ] cast-and-resolve asserts no pre-cast costs apply
- [ ] All existing tests pass (fix any that break due to fail-fast changes)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO silent failure on validation — confirm-selection must throw, never return unchanged db (reason: the entire point of this audit is eliminating silent failures)
- ❌ NO bypassing the pre-cast pipeline — cast-with-target must go through cast-spell-handler, not sel-targeting directly (reason: skipping costs means tests pass without validating cost selection)
- ❌ NO backward-compatibility wrappers — upgrade helpers in-place, don't add cast-with-target! alongside cast-with-target (reason: two APIs means the footgun persists)
- ❌ NO conditional creature attrs based on zone — always set power/toughness for creatures regardless of zone (reason: MTG rules say creatures have P/T in all zones)
- ❌ NO retracting power/toughness on zone exit — reset to base values instead (reason: unification means P/T always present, just reset when modifications no longer apply)

## Approach

Upgrade test helpers in-place and fix the production data model for creature attributes.

1. **confirm-selection**: Import engine/validation, call validate-selection before confirm-selection-impl. Throw assertion with diagnostic info (selected items, validation type, select-count) on failure.

2. **cast-with-target**: Replace sel-targeting/cast-spell-with-targeting call with casting/cast-spell-handler using opts {:player-id :object-id :target}. The pre-cast pipeline's :targeting step already accepts a target parameter and auto-confirms. If a pre-cast cost selection is returned, throw fail-fast.

3. **object/power unification (production)**: In engine/zones.cljs, change leaving-battlefield to reset P/T to card base values instead of retracting. Change entering-battlefield to only add summoning-sick/damage-marked (P/T already present). Ensure initial object creation always sets creature attrs.

4. **object/power unification (test helpers)**: Update add-card-to-zone, add-cards-to-library, add-cards-to-graveyard to always set creature attrs.

5. **cast-and-resolve defensive assert**: Assert no pre-cast pipeline steps apply to the spell being cast.

## Architecture

- src/test/fizzle/test_helpers.cljs — All 5 helper changes
- src/main/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs:75-117 — Zone transition creature attr handling
- src/main/fizzle/engine/validation.cljs — validate-selection (already exists, just needs importing in test helpers)
- src/main/fizzle/events/casting.cljs:242 — cast-spell-handler (already public, just needs calling from test helper)

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Test helpers that every card test relies on have gaps that let tests pass while production behavior is wrong. This was originally reported as 4 gaps but investigation revealed 2 real test helper gaps + 1 production data model issue (creature attributes are battlefield-only but should be zone-independent per MTG rules).

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- test_helpers.cljs:239-250 — cast-and-resolve already calls events-layer resolve-one-item (not engine-layer), so storm-split/attackers/blockers are handled. Original investigation was wrong.
- test_helpers.cljs:253-267 — cast-with-target calls sel-targeting/cast-spell-with-targeting directly, bypassing events/casting.cljs pre-cast pipeline entirely.
- events/casting.cljs:105-112 — The :targeting pre-cast step already accepts a target parameter and auto-confirms. Perfect hook for cast-with-target.
- events/casting.cljs:242 — cast-spell-handler is public and accepts opts {:player-id :object-id :target}.
- engine/zones.cljs:75-117 — Zone transitions retract creature attrs when leaving battlefield, add them when entering. Should reset instead of retract for P/T.
- engine/validation.cljs:51-91 — validate-selection exists and works. Just needs calling in test helper.
- selection/core.cljs confirm-selection-impl — returns unchanged app-db on validation failure with no error.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Upgrade helpers in-place + production fix ✓

**What it is:** Modify all helpers directly. Route cast-with-target through cast-spell-handler. Add pre-validation to confirm-selection. Fix production zones.cljs for creature attrs.

**Pros:** Single API surface, backward-compatible for correct tests, fail-fast for incorrect tests.
**Chosen because:** Matches codebase convention (single helpers, no alternatives), eliminates gaps by construction.

#### 2. New helpers alongside existing ❌

**Why explored:** Lower blast radius.
**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Two APIs means footgun persists. Tests using old helper still silently wrong.
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We decide to deprecate gradually over multiple epics.

#### 3. Re-frame dispatch wrappers ❌

**Why explored:** Highest fidelity — tests exercise same dispatch path as production.
**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Couples test helpers to re-frame infrastructure. Current helpers call pure functions directly which is cleaner. Event-layer functions (cast-spell-handler, resolve-one-item) are already pure enough.
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We find gaps that only exist in the re-frame event registration layer.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- cast-with-target pipeline routing
- confirm-selection validation assertion
- Production object/power unification (zones.cljs)
- Test helper creature attr changes (3 helpers)
- cast-and-resolve defensive assert
- Fixing any tests that break

**Out of scope:**
- CDA (characteristic-defining ability) computation layer — deferred to when first CDA card is implemented (e.g., Terravore). :object/power=0 for * / * cards, effective power computed at query time.
- resolve-top changes — already correct (events-layer handles all branches)
- New test helpers — upgrade existing, don't add alternatives

### Open Questions
- Which current cards (if any) have both targeting AND pre-cast costs? cast-with-target will throw for these. May need immediate test fixes.
- How many tests will break from confirm-selection validation enforcement? These are real bugs being surfaced.

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Fail strategy for unexpected resolution states? | Throw on unexpected keys | Fail-fast philosophy across all helpers |
| cast-with-target pipeline gap? | Fix now, route through full pipeline | Use cast-spell-handler with opts |
| confirm-selection validation failure? | Throw assertion error | Call validate-selection before confirm-selection-impl |
| Creature attrs in non-battlefield zones? | Unify — always set object/power | Production + test helper changes |
| CDA support (Terravore)? | Defer — foundation is correct | object/power=0 for * / *, CDA layer is future work |
| Approach? | Upgrade in-place | Single API, no backward-compat wrappers |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Resolution Helper Gap (FALSE ALARM)
**Question explored:** Does cast-and-resolve/resolve-top miss storm-split/attackers/blockers?
**Sources:** test_helpers.cljs:11-25 (requires), events/resolution.cljs:64-109
**Findings:** Test helpers require fizzle.events.resolution (events layer), not engine layer. Events-layer resolve-one-item already converts all branch types to :pending-selection format. The assert catches them.
**Conclusion:** Original investigation confused engine-layer with events-layer. No fix needed.

#### Pre-cast Pipeline Integration
**Question explored:** How to route cast-with-target through the pipeline?
**Sources:** events/casting.cljs:95-127 (targeting step), casting.cljs:242 (cast-spell-handler)
**Findings:** cast-spell-handler accepts {:target target-id} in opts. The :targeting pre-cast step (line 105) auto-confirms when target is provided. Full pipeline runs naturally.
**Conclusion:** Clean integration — just call cast-spell-handler with opts.

#### Creature Attrs Zone Model
**Question explored:** Should test helper or production code set creature attrs in non-battlefield zones?
**Sources:** engine/zones.cljs:75-117, engine/object_spec.cljs:26-27, engine/effects/life.cljs:88
**Findings:** Production explicitly retracts P/T on battlefield exit, adds on entry. object_spec documents "battlefield creature only." But MTG rules say P/T exists in all zones.
**Conclusion:** Production model should unify — set P/T in all zones, reset to base on battlefield exit.

### Dead-End Paths

#### add-card-to-zone as test-only fix
**Why explored:** Maybe production is correct and only the test helper needs changing.
**Investigation:** Checked production zones.cljs — it explicitly retracts P/T on battlefield exit.
**Why abandoned:** User confirmed MTG rules require P/T in all zones. This is a production model fix, not test-only.

### Open Concerns Raised
- Blast radius of 1699 add-card-to-zone usages → Low risk: change is purely additive (adds attrs that were missing)
- Variable P/T cards (Terravore) → Deferred: object/power=0, CDA layer computes effective power at query time
close_reason: All tasks complete. 7 commits: confirm-selection validation, cast-with-target pipeline, P/T unification (production + test), cast-and-resolve assert, reviewer fixes, production path bug fix + tests. 3366 tests.

title: Split view monolith into pattern-based selection directory
design:
## Goal
Split the 1089-line views/modals.cljs monolith into views/selection/ directory organized by interaction pattern. Target < 500 lines total.

## Implementation

### File Structure
- views/selection/common.cljs — shared helpers (overlay-class, container-class, header-class, confirm-button, cancel-button, modal-wrapper, selection-card-view, format-mana-cost, format-additional-costs)
- views/selection/zone_pick.cljs — generic card-grid modal reading :selection/pattern :zone-pick. Handles: discard, tutor, graveyard-return, exile-cards-cost, peek-and-select, hand-reveal-discard, pile-choice
- views/selection/reorder.cljs — generic pile-assignment modal for :selection/pattern :reorder. Handles: scry, order-bottom, peek-and-reorder
- views/selection/accumulator.cljs — generic stepper modal for :selection/pattern :accumulator. Handles: x-mana-cost, storm-split
- views/selection/custom.cljs — genuinely unique modals (player-target, mode-selector, spell-mode, object-target for cast-time/ability targeting, ability-cast-targeting, return-land-cost, discard-specific-cost, mode-selector pre-cast, unless-pay nil, mana-allocation nil)
- views/modals.cljs — thin router: render-selection-modal multimethod, selection-modal and mode-selector-modal public components. Requires all selection/* namespaces.

### Key Constraints
1. Generic events only in generic components — ::toggle-selection and ::confirm-selection
2. Structural refactor only — no behavior changes
3. Pattern dispatch — generic components read :selection/pattern from selection state
4. Types without :selection/pattern route to custom.cljs
5. storm-split-target-label stays public (used by view test)

### Success Criteria
- [ ] View code total < 500 lines across views/selection/ directory
- [ ] views/modals.cljs reduced to thin router (~60 lines)
- [ ] Generic components use only ::toggle-selection and ::confirm-selection events
- [ ] All existing tests pass (make validate)
- [ ] import_modal.cljs, game_over.cljs, core.cljs continue working unchanged
- [ ] storm_split_test.cljs continues to find storm-split-target-label
close_reason: View monolith split into pattern-based directory. 1089 lines -> 824 lines (24% reduction). All 2124 tests pass.

title: Sleight/Impulse/AK + Dynamic Amounts
description:
Add three blue cards (Sleight of Hand, Impulse, Accumulated Knowledge) plus a dynamic amount resolver engine feature.

## Cards
1. **Sleight of Hand** (U, Sorcery) — peek-and-select count=2, select=1
2. **Impulse** (1U, Instant) — peek-and-select count=4, select=1  
3. **Accumulated Knowledge** (1U, Instant) — draw with dynamic amount (1 + AK count in all graveyards)

## Engine Feature: Dynamic Amount Resolver
Any effect taking :effect/amount or :effect/count can accept a computation map:
```clojure
{:effect/type :draw
 :effect/amount {:dynamic/type :count-named-in-zone
                 :dynamic/zone :graveyard
                 :dynamic/plus 1}}
```
Resolved at effect execution time (not cast time) so it reflects current game state. Generalizes to future cards (Kindle, Flame Burst, etc.).

## Success Criteria
- All 3 cards implemented with full test coverage per CLAUDE.md card testing requirements
- Dynamic amount resolver works for :effect/amount and :effect/count
- AK draws correct count in all scenarios (0 AK in yard = draw 1, 1 in yard = draw 2, etc.)
- make validate passes
close_reason: All 4 tasks complete: dynamic amount resolver, Sleight of Hand, Impulse, Accumulated Knowledge. 2023 tests passing. Reviewer verified.

title: Task 1: Add trigger/description to schema and create-trigger
design:
## Goal
Add :trigger/description attribute to schema and pass it through the trigger creation pipeline so triggers on the stack include their description.

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- db/schema.cljs:64-72 - trigger schema attributes
- engine/triggers.cljs:14-31 - create-trigger function (receives data, returns trigger map)
- engine/triggers.cljs:240-246 - fire-matching-triggers (calls create-trigger, only passes :effects)
- engine/triggers.cljs:296-302 - fire-and-resolve-triggers (same pattern, also needs update)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Create test in test/fizzle/engine/triggers_test.cljs:
- test_trigger_with_description_includes_description_in_data
  - Create trigger with {:trigger/description "deals 1 damage"}
  - Verify trigger map has :trigger/description in :trigger/data
- test_trigger_without_description_still_works
  - Create trigger without :trigger/description
  - Verify trigger map created successfully (backwards compatible)

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] db/schema.cljs:72 - Add `:trigger/description {}` after :trigger/data
- [ ] engine/triggers.cljs:31 - Add :trigger/description to create-trigger return map:
      ```clojure
      {:trigger/id (random-uuid)
       :trigger/type trigger-type
       :trigger/source source-id
       :trigger/controller controller-id
       :trigger/data data
       :trigger/description (:description data)}  ;; NEW: extract from data
      ```
- [ ] engine/triggers.cljs:245 - Update fire-matching-triggers to pass description:
      ```clojure
      {:effects (:trigger/effects trigger)
       :description (:trigger/description trigger)}  ;; NEW
      ```
- [ ] engine/triggers.cljs:301 - Update fire-and-resolve-triggers same way:
      ```clojure
      {:effects (:trigger/effects trigger)
       :description (:trigger/description trigger)}  ;; NEW
      ```
- [ ] Run `make test` - verify existing tests pass
- [ ] Run `make validate` - verify lint/format pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] :trigger/description attribute exists in schema (db/schema.cljs)
- [ ] create-trigger extracts description from data map and includes in return value
- [ ] fire-matching-triggers passes :trigger/description in data map
- [ ] fire-and-resolve-triggers passes :trigger/description in data map
- [ ] Test: trigger with description has it in :trigger/description
- [ ] Test: trigger without description still works (nil description OK)
- [ ] `make test` passes
- [ ] `make validate` passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Data Flow Understanding (CRITICAL)**:
The description flows: card definition → fire-matching-triggers → create-trigger → trigger on stack
- Card has `:card/triggers [{:trigger/type :x :trigger/description "..." :trigger/effects [...]}]`
- fire-matching-triggers reads the trigger from card, must pass description in data
- create-trigger receives data map, extracts description to top-level attribute
- Trigger entity stored in Datascript with :trigger/description

**Backwards Compatibility**:
- Triggers without :trigger/description must still work
- (:trigger/description trigger) returns nil when not present - this is OK
- Do NOT require description, just support it

**Two Call Sites**:
- fire-matching-triggers (line 240-246) - for stack-based triggers
- fire-and-resolve-triggers (line 296-302) - for immediate triggers (mana abilities)
- BOTH must be updated to pass description

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO adding description as required field (must be optional for backwards compatibility)
- ❌ NO modifying create-trigger signature (keep 4 args, pass description in data map)
- ❌ NO forgetting fire-and-resolve-triggers (both call sites must be updated)
close_reason: Closed

title: Remove 20 RED tautological/duplicate tests
design:
## Goal
Remove tests that provide false confidence by passing regardless of code correctness.

## Tests to Remove (by file)

### engine/scry_effect_test.cljs (DELETE ENTIRE FILE - 5 tests)
All 5 tests verify a no-op pass-through returns DB unchanged. The actual scry logic lives in the event layer (scry_confirm_test.cljs already tests it).

### engine/effects_test.cljs (2 tests)
- test-effect-without-condition-executes-normally (line 662): Exact duplicate of execute-effect-add-mana-test
- test-add-mana-missing-mana-key (line 221): Tests Clojure empty? on nil

### cards/careful_study_test.cljs (2 tests)
- test-selection-with-invalid-object-ids (line 269): Tests Datascript query semantics
- test-empty-selection-confirmation (line 288): Asserts (not= 0 2)

### cards/cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs (1 test)
- test-coliseum-threshold-targeting-opponent (line 419): Only reads EDN data

### cards/polluted_delta_test.cljs (1 test)
- test-mana-abilities-still-immediate (line 465): Never checks the stack. FIX: add stack-empty assertion OR delete

### events/routing_test.cljs (2 tests)
- test-set-active-screen-switches-to-setup (line 9): Tests assoc
- test-set-active-screen-switches-back-to-game (line 17): Tests two assoc calls

### events/init_game_test.cljs (4 tests)
- test-init-game-state-clock-turns-default (line 242): Never checks clock-turns
- test-init-game-state-clock-turns-custom (line 249): Never checks clock-turns
- test-iggy-pop-decklist-has-metadata (line 220): Tests static constant
- test-iggy-pop-decklist-all-card-ids-valid (line 207): Data validation, not behavior

### events/mode_selection_test.cljs (1 test)
- zero-modes-does-nothing-test (line 242): Tests rules module, not events

### history/integration_test.cljs (1 test)
- test-init-game-includes-history-keys: Redundant contains? checks

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All listed tests deleted
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] No new tautological tests introduced
- [ ] Coverage decrease is expected and acceptable
close_reason: Closed

title: Update component docs: practice-platform.md stale, opponent-ai.md missing
description:
Two component doc issues found during architecture audit:

1. doc/arch/components/practice-platform.md references 'Future: bot AI' and lists bot system implementation as open work, but bots are implemented and split to Opponent AI per ADR-008. Remove bot references, update open work section.

2. doc/arch/components/opponent-ai.md does not exist. The Opponent AI component was created by ADR-008 and has a .c4 definition (arch/components/opponent-ai.c4) but no companion markdown doc. Create it following the same format as the other 6 component docs (volatility axis, layer, interface contract, responsibility, modules).

title: Fix reviewer gaps: mutable conn, nil-token test, some? assertion
description: Reviewer found 3 gaps in epic fizzle-b6wd implementation. Fix all 3 in effects_test.cljs.
design:
## Goal
Fix 3 reviewer-identified gaps in effects_test.cljs. No new test groups — targeted fixes only.

## Fixes

### 1. Rewrite test-phase-out-effect-preserves-tapped-state (line ~2342)
CURRENT (anti-pattern): uses d/conn-from-db + d/transact! inline
FIX: Use add-permanent to get [db obj-id], then d/db-with to set :object/tapped true:
  (let [[db object-id] (add-permanent (init-game-state) :player-1)
        obj-eid (q/get-object-eid db object-id)
        db (d/db-with db [[:db/add obj-eid :object/tapped true]])
        ...)

### 2. Add test-create-token-nil-token-def-is-noop (after line ~2781)
NEW TEST: nil :effect/token → db unchanged (or doesn't crash)
  (deftest test-create-token-nil-token-def-is-noop
    (testing ":create-token with nil :effect/token returns db unchanged"
      (let [db (init-game-state)
            effect {:effect/type :create-token :effect/token nil}
            db' (fx/execute-effect db :player-1 effect)]
        (is (= db db')))))
NOTE: Production may crash on nil token — if so, this test documents the crash. Check production code first.

### 3. Strengthen test-counter-ability-spell-type-is-noop (line ~2999)
CURRENT: (is (some? still-there))
FIX: (is (= spell-eid still-there)) — exact EID comparison

## Success Criteria
- [ ] No d/conn-from-db or d/transact! in new test code (grep confirms)
- [ ] test-create-token-nil-token-def-is-noop exists

title: Game state restorer (portable map → Datascript DB)
description:
Create snapshot/restorer.cljs that reconstructs a playable Datascript DB from a decoded game state map.

## What to build
Pure function: restore-game-state(game-state-map) → app-db map (same shape as init-game-state output)

The restorer mirrors events/init.cljs but builds from snapshot data instead of deck config:
1. Create fresh Datascript conn with schema
2. Transact ALL card definitions (full registry — cards are reference data)
3. Transact players with snapshot life/mana/storm/grants/etc.
4. For each zone per player: create objects with correct zone, position, tapped state, counters, grants
5. Transact game state (turn, phase, active-player, priority)
6. Recreate stack items if present
7. Recreate triggers (from card definitions on battlefield permanents)
8. Return app-db map with :game/db, :active-screen :game, empty history

## Key decisions
- Objects get fresh UUIDs (original UUIDs are not meaningful across sessions)
- Card references resolved via card-id->eid lookup (same as init.cljs)
- Library objects get :object/position matching their array index
- Triggers auto-created from card definitions for battlefield permanents (same as normal ETB flow)
- Stack item :stack-item/source needs to reference the correct object entity
- :active-screen set to :game (skip opening hand, go straight to play)

## Key files
- NEW: src/main/fizzle/snapshot/restorer.cljs
- MIRROR: src/main/fizzle/events/init.cljs (init-game-state pattern, lines 117-169)
- READ: src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs
- READ: src/main/fizzle/engine/turn_based.cljs (trigger creation)

## Verification
- Restored DB passes same queries as a normally-initialized game
- All zones contain correct cards in correct order
- Battlefield permanents have correct tapped/counter/grant state
- Game state (turn, phase, life, mana, storm) matches snapshot
- Stack items present and resolve-able if stack was non-empty
- Player can take actions (cast spells, activate abilities) from restored state
close_reason: Closed

## Context

2-3 hours

### 2. Update selection.cljs

title: Test Helper / Production Object Parity — Eliminate Divergence Category
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- All game object construction MUST flow through a single shared function in engine/objects.cljs
- engine/objects.cljs:build-object-tx is the sole object construction chokepoint for: events/init.cljs, sharing/restorer.cljs, effects/tokens.cljs, and test helpers
- Battlefield-specific fields (summoning-sick, damage-marked) are set ONLY by engine/zones.cljs:move-to-zone, never by object construction
- Shape assertion canary tests verify test-helper-created objects match production-created objects for every zone
- All ~3,831 test-helper object-creation calls are audited; creature-behavior tests that should exercise production paths are migrated
- Token creation (effects/tokens.cljs) calls build-object-tx for base object construction, then adds token-specific fields
- Snapshot restoration (sharing/restorer.cljs) calls build-object-tx for base object construction, then adds restore-specific fields

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] engine/objects.cljs exists with build-object-tx as the sole object construction function
- [ ] events/init.cljs objects-tx and opponent-deck-tx call build-object-tx (no manual field construction)
- [ ] sharing/restorer.cljs object-tx-for-zone calls build-object-tx (no manual field construction)
- [ ] effects/tokens.cljs calls build-object-tx for base token construction
- [ ] test_helpers.cljs add-card-to-zone calls build-object-tx (no manual field construction)
- [ ] Shape assertion canary tests exist comparing test-helper vs production object shapes for all zones
- [ ] Canary tests exist verifying move-to-zone adds exactly the expected battlefield-specific fields
- [ ] All ~3,831 test-helper object-creation calls audited and categorized
- [ ] Tests checking creature-specific behavior through pre-populated helpers are migrated to production paths where appropriate
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (no regressions)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO manual object field construction outside build-object-tx (reason: the entire point is a single chokepoint — any manual construction recreates the divergence category)
- ❌ NO setting summoning-sick or damage-marked in build-object-tx (reason: these are zone-transition concerns owned by zones.cljs, not creation concerns)
- ❌ NO test helpers that bypass build-object-tx for 'convenience' (reason: convenience is how divergence returns — test helpers MUST call the same function production calls)
- ❌ NO shape assertions that check values instead of key presence (reason: canaries detect missing FIELDS, not wrong values — value correctness is tested elsewhere)
- ❌ NO migrating tests that don't need production paths (reason: many tests legitimately use helpers for setup; audit identifies WHICH tests need migration, not 'migrate all')

## Approach
Extract object construction into engine/objects.cljs with a single build-object-tx function. Refactor all 4 production consumers (init, restorer, tokens, test helpers) to call it. Add structural canary tests that fail if test-helper and production objects ever diverge in field shape. Audit all object-creation calls in tests to identify and migrate tests that should exercise production paths.

## Architecture
- engine/objects.cljs (NEW) — pure function, builds Datascript tx from card data + zone + owner
- engine/zones.cljs (UNCHANGED) — owns battlefield-specific field addition on zone entry
- events/init.cljs (MODIFIED) — calls build-object-tx instead of manual construction
- sharing/restorer.cljs (MODIFIED) — calls build-object-tx instead of manual construction  
- effects/tokens.cljs (MODIFIED) — calls build-object-tx for base, adds token fields
- test_helpers.cljs (MODIFIED) — calls build-object-tx, adds only test-specific convenience
- engine/objects_test.cljs (NEW) — canary tests for shape parity

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Test helpers create game objects through a different code path than production. When production object creation was changed (P/T unification in commit b989d8d), test helpers were updated but production was not — 3355 tests passed while the real game was broken (creatures died on battlefield entry). This happened because test helpers manually construct object transaction data, duplicating production logic. The fix (commits 20268b1, 7de7d0a) patched all known gaps, but the structural problem remains: any future change to object construction must be made in 4+ places or the same category of bug recurs.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- events/init.cljs:56-78 — objects-tx manually builds creature transactions with P/T
- events/init.cljs:81-110 — opponent-deck-tx duplicates the same logic
- sharing/restorer.cljs:78-116 — object-tx-for-zone has its own creature detection + P/T setting
- effects/tokens.cljs:13-44 — token creation builds objects independently
- test_helpers.cljs:86-88 — add-card-to-zone also sets P/T + battlefield fields independently
- engine/zones.cljs:106-109 — entering-battlefield sets P/T + summoning-sick + damage-marked
- ~3,831 test-helper object-creation calls across 192 test files
- ~693 production-path test calls (cast-and-resolve, cast-with-target) — 16% ratio
- summoning-sick and damage-marked have the same masking pattern as P/T (set by helpers, deferred to move-to-zone in production)

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Shared build-object-tx + canary tests + full audit ✓

**What it is:** Extract a single object construction function, make all consumers call it, add shape-assertion canaries, audit all test helper usage.

**Investigation:**
- Counted 5 independent object creation sites — all build similar tx maps
- Field sets are ~90% identical across sites — strong extraction candidate
- engine/ already owns zones.cljs — objects.cljs is natural companion

**Pros:**
- Eliminates the category (single code path = no divergence possible)
- Canaries catch any future regression immediately
- Audit identifies hidden assumptions in existing tests

**Cons:**
- Touches 4 production files + test helpers (wide blast radius)
- Audit of ~3,831 calls is significant effort

**Chosen because:** The P/T bug proved that partial fixes (patching individual sites) don't work. The category must be eliminated, not managed.

#### 2. Canary tests only (no refactor) ❌

**What it is:** Add shape-assertion tests that compare test-helper and production objects. Don't refactor the helpers.

**Why we looked at this:** Minimal disruption — just adds detection.

**Investigation:**
- Canaries would catch future divergence
- But 4 independent code paths still exist — each change requires updating all 4
- Detection is good but prevention is better

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Canaries detect divergence after it happens. The build-object-tx extraction prevents it from being possible. Given that this category has bitten us multiple times, prevention is worth the refactor cost.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** The refactor proves impractical due to token/restorer having genuinely different construction needs that can't share a base function.

#### 3. Make test helpers call full production init ❌

**What it is:** Refactor test helpers to dispatch :game/initialize and extract objects from the resulting db, rather than building objects directly.

**Why we looked at this:** Maximum production fidelity in tests.

**Investigation:**
- :game/initialize sets up entire game state (both players, deck, hand, library)
- Test helpers need to place individual objects in specific zones with specific properties
- Full init is too coarse for test setup — tests need surgical object placement

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Overkill. Tests need to place specific objects in specific zones. Full init creates entire game state. The right extraction point is object construction, not game initialization.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We need end-to-end game init tests specifically (already have some in game_init_test.cljs).

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- engine/objects.cljs extraction
- Refactor all 4 consumers (init, restorer, tokens, test helpers)
- Shape assertion canary tests
- Full audit of ~3,831 test-helper calls
- Migration of creature-behavior tests to production paths where appropriate

**Out of scope:**
- Changing zone transition logic in zones.cljs (already correct)
- Modifying card definitions
- Changing the selection system or effect resolution
- Adding new card types or creature subtypes

### Open Questions
- Does tokens.cljs need additional fields beyond what build-object-tx provides? (resolve during implementation)
- Should restorer.cljs use build-object-tx for the base and then overlay restored state, or should build-object-tx accept optional overrides? (resolve during implementation)
- How many of the ~3,831 calls will the audit identify as needing migration? (discover during audit)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

> Detailed context from brainstorming for task creation and obstacle handling.

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Close as verified vs harden? | Harden — 'we managed to delude ourselves over and over' | This is a structural fix, not a one-time patch |
| Approach: canary, refactor, or both? | Both — belt and suspenders | build-object-tx + shape assertions |
| Scope: canary only, key tests, or full audit? | Full audit of all object-creation calls | Comprehensive — close the category entirely |
| Where should shared function live? | engine/objects.cljs | New file, pure function, natural companion to zones.cljs |
| Include tokens.cljs? | Yes | All object creation goes through build-object-tx |
| Include restorer.cljs? | Yes | All object creation goes through build-object-tx |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Production Object Creation Paths
**Question explored:** How many independent object creation sites exist?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/init.cljs:56-110 — two functions (objects-tx, opponent-deck-tx)
- sharing/restorer.cljs:78-116 — object-tx-for-zone
- effects/tokens.cljs:13-44 — token creation
- test_helpers.cljs:80-95 — add-card-to-zone

**Findings:**
- 5 independent sites, all building similar Datascript tx maps
- Field sets ~90% identical (id, card, zone, owner, controller, tapped, position, P/T for creatures)
- Each site independently detects creatures and adds P/T — duplication is the root cause

**Conclusion:** Strong extraction candidate. Single function eliminates category.

#### Test Helper vs Production Divergence
**Question explored:** What fields are set differently by test helpers vs production?
**Sources consulted:**
- test_helpers.cljs:86-88 — battlefield creature setup
- events/init.cljs:56-78 — production creature setup
- engine/zones.cljs:106-109 — zone transition field setup

**Findings:**
- summoning-sick and damage-marked: set by test helpers for battlefield creatures, deferred to move-to-zone in production
- P/T: now aligned after fix, but was the original divergence point
- All other fields: identical across paths

**Conclusion:** Test helpers conflate creation and zone-transition concerns. Refactor separates them.

### Dead-End Paths

#### Full production init in test helpers
**Why explored:** Maximum fidelity — if tests use the same init as production, divergence is impossible
**Investigation:** :game/initialize creates entire game state (both players, decks, hands). Tests need surgical object placement in specific zones.
**Why abandoned:** Too coarse for test setup. The right extraction point is object construction, not game initialization.

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'How do we avoid this in the future?' → Single chokepoint (build-object-tx) + structural canary tests
- 'We managed to delude ourselves over and over' → Full audit ensures no hidden assumptions remain in existing tests
- summoning-sick / damage-marked masking → Not bugs today, but same structural risk. Canary tests will catch if they ever diverge.
close_reason: EPIC COMPLETE: All 11 success criteria met, reviewer APPROVED. Single build-object-tx chokepoint established across all 5 consumers. 10 canary tests guard against future divergence. 2191 calls audited. make validate exits 0 (3387 tests, 0 failures, 0 errors). 6 commits.

title: Extract modal components from core.cljs
description:
## Problem
core.cljs is 1,592 lines with 13 modal components that are 80-95% identical. Massive DRY violations in UI code.

## Specifics
- 3 player-target modals (player-target-modal, ability-player-target-modal, cast-time-player-target-modal) are structurally identical with different :on-click handlers
- 2 object-target modals share 95% code
- Modal overlay style (position fixed, rgba backdrop, z-index 1000) copy-pasted 13 times
- Modal container style (#1a1a2a, 2px solid #4A9BD9, border-radius 12px) copy-pasted 13 times
- Confirm/cancel button pattern repeated 8+ times with only event keyword changing
- Card selection grid rendering: identical for loop in 5+ modals

## Proposed Refactoring
- Extract shared style constants (modal-overlay-style, modal-container-style)
- Create generic modal-base component
- Create parameterized player-target-modal and object-target-modal factories
- Extract confirm-button and cancel-button helpers
- Extract card-selection-grid helper
- Split into views/modals.cljs, views/zones.cljs, views/controls.cljs
- Keep core.cljs as ~200 line router/init

## Success Criteria
- core.cljs under 300 lines
- No duplicated modal structure
- Shared style constants
- Estimated 400-500 LOC net reduction
- All modals still function correctly

title: Condition evaluator engine
description:
Create src/main/fizzle/bots/rules.cljs with a condition evaluator multimethod dispatching on :check keyword. Pure functions, no re-frame dependency.

## New File: src/main/fizzle/bots/rules.cljs
Namespace: fizzle.bots.rules
Requires: fizzle.db.queries, fizzle.engine.mana, fizzle.engine.stack, datascript.core

### Functions:
- (evaluate-condition condition context) -> boolean
  Multimethod dispatching on (:check condition)
  Context: {:db game-db :player-id player-id}

- Defmethods for :check values:
  :zone-contains — {:check :zone-contains :zone :hand :player :self :card-id :lightning-bolt}
    Resolve :self/:opponent to actual player-id, query zone for card-id match
  :has-mana-for — {:check :has-mana-for :card-id :lightning-bolt}
    Look up card cost, check mana/can-pay? OR check has-untapped-source for the cost colors
  :stack-empty — {:check :stack-empty}
    Delegate to queries/stack-empty?
  :has-untapped-source — {:check :has-untapped-source :color :red}
    Query battlefield for untapped lands producing color (move logic from protocol.cljs)
  :zone-count — {:check :zone-count :zone :graveyard :player :self :gte 7}
    Count objects in zone, compare with :gte/:lte/:eq
  :stack-has — {:check :stack-has :owner :self :type :trigger}
    Query stack for items matching owner + type

- (evaluate-conditions conditions context) -> boolean
  Returns true if ALL conditions evaluate to true (implicit AND)
  Returns true for empty conditions list (goldfish has no rules)

## New Test File: src/test/fizzle/bots/rules_test.cljs
Test each predicate against real game-db via test-helpers:
- zone-contains: card in hand (true), card not in hand (false), opponent zone
- has-mana-for: sufficient mana (true), insufficient (false)
- stack-empty: empty (true), spell on stack (false)
- has-untapped-source: untapped mountain (true), tapped (false), wrong color (false)
- zone-count: graveyard >= 7 (true), < 7 (false)
- stack-has: own trigger on stack, opponent spell on stack
- evaluate-conditions: AND semantics, empty list = true

## Verification
make validate
close_reason: Closed
