title: Rename bots/interceptor.cljs to reflect pure-computation role + update docstring
description: Rename bots/interceptor.cljs to bots/decisions.cljs (or similar). Update ALL requires across codebase (director.cljs, test files, core.cljs if still present). Rename interceptor_test.cljs accordingly. Update module docstring to describe current architecture: pure computation functions called inline by director, no event handlers. Run make validate.
design:
## Goal

Rename src/main/fizzle/bots/interceptor.cljs to src/main/fizzle/bots/decisions.cljs and update the namespace declaration, all requires pointing to the old namespace, the test file name and ns, and the director.cljs require alias. Update the module docstring to accurately describe the current architecture.

## Effort Estimate

1-2 hours. Pure rename + require updates — no logic changes.

## Definitive Target Name

New filename: decisions.cljs
New namespace: fizzle.bots.decisions
New test file: src/test/fizzle/bots/decisions_test.cljs
New test namespace: fizzle.bots.decisions-test
Director alias: :as bot-decisions (replaces :as bot-interceptor)

Rationale: the file provides bot-decide-action, bot-should-act?, find-tap-sequence — pure decision computation.
The name 'interceptor' was an artifact of the old re-frame interceptor architecture. 'decisions' describes what the module actually does.

## Files to Change

**src/main/fizzle/bots/interceptor.cljs → src/main/fizzle/bots/decisions.cljs**
- Rename the file (git mv)
- Change ns declaration line 1: fizzle.bots.interceptor → fizzle.bots.decisions
- Update module docstring to accurately describe current architecture (see below)

**src/main/fizzle/events/director.cljs (line 23)**
- Before: [fizzle.bots.interceptor :as bot-interceptor]
- After:  [fizzle.bots.decisions :as bot-decisions]
- Update all uses of bot-interceptor/ → bot-decisions/ in the file body
  (grep: bot-interceptor/bot-should-act?, bot-interceptor/bot-decide-action, bot-interceptor/find-tap-sequence)

**src/test/fizzle/bots/interceptor_test.cljs → src/test/fizzle/bots/decisions_test.cljs**
- Rename the file (git mv)
- Change ns declaration line 1: fizzle.bots.interceptor-test → fizzle.bots.decisions-test
- Update ns docstring to remove 'interceptor' references
- Change require line 12: [fizzle.bots.interceptor :as interceptor] → [fizzle.bots.decisions :as decisions]
- Update all uses of interceptor/ → decisions/ in test bodies
  (grep: interceptor/bot-should-act?, interceptor/bot-decide-action, interceptor/find-tap-sequence)

**NOTE: src/main/fizzle/core.cljs does NOT need changes** — the bare interceptor require was removed in fizzle-s0ac.

## Updated Module Docstring

Replace the existing docstring in decisions.cljs with:

  "Bot decision computation: pure functions called by the director.

   Exported functions:
   - bot-should-act?   — true if current priority holder is a bot
   - bot-decide-action — asks bot protocol for an action plan
   - find-tap-sequence — allocates mana-producing lands to pay a cost

   Architecture: all functions are pure (no side effects, no re-frame).
   The director (events/director.cljs) calls these inline during its loop
   and applies the resulting actions through the standard event pipeline."

## Success Criteria

- [ ] grep -rn 'fizzle.bots.interceptor' src/ returns 0 results
- [ ] grep -rn 'bot-interceptor/' src/ returns 0 results
- [ ] src/main/fizzle/bots/interceptor.cljs does not exist (file was renamed, not copied)
- [ ] src/test/fizzle/bots/interceptor_test.cljs does not exist (renamed)
- [ ] make validate passes: 0 failures, 0 errors, lint clean, fmt clean
- [ ] Test count unchanged from before rename (3134 — rename doesn't add or remove tests)

## Implementation Checklist

- [ ] Read director.cljs lines 21-35 to confirm exact alias names to update
- [ ] git mv src/main/fizzle/bots/interceptor.cljs src/main/fizzle/bots/decisions.cljs
- [ ] Edit decisions.cljs line 1: change ns to fizzle.bots.decisions
- [ ] Edit decisions.cljs: replace docstring with updated version above
- [ ] Edit director.cljs line 23: change require path and alias
- [ ] grep -n 'bot-interceptor/' src/main/fizzle/events/director.cljs — update all occurrences
- [ ] git mv src/test/fizzle/bots/interceptor_test.cljs src/test/fizzle/bots/decisions_test.cljs
- [ ] Edit decisions_test.cljs line 1: change ns to fizzle.bots.decisions-test
- [ ] Edit decisions_test.cljs ns docstring: remove interceptor references
- [ ] Edit decisions_test.cljs require: change namespace path and alias
- [ ] grep -n 'interceptor/' src/test/fizzle/bots/decisions_test.cljs — update all occurrences
- [ ] grep -rn 'fizzle.bots.interceptor' src/ — confirm 0 results
- [ ] grep -rn 'bot-interceptor/' src/ — confirm 0 results
- [ ] Run make fmt
- [ ] Run make validate — confirm 0 failures, lint clean, test count 3134

## Key Considerations

**ClojureScript namespace/filename coupling**:
In ClojureScript, the ns declaration MUST match the file path. fizzle.bots.decisions maps to bots/decisions.cljs under the source root. If the file is renamed but the ns declaration is wrong, shadow-cljs may compile but load the wrong namespace — a silent bug. Verify both the filename AND ns declaration change together.

**Director alias staleness**:
director.cljs currently uses bot-interceptor as the alias. After rename, update to bot-decisions. All call sites in director.cljs that say bot-interceptor/X must be updated to bot-decisions/X. Do a targeted grep before editing to get the exact count.

**Test discovery via :ns-regexp**:
shadow-cljs discovers tests via :ns-regexp '-test$'. The new ns fizzle.bots.decisions-test ends in -test, so test discovery will work. If you accidentally name it fizzle.bots.decisions-tests (plural), it will be silently excluded. Verify the ns ends in exactly -test (singular).

**Stale build cache**:
After renaming, if make validate shows unexpected errors, run make clean first. The old interceptor compiled output may linger in out/ or .shadow-cljs/.

**git mv, not cp+rm**:
Use git mv (not manual copy-and-delete) so git history shows a rename, not a delete+create. This preserves blame history on the kept functions (bot-should-act?, bot-decide-action, find-tap-sequence).

## Anti-patterns

- Do not leave any file named interceptor.cljs or interceptor_test.cljs after the rename
- Do not leave the alias bot-interceptor in director.cljs (stale name misleads future readers)
- Do not create a new file while leaving the old one (would compile both namespaces, causing duplicate registration risk)
- Do not rename without updating the ns declaration — filename and ns must match in ClojureScript
close_reason: Renamed interceptor.cljs→decisions.cljs + test file. Updated all requires/aliases. 3134 tests pass.

title: Conditional & Alternate-Cost Counters: Mana Leak, Daze, Foil
description: Second epic in the counterspell chain. Builds on Epic 1 (fizzle-plg3) counter foundation to add soft counters and alternate casting costs.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Unless-they-pay mechanic: counter target spell unless its controller pays {X}
- Alternate cost — return land: return an Island you control to hand instead of paying mana cost (Daze)
- Alternate cost — discard specific cards: discard an Island card and another card instead of paying mana cost (Foil)
- Mana Leak: {1}{U} instant, counter target spell unless its controller pays {3}
- Daze: {1}{U} instant, alternate cost return Island, counter unless controller pays {1}
- Foil: {2}{U}{U} instant, alternate cost discard Island + another card, counter target spell
- Both player and bot can cast these cards
- Payment choice presented to the targeted spell's controller as an interactive selection

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Unless-they-pay mechanic works as interactive selection
- [ ] Return-land alternate casting cost works (Daze)
- [ ] Discard-specific-cards alternate casting cost works (Foil)
- [ ] Mana Leak defined with correct oracle text and behavior
- [ ] Daze defined with correct oracle text, both casting modes work
- [ ] Foil defined with correct oracle text, both casting modes work
- [ ] Bot can cast these spells (alternate costs included)
- [ ] Player gets payment prompt for soft counters
- [ ] Full test suites per CLAUDE.md requirements
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO auto-resolving payment choices (interaction: player must always choose whether to pay)
- ❌ NO hardcoding Island subtype checks (data: use land subtype matching)
- ❌ NO skipping alternate cost validation (correctness: must verify player has required cards/lands before offering alternate cost)
- ❌ NO special-casing card names in engine code (architecture: cards are data)
- ❌ NO tautological test assertions (testing: assert exact values)
- ❌ NO reimplementing production handlers in tests (testing: test through production code)

## Approach
Extend the counter foundation from Epic 1. Add unless-they-pay as an interactive effect parameter on :counter-spell — when present, creates a payment selection for the targeted spell's controller. Add return-land and discard-specific as new alternate casting cost types, extending the existing casting mode system used by flashback.

## Architecture
- engine/effects.cljs — Extend :counter-spell with :effect/unless-pay parameter
- engine/rules.cljs — New alternate cost types for casting modes
- events/selection/ — Payment selection handler for unless-they-pay
- cards/blue/mana_leak.cljs, cards/blue/daze.cljs, cards/blue/foil.cljs
- cards/registry.cljs — Register new cards

## Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** Soft counters, return-land cost, discard-specific cost
**Out of scope:** Modal spells, color targeting — covered by Epic 3
**Depends on:** Epic 1 (fizzle-plg3) counter foundation

title: Unless-they-pay mechanic: counter-spell with payment selection
description: Add :effect/unless-pay parameter to :counter-spell effect. When present, creates an interactive payment selection for the targeted spell's controller instead of immediately countering. Player chooses whether to pay {X} mana.

title: Mana Leak card definition and tests
description: Create Mana Leak: {1}{U} instant - Counter target spell unless its controller pays {3}. Uses the unless-they-pay mechanic from the previous story. Full test suite per CLAUDE.md requirements.

title: Return-land alternate casting cost type
description: Add return-land as a new alternate casting cost type. For Daze: return an Island you control to hand instead of paying mana cost. Extends the existing alternate cost system (used by flashback). Requires: identifying lands with Island subtype on battlefield, adding :return-land additional cost type to costs.cljs, and selection for choosing which Island to return.

title: Daze card definition and tests
description: Create Daze: {1}{U} instant - Counter target spell unless its controller pays {1}. Alternate cost: return an Island you control to owner's hand. Uses unless-they-pay from story 1 and return-land alternate cost from story 3. Full test suite per CLAUDE.md requirements.

title: Discard-specific alternate casting cost type
description: Add discard-specific as a new alternate casting cost type. For Foil: discard an Island card and another card instead of paying mana cost. The 'Island card' is any card with Island subtype. Extends the alternate cost system with interactive card selection for choosing which cards to discard.

title: Foil card definition and tests
description: Create Foil: {2}{U}{U} instant - Counter target spell. Alternate cost: You may discard an Island card and another card rather than pay this spell's mana cost. Uses discard-specific alternate cost from story 5. Full test suite per CLAUDE.md requirements.

title: Card tests call effects/execute-effect directly bypassing resolution dispatch
description:
## Problem

Several card tests call effects/execute-effect directly with hand-built effect maps instead of going through the full cast → stack → resolve-one-item pipeline. This tests the effect logic but not:
- Whether the card's effects are correctly wired to its stack item
- Whether resolution dispatch (multimethod on :stack-item/type) routes correctly
- Whether reduce-effects handles the card's effect chain properly (pausing at interactive effects, chaining remaining effects)

## Risk: Low-Medium

Resolution dispatch is shared infrastructure and well-tested. But cards with multi-effect chains (interactive + remaining effects) are most at risk — a bug in how effects are sequenced through reduce-effects wouldn't be caught.

## Affected files
- careful_study_test.cljs (calls execute-effect directly)
- flash_of_insight_test.cljs (calls library/execute-peek-selection directly)
- impulse_test.cljs (calls execute-confirmed-selection directly)
- sleight_of_hand_test.cljs (calls library/execute-peek-selection directly)
- opt_test.cljs (calls library/execute-* directly)
- intuition_test.cljs (calls library/build-tutor-selection directly)
- merchant_scroll_test.cljs (calls library/build-tutor-selection directly)

## Fix

Add at least one happy-path test per card that goes through rules/cast-spell → game/resolve-one-item to verify the full pipeline.
close_reason: Fixed: happy-path tests now use production-path helpers (th/cast-with-target, th/resolve-top, th/confirm-selection, th/cast-mode-with-target)

title: Task 1: Implement :destroy effect type
design:
## Goal
Add :destroy effect type to effects.cljs that moves target permanent to owner's graveyard.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing effect patterns (reference)
- src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:484-528 - :grant-flashback shows target-ref resolution pattern
- src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:391-406 - :sacrifice shows zone movement pattern
- src/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs:39 - move-to-zone function signature

### 2. Write tests first (TDD) - RED phase
Create test file or add to existing effects_test.cljs:

```clojure
;; Test 1: Happy path - destroy moves target to graveyard
(deftest test-destroy-effect-moves-target-to-graveyard
  ;; Setup: enchantment on battlefield, spell targeting it
  ;; Action: execute :destroy effect
  ;; Assert: enchantment is in graveyard zone)

;; Test 2: Owner vs controller - target goes to OWNER's graveyard
(deftest test-destroy-effect-uses-owner-graveyard-not-controller
  ;; Setup: Player A owns enchantment, Player B controls it (e.g., stolen)
  ;; Action: destroy effect on the enchantment
  ;; Assert: enchantment in Player A's graveyard, not Player B's)

;; Test 3: Edge case - nil object-id returns db unchanged
(deftest test-destroy-effect-nil-object-id-is-noop
  ;; Setup: valid db
  ;; Action: execute :destroy with object-id nil
  ;; Assert: db unchanged)

;; Test 4: Edge case - missing target-ref returns db unchanged  
(deftest test-destroy-effect-missing-target-ref-is-noop
  ;; Setup: source object has no stored targets
  ;; Action: execute :destroy with target-ref :foo
  ;; Assert: db unchanged)

;; Test 5: Edge case - target object no longer exists
(deftest test-destroy-effect-missing-target-object-is-noop
  ;; Setup: stored target-id points to non-existent object
  ;; Action: execute :destroy
  ;; Assert: db unchanged, no error)
```

### 3. Implementation checklist (GREEN phase)

- [ ] effects.cljs - Add multimethod:
```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect-impl :destroy
  ;; Destroy target permanent - move it to owner's graveyard.
  ;;
  ;; Effect keys:
  ;;   :effect/target-ref - Target reference keyword to look up from
  ;;                        :object/targets on the casting object.
  ;;
  ;; Handles edge cases:
  ;;   - object-id nil: no-op (returns db unchanged)
  ;;   - No stored target for target-ref: no-op
  ;;   - Target object doesn't exist: no-op
  ;;
  ;; Note: Goes to OWNER's graveyard, not controller's. This matters
  ;; for stolen permanents (Control Magic effects).
  [db _player-id effect object-id]
  (if-not object-id
    db  ; No source object - no-op
    (let [source-obj (q/get-object db object-id)
          target-ref (:effect/target-ref effect)
          stored-targets (:object/targets source-obj)
          target-id (get stored-targets target-ref)]
      (if-not target-id
        db  ; No stored target for this ref - no-op
        (if-let [_target-obj (q/get-object db target-id)]
          (zones/move-to-zone db target-id :graveyard)
          db)))))  ; Target doesn't exist - no-op
```

- [ ] Verify zones/move-to-zone handles owner correctly (it should - zone is per-player)
- [ ] Add tests to effects_test.cljs following patterns from existing tests

## Success Criteria
- [ ] test-destroy-effect-moves-target-to-graveyard passes
- [ ] test-destroy-effect-uses-owner-graveyard-not-controller passes
- [ ] test-destroy-effect-nil-object-id-is-noop passes
- [ ] test-destroy-effect-missing-target-ref-is-noop passes
- [ ] test-destroy-effect-missing-target-object-is-noop passes
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Nil object-id**
- Source object not provided (effect run without context)
- MUST return db unchanged, not throw
- Pattern: `(if-not object-id db ...)`

**Edge Case: Missing stored targets**
- Source object exists but has no :object/targets map
- Or target-ref key doesn't exist in map
- MUST return db unchanged
- Pattern: `(if-not target-id db ...)`

**Edge Case: Target object already gone**
- Target-id stored but object moved zones/removed
- MUST return db unchanged
- Pattern: `(if-let [_target-obj (q/get-object db target-id)] ... db)`

**Owner vs Controller**
- MTG rule: destroyed permanents go to owner's graveyard
- zones/move-to-zone should handle this (zones are per-player based on owner)
- VERIFY: Check that move-to-zone uses object's owner, not controller

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow :grant-flashback pattern (lines 484-528) for target-ref resolution
- Follow :sacrifice pattern (lines 391-406) for zone movement

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN for this task)
- ❌ NO using :effect/target directly (must use :effect/target-ref with stored targets)
- ❌ NO throwing on edge cases (return db unchanged)
- ❌ NO assuming target exists (must check with q/get-object)
- ❌ NO using controller's graveyard (must be owner's)
close_reason: Closed

title: Cast & Yield requires extra yield for generic mana payment
description: When casting a spell that requires generic mana allocation (interactive mana payment), Cast & Yield does not auto-resolve after the mana selection completes. The player must click Yield again manually. Expected: Cast & Yield should resolve the spell and advance after the mana allocation selection finishes, same as it does for spells with no generic cost.
close_reason: Fixed: cast-and-yield sets yield flag on selection, allocate-mana-color dispatches cast-and-yield-resolve after allocation completes

title: Epic: Always-visible zone counts (GY, Library, Exile)
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Zone counts for Graveyard, Library, and Exile always visible on game screen without expanding any panel
- Both player and opponent counts shown
- Graveyard count visually indicates threshold status (>= 7 cards in graveyard)
- Placed below mana pool/storm count area in main left column

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Player GY, Library, Exile counts always visible on game screen
- [ ] Opponent GY, Library, Exile counts always visible on game screen
- [ ] GY count changes to accent color when >= 7 (threshold active)
- [ ] Counts update reactively as zones change (cast, draw, mill, exile, etc.)
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO putting counts inside collapsible right panels (defeats 'always visible' — the entire point of this feature)
- NO click-to-expand card lists from zone counts (keep simple — graveyard panel already exists for browsing cards)
- NO fetching card images or detailed card data for display (just integer counts)
- NO new CSS grid columns or layout restructuring (add component within existing main column flow)

## Approach
Add a new zone-counts view component below the existing mana pool / storm count area in the main left column. The component renders two compact rows — one for player zones, one for opponent zones — showing GY, Library, and Exile counts. Graveyard count uses accent color when threshold (>= 7) is met, matching the existing threshold badge style in the graveyard panel.

New re-frame subscriptions derive zone counts from game-db. A simple query helper counts entities in a zone for a given player, keeping the subscription layer thin.

## Architecture
- New: src/fizzle/views/zone_counts.cljs — zone-counts-view component
- New subs in: src/fizzle/subs/game.cljs — player/opponent zone count subscriptions
- Possibly new query in: src/fizzle/db/queries.cljs — zone-count-by-player helper
- Modified: src/fizzle/core.cljs — add zone-counts-view after mana pool flex row
- Tests: test/fizzle/views/zone_counts_test.cljs for component, test/fizzle/subs/game_test.cljs for subs

## Parallelism Map

### Independent Work Streams
1. **Subscriptions + queries** (subs/game.cljs, db/queries.cljs)
   - Add zone count subscriptions for both players
   - Estimated complexity: low
2. **View component** (views/zone_counts.cljs)
   - Build the zone-counts-view Reagent component
   - Estimated complexity: low
3. **Integration** (core.cljs)
   - Wire component into game layout
   - Estimated complexity: trivial

### Stream Dependencies
- Stream 2 depends on Stream 1 (component subscribes to zone count subs)
- Stream 3 depends on Stream 2 (layout requires component)

### Suggested Waves
- All sequential — each stream depends on previous

### Parallelism Assessment
- Independent streams: 0 (fully sequential)
- Recommendation: solo execution
- Rationale: Small feature, all streams depend on each other, no parallel opportunity

## Design Rationale
### Problem
During gameplay, players need to track graveyard size (threshold at 7+) and opponent library size (Brain Freeze lethality). Currently graveyard count is only visible when the graveyard panel is expanded, and library count is not shown at all during gameplay. Players must mentally track these critical numbers.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- views/core.cljs:57-79 — Game screen is CSS grid: 1 main column + 3 collapsible right panels
- views/graveyard.cljs — Already shows 'GRAVEYARD (N)' with threshold badge, but only when panel expanded
- subs/game.cljs — Has ::graveyard sub returning full card data, but no count-only subs
- db/queries.cljs:79 — get-objects-in-zone can fetch any zone's objects
- views/mana_pool.cljs — Mana pool + storm count rendered as flex row at bottom of main column

**Existing patterns:**
- Storm count displayed as compact inline element next to mana pool
- Threshold badge in graveyard panel uses accent color styling
- Zone queries use player entity + zone keyword to filter objects

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. New zone-counts component below mana pool ✓
**What it is:** Dedicated Reagent component in views/zone_counts.cljs, placed after mana pool flex row in main column. Two rows showing player and opponent zone counts.

**Pros:**
- Clean separation of concerns (own file, own tests)
- Follows existing pattern of small focused view components
- Slots naturally into layout flow

**Cons:**
- Adds one more visual element to main column

**Chosen because:** Matches codebase pattern of separate view components, doesn't modify existing components

#### 2. Extend mana-pool view ❌
**What it is:** Add zone counts into the existing mana-pool.cljs component as a sub-section.

**Why explored:** Would keep zone info near other game metrics without a new file.

**Pros:** Fewer files, compact

**Cons:** Mixes mana and zone concerns, mana-pool.cljs grows beyond its purpose

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Violates single-responsibility — mana pool component should only handle mana display

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We're redesigning the entire bottom panel as a unified game-stats dashboard

#### 3. Floating fixed-position bar ❌
**What it is:** CSS fixed-position bar at bottom of viewport, always visible regardless of scroll.

**Why explored:** Guarantees visibility even on small screens with scrolling.

**Pros:** Truly always visible

**Cons:** Overlaps content, non-standard layout, extra CSS complexity, fights with grid layout

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Over-engineered — the main column doesn't scroll in normal use, and a floating bar adds CSS complexity for no practical gain

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Users report scrolling issues hiding zone counts on small screens

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- GY, Library, Exile counts for both players
- Threshold visual indicator on graveyard count
- New subscriptions and view component

**Out of scope:**
- Clicking counts to open zone details (graveyard panel exists for this)
- Hand or battlefield counts (already visible on screen)
- Opponent life total in this component (already shown in opponent view)
- Zone count animations or transitions

### Open Questions
- Exact Tailwind classes for layout — resolve during implementation by matching existing mana pool styling

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Where to place counts? | Below mana pool area | Component goes after mana-pool/storm flex row in core.cljs |
| Which zones? | GY + Library + Exile | Three counts per player, six total |
| Show opponent? | All opponent zones | Need opponent-specific subscriptions |
| Threshold indicator? | Yes, color change at 7+ | GY count uses accent color class when >= 7 |

### Research Deep-Dives
#### Current Zone Display
**Question:** Where are zone counts currently shown?
**Sources:** views/graveyard.cljs, views/core.cljs, subs/game.cljs
**Findings:**
- Graveyard count only in collapsible panel header
- Library count only in opening-hand screen and game-over modal
- Exile not visualized anywhere
- No always-visible zone count component exists
**Conclusion:** New component needed — cannot enhance existing components to meet 'always visible' requirement

### Dead-End Paths
None — straightforward feature with clear requirements.

### Open Concerns Raised
None raised during brainstorming.
close_reason: All success criteria met. Zone counts always visible below mana pool for both players. Threshold indicator on GY. 11 new tests total, make validate passes.

title: Create interactive-effect? predicate and unify detection functions
design:
## Goal
Create a single `interactive-effect?` predicate in selection/resolution.cljs that is the canonical source of truth for effect interactivity. Rewrite `has-selection-effect?` and `find-selection-effect-index` as thin wrappers. Investigate :pile-choice gap.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

### Step 1: Read existing code
- `src/main/fizzle/events/selection/resolution.cljs:23-53` — current `has-selection-effect?` and `find-selection-effect-index`
- `src/main/fizzle/events/selection/core.cljs:35-54` — `build-selection-for-effect` multimethod dispatch function
- `src/main/fizzle/events/selection/library.cljs` — :pile-choice builder registration

### Step 2: Investigate :pile-choice
- Find card definitions using :pile-choice effects: `grep -r ':pile-choice' src/`
- Trace call path: does :pile-choice reach `resolve-spell-with-selection` or is it triggered differently?
- Decision: add to predicate if same path, document as separate path otherwise

### Step 3: Write tests (TDD — RED phase)
Test file: `src/test/fizzle/events/selection_resolution_test.cljs` (new file)

**Predicate tests (interactive-effect?):**
- `test-tutor-is-interactive`: `{:effect/type :tutor}` → true (catches: missing always-interactive type)
- `test-peek-and-select-is-interactive`: `{:effect/type :peek-and-select}` → true (catches: type that was missing from has-selection-effect?)
- `test-scry-positive-amount-is-interactive`: `{:effect/type :scry :effect/amount 3}` → true (catches: conditional check bug)
- `test-scry-zero-amount-is-not-interactive`: `{:effect/type :scry :effect/amount 0}` → false (catches: missing amount guard)
- `test-scry-nil-amount-is-not-interactive`: `{:effect/type :scry}` → false (catches: NPE on nil amount)
- `test-discard-player-selection-is-interactive`: `{:effect/type :discard :effect/selection :player}` → true (catches: missing :selection :player check)
- `test-discard-random-selection-is-not-interactive`: `{:effect/type :discard :effect/selection :random}` → false (catches: overly broad type matching)
- `test-return-from-graveyard-player-is-interactive`: `{:effect/type :return-from-graveyard :effect/selection :player}` → true (catches: covered by :selection :player)
- `test-return-from-graveyard-random-is-not-interactive`: `{:effect/type :return-from-graveyard :effect/selection :random}` → false (catches: type-only matching bug)
- `test-any-player-target-is-interactive`: `{:effect/type :draw :effect/target :any-player}` → true (catches: missing target check)
- `test-non-interactive-effect-types`: `{:effect/type :add-mana}`, `{:effect/type :draw}`, `{:effect/type :mill}`, `{:effect/type :exile-self}` → all false (catches: overly broad predicate)
- `test-empty-effect-map-is-not-interactive`: `{}` → false (catches: NPE on missing keys)
- `test-overlapping-conditions`: `{:effect/type :tutor :effect/selection :player}` → true (catches: double-match doesn't cause issues, predicate uses `or` so fine)

**Wrapper tests (has-selection-effect? and find-selection-effect-index):**
- `test-has-selection-effect-empty-list`: `[]` → falsy (catches: nil/empty handling)
- `test-has-selection-effect-finds-peek-and-select`: list with :peek-and-select → truthy (catches: the detection gap bug — was missing before)
- `test-find-selection-effect-index-returns-correct-position`: 3-effect list with interactive at index 1 → 1 (catches: index calculation bug)
- `test-find-selection-effect-index-nil-when-none`: list of non-interactive effects → nil

**Sync test (predicate covers all builders):**
- `test-predicate-covers-all-builder-dispatch-values`: get all dispatch values from `(methods build-selection-for-effect)`, construct representative effect map for each, verify `interactive-effect?` returns true. Catches: future drift when adding new interactive effect types.

### Step 4: Create interactive-effect? predicate (GREEN phase)
Add to resolution.cljs between line 22 (section comment) and line 23 (current has-selection-effect?):

```clojure
(defn interactive-effect?
  "Returns true if this effect requires player selection/interaction.
   This is the single source of truth for which effects pause resolution
   to collect player input via the selection system.

   Checks:
   - :effect/selection :player — explicit player-choice marker
   - :effect/type :tutor — always interactive (search library)
   - :effect/type :scry with amount > 0 — arrange top N cards
   - :effect/target :any-player — choose target player
   - :effect/type :peek-and-select — choose from revealed cards"
  [effect]
  (or (= :player (:effect/selection effect))
      (= :tutor (:effect/type effect))
      (and (= :scry (:effect/type effect))
           (pos? (or (:effect/amount effect) 0)))
      (= :any-player (:effect/target effect))
      (= :peek-and-select (:effect/type effect))))
```

Note: :pile-choice may be added here if investigation shows it uses the same path.

### Step 5: Rewrite has-selection-effect? (GREEN phase)
Replace lines 23-30 with:
```clojure
(defn has-selection-effect?
  "Check if any effect in the list requires player selection."
  [effects]
  (some interactive-effect? effects))
```

### Step 6: Rewrite find-selection-effect-index (GREEN phase)
Replace lines 33-53 with:
```clojure
(defn find-selection-effect-index
  "Find the index of the first effect that requires player selection.
   Returns nil if no selection effect found."
  [effects]
  (first (keep-indexed (fn [i effect]
                         (when (interactive-effect? effect) i))
                       effects)))
```

### Step 7: Verify (REFACTOR phase)
- `make validate` — all tests pass, lint clean, format clean
- Verify no callers of has-selection-effect? or find-selection-effect-index changed behavior

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `interactive-effect?` predicate exists in selection/resolution.cljs
- [ ] Predicate handles all 5 detection patterns: :selection :player, :tutor, :scry+pos?, :any-player target, :peek-and-select
- [ ] `has-selection-effect?` body is `(some interactive-effect? effects)`
- [ ] `find-selection-effect-index` uses `interactive-effect?` in keep-indexed
- [ ] :pile-choice investigated: either added to predicate or documented as separate code path
- [ ] 15+ new tests exist covering predicate, wrappers, and sync with builder multimethod
- [ ] Sync test verifies predicate covers all `build-selection-for-effect` dispatch values
- [ ] All existing 1265+ tests pass unchanged
- [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (task-specific)
- ❌ NO changes to engine/effects.cljs (epic anti-pattern: engine stays pure)
- ❌ NO changes to build-selection-for-effect dispatch logic (scope: detection only)
- ❌ NO new requires in resolution.cljs that could create circular dependencies
- ❌ NO second predicate — interactive-effect? is THE source of truth
- ❌ NO changing behavior of has-selection-effect? or find-selection-effect-index for existing callers beyond fixing the documented gap

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Overlapping conditions**
- An effect can match multiple conditions (e.g., `{:effect/type :tutor :effect/selection :player}`)
- The predicate uses `or` so this is safe — returns true on first match
- Test explicitly to document this behavior

**Edge Case: Malformed effect maps**
- Effect with no :effect/type key: `{}` → should return false
- Effect with nil values: `{:effect/type nil}` → should return false
- All keyword comparisons use `=` which handles nil safely (returns false)

**Edge Case: Empty effects list**
- `(has-selection-effect? [])` → `(some pred [])` → nil (falsy) ✅
- `(find-selection-effect-index [])` → `(first (keep-indexed ...))` → nil ✅

**Edge Case: :return-from-graveyard detection**
- `find-selection-effect-index` currently checks `(= :return-from-graveyard (:effect/type effect))` as a standalone condition
- But :return-from-graveyard is only interactive when `:effect/selection :player`
- The new predicate does NOT have a standalone :return-from-graveyard check — it's covered by the :selection :player check
- This means `{:effect/type :return-from-graveyard}` (no :selection key) will no longer match
- VERIFY: all :return-from-graveyard card effects that are interactive have :selection :player
- This is a behavior change in find-selection-effect-index — test to confirm correctness

**:pile-choice Investigation Guide**
- If :pile-choice goes through resolve-spell-with-selection → add to predicate
- If :pile-choice is triggered by a different path (e.g., separate event handler) → document in a code comment, skip adding to predicate
- The sync test will flag this either way

**Reference Implementation**
- Existing code: resolution.cljs:23-53
- Builder multimethod: core.cljs:35-54
- Pattern to follow: simple predicate function, same style as existing helpers
close_reason: interactive-effect? predicate created, wrappers rewritten, 26 tests added, sync test covers all builder dispatch values, :pile-choice documented as separate chained path, :return-from-graveyard over-matching fixed

title: Fix bot turn history — phase-by-phase yield for bot turns
Extract selection/targeting.cljs with cast-time and player-target domains
design:
## Goal

Extract targeting-related selection types from selection.cljs into events/selection/targeting.cljs. This includes cast-time-targeting and player-target builders, helpers, defmethods, and builder multimethod registrations.

## Context
Completed fizzle-du6w (library.cljs extraction). selection.cljs now has ~524 lines with targeting, costs, zone-ops, and shared event handlers remaining.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Create events/selection/targeting.cljs
Move these from selection.cljs:

**Builders:**
- build-cast-time-target-selection (selection.cljs ~line 238)
- build-player-target-selection (private → stays private, callers co-located) (selection.cljs ~line 186)

**Helpers:**
- cast-spell-with-targeting (selection.cljs ~line 264)
- confirm-cast-time-target (selection.cljs ~line 302)

**Defmethods on core/execute-confirmed-selection:**
- :cast-time-targeting (selection.cljs ~line 414)
- :player-target (selection.cljs ~line 375)

**Defmethods on core/build-selection-for-effect:**
- :player-target (selection.cljs ~line 216)

Requires for targeting.cljs:
- fizzle.events.selection.core :as core
- fizzle.events.selection.resolution :as resolution (used by :player-target defmethod for find-selection-effect-index, split-effects-around-index)
- datascript.core :as d (used by confirm-cast-time-target)
- fizzle.db.queries :as queries (used by cast-spell-with-targeting)
- fizzle.engine.effects :as effects (used by :player-target defmethod)
- fizzle.engine.rules :as rules (used by cast-spell-with-targeting, confirm-cast-time-target)
- fizzle.engine.targeting :as targeting (used by build-cast-time-target-selection, cast-spell-with-targeting)
- fizzle.engine.zones :as zones (used by :player-target defmethod — currently not used, check)

Does NOT need: engine/grants, engine/mana, engine/stack, clojure.set, re-frame

### 2. Update selection.cljs
Remove all moved functions and defmethods. Also remove 3 requires no longer needed:
- REMOVE: fizzle.engine.effects (only used by :player-target defmethod, moving out)
- REMOVE: fizzle.engine.targeting (only used by targeting code, moving out)
- REMOVE: fizzle.events.selection.resolution (only used by :player-target defmethod, moving out)

KEEP these requires (still used by remaining costs/zone-ops code):
- datascript.core (for :exile-cards-cost, :x-mana-cost defmethods)
- fizzle.db.queries (for builders)
- fizzle.engine.grants (for :cleanup-discard)
- fizzle.engine.mana (for :x-mana-cost)
- fizzle.engine.rules (for :exile-cards-cost, :x-mana-cost defmethods)
- fizzle.engine.zones (for :discard, :cleanup-discard, :graveyard-return, :exile-cards-cost)
- fizzle.events.selection.core (for defmethod registrations)
- re-frame.core (for event handlers)

### 3. Update game.cljs
game.cljs line 241 uses selection/build-cast-time-target-selection. Change to:
- Add [fizzle.events.selection.targeting :as sel-targeting] to requires
- Change selection/build-cast-time-target-selection → sel-targeting/build-cast-time-target-selection
NOTE: game.cljs does NOT call cast-spell-with-targeting (only test files do)

### 4. Update 2 test files
Exact callers identified:

1. src/test/fizzle/cards/recoup_test.cljs
   - selection/cast-spell-with-targeting (11 calls) → targeting/cast-spell-with-targeting
   - selection/confirm-cast-time-target (11 calls) → targeting/confirm-cast-time-target
   - Uses NO other selection functions → replace selection require with targeting

2. src/test/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis_test.cljs
   - selection/cast-spell-with-targeting (6 calls) → targeting/cast-spell-with-targeting
   - selection/confirm-cast-time-target (5 calls) → targeting/confirm-cast-time-target
   - Uses NO other selection functions → replace selection require with targeting

For each: add [fizzle.events.selection.targeting :as targeting] to requires.
Remove [fizzle.events.selection :as selection] since no other selection functions used.

### 5. Event handler namespace
targeting.cljs has NO re-frame event handlers (targeting is done via toggle/confirm on the base selection events). No namespace keyword issues.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] events/selection/targeting.cljs exists with all targeting domain code
- [ ] targeting.cljs requires only core.cljs, resolution.cljs, and engine/* (no other domain files)
- [ ] All 2 builders, 2 helpers, 3 defmethods moved
- [ ] game.cljs updated to import build-cast-time-target-selection from new namespace
- [ ] selection.cljs no longer has targeting code
- [ ] selection.cljs no longer requires engine/effects, engine/targeting, or selection.resolution
- [ ] 2 test files updated (recoup_test.cljs, deep_analysis_test.cljs)
- [ ] All ~1266 tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] No circular dependencies

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: zones require in targeting.cljs**
The :player-target execute defmethod does NOT use zones directly (it uses effects/execute-effect). Verify before adding zones to targeting.cljs requires — may not be needed. If not needed, omit (cleaner dependency graph).

**Edge Case: game.cljs alias naming**
game.cljs already has [fizzle.engine.targeting :as targeting] require. The selection targeting module needs a DIFFERENT alias. Use sel-targeting to avoid conflict.

**Edge Case: resolution.cljs require creates deeper dependency**
targeting.cljs → resolution.cljs → core.cljs. This is fine (acyclic), but verify resolution doesn't transitively depend on targeting (would be circular).

**Edge Case: build-player-target-selection visibility**
Function is defn- (private) in selection.cljs. When moved to targeting.cljs, callers (:player-target builder defmethod) are co-located, so it stays defn- (private). No visibility change needed.

**Edge Case: deep_analysis_test.cljs has fizzle.engine.targeting require**
deep_analysis_test.cljs already has [fizzle.engine.targeting :as targeting]. The new require would use same alias. Must use DIFFERENT alias like sel-targeting, or replace the engine/targeting alias since the test may use it elsewhere.

**Reference: Pattern from library extraction**
Follow exact same pattern as fizzle-du6w: create file, move functions, update callers, run make validate.

## Anti-Patterns
- NO importing domain files in targeting.cljs (only core.cljs, resolution.cljs, and engine/*)
- NO leaving orphaned defmethods in selection.cljs for targeting types
- NO keeping stale requires in selection.cljs after extraction (clean up effects/targeting/resolution)
- NO using same alias as existing engine/targeting in test files (use sel-targeting or similar)
close_reason: Closed

title: Move SBA and bot checks to :db effect handler chokepoint
description: Eliminate observer pattern for SBA and bot interceptors. Move both to the :db effect handler — the single structural chokepoint where every game-db mutation passes. Fix auto-confirm to dispatch instead of inlining confirm-selection-impl. Delete both interceptors.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- SBAs fire on EVERY game-db mutation without maintaining a trigger event set
- Bot checks fire on EVERY game-db mutation without maintaining a trigger event set
- Both checks happen at the :db effect handler chokepoint — structurally guaranteed, impossible to bypass
- Auto-confirm in toggle-selection-impl dispatches ::confirm-selection instead of calling confirm-selection-impl directly
- SBA interceptor deleted entirely (events/interceptors/sba.cljs)
- Bot interceptor deleted entirely (bots/interceptor.cljs bot-action-interceptor + register!)
- Inline SBA calls removed from reduce-effects (engine/effects.cljs:207,211) and mana_activation.cljs
- All existing tests pass or are updated to reflect new architecture
- Game-over triggers correctly for all damage sources (Fling, Lightning Bolt, Drain, etc.)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Custom :db effect handler registered in core.cljs init
- [ ] :db effect handler runs check-and-execute-sbas when game-db changes (identical? guard)
- [ ] :db effect handler queues ::bot-decide when bot should act after game-db change
- [ ] events/interceptors/sba.cljs deleted
- [ ] bot-action-interceptor and register! removed from bots/interceptor.cljs
- [ ] bot-trigger-events removed from bots/interceptor.cljs
- [ ] sba-interceptor register! removed from core.cljs
- [ ] bot-interceptor register! removed from core.cljs
- [ ] Inline SBA calls removed from engine/effects.cljs reduce-effects
- [ ] Inline SBA call removed from engine/mana_activation.cljs (if present)
- [ ] toggle-selection-impl dispatches ::confirm-selection instead of calling confirm-selection-impl
- [ ] Fling lethal damage triggers game-over (test)
- [ ] Lightning Bolt lethal damage triggers game-over (test)
- [ ] Mill to empty library triggers game-over on draw (existing tests pass)
- [ ] Bot still responds after player actions (existing bot tests pass)
- [ ] All tests passing (make validate)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO trigger event sets — the whole point is eliminating manual event whitelists
- ❌ NO inline SBA calls at individual mutation points — chokepoint handles everything
- ❌ NO observer pattern for game-state reactions — pipeline only
- ❌ NO synchronous confirm-selection-impl calls from toggle-selection-impl — must dispatch
- ❌ NO keeping dead interceptor code 'just in case' — delete it

## Approach
Override re-frame's :db effect handler (rf/reg-fx :db ...) to create a single structural chokepoint. When the new game-db differs from the old (identical? check), run check-and-execute-sbas and bot-should-act? checks. This replaces both the SBA interceptor and bot interceptor entirely.

Fix toggle-selection-impl's auto-confirm path to dispatch ::confirm-selection instead of calling confirm-selection-impl synchronously. This ensures all game-state mutations go through proper event dispatch and hit the :db effect handler.

Remove all inline SBA calls from reduce-effects and mana_activation — they're now redundant since the chokepoint handles everything.

Delete sba.cljs interceptor file, remove bot interceptor from bots/interceptor.cljs, update core.cljs init to register :db effect handler instead of interceptors.

## Architecture
- core.cljs — Register custom :db effect handler, remove interceptor register! calls
- events/interceptors/sba.cljs — DELETE
- bots/interceptor.cljs — Remove bot-action-interceptor, bot-trigger-events, register!. Keep ::bot-decide event handler and pure functions
- engine/effects.cljs — Remove SBA calls from reduce-effects (lines 207, 211)
- engine/mana_activation.cljs — Remove SBA call if present
- events/selection/core.cljs — toggle-selection-impl dispatches instead of inlining

## Design Rationale
### Problem
SBA and bot interceptors maintain manual trigger event sets that must agree with which events mutate game state. When a new event gains mutation capability (e.g., toggle-selection via auto-confirm), the set isn't updated and SBAs silently stop firing. This is an implicit protocol — the agreement between 'events that mutate' and 'events that trigger SBA' is maintained by convention, not structure.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- reduce-effects (engine/effects.cljs:207,211) already calls check-and-execute-sbas inline — partially implements pipeline approach
- SBA interceptor (events/interceptors/sba.cljs) maintains sba-trigger-events set with 11 event keywords
- Bot interceptor (bots/interceptor.cljs) maintains bot-trigger-events set with 10 event keywords
- toggle-selection-impl (selection/core.cljs:443-448) calls confirm-selection-impl synchronously on auto-confirm, bypassing event dispatch
- re-frame reg-fx :db is explicitly designed to be overridden (documented in re-frame source)
- The :db effect handler is the ONLY place where app-db atom is reset — true structural chokepoint

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. :db effect handler chokepoint ✓
**What it is:** Override re-frame's :db effect handler to run SBAs and bot checks whenever game-db changes. Single point, structurally guaranteed.

**Chosen because:** Impossible to bypass. No whitelist to maintain. Eliminates entire class of bugs.

#### 2. Inline SBA calls at each mutation point ❌
**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Same class of bug — miss a call site, miss an SBA. Just moves the manual list from interceptor to function calls.
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** re-frame stops supporting reg-fx :db override.

#### 3. Universal interceptor (no trigger set) ❌
**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Still observer pattern. Interceptor ordering is fragile and depends on registration order. Pipeline is simpler.
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** :db effect handler approach proves to have performance issues.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- :db effect handler with SBA + bot checks
- Delete SBA interceptor
- Delete bot interceptor (keep ::bot-decide handler)
- Remove inline SBA calls from reduce-effects
- Fix auto-confirm dispatch
- Update affected tests

**Out of scope:**
- History interceptor refactoring (different concern, works correctly)
- New SBA types (existing SBAs are correct, just the trigger mechanism changes)
- Bot decision logic changes (only the trigger mechanism changes)

### Open Questions
- Should the :db effect handler queue ::bot-decide via dispatch or dispatch-later? dispatch-later with 0ms delay might be needed to avoid re-entrant dispatch.
- Performance: check-and-execute-sbas runs Datascript queries. On every game-db change this should be fine but verify with profiling.

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Inline SBAs at mutation points vs chokepoint? | Chokepoint — inline still has manual list | :db effect handler is the mechanism |
| Bot interceptor: same epic or separate? | Same epic | Both interceptors deleted in one refactor |
| Auto-confirm async acceptable? | Yes | toggle-selection dispatches, tests use dispatch-sync |
| Bot check: :db handler or separate interceptor? | :db handler | Single chokepoint for both SBA and bot |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### reduce-effects already has inline SBAs
**Question:** Does reduce-effects already call SBAs?
**Finding:** Yes — lines 207 and 211 call check-and-execute-sbas after each effect and after all effects. This means the interceptor was already redundant for effect resolution. The inline calls will be removed since the chokepoint handles everything.

#### Auto-confirm hidden mutation path
**Question:** How does toggle-selection cause game-state mutation?
**Finding:** toggle-selection-impl (core.cljs:443-448) calls confirm-selection-impl directly when auto-confirm is true and select-count is 1. confirm-selection-impl can trigger continuations (like resolve-one-and-stop) that resolve spells and deal damage. This bypasses event dispatch entirely.

### Dead-End Paths
None.

### Open Concerns Raised
- Re-entrant dispatch: :db effect handler runs during do-fx :after phase. Dispatching ::bot-decide from there might cause re-entrant issues. Use dispatch (queued) not dispatch-sync. → Verify during implementation.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 1: Implement :exile-self effect type
design:
## Goal
Implement the :exile-self effect type that moves the resolving spell to the exile zone instead of the graveyard.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing effect patterns
Reference these files to understand the pattern:
- src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:106-108 - :add-mana handler (simplest example)
- src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:297-315 - :sacrifice handler (moves object to zone)
- src/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs:39-67 - move-to-zone function

**Handler signature:**
```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect-impl :exile-self
  [db player-id effect object-id]
  ;; object-id is the spell/permanent that is the SOURCE of this effect
  ;; For :exile-self, we move object-id to :exile zone
  ...)
```

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Create test file: src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs (or add to existing)

**Test scenarios:**
- test_exile_self_moves_spell_to_exile_zone: Create spell on stack, apply :exile-self effect, verify spell is in :exile zone (catches: effect doesn't move to correct zone)
- test_exile_self_with_nil_object_id_returns_db_unchanged: Pass nil object-id, verify db unchanged (catches: nil pointer exception)
- test_exile_self_with_nonexistent_object_returns_db_unchanged: Pass invalid object-id, verify db unchanged (catches: missing guard clause)

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - Add `(defmethod execute-effect-impl :exile-self ...)` handler
- [ ] Handler guards against nil object-id (return db unchanged)
- [ ] Handler guards against nonexistent object (return db unchanged)
- [ ] Handler calls `(zones/move-to-zone db object-id :exile)` for valid object
- [ ] src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs - Add 3 test cases listed above

## Success Criteria
- [ ] :exile-self effect moves spell to :exile zone (verified: query object zone after effect)
- [ ] Nil object-id returns db unchanged (no exception)
- [ ] Nonexistent object-id returns db unchanged (no exception)
- [ ] 3 unit tests pass: `make test`
- [ ] Existing tests still pass: `make test`
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing: `make validate`

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Nil object-id**
- object-id can be nil if effect is executed without a source object
- MUST guard: `(if-not object-id db ...)`
- See :grant-flashback handler at effects.cljs:411 for pattern

**Edge Case: Nonexistent object**
- Object may have been removed between effect queuing and execution
- MUST guard by checking object exists before moving
- See :sacrifice handler at effects.cljs:309-315 for pattern

**Reference Implementation**
Copy pattern from :sacrifice (effects.cljs:297-315):
```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect-impl :exile-self
  [db _player-id _effect object-id]
  (if-not object-id
    db  ; No source object - no-op
    (if (d/q '[:find ?e .
               :in $ ?oid
               :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]
             db object-id)
      (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :exile)
      db)))  ; Object doesn't exist - no-op
```

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO throwing exceptions on nil/invalid object-id (robustness: return db unchanged)
- ❌ NO skipping existence check (safety: object may not exist)
- ❌ NO hardcoding zone keyword (use :exile from effect if needed for future flexibility)
close_reason: Closed
