title: Lands integration + unit tests
description:
## Goal
Create src/test/fizzle/events/lands_test.cljs with integration (dispatch-sync) and unit tests for events/lands.cljs. This is the final task in epic fizzle-87pw.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours (smallest module: 117 LOC, 2 handlers, 3 pure fns)

## Implementation

### Setup (same pattern as phases_test.cljs / abilities_test.cljs)
File: src/test/fizzle/events/lands_test.cljs

Copy setup from phases_test.cljs:
- interceptor/register! at top level
- setup-app-db via h/create-game-scenario
- dispatch-event wrapper (reset!/dispatch-sync/deref)

Additional requires: fizzle.events.lands

### Context
lands.cljs has 2 handlers:
- ::play-land — calls play-land pure fn, creates history entry
- ::tap-permanent — calls tap-permanent pure fn

Pure fns: play-land (validates via rules/can-play-land?, moves to battlefield, registers triggers, fires ETB, dispatches land-entered), tap-permanent (sets tapped), untap-permanent (sets untapped)

### Integration Tests (dispatch-sync)
1. **play-land-dispatch-moves-to-battlefield**: Add island to hand, dispatch ::play-land. Assert island is on :battlefield, not :hand. Assert land-plays-left decremented. Bug: event handler wiring broken.

2. **play-land-creates-history-entry**: Dispatch ::play-land. Assert :history/main has entry with ::lands/play-land event-type. (Check :history/main not :history/pending-entry — interceptor consumes it.)

3. **tap-permanent-dispatch-taps-land**: Add land to battlefield, dispatch ::tap-permanent. Assert :object/tapped is true. Bug: tap handler wiring broken.

4. **play-land-fires-etb-effects**: Add City of Brass to hand. Dispatch ::play-land. Verify ETB trigger registered (City of Brass has card triggers for pain-on-tap). Bug: ETB effect wiring at lands.cljs:53-58 broken.

5. **play-land-with-triggers-registers-them**: Add a land with :card/triggers to hand. Dispatch ::play-land. Verify trigger entities exist in db after land enters battlefield. Bug: trigger registration at lands.cljs:53-58 broken.

### Unit Tests (direct function calls)
6. **play-land-not-a-land**: Call play-land with a non-land card. Assert db unchanged. Bug: can-play-land? validation broken.

7. **play-land-no-plays-left**: Set land-plays-left to 0. Call play-land with a land. Assert db unchanged. Bug: land plays counter check broken.

8. **play-land-not-in-hand**: Call play-land with land on :battlefield (not :hand). Assert db unchanged. Bug: zone check in can-play-land? broken.

9. **tap-permanent-nonexistent-object**: Call tap-permanent with a fake object-id. Assert db unchanged. Bug: nil-safety check at lands.cljs:91 broken.

10. **untap-permanent-sets-tapped-false**: Tap a permanent, then call untap-permanent. Assert :object/tapped is false. Bug: untap logic broken.

11. **untap-permanent-nonexistent-object**: Call untap-permanent with fake id. Assert db unchanged. Bug: nil-safety at lands.cljs:111 broken.

### SBA-Adjacent Verification
12. **play-land-decrements-land-plays**: Call play-land, verify land-plays-left went from 1 to 0. Bug: land-plays-left decrement at lands.cljs:49 broken.

### Key Learnings from prior tasks (APPLY)
- Check :history/main not :history/pending-entry (interceptor consumes it)
- Use (merge (setup-app-db) {:game/db modified-db}) to inject test state
- Run make fmt before make validate (Datalog query indentation)
- Destructure as [db _] when obj-id unused
- activate-ability returns {:db db :pending-selection nil} map; play-land returns db directly
- MUST commit with git before sending completion report — include actual commit hash
- Run bd close fizzle-bp8n and bd sync

## Success Criteria
- [ ] File exists at src/test/fizzle/events/lands_test.cljs
- [ ] 12 tests specified above all pass
- [ ] Integration tests (1-5) use dispatch-sync pattern
- [ ] Unit tests (6-11) call pure functions directly
- [ ] Guard conditions: not-a-land, no-plays-left, not-in-hand, nonexistent-object
- [ ] History entry tested with exact event-type assertion
- [ ] ETB + trigger registration verified via dispatch
- [ ] make validate passes (all existing + new tests green, lint clean, fmt clean)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO mocking re-frame dispatch or interceptors
- ❌ NO hand-building app-db from scratch — use h/create-game-scenario
- ❌ NO some?/string? assertions — assert exact values
- ❌ NO duplicating card test happy paths (tap_land_test/play_land_test exist — focus on dispatch-sync gap)
- ❌ NO testing pure functions via dispatch-sync or vice versa
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: Unify selection system + auto-confirm single-select
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Unify all selection state to use `:selection/selected` (set) — remove `:selection/selected-target` field entirely
- Targeting selections (player, cast-time, ability) become set-based with select-count=1 and `:selection/valid-targets`
- Replace 6 separate toggle/select handlers with one unified `::toggle-selection` handler
- Replace per-type confirm event handlers with a `confirm-selection` multimethod dispatching on `:selection/type`
- Single-select selections auto-confirm when user clicks a card/target (no separate Confirm click)
- Auto-confirm applies to: tutor (select-count=1), cast-time-targeting, player-target, ability-targeting
- Multi-select selections keep their Confirm button unchanged
- Find Nothing button preserved for single-select tutors
- Deselecting a card (clicking already-selected) does NOT trigger auto-confirm
- All existing behavior preserved — this is a refactoring, not a behavior change (except auto-confirm addition)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] No `:selection/selected-target` field anywhere in codebase
- [ ] All selection types use `:selection/selected` (set) + `:selection/select-count`
- [ ] Targeting types use `:selection/valid-targets` for validation
- [ ] One `::toggle-selection` handler replaces all 6 toggle/select handlers
- [ ] One `confirm-selection` multimethod replaces per-type confirm event handlers
- [ ] Single-select tutor auto-confirms on card click
- [ ] All targeting types (cast-time object/player, ability object/player, player-target) auto-confirm
- [ ] Multi-select types (Intuition, discard, scry, pile-choice, graveyard-return, peek-and-select, exile-cards, x-mana) unchanged
- [ ] Deselecting does not auto-confirm
- [ ] Find Nothing still works
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] No increase in total LOC in selection.cljs (should decrease)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO `:selection/selected-target` field (must be fully removed, not coexisting)
- ❌ NO per-type toggle handlers (must be one unified handler)
- ❌ NO removing Find Nothing button (fail-to-find is MTG rules-correct)
- ❌ NO auto-confirm on multi-select types
- ❌ NO auto-confirm when deselecting
- ❌ NO auto-confirm on pile-choice even when hand-count=1
- ❌ NO auto-confirm on graveyard-return or peek-and-select even with max=1 (user may want 0)
- ❌ NO circular namespace dependencies
- ❌ NO changing behavior of any existing game mechanic (refactoring only + auto-confirm addition)
- ❌ NO big-bang refactoring (each phase must leave tests green)

## Approach
Phase the refactoring so tests stay green throughout:

**Phase 1: Unify data model** — Update all builders to use `:selection/selected` (set) + `:selection/select-count`. Update confirm handlers to read from `:selection/selected`. Remove `:selection/selected-target`. Update modals and subs.

**Phase 2: Unify toggle handler** — Merge 6 toggle/select handlers into one `::toggle-selection`. It handles both set-based and targeting selections (both are now set-based). Update modals to dispatch unified event.

**Phase 3: Multimethod confirm** — Extract confirm logic from per-type reg-event-db handlers into a multimethod `execute-confirmed-selection` dispatching on `:selection/type`. Shared wrapper handles: validation, call multimethod, remaining-effects, cleanup, clear/chain. One `::confirm-selection` event handler.

**Phase 4: Auto-confirm** — In unified toggle handler, after updating selection, check if single-select is complete and type supports auto-confirm. Call confirm-selection-impl directly (synchronous, no flicker).

## Architecture

### Unified Data Model
All selections use:
- `:selection/type` — discriminator (:tutor, :discard, :player-target, :cast-time-targeting, :ability-targeting, etc.)
- `:selection/selected` — set of selected IDs (object-ids, player keywords)
- `:selection/select-count` — max items to select (1 for targeting)
- `:selection/valid-targets` — (targeting only) set of valid IDs to validate against
- `:selection/player-id`, `:selection/spell-id`, `:selection/remaining-effects`, etc. — unchanged

### Unified Toggle Handler
`::toggle-selection [_ id]`
1. If `:selection/valid-targets` exists, validate id is in set — reject invalid
2. If already selected, deselect (remove from set)
3. If select-count=1, replace selection (single-select behavior)
4. If under limit, add to selection
5. If at limit, ignore
6. Auto-confirm check: if count matches select-count AND not deselecting AND auto-confirmable type → call confirm

### Multimethod Confirm
`(defmulti execute-confirmed-selection (fn [game-db selection] (:selection/type selection)))`

Each method returns one of:
- `{:db game-db}` — standard, wrapper handles remaining-effects + cleanup
- `{:db game-db :pending-selection next-sel}` — chain to next selection
- `{:db game-db :finalized? true}` — fully handled (pre-cast, ability types)

Shared wrapper (`confirm-selection-impl`):
1. Validate selection count/candidates
2. Call multimethod
3. If `:pending-selection` → set as new pending-selection
4. If `:finalized?` → just clear pending-selection
5. Else → execute remaining-effects → cleanup-selection-source → clear pending-selection

### Files Changed
- `selection.cljs` — Major: builders, toggle, confirm, cleanup (net LOC decrease)
- `abilities.cljs` — Remove `::select-ability-object-target`, update `build-ability-target-selection`, move/keep `confirm-ability-target` as multimethod impl
- `modals.cljs` — Read `:selection/selected` instead of `:selection/selected-target`, dispatch unified toggle/confirm events
- `subs/game.cljs` — Update subscriptions exposing selection state
- Test files — Update to use unified event names and data model

## Parallelism Map

### Independent Work Streams
1. **Phase 1: Data model unification** (selection.cljs builders + confirm reads, abilities.cljs builders, modals.cljs reads, subs)
   - Estimated complexity: high
2. **Phase 2: Unified toggle** (selection.cljs, modals.cljs dispatches)
   - Estimated complexity: medium
3. **Phase 3: Multimethod confirm** (selection.cljs, abilities.cljs)
   - Estimated complexity: high
4. **Phase 4: Auto-confirm** (selection.cljs toggle handler)
   - Estimated complexity: low

### Stream Dependencies
- Phase 2 depends on Phase 1 (needs unified data model)
- Phase 3 depends on Phase 1 (confirm handlers read unified fields)
- Phase 4 depends on Phases 2 and 3 (needs unified toggle + multimethod confirm)

### Suggested Waves
- Wave 1: Phase 1 (data model — must come first)
- Wave 2: Phases 2 + 3 could parallelize (different handlers in same file)
- Wave 3: Phase 4 (auto-confirm)

### File Ownership Boundaries
| Stream | Owns (exclusive) | Shared (needs coordination) |
|--------|-------------------|-----------------------------|
| Phase 1 | builder functions, subs | selection.cljs, modals.cljs (with Phase 2) |
| Phase 2 | toggle handlers section | selection.cljs, modals.cljs dispatch (with Phase 1) |
| Phase 3 | confirm handlers section | selection.cljs (with Phase 2) |
| Phase 4 | auto-confirm logic | toggle handler (with Phase 2) |

### Parallelism Assessment
- Independent streams: 0-1 (Phases 2+3 may parallelize but touch same file)
- Recommendation: solo execution
- Rationale: Sequential phases in same file, TDD requires tests green between each phase. Safe refactoring demands incremental changes.

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The selection system has 6 toggle handlers doing variations of the same thing, 11 confirm handlers with duplicated patterns, and two incompatible field conventions (`:selection/selected` vs `:selection/selected-target`). Adding auto-confirm on top of this requires patching 4 handlers with similar logic. Refactoring first makes auto-confirm trivial and reduces ongoing maintenance burden.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- 6 toggle/select handlers: `::toggle-selection` (L1017), `::toggle-peek-card-selection` (L1109), `::toggle-exile-card-selection` (L1407), `::select-player-target` (L1341), `::select-cast-time-object-target` (L1528), `::select-ability-object-target` (abilities.cljs:281)
- `::select-cast-time-object-target` and `::select-ability-object-target` are 100% identical code
- `::select-player-target` does no validation while object selectors do
- All confirm handlers follow: validate → execute → remaining-effects → cleanup → clear
- `cleanup-selection-source` already extracted as shared helper (L709)
- `confirm-ability-target` already extracted as standalone function (abilities.cljs:150)
- Three confirm handler families: resolution-phase, pre-cast, ability

**Duplication metrics:**
- ~73 lines duplicated across confirm handlers
- Toggle handlers: 3 set-based variants, 3 scalar variants doing the same thing

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Unified data model + toggle + multimethod confirm ✓
**What it is:** Normalize all selections to set-based, one toggle, multimethod confirm, auto-confirm in toggle.
**Pros:** Maximum simplification, auto-confirm trivial, single source of truth
**Cons:** Largest refactoring surface, touches many files
**Chosen because:** User wants to do it right. The duplication is maintenance burden.

#### 2. Just unify toggles + auto-confirm ❌
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Leaves confirm handler duplication untouched, doesn't fix data model inconsistency

#### 3. Just add auto-confirm on current code ❌
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Patches 4 handlers with similar logic, adds complexity to already complex code

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** data model unification, toggle unification, confirm multimethod, auto-confirm, test updates
**Out of scope:** scry modal UX changes (two-pile drag is structurally different, keep as-is), x-mana UI (increment/decrement is different from toggle), pile-choice auto-confirm (consequential, defer)

### Open Questions
- Should Phases 2+3 be combined into one task or kept separate? (decide during execution)
- `::select-player-target` is dispatched directly from modals.cljs:79 — unified toggle needs to handle player keywords alongside object-ids in the same set

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| How should fail-to-find work? | Keep Find Nothing button | Modal still renders, button preserved |
| Auto-confirm player targets? | Yes | All targeting types auto-confirm |
| Include ability targeting? | Yes, absolutely | Ability targeting in scope |
| How much refactoring? | Major refactor first | Full unification before auto-confirm |
| Unify data model too? | Yes, go further | `:selection/selected-target` removed entirely |
| Full scope comfortable? | Yes, do it right | Touch everything: selection.cljs, abilities.cljs, modals.cljs, subs, tests |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Toggle Handler Duplication
**Question explored:** How similar are the 6 toggle handlers?
**Findings:** Set-based handlers all do toggle-in-set with max-count check. Scalar handlers all do toggle-single-value. cast-time-object and ability-object are 100% identical. player-target lacks validation that object handlers have.
**Conclusion:** All 6 can be one handler with set-based data model

#### Confirm Handler Families
**Question explored:** Can confirm handlers be unified?
**Findings:** Three families (resolution-phase, pre-cast, ability). Shared pattern: validate → execute → remaining-effects → cleanup → clear. Differences: chaining (tutor→pile, player→discard), pre-cast skips remaining-effects, ability has own flow.
**Conclusion:** Multimethod for type-specific execution, shared wrapper for common pattern. Return-value protocol handles variations.

#### Cross-Namespace Dependencies
**Question explored:** Can ability targeting move into selection.cljs?
**Findings:** `confirm-ability-target` (abilities.cljs:150) already standalone function. Selection.cljs doesn't currently require abilities. game.cljs requires both.
**Conclusion:** Ability confirm can stay in abilities.cljs, registered as multimethod implementation. Or move to selection.cljs since it's selection logic.

### Dead-End Paths
- Considered per-handler auto-confirm patching → Abandoned due to 4-way duplication
- Considered interceptor approach → Over-engineered

### Open Concerns Raised
- Pile-choice with hand-count=1 could auto-confirm → Deferred (consequential)
- Graveyard-return with max=1 could auto-confirm → Deferred (allows 0)
close_reason: All 4 phases complete: unified data model, unified toggle handler, confirm-selection multimethod, auto-confirm. All 13 success criteria verified. 1542→1454 LOC (-88). All tests pass.

title: Task 1: Implement X cost system in mana.cljs
design:
## Goal
Implement generic X cost support in the mana system so cards with X in their mana cost can be cast.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- src/main/fizzle/engine/mana.cljs:25-42 - can-pay? currently handles :colorless as generic
- src/main/fizzle/engine/mana.cljs:55-88 - pay-mana with generic cost handling
- src/test/fizzle/engine/mana_test.cljs - 19 existing tests to not regress

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

**Test file:** src/test/fizzle/engine/mana_test.cljs

Add these specific test functions:

```clojure
;; === X cost tests ===

(deftest can-pay-x-cost-always-true-test
  ;; X can always be 0, so any X cost is payable
  (testing "can-pay? returns true for X cost even with empty pool"
    (let [db (init-game-state)]
      (is (true? (mana/can-pay? db :player-1 {:x 1 :blue 1}))))))

(deftest pay-mana-x-value-zero-test
  ;; When X=0, only pay the non-X portion
  (testing "pay-mana with x-value=0 pays only fixed costs"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:blue 2}))
          db' (mana/pay-mana db :player-1 {:x 1 :colorless 1 :blue 1} 0)]
      (is (= 0 (:blue (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest pay-mana-x-value-converts-to-colorless-test
  ;; {:x 1 :colorless 1 :blue 1} with x-value=3 -> {:colorless 4 :blue 1}
  (testing "pay-mana resolves X to colorless mana"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 5 :blue 1}))
          db' (mana/pay-mana db :player-1 {:x 1 :colorless 1 :blue 1} 3)]
      ;; Total: 4 colorless (3 from X + 1 fixed) + 1 blue
      ;; Should pay 4 from black (generic), 1 from blue
      (is (= 1 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1))))
      (is (= 0 (:blue (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest pay-mana-x-value-nil-defaults-zero-test
  ;; Backwards compatibility: omitting x-value treats X as 0
  (testing "pay-mana without x-value treats X as 0"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:blue 2}))
          db' (mana/pay-mana db :player-1 {:x 1 :blue 1})]  ; No x-value arg
      ;; X=0, so just pay 1 blue
      (is (= 1 (:blue (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest pay-mana-no-x-key-unchanged-test
  ;; Backwards compatibility: costs without :x work exactly as before
  (testing "pay-mana without :x key works unchanged (backwards compat)"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 3 :blue 1}))
          db' (mana/pay-mana db :player-1 {:colorless 1 :black 1})]
      (is (= 1 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1))))
      (is (= 1 (:blue (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest pay-mana-multiple-x-multiplies-test
  ;; Future-proofing: {:x 2} means "2X" (two X variables)
  (testing "pay-mana with {:x 2} multiplies x-value by 2"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 6}))
          db' (mana/pay-mana db :player-1 {:x 2} 3)]  ; 2X where X=3 -> 6
      (is (= 0 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))
```

### 3. Implementation checklist

- [ ] mana.cljs - Add resolve-x-cost helper function:
  ```clojure
  (defn resolve-x-cost
    "Convert X in mana cost to colorless. x-value defaults to 0 if nil.
     {:x 1 :colorless 1 :blue 1} with x-value=3 -> {:colorless 4 :blue 1}"
    [mana-cost x-value]
    (let [x-count (get mana-cost :x 0)
          x-val (or x-value 0)
          x-as-colorless (* x-count x-val)
          existing-colorless (get mana-cost :colorless 0)]
      (-> mana-cost
          (dissoc :x)
          (assoc :colorless (+ existing-colorless x-as-colorless)))))
  ```

- [ ] mana.cljs - Update can-pay? to ignore :x key (X is always payable as X can be 0):
  ```clojure
  ;; In can-pay?, before processing:
  (let [cost-without-x (dissoc cost :x)
        ...]
  ```

- [ ] mana.cljs - Update pay-mana signature to accept optional x-value:
  ```clojure
  (defn pay-mana
    ([db player-id cost] (pay-mana db player-id cost nil))
    ([db player-id cost x-value]
     ;; Call resolve-x-cost before existing logic
     (let [resolved-cost (resolve-x-cost cost x-value)]
       ...existing logic with resolved-cost...)))
  ```

- [ ] Verify all 19 existing mana tests still pass (regression check)
- [ ] Verify all 6 new X cost tests pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] 6 new X cost tests pass (specific tests listed above)
- [ ] 19 existing mana tests pass (no regression)
- [ ] can-pay? handles costs with :x key (ignores X, always true for X portion)
- [ ] pay-mana accepts optional x-value parameter
- [ ] pay-mana with x-value resolves {:x N} to colorless correctly
- [ ] pay-mana without x-value defaults X to 0 (backwards compat)
- [ ] make validate passing

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Nil x-value**
- When x-value is nil or omitted, treat as 0
- This ensures backwards compatibility with existing callers
- Implementation: `(or x-value 0)` in resolve-x-cost

**Edge Case: Cost without :x key**
- Existing cards don't have :x in mana-cost
- MUST work exactly as before (resolve-x-cost is no-op when :x absent)
- Test: pay-mana-no-x-key-unchanged-test

**Edge Case: :x 0 in cost**
- Technically valid but weird (0X = always 0)
- resolve-x-cost handles correctly: (* 0 x-value) = 0
- No special handling needed

**Design Decision: :x multiplier**
- :x 1 means "1X" (standard)
- :x 2 means "2X" (e.g., a card costing XX)
- Formula: x-as-colorless = x-count * x-value
- Future-proofs for Fireball (XR) vs Blaze (XX)

**Not in scope for this task:**
- UI for choosing X value (handled at casting layer)
- Storing x-value on stack object (handled by rules.cljs casting flow)
- Minimum X validation (card-specific, handled by card definition)

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN for this task)
- ❌ NO mutating x-value parameter (pure functions only)
- ❌ NO validation of x-value >= 0 in mana.cljs (caller responsibility, like can-pay?)
- ❌ NO special-casing Flash of Insight (generic X support only)
- ❌ NO changing pay-mana's existing behavior when x-value is nil/omitted
close_reason: X cost system implemented: resolve-x-cost helper, can-pay? ignores :x, pay-mana accepts optional x-value. 6 new tests pass, 19 existing tests pass, make validate passes.

title: Implement storm trigger resolution and cast-spell integration
design:
## Goal

Implement the storm keyword behavior: when a spell with :storm is cast, create a storm trigger on the stack. When that trigger resolves, create N copies of the spell (where N = spells cast before this turn).

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context

Completed fizzle-21w: Trigger infrastructure in place (create-trigger, add-trigger-to-stack, resolve-trigger multimethod, remove-trigger, get-stack-items).

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code

Reference implementations:
- src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs - resolve-trigger multimethod pattern
- src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:38-53 - cast-spell flow
- src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:56-69 - resolve-spell flow
- src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs:31-39 - get-storm-count

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Test file: src/test/fizzle/engine/storm_test.cljs

Test cases with bug-catching rationale:
- [ ] test-cast-storm-spell-creates-trigger: Cast spell with :storm keyword creates trigger on stack (catches: missing trigger creation)
- [ ] test-storm-trigger-on-top-of-spell: Storm trigger has higher stack-order than spell (catches: wrong resolution order)
- [ ] test-resolve-storm-creates-copies: Storm trigger resolution creates N copies where N = storm count BEFORE cast (catches: off-by-one error)
- [ ] test-storm-copies-have-is-copy-flag: Copies have :object/is-copy true (catches: missing copy flag)
- [ ] test-storm-copies-do-not-increment-storm: Casting 1 spell, then storm with 1 copy, storm count still 2 (catches: copies incrementing count)
- [ ] test-storm-count-zero-creates-no-copies: Storm as first spell creates 0 copies (catches: off-by-one at boundary)
- [ ] test-storm-count-one-creates-one-copy: Storm after 1 spell creates 1 copy (catches: calculation error)
- [ ] test-storm-count-two-creates-two-copies: Storm after 2 spells creates 2 copies (catches: calculation error)
- [ ] test-storm-source-missing-returns-unchanged-db: If source object gone, resolve-trigger returns db unchanged (catches: nil reference crash)

### 3. Implementation checklist

Schema addition (src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs):
- [ ] Add :object/is-copy {} - Boolean, marks spell copies (default: false/nil)

Add to src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs:
- [ ] defmethod resolve-trigger :storm [db trigger]
  - Get source-id from (:trigger/source trigger)
  - Get copy-count from (:count (:trigger/data trigger))
  - If source object not found, return db unchanged (defensive)
  - Loop copy-count times, calling create-spell-copy for each
  - Return final db with all copies on stack
- [ ] create-spell-copy [db source-object-id controller-id] -> db with copy on stack
  - Query source object to get :object/card ref
  - Create new object with :object/id (random-uuid), :object/card same-ref, :object/zone :stack, :object/owner/controller from controller-id, :object/is-copy true
  - Transact and return db
  - Note: Copy inherits card but :object/is-copy true prevents storm cascade

Modify src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:
- [ ] Add require for fizzle.engine.triggers
- [ ] cast-spell: After increment-storm, check for :storm keyword in card
  - (when (contains? (:card/keywords card) :storm) ...)
  - Get current storm count (after increment)
  - Create trigger with {:count (dec storm-count)} - copies = spells BEFORE this one
  - Call (triggers/add-trigger-to-stack db (triggers/create-trigger :storm object-id player-id {:count (dec storm-count)}))
  - Note: Storm count is AFTER increment, so dec gives spells cast before

Modify src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs (if needed):
- [ ] Update get-stack-items to include both triggers AND spell objects, ordered by position
  - Current: Only returns triggers
  - Needed: Return [{:type :trigger :item trigger} {:type :spell :item object}] for full stack view

### 4. Key invariants

- Storm count for copies = current storm count - 1 (not including the storm spell itself)
- Copies are created by trigger resolution, NOT by cast-spell
- Copies do not trigger storm (checked via :object/is-copy flag, NOT by removing keyword)
- Copies have :object/is-copy true
- If source object missing at resolution time, return db unchanged (no crash)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Casting spell with :storm creates storm trigger on top of spell
- [ ] Storm trigger resolves to create N copies (N = spells cast BEFORE storm spell)
- [ ] Copies have :object/is-copy true
- [ ] Copies do not increment storm count (verify: cast 1 spell, cast storm, count = 2 not 3)
- [ ] Copies do not trigger storm (verify: :object/is-copy prevents cascade check)
- [ ] Edge case: Storm as first spell (count 0) creates 0 copies
- [ ] Edge case: Source object missing at resolution returns db unchanged
- [ ] 9 unit tests pass (8 functional + 1 edge case)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass: make test
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass: make validate

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Storm Count Zero**
- Storm as first spell has storm count 1 after increment
- Copies = 1 - 1 = 0
- MUST handle this gracefully (no copies created, no error)
- Test: test-storm-count-zero-creates-no-copies

**Edge Case: Source Object Missing**
- Source spell might be exiled or otherwise removed before trigger resolves
- resolve-trigger :storm MUST check if source exists
- If nil, return db unchanged (defensive, no crash)
- Test: test-storm-source-missing-returns-unchanged-db

**Preventing Storm Cascade**
- Copies must NOT trigger storm themselves
- Two options: (a) Remove :storm keyword from copy, (b) Check :object/is-copy in cast-spell
- CHOSEN: Option (b) - Don't modify card keywords, check flag instead
- Rationale: Card keywords are immutable; flag is cleaner
- Implementation: In cast-spell, only create storm trigger if NOT :object/is-copy

**Off-by-One Risk**
- Storm count is incremented BEFORE trigger creation
- So count after cast = total spells including this one
- Copies needed = count - 1 = spells BEFORE this one
- CRITICAL: Use (dec storm-count) not storm-count
- Test: test-storm-count-one-creates-one-copy verifies this

**Stack Ordering**
- Trigger must resolve BEFORE spell (copies resolve first)
- Trigger has higher stack-order than spell
- LIFO: Higher stack-order = resolves first
- Verified by test-storm-trigger-on-top-of-spell

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO incrementing storm count for copies (copies are not cast)
- ❌ NO storm cascade (copies must not trigger storm)
- ❌ NO creating copies during cast-spell (copies created by trigger resolution)
- ❌ NO removing :storm keyword from copies (check :object/is-copy flag instead)
- ❌ NO crashing on nil source (defensive check, return unchanged db)
- ❌ NO off-by-one in copy count (use dec of post-increment storm count)
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 1: Add :scry effect type to engine
design:
## Goal
Add the :scry effect type to effects.cljs that triggers player selection, following the existing selection effect pattern (like :discard and :tutor).

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
Reference implementations:
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:306-331 - Discard effect with selection pattern
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:334-349 - Tutor effect (also returns db unchanged)
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs:750-770 - Selection detection functions

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Create src/test/fizzle/engine/scry_effect_test.cljs:

```clojure
;; Test: execute-effect-impl :scry returns db unchanged (triggers selection flow)
;; Bug caught: If scry modifies db directly, selection flow breaks
(deftest scry-effect-returns-db-unchanged
  (let [db (create-test-db-with-library 5)
        effect {:effect/type :scry :effect/amount 2}
        result (effects/execute-effect db :player-1 effect)]
    (is (= db result) "Scry must return db unchanged for selection flow")))

;; Test: scry effect with amount 1 is valid
;; Bug caught: Hardcoded scry 2+ assumption
(deftest scry-effect-amount-1-valid
  (let [effect {:effect/type :scry :effect/amount 1}]
    (is (= 1 (:effect/amount effect)))))

;; Test: scry effect with amount 0 treated as no-op
;; Bug caught: Division by zero or empty array issues
(deftest scry-effect-amount-0-no-op
  (let [db (create-test-db-with-library 5)
        effect {:effect/type :scry :effect/amount 0}
        result (effects/execute-effect db :player-1 effect)]
    (is (= db result) "Scry 0 should be no-op")))

;; Test: scry effect missing amount defaults safely
;; Bug caught: NPE on nil amount
(deftest scry-effect-missing-amount-defaults-to-0
  (let [db (create-test-db-with-library 5)
        effect {:effect/type :scry} ; no :effect/amount
        result (effects/execute-effect db :player-1 effect)]
    (is (= db result) "Missing amount should be safe")))
```

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] effects.cljs - Add `(defmethod execute-effect-impl :scry [db _player-id effect] ...)`
- [ ] effects.cljs - Scry returns db unchanged (like discard :player selection)
- [ ] effects.cljs - Add docstring documenting :effect/amount key
- [ ] effects.cljs - Handle edge case: amount <= 0 returns db unchanged (no-op)
- [ ] effects.cljs - Handle edge case: missing amount defaults to 0 (no-op)
- [ ] game.cljs:753-754 - Add `(= :scry (:effect/type %))` to has-selection-effect? check
- [ ] game.cljs:767-769 - Add `(= :scry (:effect/type %))` to get-first-selection-index check

### 4. Implementation pattern to follow
```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect-impl :scry
  ;; Look at top N cards of library and rearrange them.
  ;;
  ;; Effect keys:
  ;;   :effect/amount - Number of cards to scry (default 0)
  ;;
  ;; This effect returns db UNCHANGED. The actual scry happens at the
  ;; app-db level via re-frame events when the player confirms their selection.
  ;; The calling code checks for :scry effect type and sets up pending selection
  ;; with the top N cards revealed.
  ;;
  ;; Edge cases:
  ;;   - amount <= 0: No-op (returns db unchanged, no selection needed)
  ;;   - amount > library size: Scry available cards only (handled in selection setup)
  [db _player-id effect]
  (let [amount (or (:effect/amount effect) 0)]
    (if (<= amount 0)
      db  ; No-op for scry 0 or negative
      db))) ; Return unchanged - selection handled at app-db level
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `(defmethod execute-effect-impl :scry ...)` exists in effects.cljs
- [ ] Scry effect returns db unchanged for amount > 0 (verified by test)
- [ ] Scry effect returns db unchanged for amount <= 0 (no-op, verified by test)
- [ ] Scry effect handles missing :effect/amount safely (verified by test)
- [ ] `has-selection-effect?` returns true for scry effects with amount > 0
- [ ] `get-first-selection-index` finds scry effects
- [ ] 4+ unit tests pass in scry_effect_test.cljs
- [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + fmt + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: amount <= 0**
- Scry 0 is legal in MTG (does nothing)
- Must not trigger selection UI for scry 0
- Return db unchanged without setting up selection

**Edge Case: missing :effect/amount**
- Malformed effect data should not crash
- Default to 0 (no-op) if missing
- Use `(or (:effect/amount effect) 0)`

**Edge Case: negative amount**
- Should never happen but be defensive
- Treat same as 0 (no-op)

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow exact pattern of :discard at effects.cljs:306-331
- Returns db unchanged, selection handled at app-db level
- Docstring documents all effect keys and edge cases

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN for this task)
- ❌ NO modifying db in execute-effect-impl (must return unchanged for selection)
- ❌ NO assuming amount is always present (must handle nil)
- ❌ NO assuming amount is always positive (must handle 0 and negative)
- ❌ NO hardcoding scry 1 behavior (must work for any N)
close_reason: Closed

title: Phases integration + unit tests
description:
## Goal
Create events/phases_test.cljs with both integration (dispatch-sync) and unit tests.

## Implementation

### Setup (match priority_test.cljs pattern)
- require interceptor, rf, rf-db, test-helpers, phases, queries
- Call (interceptor/register!) at top level
- Create setup-app-db using h/create-game-scenario with {:bot-archetype :goldfish}
- Create dispatch-event helper: reset! rf-db/app-db, rf/dispatch-sync, deref result

### Integration Tests (dispatch-sync)
1. **Full turn cycle E2E**: advance-phase repeatedly from :main1 through all phases to cleanup, then start-turn. Verify phase sequence, active player switch, turn counter increment.
2. **advance-phase happy path**: dispatch ::advance-phase from main1, verify phase advances and mana pool empties
3. **advance-phase skips combat**: setup with no creatures on battlefield, verify combat phase is skipped
4. **advance-phase to cleanup triggers discard**: setup with 8+ cards in hand, advance to cleanup, verify pending-selection appears for discard
5. **start-turn resets state**: dispatch ::start-turn, verify: active player switches, turn increments, storm-count=0, land-plays-left=1, mana pool empty
6. **advance-phase blocked by pending selection**: set :game/pending-selection on app-db, dispatch ::advance-phase, verify db unchanged
7. **advance-phase blocked by non-empty stack**: put a spell on stack, dispatch ::advance-phase, verify db unchanged
8. **start-turn creates history entry**: dispatch ::start-turn, verify :history/pending-entry exists with correct event type

### Unit Tests (direct function calls)
9. **next-phase boundary**: test each phase → next phase mapping, including :cleanup → :cleanup (stays)
10. **untap-all-permanents**: setup 3 tapped permanents, call untap-all-permanents, verify all untapped
11. **untap-all-permanents no tapped**: call with no tapped permanents, verify db unchanged
12. **advance-phase fires delayed effects in upkeep**: verify turn-based/fire-delayed-effects called when entering :upkeep
13. **advance-phase begins combat**: verify combat/begin-combat called when entering :combat

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All integration tests dispatch through re-frame (dispatch-sync), not direct function calls
- [ ] Full turn cycle E2E test proves complete cycle works
- [ ] SBA-adjacent checks: cleanup discard triggers when hand > 7
- [ ] Guard conditions: pending-selection blocks, non-empty stack blocks
- [ ] All tests passing (make validate)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing
design:
## Goal
Create src/test/fizzle/events/phases_test.cljs with both integration (dispatch-sync) and unit tests for events/phases.cljs.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### Setup (match priority_test.cljs pattern exactly)
File: src/test/fizzle/events/phases_test.cljs

```clojure
(ns fizzle.events.phases-test
  (:require
    [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest is testing]]
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
    [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]
    [fizzle.events.cleanup :as cleanup]
    [fizzle.events.phases :as phases]
    [fizzle.history.interceptor :as interceptor]
    [fizzle.test-helpers :as h]
    [re-frame.core :as rf]
    [re-frame.db :as rf-db]))

(interceptor/register!)

(defn- setup-app-db
  ([](setup-app-db {}))
  ([opts]
   (h/create-game-scenario (merge {:bot-archetype :goldfish} opts))))

(defn- dispatch-event [app-db event]
  (reset! rf-db/app-db app-db)
  (rf/dispatch-sync event)
  @rf-db/app-db)
```

**IMPORTANT**: create-game-scenario starts at :main1 phase. Verify this assumption in test #1 before asserting phase sequence. The phase order is: [:untap :upkeep :draw :main1 :combat :main2 :end :cleanup] (from rules.cljs:519).

### Integration Tests (dispatch-sync)

1. **full-turn-cycle-e2e**: Dispatch ::advance-phase repeatedly starting from :main1. After each dispatch, assert the phase advanced correctly. Expected sequence from :main1 (no creatures): main1 → main2 (combat skipped) → end → cleanup. Then dispatch ::start-turn. Assert:
   - Phase is :untap (new turn starts at untap)
   - Turn counter incremented (was 1, now 2)
   - Active player switched (was :player-1, now :player-2 — the bot)
   - Storm count is 0
   - Land plays left is 1
   - Mana pool is empty for new active player

2. **advance-phase-empties-mana-pool**: Setup with {:mana {:black 3}}. Dispatch ::advance-phase. Assert mana pool for active player has 0 black mana after advance.

3. **advance-phase-skips-combat-no-creatures**: Default scenario (no creatures). Dispatch ::advance-phase from :main1. Assert phase is :main2 (skipped :combat). Bug this catches: combat/has-creatures-on-battlefield? check at phases.cljs:50-53 might be broken.

4. **advance-phase-enters-combat-with-creatures**: Setup scenario, add a creature to battlefield via h/add-test-creature. Dispatch ::advance-phase from :main1. Assert phase is :combat (NOT skipped). Bug this catches: combat skip might be too aggressive (always skipping).

5. **advance-phase-cleanup-triggers-discard**: Setup scenario. Add 8 cards to hand via (h/add-card-to-zone db :island :hand :player-1) x8 (total hand > 7). Advance phases until cleanup (dispatch ::advance-phase 4 times from :main1: → main2 → end → cleanup). Assert :game/pending-selection exists with :selection/type = :discard and :selection/select-count = (hand-count - 7). Bug this catches: cleanup discard not triggering at phase boundary.

6. **start-turn-resets-game-state**: Dispatch ::start-turn from any phase. Assert all 6 conditions:
   - Active player switched (opponent is now active)
   - Turn counter incremented by 1
   - Storm count is 0 for new active player
   - Land plays left is 1 for new active player
   - Mana pool empty for new active player
   - Phase is :untap

7. **advance-phase-blocked-by-pending-selection**: Create app-db, manually assoc :game/pending-selection with a dummy selection map. Dispatch ::advance-phase. Assert phase unchanged (still :main1). Bug this catches: advance-phase ignoring in-progress selections (phases.cljs:129).

8. **advance-phase-blocked-by-non-empty-stack**: Create app-db, cast a spell (e.g., Dark Ritual) onto the stack using rules/cast-spell. Dispatch ::advance-phase. Assert phase unchanged. Bug this catches: advance-phase proceeding while stack is non-empty (phases.cljs:43).

9. **start-turn-blocked-by-pending-selection**: Same pattern as #7 but for ::start-turn. Assert phase unchanged. Bug this catches: start-turn ignoring in-progress selections (phases.cljs:153).

10. **start-turn-creates-history-entry**: Dispatch ::start-turn. Assert :history/pending-entry exists AND has :event-type = :fizzle.events.phases/start-turn AND :description is a non-empty string. Bug this catches: history wiring broken (descriptions.cljs integration).

### Unit Tests (direct function calls on game-db, not app-db)

11. **next-phase-full-mapping**: Use doseq over expected pairs: [[:untap :upkeep] [:upkeep :draw] [:draw :main1] [:main1 :combat] [:combat :main2] [:main2 :end] [:end :cleanup] [:cleanup :cleanup]]. Assert each (next-phase input) = expected. Bug this catches: phase ordering error or missing phase.

12. **untap-all-permanents-untaps-tapped**: Create test-db, add 3 permanents to battlefield (via h/add-card-to-zone + h/tap-permanent), call (phases/untap-all-permanents db :player-1). Assert all 3 are :object/tapped false. Bug this catches: untap query or transaction error.

13. **untap-all-permanents-only-for-specified-player**: Create test-db with 2 players. Tap permanents for both. Call untap-all-permanents for :player-1 only. Assert :player-1 permanents untapped, :player-2 permanents still tapped. Bug this catches: missing player filter in query (phases.cljs:79 ?controller check).

14. **advance-phase-fires-delayed-effects-on-upkeep**: Create test-db. Add a grant with delayed-effect that fires on upkeep (e.g., Rain of Filth grant). Set phase to :draw (so advancing goes to :main1, not upkeep). Then set phase to :end-of-previous-turn... Actually: create db at :untap phase, advance to :upkeep. Assert that delayed-effect grants have fired by checking the db state after (e.g., sacrifice trigger registered, mana ability granted). Bug this catches: delayed effect timing broken.

15. **start-turn-untap-phase-event-dispatched**: Call (phases/start-turn db :player-1) directly. Assert result db has phase :untap AND turn-based triggers have fired (the :untap phase-entered event dispatches untap-step trigger). Verify by checking that permanents owned by new active player are untapped. Bug this catches: phase-entered event not dispatched on start-turn.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] File exists at src/test/fizzle/events/phases_test.cljs
- [ ] 15 tests specified above all pass
- [ ] Integration tests (1-10) all use dispatch-sync pattern (reset!/dispatch-sync/deref), NOT direct function calls
- [ ] Unit tests (11-15) call pure functions on game-db directly
- [ ] Full turn cycle E2E (#1) verifies complete phase sequence + turn switch + state reset
- [ ] Cleanup discard test (#5) verifies :game/pending-selection with exact :selection/type and :selection/select-count
- [ ] Guard tests (#7, #8, #9) verify db is UNCHANGED after blocked dispatch
- [ ] History entry test (#10) verifies entry shape (event-type + description), not just existence
- [ ] Phase mapping test (#11) covers ALL 8 phases via doseq (no copy-paste)
- [ ] make validate passes (all existing + new tests green, lint clean, fmt clean)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Starting phase assumption**: create-game-scenario starts at :main1. Tests 1-5 depend on this. If this changes, tests break. The E2E test (#1) should assert starting phase before advancing.

**Combat skip with creatures**: Test #4 requires a creature on battlefield. Use h/add-test-creature which calls build-object-tx. This is the inverse of #3 — both are needed to prove the combat-skip logic is conditional, not always-on.

**Cleanup discard card count**: Test #5 needs exactly 8+ cards in hand. Default hand size max is 7 (MTG rules). Add cards via loop, not one-by-one hardcoded calls. Use (reduce (fn [db _] (first (h/add-card-to-zone db :island :hand :player-1))) db (range 8)).

**Player switching edge case**: start-turn switches active player via get-other-player-id. With create-game-scenario (which adds a bot opponent), this works. If ever tested without opponent, get-other-player-id returns nil and start-turn falls back to same player. Test #1 verifies the 2-player case.

**Delayed effects in upkeep (test #14)**: This is the hardest test. Delayed effects come from grants with :grant/delayed-effect. Need to set up a card that creates such a grant (e.g., Rain of Filth), or manually add a grant to a permanent. If setup is too complex, this test can be deferred to abilities_test.cljs or a follow-up. Document the decision.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO mocking re-frame dispatch or interceptors (defeats purpose of integration testing)
- ❌ NO hand-building app-db from scratch — use h/create-game-scenario (consistency with priority_test.cljs)
- ❌ NO some?/string? assertions on history entries — assert exact :event-type keyword and non-empty :description
- ❌ NO testing pure functions via dispatch-sync or vice versa — keep layers separate
- ❌ NO copy-pasting phase pairs in test #11 — use doseq over expected pairs vector
close_reason: Closed

title: Create db/game_state.cljs and update events/init.cljs to use it
design:
## Goal
Create the shared module db/game_state.cljs with identity constants and entity factories. Update events/init.cljs as the first consumer to validate the factory shape works. This fixes the opponent Turn 1 land-play bug and establishes the pattern for subsequent consumer migrations.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - events/init.cljs:61-74 — current player-tx and empty-mana-pool
   - events/init.cljs:117-169 — current init-game-state using inline construction
   - events/init.cljs:157-158 — turn-based trigger registration uses player-id keyword for event filtering
   - events/init.cljs:159-160 — stops loading uses :player/:opponent as STORAGE ROLE KEYS (NOT player-ids, do NOT change)
   - db/storage.cljs:90-95 — default-stops uses :player/:opponent as role keys
   - test_helpers.cljs:26-28,31-60 — current test player construction for comparison

2. Write tests first (TDD)
   File: src/test/fizzle/db/game_state_test.cljs

   Constants tests:
   - test-human-player-id-is-player-1: (is (= :player-1 gs/human-player-id)) — catches accidental rename
   - test-opponent-player-id-is-player-2: (is (= :player-2 gs/opponent-player-id)) — catches id collision with effect keywords
   - test-player-ids-differ: (is (not= gs/human-player-id gs/opponent-player-id)) — catches copy-paste error

   Factory tests — create-player-tx:
   - test-create-player-tx-defaults: verify ALL default values explicitly (life=20, mana=empty, storm=0, land-plays=1, max-hand-size=7). Bug this catches: entity shape drift (the root cause of this epic)
   - test-create-player-tx-overrides-merge: pass {:player/life 10 :player/is-opponent true} and verify life=10, is-opponent=true, AND other defaults still present. Bug this catches: overrides clobbering base defaults
   - test-create-player-tx-mana-override: pass {:player/mana-pool {:blue 3 :black 0 ...}} and verify exact mana pool. Bug this catches: partial mana pool merge issues
   - test-create-player-tx-returns-vector: verify (vector? result) since d/transact! requires vector. Bug this catches: wrong return shape breaks transact
   - test-create-player-tx-bot-archetype-override: pass {:player/bot-archetype :burn :player/is-opponent true} and verify both set. Bug this catches: bot setup regression

   Factory tests — create-game-entity-tx:
   - test-create-game-entity-tx-defaults: verify turn=1, phase=:main1, human-player-id=human-player-id constant. Bug this catches: game entity drift
   - test-create-game-entity-tx-uses-active-player: pass a mock eid, verify :game/active-player and :game/priority set. Bug this catches: wrong player gets priority
   - test-create-game-entity-tx-overrides: pass {:game/phase :combat} and verify phase overridden. Bug this catches: override mechanism broken

   Integration test:
   - test-init-game-state-creates-opponent-as-player-2: call init-game-state with a minimal deck, query opponent player-id, verify = :player-2. Bug this catches: init not using shared module

3. Implementation checklist
   - [ ] Create src/main/fizzle/db/game_state.cljs:
         - def human-player-id :player-1
         - def opponent-player-id :player-2
         - def empty-mana-pool {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
         - defn create-player-tx [player-id overrides] -> vector of tx-map with merged defaults
         - defn create-game-entity-tx [active-player-eid overrides] -> vector of tx-map
   - [ ] Create src/test/fizzle/db/game_state_test.cljs with all tests above
   - [ ] Run tests — they must FAIL (RED phase: module doesn't exist yet)
   - [ ] Implement db/game_state.cljs — run tests, they must PASS (GREEN)
   - [ ] Update events/init.cljs require: add [fizzle.db.game-state :as game-state]
   - [ ] Replace init.cljs line 62 (empty-mana-pool) — delete, use game-state/empty-mana-pool
   - [ ] Replace init.cljs lines 65-74 (player-tx fn) — delete, use game-state/create-player-tx
   - [ ] Replace init.cljs line 131: (player-tx :player-1 ...) -> (game-state/create-player-tx game-state/human-player-id {:player/name "Player" :player/max-hand-size 7})
   - [ ] Replace init.cljs line 132-134: (player-tx :opponent ...) -> (game-state/create-player-tx game-state/opponent-player-id {:player/name "Opponent" :player/is-opponent true :player/bot-archetype bot-archetype})
   - [ ] Replace init.cljs line 137: (get-player-eid db :opponent) -> (get-player-eid db game-state/opponent-player-id)
   - [ ] Replace init.cljs line 154-156: inline game entity -> (game-state/create-game-entity-tx player-eid {})
   - [ ] Replace init.cljs line 157: :player-1 -> game-state/human-player-id
   - [ ] Replace init.cljs line 158: :opponent -> game-state/opponent-player-id
   - [ ] DO NOT CHANGE init.cljs line 159-160: (:player stops) and (:opponent stops) are STORAGE ROLE KEYS, not player-ids
   - [ ] Run make validate — all tests must pass

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**CRITICAL: :opponent as storage role key vs player-id**
init.cljs line 159-160 uses (:opponent stops) where :opponent is a key in the stops map from db/storage.cljs:94 (default-stops returns {:player #{...} :opponent #{...}}). These are STORAGE ROLE KEYS that predate the player-id system. Do NOT change these to :player-2 — that would break stops loading. The storage role keys :player/:opponent are separate from player-id keywords and will be addressed in a later task if needed.

**Edge Case: Opponent land-plays-left change from 0 to 1**
Production currently gives opponent land-plays-left: 0. The shared factory default of 1 changes behavior: the opponent can now play lands on Turn 1. This is a bug fix (confirmed: bot calls can-play-land? which requires pos? check). However, verify no tests assert opponent land-plays-left = 0 — if any do, they are testing the buggy behavior and should be updated.

**Edge Case: create-player-tx override precedence**
The factory uses (merge defaults overrides). Verify that callers passing {:player/mana-pool {:blue 3}} get ONLY their mana pool, not a merge with empty-mana-pool. ClojureScript merge replaces map values entirely, so {:player/mana-pool {:blue 3}} would lose other colors. Test helpers currently pass full mana pools like {:blue 1 :black 3}, and merge with empty-mana-pool handles this: (merge {:white 0 :blue 0 ...} {:blue 3}) = {:white 0 :blue 3 ...}. BUT if a test passes ONLY {:blue 3}, the resulting pool would be {:blue 3} (no :white/:black etc). This is likely fine since the factory default includes all colors and merge replaces the whole :player/mana-pool key. Document this behavior.

**Edge Case: init-game-state returns app-db map, not just game-db**
init-game-state returns a map with :game/db plus UI state keys. The factory only creates transaction data for the game-db. The app-db wrapper remains in init-game-state. Do not try to move UI state into the shared module.

**Edge Case: create-game-entity-tx needs active-player-eid**
The game entity requires :game/active-player and :game/priority which are entity IDs (integers), not player-id keywords. These must be looked up AFTER player entities are transacted. The factory takes active-player-eid as a parameter — callers must ensure the player exists in the db before calling this.

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO using :opponent as a player-id (ADR-016: collision with effect target keyword)
- ❌ NO role-based dispatch in create-player-tx (no :human/:opponent parameter — just player-id + overrides)
- ❌ NO changing storage role keys :player/:opponent in stops (these are a separate namespace)
- ❌ NO duplicating empty-mana-pool or player defaults outside db/game_state.cljs
- ❌ NO moving UI/app-db state into the shared module (only game-db transaction data)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] db/game_state.cljs exists with 5 public defs: human-player-id, opponent-player-id, empty-mana-pool, create-player-tx, create-game-entity-tx
- [ ] events/init.cljs requires fizzle.db.game-state and uses its exports for all player/game entity construction
- [ ] events/init.cljs has zero local definitions of empty-mana-pool or player-tx
- [ ] Opponent created with player-id :player-2 in init (verified: grep ':opponent' init.cljs returns only the stops storage key on line 160)
- [ ] Opponent gets land-plays-left 1 and max-hand-size 7 from factory defaults
- [ ] 11+ unit tests in game_state_test.cljs pass, covering: constants (3), create-player-tx (5), create-game-entity-tx (3), integration (1)
- [ ] No test in game_state_test.cljs is tautological (every test asserts exact values, not some?/any?)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)

title: Task 1: Set up re-frame infrastructure and entry point
design:
## Goal Create the foundational infrastructure for the UI: add re-frame dependency, create index.html, create core.cljs entry point that initializes re-frame and mounts a minimal root component. ## Effort Estimate 2-3 hours ## Implementation ### 1. Add re-frame dependency Edit shadow-cljs.edn to add re-frame to dependencies: ```clojure :dependencies [[datascript "1.7.3"] [reagent "1.2.0"] [re-frame "1.4.3"]] ``` ### 2. Create resources/public directory and index.html ```bash mkdir -p resources/public ``` ```html Fizzle
``` ### 3. Create src/main/fizzle/core.cljs ```clojure (ns fizzle.core (:require [reagent.dom :as rdom] [re-frame.core :as rf])) ;; Placeholder app-db (will add game events in next task) (rf/reg-event-db ::init-db (fn [_ _] {:message "Fizzle loading..."})) (rf/reg-sub ::message (fn [db _] (:message db))) (defn app [] (let [msg @(rf/subscribe [::message])] [:div {:style {:padding "20px"}} [:h1 "Fizzle"] [:p msg]])) (defn ^:dev/after-load mount-root [] (rf/clear-subscription-cache!) (rdom/render [app] (.getElementById js/document "app"))) (defn init [] (rf/dispatch-sync [::init-db]) (mount-root)) ``` ### 4. Verify it works Run `make dev` and open http://localhost:8080 Should see "Fizzle" heading and "Fizzle loading..." message ## Success Criteria - [ ] `make dev` starts without errors (watch for "Build completed" in terminal) - [ ] Browser at http://localhost:8080 shows "Fizzle" heading and "Fizzle loading..." text - [ ] Hot reload works: edit message string in core.cljs, save, browser updates without refresh - [ ] `make test` passes (all 75 existing tests still green) - [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + format-check + tests) ## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN) - ❌ NO game logic in core.cljs (this is just infrastructure - game events come in next task) - ❌ NO direct DOM manipulation (use Reagent's rdom/render, not js interop) - ❌ NO hardcoded port numbers in code (shadow-cljs.edn handles this) ## Key Considerations (Edge Cases & Troubleshooting) **If shadow-cljs fails to compile:** - Check re-frame version compatibility (1.4.3 works with Reagent 1.2.0) - Run `npm install` to ensure shadow-cljs is installed - Check for typos in namespace requires **If port 8080 in use:** - shadow-cljs will show error with port conflict - Either kill the process using 8080 or edit shadow-cljs.edn :dev-http port - Use `lsof -i :8080` to find conflicting process **If hot reload doesn't work:** - Ensure `^:dev/after-load` metadata is on mount-root function - Check browser console for errors - Hard refresh (Cmd+Shift+R) to clear cache **Reference:** - shadow-cljs.edn already configured for :browser target with fizzle.core/init entry point - Existing source at src/main/fizzle/ (db/, engine/, cards/)
close_reason: Re-frame infrastructure set up. shadow-cljs.edn updated with re-frame dep, package.json updated with react/react-dom deps, index.html created, core.cljs entry point with placeholder app working. make dev serves at localhost:8080, all 75 tests pass, make validate passes.

title: Task 1: Implement basic lands (Island, Swamp)
design:
## Goal
Add Island and Swamp basic land cards following existing land patterns.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Context
Existing patterns in iggy_pop.cljs show land structure (City of Brass, Gemstone Mine).
Basic lands are simpler - just tap for single color, no triggers or counters.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:57-76 (City of Brass pattern)
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:82-105 (Gemstone Mine pattern)
- Note: basics have :card/supertypes #{:basic} and :card/subtypes #{:island}

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Add tests to: src/test/fizzle/cards/basic_lands_test.cljs (new file)

Test cases:
```clojure
;; test_island_taps_for_blue_mana
;; Setup: Island on battlefield, untapped
;; Action: activate-mana-ability with :blue
;; Verify: mana pool has {:blue 1}, island is tapped

;; test_swamp_taps_for_black_mana
;; Setup: Swamp on battlefield, untapped
;; Action: activate-mana-ability with :black
;; Verify: mana pool has {:black 1}, swamp is tapped

;; test_basic_land_played_from_hand
;; Setup: Island in hand, land plays remaining
;; Action: play-land
;; Verify: Island on battlefield, land plays decremented

;; test_tapped_land_cannot_tap_again
;; Setup: Island on battlefield, already tapped
;; Action: attempt activate-mana-ability
;; Verify: returns unchanged db (can-activate? false)

;; test_land_in_hand_cannot_activate_mana
;; Setup: Island in hand (not battlefield)
;; Action: attempt activate-mana-ability
;; Verify: returns unchanged db (zone check fails)
```

### 3. Add card definitions
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

```clojure
(def island
  {:card/id :island
   :card/name "Island"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/subtypes #{:island}
   :card/supertypes #{:basic}
   :card/text "{T}: Add {U}."
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true}
                     :ability/produces {:blue 1}}]})

(def swamp
  {:card/id :swamp
   :card/name "Swamp"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/subtypes #{:swamp}
   :card/supertypes #{:basic}
   :card/text "{T}: Add {B}."
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true}
                     :ability/produces {:black 1}}]})
```

### 4. Update all-cards vector
Add island and swamp to the all-cards def at bottom of iggy_pop.cljs:
```clojure
(def all-cards
  [dark-ritual brain-freeze cabal-ritual city-of-brass gemstone-mine island swamp])
```

### 5. Run validation
- make test
- make validate

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Island definition exists in iggy_pop.cljs with :card/supertypes #{:basic}
- [ ] Swamp definition exists in iggy_pop.cljs with :card/supertypes #{:basic}
- [ ] Island has :card/subtypes #{:island}
- [ ] Swamp has :card/subtypes #{:swamp}
- [ ] Both included in all-cards vector
- [ ] 5 tests in basic_lands_test.cljs all pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + fmt + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in engine code (data-over-code: cards are pure data)
- ❌ NO skipping TDD (write failing tests first, then implement)
- ❌ NO modifying activate-mana-ability for basic lands (should work generically)

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Already Tapped**
- Existing can-activate? checks :object/tapped via costs/can-pay?
- Verify this works for basic lands (test_tapped_land_cannot_tap_again)

**Edge Case: Wrong Zone**
- Mana abilities only work on battlefield
- activate-mana-ability checks :object/zone in validation
- Verify with test_land_in_hand_cannot_activate_mana

**Pattern Consistency**
- Follow exact structure from City of Brass / Gemstone Mine
- Use :ability/produces for UI display (shows mana symbol on land)
- Use :ability/cost {:tap true} not {:cost/tap true}

**Reference Implementation**
- City of Brass at iggy_pop.cljs:57-76 is closest pattern
- Note: basic lands don't need :card/triggers (no damage on tap)
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 1: Create Makefile with Core Commands
design:
## Goal

Create Makefile with core development commands: repl, dev, test, clean. This establishes the primary interface before adding linting/formatting.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Create Makefile

```makefile
.PHONY: repl dev test clean help

# Detect Java - try common locations
JAVA_HOME ?= $(shell \
  if [ -d "/usr/local/opt/openjdk" ]; then echo "/usr/local/opt/openjdk"; \
  elif [ -d "/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk" ]; then echo "/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk"; \
  elif [ -n "$$JAVA_HOME" ]; then echo "$$JAVA_HOME"; \
  else echo ""; fi)

ifneq ($(JAVA_HOME),)
  export PATH := $(JAVA_HOME)/bin:$(PATH)
endif

help:
	@echo "Fizzle Development Commands"
	@echo ""
	@echo "  make repl     - Start ClojureScript REPL (node)"
	@echo "  make dev      - Start browser dev server"
	@echo "  make test     - Run all tests"
	@echo "  make clean    - Remove build artifacts"
	@echo ""
	@echo "Validation (after linting added):"
	@echo "  make validate - Run all checks (lint + fmt + test)"

repl:
	npx shadow-cljs node-repl

dev:
	npx shadow-cljs watch app

test:
	npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js

clean:
	rm -rf out/ .shadow-cljs/ resources/public/js/
```

### 2. Update CLAUDE.md

Add this section after Build Commands:

```markdown
## Development Commands

Use these make commands instead of hand-rolling shadow-cljs calls:

\`\`\`bash
# Development
make repl      # Start node REPL - ready to eval immediately
make dev       # Start browser dev server with hot reload
make test      # Run all tests
make clean     # Remove build artifacts (out/, .shadow-cljs/)

# Validation (after Phase 2)
make validate  # Run lint + format-check + tests
\`\`\`

**IMPORTANT**: Always use \`make test\` instead of raw shadow-cljs commands.
```

### 3. Verify all commands work

- [ ] `make help` shows available commands
- [ ] `make repl` starts REPL, can eval `(+ 1 2)` and `(require '[fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state]])`
- [ ] `make test` runs 40 tests, all pass
- [ ] `make dev` starts watch (ctrl-c to exit)
- [ ] `make clean` removes out/ and .shadow-cljs/

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Makefile exists at project root with repl, dev, test, clean, help targets
- [ ] `make repl` starts REPL in one command without PATH export
- [ ] `make test` runs all 40 tests, outputs pass count
- [ ] `make help` displays all available commands
- [ ] `make clean` removes out/, .shadow-cljs/, resources/public/js/
- [ ] CLAUDE.md has "Development Commands" section with make commands
- [ ] CLAUDE.md instructs agent to use make commands
- [ ] Changes committed

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Java Not Installed**
- Makefile attempts to detect Java in common locations
- If Java not found, shadow-cljs will fail with clear error message
- User must install Java separately (documented in README)

**Edge Case: Different JAVA_HOME Locations**
- macOS Intel Homebrew: /usr/local/opt/openjdk
- macOS ARM Homebrew: /opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk
- Linux: Typically in PATH already or JAVA_HOME set
- Makefile checks multiple locations, falls back to existing JAVA_HOME/PATH

**Edge Case: npm not installed**
- npx requires Node.js/npm
- Error message will be clear: "npx: command not found"
- Prerequisite, not something Makefile can fix

**Portability**
- Makefile uses POSIX shell commands (works on macOS/Linux)
- Windows users need WSL or alternative (out of scope)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO hard-coded absolute paths without fallbacks (portability)
- ❌ NO manual PATH exports required before running make (usability)
- ❌ NO npm scripts duplicating Makefile commands (single source of truth)
- ❌ NO undocumented commands (help target lists all)
close_reason: Closed

title: Bug: Bot casts produce duplicate Yield history entries instead of Cast/Resolve
description: Bot casts inline in yield-impl produce two Yield entries instead of one Cast and one Resolve entry
design:
## Goal
Bot casts should produce proper Cast/Resolve history entries instead of duplicate Yield entries. Currently, bot casts happen inline in yield-impl, producing ::yield history entries formatted as "Yield: Lightning Bolt -> Main 1" instead of "Cast Lightning Bolt" and "Resolve Lightning Bolt".

## Root Cause
After fizzle-ipoo fix, bot casts move into handle-loop-state :bot-phase. Two history entry creation paths still exist:

1. **yield-loop** (game.cljs:765-797): Used by ::yield-all/F6. Creates entries for each yield-impl iteration where game-db changes. Uses describe-event([::yield], pre-db, post-db, nil, nil).

2. **history interceptor** (interceptor.cljs:53-115): Used by single ::yield clicks. Captures each ::yield event where game-db changes. Uses describe-event(event, pre-db, post-db, selection-type, casting-spell-id).

Both paths call describe-yield (descriptions.cljs:165-182) which formats "Yield: [card] -> [phase]". For bot casts, the correct format should be "Cast [card]" or "Resolve [card]".

**Duplicate entry problem**: For single ::yield clicks, the ::yield event handler re-dispatches when continue-yield? is true. Each dispatch triggers the interceptor. A bot cast followed by priority transfer creates TWO interceptor entries. The priority-transfer entry (stack unchanged, just priority bit flipped) is noise.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Bot cast produces exactly ONE history entry described as "Cast Lightning Bolt"
- [ ] Bot bolt resolution produces exactly ONE history entry described as "Resolve Lightning Bolt"
- [ ] Priority-transfer-only changes (stack unchanged, phase unchanged, turn unchanged) are filtered from history
- [ ] Human casts still produce correct "Cast [name]" entries (regression check)
- [ ] Human yields that resolve spells still produce correct "Yield: [name] -> [phase]" entries
- [ ] yield-loop path (::yield-all) and interceptor path (::yield) both produce correct descriptions
- [ ] 5+ tests: bot-cast-description, bot-resolve-description, no-priority-transfer-noise, human-cast-not-affected, human-yield-resolve-description
- [ ] make validate clean

## Implementation Checklist

### Step 1: Extend describe-yield to detect bot context
- [ ] Add active-is-bot? parameter to describe-event signature (6th parameter, optional, defaults to nil for backward compat):
  ```clojure
  ([event pre-game-db game-db-after selection-type casting-spell-id]
   (describe-event event pre-game-db game-db-after selection-type casting-spell-id nil))
  ([event pre-game-db game-db-after selection-type casting-spell-id active-is-bot?]
   ...)
  ```
- [ ] Pass active-is-bot? to describe-yield when event is ::yield
- [ ] In describe-yield, when active-is-bot? is true:
  - If pre-db stack empty AND post-db stack non-empty: return "Cast [card-name from post-db stack top]"
  - If pre-db stack non-empty AND post-db stack empty: return "Resolve [card-name from pre-db stack top]"
  - Otherwise: use existing format (e.g., bot phase advance)
- [ ] When active-is-bot? is false/nil: keep existing describe-yield behavior unchanged

### Step 2: Update yield-loop to pass bot context
- [ ] In yield-loop (game.cljs), determine active-is-bot? from post-db:
  ```clojure
  (let [active-pid (queries/get-active-player-id post-db)
        active-is-bot? (boolean (bot/get-bot-archetype post-db active-pid))]
    (descriptions/describe-event [::yield] pre-db post-db nil nil active-is-bot?))
  ```

### Step 3: Update interceptor to pass bot context
- [ ] In interceptor.cljs :after, determine active-is-bot? from game-db-after:
  ```clojure
  (let [active-pid (queries/get-active-player-id game-db-after)
        active-is-bot? (boolean (bot/get-bot-archetype game-db-after active-pid))]
    (descriptions/describe-event event pre-game-db game-db-after
                                 selection-type casting-spell-id active-is-bot?))
  ```
- [ ] Import bot/get-bot-archetype in interceptor.cljs (add require for fizzle.bots.protocol)

### Step 4: Filter priority-transfer noise
- [ ] Update should-create-history-entry? in game_loop.cljs to also filter priority-transfer-only changes:
  - Condition: is-bot AND pre-stack-state == post-stack-state (both empty or both non-empty with same items) AND same phase AND same turn
  - Simplest check: if the only datascript changes are to :game/priority or :game/passed, filter it out
  - Actually simpler: expand the existing filter. Currently filters: bot + both stacks empty + same turn. ADD: bot + both stacks non-empty + same phase + same turn (this catches priority transfers during stack resolution)
  ```clojure
  (let [same-stack-state (or (and pre-stack-empty post-stack-empty)
                             (and (not pre-stack-empty) (not post-stack-empty)
                                  (= pre-phase post-phase)))]
    (not (and is-bot same-stack-state (= pre-turn post-turn))))
  ```

### Step 5: Add tests
- [ ] In game_loop_test.cljs: test describe-yield with bot context produces "Cast [name]"
- [ ] In game_loop_test.cljs: test describe-yield with bot context produces "Resolve [name]"
- [ ] In game_loop_test.cljs: test should-create-history-entry? filters priority-transfer noise
- [ ] In burn_integration_test.cljs: integration test verifying history entries for a full bot cast+resolve cycle
- [ ] In game_loop_test.cljs: regression test that human yields still produce "Yield: [name]" format

## Key Considerations

**Two history paths require same fix**:
- yield-loop and interceptor both call describe-event. By fixing describe-event/describe-yield, both paths get the fix automatically.
- The active-is-bot? parameter propagates through both paths.

**Priority-transfer noise is a separate concern from descriptions**:
- Even with correct descriptions, the priority-transfer entry is noise (nothing meaningful happened from the user's perspective).
- The should-create-history-entry? enhancement catches this case.

**Backward compatibility**:
- describe-event's new 6th parameter defaults to nil (backward compat for existing callers).
- should-create-history-entry?'s enhanced filter is a superset of the existing filter — no regression risk.
- The interceptor already imports game-loop; adding bot protocol import is a new dependency but it's a lightweight query.

## Edge Cases (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Storm spells**:
- Storm creates multiple stack items. Bot doesn't use storm currently, but describe-yield should handle post-db stack having multiple items. Using get-stack-top-info on post-db for cast detection (gets the spell, not storm trigger) is correct.

**describe-yield with nil game-dbs**:
- Both pre-game-db and game-db-after can be nil in edge cases. Existing nil guards in describe-yield handle this. The new active-is-bot? path should also guard against nil dbs.

**Multiple bot casts in one loop iteration**:
- If the bot casts 2 bolts (2 mountains, 2 bolts), the priority loop in handle-loop-state runs twice. yield-loop sees one db change (from no stack to 2 items on stack). describe-yield with bot context: pre-stack empty, post-stack non-empty -> "Cast [top item name]". Only one entry, which is correct (both casts are one logical action from the game loop's perspective).

## Anti-patterns
- Do NOT add bot-specific if/else branching in the interceptor (pass data through describe-event instead)
- Do NOT create a separate ::bot-cast event (keep bot actions in the game loop)
- Do NOT duplicate description logic (reuse describe-cast-spell or get-card-name from descriptions.cljs)
- Do NOT remove should-create-history-entry? — enhance it to also filter priority-transfer noise
close_reason: Closed

title: Bot Scenario Configuration
description:
Configurable opponent scenarios: specify cards in the bot's deck, sculpt starting hand, set behavior rules. The bot system is about testing against specific cards and disruption, not simulating a real opponent deck.

## What's Already Built
- Priority system (yield/yield-all, phase stops)
- Bot protocol (IBot multimethod) with goldfish and burn archetypes
- Bot turn cycle with active-player switching
- Burn bot casts through full engine path
- Setup screen: Goldfish/Burn dropdown + clock turns

## What This Epic Adds
- Bot deck configuration (specify which cards the opponent has)
- Bot starting hand sculpting (deterministic scenarios)
- Behavior rule configuration (how bot plays its cards)
- Hate piece scenarios (bot starts with permanents like Seal of Cleansing, Chalice)
- Counterspell scenarios (bot holds up countermagic)
- Named preset scenarios (out-of-the-box configs: Goldfish, Burn, Hate Pieces, Dreadnought)
- UX for configuring all of the above (open design question — needs brainstorm)

## Key Design Decisions (from brainstorm 2026-02-20)
- Test against cards, not decks — bot delivers specific hate cards, not realistic opponent play
- Player-directed interaction for permanents — player activates opponent's abilities directly
- Engine supports robust rule-based behavior; UX challenge is exposing it without raw EDN
- Dreadnought clock as a target out-of-box scenario (requires creatures/combat)

## Dependencies
- Creatures and Combat (fizzle-u07p) needed for creature clock scenarios
- Some hate pieces may need new card implementations (Chalice, Sphere of Resistance)
