title: Add creature P/T display with color-coded modification indicator on battlefield
description: Add effective P/T display below card name on battlefield creatures. Color-code: green when buffed (effective > base), red when debuffed, default when equal. Requires new creature-display-data subscription bundling P/T comparison.
design:
## Goal
Display effective power/toughness on all battlefield creatures with color coding for modifications. Foundation for all subsequent creature UX tasks.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing code:
   - src/fizzle/views/battlefield.cljs:102-152 — permanent-view component (renders card name, counters, abilities)
   - src/fizzle/subs/game.cljs — subscription pattern using :<- dependency injection and ::game-db base
   - src/fizzle/engine/creatures.cljs — effective-power/effective-toughness (pure functions: db + object-id → int or nil)
   - src/fizzle/subs/game.cljs ::battlefield subscription — already groups objects into {:creatures :other :lands}
   - src/test/fizzle/subs/game_test.cljs — sub-value helper pattern for testing subscriptions

2. Write tests first (TDD):

   **Subscription tests** (in src/test/fizzle/subs/game_test.cljs or new file):
   - test-creature-display-data-base-pt: Create Nimble Mongoose (1/1) on battlefield, verify subscription returns {:effective-power 1 :effective-toughness 1 :base-power 1 :base-toughness 1 :power-mod nil :toughness-mod nil}
   - test-creature-display-data-buffed: Create creature with +1/+1 counter, verify :power-mod :buffed, :toughness-mod :buffed, effective values = base + 1
   - test-creature-display-data-debuffed: Create creature with -1/-1 counter, verify :power-mod :debuffed, :toughness-mod :debuffed
   - test-creature-display-data-mixed-mods: Create creature with +1/+1 and -1/-1 counters that cancel out, verify :power-mod nil (net zero = unmodified)
   - test-creature-display-data-split-mods: Create creature with grant boosting only power (+2/+0), verify :power-mod :buffed, :toughness-mod nil
   - test-creature-display-data-non-creature: Create a land on battlefield, verify subscription returns nil for it
   - test-creature-display-data-zero-toughness: Create creature debuffed to 0 toughness, verify still shows correct values (SBAs handle death separately)

   **View tests** (in src/test/fizzle/views/battlefield_test.cljs):
   - test-permanent-view-creature-shows-pt: Render permanent-view with creature object, assert hiccup output contains P/T string (e.g. "1/1")
   - test-permanent-view-land-no-pt: Render permanent-view with land object, assert hiccup output does NOT contain P/T div
   - test-permanent-view-buffed-pt-green: Render with buffed creature data, assert P/T div has green text class
   - test-permanent-view-debuffed-pt-red: Render with debuffed creature data, assert P/T div has red text class

3. Implementation checklist:
   - [ ] subs/game.cljs — Register ::creature-display-data subscription
         Input: game-db, object-id
         Output: {:effective-power int, :effective-toughness int, :base-power int, :base-toughness int, :power-mod (:buffed|:debuffed|nil), :toughness-mod (:buffed|:debuffed|nil)}
         Pattern: Use :<- [::game-db] dependency, call creatures/effective-power and creatures/effective-toughness
         Modification detection: compare effective vs base, return :buffed if >, :debuffed if <, nil if equal
         Return nil for non-creatures (effective-power returns nil)
   - [ ] views/battlefield.cljs — Add P/T display in permanent-view AFTER the counter display section (line ~135), BEFORE the ability buttons
         Only render when object is a creature (check :card/types contains :creature)
         Format: "P/T" e.g. "3/2" in a div with text-[13px] font-bold
         Color classes: text-green-400 for :buffed, text-red-400 for :debuffed, inherit for nil
         Power and toughness colored independently (power can be green while toughness is red)
   - [ ] views/battlefield.cljs — Pass creature display data into permanent-view
         Option A: Call subscription inline (if permanent-view is a form-2 component)
         Option B: Compute in parent and pass as prop (if keeping permanent-view as form-1)
         Prefer Option B to match existing pattern — ::battlefield subscription already provides full object data, can enrich there or compute in view

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All battlefield creatures show effective P/T (e.g. "3/2") below card name
- [ ] P/T values independently color-coded: green (text-green-400) when effective > base, red (text-red-400) when effective < base, default when equal
- [ ] Non-creature permanents (lands, artifacts, enchantments) do not render P/T section
- [ ] Creatures with +1/+1 counters show buffed (green) P/T values
- [ ] Creatures with net-zero modifications show default color
- [ ] Subscription returns nil for non-creatures (no render attempted)
- [ ] All existing tests pass: make validate
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Negative or Zero P/T**
- Creatures can be debuffed to 0 or negative effective P/T (e.g. 1/1 with two -1/-1 counters = -1/-1)
- Display the actual computed value (even negative) — SBAs handle death when toughness <= 0
- Do NOT clamp to 0 or hide negative values

**Edge Case: Mixed Modifications**
- Power can be buffed while toughness is debuffed (e.g. +2/+0 grant on a 1/1 with -1/-1 counter = 2/0)
- Color each value independently, not as a pair

**Edge Case: Token Creatures**
- Tokens have object/power and object/toughness set at creation
- effective-power/effective-toughness work identically for tokens — no special handling needed

**Edge Case: Creature Without Base P/T**
- effective-power defaults to 0 via (or :object/power 0) — safe
- This edge case is already handled by the engine

**Performance: Subscription Granularity**
- Do NOT create per-creature subscriptions that each independently query game-db
- Preferred: Compute creature display data within existing ::battlefield subscription or create a single subscription that returns a map of {object-id -> display-data} for all battlefield creatures
- This avoids N subscription re-evaluations when game-db changes

**Reference Implementation**
- Counter display pattern at battlefield.cljs:132-137 — conditional rendering with (when (and counters (seq counters)))
- Follow same pattern for P/T: (when creature-display-data [:div ...])

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO calling effective-power/effective-toughness directly in the view (architecture: views subscribe to data, don't query engine)
- ❌ NO per-creature re-frame subscriptions via (subscribe [:creature-pt creature-id]) in a loop (performance: creates N reactive subscriptions)
- ❌ NO hardcoding P/T values or reading :object/power directly (correctness: must use engine computation that includes counters, grants, static abilities)
- ❌ NO storing computed P/T in Datascript (architecture: P/T is derived, computed on query, never persisted)
- ❌ NO clamping P/T to zero (correctness: display actual computed value, SBAs handle death)
close_reason: Closed

title: Add missing corner cases for mana abilities
description:
## Goal
Add missing corner case tests for mana ability activation.

## Corner Cases to Add

### Invalid requests
- test-mana-ability-wrong-color-request
  Verify requesting a color the land doesn't produce is handled
  (e.g., requesting :blue from a Swamp)

- test-colorless-mana-from-colored-only-land
  Verify requesting colorless from a land that only produces colored

### Timing edge cases
- test-ability-activation-object-destroyed-between-cost-and-effect
  Verify behavior if object is somehow removed after paying cost
  but before effect resolves (may be N/A for mana abilities which don't use stack)

## Implementation
Add to tap_land_test.cljs or create new mana_abilities_test.cljs
Use existing test patterns

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All corner case tests written and passing
- [ ] Tests verify actual production behavior
- [ ] Edge cases documented
close_reason: Added 3 corner case tests: wrong color request, colorless from colored-only land, and atomic resolution documentation

title: Extract selection/core.cljs with mechanism, multimethods, and data-driven validation
design:
## Goal

Create events/selection/core.cljs containing the selection mechanism: multimethod definitions, confirm-selection-impl wrapper, validate-selection, and cleanup helpers. Make validation and auto-confirm data-driven so core has zero knowledge of specific selection types.

This is the foundational file that all domain modules will register methods on. After this task, the multimethods and mechanism functions exist in core.cljs, but the original selection.cljs still contains everything else — subsequent tasks will extract domain code.

**CRITICAL BRIDGE CONSTRAINT**: Event handler REGISTRATIONS stay in selection.cljs during migration (keywords must not change). Core exports implementation functions only.

## Effort Estimate

4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code

Key references:
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs:1013-1025 — execute-confirmed-selection multimethod definition
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs:1205-1241 — confirm-selection-impl wrapper
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs:1248-1454 — event handlers (STAY in selection.cljs during bridge)
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs:59-70 — remove-spell-stack-item
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs:711-729 — cleanup-selection-source
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs:1254-1257 — auto-confirm-types static set (to be replaced with :selection/auto-confirm? flag)
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs:1312-1369 — confirm-selection validation (to be replaced with validate-selection)
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs:1268-1270 — pile-choice max-count type-specific logic (to be normalized)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

**Data-driven validation tests** (new test file: test/fizzle/events/selection/core_test.cljs):
- test_exact_validation_passes_when_count_equals_select_count — catches regression if :exact logic wrong
- test_exact_validation_fails_when_count_differs — catches missing count check
- test_at_most_validation_passes_when_count_under_limit — catches regression for peek/graveyard types
- test_at_most_validation_fails_when_count_exceeds — catches missing upper-bound check
- test_at_least_one_validation_passes_with_one — catches regression for exile-cards
- test_at_least_one_validation_fails_when_empty — catches missing empty check
- test_always_validation_passes_with_empty — catches regression for scry/x-mana
- test_exact_or_zero_passes_with_zero — catches tutor fail-to-find regression
- test_exact_or_zero_passes_with_exact_count — catches tutor normal select regression
- test_exact_or_zero_fails_with_partial — catches missing partial-select rejection
- test_nil_validation_rejects — catches missing default-reject for unset builders (safety)
- test_candidate_membership_rejects_non_candidate — catches correctness regression where selected items not in candidate pool
- test_candidate_membership_skipped_when_no_candidates — catches false rejection for types without candidates (x-mana, scry)

**Auto-confirm tests** (same file):
- test_auto_confirm_flag_triggers_confirm — catches regression if auto-confirm logic broken
- test_no_auto_confirm_without_flag — catches false auto-confirm for types that shouldn't

**Bridge compatibility test**:
- test_existing_selection_events_still_dispatch — verifies ::selection/toggle-selection keyword still works

### 3. Implementation checklist

- [ ] Create src/main/fizzle/events/selection/core.cljs with requires: engine/zones, engine/stack, engine/effects, engine/grants, db/queries, re-frame
- [ ] Move execute-confirmed-selection multimethod def to core.cljs (just the defmulti, NOT the defmethods)
- [ ] Define build-selection-for-effect multimethod (NEW):
      ```clojure
      (defmulti build-selection-for-effect
        "Build selection state for an effect requiring player interaction.
         Dispatches on effect type or :player-target for :any-player targeting."
        (fn [_db _player-id _object-id effect _remaining-effects]
          (if (= :any-player (:effect/target effect))
            :player-target
            (:effect/type effect))))
      ```
- [ ] Move confirm-selection-impl wrapper to core.cljs (keep public)
- [ ] Move remove-spell-stack-item to core.cljs
- [ ] Move cleanup-selection-source to core.cljs
- [ ] Create validate-selection function:
      ```clojure
      (defn validate-selection [selection]
        (let [selected (:selection/selected selection)
              count-selected (count selected)
              select-count (:selection/select-count selection 0)
              candidates (or (:selection/candidates selection)
                            (:selection/candidate-ids selection))
              ;; Universal candidate membership check
              candidates-valid? (if candidates
                                  (every? #(contains? candidates %) selected)
                                  true)
              ;; Count validation based on :selection/validation
              count-valid? (case (:selection/validation selection)
                            :exact (= count-selected select-count)
                            :at-most (<= count-selected select-count)
                            :at-least-one (pos? count-selected)
                            :always true
                            :exact-or-zero (or (zero? count-selected)
                                              (= count-selected select-count))
                            ;; nil or unknown: reject (safe default)
                            false)]
          (and candidates-valid? count-valid?)))
      ```
- [ ] Create toggle-selection-impl function (extracted logic from ::toggle-selection):
      - Use :selection/select-count universally (no pile-choice type check)
      - Use :selection/auto-confirm? flag instead of auto-confirm-types set
      - Preserve :selection/exact? false handling for unlimited select
      - Return updated app-db
- [ ] Create confirm-selection-handler function that calls validate-selection then confirm-selection-impl
- [ ] Update selection.cljs:
      - Add require: [fizzle.events.selection.core :as core]
      - Change all defmethod registrations to use core/execute-confirmed-selection
      - Replace ::toggle-selection handler body with call to (core/toggle-selection-impl db id)
      - Replace ::confirm-selection handler body with call to (core/confirm-selection-handler db)
      - Keep ALL rf/reg-event-db calls in selection.cljs (keywords must stay as :fizzle.events.selection/*)
      - Remove: auto-confirm-types def, inline validation logic, old confirm-selection-impl, remove-spell-stack-item, cleanup-selection-source
- [ ] Update ALL existing builders in selection.cljs to set new data-driven fields:
      - :selection/auto-confirm? true/false
      - :selection/validation (:exact, :at-most, :at-least-one, :always, :exact-or-zero)
      - :selection/select-count normalized (pile-choice: set to hand-count value)
- [ ] Ensure all existing tests pass with the bridge in place
- [ ] Run make validate

### 4. Bridge strategy (REVISED)

During migration, selection.cljs keeps ALL event registrations (preserving keyword namespaces). Event handlers become thin wrappers calling core functions:

```clojure
;; In selection.cljs (bridge phase):
(require '[fizzle.events.selection.core :as core])

;; Event registrations STAY HERE — keywords unchanged
(rf/reg-event-db ::toggle-selection
  (fn [db [_ id]] (core/toggle-selection-impl db id)))

(rf/reg-event-db ::confirm-selection
  (fn [db _] (core/confirm-selection-handler db)))

;; Defmethod registrations change target:
(defmethod core/execute-confirmed-selection :discard ...)
```

Event registrations move to core.cljs ONLY in the final import-update task when all keyword references are updated.

**Builder backward compat**: All builders in selection.cljs must be updated IN THIS TASK to set :selection/validation and :selection/auto-confirm? — the new validate-selection requires these fields (nil → reject). This is safe because we're editing the same file that defines the builders.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] events/selection/core.cljs exists with: both multimethods, confirm-selection-impl, toggle-selection-impl, confirm-selection-handler, validate-selection, cleanup helpers
- [ ] validate-selection handles all 5 validation types + nil→reject + candidate membership check
- [ ] toggle-selection-impl uses :selection/auto-confirm? flag (no static type set)
- [ ] toggle-selection-impl uses :selection/select-count universally (no pile-choice type check)
- [ ] confirm-selection-handler uses validate-selection (no type-specific branches)
- [ ] selection.cljs requires core.cljs, all defmethods register on core/execute-confirmed-selection
- [ ] ALL event keywords remain as :fizzle.events.selection/* (bridge preserves keywords)
- [ ] ALL existing builders updated with :selection/validation and :selection/auto-confirm? fields
- [ ] All existing ~1265 tests pass (behavior-preserving)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] core.cljs has zero type-specific logic (no cond/case on :selection/type)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Candidate Membership Regression**
Current confirm-selection checks every? in candidates for tutor, peek, pile-choice, graveyard-return. The new validate-selection MUST include universal candidate membership check when :selection/candidates or :selection/candidate-ids is present. Without this, users could confirm selections with invalid items.

**Edge Case: Pile-choice max-count Normalization**
Toggle-selection line 1268-1270 has: (if (= type :pile-choice) hand-count select-count). This is type-specific. Fix: pile-choice builder must set :selection/select-count to hand-count value. Core reads :selection/select-count universally. Builder can also store :selection/hand-count separately for the execution defmethod to use.

**Edge Case: nil :selection/validation**
If a builder forgets to set :selection/validation, validate-selection returns false (safe reject). This prevents accidentally allowing unvalidated selections. All builders MUST be updated in this task.

**Edge Case: :selection/exact? false (unlimited select)**
Toggle-selection line 1290-1294 checks (false? (:selection/exact? selection)) for exile-cards unlimited add. This is already data-driven and must be preserved in toggle-selection-impl.

**Edge Case: Event keyword preservation**
:fizzle.events.selection/toggle-selection MUST remain the dispatched keyword. If core.cljs registers with ::toggle-selection, the keyword becomes :fizzle.events.selection.core/toggle-selection — breaking all views and tests. Bridge keeps registrations in selection.cljs.

**Reference implementation for bridge pattern:**
game.cljs already does this: bare requires for abilities.cljs ensure namespace loading. The same pattern applies — domain files loaded via bare requires, event registrations stay in the entry-point namespace during migration.

## Anti-Patterns

- ❌ NO type-specific logic in core.cljs (no cond/case on :selection/type; if core knows about :tutor or :scry, the separation failed)
- ❌ NO event registrations in core.cljs during bridge phase (would change keyword namespaces, breaking dispatchers)
- ❌ NO validate-selection without candidate membership check (correctness regression for tutor, peek, pile-choice, graveyard-return)
- ❌ NO ignoring nil :selection/validation (must reject as safe default)
- ❌ NO pile-choice special-casing in toggle handler (builders must normalize select-count)
- ❌ NO forward declarations
close_reason: Extracted core.cljs with multimethods, data-driven validation (5 types), auto-confirm flag, cleanup helpers. Bridge in place. All 1266 tests pass, make validate green.

title: Vendetta card + tests
description: Implement Vendetta ({B} Instant) card definition and full test suite.
design:
## Goal
Implement Vendetta ({B} Instant): Destroy target nonblack creature. You lose life equal to that creature's toughness.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours (card definition + 10 tests + registry entry)

## Implementation

### Card Definition
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/black/vendetta.cljs

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.black.vendetta)

(def card
  {:card/id :vendetta
   :card/name "Vendetta"
   :card/cmc 1
   :card/mana-cost {:black 1}
   :card/colors #{:black}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/text "Destroy target nonblack creature. It can't be regenerated. You lose life equal to that creature's toughness."
   :card/targeting [{:target/id :target-creature
                     :target/type :object
                     :target/zone :battlefield
                     :target/controller :any
                     :target/criteria {:match/types #{:creature}
                                       :match/not-colors #{:black}}
                     :target/required true}]
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :destroy
                   :effect/target-ref :target-creature}
                  {:effect/type :lose-life-equal-to-toughness
                   :effect/target-ref :target-creature}]})
```

NOTE: The :lose-life-equal-to-toughness effect reads the caster (player-id) as the life loser — no :effect/target :self needed. The target-ref resolves to the creature for reading toughness.

### Registry
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/registry.cljs
- Add require for fizzle.cards.black.vendetta
- APPEND card to END of all-cards (never insert — preserves card-index for URL encoding)

### Test File
File: src/test/fizzle/cards/black/vendetta_test.cljs

**Tests (10 — targeted spell with creature interaction):**

A. Card definition — all fields exact values

B. Cast-resolve happy path — opponent has 2/3 white creature → cast-with-target → creature destroyed, caster loses 3 life
   - Use th/add-opponent, th/add-card-to-zone with a creature card
   - Verify creature in graveyard after resolve
   - Verify caster life decreased by toughness

C1. Cannot cast — no mana
C2. Cannot cast — no valid targets (no creatures on battlefield)
C3. Cannot cast — only black creatures on battlefield (nonblack restriction)

D. Storm count increments

F1. Cannot target black creature (match/not-colors #{:black})
F2. Can target multicolor creature that is NOT black (e.g., red/green)

G1. Target creature has high toughness (0/7) → caster loses 7 life
G2. Life goes below 0 (caster at 2 life, creature toughness 5 → caster at -3)

**Key testing notes:**
- Need a creature card definition for tests. Use Goblin Welder (red creature, 1/1) which already exists, or create a test-only creature via th/add-card-to-zone with inline card data
- Verify BOTH effects: creature destroyed AND life lost
- The destroy happens first, then life loss reads :card/toughness (persists in graveyard)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Card definition with all required fields, exact values
- [ ] 10+ tests all passing
- [ ] Card registered in registry.cljs (appended to end)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ No (is (some? x)) — use exact value checks
- ❌ No reimplementing handlers — use th/cast-with-target
- ❌ Do NOT read :object/toughness — the effect reads :card/toughness (persists after destroy)
- ❌ Do NOT add :effect/target :self to the lose-life effect — player-id handles it
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 3: Add trigger descriptions to iggy_pop.cljs

### 2. Add descriptions

```clojure
 :trigger/description "deals 1 damage to you"

                    :effect/amount 1

```

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

title: Epic: Fill test coverage gaps (queries, cards, regressions)
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Dedicated test file for all 19 public query functions in db/queries.cljs
- Unit test for remove-object in engine/zones.cljs
- Card integration tests (cast-resolve cycle) for 7 untested cards: brain-freeze, dark-ritual, cabal-ritual, city-of-brass, gemstone-mine, underground-river, ill-gotten-gains
- Regression tests for bug fixes from commits 46a281f (selection clearing, storm copies, sorcery speed) and earlier peek-and-select fixes
- Subscription testing explicitly deferred (out of scope)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] queries_test.cljs exists covering all 19 public functions with positive and negative cases
- [ ] Brain Freeze storm integration test verifies mill + storm copies
- [ ] Cabal Ritual threshold integration test verifies conditional effects
- [ ] City of Brass trigger integration test verifies damage-on-tap
- [ ] remove-object unit test verifies entity removal and no-op on missing ID
- [ ] Regression test: storm copies resolve correctly (not infinite loop)
- [ ] Regression test: sorcery-speed enforcement during non-main phases
- [ ] All 742+ existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO mocking Datascript (reason: queries are pure functions against real DB - mocking defeats purpose)
- NO test-only methods in production code (reason: tests should exercise public API only)
- NO subscription tests in this epic (reason: deferred by design - subs are thin wrappers over queries)
- NO duplicating test helpers across files (reason: extract shared helpers to a test-helpers namespace if needed)
- NO testing cards that are already well-covered as fixtures (reason: Dark Ritual is used in rules_test but needs its OWN integration test verifying BBB mana production)
- NO skipping edge cases for queries (reason: nil inputs, missing players, empty zones must be tested)

## Approach
Bottom-up by layer: start with query function tests (pure Datascript, simplest), then remove-object, then card integration tests, then regression tests. Each layer validates the foundation for the next.

Test pattern follows existing codebase convention: create Datascript DB directly, transact test state, call functions, assert results. No re-frame involvement. Card tests use cast-spell + resolve-spell cycle.

## Architecture
- src/test/fizzle/db/queries_test.cljs - NEW: comprehensive query function tests
- src/test/fizzle/engine/zones_test.cljs - EXTEND: add remove-object tests
- src/test/fizzle/cards/brain_freeze_test.cljs - NEW: storm win condition
- src/test/fizzle/cards/dark_ritual_test.cljs - NEW: BBB mana production
- src/test/fizzle/cards/cabal_ritual_test.cljs - NEW: threshold conditional effects
- src/test/fizzle/cards/city_of_brass_test.cljs - NEW: tap trigger damage
- src/test/fizzle/cards/gemstone_mine_test.cljs - NEW: counter depletion
- src/test/fizzle/cards/underground_river_test.cljs - NEW: pain land
- src/test/fizzle/cards/ill_gotten_gains_test.cljs - NEW: symmetric effect
- src/test/fizzle/events/regression_test.cljs - NEW or extend existing: regression tests

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Significant test coverage gaps exist in foundational layers. 19 query functions have 0 dedicated tests. 7 of 24 cards lack any test coverage, including Brain Freeze (the deck's win condition). Recent bug fixes went in without regression tests. This blocks the P1 stack unification feature (fizzle-d3i) which needs a solid test foundation.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- db/queries.cljs:1-269 - 19 public query functions, all pure (db, args) -> result
- engine/zones.cljs:78-90 - remove-object, retracts entity from datascript
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - 24 card defs, 17 have tests, 7 don't
- Test pattern: create-test-db helper, d/create-conn + transact, direct function calls
- subs/game.cljs - 15+ subscriptions, thin wrappers over queries (deferred)

**Test infrastructure:**
- cljs.test framework with deftest/testing/is macros
- Datascript for in-memory DB
- No re-frame test utilities currently
- Test helpers duplicated across card test files (create-test-db, add-card-to-zone, etc.)

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Bottom-up by layer ✓
**What it is:** Test queries first (foundation), then zones/remove-object, then cards, then regressions.
**Investigation:** Reviewed existing test patterns - all use pure Datascript. Query tests will be simplest (pure functions, no game state setup needed beyond basic player/objects).
**Pros:** Validates foundation first. Simplest tests first establishes patterns. Card tests further validate queries.
**Cons:** Defers highest-risk items (Brain Freeze, storm) to later tasks.
**Chosen because:** Queries are the foundation everything builds on. Testing them first catches data layer bugs before they confuse card/regression tests.

#### 2. Risk-priority ordering ❌
**What it is:** Start with Brain Freeze + storm regressions (highest impact), then backfill.
**Why we looked at this:** Brain Freeze is the win condition - untested win condition is highest risk.
**Investigation:** Storm tests require cast-resolve cycle + storm trigger dispatch - most complex test setup.
**Pros:** Covers highest risk first.
**Cons:** Jumps into complex integration tests. May discover query bugs during card tests.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Starting with complex tests means debugging both the test setup AND potential query bugs simultaneously.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Query tests reveal no issues and we want to reprioritize.

#### 3. Feature-area grouping ❌
**What it is:** Group by subsystem (mana, storm, lands) covering queries+cards+regressions per area.
**Why we looked at this:** Natural mental model, complete coverage per area.
**Investigation:** Queries like get-player-eid, get-object, get-game-state don't map to one area.
**Pros:** Complete per-area coverage.
**Cons:** Mixes complexity levels. General-purpose queries don't fit.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Poor fit for cross-cutting query functions. Harder to track progress.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We reorganize queries by domain.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- All 19 public query functions in db/queries.cljs
- remove-object in engine/zones.cljs
- 7 untested cards (integration tests)
- Regression tests for recent bug fix commits

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Subscription testing (deferred - thin wrappers over queries, low risk)
- Card data validation tests (card defs are static EDN, low value)
- Performance testing of queries (premature)
- Testing private functions like matches-criteria? directly (test through public API)

### Open Questions
- Should test helpers be extracted to a shared namespace? (decide during query test implementation)
- How to test ill-gotten-gains symmetric effects without opponent bot? (decide during card test task)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Include subscription testing? | Defer (recommended) | Focus on queries, cards, regressions only |
| Card test depth? | Integration tests only | Cast-resolve cycle, no card data validation |
| Regression test scope? | Cover all recent fixes | Include selection clearing, storm copies, sorcery speed |
| Approach? | Bottom-up by layer | Queries -> remove-object -> cards -> regressions |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Query Function Coverage
**Question explored:** What query functions exist and how are they tested?
**Sources consulted:**
- db/queries.cljs - 19 public functions
- db/schema_test.cljs - only tests schema, not queries
- engine/rules_test.cljs - uses queries as fixtures but doesn't test them directly

**Findings:**
- All query functions are pure (db, args) -> result
- Many return nil for missing entities - edge case coverage needed
- stack-empty? composes get-stack-items + get-objects-in-zone
- matches-criteria? is private, tested through query-zone-by-criteria

**Conclusion:** Dedicated queries_test.cljs with positive, negative, and nil-input cases

#### Card Test Patterns
**Question explored:** How do existing card tests work?
**Sources consulted:**
- cards/led_test.cljs - exemplar: create-test-db, add-card-to-zone, activate-mana-ability
- cards/careful_study_test.cljs - cast-resolve with selection effects
- engine/rules_test.cljs - simpler cast-resolve without selection

**Findings:**
- Test helpers duplicated across files (create-test-db in led_test, rules_test, etc.)
- Card tests use full cast-resolve cycle through rules/cast-spell + rules/resolve-spell
- Selection-based cards (Careful Study) test through events/selection dispatch
- Simple effect cards (Dark Ritual) can test purely through rules layer

**Conclusion:** Follow existing patterns. Consider helper extraction if duplication becomes excessive.

### Dead-End Paths
None - straightforward coverage gap filling.

### Open Concerns Raised
- 'Will stack unification rewrite invalidate regression tests?' → Regression tests validate current behavior. Stack unification should preserve behavior, so tests remain valid as contracts.
- 'Are card tests redundant with rules_test coverage?' → No - rules_test uses Dark Ritual as fixture but doesn't test its specific effects (BBB production). Dedicated tests verify card-specific behavior.
close_reason: Epic complete. All 4 child tasks closed. 85 new tests added across 7 new test files. All 7 untested cards now covered (Dark Ritual, Cabal Ritual, Brain Freeze, City of Brass, Gemstone Mine, Underground River, Ill-Gotten Gains). All 19 query functions tested. remove-object tested. Storm copy and sorcery-speed regressions covered. make validate: 826 tests, 1826 assertions, 0 failures.

title: Feature: Implement Opt with Scry Effect
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Scry effect type exists and works for any amount N
- Scry N reveals top N cards, player assigns each to top or bottom pile
- Click order determines arrangement order within each pile
- Opt card defined: 1 blue instant, Scry 1 then draw 1
- Scry handles edge cases: fewer cards than N, empty library

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] :scry effect type implemented in effects.cljs
- [ ] Selection system supports :selection/type :scry
- [ ] Scry UI shows all cards with top/bottom buttons
- [ ] Click order determines final arrangement
- [ ] Opt card casts and resolves correctly (scry 1, draw 1)
- [ ] Edge cases handled (library < N cards)
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing (make validate)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO auto-scry without player choice (defeats purpose of scry)
- ❌ NO graveyard interaction for scry (scry never touches graveyard)
- ❌ NO hardcoding scry 1 only (must work for scry N)
- ❌ NO blocking confirm until cards assigned (UX: confirm disabled until all assigned)
- ❌ NO skipping edge case tests (library size edge cases are common in storm)

## Approach
Add new :scry effect type following existing selection pattern (like :discard with :effect/selection :player). The effect pauses execution, creates :selection/type :scry state with revealed cards. UI renders scry modal showing all N cards with top/bottom buttons. Player clicks assign cards to piles - click order is arrangement order. On confirm, library is reordered and remaining effects execute. Opt card uses this effect followed by :draw.

## Architecture
- engine/effects.cljs - Add :scry effect type (returns db unchanged, like selection effects)
- events/game.cljs - Add :scry to selection detection, create scry selection state
- events/game.cljs - Add scry confirmation handler to reorder library
- views/ - Add scry modal component (cards + top/bottom buttons + confirm)
- cards/opt.cljs - Opt card definition
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Add opt to all-cards

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Opt is a common cantrip in Premodern storm decks. It requires scry which doesn't exist in the engine yet. Scry is also needed for future cards (Preordain, Serum Visions, etc).

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- effects.cljs:308-329 - Selection pattern for player choice effects
- events/game.cljs:750-770 - has-selection-effect?, get-first-selection-index
- events/game.cljs:977-983 - Selection state creation for discard
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Card definition patterns, all-cards vector

**External:**
- MTG Scry rules: Look at top N, put any number on bottom in any order, rest on top in any order

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. New :scry effect type with selection ✓
**What it is:** Add scry as first-class effect type using existing selection system pattern.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed discard selection in effects.cljs:308-329
- Reviewed selection state handling in game.cljs:977-983
- Pattern is proven and extensible

**Pros:**
- Matches existing codebase patterns
- Reusable for all scry cards
- Clean separation of effect/selection/UI

**Cons:**
- Requires changes to multiple files

**Chosen because:** Follows established patterns, future-proof for other scry cards

#### 2. Composite effect using existing primitives ❌
**What it is:** Hack scry using mill + return-from-graveyard

**Why we looked at this:** Avoid adding new effect type

**Investigation:**
- Mill moves to graveyard, triggers graveyard effects
- Return-from-graveyard would fire triggers incorrectly

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Incorrect game semantics - scry never touches graveyard

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Complete rewrite of zone system

#### 3. Simplified always-bottom scry ❌
**What it is:** Auto-bottom without player choice

**Why we looked at this:** Faster to implement

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Defeats strategic purpose of scry in storm deck practice

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** User explicitly requests simplified mode

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Scry effect for any N
- Scry UI with full arrangement control
- Opt card implementation

**Out of scope:**
- Other scry cards (Preordain, etc) - separate cards, same effect
- Scry triggers (no cards in Premodern trigger on scry)

### Open Questions
- None - design is complete

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| How to handle missing scry? | Implement full scry effect | New effect type needed |
| Scry 1 UI? | Reveal card, choose top/bottom | Simple two-button UI |
| Scry N UI? | Show all cards, top/bottom buttons each, click order = arrangement | Full control, order matters |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Selection System Pattern
**Question explored:** How does player choice work in effects?
**Sources consulted:**
- effects.cljs:308-329 - discard with :effect/selection :player
- game.cljs:750-770 - selection detection functions
- game.cljs:977-983 - selection state creation

**Findings:**
- Effects return db unchanged when selection needed
- Selection state tracks type, player, and parameters
- UI handles actual selection, confirms via event

**Conclusion:** Scry follows same pattern - effect triggers selection state, UI handles choice, confirmation event reorders library

### Dead-End Paths

#### Mill-based scry
**Why explored:** Reuse existing effects
**Investigation:** Mill puts cards in graveyard, would trigger graveyard effects incorrectly
**Why abandoned:** Wrong game semantics

### Open Concerns Raised
- Multi-card scry arrangement → Solved: click order determines pile order
close_reason: Closed

title: Add re-frame events and subscriptions for history navigation
design:
## Goal
Wire up history/core.cljs pure functions as re-frame events and expose history state via subscriptions. This is the data layer that views will subscribe to.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Create history/events.cljs (NOT events/history.cljs)

**CRITICAL**: File MUST be at `src/main/fizzle/history/events.cljs` with namespace `fizzle.history.events`.
The interceptor at `history/interceptor.cljs:10-21` skips events whose keyword namespace starts with `"fizzle.history"`.
If placed in `events/history.cljs` (namespace `fizzle.events.history`), navigation events would be RECORDED as history entries, corrupting the action log.

New re-frame events that wrap history/core.cljs operations:

- `::step-back` — Compute `(dec position)`, call `history/step-to` with that value. step-to already restores :game/db (see line 74).
- `::step-forward` — Compute `(inc position)`, call `history/step-to`. Same — step-to handles :game/db.
- `::switch-branch` — Takes fork-id arg, calls `history/switch-branch`. switch-branch already restores :game/db at tip (see line 126).

**Key insight**: Both `step-to` and `switch-branch` return a modified db that includes updated `:game/db`. Events just need to:
1. Extract history state from app-db (the 4 history keys are stored flat at top-level: `:history/main`, `:history/forks`, `:history/current-branch`, `:history/position`)
2. Call the pure function (which also reads/writes `:game/db` from the same map)
3. Merge result back to app-db

Since app-db IS the map that history functions operate on (history keys + :game/db are all top-level), event handlers can pass the full app-db directly to history functions.

**Pattern to follow**: See events/game.cljs line 1-22 for require/ns pattern. Use `rf/reg-event-db` for all three events.

**Guards**: step-back should check `(history/can-step-back? db)` before calling step-to. step-forward should check `(history/can-step-forward? db)`. If guard fails, return db unchanged.

### 2. Create subs/history.cljs

Subscriptions exposing history state to views.

**Pattern to follow**: See subs/game.cljs:10-11 for Layer 2 (raw extraction) and lines 16-20 for Layer 3 (derived with :\<-).

Layer 2 (raw access from app-db):
- `::entries` — `(fn [db _] (history/effective-entries db))` — calls history/core pure fn
- `::position` — `(fn [db _] (:history/position db))`
- `::current-branch` — `(fn [db _] (:history/current-branch db))`
- `::forks` — `(fn [db _] (history/list-forks db))`

Layer 3 (derived):
- `::can-step-back?` — `:<- [::position]` — `(fn [pos _] (and pos (> pos 0)))`
- `::can-step-forward?` — `:<- [::entries] :<- [::position]` — `(fn [[entries pos] _] (and pos entries (< pos (dec (count entries)))))`
- `::at-tip?` — `:<- [::entries] :<- [::position]` — `(fn [[entries pos] _] (or (and (= pos -1) (empty? entries)) (= pos (dec (count entries)))))`
- `::entry-count` — `:<- [::entries]` — `(fn [entries _] (count (or entries [])))`

**Edge case**: Before game init, app-db has no history keys. Subscriptions must handle nil gracefully — return nil, false, 0, or empty as appropriate for each sub.

### 3. Register in core.cljs

Add bare require `[fizzle.history.events]` in core.cljs ns form (follow pattern of existing bare require `[fizzle.events.abilities]` at line 19). Also add `[fizzle.subs.history]` require.

### 4. Write tests

File: `src/test/fizzle/history/events_test.cljs`

**Event tests** (use day9/re-frame-test or manual app-db manipulation):

Test setup helper: Create an app-db with history containing N entries at various positions. Use `history/make-entry` and `history/append-entry` to build state (see interceptor_test.cljs:91-97 for pattern).

Specific tests:
1. `test-step-back-decrements-position` — From position 2 with 3 entries, step-back goes to position 1 and :game/db equals entry-1 snapshot. Catches: position not updated.
2. `test-step-back-restores-snapshot` — From position 2, step-back restores :game/db to the snapshot stored in entry at position 1. Catches: snapshot not applied.
3. `test-step-back-at-zero-is-noop` — From position 0, step-back returns db unchanged (position still 0, :game/db unchanged). Catches: boundary underflow.
4. `test-step-forward-increments-position` — From position 1 with 3 entries, step-forward goes to position 2 and :game/db equals entry-2 snapshot. Catches: position not updated.
5. `test-step-forward-at-tip-is-noop` — From position 2 (tip of 3 entries), step-forward returns db unchanged. Catches: boundary overflow.
6. `test-switch-branch-changes-branch-and-restores` — Create a fork, switch to it, verify :history/current-branch is fork-id and :game/db is tip snapshot of fork. Catches: branch not switched or wrong snapshot.
7. `test-switch-branch-to-main` — From a fork, switch to nil (main), verify :history/current-branch is nil and :game/db is main tip snapshot. Catches: can't return to main.
8. `test-switch-branch-invalid-fork-id-is-noop` — Switch to non-existent fork-id, verify db unchanged. Catches: crash on bad input.

File: `src/test/fizzle/subs/history_test.cljs`

**Subscription tests**:
9. `test-entries-returns-effective-entries` — Verify ::entries returns entries for current branch. Catches: wrong entries returned.
10. `test-position-returns-current-position` — Verify ::position extracts position. Catches: wrong key read.
11. `test-can-step-back-false-at-zero` — Position 0, verify false. Catches: boundary bug.
12. `test-can-step-back-true-at-nonzero` — Position 2, verify true. Catches: always-false bug.
13. `test-can-step-forward-false-at-tip` — Position at tip, verify false. Catches: boundary bug.
14. `test-can-step-forward-true-when-not-at-tip` — Position 1 of 3, verify true. Catches: always-false bug.
15. `test-forks-returns-all-forks` — Create 2 forks, verify ::forks returns both. Catches: missing forks.
16. `test-subscriptions-handle-nil-history` — Empty app-db (no history keys), verify subscriptions return nil/false/0 without throwing. Catches: NPE before game init.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] history/events.cljs exists at src/main/fizzle/history/events.cljs (namespace fizzle.history.events)
- [ ] Events: step-back, step-forward, switch-branch registered as rf/reg-event-db
- [ ] Events use guard checks (can-step-back?, can-step-forward?) before calling history functions
- [ ] subs/history.cljs has entries, position, current-branch, forks, can-step-back?, can-step-forward?, at-tip?, entry-count subscriptions
- [ ] Subscriptions handle nil/missing history state gracefully (no throws before game init)
- [ ] Events correctly restore :game/db snapshot when navigating (via step-to/switch-branch return value)
- [ ] Navigation events are NOT recorded by the interceptor (verify keyword namespace starts with fizzle.history)
- [ ] 16 tests covering all events and subscriptions including boundary cases
- [ ] Namespaces registered in core.cljs (bare requires)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO placing events in fizzle.events.history namespace (MUST be fizzle.history.events — interceptor skip depends on this)
- NO manual :game/db restoration in events (step-to and switch-branch already handle this)
- NO history logic in events (delegate entirely to history/core.cljs pure functions)
- NO ignoring nil history state in subscriptions (must handle pre-game-init gracefully)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**CRITICAL: Namespace placement**
The interceptor at history/interceptor.cljs:10-21 uses `str/starts-with? "fizzle.history"` to detect history events. Events MUST be in namespace `fizzle.history.events` so keywords like `:fizzle.history.events/step-back` are correctly skipped. Wrong namespace = navigation pollutes history = broken feature.

**Edge Case: Empty history (position -1)**
Before any game actions, position is -1 and entries is []. Both step-back and step-forward should be no-ops. Guard with can-step-back?/can-step-forward? which handle this (can-step-back? checks > 0, can-step-forward? checks < dec(count)).

**Edge Case: Pre-game-init subscriptions**
Before ::init-game fires, app-db has no history keys. Subscriptions reading :history/position get nil. Derived subs must handle nil gracefully — e.g., `(and pos (> pos 0))` not just `(> pos 0)`.

**Design: Events pass full app-db to history functions**
History functions expect a map with :history/* keys and :game/db. Since app-db IS that map, events can pass db directly to history/step-to etc. No extraction/re-assembly needed.

## Reference Implementations
- Event registration pattern: events/game.cljs:1-22
- Subscription pattern: subs/game.cljs:10-20 (Layer 2 raw, Layer 3 derived)
- History test setup: history/interceptor_test.cljs:28-31 (make-db-with-history), lines 91-97 (building multi-entry state)
- Guard functions: history/core.cljs:78-85 (can-step-back?, can-step-forward?)
close_reason: All 3 files created (history/events.cljs, subs/history.cljs, core.cljs updated). 16 tests pass. make validate clean.

title: Cleanup: extensible trigger map, flatten activate-ability, docstring resolve-all
description:
## Goal
Three independent low-complexity cleanup items from the architecture hardening epic:

### A. Make trigger-type->event-type extensible (trigger_db.cljs:8-12)
Replace the closed `def` map with a multimethod or open registry pattern so adding new trigger types doesn't require modifying a hardcoded map. Currently only 2 types: `:becomes-tapped -> :permanent-tapped`, `:land-entered -> :land-entered`.

### B. Flatten activate-ability nesting (events/abilities.cljs:224-290)
Currently 6+ levels of nested if/let. Epic requires max 3 nesting levels. Use early returns / guard clauses / extracted helpers to flatten.

### C. Add invariant docstring to resolve-all-handler (events/game.cljs:526-558)
Document WHY this handler bypasses the standard history interceptor. It manually creates and applies history entries instead of using the interceptor pattern (compare to ::resolve-top which uses the interceptor).

## Implementation

### A. Trigger extensibility
- Replace `(def trigger-type->event-type {...})` with a multimethod `(defmulti trigger-type->event-type identity)`
- Add defmethods for :becomes-tapped and :land-entered
- Update caller in create-triggers-for-card-tx to call multimethod (currently uses `(get trigger-type->event-type trigger-type trigger-type)`)
- Default method returns the trigger-type itself (preserving current fallback behavior)

### B. Flatten activate-ability
- Extract guard clause: return early if obj/player-eid invalid
- Extract guard clause: return early if ability not found or not activatable
- Extract helper for cost payment (mana + non-mana costs)
- Extract helper for building ability pending-selection
- Result: top-level function max 3 nesting levels

### C. resolve-all-handler docstring
- Add explicit invariant comment explaining:
  - WHY it bypasses the history interceptor (batches multiple resolutions into single operation)
  - WHAT invariant it maintains (creates individual history entries per resolved item, handles fork logic)
  - WHEN this could break (if interceptor adds new behavior that resolve-all doesn't replicate)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] trigger-type->event-type is a multimethod (or open registry) not a closed def map
- [ ] activate-ability has max 3 levels of conditional nesting
- [ ] resolve-all-handler has invariant docstring explaining history bypass
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes
design:
## Approach
Three independent changes in separate files — no conflict risk.

For trigger extensibility: multimethod is the most natural fit since the project already uses multimethods for effects, costs, and conditions.

For activate-ability: extract helper functions to flatten the deep nesting. ClojureScript has no early returns; instead extract the cost-payment-and-stack logic into a helper, and use cond to flatten branches.

For resolve-all docstring: reference the contrast with ::resolve-top to make the invariant clear.

## Implementation Details

### A. Trigger extensibility (trigger_db.cljs)
Replace the def map with a multimethod:

```clojure
(defmulti trigger-type->event-type
  "Map card trigger type to event type. Extensible via defmethod.
   Default returns the trigger-type unchanged."
  identity)

(defmethod trigger-type->event-type :becomes-tapped [_] :permanent-tapped)
(defmethod trigger-type->event-type :land-entered [_] :land-entered)
(defmethod trigger-type->event-type :default [type] type)
```

Update caller at line 87 from:
  `(get trigger-type->event-type trigger-type trigger-type)`
to:
  `(trigger-type->event-type trigger-type)`

Only ONE caller exists (confirmed via grep).

### B. Flatten activate-ability (events/abilities.cljs:200-290)
Current nesting: 6+ levels of if/let inside activate-ability.
Target: max 3 levels of conditional nesting in any function.

Strategy: Extract two helpers:
1. `pay-costs-and-create-stack-item` — handles the 3 cost-payment paths (targeting, generic mana, direct pay) and creates the stack-item. This is lines 246-288 currently.
2. Keep `build-ability-target-selection` as-is (already extracted).

Flattened structure of `activate-ability`:
```
(let [obj player-eid]                           ; level 1
  (when (valid-activation? ...)                  ; level 2 (guard)
    (let [ability ...]
      (when (activatable? ...)                   ; level 2 (guard)
        (cond                                    ; level 2 (flat branch)
          (has-targeting?) → build-target-selection
          (has-generic-mana?) → pay-and-allocate-mana helper
          :else → pay-and-create-stack-item helper)))))
```

Return `{:db db :pending-selection nil}` from guards (existing pattern).

### C. resolve-all-handler docstring (events/game.cljs:526-558)
Add to existing docstring:

```
INVARIANT: This handler bypasses the standard event-interceptor (used by
::resolve-top and all other game events) because it batches multiple
stack-item resolutions into a single operation. It replicates the
interceptor's history-recording behavior manually:
- Creates individual history entries per resolved item (make-resolve-entry)
- Handles fork creation when not at history tip (apply-history-entries)
- Dispatches pending-selection when interactive effect encountered

If the event-interceptor gains new behavior (e.g., new side effects,
validation, or logging), resolve-all-handler must be updated to match.
Compare with ::resolve-top (line ~561) which uses the interceptor.
```

## Edge Cases (SRE Review)

**Trigger multimethod:**
- What if trigger-type is nil? Default method returns nil (same as current `(get map nil nil)` behavior). No change in behavior.
- What if someone adds a defmethod that conflicts with an existing one? ClojureScript warns on multimethod override. Acceptable.

**activate-ability flattening:**
- Guard clauses must return `{:db db :pending-selection nil}` not bare `db` (existing contract).
- All 3 cost-payment paths must be preserved exactly: targeting, generic-mana, and direct-pay.
- The `sel-costs/has-generic-mana-cost?` + `sel-costs/build-mana-allocation-selection` + nil-check fallback path must be kept.

**resolve-all docstring:**
- No code changes, just documentation. No behavioral risk.

## Test Verification

Verify these test files pass after changes:
- engine/abilities_test.cljs
- cards/polluted_delta_test.cljs (fetchland — activate-ability)
- cards/cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs (activated ability with targeting)
- cards/seal_of_cleansing_test.cljs (activated ability)
- events/integration/mana_allocation_test.cljs
- history/interceptor_test.cljs
- history/descriptions_test.cljs
- engine/stack_test.cljs
- All other tests via `make validate`

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO behavior changes — these are purely structural refactors (trigger) and documentation (docstring)
- ❌ NO new atoms or mutable state in engine/
- ❌ NO changing the return type contract of activate-ability ({:db :pending-selection})
- ❌ NO removing any cost-payment path from activate-ability (all 3 paths must remain)
- ❌ NO adding card-specific logic in the flattened helpers
close_reason: All 3 items complete: trigger-type->event-type converted to multimethod, activate-ability flattened from 6+ to max 3 nesting levels, resolve-all-handler invariant docstring added. 1446 tests pass, make validate clean.

title: Layout Rearrange: Stack Inline, Phase Bar Down, GY Left
description: Rearrange the game layout to reduce visual scanning during combo turns. Three changes: (1) Stack moves from right sidebar to inline horizontal row between battlefields, (2) Phase bar moves from between battlefields to above hand, (3) Graveyard moves from right sidebar to collapsible left sidebar.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Requirement 1: Stack renders as a horizontal row between opponent battlefield and player battlefield, items flowing left-to-right (oldest to newest)
- Requirement 2: Stack row has fixed height (single row of items) with horizontal scroll on overflow — height is constant whether stack is empty or full
- Requirement 3: Phase bar with life totals renders between player battlefield and hand (below battlefield, above hand)
- Requirement 4: Graveyard renders as a collapsible left sidebar (mirroring existing right sidebar behavior)
- Requirement 5: History remains as collapsible right sidebar (unchanged behavior)
- Requirement 6: Stack panel is removed from right sidebar columns
- Requirement 7: Graveyard panel is removed from right sidebar columns

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Stack displays inline between battlefields as horizontal row
- [ ] Stack items flow left-to-right (oldest first)
- [ ] Stack row maintains constant height when empty vs populated
- [ ] Horizontal scroll appears when stack items overflow one row
- [ ] Phase bar renders above hand, below player battlefield
- [ ] Phase bar still shows life totals, turn number, phase indicators, and stop dots
- [ ] Graveyard is a collapsible left sidebar
- [ ] History remains as collapsible right sidebar
- [ ] CSS grid updated for 3-column layout (left sidebar, center content, right sidebar)
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO changing the stack item rendering content or data — only layout/positioning changes (scope: this is a layout epic, not a stack redesign)
- ❌ NO changing phase bar functionality — only its position in the DOM (scope: phase stops, life totals, turn display must work identically)
- ❌ NO changing graveyard content or behavior — only its sidebar position (scope: graveyard selection, flashback highlighting must work identically)
- ❌ NO variable-height stack row — must be fixed height with horizontal scroll (UX: consistent layout prevents muscle memory disruption)
- ❌ NO removing the empty state indicator for the stack — show 'Empty' text when stack has no items (UX: user must always know where the stack zone is)
- ❌ NO changing permanent-view, hand-view, controls-view, or mana-pool-view components (scope: only layout structure changes)

## Approach
Modify 4 files to restructure the game layout:

1. **core.cljs** — Restructure game-screen grid: change from 4-column grid (content + 3 right panels) to 3-column grid (left sidebar + center content + right sidebar). Move graveyard to left sidebar. Remove stack from right sidebar. In center column, render: battlefield-view, stack-view (inline), phase-bar-section, hand-view, controls, mana/storm, zones.

2. **app.css** — Update .game-grid from 'grid-template-columns: 1fr auto auto auto' to 'auto 1fr auto'. Add left-col-collapsed/left-col-expanded classes mirroring right-col classes. Add stack-row class for fixed-height horizontal scrolling container.

3. **battlefield.cljs** — Extract phase-bar-section from battlefield-view. Make phase-bar-section public (remove defn- private marker). battlefield-view renders only the 6 permanent rows (opp lands/other/creatures + player creatures/other/lands) without phase bar.

4. **stack.cljs** — Change stack-view from vertical list to horizontal flex row. Add fixed height and overflow-x-auto for horizontal scrolling. Stack items render side-by-side instead of stacked.

## Architecture
- core.cljs owns the layout grid structure
- battlefield.cljs exports phase-bar-section as public fn
- stack.cljs changes from vertical to horizontal rendering
- app.css defines grid and sidebar collapse classes
- No event/subscription/engine changes — purely view layer

## Design Rationale
### Problem
During combo turns, the player's hot loop is Hand → Stack → Controls. Currently the stack is in a right sidebar, forcing a horizontal eye jump on every cast. With 15+ spells in a storm turn, this creates significant visual scanning overhead.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- core.cljs:52-73 — game-screen uses CSS grid with 4 columns (1fr auto auto auto)
- core.cljs:30-41 — collapsible-right-column helper used for all 3 right panels
- battlefield.cljs:205-222 — battlefield-view embeds phase-bar-section between opponent and player rows
- stack.cljs:22-32 — stack-view renders vertical list in right panel
- app.css:110-115 — .game-grid defines the grid structure
- app.css:117-126 — .right-col-collapsed/expanded CSS classes

**External:**
- No external dependencies — pure CSS grid + Tailwind changes

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Restructure grid to 3-column with inline stack ✓

**What it is:** Change the CSS grid from 4 columns to 3 columns (left sidebar + center + right sidebar). Move stack inline between battlefields, move phase bar down, move graveyard to left sidebar.

**Investigation:**
- Verified collapsible-right-column helper can be adapted for left sidebar with border-r instead of border-l
- Confirmed phase-bar-section has no dependencies on being inside battlefield-view
- Stack items are simple text-based — work horizontally without redesign

**Pros:**
- Minimal component changes (4 files)
- No event/subscription/engine changes
- Reuses existing collapsible sidebar pattern

**Cons:**
- Stack horizontal scroll on storm turns may feel unfamiliar initially

**Chosen because:** Minimal changes, addresses the core visual scanning problem, reuses existing patterns

#### 2. Two-panel layout (no sidebars) ❌

**What it is:** Eliminate sidebars entirely, put graveyard and history inline below zone counts.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler grid (single column)

**Investigation:**
- Graveyard can be long (20+ cards) — would push content below fold
- History panel is frequently consulted — needs persistent visibility

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Graveyard and history are reference panels that need to be visible alongside the main game area, not below it.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Screen real estate becomes so constrained that sidebars don't fit at all.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Stack position (right sidebar → inline between battlefields)
- Phase bar position (between battlefields → above hand)
- Graveyard position (right sidebar → left sidebar)
- CSS grid restructure

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Stack item visual redesign — deferred to future polish
- Hand position changes — not requested
- Controls position changes — not requested
- Mana pool / storm count position — not requested
- Keyboard shortcuts — separate issue (fizzle-txiw)

### Open Questions
- Exact pixel height for fixed stack row — determine during implementation by measuring current stack item height + padding

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

> Detailed context from brainstorming for task creation and obstacle handling.

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| What's the pain point? | Visual scanning — eyes jump too much | Core problem is eye travel distance during combo turns |
| Eye path during combo? | Hand → Stack → Controls | These three elements should be vertically close |
| Opponent battlefield importance? | Depends on scenario | Opp battlefield should exist but not dominate — varies by matchup |
| Stack between battlefields or hand/controls? | Between battlefields (as described in issue) | Stack goes at thematic position (shared zone), not optimized for hot loop proximity |
| Phase bar should be above hand, not controls | User corrected — above hand, not controls | Phase bar sits between battlefield and hand |
| Graveyard position? | Collapsible left sidebar | Mirror of right sidebar pattern |
| Empty stack behavior? | Always take same space | Fixed-height row for layout consistency / muscle memory |
| Stack overflow behavior? | Single row, scroll if overflow | Horizontal scroll, no wrapping — keeps layout height locked |
| Scope? | Open to suggestions beyond the two changes | Added graveyard move to left sidebar |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Layout Grid Structure
**Question explored:** How is the current game layout structured?
**Sources consulted:**
- core.cljs:52-73 — game-screen component
- app.css:110-115 — .game-grid CSS

**Findings:**
- 4-column CSS grid: 1fr + 3 auto columns for right panels
- Left column contains all game elements vertically
- Right panels are collapsible with shared helper function
- Grid rows: 1fr + auto (bottom reserved for calculator)

**Conclusion:** Grid needs to change to 3 columns (auto + 1fr + auto) for left/center/right

#### Phase Bar Extraction
**Question explored:** Can phase-bar-section be extracted from battlefield-view?
**Sources consulted:**
- battlefield.cljs:169-222 — phase-bar-section and battlefield-view

**Findings:**
- phase-bar-section is a self-contained component (defn- private)
- Uses its own subscriptions — no dependency on battlefield-view's let bindings
- Simply needs to be made public (defn instead of defn-)

**Conclusion:** Clean extraction — make public, render from core.cljs instead

### Dead-End Paths
None — straightforward layout restructure with no dead ends.

### Open Concerns Raised
- Stack horizontal scroll during storm turns (15+ items) may feel unfamiliar → Accept: user will adapt, locked height is the priority
close_reason: All layout changes complete: stack inline, phase bar above hand, graveyard left sidebar, viewport-constrained scrolling

title: Add defensive assert to cast-and-resolve: no pre-cast costs
description: cast-and-resolve calls rules/cast-spell directly, bypassing the pre-cast pipeline. Add a defensive assert that the spell has no pre-cast cost requirements. If it does, throw directing to cast-with-target or manual pipeline.
design:
## Goal
Add a defensive assertion to cast-and-resolve that verifies the spell has no pre-cast costs that would require the pipeline. This prevents silent bypass of costs when someone uses cast-and-resolve for a spell that should go through cast-with-target.

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing code:
   - test_helpers.cljs:239-250 — current cast-and-resolve
   - events/casting.cljs:40-44 — pre-cast-pipeline steps
   - events/casting.cljs:47-127 — evaluate-pre-cast-step defmethods (each returns nil when step doesn't apply)

2. Implementation in test_helpers.cljs:
   - After the can-cast? assert, before calling rules/cast-spell:
   - Get the casting mode: (first (rules/get-casting-modes db player-id obj-id))
   - Build context: {:game-db db :player-id player-id :object-id obj-id :mode mode}
   - Check if any pre-cast step applies by evaluating the pipeline steps that come BEFORE mana-allocation
   - Steps to check: :exile-cards-cost :return-land-cost :discard-specific-cost :sacrifice-permanent-cost :pay-x-life :x-mana-cost :targeting
   - If any returns non-nil, throw ex-info directing to cast-with-target
   - NOTE: :mana-allocation is NOT checked — simple spells with generic mana can use cast-and-resolve (the helper bypasses mana payment by design for test convenience)

3. Write test:
   - Test: cast-and-resolve throws for a targeted spell (e.g., Lightning Bolt)
   - Test: cast-and-resolve does NOT throw for a simple spell (e.g., Dark Ritual)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] cast-and-resolve asserts no pre-cast costs apply
- [ ] Throws ex-info with diagnostic info if spell has targeting/costs
- [ ] Simple spells (Dark Ritual, Cabal Ritual) still work
- [ ] Targeted spells (Lightning Bolt) are caught
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO checking mana-allocation — simple spells with generic mana should still work via cast-and-resolve
- ❌ NO adding a bypass parameter
close_reason: Defensive assert added to cast-and-resolve. Commit ca93c1c. 0 tests broken.

title: Extend cost modifier engine for :decrease and :applies-to
description: Engine foundation for cost reduction. Must be done before card definitions.
design:
## Goal
Extend static_abilities.cljs and card_spec.cljs to support cost DECREASE modifiers and ownership filtering (:applies-to). This is the foundation all card definitions depend on.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. card_spec.cljs changes (src/main/fizzle/engine/card_spec.cljs)
- Line 229-230: Add :decrease to valid-modifier-directions: \#{:increase :decrease}
- After line 230: Add valid-applies-to set: \#{:controller :all}
- After line 237: Add spec: (s/def :modifier/applies-to valid-applies-to)
- Line 240-242: Add :modifier/applies-to to ::static-ability :opt keys vector

### 2. static_abilities.cljs — modifier-applies? (src/main/fizzle/engine/static_abilities.cljs)
- Lines 42-76: Add :applies-to check BEFORE existing criteria/condition checks
- Logic: (let [applies-to (get-in modifier-entry [:static-ability :modifier/applies-to] :all)] ...)
- When :controller, check (= caster-id (:source/controller modifier-entry))
- When :all (or absent — default :all via get with default), always passes
- Existing criteria and condition checks remain unchanged

### 3. static_abilities.cljs — apply-cost-modifiers (lines 79-102)
Replace current implementation with increases-then-decreases:
- Partition applicable modifiers by :modifier/direction
- Sum total-increase from :increase modifiers
- Sum total-decrease from :decrease modifiers
- If (pos? total-increase): (update base-cost :colorless (fnil + 0) total-increase)
- If (pos? total-decrease): (update cost :colorless (fn [c] (max 0 (- (or c 0) total-decrease))))
- Update the docstring to reflect both increase and decrease

### 4. Tests (TDD — write failing tests first)
Add tests to the existing sphere_of_resistance_test.cljs file (which already has engine-level static_abilities tests):

**Decrease mechanics (new section):**
- test: decrease reduces generic mana on spell with :colorless in base cost (e.g., base {:colorless 2 :blue 1} with amount-1 decrease → {:colorless 1 :blue 1})
- test: decrease on pure-colored-cost spell is a no-op (e.g., base {:blue 2} with decrease → {:blue 2 :colorless 0} — colorless floors at 0, cost functionally unchanged)
- test: decrease floors at 0, does not go negative (e.g., base {:colorless 1} with amount-2 decrease → {:colorless 0})
- test: multiple decreases stack (two amount-1 decreases on {:colorless 3} → {:colorless 1})

**Increase-then-decrease ordering:**
- test: Sphere increase + decrease cancel out (amount-1 increase + amount-1 decrease on {:blue 2} → {:blue 2 :colorless 0} — increase adds 1, decrease removes 1)
- test: increase applied before decrease matters for floor (amount-1 increase + amount-2 decrease on {:blue 2} → {:blue 2 :colorless 0} — increase adds 1 to get colorless=1, decrease would take 2 but floors at 0)

**:applies-to ownership filtering:**
- test: :applies-to :controller only applies when caster == source controller (need th/add-opponent, medallion-like modifier on player-1 battlefield, player-2 casting → does NOT apply)
- test: :applies-to :controller applies when caster == source controller (player-1 modifier, player-1 casting → applies)
- test: :applies-to :all applies to both players (like current Sphere behavior)
- test: :applies-to absent defaults to :all — existing Sphere card (which has no :applies-to field) continues to work identically (backward compat regression test)

**Mixed criteria + applies-to:**
- test: :applies-to :controller + :criteria/spell-color both must match (medallion-like: controller's blue spells only. Test with controller's red spell → no reduction. Test with controller's blue spell → reduction.)

**Spec validation:**
- test: card with :modifier/direction :decrease passes validate-cards!
- test: card with :modifier/applies-to :controller passes validate-cards!
- test: existing cards (Sphere, Defense Grid, Chill) without :modifier/applies-to still pass validate-cards!

## Files to modify
- src/main/fizzle/engine/card_spec.cljs — lines 229-242
- src/main/fizzle/engine/static_abilities.cljs — lines 42-102
- src/test/fizzle/cards/artifacts/sphere_of_resistance_test.cljs — add new test sections

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: :colorless key absence**
Base costs may or may not have a :colorless key (e.g., Counterspell is {:blue 2}, no :colorless). The decrease path must handle this with (or c 0) or fnil. After decrease, a {:colorless 0} entry may appear in the cost map — this is harmless (mana/can-pay? and mana/pay-mana handle it correctly).

**Edge Case: Pure colored cost with only decrease**
Counterspell {:blue 2} with Medallion but no Sphere: increase=0, decrease=1. Since no increase adds :colorless, the decrease path would see colorless=0 and try to subtract 1, flooring at 0. Result: {:blue 2 :colorless 0}. Functionally correct — Medallion on pure-colored-cost spell has no effect (as per MTG rules).

**Edge Case: Backward compatibility for :applies-to**
Existing cards (Sphere, Defense Grid, Chill) do NOT have :modifier/applies-to. The spec must keep this field optional. Runtime code must default absent :applies-to to :all. This is critical — all existing tests must pass unchanged.

**Edge Case: Multiple modifiers from different sources**
Player-1 has Medallion (:controller, blue), Player-2 has Sphere (:all). Player-1 casts blue spell: both apply (increase 1 + decrease 1 = net 0). Player-2 casts blue spell: only Sphere applies (increase 1, Medallion skipped because caster != controller).

**Reference implementation pattern:**
Study existing sphere_of_resistance_test.cljs lines 127-178 for apply-cost-modifiers test patterns. Use th/add-opponent (line 339) for two-player tests.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] card_spec valid-modifier-directions includes :decrease
- [ ] card_spec has valid-applies-to set with :controller and :all
- [ ] card_spec ::static-ability :opt includes :modifier/applies-to
- [ ] modifier-applies? returns false when :applies-to :controller and caster != source controller
- [ ] modifier-applies? returns true when :applies-to absent (defaults to :all)
- [ ] apply-cost-modifiers sums increases first, then decreases
- [ ] apply-cost-modifiers floors :colorless at 0 (never negative)
- [ ] Existing Sphere/Defense Grid/Chill cards pass validate-cards! (no regression)
- [ ] All existing sphere_of_resistance_test tests pass unchanged
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO modifying mana.cljs or costs.cljs (changes confined to static_abilities and card_spec)
- ❌ NO breaking existing card definitions (spec changes must be additive/optional)
- ❌ NO hardcoding card names in engine logic (data-driven only)
- ❌ NO removing :colorless 0 entries from cost maps (harmless, adds complexity for no benefit)
close_reason: Closed

title: Eliminate remaining history imports from events/game.cljs
description:
ADR-004 drift (partial): resolve-all recursive dispatch is done, but events/game.cljs still imports history/core for two remaining uses:

1. **init-history** (line 185): Called from init-game-state. ADR-004 says move to core.cljs as wiring concern.

2. **start-turn manual history entry** (lines 669-672): Creates history/make-entry manually instead of going through the interceptor. The apply-history-entries helper (lines 496-507) duplicates interceptor fork/append logic and only serves this one call site.

**Goal:** Zero history/ imports in events/game.cljs. After this:
- init-history moves to core.cljs (bootstrap wiring)
- start-turn either becomes a multi-dispatch (like resolve-all) so each transition gets its own interceptor entry, or the interceptor gains awareness of start-turn's compound transition
- apply-history-entries helper is deleted

**References:** ADR-004, events/game.cljs lines 31, 185, 496-507, 669-672
close_reason: Closed

title: Gemstone Mine counter tracking and sacrifice
design:
## Goal
Gemstone Mine enters with mining counters and sacrifices when depleted.

## Context

2. GREEN: Add counter initialization on ETB
3. RED: Write test that tapping removes a counter and adds mana
4. GREEN: Integrate counter removal with tap ability
5. RED: Write test that Gemstone Mine sacrifices at 0 counters
6. GREEN: Implement sacrifice trigger when counters depleted
7. VERIFY: All tests pass

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Each tap removes 1 counter and adds mana
