title: Fix reviewer gaps: migrate all storm count tests + resolve-spell remnants
description: Reviewer found systemic gap: ~38 files use rules/cast-spell in storm count tests. Epic requires zero direct API calls. Also fix ray_of_revelation resolve-spell, brain_freeze resolve-spell, testing-strategy.md template, and verify gold-standard references are clean.
design:
## Goal
Fix all gaps identified by the reviewer to achieve full epic compliance.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Gap List

### 1. Storm count tests (~38 files)
All non-interactive spell storm count tests use rules/cast-spell directly. Migrate to th/cast-and-resolve.

Pattern:
```clojure
;; BEFORE (legacy)
(let [db-cast (rules/cast-spell db :player-1 obj-id)]
  (is (= 1 (q/get-storm-count db-cast))))

;; AFTER (production path)
(let [db-resolved (th/cast-and-resolve db :player-1 obj-id)]
  (is (= 1 (q/get-storm-count db-resolved))))
```

EXCEPTION: Interactive spells that pause for selection (IGG, hunting_pack storm copies, etc.) CANNOT use th/cast-and-resolve — they need rules/cast-spell for the cast step only. For these, keep rules/cast-spell but document with a comment why.

### 2. ray_of_revelation_test.cljs:250
Replace rules/resolve-spell + manual d/db-with targeting with th/cast-mode-with-target or th/cast-with-target (production targeting path).

### 3. brain_freeze_test.cljs:308
Replace rules/resolve-spell with resolution/resolve-one-item for copy lifecycle test.

### 4. testing-strategy.md template (line ~324)
Replace rules/cast-spell in Category D template with th/cast-and-resolve.

### 5. Verify gold-standard references
After fixes, confirm cabal_ritual_test.cljs and all other gold-standard files have zero rules/cast-spell.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] grep -r 'rules/cast-spell' src/test/fizzle/cards/ returns ONLY documented interactive-spell exceptions (each with comment)
- [ ] grep -r 'rules/resolve-spell' src/test/fizzle/cards/ returns zero results
- [ ] testing-strategy.md Category D template uses th/cast-and-resolve
- [ ] All CLAUDE.md gold-standard references have zero rules/cast-spell (except documented exceptions)
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] Test count >= 3339

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO removing passing tests
- NO changing card definitions
- NO migrating interactive spell storm tests (those legitimately need rules/cast-spell)
- NO skipping make validate
close_reason: Reviewer gaps fixed. Commit 84fd845. Zero rules/resolve-spell. All rules/cast-spell documented. 3339 tests.

title: Create engine/abilities.cljs with generic mana ability activation
design:
## Goal
Implement engine/abilities.cljs with generic ability activation that reads card data and delegates to costs.cljs for payment.

## Context
Completed fizzle-31d: engine/costs.cljs now handles :tap and :remove-counter costs via multimethod dispatch. Now need the abilities layer that reads :card/abilities from card data and uses costs.cljs to pay costs.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Implementation Steps

### 1. RED: Write tests for abilities.cljs
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/abilities_test.cljs (new file)

Follow pattern from costs_test.cljs - use add-permanent helper.

**can-activate? tests (6 tests):**
```clojure
(deftest test-can-activate-tap-ability
  (testing "can-activate? returns true for untapped permanent with :tap cost"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent on battlefield
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:tap true}}
    ;; Assert: (abilities/can-activate? db object-id ability) => true
    ;; Catches: Missing delegation to costs/can-pay?
    ))

(deftest test-can-activate-already-tapped
  (testing "can-activate? returns false for tapped permanent with :tap cost"
    ;; Setup: tapped permanent on battlefield
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:tap true}}
    ;; Assert: (abilities/can-activate? db object-id ability) => false
    ;; Catches: Not checking cost payability
    ))

(deftest test-can-activate-counter-ability
  (testing "can-activate? returns true when permanent has sufficient counters"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 3}
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}}
    ;; Assert: true
    ;; Catches: Not delegating counter check to costs.cljs
    ))

(deftest test-can-activate-counter-ability-insufficient
  (testing "can-activate? returns false when insufficient counters"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 0}
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}}
    ;; Assert: false
    ;; Catches: Not checking counter count
    ))

(deftest test-can-activate-nil-cost
  (testing "can-activate? returns true for ability with nil cost (free activation)"
    ;; Setup: any permanent
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost nil}  ;; or ability without :ability/cost key
    ;; Assert: true
    ;; Catches: NPE on nil cost map, treating nil as unpayable
    ))

(deftest test-can-activate-invalid-object-id
  (testing "can-activate? returns false for non-existent permanent"
    ;; Setup: db with no matching object
    ;; Action: (abilities/can-activate? db (random-uuid) ability)
    ;; Assert: false (not crash)
    ;; Catches: NPE on object lookup
    ))
```

**pay-all-costs tests (4 tests):**
```clojure
(deftest test-pay-all-costs-single
  (testing "pay-all-costs with single :tap cost taps permanent"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent
    ;; Action: (abilities/pay-all-costs db object-id {:tap true})
    ;; Assert: returns db with permanent tapped
    ;; Catches: Not calling costs/pay-cost
    ))

(deftest test-pay-all-costs-multiple
  (testing "pay-all-costs with :tap and :remove-counter pays both"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent with {:mining 3}
    ;; Action: (abilities/pay-all-costs db object-id {:tap true :remove-counter {:mining 1}})
    ;; Assert: permanent tapped AND counters at {:mining 2}
    ;; Catches: Only paying first cost
    ))

(deftest test-pay-all-costs-fails-partial
  (testing "pay-all-costs returns nil if any cost fails, no partial payment"
    ;; Setup: tapped permanent with {:mining 3}
    ;; Action: (abilities/pay-all-costs db object-id {:tap true :remove-counter {:mining 1}})
    ;; Assert: returns nil, counters still {:mining 3} (no change)
    ;; Catches: Paying counter cost even when tap fails
    ))

(deftest test-pay-all-costs-empty
  (testing "pay-all-costs with empty/nil costs returns db unchanged"
    ;; Setup: any permanent
    ;; Action: (abilities/pay-all-costs db object-id {})
    ;; Assert: returns original db unchanged
    ;; Catches: NPE on empty map, returning nil for free costs
    ))
```

**activate-ability tests (4 tests):**
```clojure
(deftest test-activate-tap-ability
  (testing "activate-ability pays :tap cost and applies mana effect"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:tap true} :ability/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:black 1}}]}
    ;; Action: (abilities/activate-ability db object-id ability :player-1)
    ;; Assert: permanent tapped AND player has 1 black mana
    ;; Catches: Not applying effects after paying costs
    ))

(deftest test-activate-tap-already-tapped
  (testing "activate-ability returns nil when cost cannot be paid"
    ;; Setup: tapped permanent
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:tap true} :ability/effects [...]}
    ;; Action: (abilities/activate-ability db object-id ability :player-1)
    ;; Assert: nil, mana pool unchanged
    ;; Catches: Applying effects even when costs fail
    ))

(deftest test-activate-counter-ability
  (testing "activate-ability removes counter and applies mana effect"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 3}
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}} :ability/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:any 1}}]}
    ;; Action: (abilities/activate-ability db object-id ability :player-1)
    ;; Assert: counters at {:mining 2}, mana in pool
    ;; Catches: Counter not removed, effect not applied
    ))

(deftest test-activate-ability-empty-effects
  (testing "activate-ability with no effects still pays costs (for triggered abilities later)"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:tap true} :ability/effects []}
    ;; Action: (abilities/activate-ability db object-id ability :player-1)
    ;; Assert: returns db with permanent tapped (not nil)
    ;; Catches: Returning nil when effects list empty
    ))
```

### 2. GREEN: Implement engine/abilities.cljs

Create new file: src/main/fizzle/engine/abilities.cljs

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.abilities
  "Generic ability activation for Fizzle.

   Provides can-activate?, pay-all-costs, and activate-ability functions
   that work with any ability defined as data."
  (:require
    [fizzle.engine.costs :as costs]
    [fizzle.engine.effects :as effects]))
```

### 3. Implement can-activate?

```clojure
(defn can-activate?
  "Check if an ability can be activated.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     object-id - UUID of the permanent
     ability - Map with :ability/cost and optionally :ability/effects
   
   Returns:
     Boolean - true if all costs can be paid (or no costs)."
  [db object-id ability]
  (let [cost (:ability/cost ability)]
    (if (or (nil? cost) (empty? cost))
      true  ;; Free activation
      (every? (fn [[cost-type cost-value]]
                (costs/can-pay? db object-id {cost-type cost-value}))
              cost))))
```

### 4. Implement pay-all-costs

```clojure
(defn pay-all-costs
  "Pay all costs for an ability.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     object-id - UUID of the permanent
     costs - Map of costs {:tap true :remove-counter {:mining 1}}
   
   Returns:
     New db with all costs paid, or nil if any cost cannot be paid.
     Empty/nil costs return db unchanged."
  [db object-id costs]
  (if (or (nil? costs) (empty? costs))
    db  ;; No costs to pay
    (reduce (fn [db' [cost-type cost-value]]
              (if-let [new-db (costs/pay-cost db' object-id {cost-type cost-value})]
                new-db
                (reduced nil)))
            db
            costs)))
```

### 5. Implement activate-ability

```clojure
(defn activate-ability
  "Activate an ability on a permanent.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     object-id - UUID of the permanent
     ability - Map with :ability/cost and :ability/effects
     player-id - The player activating
   
   Returns:
     New db with costs paid and effects applied, or nil if cannot activate."
  [db object-id ability player-id]
  (when (can-activate? db object-id ability)
    (let [cost (:ability/cost ability)
          db-after-costs (pay-all-costs db object-id cost)]
      (when db-after-costs
        ;; Apply effects (empty list is fine)
        (reduce (fn [db' effect]
                  (effects/execute-effect db' player-id effect))
                db-after-costs
                (:ability/effects ability []))))))
```

### 6. VERIFY
- Run: make validate
- Confirm all 14 new tests pass
- Confirm all 175+ existing tests still pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] engine/abilities.cljs exists at src/main/fizzle/engine/abilities.cljs
- [ ] can-activate? with :tap cost on untapped permanent returns true
- [ ] can-activate? with :tap cost on tapped permanent returns false
- [ ] can-activate? with nil/empty cost returns true (free activation)
- [ ] can-activate? with invalid object-id returns false (not crash)
- [ ] pay-all-costs with single cost pays it and returns db
- [ ] pay-all-costs with multiple costs pays all or returns nil
- [ ] pay-all-costs with nil/empty costs returns db unchanged
- [ ] activate-ability pays costs, applies effects, returns db
- [ ] activate-ability with unpayable cost returns nil, no side effects
- [ ] activate-ability with empty effects list still works (returns db)
- [ ] 14 tests pass in abilities_test.cljs
- [ ] All 175+ existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO card ID checks (data over code)
- ❌ NO duplicating cost logic (delegate to costs.cljs)
- ❌ NO hardcoded ability types
- ❌ NO partial cost payment (all-or-nothing)
- ❌ NO crashing on nil/empty costs or effects (handle gracefully)
- ❌ NO applying effects if costs cannot be paid

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Nil/Empty Cost Map**:
- Ability with no cost (nil :ability/cost or {}) should be activatable
- pay-all-costs with {} or nil should return db unchanged
- Test: test-can-activate-nil-cost, test-pay-all-costs-empty

**Multiple Costs - All-or-Nothing**:
- If :tap paid but :remove-counter fails, entire activation fails
- reduce returns nil on first failure via (reduced nil)
- Original db is never modified (immutable)
- Test: test-pay-all-costs-fails-partial

**Empty Effects List**:
- Ability with :ability/effects [] or nil is valid
- Should still pay costs and return db (not nil)
- Test: test-activate-ability-empty-effects

**Invalid Object ID**:
- can-activate? with non-existent object-id should return false
- Should not crash or throw exception
- Test: test-can-activate-invalid-object-id

**Effect Integration**:
- Effects use existing effects/execute-effect multimethod
- Mana abilities produce {:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:black 1}}
- Unknown effect types are no-op (graceful degradation)

**NOTE: Trigger firing is OUT OF SCOPE**:
- :becomes-tapped triggers will be handled by the trigger system
- This task focuses on ability activation only
- Triggers will be added in a separate task when we implement trigger firing

## Reference Implementation
- Study engine/costs.cljs for cost payment patterns
- Study engine/effects.cljs for effect execution pattern
- Study test helpers in costs_test.cljs for add-permanent helper
close_reason: Closed

title: Unify spell disposition: idempotent move-resolved-spell + explicit source-type
description: From intuition audit Tension 5 (Pass 9+10 linked finding): Three parallel spell disposition mechanisms create a workaround cascade in cleanup-selection-source. Approach: make move-resolved-spell idempotent (zone guard) + explicit source-type enum. See --design for full rationale.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- move-resolved-spell in engine/resolution.cljs handles already-moved spells as a no-op (zone guard at function entry)
- cleanup-selection-source uses explicit source-type enum (:spell/:ability/nil) instead of nil-as-implicit-spell
- All existing card tests pass unchanged (behavior-preserving refactor)
- Copy handling preserved: copies still on stack get removed, copies already removed produce no-op
- Console.warn for unexpected zones moves from cleanup into move-resolved-spell

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] move-resolved-spell returns unchanged db when spell is not on :stack
- [ ] cleanup-selection-source has exactly 3 branches: :ability, :spell, default
- [ ] :selection/source-type explicitly set to :spell or :ability (no nil-as-spell)
- [ ] source-type propagates through selection chains in standard-path
- [ ] Flashback + exile-self cards resolve correctly (no double-move, no silent no-op)
- [ ] Copy cease-to-exist handled (nil object → no-op)
- [ ] All 3134+ tests passing
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO new fields on stack-items for disposition (use existing :mode/on-resolve via cast mode)
- NO changes to :exile-self effect implementation (it must remain unaware of cleanup)
- NO builder contract changes (don't add cleanup keys to selection maps — worsens Tension 2)
- NO moving cleanup into the continuation protocol (ADR-020 is for game flow, not bookkeeping)

## Approach
Make move-resolved-spell idempotent by adding a zone guard: if the spell is not on :stack, return db unchanged. This moves the already-moved detection into the engine function where disposition logic already lives. cleanup-selection-source becomes a simple 3-way case on explicit source-type (:spell, :ability, nil/none). The reactive zone detection, console.warn, and nil guards in cleanup reduce to a single nil-check on the object (for copies that ceased to exist). The console.warn migrates to move-resolved-spell.

Make source-type explicit: resolution.cljs:build-selection-from-result sets :selection/source-type :spell for spell selections (currently nil). standard-path in core.cljs propagates source-type to chained selections.

## Architecture
- engine/resolution.cljs: move-resolved-spell gains zone guard (2 lines) + console.warn migration
- events/selection/spell_cleanup.cljs (after ADR-022): simplified 3-branch cleanup
- events/resolution.cljs: build-selection-from-result sets :selection/source-type :spell
- events/selection/core.cljs: standard-path propagates source-type to chained selections

## Architecture Context
- Component: events_selection (orchestration layer, exploring stability)
- Adjacent: engine/resolution (downward dependency), events/casting (peer)
- Relevant ADRs: ADR-019 (pure selection pipeline), ADR-020 (continuations), ADR-022 (extract spell cleanup)
- Dependency on ADR-022: this work happens AFTER or DURING the spell cleanup extraction

## Architecture Update Checklist
After work completes:
1. Did this work change a component's public interface? → move-resolved-spell signature unchanged but behavior changes (idempotent)
2. Did this work add or remove a dependency? → No
3. Did this work create/remove a component? → No (may overlap with ADR-022 extraction)
4. Did this work move responsibility? → Zone detection moves from selection→engine
5. Did this work create a new request path? → No

## Design Rationale
### Problem
cleanup-selection-source has 5 branches detecting zone state reactively after effect execution, creating a workaround cascade. Three parallel disposition mechanisms exist (declaration via :mode/on-resolve, standard cleanup via move-resolved-spell, effect-driven movement via :exile-self). Cleanup ignores the existing declaration mechanism.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- engine/resolution.cljs:106-152 — move-resolved-spell is SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for zone transitions
- engine/resolution.cljs:127 — already reads :mode/on-resolve for flashback disposition
- engine/effects/zones.cljs:51-57 — :exile-self is the ONLY effect that moves spells during execution (3 cards)
- events/selection/core.cljs:263-304 — 4 branches + console.warn detecting zone state

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Idempotent move-resolved-spell + explicit source-type ✓
**What it is:** Zone guard in move-resolved-spell (not-on-stack → no-op). Explicit source-type enum in selection system.
**Chosen because:** Least invasive. No new fields. Puts zone safety in the engine where disposition logic lives. Only 2 files changed significantly.

#### 2. Declared disposition on stack-item ❌
**What it is:** Stack-item carries :stack-item/disposition. :exile-self updates it to :handled.
**Why we looked at this:** Data-driven disposition is cleaner than detection.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Requires :exile-self to know about stack-items (currently doesn't). Adds a write during effect execution. Creates a second source of truth alongside :mode/on-resolve.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Stack-items gain other mutable metadata that effects need to update.

#### 3. Builder-declared cleanup callback ❌
**What it is:** Selection map carries {:selection/cleanup {:type :spell ...}} declaring cleanup strategy.
**Why we looked at this:** Moves cleanup decision to builder (knows whether it's spell/ability).
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Adds more keys to implicit builder contract (directly worsens Tension 2 from audit). Duplicates data already available on the selection.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Builder contract gets explicit validation (spec adoption resolves Tension 2).

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** move-resolved-spell zone guard, cleanup simplification, source-type migration
**Out of scope:** ADR-022 extraction (separate task, can happen first or concurrently), spec adoption (separate brainstorm fizzle-zh81)

### Open Questions
- Should this work be combined with ADR-022 (spell cleanup extraction) into one task?
- Does the zone guard in move-resolved-spell need a console.warn for unexpected zones (not :exile/:graveyard)?

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Core concern? | Both spell/ability routing AND already-moved detection | Design addresses both concerns |
| Approach? | Idempotent move-resolved-spell | No new fields, least invasive |
| Copy handling? | Must play nice | Nil check on object preserved for ceased-to-exist copies |

### Dead-End Paths

#### Path: Continuation-based cleanup
**Why explored:** Continuation protocol (ADR-020) designed for composition after selection.
**Why abandoned:** Continuations are for game flow, not bookkeeping. Would mix concerns in the continuation protocol.

title: Separate bot/human opponent-turn stops
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
1. Human player has a separate `:player/opponent-stops` attribute storing phases where they want priority during the opponent's turn
2. Bot's `:player/stops` is purely bot-derived (from phase-actions at init), never modified by UI toggle
3. `advance-with-stops` pauses at phases where EITHER the active player has a stop OR the non-active player has an opponent-stop
4. `toggle-stop` for opponent role writes to the human's `:player/opponent-stops`, not the bot's `:player/stops`
5. F6 (yield-all) skips both own-stops and opponent-stops for the human player
6. Opponent-stop preferences persist in localStorage and survive game resets
7. Subscriptions and phase bar UI read opponent stops from the human's `:player/opponent-stops`
8. Restorer loads opponent-stops onto the human entity, not the bot entity
9. Same mechanism for all player types — no bot-specific code paths in advance-with-stops

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] toggle-stop on opponent phases does NOT modify bot's :player/stops
- [ ] Human receives priority during bot phases where they have opponent-stops set
- [ ] F6 skips both own-stops and opponent-stops
- [ ] Opponent-stops persist across game resets via localStorage
- [ ] Bot-derived stops set at init are never modified by UI
- [ ] Phase bar UI displays opponent stops from human's :player/opponent-stops
- [ ] Restorer correctly loads opponent-stops onto human entity
- [ ] Old localStorage format (with :opponent key) is handled gracefully
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO storing human preferences on bot entity (semantic: human's priority preferences belong to human player)
- ❌ NO bot-specific code in advance-with-stops (uniformity: same mechanism for all player types — check active player's stops AND non-active player's opponent-stops)
- ❌ NO modifying bot's :player/stops from toggle-stop (separation: bot stops are engine-derived from phase-actions, not UI-driven)
- ❌ NO separate advance-with-stops code path for opponent-stops vs own-stops (mechanism: single loop checks both)
- ❌ NO breaking existing localStorage — old format must load gracefully (backwards compat: users have saved stop preferences)

## Approach
Add `:player/opponent-stops` attribute to schema. Human player stores opponent-turn stop preferences here. Bot's `:player/stops` stays bot-derived. `advance-with-stops` checks both the active player's `:player/stops` and the non-active player's `:player/opponent-stops` in its phase loop. `toggle-stop` for opponent role writes to the human's new attribute. localStorage persists both `:player` and `:opponent-stops` keys. F6 flag on human skips both stop types. Restorer loads opponent-stops onto human entity.

## Architecture
- `db/schema.cljs` — Add `:player/opponent-stops` to schema
- `engine/priority.cljs` — Update `advance-with-stops` to check non-active player's opponent-stops; add/modify `check-stop` for opponent-stops
- `events/ui.cljs` — `toggle-stop` for opponent role writes to human's `:player/opponent-stops`
- `db/storage.cljs` — Update persistence: `:opponent-stops` key, migration from old `:opponent` key
- `events/init.cljs` — Load opponent-stops from localStorage onto human entity
- `subs/game.cljs` — `::opponent-stops` reads from human's `:player/opponent-stops`
- `sharing/restorer.cljs` — Load opponent-stops onto human entity, not bot
- `views/phase_bar.cljs` — No change needed if subscription source is updated

## Design Rationale
### Problem
`:player/stops` on the bot entity serves two conflated purposes: (1) phases where bot needs to act (engine), (2) human's opponent-turn stop preferences (UI). toggle-stop overwrites the bot's functional stops. Additionally, the human never receives priority during the bot's turn because advance-with-stops only checks the active player's stops.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- `engine/priority.cljs` — `advance-with-stops` checks only active player's `:player/stops`
- `events/ui.cljs:69-72` — toggle-stop resolves :opponent role to bot entity, writes to bot's :player/stops
- `events/init.cljs:145-146` — Bot stops derived from phase-actions; localStorage :opponent value ALREADY IGNORED at init
- `db/storage.cljs:90-125` — localStorage shape `{:player #{...} :opponent #{}}`; :opponent key written by toggle-stop but never read at init
- `sharing/restorer.cljs:204` — Restorer transacts localStorage :opponent onto bot entity (incorrect)
- `bots/protocol.cljs:59-68` — `bot-stops` derives from phase-actions spec keys

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Opponent-stops on human player entity ✓

**What it is:** New `:player/opponent-stops` attribute on human player. toggle-stop writes opponent preferences here. advance-with-stops checks both active player's stops and non-active player's opponent-stops.

**Investigation:**
- Confirmed advance-with-stops loop can easily check a second player's attribute
- Priority flow already alternates between players — pausing at a phase gives both players priority naturally
- F6 flag is on the human entity, easy to check when evaluating opponent-stops

**Pros:**
- Semantically correct (human preferences on human entity)
- Clean separation — bot code untouched
- Same mechanism for all players — no type dispatch
- Easy to persist and restore

**Cons:**
- advance-with-stops needs to look up the non-active player (minor — one query)

**Chosen because:** Aligns with project principle #4 (separate mechanism from policy). Same priority mechanism, different input policies (UI vs phase-actions).

#### 2. Protected union on bot entity ❌

**What it is:** Bot entity stores union of bot-derived + human preferences. New `:player/derived-stops` stores immutable bot stops. toggle-stop preserves derived stops in the union.

**Why we looked at this:** Minimal change to advance-with-stops (still checks active player only).

**Investigation:**
- advance-with-stops pausing at a union-stop gives the BOT priority first, which is mechanically correct (active player gets priority first in Magic)
- But F6 handling is awkward: must compute `stops - derived-stops` to skip only human preferences
- Human preferences stored on wrong entity — breaks if bot changes or multiplayer added

**Pros:**
- No change to advance-with-stops lookup logic

**Cons:**
- Semantically wrong (human preferences on bot entity)
- F6 handling complex (set difference to skip human-only stops)
- Persistence and restoration more complex
- Breaks if bot entity changes

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Violates principle #4 (mechanism and policy conflated on same entity). F6 handling is unnecessarily complex.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We abandon the concept of opponent-stops as human priority preferences.

#### 3. Minimal fix — protect bot stops from toggle ❌

**What it is:** toggle-stop preserves bot-derived stops by only modifying the non-derived subset.

**Why we looked at this:** Smallest possible change to fix the immediate bug.

**Investigation:**
- Fixes the overwrite bug but doesn't give human priority during bot's turn
- User explicitly wants opponent-stops to mean 'I want priority to respond'

**Pros:**
- Smallest change, minimal risk

**Cons:**
- Doesn't deliver the actual feature (human priority on opponent's turn)
- Still stores human preferences on wrong entity

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** User requirement is 'human gets priority during opponent phases with stops set', not just 'prevent overwrite'.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Feature requirement changes to only fixing the overwrite bug.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Schema change, advance-with-stops, toggle-stop, localStorage, subs, restorer
- Backward-compatible localStorage migration

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Bot opponent-stops (bot responding during human's turn) — deferred to counter-spell bot epic
- Multiplayer stops — not applicable to 2-player model
- Auto-stops based on hand contents — too complex, not requested

### Open Questions
- Should old localStorage :opponent key be renamed to :opponent-stops on first load? (yes — migration in load-stops)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

> Detailed context from brainstorming for task creation and obstacle handling.

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Opponent stop semantics? | Player may have an effect — wants priority to respond | Stops are human priority preferences, stored on human entity |
| Check both players' stops? | Yes — check both | advance-with-stops checks non-active player's opponent-stops |
| F6 behavior? | Skips everything | F6 flag skips both own-stops and opponent-stops |
| Approach? | Opponent-stops on human entity | New :player/opponent-stops attribute, clean separation |
| Bot-specific code? | No — same mechanism, different policy | No bot-specific handling in advance-with-stops |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Current Stops Implementation
**Question explored:** How do stops flow through the system?
**Sources consulted:**
- engine/priority.cljs — advance-with-stops, check-stop, set-player-stops
- events/ui.cljs — toggle-stop event handler
- events/init.cljs — game initialization
- db/storage.cljs — localStorage persistence
- sharing/restorer.cljs — snapshot restoration

**Findings:**
- advance-with-stops only checks active player's stops (bot's turn → bot's stops)
- toggle-stop for opponent role writes to bot's :player/stops (conflation)
- init.cljs already ignores localStorage :opponent — bot stops derived from phase-actions
- restorer.cljs transacts localStorage :opponent onto bot entity (incorrect)
- Priority flow naturally alternates — pausing at phase gives both players priority

**Conclusion:** Clean separation is possible with minimal changes. Most complexity is in advance-with-stops (add one more check) and toggle-stop (change target entity).

### Dead-End Paths
None — all three approaches evaluated, approach 1 clearly best fit.

### Open Concerns Raised
- localStorage migration — addressed by reading old :opponent key and mapping to :opponent-stops on first load
- Bot-specific code concern — confirmed: zero bot-specific code in new design
close_reason: All success criteria met. Reviewer APPROVED. Commits: 8cc5104, c886ece, ec9017a. 3051 tests passing.

title: Phase 7: Implement Recoup card definition
description:
Implement the Recoup card (1R Sorcery) that grants flashback to target sorcery in graveyard.

## Oracle Text
Target sorcery card in your graveyard gains flashback until end of turn. The flashback cost is equal to the card's mana cost. Flashback {3}{R}

## Prerequisites (All Completed)
- Grant system (engine/grants.cljs) ✅
- Cleanup/expiration system (triggers.cljs, turn_based.cljs) ✅
- Targeting system (targeting.cljs) ✅
- :grant-flashback effect type (effects.cljs) ✅
- Deep Analysis and Cephalid Coliseum migrations ✅

## Implementation Steps

1. Create src/main/fizzle/cards/recoup.cljs with card definition:
   - :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :red 1}
   - :card/types #{:sorcery}
   - :card/targeting [{:target/id :graveyard-sorcery
                       :target/type :object
                       :target/zone :graveyard
                       :target/controller :self
                       :target/criteria {:card/types #{:sorcery}}
                       :target/required true}]
   - :card/effects [{:effect/type :grant-flashback
                     :effect/target-ref :graveyard-sorcery}]
   - :card/alternate-costs for Recoup's own flashback {3}{R}

2. Add Recoup to iggy-pop.cljs cards collection

3. Create src/test/fizzle/cards/recoup_test.cljs with comprehensive tests:
   - Basic flow: cast Recoup, grant flashback, cast target via flashback
   - Targeting fizzle: target removed before resolution
   - Flashback Recoup: cast from graveyard, verify exiles
   - End of turn cleanup: granted flashback expires
   - Multiple grants: verify grants work independently across turns

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Recoup card file exists with correct definition
- [ ] Card added to iggy-pop collection
- [ ] All 5 test scenarios pass
- [ ] Existing tests still pass (make test)
design:
Implement the Recoup card (1R Sorcery) that grants flashback to target sorcery in graveyard.

## Oracle Text
Target sorcery card in your graveyard gains flashback until end of turn. The flashback cost is equal to the card's mana cost. Flashback {3}{R}

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Prerequisites (All Completed)
- Grant system (engine/grants.cljs) ✅
- Cleanup/expiration system (triggers.cljs, turn_based.cljs) ✅
- Targeting system (targeting.cljs) ✅
- :grant-flashback effect type (effects.cljs) ✅
- Deep Analysis and Cephalid Coliseum migrations ✅

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Create src/main/fizzle/cards/recoup.cljs

Follow the pattern from deep_analysis.cljs:

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.recoup
  "Recoup card definition.

   Recoup: 1R - Sorcery
   Target sorcery card in your graveyard gains flashback until end of turn.
   The flashback cost is equal to the card's mana cost.
   Flashback {3}{R}")

(def recoup
  {:card/id :recoup
   :card/name "Recoup"
   :card/cmc 2
   :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :red 1}
   :card/colors #{:red}
   :card/types #{:sorcery}
   :card/text "Target sorcery card in your graveyard gains flashback until end of turn. The flashback cost is equal to the card's mana cost. Flashback {3}{R}"

   ;; Cast-time targeting: target sorcery in your graveyard
   :card/targeting [{:target/id :graveyard-sorcery
                     :target/type :object
                     :target/zone :graveyard
                     :target/controller :self
                     :target/criteria {:card/types #{:sorcery}}
                     :target/required true}]

   ;; Effect: grant flashback to target
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :grant-flashback
                   :effect/target-ref :graveyard-sorcery}]

   ;; Recoup's own flashback
   :card/alternate-costs [{:alternate/id :flashback
                           :alternate/zone :graveyard
                           :alternate/mana-cost {:colorless 3 :red 1}
                           :alternate/on-resolve :exile}]})
```

### Step 2: Add to iggy-pop.cljs

1. Add require: `[fizzle.cards.recoup :as recoup]`
2. Add to all-cards vector: `recoup/recoup`

### Step 3: Create src/test/fizzle/cards/recoup_test.cljs

Follow pattern from deep_analysis_test.cljs for test helpers.

## Success Criteria

**Card Definition Tests (catch definition bugs):**
- [ ] test_recoup_card_definition - Verifies :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :red 1}, :card/types #{:sorcery}, :card/colors #{:red}
- [ ] test_recoup_has_targeting_requirement - Verifies :card/targeting with :graveyard-sorcery target
- [ ] test_recoup_has_grant_flashback_effect - Verifies :effect/type :grant-flashback
- [ ] test_recoup_has_own_flashback - Verifies :alternate/mana-cost {:colorless 3 :red 1}

**Cast Requirements Tests (catch targeting bugs):**
- [ ] test_recoup_requires_valid_target - Cannot cast if no sorceries in graveyard
- [ ] test_recoup_finds_valid_sorcery_target - Finds sorcery in graveyard as valid target
- [ ] test_recoup_does_not_target_instants - Instants in graveyard are NOT valid targets
- [ ] test_recoup_does_not_target_opponent_graveyard - Only targets self graveyard

**Grant Flashback Tests (catch grant system bugs):**
- [ ] test_recoup_grants_flashback_on_resolution - Target sorcery has :granted-flashback alternate cost after resolution
- [ ] test_granted_flashback_uses_original_mana_cost - If target costs {2BB}, granted flashback costs {2BB}
- [ ] test_granted_flashback_exiles_on_resolve - Target card using granted flashback exiles after resolution

**Fizzle Tests (catch targeting validation bugs):**
- [ ] test_recoup_fizzles_if_target_removed - If target exiled before resolution, Recoup has no effect and goes to graveyard
- [ ] test_recoup_still_resolves_if_other_sorceries_exist - Fizzle is per-target, not based on other cards

**Cleanup Tests (catch expiration bugs):**
- [ ] test_granted_flashback_expires_at_cleanup - Grant removed when cleanup phase reached
- [ ] test_granted_flashback_does_not_expire_before_cleanup - Grant still active during main phase

**Flashback Recoup Tests (catch Recoup-casting-itself bugs):**
- [ ] test_flashback_recoup_castable_from_graveyard - Recoup is castable for {3}{R} from graveyard
- [ ] test_flashback_recoup_exiles_after_resolution - Flashback Recoup exiles, not goes to graveyard
- [ ] test_flashback_recoup_can_target_sorceries - Flashback Recoup can still target sorceries

**Integration Tests (catch full flow bugs):**
- [ ] test_full_flow_cast_recoup_grant_flashback_use_it - Complete storm combo scenario

**Verification Commands:**
```bash
make test                          # All tests pass
make lint                          # No linter warnings
grep -c "deftest" src/test/fizzle/cards/recoup_test.cljs  # Should be >= 18
```

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: No Valid Targets**
- If player has no sorceries in graveyard, Recoup cannot be cast
- has-valid-targets? should return false
- Test: test_recoup_requires_valid_target

**Edge Case: Target Removed Before Resolution**
- If target is exiled (e.g., opponent's Tormod's Crypt effect) before Recoup resolves
- Recoup fizzles: no effect, goes to graveyard (not exile, since not flashback cast)
- Test: test_recoup_fizzles_if_target_removed

**Edge Case: Recoup Targeting Itself**
- Recoup is a sorcery, but it's on the stack when casting, not in graveyard
- Cannot target itself - targeting happens at cast time when Recoup not in graveyard yet
- This is handled naturally by the targeting system - no special case needed

**Edge Case: Granted Flashback on Instant vs Sorcery**
- Recoup only targets sorceries (:card/types #{:sorcery})
- Instants should NOT be valid targets
- Test: test_recoup_does_not_target_instants

**Reference Pattern:**
- deep_analysis.cljs for card structure
- deep_analysis_test.cljs for test patterns and helpers

## Anti-patterns

- ❌ Do NOT hardcode mana costs - use target's :card/mana-cost
- ❌ Do NOT skip targeting validation - use targeting/all-targets-legal?
- ❌ Do NOT duplicate grant system code - use grants/add-grant
- ❌ Do NOT forget :target/controller :self - only own graveyard
- ❌ Do NOT test only happy path - fizzle and edge cases are critical
close_reason: Closed

title: Bug: Turn numbering counts both players as same turn
description: Turn 1 shows both human and bot turns. Human turn should be turn 1, bot turn should be turn 2, etc. Each player's turn should increment the turn counter.
design:
## Goal
Fix turn numbering so each player's turn gets its own turn number. Currently both human and bot show the same turn number in the history panel.

## Root Cause Analysis
The turn counter lives in start-turn (events/game.cljs:600-601):
    current-turn (or (:game/turn game-state) 0)
    new-turn (inc current-turn)

Game starts at turn=1 (line 173). start-turn is called when a player's turn ends (advance-with-stops cleanup handling, line 693). start-turn increments counter and switches active player.

The history groups entries by :entry/turn (history/core.cljs:186-192, entries-by-turn fn). views/history.cljs line 65 displays "Turn N" headers.

**Two confirmed bugs:**
1. **Turns may not be incrementing for bot→human transitions** — needs investigation via test. The code path: handle-loop-state :bot-phase → advance-with-stops → (when cleanup) start-turn. Must verify this actually fires and the incremented turn propagates to history entries.
2. **new-game-handler (events/setup.cljs:253) missing bot-archetype** — confirmed by reading code. Re-dealing with "New Game" defaults to :goldfish even when :burn was selected. The config map passed to init-game-state is:
    {:main-deck ... :clock-turns ... :must-contain ...}
   Missing :bot-archetype key.

## Fix

### Fix 1: Investigate and fix turn counter
Write a test that traces :game/turn through a full human→bot→human cycle using yield-impl or yield-loop. The test will reveal if/where the counter fails to increment.

Likely suspects:
- handle-loop-state :bot-phase calling advance-with-stops which calls start-turn — does the returned db propagate correctly?
- yield-loop creating history entries — does the turn from post-db reflect the incremented value?
- The entry for "end of human turn" uses post-db turn (which is the NEW turn), so it appears under the bot's turn group — this may be correct but look wrong to the user

### Fix 2: new-game-handler bot-archetype
In events/setup.cljs:253, add :bot-archetype to the config map:
    (game/init-game-state {:main-deck (:setup/main-deck config)
                           :clock-turns (:setup/clock-turns config)
                           :bot-archetype (get config :setup/bot-archetype :goldfish)
                           :must-contain (get config :setup/must-contain {})})

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Test proves: after human cleanup → start-turn, :game/turn increments from 1 to 2 and active player switches to bot
- [ ] Test proves: after bot cleanup → start-turn, :game/turn increments from 2 to 3 and active player switches to human
- [ ] History entries for human's turn have :entry/turn = 1, bot's turn entries have :entry/turn = 2, next human turn has :entry/turn = 3
- [ ] new-game-handler passes :bot-archetype from stashed config — test: create game with :burn, re-deal via new-game-handler, verify opponent has :burn archetype
- [ ] 4+ new tests: turn progression (2 tests), history entry turn values (1 test), new-game-handler bot-archetype (1 test)
- [ ] All existing tests pass (make validate clean)

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] In test/fizzle/bots/burn_integration_test.cljs (or new file), add turn progression tests:
  - turn-increments-after-human-turn-ends:
    Setup: create-test-db with burn bot, human active at turn 1
    Action: advance human through all phases to cleanup (via advance-with-stops loop or start-turn directly)
    Assert: :game/turn is 2 in result db, active player is :player-2 (bot)
  - turn-increments-after-bot-turn-ends:
    Setup: from previous test state (or fresh with bot active at turn 2)
    Action: run bot through all phases to cleanup
    Assert: :game/turn is 3 in result db, active player is :player-1 (human)
- [ ] Add history entry turn attribution test:
  - history-entries-have-correct-turn-values:
    Setup: create game with burn bot
    Action: run yield-loop through at least one full human→bot→human cycle
    Assert: entries grouped by turn show turns 1, 2, 3 with correct grouping
- [ ] In test/fizzle/events/setup_test.cljs, add new-game-handler test:
  - new-game-handler-preserves-bot-archetype:
    Setup: create game with :burn archetype, stash config
    Action: call new-game-handler
    Assert: opponent player has :player/bot-archetype :burn (not :goldfish)
- [ ] Fix events/setup.cljs:253 — add :bot-archetype to new-game-handler config map
- [ ] If turn investigation reveals a bug, fix the root cause
- [ ] Run make validate

## Key Considerations

**Turn Counter Mechanism:**
start-turn (game.cljs:580-611) does:
1. Switch active player: next-player-id = get-other-player-id
2. Increment: new-turn = (inc current-turn)
3. Transact: [:db/add game-eid :game/turn new-turn]
4. Reset: phase to :untap, storm to 0, land-plays to 1
5. Dispatch: untap phase trigger

This is a pure function called in advance-with-stops (line 693). The result propagates through handle-loop-state → yield-impl → yield-loop.

**History Entry Turn Attribution:**
yield-loop (game.cljs:781-782) reads turn from post-db. When the db change IS start-turn (which increments the turn), the entry is attributed to the NEW turn number. So the "start of bot's turn" entry appears under "Turn 2" — this is correct behavior.

**Edge Case: First Turn**
Game starts at turn=1, phase=main1. No start-turn is called for the first turn — it's set directly in init-game-state (line 173). This is correct.

**Edge Case: Game Over During Bot Turn**
If human reaches 0 life during bot's turn, the turn counter is irrelevant. No special handling needed.

**new-game-handler Bug Detail (events/setup.cljs:249-257):**
    (game/init-game-state {:main-deck (:setup/main-deck config)
                           :clock-turns (:setup/clock-turns config)
                           :must-contain (get config :setup/must-contain {})})
Missing :bot-archetype. The stashed config (stash-setup-config, line 211-222) DOES include :setup/bot-archetype. So the data is available, just not being passed through.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Do NOT change the turn model to "round-based" — user wants each player-turn to have its own number
- ❌ Do NOT add display-only turn numbering — fix the actual counter/attribution
- ❌ Do NOT skip testing the full cycle — the bug may only manifest through yield-loop
- ❌ Do NOT hardcode player IDs in tests — use helper functions
close_reason: Closed

title: Cost Reduction Artifacts: Medallion Cycle + Helm of Awakening
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- All 5 Medallions (Sapphire, Ruby, Jet, Emerald, Pearl) implemented as cycle file
- Helm of Awakening implemented as individual card file
- Engine supports :decrease direction in cost modifier system
- Cost calculation order: increases applied before decreases (MTG rules)
- Generic mana portion floors at 0 (cannot go negative)
- Medallions use :modifier/applies-to :controller (only controller's spells)
- Helm uses :modifier/applies-to :all (all players' spells)
- Existing cost increase cards (Sphere, Defense Grid, Chill) unaffected
- Cost reduction applies to flashback costs (already flows through get-effective-mana-cost)
- Multiple cost reducers stack additively

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] card_spec.cljs accepts :decrease direction and :modifier/applies-to field
- [ ] static_abilities/apply-cost-modifiers handles increases-then-decreases with floor at 0
- [ ] static_abilities/modifier-applies? checks :modifier/applies-to against caster vs source controller
- [ ] All 5 Medallions defined in cards/artifacts/medallions.cljs with correct oracle text
- [ ] Helm of Awakening defined in cards/artifacts/helm_of_awakening.cljs with correct oracle text
- [ ] Registry updated with both cycle and individual card
- [ ] Medallion tests use cycle doseq pattern (not copy-paste per card)
- [ ] Interaction tests: medallion+sphere cancel, multiple medallions stack, helm affects both players
- [ ] All existing tests pass (no regressions in Sphere/Defense Grid/Chill)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO modifying mana.cljs or costs.cljs (cost reduction hooks through static_abilities chokepoint only)
- ❌ NO breaking backward compat for existing cost increase cards (Sphere, Defense Grid, Chill must work identically)
- ❌ NO per-card logic in the engine (cost reduction is data-driven via :static-abilities, not card-name checks)
- ❌ NO grants for static abilities (grants are temporary; static abilities are inherent to battlefield permanents)
- ❌ NO copy-paste test variants per medallion (use doseq over cycle data vector)
- ❌ NO tautological assertions (test exact values, not some?/string?)

## Approach
Extend the existing static ability cost modifier system in static_abilities.cljs. The system already handles :increase via Sphere of Resistance, Defense Grid, and Chill. Adding :decrease is a clean extension of the same mechanism.

Engine changes: (1) card_spec.cljs adds :decrease to valid-modifier-directions and adds valid-applies-to set. (2) static_abilities.cljs modifier-applies? checks :modifier/applies-to ownership filter. (3) static_abilities.cljs apply-cost-modifiers sums increases first (add to generic), then sums decreases (subtract from generic, floor at 0).

Card definitions follow existing patterns exactly: medallions as cycle file (like pain_lands.cljs), Helm as individual file (like sphere_of_resistance.cljs).

## Architecture
- engine/card_spec.cljs — Add :decrease to valid-modifier-directions, add valid-applies-to
- engine/static_abilities.cljs — Extend modifier-applies? and apply-cost-modifiers
- cards/artifacts/medallions.cljs — Cycle file (5 medallion defs + cards vector)
- cards/artifacts/helm_of_awakening.cljs — Individual card file
- cards/registry.cljs — Add medallions/cards and helm-of-awakening/card

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The engine supports cost increases (Sphere, Defense Grid, Chill) but not cost decreases. Medallions and Helm of Awakening are essential storm combo cards that reduce spell costs, enabling faster combo turns.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- static_abilities.cljs:79-102 — apply-cost-modifiers only handles :increase, clean extension point
- static_abilities.cljs:42-76 — modifier-applies? already receives caster-id and source/controller
- card_spec.cljs:229-230 — valid-modifier-directions currently #{:increase}
- rules.cljs:163,350,370 + game.cljs:357 — get-effective-mana-cost called in exactly 4 places (single chokepoint)
- Chill uses :criteria/type :spell-color — same criteria works for Medallions

**External (Scryfall/rulings):**
- All Medallions: '{Color} spells you cast cost {1} less to cast.' CMC 2 artifacts
- Helm of Awakening: 'Spells cost {1} less to cast.' CMC 2 artifact, affects all players
- Cost reduction only reduces generic mana, not colored
- Increases applied before decreases (official MTG rule)
- Applies to alternative costs (flashback)
- Multiple reducers stack

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Extend existing static ability system ✓
**What it is:** Add :decrease to the existing cost modifier mechanism in static_abilities.cljs. 3 small engine changes + card data.
**Chosen because:** System was designed for this extension. Minimal code, maximum consistency.

#### 2. Grant-based cost reduction ❌
**Why explored:** Grants handle temporary effects from permanents.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Static abilities are inherent to permanents, not temporary. Sphere/Defense Grid/Chill don't use grants. Would create inconsistent dual systems.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We need temporary cost reduction (unlikely for these cards).

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** 5 Medallions, Helm of Awakening, engine :decrease support, :applies-to ownership filter
**Out of scope:** Type-based criteria (creature spells cost less), CMC-based criteria, conditional reduction

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Ownership filter model? | :modifier/applies-to with :controller/:all | New enum field, default :all for backward compat |
| Cost calculation order? | Increases then decreases | Official MTG rules, sum increases first then decreases |
| Helm symmetry? | Affects all players | Oracle-accurate, bolt costs 0 with Helm out |
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 1: Extract Shared Test Helpers and Migrate All Test Files
design:
## Goal
Create src/test/fizzle/test_helpers.cljs with all commonly duplicated test helpers, then migrate all test files to use the shared namespace. Zero remaining helper duplication when complete.

## Effort Estimate
8-12 hours (2-3 hours creating shared namespace, 6-9 hours mechanical migration across 29+ files)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns and resolve signature conflicts

**Reference files:**
- src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs — uses db/init.cljs init-game-state, good model
- src/test/fizzle/cards/brain_freeze_test.cljs — exemplar card test with local helpers
- src/test/fizzle/cards/dark_ritual_test.cljs — typical card test with standard helpers
- src/test/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis_test.cljs — uses DIFFERENT add-card-to-zone signature (CRITICAL)

**CRITICAL: Signature conflicts to resolve before coding:**

add-card-to-zone has TWO variants:
- Standard (75% of files): [db card-id zone player-id] returns [@conn obj-id]
- deep_analysis/ray_of_revelation variant: [db player-id card zone] returns [obj-id @conn]
- DECISION: Use standard signature [db card-id zone player-id] returning [db obj-id].
- deep_analysis/ray_of_revelation tests must be updated to match standard signature.
- Note: their variant also adds :object/position 0 for library zone — handle this in add-card-to-zone when zone is :library.

add-cards-to-library has TWO patterns:
- Pattern A (9 files): [db card-ids player-id] takes vector of card keywords, returns [db object-ids]
- Pattern B (2 files: brain_freeze, effects): [db player-id n] takes integer count, uses :dark-ritual, returns db only
- DECISION: Support Pattern A as the primary (more flexible). Files using Pattern B either switch to Pattern A or keep a local helper.

Return value convention:
- ALL shared helpers return [db result] tuples (db first, consistent with Datascript patterns)
- Tests expecting [obj-id db] must be updated to [db obj-id]

### 2. Create test_helpers.cljs with these helpers:

File: src/test/fizzle/test_helpers.cljs
Namespace: fizzle.test-helpers

Requires:
- [datascript.core :as d]
- [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
- [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
- [fizzle.cards.iggy-pop :as cards]

Functions:

(create-test-db) and (create-test-db opts)
- No-arg version: all-zero mana pool, standard player (:player-1, 20 life, storm 0, land-plays 1)
- opts map supports: {:mana {:blue 1 :black 3} :life 20 :storm-count 0 :land-plays 1}
- Loads all card defs from cards/all-cards
- Creates game entity with turn 1, phase :main1, active-player = player-1
- Returns immutable db value (NOT conn)

(add-card-to-zone db card-id zone owner)
- card-id: keyword like :dark-ritual
- zone: keyword like :hand, :battlefield, :graveyard, :library, :exile
- owner: keyword like :player-1
- When zone is :library, adds :object/position 0 (top of library)
- Returns [db obj-id] tuple

(add-cards-to-library db card-ids owner)
- card-ids: vector of card-id keywords
- Adds with sequential positions (0 = top, 1, 2, ...)
- Returns [db object-ids] tuple

(add-cards-to-graveyard db card-ids owner)
- card-ids: vector of card-id keywords
- Returns [db object-ids] tuple

(get-zone-count db zone owner)
- Returns integer count of objects in zone for owner

(get-object-zone db obj-id)
- Returns zone keyword for given object

(get-hand-count db owner)
- Shorthand for (get-zone-count db :hand owner)

(add-opponent db)
- Adds :player-2 with name "Opponent", life 20, zero mana, is-opponent true
- Returns updated db

### 3. Write tests for test_helpers.cljs (TDD)

File: src/test/fizzle/test_helpers_test.cljs

Tests:
- create-test-db-returns-valid-db: verify db has player entity, game entity, card definitions loaded. Specific: (q/get-player-eid db :player-1) returns non-nil, game phase is :main1
- create-test-db-with-mana-opts: (create-test-db {:mana {:blue 3}}) sets blue to 3, other colors to 0
- create-test-db-with-life-opts: (create-test-db {:life 10}) sets life to 10
- add-card-to-zone-hand: adds Dark Ritual to hand, verify (get-zone-count db :hand :player-1) is 1, obj-id is UUID
- add-card-to-zone-library-sets-position: adds card to library, verify :object/position is 0
- add-card-to-zone-invalid-card-id: what happens when card-id doesn't exist? Should throw or return nil (decide and document)
- add-cards-to-library-ordering: add [:dark-ritual :cabal-ritual :lions-eye-diamond], verify positions 0,1,2 respectively
- add-cards-to-library-empty: add empty vector, returns [db []] with db unchanged
- add-cards-to-graveyard-multiple: add 3 cards, verify graveyard count is 3
- get-zone-count-empty: no cards added, count is 0
- add-opponent-creates-player-2: verify :player-2 entity exists with is-opponent true

### 4. Migrate all 29 create-test-db files (mechanical extraction)

Complete list of files to migrate:
1. src/test/fizzle/cards/basic_lands_test.cljs
2. src/test/fizzle/cards/brain_freeze_test.cljs
3. src/test/fizzle/cards/cabal_ritual_test.cljs
4. src/test/fizzle/cards/careful_study_test.cljs (uses custom mana - switch to opts)
5. src/test/fizzle/cards/cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs
6. src/test/fizzle/cards/city_of_traitors_test.cljs
7. src/test/fizzle/cards/dark_ritual_test.cljs
8. src/test/fizzle/cards/flash_of_insight_test.cljs
9. src/test/fizzle/cards/ill_gotten_gains_test.cljs
10. src/test/fizzle/cards/intuition_test.cljs (uses custom mana - switch to opts)
11. src/test/fizzle/cards/led_test.cljs
12. src/test/fizzle/cards/lotus_petal_test.cljs
13. src/test/fizzle/cards/mental_note_test.cljs
14. src/test/fizzle/cards/merchant_scroll_test.cljs
15. src/test/fizzle/cards/opt_test.cljs
16. src/test/fizzle/cards/orims_chant_test.cljs
17. src/test/fizzle/cards/polluted_delta_test.cljs
18. src/test/fizzle/cards/recoup_test.cljs
19. src/test/fizzle/cards/underground_river_test.cljs
20. src/test/fizzle/db/queries_test.cljs
21. src/test/fizzle/engine/cob_event_trigger_test.cljs
22. src/test/fizzle/engine/trigger_db_integration_test.cljs
23. src/test/fizzle/events/integration/mana_allocation_test.cljs
24. src/test/fizzle/events/pile_choice_test.cljs
25. src/test/fizzle/events/play_land_test.cljs
26. src/test/fizzle/events/selection/costs_allocation_test.cljs
27. src/test/fizzle/events/selection/storm_split_test.cljs
28. src/test/fizzle/events/tap_land_test.cljs
29. src/test/fizzle/events/tutor_multi_select_test.cljs

For each file:
a. Add [fizzle.test-helpers :as th] to requires
b. Remove local create-test-db, add-card-to-zone, add-cards-to-library, add-cards-to-graveyard, get-zone-count, get-object-zone, get-hand-count, add-opponent definitions
c. Replace calls: create-test-db -> th/create-test-db, add-card-to-zone -> th/add-card-to-zone, etc.
d. For files with custom mana (careful_study, intuition): change (create-test-db) to (th/create-test-db {:mana {:blue 1}})
e. For files with reversed return tuple order (deep_analysis, ray_of_revelation): update destructuring from [obj-id db] to [db obj-id]
f. Remove zone-specific helpers (add-artifact-to-battlefield, add-land-to-battlefield) — replace with (th/add-card-to-zone db card-id :battlefield player-id)
g. Preserve any file-specific helpers that are NOT duplicated elsewhere (e.g., helpers that set up specific game scenarios)
h. Run make validate after each batch of 5 files

### 5. Handle files using init-game-state (32 files)

These 32 files use fizzle.db.init/init-game-state instead of create-test-db. They are NOT migrated in this task because:
- init-game-state serves a different purpose (minimal engine-level setup)
- These files are mainly engine/* and events/* tests that don't need card definitions
- Consolidating init-game-state into test_helpers would be a SEPARATE decision

DO NOT touch these files. They remain as-is.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] src/test/fizzle/test_helpers.cljs exists with all 8 listed helpers
- [ ] src/test/fizzle/test_helpers_test.cljs exists with 11+ tests for helper behavior
- [ ] Zero test files define their own create-test-db (verify: grep -r "defn create-test-db" src/test/ returns 0 results)
- [ ] Zero test files define their own add-card-to-zone (verify: grep -r "defn add-card-to-zone" src/test/ returns 0 results, excluding test_helpers.cljs)
- [ ] Zero test files define their own add-cards-to-library (verify: grep same pattern)
- [ ] Zero test files define their own add-cards-to-graveyard (verify: grep same pattern)
- [ ] All return types consistent: [db result] tuple convention
- [ ] All existing tests pass identically (make validate green)
- [ ] No lint or format warnings (make lint && make fmt-check)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Files with custom mana in create-test-db**
- careful_study_test.cljs and intuition_test.cljs initialize with non-zero mana
- MUST use opts parameter: (th/create-test-db {:mana {:blue 1}})
- Verify these tests still pass with the parameterized version

**Edge Case: Return tuple order differences**
- deep_analysis_test.cljs and ray_of_revelation_test.cljs expect [obj-id db]
- Shared helper returns [db obj-id] — MUST update destructuring in these files
- Search for all let bindings that destructure add-card-to-zone results and verify order

**Edge Case: Zone-specific helpers**
- add-artifact-to-battlefield and add-land-to-battlefield are just add-card-to-zone with hardcoded :battlefield
- Replace with (th/add-card-to-zone db card-id :battlefield player-id)
- May need to update variable names in let bindings

**Edge Case: Library position handling**
- Standard add-card-to-zone does NOT set position for non-library zones
- deep_analysis variant DOES set position 0 for library zone
- Shared version MUST set :object/position 0 when zone is :library

**Edge Case: Invalid card-id passed to add-card-to-zone**
- If card-id doesn't match any card definition, d/q returns nil
- Shared helper should throw informative error: "Card not found: :invalid-card"
- Prevents silent nil entity creation that causes confusing test failures downstream

**Performance: Batch migration strategy**
- Migrate in batches of ~5 files, run make validate between batches
- If a batch fails, narrow to individual file to find the issue
- Keep git commits per batch so failures can be bisected

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in shared helpers (helpers must be card-agnostic)
- ❌ NO breaking existing test assertions during migration (pure extraction)
- ❌ NO changing test logic — only change helper imports and call sites
- ❌ NO mutable conn leaking from helpers (return immutable db values, not connections)
- ❌ NO hard-coded card IDs in shared helpers (always pass as parameters)
- ❌ NO silently returning nil when card-id is invalid (throw with message)
close_reason: Superseded: epic restructured from wave-based to linear execution model

title: Fill test coverage gaps (queries, cards, subscriptions)
description:
## Problem
Significant test coverage gaps exist, especially in foundational layers. Many recent bug fixes went in without regression tests.

## Critical Gaps (HIGH)
- Database queries: 0 tests for 19 query functions (get-hand, get-object, get-player-eid, stack-empty?, get-max-hand-size, etc.)
- Subscriptions: completely untested
- remove-object function: added in commit 46a281f with no test

## Untested Cards (9/25)
- Brain Freeze (the win condition!)
- Dark Ritual (foundation card)
- Cabal Ritual (threshold mechanic)
- City of Brass, Gemstone Mine, Ill-Gotten Gains
- 3 others

## Bug Fixes Without Regression Tests
Recent commits fixed selection clearing, storm copies, sorcery speed, peek-and-select. The cleanup step commit had good tests, but earlier fixes are uncovered.

## Success Criteria
- queries_test.cljs exists covering all 19 query functions
- Brain Freeze and Dark Ritual have integration tests
- remove-object has a unit test
- Regression tests for recent bug fixes (storm copies, sorcery speed, selection clearing)
- All new tests pass
close_reason: All coverage gaps filled. 4 tasks completed: queries_test (51 tests), remove-object + Dark/Cabal Ritual (14 tests), Brain Freeze + storm regression (10 tests), Underground River + IGG (10 tests). Total: 826 tests, 1826 assertions. All epic success criteria met.
