title: Extract game loop state machine from yield-impl
description: Extract the three concerns from yield-impl into a derived state machine in engine/game_loop.cljs. yield-impl becomes a thin orchestrator that negotiates priority, derives state, and dispatches to multimethod handlers.
design:
## Goal
Extract yield-impl's three post-priority concerns (stack resolution, phase advancement, bot action) into a derived state machine using multimethods. yield-impl shrinks to ~15-20 lines.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours (pure extraction refactor, no behavior change)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- events/game.cljs:719-845 — current yield-impl (4 concerns interleaved)
- events/game.cljs:658-708 — advance-with-stops (called by phase advancement path)
- events/game.cljs:860-889 — yield-loop (calls yield-impl in sync loop — must stay pure)
- events/game.cljs:711-717 — bot-turn? helper
- events/game.cljs:369-386 — resolve-one-item (called by stack resolution)
- events/game.cljs:1011-1025 — execute-bot-phase-action (called by bot phase)
- events/game.cljs:903-913 — resolve-one-and-stop (called by cast-and-yield)

### 2. Create engine/game_loop.cljs with TDD

**File requires:**
```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.game-loop
  (:require [fizzle.engine.priority :as priority]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as queries]
            [fizzle.bots.protocol :as bot]))
```

**State derivation:**
- derive-loop-state [game-db] — returns :stack-resolution, :bot-phase, or :phase-advancement
  - :stack-resolution when stack non-empty (regardless of whose turn — stack resolution always takes precedence)
  - :bot-phase when active player is bot AND stack empty
  - :phase-advancement when stack empty AND active player is human (or single player)

**Priority negotiation (extract from yield-impl lines 746-769):**
- negotiate-priority [app-db] — returns {:app-db updated-app-db, :all-passed? bool}
  - Gets priority holder eid, adds to passed set
  - Gets active-player-id, opponent-player-id
  - If opponent-player-id is nil (single player): one pass suffices, all-passed? = true
  - If auto-mode or bot-turn: opponent auto-passes
  - If opponent is bot (not bot-turn): query bot-priority-decision, auto-pass if :pass
  - If not all-passed: transfers priority to opponent, returns {:app-db, :all-passed? false}

**Multimethod:**
```clojure
(defmulti handle-loop-state (fn [state app-db opts] state))
```

Each handler returns {:app-db, :continue-yield?} consistent with current yield-impl contract.

**:stack-resolution handler (extract from yield-impl lines 773-792):**
- Calls resolve-one-item (passed as opt or via require)
- If pending-selection: clear auto-mode, return selection on app-db (no continue-yield?)
- If resolved + more stack items: {:continue-yield? true}
- If resolved + stack empty + auto-mode :resolving: clear auto-mode, call maybe-continue-cleanup
- If resolved + stack empty + no auto-mode: call maybe-continue-cleanup

**:bot-phase handler (extract from yield-impl lines 820-833):**
- Gets current phase from game state
- Gets bot archetype
- Calls execute-bot-phase-action (passed as opt or via require)
- Gets bot player eid, checks priority/check-stop (for human's opponent-turn stops)
- If has-stop AND not auto-mode: pause (no continue-yield?)
- Else: {:continue-yield? true}

**:phase-advancement handler (extract from yield-impl lines 794-843):**
- Gets F6 mode from auto-mode
- Calls advance-with-stops
- Detects turn boundary (active player changed)
- If pending-selection from advance-with-stops (cleanup discard): return it (no continue-yield?)
- If turn boundary crossed + new player is bot: {:continue-yield? true}
- If turn boundary crossed + new player is human + F6: clear auto-mode, {:continue-yield? true}
- If turn boundary crossed + new player is human: {:continue-yield? true}
- If same player + bot phase: SHOULD NOT HAPPEN (derive-loop-state handles this)
- If same player + F6 (hit end of turn boundary somehow): clear auto-mode
- If same player + normal (stop hit): return (no continue-yield?)

### 3. Refactor yield-impl to use game_loop

yield-impl becomes:
```clojure
(defn yield-impl [app-db]
  (let [result (game-loop/negotiate-priority app-db)]
    (if (:all-passed? result)
      (let [game-db (:game/db (:app-db result))
            gdb (priority/reset-passes game-db)
            state (game-loop/derive-loop-state gdb)]
        (game-loop/handle-loop-state state
          (assoc (:app-db result) :game/db gdb)
          {:resolve-one-item resolve-one-item
           :advance-with-stops advance-with-stops
           :execute-bot-phase-action execute-bot-phase-action
           :maybe-continue-cleanup maybe-continue-cleanup}))
      {:app-db (:app-db result)})))
```

### 4. Helper function placement (DECIDED)
resolve-one-item, advance-with-stops, execute-bot-phase-action, maybe-continue-cleanup STAY in events/game.cljs. They have dependencies on event-layer code (selection builders, cleanup logic). The handlers in game_loop.cljs receive them via the opts map to avoid circular dependencies between engine/ and events/.

Alternative: handlers could call these via dynamic dispatch or protocol, but opts map is simpler and explicit.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] derive-loop-state returns :stack-resolution when stack non-empty (even during bot turn)
- [ ] derive-loop-state returns :bot-phase when bot's turn and stack empty
- [ ] derive-loop-state returns :phase-advancement when human's turn and stack empty
- [ ] negotiate-priority auto-passes bot opponent in normal mode
- [ ] negotiate-priority auto-passes both in auto-mode (:resolving, :f6)
- [ ] negotiate-priority auto-passes both during bot turn
- [ ] negotiate-priority handles single-player (no opponent) — one pass suffices
- [ ] negotiate-priority transfers priority when not all passed
- [ ] :stack-resolution handler resolves one item and signals continue-yield? when more remain
- [ ] :stack-resolution handler clears :resolving auto-mode when stack empties
- [ ] :stack-resolution handler pauses with pending-selection when selection needed
- [ ] :bot-phase handler executes bot action and checks human's opponent-turn stops
- [ ] :bot-phase handler pauses when human has stop on current phase
- [ ] :phase-advancement handler advances to next stop on human turn
- [ ] :phase-advancement handler handles turn boundary → bot turn (continue-yield?)
- [ ] :phase-advancement handler handles turn boundary → human turn with F6 (clears auto-mode)
- [ ] :phase-advancement handler handles cleanup discard (returns pending-selection)
- [ ] yield-impl is ≤20 lines
- [ ] yield-loop still works — bot turns produce separate history entries per phase
- [ ] All 25 existing priority_test.cljs tests pass unchanged
- [ ] All existing tests pass (make validate clean)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Stack non-empty during bot turn**
derive-loop-state must return :stack-resolution, NOT :bot-phase. Stack resolution always takes precedence regardless of whose turn it is. This is critical for future burn bot where bot casts spells that go on the stack.
Test: set up bot turn with item on stack, verify derive-loop-state returns :stack-resolution.

**Edge Case: Single player (no opponent)**
negotiate-priority must handle nil opponent-player-id. Current yield-impl has this check at line 767-769. Must be preserved in extraction.
Test: create game with no opponent, call negotiate-priority, verify all-passed? is true after single pass.

**Edge Case: Cleanup discard during phase advancement**
advance-with-stops can return {:app-db with :game/pending-selection} when hand size exceeds max at cleanup. The :phase-advancement handler must check for this and return it WITHOUT continue-yield?.
Test: set up hand > 7 cards, advance to cleanup, verify pending-selection returned.

**Edge Case: maybe-continue-cleanup placement**
maybe-continue-cleanup (game.cljs:791) is called after stack empties in :stack-resolution. It checks if a cleanup discard was interrupted by stack resolution and needs to resume. Must be called in :stack-resolution handler when stack empties, NOT in :phase-advancement.

**Edge Case: F6 mode across turn boundary**
When F6 is active and turn boundary is crossed to human player, F6 must be cleared. When crossing to bot player, F6 stays (bot turn completes then F6 is relevant again for human).

**Edge Case: auto-mode :resolving clears only when stack empties**
:resolving auto-mode should only be cleared in :stack-resolution handler when stack empties. NOT when phase-advancement handler runs (stack is already empty at that point, but auto-mode might be :f6).

**Circular Dependency Prevention**
engine/game_loop.cljs must NOT require events/* — that would create circular deps. The handlers receive event-layer functions (resolve-one-item, etc.) via opts map. This is the same pattern as how engine/resolution.cljs is pure but called by events.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO behavior changes — this is a pure extraction refactor. Every codepath through yield-impl must produce identical results. If any existing test needs modification, the extraction changed behavior.
- ❌ NO circular dependencies between engine/ and events/ — game_loop.cljs must only require engine/* and db/* and bots/*
- ❌ NO moving resolve-one-item or advance-with-stops to engine/ — they have event-layer dependencies (selection builders, cleanup logic) that would create circular deps
- ❌ NO stored loop state — state is derived from game-db, not stored separately
- ❌ NO modifying existing test assertions — if tests fail, the refactor changed behavior
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 1: Datascript Schema and Initial Game State
design:
## Goal

Create the Datascript schema and initial game state with Dark Ritual in hand, providing the foundation for all engine functions.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Project Setup (if needed)

Configure shadow-cljs.edn for test build:
```clojure
;; shadow-cljs.edn - add test build if not present
{:source-paths ["src/main" "src/test"]
 :dependencies [[datascript "1.6.5"]
                [reagent "1.2.0"]]
 :builds
 {:app {...}
  :test {:target :node-test
         :output-to "out/test.js"
         :ns-regexp ".*-test$"
         :autorun true}}}
```

### 2. Study existing patterns

Reference design doc schema (fizzle-design.md lines 231-274):
- Card definitions with :card/id, :card/name, :card/effects
- Game objects with :object/id, :object/zone, :object/card
- Players with :player/id, :player/mana-pool, :player/storm-count
- Game state with :game/phase, :game/active-player

### 3. Write tests first (TDD)

```clojure
;; test/fizzle/db/schema_test.cljs

(ns fizzle.db.schema-test
  (:require [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
            [datascript.core :as d]
            [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
            [fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state]]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]))

;; Schema validity tests
(deftest schema-creates-valid-connection-test
  (testing "schema creates connection that accepts transactions"
    (let [conn (d/create-conn schema)]
      (is (some? conn))
      ;; Actually verify we can transact - not just that conn exists
      (d/transact! conn [{:player/id :test-player
                         :player/life 20}])
      (is (= 20 (-> @conn
                    (d/q '[:find ?life .
                           :where [?e :player/id :test-player]
                                  [?e :player/life ?life]])))))))

;; Initial state tests
(deftest initial-state-has-player-with-empty-mana-pool-test
  (testing "player mana pool starts with all zeros"
    (let [db (init-game-state)]
      (is (= {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
             (q/get-mana-pool db :player-1))))))

(deftest initial-state-has-dark-ritual-in-hand-test
  (testing "Dark Ritual is in player's hand"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)]
      (is (= 1 (count hand)))
      (is (= "Dark Ritual" (:card/name (first hand)))))))

(deftest initial-state-has-zero-storm-count-test
  (testing "storm count starts at 0"
    (let [db (init-game-state)]
      (is (= 0 (q/get-storm-count db :player-1))))))

;; Query edge case tests
(deftest get-mana-pool-nonexistent-player-test
  (testing "get-mana-pool returns nil for nonexistent player"
    (let [db (init-game-state)]
      (is (nil? (q/get-mana-pool db :nonexistent-player))))))

(deftest get-hand-empty-returns-empty-vector-test
  (testing "get-hand returns empty vector when no cards in hand"
    (let [conn (d/create-conn schema)]
      (d/transact! conn [{:player/id :empty-player
                         :player/life 20
                         :player/mana-pool {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 
                                            :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
                         :player/storm-count 0}])
      (is (= [] (q/get-hand @conn :empty-player))))))

(deftest get-card-by-object-id-test
  (testing "get-card retrieves card definition from game object"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          obj-id (:object/id (first hand))]
      (is (= "Dark Ritual" (:card/name (q/get-card db obj-id)))))))
```

### 4. Implementation checklist

- [ ] shadow-cljs.edn - Add :test build and datascript dependency
  - Target: :node-test for fast CLI testing
  - Dependencies: datascript 1.6.5
  
- [ ] src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs - Datascript schema
  ```clojure
  (def schema
    {;; Card definitions (templates)
     :card/id         {:db/unique :db.unique/identity}
     :card/name       {:db/index true}
     :card/cmc        {}
     :card/mana-cost  {}  ; {:black 1} for B cost
     :card/colors     {:db/cardinality :db.cardinality/many}
     :card/types      {:db/cardinality :db.cardinality/many}
     :card/text       {}
     :card/effects    {}  ; EDN vector of effect maps
     :card/keywords   {:db/cardinality :db.cardinality/many}
     
     ;; Game objects (instances of cards in a game)
     :object/id       {:db/unique :db.unique/identity}
     :object/card     {:db/valueType :db.type/ref}
     :object/zone     {}  ; :hand, :stack, :graveyard, :battlefield, :library, :exile
     :object/owner    {:db/valueType :db.type/ref}
     :object/controller {:db/valueType :db.type/ref}
     :object/tapped   {}
     
     ;; Players
     :player/id       {:db/unique :db.unique/identity}
     :player/name     {}
     :player/life     {}
     :player/mana-pool {}  ; {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
     :player/storm-count {}
     
     ;; Game state (singleton)
     :game/id         {:db/unique :db.unique/identity}
     :game/turn       {}
     :game/phase      {}  ; :main1, :main2, etc.
     :game/active-player {:db/valueType :db.type/ref}})
  ```

- [ ] src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Dark Ritual definition
  ```clojure
  (def dark-ritual
    {:card/id :dark-ritual
     :card/name "Dark Ritual"
     :card/cmc 1
     :card/mana-cost {:black 1}
     :card/colors #{:black}
     :card/types #{:instant}
     :card/text "Add {B}{B}{B}."
     :card/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana
                     :effect/mana {:black 3}}]})
  ```

- [ ] src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs - Query helpers
  ```clojure
  (defn get-mana-pool [db player-id]
    (d/q '[:find ?pool .
           :in $ ?pid
           :where [?e :player/id ?pid]
                  [?e :player/mana-pool ?pool]]
         db player-id))
  
  (defn get-hand [db player-id]
    (let [player-eid (d/q '[:find ?e .
                           :in $ ?pid
                           :where [?e :player/id ?pid]]
                         db player-id)]
      (when player-eid
        (->> (d/q '[:find [(pull ?obj [* {:object/card [*]}]) ...]
                   :in $ ?owner
                   :where [?obj :object/owner ?owner]
                          [?obj :object/zone :hand]]
                 db player-eid)
             (vec)))))
  
  (defn get-storm-count [db player-id]
    (d/q '[:find ?count .
           :in $ ?pid
           :where [?e :player/id ?pid]
                  [?e :player/storm-count ?count]]
         db player-id))
  
  (defn get-card [db object-id]
    (d/q '[:find (pull ?card [*]) .
           :in $ ?oid
           :where [?obj :object/id ?oid]
                  [?obj :object/card ?card]]
         db object-id))
  ```

- [ ] src/main/fizzle/db/init.cljs - Initial state creation
  ```clojure
  (defn init-game-state []
    (let [conn (d/create-conn schema)]
      ;; Transact card definition
      (d/transact! conn [dark-ritual])
      
      ;; Transact player
      (d/transact! conn [{:player/id :player-1
                         :player/name "Player"
                         :player/life 20
                         :player/mana-pool {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 
                                            :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
                         :player/storm-count 0}])
      
      ;; Get entity IDs for refs
      (let [player-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :where [?e :player/id :player-1]] @conn)
            card-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :where [?e :card/id :dark-ritual]] @conn)]
        
        ;; Transact game object (Dark Ritual in hand)
        (d/transact! conn [{:object/id (random-uuid)
                           :object/card card-eid
                           :object/zone :hand
                           :object/owner player-eid
                           :object/controller player-eid}])
        
        ;; Transact game state
        (d/transact! conn [{:game/id :game-1
                           :game/turn 1
                           :game/phase :main1
                           :game/active-player player-eid}]))
      
      ;; Return db value (immutable snapshot)
      @conn))
  ```

- [ ] test/fizzle/db/schema_test.cljs - Unit tests (see above)

### 5. Verification

```bash
# Run tests
npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js

# Or from REPL:
npx shadow-cljs cljs-repl app
(require '[fizzle.db.init :as init])
(def db (init/init-game-state))
(require '[fizzle.db.queries :as q])
(q/get-hand db :player-1)  ;; => [{:card/name "Dark Ritual" ...}]
(q/get-mana-pool db :player-1)  ;; => {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 ...}
(q/get-storm-count db :player-1)  ;; => 0
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `npx shadow-cljs compile test` completes without errors
- [ ] `node out/test.js` shows all 7 tests passing
- [ ] init-game-state returns Datascript db value (verified: `(d/db? db)` is true)
- [ ] `(q/get-hand db :player-1)` returns vector with one Dark Ritual object
- [ ] `(q/get-mana-pool db :player-1)` returns `{:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}`
- [ ] `(q/get-storm-count db :player-1)` returns `0`
- [ ] `(q/get-card db obj-id)` returns Dark Ritual card definition
- [ ] `(q/get-mana-pool db :nonexistent)` returns `nil` (edge case)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO hardcoded entity IDs (use queries to find entities by :db/unique attributes)
- ❌ NO mutable state outside Datascript conn (all state in one place)
- ❌ NO inline card definitions in init.cljs (cards live in cards/ namespace)
- ❌ NO ignoring nil returns from queries (handle missing entities gracefully)
- ❌ NO skipping tests for edge cases (nonexistent players, empty hands)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Nonexistent Player**
- Queries must handle player-id that doesn't exist
- Return nil or empty collection, don't throw
- Test: `get-mana-pool` with `:nonexistent-player` returns nil

**Edge Case: Empty Hand**
- `get-hand` should return empty vector `[]`, not nil
- Ensures consistent type for consumers
- Test: Player with no objects in hand returns `[]`

**Edge Case: Object Without Card Reference**
- Shouldn't happen in normal flow, but `get-card` should handle
- Return nil if object has no :object/card ref
- Defensive programming for corrupted state

**Schema Evolution**
- Datascript schema is additive - can add attributes later
- Don't worry about getting every attribute perfect now
- Focus on attributes needed for Dark Ritual flow

**Datascript Quirks**
- `d/q` returns nil for scalar queries (`. `) when no match
- `d/q` returns empty set `#{}` for collection queries when no match
- Always use appropriate return type hint in query

**Reference Implementation**
- Datascript docs: https://github.com/tonsky/datascript
- Design doc schema: fizzle-design.md lines 231-274
close_reason: All tests passing, committed

title: Add GitHub Actions deploy workflow
description: Create .github/workflows/deploy.yml that builds and deploys to GitHub Pages on push to main. Uses modern actions/deploy-pages artifact pattern with two-job workflow (build + deploy). Requires Java (shadow-cljs JVM) and Node.js.
design:
## Goal
Create a GitHub Actions workflow that automatically builds and deploys Fizzle to GitHub Pages on every push to main.

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours

## Implementation

1. Create directory: .github/workflows/

2. Create .github/workflows/deploy.yml with two-job pattern:

   **Build job:**
   - Trigger: push to main branch
   - actions/checkout@v4
   - actions/setup-java@v4 (distribution: temurin, java-version: 21) — shadow-cljs requires JVM
   - actions/setup-node@v4 (node-version: 20, cache: npm)
   - npm ci
   - make release (chains: clean → build-css → shadow-cljs release app)
   - actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3 with path: resources/public

   **Deploy job:**
   - needs: build
   - actions/deploy-pages@v4
   - Environment: github-pages
   - Outputs page_url

   **Permissions (top-level):**
   - pages: write
   - id-token: write
   - contents: read

3. Verify YAML syntax: yamllint or manual review
4. Verify workflow triggers correctly by examining structure

## Success Criteria
- [ ] .github/workflows/deploy.yml exists and is valid YAML
- [ ] Workflow triggers on push to main only
- [ ] Build job sets up Java 21 (temurin) + Node 20 with npm cache
- [ ] Build job runs npm ci then make release
- [ ] Build job uploads resources/public/ as pages artifact
- [ ] Deploy job uses actions/deploy-pages@v4 with github-pages environment
- [ ] No hardcoded usernames or repo names anywhere in workflow
- [ ] Permissions include pages: write, id-token: write, contents: read
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (make test)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO gh-pages branch deployment (use actions/deploy-pages artifact pattern)
- ❌ NO hardcoded username or repo name (must work for any fork)
- ❌ NO test/lint steps in this workflow (out of scope per epic — separate concern)
- ❌ NO single-job pattern (two-job build+deploy is the recommended GitHub Pages pattern)
- ❌ NO manual npm install instead of npm ci (ci is correct for CI environments)
- ❌ NO skipping Java setup (shadow-cljs requires JVM even though npm package exists)

## Key Considerations

**GitHub Pages artifact pattern:**
- actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3 creates a tar archive of the specified path
- actions/deploy-pages@v4 consumes that artifact and deploys it
- The deploy job MUST have permissions: pages: write, id-token: write
- The deploy job MUST specify environment: name: github-pages

**Java requirement:**
- shadow-cljs is a ClojureScript compiler that runs on the JVM
- The npm package (shadow-cljs) is a launcher that invokes the JVM
- Without Java, npm ci succeeds but npx shadow-cljs release app fails
- Use temurin distribution (Eclipse Adoptium, widely available)

**npm ci vs npm install:**
- npm ci is deterministic (uses package-lock.json exactly)
- npm ci is faster in CI (skips resolution step)
- npm ci requires package-lock.json to exist (it does)

**Workflow dispatch:**
- Include workflow_dispatch trigger for manual re-deploys
- This allows deploying without a code change if needed
close_reason: Complete — commit 44d06a9. GitHub Actions deploy workflow created. Two-job pattern, no hardcoded values, all tests pass.

title: Task 6: Add graveyard view with threshold indicator
design:
## Goal
Implement graveyard view component showing all cards in graveyard zone with threshold tracking.

## Context
Epic fizzle-6vv requires graveyard visibility for threshold tracking. Cards like Cabal Ritual check threshold (7+ cards in graveyard). Player needs to see graveyard contents and count.

## Implementation
1. Add subscription for graveyard contents (query :graveyard zone)
2. Add graveyard-view component to core.cljs showing:
   - List of card names in graveyard
   - Card count (e.g., 'Graveyard (5)')
   - Threshold indicator when 7+ cards present
3. Add to app layout between controls and mana pool

## Verification Commands
- make test
- make validate

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Graveyard view shows all cards in graveyard zone
- [ ] Card count displayed
- [ ] Threshold indicator appears when 7+ cards
- [ ] make validate passes
close_reason: Implemented graveyard view with threshold indicator

title: Modal card tests assign chosen-mode directly via d/db-with
description:
## Problem

Blue Elemental Blast, Hydroblast, and Red Elemental Blast tests assign :object/chosen-mode directly via d/db-with instead of going through the modal selection system (cast-spell-handler → mode selection → confirm). This bypasses:
- Mode presentation logic (which modes are available given game state)
- Mode validation (is this mode legal?)
- Mode → targeting chain (some modes have targeting, some don't)

## Risk: Low

Mode selection is shared infrastructure tested in selection tests. The modal cards all use established patterns. But if a modal card had a mode definition that didn't match what the selection system expects, the per-card tests wouldn't catch it.

## Affected files
- blue_elemental_blast_test.cljs
- hydroblast_test.cljs
- red_elemental_blast_test.cljs (likely same pattern)

## Fix

Add at least one happy-path test per modal card that goes through the production cast-spell-handler flow including mode selection.
close_reason: Fixed: happy-path tests now use production-path helpers (th/cast-with-target, th/resolve-top, th/confirm-selection, th/cast-mode-with-target)

title: Architecture Debt Cleanup: Decomposition, ADR Drift, and Bug Fixes
description:
Clean up accumulated architecture debt across 5 work items:

1. **ADR-003 Query Relocation** (fizzle-fmq5): Move stack/priority queries from engine to db/queries, eliminating undeclared Practice Platform → Interpretation Core dependency in history/interceptor.cljs and history/descriptions.cljs.

2. **Decompose events/game.cljs** (1,347 → ~710 lines): Extract 5 modules — init.cljs (game setup), ui.cljs (toggles/stops), cleanup.cljs (cleanup step), phases.cljs (turn management), lands.cljs (land play + tapping). game.cljs retains priority/yield orchestration, spell casting pipeline, resolution events.

3. **Decompose engine/effects.cljs** (978 → 7 files): Split into effects/ directory — core.cljs (multimethod base), zones.cljs (zone ops), life.cljs (life/damage), grants.cljs (grants/counters), stack.cljs (counter/chain), selection.cljs (deferred stubs), simple.cljs (mana/info).

4. **Fix opponent SBA checking** (fizzle-4nel): State-based actions not checked after bot events — opponent doesn't lose from empty library draw. Root cause: SBA interceptor only fires on human-triggered events.

5. **Fix branch switching** (fizzle-30m0): Clicking branches in history panel has no effect. Event handlers look correct; likely subscription or view rendering issue.

**Immutable requirements:**
- All tests pass after each extraction (behavior-preserving refactoring)
- No new dependencies introduced — extractions only move existing code
- ADR-003 violation eliminated
- effects.cljs multimethod dispatch continues via require chain
- SBA fix covers all bot-triggered events
close_reason: Closed

title: Sideboard Cards: Tormod's Crypt, Chain of Vapor, Crumble, Duress, Abeyance
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Implement 5 sideboard cards with full oracle-accurate text: Abeyance, Tormod's Crypt, Crumble, Duress, Chain of Vapor
- All cards must pass Scryfall oracle text validation via implement-card skill
- Each card must have a dedicated test file following mandatory test categories (definition, cast-resolve, cannot-cast, storm count, edge cases)
- Migrate all targeting/selection criteria from :card/types to :match/types namespace
- Add :match/not-types support for negative type filtering (future-proof for new card types like planeswalkers, handles man-lands correctly)
- Abeyance must restrict both instant/sorcery casting AND non-mana activated abilities
- Duress must show the full opponent hand (all cards visible) with only noncreature/nonland cards selectable
- Chain of Vapor must implement the full chain mechanic (controller may sacrifice a land to copy, with new target)
- Crumble must compute life gain from the destroyed artifact's CMC
- Tormod's Crypt is a 0-cost artifact permanent with activated ability (tap + sacrifice)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] All 5 cards implemented with oracle-accurate text
- [ ] Criteria namespace migrated: all :card/types in targeting criteria → :match/types
- [ ] :match/not-types exclusion working and tested
- [ ] 6 new engine capabilities working: type-specific restriction, ability restriction, :exile-zone, :bounce, computed effect amounts, hand-reveal selection, chain/copy mechanic
- [ ] Each card has dedicated test file with minimum test counts per CLAUDE.md
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (no regressions from criteria migration)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO enumerating permanent types to mean nonland — use :match/not-types #{:land} (future-proof: breaks when planeswalkers added, wrong for man-lands)
- ❌ NO mixing :card/ namespace keys with :match/ namespace keys in criteria maps (consistency: criteria is a match spec, not card data)
- ❌ NO hardcoded card names in engine code — all card-specific behavior must be in card data/effects (data over code: engine interprets data)
- ❌ NO mocking the engine in card tests — test through production code paths: rules/cast-spell, abilities (testing anti-pattern: test real behavior)
- ❌ NO tautological assertions like (is (some? x)) — use exact value checks (testing requirement from CLAUDE.md)
- ❌ NO skipping the chain mechanic on Chain of Vapor — full oracle text implementation required
- ❌ NO showing only valid targets for Duress — must show full hand with selectable/non-selectable distinction

## Approach
Incremental card-by-card implementation, ordered from simplest to most complex engine additions:
1. Migrate criteria to :match/ namespace + add :match/not-types support (foundation)
2. Abeyance — introduces type-specific restriction + ability activation restriction
3. Tormod's Crypt — introduces :exile-zone effect type
4. Crumble — introduces computed effect amounts
5. Duress — introduces hand-reveal selection type
6. Chain of Vapor — introduces :bounce effect + chain/copy mechanic

Each card builds on prior engine work. TDD throughout: failing tests first, then implementation.

## Architecture

### Engine Additions
1. **Criteria namespace migration** — db/queries.cljs matches-criteria? updated to read :match/types and :match/not-types. All existing card targeting criteria migrated.
2. **Type-specific restriction** — New restriction type :cannot-cast-instants-sorceries in engine/effects.cljs. Rules engine can-cast? checks card types against restriction.
3. **Ability activation restriction** — New restriction type :cannot-activate-non-mana-abilities. Ability activation path checks restrictions, exempts mana abilities.
4. **:exile-zone effect** — New effect type in engine/effects.cljs. Takes target player + zone, moves all objects to exile.
5. **:bounce effect** — New effect type. Returns target permanent from battlefield to owner's hand via zones/move-to-zone.
6. **Computed effect amounts** — Effects can reference target properties (e.g., :effect/amount-from :target-cmc for Crumble's life gain).
7. **Hand-reveal selection** — New selection type with :selection/visible-objects (full hand) and :selection/selectable-objects (filtered by :match/ criteria).
8. **Chain/copy mechanic** — Post-resolution selection: controller may sacrifice a land to create a copy of the spell on the stack with new target.

### Card Files
- cards/white/abeyance.cljs
- cards/artifacts/tormods_crypt.cljs
- cards/green/crumble.cljs
- cards/black/duress.cljs
- cards/blue/chain_of_vapor.cljs
- cards/registry.cljs (updated with new requires)

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The card pool (47 cards) supports only one deck (Iggy Pop storm). Sideboard cards are needed for realistic practice: protection (Abeyance, Duress), removal (Chain of Vapor, Crumble), and graveyard hate (Tormod's Crypt). These also stress-test the engine with new mechanics.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- db/queries.cljs:250-267 — matches-criteria? uses :card/types for OR-based type matching
- engine/effects.cljs — 19 effect types, all dispatched via multimethod on :effect/type
- cards/white/orims_chant.cljs — existing :add-restriction with :cannot-cast-spells pattern
- cards/white/seal_of_cleansing.cljs — enchantment permanent with activated sac ability pattern
- cards/artifacts/lotus_petal.cljs — artifact permanent with mana ability pattern
- events/selection/ — 6 modules handling player choices, all caster-driven currently

**External:**
- Scryfall oracle text for all 5 cards verified
- Abeyance restricts BOTH spell casting (instants/sorceries) AND non-mana activated abilities

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Incremental card-by-card ✓
**What it is:** Implement cards from simplest to most complex, building engine features as each card needs them.
**Chosen because:** Natural TDD flow, always-green tests, each card adds exactly one new engine concept. Matches project's data-driven card pattern.

#### 2. Engine-first batch ❌
**What it is:** Build all 6 engine capabilities first, then implement all 5 cards.
**Why explored:** Clean layer separation appealing.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Building engine features speculatively without card consumers to test against. Harder to validate correctness.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Engine features have consumers beyond these 5 cards.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- 5 specific sideboard cards with full oracle text
- Engine additions needed by these cards
- Criteria namespace migration to :match/
- Test files for each card

**Out of scope:**
- Other sideboard cards beyond these 5
- Creature/combat support (separate epic fizzle-u07p)
- UI changes for hand-reveal (use existing selection UI patterns)
- Bot AI for playing these cards (bot rules are separate)

### Open Questions
- Exact computed-amount key name (:effect/amount-from vs :effect/amount-fn vs :effect/computed-amount)
- Whether chain copies should be full stack items or simplified
- Handling when Duress target has no valid cards (creatures + lands only) — should resolve with no effect

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Chain of Vapor scope? | Full chain mechanic | Need copy/sac-land post-resolution selection |
| Duress scope? | Full hand reveal | Need visible + selectable distinction in selection |
| Abeyance abilities? | Restrict non-mana abilities too | Need ability activation restriction in engine |
| Nonland targeting? | Use :match/not-types exclusion | Future-proof for new card types, handles man-lands |
| Criteria namespace? | :match/ for all filter specs | Migrate existing :card/types in criteria to :match/types |
| Implementation order? | Incremental card-by-card | Abeyance → Tormod's Crypt → Crumble → Duress → Chain of Vapor |

### Research Deep-Dives
#### Criteria Namespace Design
**Question explored:** How to represent nonland permanent in targeting without enumeration?
**Sources:** Existing matches-criteria? in db/queries.cljs, Rich Hickey design principles
**Findings:** Enumerating #{:artifact :creature :enchantment} breaks for planeswalkers and man-lands. :match/ namespace separates filter specs from card data. Scales to CMC, power, color criteria.
**Conclusion:** :match/types + :match/not-types under dedicated namespace

#### Restriction System Scope
**Question explored:** What does Abeyance actually restrict?
**Sources:** Scryfall oracle text, existing Orim's Chant implementation
**Findings:** Abeyance restricts instants/sorceries AND non-mana activated abilities. Current :cannot-cast-spells is too broad. Need type-specific + ability-specific restrictions.
**Conclusion:** Two new restriction types, ability activation must also check restrictions

### Dead-End Paths
#### Positive type enumeration for nonland
**Why explored:** Simplest approach — list all non-land types in :card/types
**Why abandoned:** Breaks when new card types added (planeswalkers), wrong for man-lands. User caught this design flaw.

### Open Concerns Raised
- Man-lands (Mishra's Factory) — :match/not-types #{:land} correctly handles because land type present regardless of creature status
- Future card types (planeswalkers) — :match/not-types approach is forward-compatible
close_reason: All 5 sideboard cards implemented with full oracle text, criteria namespace migrated, 6 engine capabilities added. All children closed.

title: Spec Phase 2: Selection map multi-spec with chokepoint validation
description: Add cljs.spec for selection maps with multi-spec on :selection/type. Validate at event handler chokepoint (assoc :game/pending-selection). 21 types consolidatable to ~4 patterns. Phase 2 of spec adoption.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Multi-spec dispatching on :selection/type for all concrete selection types (~31)
- Base spec requires: :selection/type, :selection/lifecycle, :selection/selected, :selection/validation, :selection/player-id
- Per-type specs add type-specific required keys (e.g., :selection/candidate-ids for zone-pick types, :selection/allocation for mana-allocation)
- set-pending-selection helper function validates via spec (dev-only via goog.DEBUG) then assocs onto app-db
- Makefile lint rule prevents direct assoc :game/pending-selection outside spec.cljs
- All 12 existing setter sites across 7 files migrated to use set-pending-selection
- All existing card tests pass unchanged (builders already produce valid maps)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Multi-spec for ::selection dispatches on :selection/type
- [ ] All ~31 selection types have individual defmethods (one per type, no hierarchy reuse)
- [ ] set-pending-selection helper validates in dev, assocs onto app-db
- [ ] Makefile lint rule catches direct assoc :game/pending-selection outside spec.cljs
- [ ] All 12 setter sites migrated to set-pending-selection
- [ ] Chokepoint catches: missing :selection/lifecycle, wrong type for :selection/selected, missing type-specific keys
- [ ] All existing card tests pass (builders produce valid selections)
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] Generative: (s/exercise ::selection) produces valid selection maps

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO changing builder output shapes to fit spec (spec fits the code, not vice versa)
- NO runtime validation in production (dev-only via goog.DEBUG — selection validation is per-action, not per-load)
- NO re-frame interceptors for validation (bug factories — use pure helper function)
- NO hierarchy-aware spec dispatch (one defmethod per concrete type, hierarchy question deferred to fizzle-hy7z)
- NO coupling spec to view layer (spec validates data, not UI)

## Approach
New file: events/selection/spec.cljs. One defmulti selection-type-spec dispatching on :selection/type, one defmethod per concrete executor type (~31). set-pending-selection helper function validates via s/valid? under goog.DEBUG, then assocs :game/pending-selection onto app-db. Makefile lint rule (grep) prevents direct assoc :game/pending-selection outside spec.cljs. All 12 setter sites across 7 files migrated to call set-pending-selection.

## Architecture
- events/selection/spec.cljs — NEW: multi-spec + set-pending-selection helper
- Makefile — ADD: lint rule for direct pending-selection assoc
- events/casting.cljs (2 sites), events/cleanup.cljs (1), events/director.cljs (3), events/selection/core.cljs (2), events/resolution.cljs (2), events/abilities.cljs (1), events/phases.cljs (1) — MIGRATE: use set-pending-selection

## Design Rationale
### Problem
15+ builders produce selection maps with no contract validation. Historic bugs: missing :selection/card-source (3d33f63), vector vs set for candidates (c6e1003). 12 setter sites across 7 files can grow without enforcement.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- card_spec.cljs:124-405 — Phase 1 pattern: defmulti effect-type-spec, 29 defmethods, s/multi-spec wrapper
- events/selection/core.cljs:31-64 — selection-hierarchy with derive (zone-pick, accumulator, reorder, chain groups)
- 31 defmethod execute-confirmed-selection across selection/*.cljs, casting.cljs, abilities.cljs
- 12 assoc :game/pending-selection sites across 7 files (casting, cleanup, director, selection/core, resolution, abilities, phases)
- q-safe/pull-safe pattern — precedent for helper + Makefile lint rule enforcement

**External:** None needed — pure internal spec work following established Phase 1 pattern

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Helper function + Makefile lint ✓
**What it is:** Pure function set-pending-selection validates via spec in dev, assocs onto app-db. Makefile grep rule prevents bypassing. Replace all 12 direct assoc sites.
**Chosen because:** Matches q-safe/pull-safe precedent. Pure function, no control flow changes. Mechanical migration of 12 call sites. Lint rule prevents growth.

#### 2. Re-frame interceptor ❌
**Why we looked at this:** Cross-cutting validation without changing call sites.
**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Interceptors are bug factories (direct user feedback). Hidden validation, hard to debug.
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Never.

#### 3. Single re-frame event handler ❌
**Why we looked at this:** Centralizes all selection setting into one event.
**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Introduces async indirection. Current setter sites are in synchronous reg-event-db handlers that compute app-db transformations. Restructuring control flow in all 7 files is too much blast radius for a validation feature.
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Selection setting is already being refactored to event-based architecture for other reasons.

#### 4. Hierarchy-aware spec dispatch ❌
**Why we looked at this:** Reuse selection-hierarchy for spec multimethod. ~8 parent defmethods instead of ~31.
**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Only zone-pick children (3 types) truly share identical key shapes. Accumulator and reorder children diverge. Net savings ~2 fewer defmethods. Phase 1 consistency (one-per-type) wins. Hierarchy's long-term value is separately questioned (fizzle-hy7z).
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** fizzle-hy7z concludes hierarchy should be deepened.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** Selection multi-spec, set-pending-selection helper, Makefile lint, 12 call-site migration
**Out of scope:** Effect specs (Phase 1 done), stack items (Phase 3), hierarchy refactoring (fizzle-hy7z), test helper rewrite

### Open Questions
- Exact base keys vs per-type keys will be determined by reading each builder during implementation
- Whether set-pending-selection should log the spec explain-str or just console.error

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Chokepoint strategy? | Helper function + Makefile lint | No interceptors, no event handlers. Pure fn + grep enforcement |
| Hierarchy-aware specs? | No — one defmethod per type | Consistency with Phase 1, no hidden inheritance |
| Hierarchy long-term value? | Defer to separate investigation | Created fizzle-hy7z (P4) |

### Dead-End Paths
#### Re-frame interceptor
**Why explored:** Cross-cutting validation without call-site changes
**Why abandoned:** User feedback: interceptors are bug factories

#### Hierarchy-aware spec dispatch
**Why explored:** Reduce defmethod count by reusing selection-hierarchy
**Why abandoned:** Only zone-pick children share shape; accumulator/reorder diverge; net savings minimal

### Open Concerns Raised
- Selection hierarchy long-term value → Deferred to fizzle-hy7z (P4 backlog)
close_reason: All criteria met. 3 commits: a12a827 (spec+helper+lint), 48e17fd (13 site migration), c71fed1 (test fixes). Reviewer APPROVED.

title: Strengthen YELLOW tests with weak assertions
design:
## Goal
Replace weak assertions with meaningful ones that catch real bugs.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Tests to Strengthen

### 1. move-to-zone-same-zone-no-op-test (zones_test.cljs:48-55)
**Current:** Verifies zone and count only
**Issue:** Doesn't verify `db = db'` - could modify other state silently
**Upgrade:** Add `(is (= db db') "Same-zone move should be exact no-op")`
**File:** src/test/fizzle/engine/zones_test.cljs

### 2. test-add-counters-to-permanent (effects_test.cljs)
**Current:** Only tests adding new counters to object with no counters
**Issue:** No edge case for adding to object that already has counters
**Upgrade:** Add test case where object already has 2 counters, add 3 more, verify 5 total
**File:** src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs

### 3. Threshold boundary regression (conditions_test.cljs)
**Current:** Tests 0, 6, 7, 10+ cards in graveyard
**Issue:** No test for crossing the boundary (7 -> 6 via removal)
**Upgrade:** Add test: start with 7 cards (threshold=true), remove 1, verify threshold=false
**File:** src/test/fizzle/engine/conditions_test.cljs

### 4. Helper function validation
**Issue:** Many tests use `add-cards-to-library`, `add-card-to-zone` helpers. If helper buggy, all tests using it have false confidence.
**Upgrade:** Add sanity check at top of test files that helpers work:
```clojure
(deftest test-helper-add-card-to-zone-works
  (testing "Sanity check: helper adds card to correct zone"
    (let [db (create-test-db)
          [db' obj-id] (add-card-to-zone db :dark-ritual :hand :player-1)]
      (is (= :hand (get-object-zone db' obj-id))))))
```

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read zones_test.cljs:48-55 and add db equality assertion
- [ ] Find add-counters test in effects_test.cljs and add cumulative counter case
- [ ] Add threshold boundary regression test to conditions_test.cljs
- [ ] Add helper validation test to merchant_scroll_test.cljs (uses most helpers)
- [ ] Run `make test` after each change
- [ ] Run `make validate` at the end

## Success Criteria
- [ ] zones_test.cljs: move-to-zone-same-zone-no-op-test verifies `(= db db')`
- [ ] effects_test.cljs: counter test includes cumulative add case
- [ ] conditions_test.cljs: new test `test-threshold-boundary-regression` exists and passes
- [ ] At least one test file has helper sanity check
- [ ] `make test` passes
- [ ] `make validate` passes

## Key Considerations

**db Equality in ClojureScript:**
Datascript databases are immutable and support `=` comparison. Adding `(is (= db db'))` works directly.

**Threshold Regression Test Structure:**
```clojure
(deftest test-threshold-boundary-regression
  (testing "threshold? changes from true to false when card removed"
    (let [db-7 (-> (init-game-state)
                   (add-cards-to-graveyard :player-1 7))
          _ (is (true? (conditions/threshold? db-7 :player-1))
                "Precondition: should have threshold with 7 cards")
          ;; Remove 1 card by moving to exile
          gy-card (first (q/get-graveyard db-7 :player-1))
          db-6 (zones/move-to-zone db-7 (:object/id gy-card) :exile)]
      (is (= 6 (count (q/get-graveyard db-6 :player-1)))
          "Should have 6 cards after removal")
      (is (false? (conditions/threshold? db-6 :player-1))
          "Should lose threshold when dropping below 7"))))
```

**Counter Edge Case:**
Storm counts are tracked in mana_pool, not counters. Focus on :loyalty-counters or similar if implemented.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Replacing `!= nil` with `!= undefined` (still weak)
- ❌ Adding edge cases without meaningful assertions
- ❌ Weakening tests to make them pass
- ❌ Testing helper functions MORE than production code
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement Lightning Bolt card definition
design:
## Goal
Implement Lightning Bolt as the first targeted damage spell. Validates :deal-damage + stored-targets pipeline end-to-end.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (card definition + 10-12 tests)

## Implementation

### 1. Verify against Scryfall
- Lightning Bolt: R, Instant, 'Lightning Bolt deals 3 damage to any target.'
- CMC: 1, Colors: Red (\#{:red}), Types: Instant (\#{:instant})

### 2. Card definition: src/main/fizzle/cards/lightning_bolt.cljs

Follow seal_of_cleansing.cljs pattern. Key structure:

```clojure
{:card/id :lightning-bolt
 :card/name "Lightning Bolt"
 :card/cmc 1
 :card/mana-cost {:red 1}
 :card/colors \#{:red}
 :card/types \#{:instant}
 :card/text "Lightning Bolt deals 3 damage to any target."
 :card/targeting [{:target/id :target
                   :target/type :player
                   :target/options \#{:any-player}
                   :target/required true}]
 :card/effects [{:effect/type :deal-damage
                 :effect/amount 3
                 :effect/target-ref :target}]}
```

Note: :target/type is :player (not :any-target) because creatures don't exist yet. When creatures arrive in Epic 5, update to support object targeting too. The :target/id :target will be used as the key in stored-targets map.

### 3. Register in cards/iggy_pop.cljs
- Add require: [fizzle.cards.lightning-bolt :as lightning-bolt]
- Add to all-cards vector: lightning-bolt/lightning-bolt

### 4. Tests: src/test/fizzle/cards/lightning_bolt_test.cljs

Reference orims_chant_test.cljs for the cast-time targeting test pattern:
- Use th/add-opponent to create second player
- Build selection manually: {:selection/type :cast-time-targeting, :selection/player-id, :selection/object-id, :selection/mode, :selection/target-requirement, :selection/selected \#{target}}
- Call sel-targeting/confirm-cast-time-target to cast with stored target
- Resolve via game/resolve-one-item

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Card definition matches Scryfall oracle text exactly (all 8 fields verified in test)
- [ ] 10+ tests covering categories A-F
- [ ] Cast+resolve via production path: sel-targeting/confirm-cast-time-target → game/resolve-one-item
- [ ] Stored targets on stack-item: {:target :player-2} verified in test
- [ ] :deal-damage reduces opponent life by 3 (20 → 17)
- [ ] :game/loss-condition set to :life-zero when life reaches 0
- [ ] Card registered in all-cards (card-spec validation passes)
- [ ] make validate passes (0 lint warnings, format clean, all tests green)

## Test Specifications

### A. Card definition (catches: field drift, wrong values)
- test-lightning-bolt-card-definition: verify :card/id :lightning-bolt, :card/name, :card/cmc 1, :card/mana-cost {:red 1}, :card/colors \#{:red}, :card/types \#{:instant}, :card/text exact match
- test-lightning-bolt-targeting-structure: verify :card/targeting vector with :target/type :player, :target/options \#{:any-player}, :target/required true, :target/id :target

### B. Cast-resolve happy path (catches: broken cast pipeline, effect not applied)
- test-cast-bolt-targeting-opponent-deals-3-damage: create db with opponent (th/add-opponent), add bolt to hand, add {:red 1} mana, build selection targeting :player-2, confirm-cast-time-target, verify on stack, resolve-one-item, assert opponent life = 17 (was 20)
- test-cast-bolt-targeting-self-deals-3-damage: same flow but target :player-1, assert own life = 17

### C. Cannot-cast guards (catches: casting without resources)
- test-cannot-cast-without-red-mana: add bolt to hand, no mana, assert (false? (rules/can-cast? db :player-1 obj-id))
- test-cannot-cast-from-graveyard: add bolt to graveyard, add {:red 1}, assert can-cast? false
- test-cannot-cast-from-battlefield: add bolt to battlefield, assert can-cast? false

### D. Storm count (catches: storm tracking regression)
- test-casting-bolt-increments-storm-count: add bolt to hand, add mana, cast via confirm-cast-time-target, assert storm count = 1

### E. Targeting (catches: targeting system integration bugs)
- test-no-valid-targets-without-opponent: create db WITHOUT th/add-opponent, add bolt to hand, add mana, check targeting/has-valid-targets? with player targeting — should still have :player-1 as valid target (:any-player includes self)
- test-find-valid-targets-returns-both-players: create db with opponent, check targeting/find-valid-targets returns [:player-1 :player-2] (or equivalent set)

### F. Edge cases (catches: life boundary bugs, game-end detection)
- test-bolt-kills-at-exactly-3-life: set opponent life to 3, cast+resolve bolt, assert life = 0, assert :game/loss-condition = :life-zero
- test-bolt-overkills-below-zero: set opponent life to 1, cast+resolve bolt, assert life = -2 (no clamping)
- test-bolt-on-already-dead-opponent: set opponent life to 0 (loss-condition already set), cast+resolve bolt, assert life = -3 (still deals damage)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Targeting structure must match resolve-target-for-effect expectations:**
- :effect/target-ref :target in the effect must match :target/id :target in targeting
- Stored targets format: {:target selected-player-id} on :stack-item/targets
- resolve-target-for-effect (stack.cljs:80-105) looks up :effect/target-ref in stored-targets

**Test setup for targeted spells (reference: orims_chant_test.cljs:222-257):**
- Must call th/add-opponent to create second player
- Must manually build selection map (no UI in tests)
- Must use rules/get-casting-modes to get the mode for confirm-cast-time-target
- confirm-cast-time-target stores targets and puts spell on stack in one call

**Life tracking for game-end:**
- :deal-damage effect (effects.cljs:238-259) already handles life-zero detection
- Life can go negative (no clamping) — test this explicitly
- :game/loss-condition :life-zero is set when life <= 0

**Effect structure differs from Orim's Chant:**
- Orim's Chant uses {:player :player-2} in stored-targets (target-id is :player)
- Lightning Bolt should use {:target :player-2} (target-id is :target)
- The target-id in targeting requirement MUST match the key in stored-targets

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO tautological assertions like (is (some? card)) — verify exact values (e.g., (is (= :lightning-bolt (:card/id card))))
- ❌ NO reimplementing cast/resolve logic in tests — use rules/cast-spell, confirm-cast-time-target, resolve-one-item
- ❌ NO hardcoded player-ids in card definition — effects use :effect/target-ref which resolves at runtime
- ❌ NO skipping th/add-opponent for targeting tests — targeting needs a second player to be meaningful
close_reason: Closed
