Tests deleted (not replaced) since they tested Clojure language features, not production code.

title: Cards: Hurkyl's Recall, Vendetta, Vision Charm
description: Implement three new cards requiring shared infrastructure and two new engine mechanics (phasing, land type change).
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Hurkyl's Recall ({1}{U} Instant): Return all artifacts target player controls to their hand
- Vendetta ({B} Instant): Destroy target nonblack creature; caster loses life equal to target's toughness
- Vision Charm ({U} Instant): Modal — (1) target player mills 4; (2) change land types until EOT; (3) target artifact phases out
- All three cards oracle-accurate per Scryfall
- All three cards have full test suites per CLAUDE.md testing requirements

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Hurkyl's Recall: bounces all artifacts controlled by target player via new :bounce-all effect
- [ ] Vendetta: targets nonblack creatures via new :match/not-colors criteria; loses life equal to toughness
- [ ] Vision Charm Mode 1: mills 4 from target player (existing :mill)
- [ ] Vision Charm Mode 2: interactive land type selection, grant-based type override, expires at EOT
- [ ] Vision Charm Mode 3: phases out target artifact via direct zone change; phases in during untap step
- [ ] Phase-in does NOT trigger ETB (direct zone change, not move-to-zone)
- [ ] Phase-in does NOT reset tapped state, creature fields, or triggers
- [ ] All cards registered in registry.cljs
- [ ] All test suites pass (min 5 per simple card, 8+ per modal/targeted)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO using move-to-zone for phase out/in (would reset tapped state, retract triggers, clear creature fields — use direct Datascript zone changes instead)
- ❌ NO ETB dispatch on phase-in (phasing is NOT entering the battlefield — don't call permanent-entered event)
- ❌ NO case statements on card names in engine code (cards are data, engine interprets)
- ❌ NO mutable state outside Datascript for phased-out tracking (all game state in db)
- ❌ NO tautological test assertions (is (some? x)) — use exact value checks
- ❌ NO reimplementing production handlers in tests — use th/cast-and-resolve, th/cast-with-target, th/cast-mode-with-target
- ❌ NO hardcoded land type lists in engine — define once, reference everywhere

## Approach
Six incremental steps: (1) Shared infrastructure extensions (match/not-colors criteria, bounce-all effect, lose-life-equal-to-toughness effect), (2) Hurkyl's Recall card+tests, (3) Vendetta card+tests, (4) Phase out mechanic (direct zone changes + untap step phase-in), (5) Land type change mechanic (selection + grant-based override + EOT cleanup), (6) Vision Charm card+tests.

## Architecture

### New Effect Types
- :bounce-all — follows :tap-all/:untap-all pattern. Iterates target player's battlefield, filters by :effect/criteria, moves matching objects to hand
- :lose-life-equal-to-toughness — follows :gain-life-equal-to-cmc pattern. Reads target's toughness at resolution, caster loses that much life
- :phase-out — direct zone change to :phased-out (bypasses move-to-zone to preserve state)
- :change-land-types — interactive effect, returns :needs-selection. Selection type for choosing source/target land types. Applies :land-type-override grants to matching lands

### New Criteria
- :match/not-colors — exclusion filter in matches-criteria? (queries.cljs). Object must NOT have ANY of the specified colors. Follows :match/not-types pattern exactly.

### Phase Out Mechanic
- phase-out fn: (d/db-with db [[:db/add obj-eid :object/zone :phased-out]])
- phase-in fn: (d/db-with db [[:db/add obj-eid :object/zone :battlefield]])
- Phase-in hook: during untap step, before untapping, scan for :phased-out objects owned by active player and phase them in
- No ETB triggers, no state reset — preserves tapped state, counters, creature fields, triggers

### Land Type Change Mechanic
- New selection type for choosing two land types (source type, target basic type)
- Limited to 5 basic land types (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, Forest) for both choices
- Effect: find all lands with source subtype on battlefield, apply :land-type-override grant to each
- Grant data: {:grant/type :land-type-override, :grant/data {:original-subtype :island :new-subtype :mountain :new-mana-ability {...}}, :grant/expires {:expires/turn N :expires/phase :cleanup}}
- Mana ability resolution checks for :land-type-override grants before using card-defined abilities
- EOT cleanup via existing expire-grants

### Card Data Structures

Hurkyl's Recall:
{:card/id :hurkyls-recall, :card/name "Hurkyl's Recall", :card/cmc 2, :card/mana-cost {:generic 1 :blue 1}, :card/colors #{:blue}, :card/types #{:instant}, :card/targeting [{:target/id :target-player :target/type :player :target/required true}], :card/effects [{:effect/type :bounce-all :effect/target-ref :target-player :effect/criteria {:match/types #{:artifact}}}]}

Vendetta:
{:card/id :vendetta, :card/name "Vendetta", :card/cmc 1, :card/mana-cost {:black 1}, :card/colors #{:black}, :card/types #{:instant}, :card/targeting [{:target/id :target-creature :target/type :object :target/zone :battlefield :target/controller :any :target/criteria {:match/types #{:creature} :match/not-colors #{:black}} :target/required true}], :card/effects [{:effect/type :destroy :effect/target-ref :target-creature} {:effect/type :lose-life-equal-to-toughness :effect/target-ref :target-creature :effect/target :self}]}

Vision Charm:
{:card/id :vision-charm, :card/name "Vision Charm", :card/cmc 1, :card/mana-cost {:blue 1}, :card/colors #{:blue}, :card/types #{:instant}, :card/modes [{:mode/label "Target player mills four cards" :mode/targeting [...] :mode/effects [{:effect/type :mill :effect/amount 4 :effect/target-ref :mill-player}]} {:mode/label "Change land types until end of turn" :mode/effects [{:effect/type :change-land-types}]} {:mode/label "Target artifact phases out" :mode/targeting [...] :mode/effects [{:effect/type :phase-out :effect/target-ref :target-artifact}]}]}

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Expanding the card pool for Premodern combo practice. These three cards cover bounce (Hurkyl's), removal (Vendetta), and utility (Vision Charm) — filling gaps in the current 50-card pool.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- engine/effects/zones.cljs:143 — :tap-all pattern: iterate battlefield by type+player, apply transform
- engine/effects/life.cljs:66 — :gain-life-equal-to-cmc pattern: read target's card data at resolution
- engine/zones.cljs:43 — move-to-zone has battlefield cleanup (triggers, creature fields, tapped state) that must be bypassed for phasing
- engine/grants.cljs:14 — grant system supports EOT expiration via :grant/expires {:expires/turn N :expires/phase :cleanup}
- db/queries.cljs:270 — matches-criteria? supports :match/types, :match/not-types, :match/colors, :match/subtypes — no :match/not-colors yet

**External (Scryfall):**
- Hurkyl's Recall: 'Return all artifacts target player owns to their hand' — owner=controller in Fizzle (no control-changing effects)
- Vendetta: 'Destroy target nonblack creature. It can't be regenerated. You lose life equal to that creature's toughness.' — regeneration not in engine, text-only
- Vision Charm: 3 modes — mill 4, land type change (any→basic until EOT), artifact phases out

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Direct zone change for phasing ✓
**What it is:** Phase out = set zone to :phased-out directly via d/db-with. Phase in = set zone back to :battlefield. Bypasses move-to-zone entirely.
**Chosen because:** move-to-zone has battlefield enter/leave logic (trigger retraction, creature field cleanup, tapped state reset) that violates phasing semantics. Direct zone change preserves all state. Existing battlefield queries automatically exclude :phased-out zone.

#### 2. :object/phased-out boolean flag ❌
**Why explored:** Seemed simpler — just add a flag.
**Why abandoned:** Requires auditing ALL battlefield queries to filter out phased-out objects. Invasive and error-prone.
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Zone-based approach proves insufficient for a specific interaction.

#### 3. Grant-based land type override ✓
**What it is:** Apply :land-type-override grants to affected lands. Grants expire at EOT via existing expire-grants. Mana ability resolution checks for override grants.
**Chosen because:** Leverages existing grant infrastructure and EOT cleanup. Preserves card data immutability.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** All 3 cards, all 3 Vision Charm modes, phasing mechanic, land type change mechanic
**Out of scope:** Regeneration (not in engine), non-basic land types for Vision Charm Mode 2 selection, auras/equipment phasing out with host

### Open Questions
- Exact selection UI for land type picking (how to present two sequential choices)
- Whether mana ability override needs to handle dual lands (lands with multiple subtypes)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Vision Charm modes? | All 3 | Need phasing + land type change engine mechanics |
| Owner vs controller? | Controller = Owner | bounce-all targets controller's artifacts, no ownership tracking needed |
| Phasing approach? | Direct zone change | Bypass move-to-zone, preserve all object state |

### Research Deep-Dives
#### Phase Out Implementation
**Question:** How to implement phasing without triggering ETB?
**Sources:** engine/resolution.cljs (ETB path), engine/zones.cljs (move-to-zone cleanup)
**Findings:** ETB fires only through resolution path. move-to-zone has battlefield cleanup that would break phasing. Direct zone change is clean.
**Conclusion:** Direct d/db-with zone change, bypassing move-to-zone entirely.

#### Land Type Change
**Question:** How to temporarily change land types?
**Sources:** engine/grants.cljs (grant system), engine/zones.cljs (no mutation of card data)
**Findings:** Grant system supports EOT expiration. Card data is immutable (on :object/card). Grants add temporary modifications.
**Conclusion:** :land-type-override grant with new subtype + mana ability, checked during mana ability resolution.

### Dead-End Paths
#### :object/phased-out Boolean Flag
**Why explored:** Simpler than new zone
**Why abandoned:** Requires modifying all battlefield queries to filter — invasive and error-prone

### Open Concerns Raised
- 'How do you make sure ETB doesn't trigger?' → Direct zone change bypasses both resolution.cljs ETB path AND move-to-zone cleanup. No ETB possible.
- 'Can't be regenerated' on Vendetta → Regeneration not in engine, text-only notation
close_reason: All 6 tasks complete. 3 cards implemented with full test suites. 2 new engine mechanics (phasing, land type change). Reviewer approved.

title: Combat damage resolution and creature death SBAs
description:
Implement combat-damage resolution with auto-lethal damage assignment, trample, and creature death SBAs. Damage clears at cleanup step.

## Scope
- :combat-damage resolution replaces stub with real implementation
- Unblocked attackers deal effective power as damage to defending player
- Blocked attackers: auto-assign lethal damage to blockers in order
- Trample: excess damage beyond lethal to all blockers goes to defending player
- Blockers deal their effective power as damage to the attacker they block
- Creature death SBA: creatures with damage-marked >= effective toughness move to graveyard
- Zero toughness SBA: creatures with effective toughness <= 0 move to graveyard
- Damage clears at cleanup step (end of turn)

## Files
- engine/combat.cljs — assign-damage-auto-lethal, calculate-combat-damage, deal-combat-damage
- engine/resolution.cljs — replace :combat-damage stub
- engine/state_based.cljs — lethal-damage SBA, zero-toughness SBA
- events/cleanup.cljs — clear damage-marked at cleanup
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature: Card Implementation Skill with Scryfall Validation
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Skill at .claude/skills/implement-card/SKILL.md provides complete card implementation workflow
- DSL reference at docs/card-dsl.md documents all effect/ability/cost/trigger types with examples
- Skill fetches card data from Scryfall API before implementation
- Skill fetches rulings from Scryfall to inform test cases
- Skeleton generation includes Scryfall-verified attributes marked with comments
- Tests must be derived from oracle text and rulings only - no hallucinated rules
- Consultation checkpoints when DSL doesn't support oracle text behavior
- Verification phase cross-checks final implementation against Scryfall data
- Skill blocks completion until all required tests pass

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] .claude/skills/implement-card/SKILL.md exists with complete workflow
- [ ] docs/card-dsl.md exists with comprehensive DSL reference
- [ ] Skill workflow: Scryfall lookup → skeleton → implement → test → verify
- [ ] DSL docs cover: effects, abilities, costs, triggers, conditions, gotchas
- [ ] Skill includes consultation checkpoint format for simplifications
- [ ] Skill references comprehensive rules URL
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hallucinated MTG rules (validation: all behavior must trace to oracle text, rulings, or comprehensive rules)
- ❌ NO hardcoded card data in skill (maintainability: must fetch from Scryfall at runtime)
- ❌ NO tests that assume rules not in oracle/rulings (correctness: prevents mistakes like 'cannot activate without targets')
- ❌ NO skipping consultation for unsupported behaviors (alignment: user decides simplifications)
- ❌ NO DSL docs embedded in skill file (separation: docs/card-dsl.md is single source of truth)
- ❌ NO completion without passing tests (quality: tests are blocking requirement)

## Approach
Create two deliverables: (1) comprehensive DSL documentation at docs/card-dsl.md covering all current effect types, ability types, cost types, and trigger types with examples from existing cards; (2) card implementation skill at .claude/skills/implement-card/SKILL.md that orchestrates the workflow from Scryfall lookup through verification.

The skill enforces Scryfall validation at both ends - generating skeleton with verified attributes upfront, and cross-checking the final implementation. Tests are derived solely from oracle text and Scryfall rulings to prevent rule hallucination.

## Architecture
- docs/card-dsl.md - Living documentation, updated as DSL evolves
- .claude/skills/implement-card/SKILL.md - Workflow skill with 5 phases
- Scryfall API integration via WebFetch in skill
- Comprehensive rules reference: https://media.wizards.com/2026/downloads/MagicCompRules%2020260116.txt

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Card implementations have had errors in basic attributes (mana costs) and effect DSL usage. No systematic validation against canonical card data. Tests have included hallucinated rules that don't match actual MTG behavior.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - All card definitions, pure EDN data
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - Effect interpreter with multimethod dispatch
- src/main/fizzle/engine/abilities.cljs - Mana and activated ability handling
- src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs - Cost payment system
- src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs - Trigger system for events
- src/test/fizzle/cards/ - Card-specific test files

**External:**
- Scryfall API (api.scryfall.com) - Canonical card data, oracle text, rulings
- Comprehensive Rules - Official game rules document

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Standalone local skill with separate DSL docs ✓

**What it is:** Skill at .claude/skills/implement-card/SKILL.md handles workflow. Comprehensive DSL reference at docs/card-dsl.md can evolve with codebase.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed existing cards for DSL patterns
- Identified all effect/ability/cost/trigger types in use
- Confirmed Scryfall API provides needed data

**Pros:**
- Clean separation of workflow vs reference
- DSL docs can be updated as system evolves
- Local to repo (project-specific)

**Cons:**
- Two files to maintain

**Chosen because:** DSL reference needs to evolve with codebase; skill workflow is relatively stable.

#### 2. Extend hyperpowers:brainstorming directly ❌

**What it is:** Add card-specific sections to the brainstorming skill in the hyperpowers plugin.

**Why we looked at this:** Avoids creating new skill file

**Pros:**
- Single skill file

**Cons:**
- Bloats generic brainstorming skill
- Card-specific logic mixed with general workflow
- Not local to Fizzle repo

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Card implementation is Fizzle-specific; doesn't belong in generic hyperpowers plugin.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We're building a generic MTG card tool that others would use.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Skill workflow for implementing new cards
- Comprehensive DSL documentation
- Scryfall integration for validation
- Test derivation from oracle/rulings

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Automated effect implementation from oracle text (deferred - would require NLP)
- Bulk card import (deferred - do one card at a time first)
- Card legality checking beyond Premodern (not needed for Iggy Pop focus)

### Open Questions
- Should DSL docs include effect implementation code examples? (decide during task 1)
- How detailed should consultation checkpoint prompts be? (decide during skill writing)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Scryfall timing? | Both before and after | Skeleton generation + final verification |
| Pain points? | Attributes, DSL syntax, test gaps | Focus areas for docs and skill |
| Code generation? | Yes, skeleton with TODOs | Skill generates card def template |
| DSL docs location? | Separate file in repo | docs/card-dsl.md, skill references it |
| Integration? | Extension to brainstorming | Brainstorming calls skill for card tasks |
| Test enforcement? | Mandatory, derived from oracle/rulings only | No hallucinated rules in tests |
| Consultation? | Required for simplifications | User decides where Fizzle deviates from rules |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Card DSL Structure
**Question explored:** How are cards defined in Fizzle?
**Sources consulted:**
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Full card definitions
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - Effect multimethod
- Recent commits for card additions

**Findings:**
- Cards are pure EDN maps with :card/* namespaced keys
- Effects dispatch on :effect/type keyword
- Abilities are :mana (don't use stack) or :activated (use stack)
- Triggers have :trigger/type and :trigger/effects

**Conclusion:** DSL is well-structured; needs documentation to prevent misuse

#### Scryfall API
**Question explored:** What data does Scryfall provide?
**Sources consulted:**
- Scryfall API documentation
- Sample card responses

**Findings:**
- /cards/named?exact=X returns full card data
- rulings_uri provides separate rulings endpoint
- All needed fields available: mana_cost, cmc, colors, type_line, oracle_text

**Conclusion:** Scryfall is canonical source for card data and rulings

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Example tests were wrong (rules hallucination)' → Tests must derive from oracle/rulings only, not assumed behavior
- 'Fizzle is a model, not the full game' → Consultation checkpoints for intentional simplifications
close_reason: Closed

title: Test Quality: Selection System — 1 RED, 13 YELLOW, 12 missing corners
description: REVISED after deeper investigation. The real gap is not individual test quality (93% GREEN) but systemic test architecture: tests enter production code at the wrong layer, bypassing event handlers, SBA execution, spec validation, and auto-confirm dispatch. 26 direct execute-confirmed-selection calls, 0 tests run SBAs, hand-built selections skip set-pending-selection chokepoint.
close_reason: All 9 tasks complete. +22 net tests (3286→3317). Key wins: integration tests through full event chain+SBAs, toggle→auto-confirm chain tests, builder-aligned executor tests, stale :drain cleanup, corner cases. Format gap fixed post-review.

title: Circular dependency: casting.cljs cannot require director/priority-flow
description: casting.cljs sits at the bottom of the dep graph (director→casting, priority-flow→casting). The non-targeted cast-and-yield path uses rf/dispatch as a runtime escape hatch (casting.cljs:236) to call resolve-one-and-stop in priority-flow. The targeted path uses a continuation correctly. Worth restructuring the dep graph so casting can call resolve-one-and-stop purely, but not urgent — workaround is documented and tested via rf/dispatch-sync.
close_reason: Superseded by epic fizzle-bcz9 (ADR-020 composable continuation chaining)

title: URL hash integration + Share button UI
description:
Wire up snapshot encoding/decoding to URL hash fragments and add Share button to the UI.

## What to build

### URL Hash Reading (on app init)
- In core.cljs init(): check window.location.hash for #s=<encoded>
- If present: decode → restore → dispatch init with restored state instead of normal setup
- If decode fails: log warning, proceed with normal app init
- After restoring, clear the hash (replaceState) so refreshing doesn't re-restore

### Share Button
- Add 'Share' button in the history sidebar (views/history.cljs), next to step controls
- Button visible when game is active and at a history position (fork point)
- On click: extract current game-db → encode → construct URL with hash → copy to clipboard
- Show brief feedback: 'Copied\!' text that fades after 2 seconds
- If encoding fails (too large): show 'State too large to share' error message

### Re-frame Events
- ::snapshot/share-position — extracts game-db, encodes, writes to clipboard
- ::snapshot/restore-from-hash — decodes hash, restores game state
- Subscription: ::snapshot/share-status (:idle | :copied | :error)

### Clipboard API
- Use js/navigator.clipboard.writeText (async, returns Promise)
- Fallback: create textarea, select, execCommand('copy') for older browsers

## Key files
- EDIT: src/main/fizzle/core.cljs (init function, lines 125-131)
- EDIT: src/main/fizzle/views/history.cljs (add share button near step-controls, line 19)
- NEW: src/main/fizzle/snapshot/events.cljs (re-frame events for share/restore)
- USE: snapshot/encoder.cljs, snapshot/decoder.cljs, snapshot/restorer.cljs, snapshot/extractor.cljs

## Verification
- Share button appears in history sidebar during active game
- Clicking Share copies a URL with #s= hash to clipboard
- Opening that URL in a new tab restores the exact game state
- Error message shown when state is too large
- Normal app init works when no hash is present
- Malformed hash doesn't break app init
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 4 Epic 1: Tailwind + UI Component Architecture
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Tailwind CSS v4 integrated with shadow-cljs build pipeline via PostCSS
- core.cljs game UI extracted into one-file-per-zone view components under views/
- All inline styles across ALL view files (core.cljs, modals.cljs, history.cljs) migrated to Tailwind utility classes
- Tailwind v4 @theme directive defines semantic color tokens (surface, accent, mana colors, etc.)
- CSS Grid layout with left primary column (opponent, battlefield, hand, mana/storm) and three right reference columns (stack, graveyard, history) — all collapsible
- Right-column zones expand horizontally when neighbors collapse
- Two-screen routing (setup placeholder vs game) via re-frame :active-screen key
- Dark theme preserved using semantic @theme tokens
- Zone components are layout-agnostic (know nothing about grid position)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Tailwind classes render correctly in dev and prod builds
- [ ] PostCSS pipeline runs in parallel with shadow-cljs (make dev)
- [ ] core.cljs reduced to routing + app shell (no game rendering logic)
- [ ] Each game zone is its own view file under views/
- [ ] Zero inline :style maps remaining in any view component
- [ ] @theme tokens used for all colors (no hardcoded hex strings in components)
- [ ] CSS Grid layout matches approved wireframe (left primary + 3 right columns)
- [ ] Collapsible zones work (collapse/expand, neighbors fill space)
- [ ] App can switch between setup and game screens
- [ ] All existing functionality preserved (no regressions)
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] make validate passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO inline :style maps in view components (consistency: everything uses Tailwind utility classes)
- ❌ NO hardcoded hex color strings in components (maintainability: use @theme semantic tokens like bg-surface, text-accent)
- ❌ NO game logic in view components (separation: views subscribe and dispatch only)
- ❌ NO breaking existing event/subscription contracts (stability: views change, data layer stays)
- ❌ NO floating/draggable panels (UX: MTGO-style floating windows are explicitly rejected — zones fill grid cells)
- ❌ NO per-archetype layout configurations (simplicity: one universal layout that works for all combo types)
- ❌ NO layout positioning knowledge inside zone components (composability: grid parent decides placement, zones just render content)
- ❌ NO Tailwind v3 patterns like tailwind.config.js content arrays (currency: use v4 CSS-first @theme and auto-detection)

## Approach
Set up Tailwind CSS v4 with @tailwindcss/postcss in the shadow-cljs pipeline. Extract each game zone from core.cljs into dedicated view files under views/. Convert all inline style maps to Tailwind utility classes using semantic @theme color tokens. Build a CSS Grid layout with a left column for primary interaction zones (opponent, battlefield, hand, mana/storm) and three right columns for vertical reference lists (stack, graveyard, history). All right-column zones are collapsible — when collapsed, remaining columns expand horizontally. Phase bar and controls anchor at the bottom. Setup screen is a routing placeholder (Epic 2 fills it in).

## Architecture

### Layout (CSS Grid)
```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Header: Fizzle                            [Setup] [Game]      │
├──────────────────┬─────────┬────────────┬──────────────────────┤
│  Opponent Info    │         │            │                      │
├──────────────────┤  Stack  │ Graveyard  │  History/Action Log  │
│  Battlefield     │ (coll.) │  (coll.)   │  (coll.)             │
│  (collapsible)   │         │            │                      │
├──────────────────┤         │            │                      │
│  ══ HAND ══      │         │            │                      │
├──────────────────┤         │            │                      │
│  Mana Pool│Storm │         │            │                      │
├──────────────────┴─────────┴────────────┴──────────────────────┤
│  Phase Bar  ·  Controls (Cast/Resolve/Play Land)               │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  [Future: Calculator panel]                                    │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Left column: Primary interaction (opponent, battlefield, hand, mana/storm)
Three right columns: Stack, graveyard, history — vertical scrollable lists, full height, collapsible

### Component Files
```
views/app.cljs          - Root: routing + grid layout
views/header.cljs       - Logo, nav buttons
views/hand.cljs         - Hand cards
views/battlefield.cljs  - Permanents
views/mana_pool.cljs    - Mana pool + storm count
views/opponent.cljs     - Life, clock, bot actions
views/stack.cljs        - Stack items (collapsible)
views/graveyard.cljs    - Graveyard cards (collapsible)
views/history.cljs      - Already exists, migrate styles (collapsible)
views/controls.cljs     - Cast, Play Land, Resolve
views/phase_bar.cljs    - Phase strip
views/modals.cljs       - Already exists, migrate styles
views/common.cljs       - Zone wrapper, collapse toggle
```

### Theme (@theme in CSS)
Semantic tokens: surface, surface-raised, surface-hover, border, border-accent, border-selected, text, text-muted, accent, gold, mana-white, mana-blue, mana-black, mana-red, mana-green, mana-colorless

### Build Pipeline
- PostCSS with @tailwindcss/postcss plugin
- src/css/app.css with @import tailwindcss + @theme
- make dev runs shadow-cljs watch + postcss watch in parallel
- make build runs both for production

## Design Rationale

### Problem
core.cljs is a 457-line monolith with 13 inline-styled game components. No CSS infrastructure exists. All colors are hardcoded hex strings scattered across 1,567 lines of view code (core + modals + history). Components cannot be rearranged without rewriting layout code. This blocks all Phase 4 UI work.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- core.cljs:20-444 — 13 view sub-components, all inline :style maps
- views/modals.cljs:11-40 — Style helper functions (partial extraction pattern exists)
- views/history.cljs — Recently added, inline styles, fork/replay UI
- shadow-cljs.edn — No CSS pipeline configured
- package.json — No CSS tooling present

**External:**
- Tailwind CSS v4 (Jan 2025) — Stable, CSS-first config via @theme, auto content detection
- @tailwindcss/postcss — Separate package for PostCSS integration
- shadow-cljs + Tailwind — Proven pattern: parallel watchers, scans compiled JS for classes
- Reagent hiccup + Tailwind — No gotchas, classes work naturally in hiccup syntax

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. CSS Grid with universal tiled layout ✓

**What it is:** Single CSS Grid layout with named areas. Left column for primary interaction zones, three right columns for vertical reference lists (stack, graveyard, history). Collapsible zones.

**Investigation:**
- CSS Grid named areas support rectangular zones — fits our layout
- Tailwind v4 has full grid utility support
- Collapsible zones can toggle grid columns via Tailwind classes

**Pros:**
- Universal layout works for storm, Devourer, and future archetypes
- Zones are layout-agnostic (composable)
- Collapsible zones let users adapt to their deck
- Native CSS, no JS layout library needed

**Cons:**
- Grid areas must be rectangular (CSS constraint, not a problem for this layout)

**Chosen because:** Matches requirement for one universal layout with collapsible zones. No per-archetype config needed. Prevents MTGO-style floating panel problems.

#### 2. Per-archetype layout presets ❌

**What it is:** Predefined CSS Grid templates per archetype (storm layout, Devourer layout). Switch with a button.

**Why we looked at this:** Different combos have different zone priorities.

**Investigation:**
- User explicitly rejected per-archetype configs
- Collapsible universal layout achieves same flexibility without config maintenance

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** User wants one layout, not config per archetype. Collapsible zones solve the same problem.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We discover the universal layout fundamentally cannot serve a new archetype.

#### 3. Flexbox nesting ❌

**What it is:** Nested flexbox containers instead of CSS Grid.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler mental model.

**Investigation:**
- Flex nesting gets deep for this layout (4+ levels)
- Rearranging zones requires restructuring nesting
- Grid is more natural for 2D layouts

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Less flexible for zone rearrangement, deeper nesting, harder to make collapsible zones fill space.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We hit a specific CSS Grid limitation.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Tailwind v4 + PostCSS pipeline setup
- All view files migrated to Tailwind (core.cljs, modals.cljs, history.cljs)
- Component extraction (one file per zone)
- CSS Grid layout with collapsible zones
- @theme semantic color tokens
- Setup/game screen routing shell
- Makefile updates for dev/build

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Hypergeometric calculator (future epic, bottom panel slot reserved in grid)
- Setup screen content (Epic 2)
- Card images / Scryfall integration
- Mobile/tablet responsive layout (future — desktop first)
- Deck-specific layout persistence

### Open Questions
- Exact grid column proportions (left vs right columns) — decide during implementation
- Collapse animation style (instant toggle vs transition) — decide during implementation
- Whether storm count stays in mana_pool.cljs or gets own component — decide during extraction

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Style migration scope? | All view files | Modals + history also migrated. Larger scope. |
| Match design doc layout? | Iterate on layout, quality components | Components must be layout-agnostic, composable. |
| Theme approach? | Tailwind v4 @theme | Semantic tokens, no hex in components. |
| File structure? | One file per zone | 12+ view files, self-contained components. |
| Layout flexibility? | Zone-based/tiled, NO floating panels | CSS Grid. Anti-pattern: MTGO floating windows. |
| Device target? | Desktop first, mobile eventually | Start 1280px+, responsive comes later. |
| Action log vs history? | History IS the action log | One panel, simplifies layout. |
| Per-archetype layouts? | No, one universal layout | Collapsible zones provide per-deck flexibility instead. |
| Variable zone handling? | Collapsible zones | Zones collapse to header, neighbors fill space. |
| Hand position? | Central, depends on vertical space | Hand in middle of left column. |
| Hypergeometric calculator? | Future epic, bottom panel | Reserve grid slot at bottom for future panel. |
| Right column structure? | Three side-by-side columns | Stack, graveyard, history as vertical columns, not stacked. |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Tailwind v4 + shadow-cljs Integration
**Question explored:** Best way to add Tailwind to a shadow-cljs project in 2026?
**Sources consulted:**
- Tailwind CSS v4 docs — CSS-first config, @theme directive, auto content detection
- @tailwindcss/postcss package — separate from main tailwindcss package
- jacekschae/shadow-cljs-tailwindcss — reference implementation

**Findings:**
- PostCSS approach recommended (not standalone CLI) for shadow-cljs
- Tailwind v4 auto-detects classes in compiled JS output
- Parallel watchers (shadow-cljs + postcss) work seamlessly for hot reload

**Conclusion:** PostCSS pipeline with @tailwindcss/postcss, parallel watchers via concurrently

#### Combo Archetype Layout Needs
**Question explored:** Do different combo archetypes need different layouts?
**Sources consulted:**
- User domain knowledge (storm vs Devourer)

**Findings:**
- Storm: hand + mana + storm critical, stack gets long, graveyard matters for threshold
- Devourer: battlefield critical, stack short, graveyard less important
- Universal layout with collapsible zones handles both

**Conclusion:** One layout, collapsible zones, no per-archetype config

### Dead-End Paths

#### Per-archetype Layout Presets
**Why explored:** Different combos prioritize different zones
**Investigation:** User explicitly rejected maintaining per-archetype configs
**Why abandoned:** Collapsible universal layout achieves same flexibility with less maintenance

### Open Concerns Raised
- Combo archetype differences → Collapsible zones handle this without per-archetype config
- Hypergeometric calculator → Deferred to future epic, grid slot reserved at bottom
- Mobile support → Deferred, desktop-first but Tailwind responsive enables future work
close_reason: All 4 child tasks complete. 10/11 success criteria pass. The single exception is 1 dynamic inline style in history.cljs (depth-based padding that cannot be static Tailwind). 1000 tests, 2266 assertions, make validate clean.

title: Epic: Phase 1 Core Engine - Dark Ritual End-to-End
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- Dark Ritual can be cast from hand when player has B available
- Casting Dark Ritual moves it from hand → stack → graveyard
- Resolving Dark Ritual adds BBB to player's mana pool
- Mana pool tracks all five colors plus colorless
- Storm count increments when spells are cast
- Game state is queryable via Datascript
- All engine functions are pure: (db, args) -> db
- No UI required - all interaction via REPL

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] Can create initial game state with Dark Ritual in hand
- [ ] can-cast? returns true when player has B, false otherwise
- [ ] cast-spell moves card to stack and pays mana
- [ ] resolve-spell executes add-mana effect and moves to graveyard
- [ ] Mana pool correctly shows BBB after resolution
- [ ] Storm count is 1 after casting
- [ ] All functions testable via REPL with direct calls
- [ ] Unit tests pass for mana system
- [ ] Unit tests pass for zone transitions
- [ ] Unit tests pass for effect interpreter (add-mana)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO internal randomness in engine functions (determinism: fork/replay requires all RNG as parameters)
- ❌ NO re-frame dependency in engine namespace (isolation: engine must be pure, re-frame wraps later)
- ❌ NO hardcoded card logic in engine (data-driven: cards are data, engine interprets)
- ❌ NO mutable state outside Datascript (simplicity: single source of truth)
- ❌ NO UI code in this phase (scope: REPL-only validates engine before UI work)
- ❌ NO skipping Datascript for 'simpler' atoms (architecture: Datascript enables declarative queries)

## Approach

Engine-first development with deferred re-frame integration. Build pure functions that transform Datascript state, validate via REPL and unit tests. The engine functions will later be wrapped by re-frame events, but remain independently testable.

All randomness passed as parameters to enable deterministic replay. Action granularity matches user intent (cast-spell is one action) even though engine does multiple db updates internally.

Start with Dark Ritual only - the simplest spell that exercises the full cast→resolve→effect pipeline.

## Architecture

```
src/fizzle/
├── db/
│   ├── schema.cljs     # Datascript schema for cards, objects, players, game
│   └── queries.cljs    # Common Datalog queries
├── engine/
│   ├── mana.cljs       # Mana pool: add, pay, can-pay?
│   ├── zones.cljs      # Zone transitions: move-to-zone
│   ├── rules.cljs      # Core rules: can-cast?, cast-spell, resolve-spell
│   └── effects.cljs    # Effect interpreter: execute-effect multimethod
└── cards/
    └── iggy_pop.cljs   # Card definitions (starting with Dark Ritual)
```

Key data flow:
1. Initial state created with Datascript schema + Dark Ritual in hand
2. can-cast? queries db for card in hand + sufficient mana
3. cast-spell: pay-mana → move-to-zone :stack → increment storm
4. resolve-spell: execute-effects → move-to-zone :graveyard
5. Effect interpreter dispatches on :effect/type, executes :add-mana

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Need a working game engine foundation before building UI, fork/replay, or additional cards. Dark Ritual is the simplest spell that validates the entire pipeline.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- Project bootstrapped with shadow-cljs but no source files yet
- Design doc at fizzle-design.md defines schema, effect system, architecture
- src/main/ and src/test/ directories exist but are empty

**Design Doc Key Patterns:**
- Datascript schema (fizzle-design.md lines 231-274)
- Effect interpreter multimethod (lines 480-528)
- Card definition format (lines 277-345)
- Engine function signatures (lines 435-475)

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Engine-First with Deferred re-frame ✓

**What it is:** Build pure engine functions first, drive from REPL, add re-frame wrapper later.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed design doc - engine functions are pure (db, args) -> db
- re-frame layer is thin wrapper around engine calls
- Testing pure functions simpler than testing through re-frame

**Pros:**
- Maximum testability
- Faster iteration on engine design
- Clear separation of pure logic vs app wiring

**Cons:**
- Delays validating re-frame integration

**Chosen because:** Testing is priority; re-frame wrapper is mechanical given clear engine API.

#### 2. Full re-frame from Start ❌

**What it is:** Wire events, subscriptions, and effects immediately alongside engine.

**Why we looked at this:** Validates integration early, events become action log.

**Investigation:**
- More ceremony for REPL-driven development
- Tests need re-frame test helpers OR test pure functions anyway
- Design doc already specifies event structure clearly

**Pros:**
- Validates event design early
- No integration surprises

**Cons:**
- More upfront wiring
- Tests more complex
- Slower iteration

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Given detailed design doc, integration risk is low. Pure engine functions are the hard work; re-frame wrapper is mechanical.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We discover engine APIs that don't compose well with re-frame's event model.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Datascript schema for cards, objects, players, mana
- Mana system (pool, payment, generation)
- Zone transitions (hand → stack → graveyard)
- Effect interpreter (add-mana only)
- Dark Ritual card definition
- Unit tests for all engine functions

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- re-frame events/subscriptions - Phase 1b
- UI components - Phase 1c
- Additional cards (Cabal Ritual, Brain Freeze) - after Dark Ritual works
- Storm copies - after basic storm count works
- Fork/replay - Phase 3

### Open Questions

- Exact Datascript entity ID generation strategy (random UUID vs sequential?)
- Should card definitions live in Datascript or separate registry? (design doc shows both)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| MVP scope? | Single card (Dark Ritual) | Smallest vertical slice through full pipeline |
| re-frame/Datascript integration? | Datascript inside app-db | Single atom when re-frame added |
| UI priority? | REPL-only first | No UI code in Phase 1 |
| re-frame timing? | Defer to later | Engine functions pure, testable in isolation |

### Constraints for Future Integration

1. **All randomness as parameters** - no internal rand calls
2. **Functions are (db, explicit-args) -> db** - no hidden state
3. **Granularity matches user intent** - cast-spell is one conceptual action

### Dead-End Paths

None yet - this is greenfield implementation.

### Open Concerns Raised

- Integration risk when adding re-frame later → Mitigated by constraints above and clear design doc
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature: Implement Ill-Gotten Gains card
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Card definition for Ill-Gotten Gains with correct oracle text and mana cost (2BB)
- :exile-self effect type that moves spell to exile zone on resolution
- :discard-hand effect type that discards entire hand (no player choice)
- :return-from-graveyard effect type with player selection (0 to max-count cards)
- Graveyard selection UI for player to choose cards to return
- Random selection for opponent's graveyard return
- Card registered in Iggy Pop deck

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Ill-Gotten Gains card casts and resolves correctly
- [ ] Spell exiles itself (not to graveyard)
- [ ] Both players discard their hands
- [ ] Player can select 0-3 cards from their graveyard to return to hand
- [ ] Opponent returns 0-3 random cards from graveyard to hand
- [ ] Graveyard selection UI follows existing selection patterns (tutor/discard)
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] New tests cover the three new effect types
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO generalized zone selection system (complexity: hidden vs public zones have different rules - fail to find)
- ❌ NO :each-player effect wrapper (deferred: can add later if needed for more cards)
- ❌ NO card-specific handlers in effects.cljs (consistency: use data-driven effect types)
- ❌ NO skipping graveyard selection UI (completeness: player must be able to choose cards)
- ❌ NO mocking zone movements in tests (validation: test actual zone transitions)

## Approach
Implement Ill-Gotten Gains using sequential per-player effects following APNAP order (self then opponent). Build three new reusable effect types: :exile-self, :discard-hand, and :return-from-graveyard. The graveyard selection UI will follow the existing tutor/discard selection patterns but for public zone selection (no fail-to-find).

## Architecture
- Card definition in src/fizzle/cards/ill_gotten_gains.cljs
- Effect handlers in src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs
- Selection state and handlers in src/fizzle/events/game.cljs
- Graveyard selection UI component in src/fizzle/views/
- Effects execute sequentially: exile-self → discard-hand (self) → discard-hand (opponent) → return-from-graveyard (self, player choice) → return-from-graveyard (opponent, random)

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Ill-Gotten Gains is a key combo piece for Iggy Pop storm deck. It enables returning combo pieces from graveyard (rituals, draw spells, win conditions) while disrupting opponent. Currently no way to implement this card - missing exile-self, discard-hand, and return-from-graveyard effects.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- src/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Card definition pattern with EDN data
- src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - Effect handler multimethod pattern
- src/fizzle/events/game.cljs:build-tutor-selection - Selection state pattern
- src/fizzle/events/game.cljs:build-discard-selection - Player choice selection

**External:**
- Scryfall API - Oracle text: 'Exile Ill-Gotten Gains. Each player discards their hand, then returns up to three cards from their graveyard to their hand.'
- MTG rules - APNAP ordering for simultaneous choices (active player first)

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Sequential Player Effects ✓
**What it is:** Model 'each player' as explicit sequential effects in card definition (self then opponent). Build three new effect types.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed existing card patterns - effects are explicit per-target
- Checked APNAP rules - for sorceries, caster is always active player
- Verified effect system supports :effect/target parameter

**Pros:**
- Follows existing codebase patterns
- Clear execution order in card definition
- Each effect type reusable independently

**Cons:**
- Verbose for 'each player' effects

**Chosen because:** Matches existing patterns, simpler than alternatives, caster is always active player for sorceries so explicit self→opponent order is correct APNAP.

#### 2. Generalized Zone Selection ❌
**What it is:** Single :select-from-zone effect type with source/dest zone parameters. Would unify tutor and graveyard-return.

**Why we looked at this:** Tutor and graveyard-return are conceptually similar (select cards from zone, move to destination).

**Investigation:**
- Identified key difference: library (hidden) allows fail-to-find, graveyard (public) does not
- Would need conditional logic for hidden vs public zones
- Adds complexity without immediate benefit

**Pros:**
- Single effect type for multiple use cases
- More declarative

**Cons:**
- Hidden vs public zone rules add complexity
- Over-engineering for current needs

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Hidden zones (library) vs public zones (graveyard) have fundamentally different rules. Fail-to-find only applies to hidden zones. Complexity not worth it for one card.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Adding 3+ cards that would benefit from generalization AND willing to handle hidden/public zone distinction properly.

#### 3. :each-player Effect Wrapper ❌
**What it is:** New effect type that iterates over players and applies sub-effects with APNAP ordering.

**Why we looked at this:** More faithful to MTG rules, cleaner card definitions for 'each player' effects.

**Investigation:**
- Would need turn state tracking for APNAP order
- Sub-effect selection handling adds complexity
- Only one card currently needs this

**Pros:**
- More rules-faithful
- Cleaner card definitions

**Cons:**
- More complex effect system changes
- Overkill for single card

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Over-engineering for one card. Sequential effects achieve same result for sorceries.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Adding multiple 'each player' cards including instants (where APNAP order depends on whose turn it is).

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Ill-Gotten Gains card definition
- :exile-self, :discard-hand, :return-from-graveyard effect types
- Graveyard selection UI for player
- Random selection for opponent
- Tests for new effect types

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Generalized zone selection system (deferred - complexity)
- :each-player effect wrapper (deferred - not needed yet)
- Opponent strategic selection (never - bot picks randomly per requirements)

### Open Questions
- None - all clarified during brainstorming

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Opponent card selection handling? | Random selection | :effect/selection :random for opponent effects |
| General infrastructure vs card-specific? | General infrastructure | Build reusable effect types, not special-case handlers |
| Fewer than 3 cards in graveyard? | Return up to available | :effect/min-count 0, select from what exists |
| Generalized zone selection? | No - too complex | Keep :return-from-graveyard separate from tutor |
| :each-player wrapper vs sequential? | Sequential for now | Explicit self→opponent effects, can add wrapper later |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Existing Selection Patterns
**Question explored:** How does the codebase handle player choices during spell resolution?
**Sources consulted:**
- src/fizzle/events/game.cljs - build-tutor-selection, build-discard-selection functions
- src/fizzle/cards/intuition.cljs - pile-choice selection pattern

**Findings:**
- Selection state stored with :selection/type, zone, count, selected set
- :selection/remaining-effects preserves effect queue
- UI renders based on selection type
- Confirm handler processes selection and continues effects

**Conclusion:** Follow same pattern for graveyard selection - build selection state, pause for UI, continue on confirm

### Dead-End Paths

#### Generalized Zone Selection
**Why explored:** User suggested tutor and graveyard-return are same problem with different zones
**Investigation:**
- Identified source-zone/dest-zone parameterization
- Realized hidden vs public zones have different fail-to-find rules

**Why abandoned:** Complexity of handling hidden vs public zone distinction not worth it for one card

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Is there generalization between this and tutor?' → Decided to keep separate due to hidden/public zone complexity
- 'How does :each-player handle APNAP?' → Using sequential effects instead, correct for sorceries
close_reason: Ill-Gotten Gains fully implemented and tested - card definition, graveyard selection UI, and bug fixes all working

title: Task 1: Add zone count subscriptions and query helpers
design:
## Goal
Add re-frame subscriptions that provide zone counts (GY, Library, Exile) for both player and opponent. These are the data layer that the zone-counts view component will subscribe to.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns
- subs/game.cljs:22-25 — `::hand` sub: Layer 3, derives from `::game-db`, calls queries with `:player-1`
- subs/game.cljs:232-245 — `::graveyard` sub: same pattern, uses `get-objects-in-zone`
- subs/game.cljs:141 — game-over already does `(count (get-objects-in-zone game-db :opponent :library))`
- subs/game.cljs:169-173 — `::opponent-life` sub: uses `:opponent` as player-id
- db/queries.cljs:79-90 — `get-objects-in-zone` takes `(db player-id zone)`, returns vector of objects
- Schema (schema.cljs:35) — `:object/zone` values: `:hand :stack :graveyard :battlefield :library :exile`

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Test file: test/fizzle/subs/game_test.cljs (add to existing file)

**Specific test cases:**
- `test-player-zone-counts-after-init`: After init-game, verify graveyard=0, exile=0, library=53 (60 deck - 7 hand), threshold?=false
- `test-player-zone-counts-graveyard-threshold-boundary`: Set up game-db with exactly 6 cards in GY -> threshold?=false; add 1 more -> threshold?=true
- `test-player-zone-counts-after-mill`: Mill 3 cards, verify library decreases by 3, graveyard increases by 3
- `test-opponent-zone-counts-after-init`: After init-game, opponent library=60, graveyard=0, exile=0
- `test-opponent-zone-counts-after-brain-freeze`: Mill opponent 3 cards, verify opponent library decreases by 3
- `test-zone-counts-nil-when-no-game-db`: When game-db is nil, both subs return nil (not crash)
- `test-player-zone-counts-exile-after-exile-effect`: After exiling a card, verify exile=1

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] subs/game.cljs — Add `::player-zone-counts` Layer 3 sub:
  ```clojure
  (rf/reg-sub
    ::player-zone-counts
    :<- [::game-db]
    (fn [game-db _]
      (when game-db
        (let [gy-count (count (queries/get-objects-in-zone game-db :player-1 :graveyard))
              lib-count (count (queries/get-objects-in-zone game-db :player-1 :library))
              exile-count (count (queries/get-objects-in-zone game-db :player-1 :exile))]
          {:graveyard gy-count
           :library lib-count
           :exile exile-count
           :threshold? (>= gy-count 7)}))))
  ```
- [ ] subs/game.cljs — Add `::opponent-zone-counts` Layer 3 sub (same pattern, use `:opponent` as player-id)
- [ ] test/fizzle/subs/game_test.cljs — Add 7 test cases listed above
- [ ] Run `make validate` to verify all tests pass

### Design decision: No new query helper needed
Existing code already uses `(count (get-objects-in-zone ...))` pattern (see subs/game.cljs:141). Adding a dedicated count query would be premature optimization for a 60-card deck. Follow existing pattern.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `::player-zone-counts` returns `{:graveyard N :library N :exile N :threshold? bool}` with correct counts
- [ ] `::opponent-zone-counts` returns `{:graveyard N :library N :exile N}` with correct counts
- [ ] Threshold flag true when player GY >= 7, false when < 7 (boundary test at exactly 6 and 7)
- [ ] Both subs return nil when game-db is nil (game not started)
- [ ] All 7 new tests pass with meaningful assertions on specific count values
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
- [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO creating a new Datascript aggregate query just for counts (YAGNI — `(count (get-objects-in-zone ...))` matches existing pattern and is fast enough for 60-card decks)
- NO separate subscription per zone per player (one composite sub per player keeps subscription count manageable)
- NO using `:player-2` for opponent (existing codebase uses `:opponent` — see subs/game.cljs:173)
- NO returning 0 when game-db is nil (return nil to signal 'no game' — callers should handle nil)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: game-db nil**
- Before game starts, `::game-db` returns nil
- Both subs MUST return nil (not `{:graveyard 0 ...}`) so view layer can distinguish 'no game' from 'empty zones'
- Guard with `(when game-db ...)` matching all other Layer 3 subs

**Edge Case: Empty exile zone**
- Most games never exile cards — exile count will be 0
- `get-objects-in-zone` returns empty vector `[]` for empty zones, so `(count [])` = 0 ✅

**Edge Case: Opponent zones**
- Opponent player-id is `:opponent` (verified in subs/game.cljs:173, queries.cljs:135-145)
- Opponent starts with full deck (60 cards in library for goldfish)
- Opponent graveyard starts empty

**Reference Implementation:**
- Follow exact pattern of `::opponent-life` sub at subs/game.cljs:169-173
- Follow exact pattern of `::hand` sub at subs/game.cljs:21-25 for Layer 3 derivation
close_reason: Two new subscriptions implemented: ::player-zone-counts and ::opponent-zone-counts. 8 new tests, all passing. make validate clean.

title: Enhance graveyard with castable partition and entry/sorted toggle
description:
## Goal
Add a castable-cards partition to the top of the graveyard view (sorted), and a toggle between entry order and CMC+name sorted order for the remaining cards.

## Context
Completed fizzle-x19c (sort utility) and fizzle-gcs8 (battlefield split). sort-cards and group-by-land exist in engine/sorting.cljs, already imported in subs/game.cljs. Graveyard subscription (::graveyard) currently returns raw unsorted cards. ::flashback-castable-ids subscription already exists.

## Implementation

1. Add :graveyard/sort-mode to app-db initialization (default :entry)
   - In events/game.cljs or wherever app-db defaults are set
   - Values: :entry (original order) or :sorted (CMC+name)

2. Create new event ::toggle-graveyard-sort in events/game.cljs
   - Toggles :graveyard/sort-mode between :entry and :sorted

3. Create new subscription ::graveyard-sort-mode in subs/game.cljs
   - Returns :graveyard/sort-mode from app-db (default :entry)

4. Modify ::graveyard subscription in subs/game.cljs (line 140-144)
   - Current: returns flat vector from queries/get-objects-in-zone
   - New: return map {:castable [...] :remainder [...]}
   - :castable = cards whose :object/id is in flashback-castable-ids, sorted
   - :remainder = rest of cards, ordered by sort-mode

   Change to composite subscription:
     (rf/reg-sub
       ::graveyard
       :<- [::game-db]
       :<- [::graveyard-sort-mode]
       (fn [[game-db sort-mode] _]
         (when game-db
           (let [all (queries/get-objects-in-zone game-db :player-1 :graveyard)
                 castable-ids (set (map :object/id (filter #(rules/can-cast? game-db :player-1 (:object/id %)) all)))
                 castable (filterv #(contains? castable-ids (:object/id %)) all)
                 remainder (filterv #(not (contains? castable-ids (:object/id %))) all)]
             {:castable (sorting/sort-cards castable)
              :remainder (if (= sort-mode :sorted)
                           (sorting/sort-cards remainder)
                           remainder)}))))

5. Update graveyard-view in views/graveyard.cljs
   - Destructure {:keys [castable remainder]} from ::graveyard subscription
   - Render castable section first with a "CASTABLE" label
   - Render remainder section below with toggle button
   - Toggle button dispatches ::toggle-graveyard-sort

6. Update any other subscribers of ::graveyard
   - Check: only views/graveyard.cljs subscribes (confirmed)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Graveyard shows castable cards in sorted partition at top
- [ ] Graveyard remainder toggles between entry order and sorted
- [ ] Toggle button visible and functional
- [ ] Count still shows total graveyard cards
- [ ] Threshold badge still works
- [ ] make validate passes
close_reason: Implemented graveyard castable partition + entry/sorted toggle. 12 tests, make validate passes.

title: Land Cycle Consolidation: Basic Lands, Pain Lands, Fetch Lands
description: Consolidate land card cycles into shared files, merge existing individual cards, and expand pain land and fetch land cycles to all 10 members each.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- All 5 basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, Forest) defined in a single basic_lands.cljs file
- All 10 pain lands defined in a single pain_lands.cljs file, following the Underground River pattern (colorless free, each color with 1 damage to controller)
- All 10 fetch lands defined in a single fetch_lands.cljs file, following the Polluted Delta pattern (tap + sacrifice + pay 1 life → tutor for one of two basic subtypes to battlefield, shuffle)
- Island and Swamp merged from separate files into basic_lands.cljs; island.cljs and swamp.cljs deleted
- Underground River merged from separate file into pain_lands.cljs; underground_river.cljs deleted
- Polluted Delta merged from separate file into fetch_lands.cljs; polluted_delta.cljs deleted
- Registry updated to use cycle `cards` vectors instead of individual card requires
- Each cycle has a single test file using doseq for cycle-wide assertions
- All existing tests continue to pass (no behavioral changes)
- Oracle text accuracy for all new card definitions

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] basic_lands.cljs exports 5 cards; island.cljs and swamp.cljs deleted
- [ ] pain_lands.cljs exports 10 cards; underground_river.cljs deleted
- [ ] fetch_lands.cljs exports 10 cards; polluted_delta.cljs deleted
- [ ] registry.cljs updated — no individual requires for merged cards
- [ ] Consolidated test files with doseq-based cycle tests
- [ ] All tests passing (make test)
- [ ] Validation passing (make validate)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO per-card files for cycle members (consistency: cycles share a single file, established by basic_lands.cljs precedent)
- ❌ NO copy-paste test variants per card (DRY: use doseq to iterate over cycle members with identical mechanics)
- ❌ NO engine changes (scope: this is pure data definition using existing patterns)
- ❌ NO inventing oracle text (accuracy: card text must match Scryfall oracle text)
- ❌ NO breaking existing decklists (safety: decklists reference card IDs which must remain stable)

## Approach
Three consolidation steps, each following the same pattern: define cards as EDN data in a shared cycle file, export a `cards` vector, register in registry.cljs, test with doseq.

1. Basic Lands cleanup: merge Island + Swamp into existing basic_lands.cljs
2. Pain Lands: create pain_lands.cljs with all 10 pain lands (9 new + Underground River merged in)
3. Fetch Lands: create fetch_lands.cljs with all 10 fetch lands (9 new + Polluted Delta merged in)

## Architecture
- cards/lands/basic_lands.cljs — 5 basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, Forest)
- cards/lands/pain_lands.cljs — 10 pain lands (Adarkar Wastes, Battlefield Forge, Brushland, Caves of Koilos, Karplusan Forest, Llanowar Wastes, Shivan Reef, Sulfurous Springs, Underground River, Yavimaya Coast)
- cards/lands/fetch_lands.cljs — 10 fetch lands (Flooded Strand, Polluted Delta, Bloodstained Mire, Wooded Foothills, Windswept Heath, Verdant Catacombs, Scalding Tarn, Misty Rainforest, Arid Mesa, Marsh Flats)
- cards/registry.cljs — updated to use cycle card vectors
- Deleted: island.cljs, swamp.cljs, underground_river.cljs, polluted_delta.cljs

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Land cards are split inconsistently — basic lands partially in one file, pain/fetch lands as individual files. Adding 18 new cards as individual files would create unnecessary file bloat when all cycle members share identical mechanics.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- basic_lands.cljs — existing precedent for cycle file (Plains, Mountain, Forest share one file)
- underground_river.cljs — pain land pattern: 3 mana abilities (colorless free, 2 colors with deal-damage)
- polluted_delta.cljs — fetch land pattern: activated ability with tap+sacrifice+pay-life, tutor to battlefield
- registry.cljs — concat individual cards + cycle cards vectors

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Combined cycle files ✓
Cycles share a single file with `cards` vector export. Tests use doseq.
**Chosen because:** Matches basic_lands.cljs precedent, DRY tests, minimal file count.

#### 2. Per-card files ❌
Each of 18 new cards gets its own file and test file.
**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** 36 new files for mechanically identical cards. Tests would be copy-paste.
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Cards gain unique mechanics that diverge from cycle pattern.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** basic land merge, 10 pain lands, 10 fetch lands, registry + test consolidation
**Out of scope:** new decklists, engine changes, other land cycles

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Pain land scope? | All 10 (enemy + allied) | Include both Ice Age and Apocalypse pain lands |
| Fetch land scope? | All 10 (Onslaught + Zendikar) | Include enemy fetches even though not Premodern-legal |
| File organization? | Combined cycle files | One file per cycle, not per card |
| Merge existing cards? | Yes, merge Underground River and Polluted Delta | Consolidate into cycle files, delete individual files |

### Pain Land Cycle (Oracle Reference)
- Adarkar Wastes: WU — {T}: Add {C}. {T}: Add {W} or {U}. Adarkar Wastes deals 1 damage to you.
- Battlefield Forge: WR — {T}: Add {C}. {T}: Add {R} or {W}. Battlefield Forge deals 1 damage to you.
- Brushland: WG — {T}: Add {C}. {T}: Add {G} or {W}. Brushland deals 1 damage to you.
- Caves of Koilos: WB — {T}: Add {C}. {T}: Add {W} or {B}. Caves of Koilos deals 1 damage to you.
- Karplusan Forest: RG — {T}: Add {C}. {T}: Add {R} or {G}. Karplusan Forest deals 1 damage to you.
- Llanowar Wastes: BG — {T}: Add {C}. {T}: Add {B} or {G}. Llanowar Wastes deals 1 damage to you.
- Shivan Reef: UR — {T}: Add {C}. {T}: Add {U} or {R}. Shivan Reef deals 1 damage to you.
- Sulfurous Springs: BR — {T}: Add {C}. {T}: Add {B} or {R}. Sulfurous Springs deals 1 damage to you.
- Underground River: UB — already exists, merge into cycle file
- Yavimaya Coast: UG — {T}: Add {C}. {T}: Add {G} or {U}. Yavimaya Coast deals 1 damage to you.

### Fetch Land Cycle (Oracle Reference)
Onslaught (allied):
- Flooded Strand: WU — Search for Plains or Island
- Polluted Delta: UB — already exists, merge into cycle file
- Bloodstained Mire: BR — Search for Swamp or Mountain
- Wooded Foothills: RG — Search for Mountain or Forest
- Windswept Heath: WG — Search for Forest or Plains

Zendikar (enemy):
- Verdant Catacombs: BG — Search for Swamp or Forest
- Scalding Tarn: UR — Search for Island or Mountain
- Misty Rainforest: UG — Search for Forest or Island
- Arid Mesa: WR — Search for Mountain or Plains
- Marsh Flats: WB — Search for Plains or Swamp
close_reason: All success criteria met. Reviewer approved. 3 commits: basic lands merge, pain lands cycle, fetch lands cycle.
