title: Events Layer Integration Tests — phases, abilities, lands have zero dispatch coverage
description:
Systemic impl-level testing gap confirmed across entire events layer. Only 2 of ~30 event test files call db-effect/register! (both from fizzle-80jj). This epic covers the 3 highest-priority modules: phases, abilities, and lands.

## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Integration tests (rf/dispatch-sync) for all 7 handlers across phases.cljs, abilities.cljs, lands.cljs
- Unit tests (direct function calls) for guard conditions and edge cases in all 3 modules
- SBA verification: integration tests assert that state-based actions fire correctly after event dispatch
- Full turn cycle E2E test proving untap→upkeep→draw→main1→combat→main2→cleanup→new-turn works
- All tests use interceptor/register! + dispatch-sync pattern (matching priority_test.cljs)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] events/phases_test.cljs exists with integration + unit tests
- [ ] events/abilities_test.cljs exists with guard condition + SBA tests
- [ ] events/lands_test.cljs exists with integration + unit tests
- [ ] Full turn cycle E2E test passes
- [ ] SBA triggers verified after ability dispatch (e.g., sacrifice → graveyard check)
- [ ] Guard conditions tested: wrong zone, wrong controller, no priority, restrictions, non-empty stack
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (make validate)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO mocking re-frame dispatch or interceptors (integration: defeats purpose of dispatch-sync testing)
- ❌ NO testing pure functions only without dispatch-sync layer (gap: event handler wiring is what's untested)
- ❌ NO hand-building app-db from scratch (consistency: use h/create-game-scenario like priority_test.cljs)
- ❌ NO skipping SBA verification after game-state-changing dispatches (coverage: SBA gap is the #1 risk identified)
- ❌ NO duplicating card test coverage for ability/land happy paths (redundancy: card tests already cover those; focus on guards + SBAs)

## Approach
Three test files, one per module. Integration layer uses dispatch-sync through re-frame for happy paths and SBA verification. Unit layer calls pure functions directly for edge cases and guard conditions. Follows proven pattern from priority_test.cljs (interceptor/register!, create-game-scenario, dispatch-event wrapper).

## Architecture
- Test files: src/test/fizzle/events/{phases,abilities,lands}_test.cljs
- Pattern source: src/test/fizzle/events/priority_test.cljs (dispatch-sync pattern)
- Modules under test: src/main/fizzle/events/{phases,abilities,lands}.cljs
- Helper: src/test/fizzle/test_helpers.cljs (create-game-scenario, add-card-to-zone, etc.)

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Events layer has zero dispatch-sync test coverage for phases, abilities, and lands. Card tests exercise these paths indirectly but don't verify: (1) re-frame handler wiring, (2) SBA triggers after dispatch, (3) guard condition behavior at the event boundary, (4) history entry creation.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- priority_test.cljs — Proven dispatch-sync pattern: interceptor/register!, setup-app-db via create-game-scenario, dispatch-event wrapper
- 10 existing test files use dispatch-sync; 2 use db-effect/register! (from fizzle-80jj epic)
- phases.cljs: 2 handlers (::advance-phase, ::start-turn), 4 pure fns (next-phase, advance-phase, untap-all-permanents, start-turn)
- abilities.cljs: 3 handlers (::activate-ability, ::activate-mana-ability, ::activate-granted-mana-ability), complex pure fns (activate-ability with 4-way dispatch: sacrifice/targeting/generic-mana/direct)
- lands.cljs: 2 handlers (::play-land, ::tap-permanent), 3 pure fns (play-land, tap-permanent, untap-permanent)

### Approaches Considered
#### 1. Both-layer testing (integration + unit) ✓
Integration tests for happy paths and SBA verification. Unit tests for guard conditions and edge cases.
Chosen because: provides both wiring verification AND thorough edge case coverage without redundancy with card tests.

#### 2. Integration-only ❌
Only dispatch-sync tests. Would miss edge case coverage at pure function level.
REJECTED BECAUSE: Guard conditions (wrong zone, no priority, restrictions) are easier to test at unit level. Integration-only would need complex setup for each guard.
DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS: Test helper makes guard-condition integration setup trivial.

#### 3. Unit-only ❌
Only direct function calls. Would not verify re-frame handler wiring or SBA integration.
REJECTED BECAUSE: The entire point of this epic is to close the dispatch coverage gap. Unit-only perpetuates the problem.
DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS: Never — this contradicts the epic's purpose.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** phases.cljs, abilities.cljs, lands.cljs — the 3 modules named in the original epic
**Out of scope:**
- casting.cljs, priority_flow.cljs, resolution.cljs — tracked as separate issues
- selection/ submodules — separate epic
- UI event tests — lowest priority, separate issue

### Open Questions
- Exact test count per file will emerge during implementation
- Whether cleanup discard selection needs its own integration test or is covered by phases advance-phase → cleanup path
close_reason: Closed

title: Unify condition checking into single multimethod in conditions.cljs
design:
## Goal
Consolidate 3 separate condition-checking implementations into a single check-condition multimethod in engine/conditions.cljs. After this task, all condition checks throughout the codebase dispatch through one multimethod.

## Effort Estimate
3-5 hours

## Current State (3 implementations)

### 1. effects.cljs:19-52 — multimethod (THE RIGHT PATTERN)
Signature: (db, player-id, effect, condition)
Handles: :threshold, :no-counters, :default
Note: :no-counters reads :effect/target from the effect parameter to find the object

### 2. abilities.cljs:12-22 — case function (DUPLICATE)
Signature: (db, player-id, condition)
Handles: :threshold only (via conditions/threshold?)
Note: No effect parameter — abilities check conditions on the permanent, not an effect

### 3. rules.cljs:427-440 — inline threshold check (DUPLICATE)
Uses :card/conditional-effects map keyed by condition keyword: {:threshold [...effects...]}
Note: Different data shape — condition is a keyword key, not a {:condition/type ...} map

## CRITICAL: Signature Unification

The effects.cljs multimethod takes (db, player-id, effect, condition) because :no-counters reads :effect/target from the effect. abilities.cljs takes (db, player-id, condition) because abilities check conditions on the permanent itself.

**Solution: Caller enriches condition with runtime context before calling.**

Unified signature: `(db player-id condition)`

For :no-counters, the caller (execute-effect in effects.cljs) must merge the target into the condition before calling:
```clojure
;; In effects.cljs execute-effect, before calling check-condition:
(let [enriched-condition (cond-> condition
                           (:effect/target effect)
                           (assoc :condition/target (:effect/target effect)))]
  (conditions/check-condition db player-id enriched-condition))
```

The :no-counters defmethod then reads :condition/target instead of :effect/target:
```clojure
(defmethod check-condition :no-counters
  [db _player-id condition]
  (let [target-id (:condition/target condition)
        counter-type (:condition/counter-type condition)]
    ;; ... same logic, reads from condition instead of effect
```

For abilities.cljs, the caller enriches with object-id:
```clojure
(let [enriched-condition (assoc condition :condition/target object-id)]
  (conditions/check-condition db player-id enriched-condition))
```

## Implementation

### Step 1: Study existing code (READ ONLY)
- engine/conditions.cljs (23 lines) — has threshold? function
- engine/effects.cljs:19-52 — check-condition multimethod (source pattern)
- engine/effects.cljs:96-103 — execute-effect caller of check-condition
- engine/abilities.cljs:12-22 — private check-condition function
- engine/abilities.cljs:49-60 — can-activate? caller of check-condition
- engine/rules.cljs:416-440 — get-active-effects with inline threshold check
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:63 — :card/conditional-effects data shape {:threshold [...]}

### Step 2: Write failing tests first (RED)
Create test/fizzle/engine/conditions_test.cljs:

Test 1 — test-threshold-condition-dispatches:
  Setup: game-db with player having 7+ cards in graveyard
  Call: (conditions/check-condition db :player-1 {:condition/type :threshold})
  Assert: returns true
  Bug caught: multimethod dispatch wiring for :threshold

Test 2 — test-threshold-condition-not-met:
  Setup: game-db with player having 0 cards in graveyard
  Call: (conditions/check-condition db :player-1 {:condition/type :threshold})
  Assert: returns false
  Bug caught: threshold? logic correctness when not met

Test 3 — test-no-counters-condition-with-zero-counters:
  Setup: game-db with permanent having {:mining 0} counters
  Call: (conditions/check-condition db :player-1 {:condition/type :no-counters :condition/counter-type :mining :condition/target object-id})
  Assert: returns true
  Bug caught: :no-counters reads :condition/target (new key) instead of :effect/target

Test 4 — test-no-counters-condition-with-counters:
  Setup: game-db with permanent having {:mining 3} counters
  Call: (conditions/check-condition db :player-1 {:condition/type :no-counters :condition/counter-type :mining :condition/target object-id})
  Assert: returns false
  Bug caught: :no-counters correctly rejects when counters present

Test 5 — test-unknown-condition-type-returns-false:
  Call: (conditions/check-condition db :player-1 {:condition/type :invented-condition})
  Assert: returns false
  Bug caught: default method exists and fails closed

Test 6 — test-nil-condition-returns-true:
  Call: (conditions/check-condition db :player-1 nil)
  Assert: returns true
  Bug caught: nil condition = no condition = always met (matches abilities.cljs behavior)

### Step 3: Enhance conditions.cljs (GREEN)
Add to engine/conditions.cljs:
1. defmulti check-condition dispatching on (:condition/type condition)
2. Add nil dispatch: when condition is nil, return true (no condition = always met)
3. defmethod :threshold — calls existing threshold? function
4. defmethod :no-counters — move from effects.cljs, reads :condition/target instead of :effect/target
5. defmethod :default — returns false (fail closed)

Requires: datascript.core (for :no-counters queries)

### Step 4: Update effects.cljs
1. Remove lines 14-52 (check-condition multimethod and defmethods)
2. Add require: [fizzle.engine.conditions :as conditions]
3. Update execute-effect (line 96-103):
   - Before calling conditions/check-condition, enrich condition with :condition/target from effect:
   ```clojure
   (let [enriched (cond-> condition
                    (:effect/target effect) (assoc :condition/target (:effect/target effect)))]
     (conditions/check-condition db player-id enriched))
   ```

### Step 5: Update abilities.cljs
1. Remove lines 12-22 (private check-condition function)
2. Already has require for conditions namespace
3. In can-activate? (line 49-60), replace:
   ```clojure
   ;; OLD:
   (check-condition db player-id condition)
   ;; NEW:
   (conditions/check-condition db player-id condition)
   ```
   Note: abilities pass condition directly (no enrichment needed for :threshold).
   If future ability conditions need object context, enrich with :condition/target object-id.

### Step 6: Update rules.cljs get-active-effects
Replace inline threshold check (lines 433-436):
```clojure
;; OLD:
(and (:threshold conditional-effects)
     (conditions/threshold? db player-id))
(:threshold conditional-effects)

;; NEW: iterate conditional-effects map, dispatch each through multimethod
(some (fn [[condition-key effects]]
        (when (conditions/check-condition db player-id {:condition/type condition-key})
          effects))
      conditional-effects)
```
This handles future condition types automatically — no code change needed when adding a new condition keyword to :card/conditional-effects.

### Step 7: Verify (make validate)
Run: make validate (lint + format + tests)
All 1265+ existing tests must pass.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Single check-condition multimethod in engine/conditions.cljs with defmethods for :threshold, :no-counters, nil, and :default
- [ ] effects.cljs has no local check-condition (no multimethod, no defmethods) — delegates to conditions/check-condition with enriched condition
- [ ] abilities.cljs has no local check-condition function — delegates to conditions/check-condition
- [ ] rules.cljs get-active-effects iterates :card/conditional-effects through conditions/check-condition (no inline threshold? call)
- [ ] 6+ new tests in conditions_test.cljs covering: threshold met, threshold not met, no-counters with zero, no-counters with counters, unknown type, nil condition
- [ ] All existing tests pass (make test)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: nil condition**
- abilities.cljs currently returns true for nil condition (no condition = always met)
- effects.cljs doesn't check for nil (condition comes from :effect/condition which can be nil)
- MUST handle nil: add a defmethod or dispatch guard that returns true for nil
- Approach: check for nil before dispatching (multimethods can't dispatch on nil :condition/type)

**Edge Case: :no-counters with missing object**
- Current code returns true if object doesn't exist (vacuously true)
- Preserve this behavior in the moved defmethod
- Test with non-existent object-id

**Edge Case: :card/conditional-effects with multiple conditions**
- Currently only :threshold exists, but the map could have multiple keys
- The some iteration in Step 6 returns the FIRST matching condition's effects
- This matches current behavior (first-match wins) but document that this is intentional

**Edge Case: :condition/target not enriched by caller**
- If a caller forgets to enrich :condition/target, :no-counters gets nil target
- Current :no-counters returns true for nil target (object not found → vacuously true)
- This is safe (fail-open for vacuous case) but add a comment documenting why

**Signature Migration Risk**
- effects.cljs changes from check-condition(db, player-id, effect, condition) to conditions/check-condition(db, player-id, enriched-condition)
- The effect parameter is NOT passed to the new function — only the enriched condition
- If any other code calls check-condition with the old 4-arg signature, it will get a wrong-arity error (LOUD failure — good)

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO passing the full effect to check-condition (conditions must be self-contained via enrichment)
- ❌ NO case/cond dispatch on :condition/type anywhere except the multimethod (single source of truth)
- ❌ NO importing conditions/threshold? directly — always go through check-condition multimethod
- ❌ NO changing card data shapes (:effect/condition and :card/conditional-effects stay as-is)
close_reason: Unified 3 condition-checking implementations into single check-condition function in conditions.cljs. Effects.cljs and abilities.cljs now delegate, rules.cljs iterates conditional-effects via multimethod. 6 new tests added, all 1435 tests pass, make validate clean.

title: Game state extractor (Datascript DB → portable map)
description:
Create snapshot/extractor.cljs that pulls a portable game state map from a Datascript DB.

## What to build
Pure function: extract-game-state(game-db) → map with:

```clojure
{:version 1
 :players [{:id :player-1 :life 20 :mana-pool {:white 0 ...} :storm-count 0
            :land-plays-left 1 :max-hand-size 7 :is-opponent false
            :bot-archetype nil :grants [...] :drew-from-empty false}
           {:id :opponent ...}]
 :game {:turn 1 :phase :main1 :step nil :active-player :player-1
        :priority :player-1 :passed #{} :auto-mode nil}
 :zones {:player-1 {:hand [{:card-id :dark-ritual}]
                     :battlefield [{:card-id :swamp :tapped true :counters {:charge 3}
                                    :grants [...] :summoning-sick false
                                    :power nil :toughness nil :damage-marked 0
                                    :x-value nil :cast-mode nil :chosen-mode nil}]
                     :graveyard [{:card-id :dark-ritual}]
                     :exile [{:card-id :lotus-petal}]
                     :library [{:card-id :island} {:card-id :swamp} ...]}
         :opponent {:hand [...] ...}}
 :stack [{:type :spell :card-id :brain-freeze :controller :player-1
          :is-copy false :targets {} :cast-mode nil :chosen-x nil
          :effects [...] :description "Brain Freeze"}]
 :triggers [...]}
```

## Key decisions
- Card references use :card/id keywords (encoder converts to indices later)
- Library cards in position order (index 0 = top)
- Battlefield cards include ALL state: tapped, counters, grants, summoning-sick, power, toughness, damage-marked, attacking, blocking, is-token, x-value, cast-mode, chosen-mode
- Stack items include effects as-is (EDN) — encoder handles serialization
- Player grants included for restrictions (Orim's Chant etc.)
- Triggers extracted for completeness (needed to restore triggered abilities)

## Key files
- NEW: src/main/fizzle/snapshot/extractor.cljs
- READ: src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs (all query functions)
- READ: src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs (entity shapes)

## Verification
- Extracts all players with all attributes
- Extracts all zones with correct card ordering (library by position)
- Extracts stack items with all metadata
- Handles empty zones, empty stack, empty grants gracefully
close_reason: Closed

title: Extract events/selection.cljs from game.cljs
design:
## Goal
Extract all selection logic (~1,500 lines) from events/game.cljs into a new events/selection.cljs namespace. This is the largest extraction and includes: selection detection, all builders, spell/ability resolution with selection, all confirmation handlers, X-cost logic, and cast-time targeting.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours (larger than abilities extraction due to size and forward declaration complexity)

## Context from Previous Task
- Extracted abilities.cljs successfully (302 lines, 8 functions/handlers)
- Event keyword namespace change pattern established: update core.cljs dispatches + test file imports
- Bare require in game.cljs pattern works for ensuring namespace loading
- Linter catches unused requires cleanly — expect some game.cljs requires to become unused

## CRITICAL: Forward Declaration Resolution
game.cljs currently has 13 forward declarations. Most reference selection functions:
- resolve-spell-with-selection (moves to selection.cljs)
- resolve-activated-ability-with-selection (moves to selection.cljs)
- find-selection-effect-index (moves to selection.cljs)
- begin-cleanup (stays in game.cljs — selection dispatches back via rf/dispatch)
- build-tutor-selection (moves to selection.cljs)
- build-cast-time-target-selection (moves to selection.cljs)
- confirm-cast-time-target (moves to selection.cljs)
- has-exile-cards-x-cost? (moves to selection.cljs)
- get-exile-cards-x-cost (moves to selection.cljs)
- build-exile-cards-selection (moves to selection.cljs)
- has-mana-x-cost? (moves to selection.cljs)
- build-x-mana-selection (moves to selection.cljs)
- execute-peek-selection (moves to selection.cljs)

After extraction: game.cljs should have ZERO forward declarations. game.cljs requires selection.cljs (for resolve-spell-with-selection etc.), selection.cljs dispatches re-frame events back to game (runtime, no compile-time dep).

## What Moves to selection.cljs
Everything from the Selection System section through cast-time targeting:
- Selection detection and management (~95 lines)
- X-cost detection and builders (~90 lines)
- Selection builders: tutor, discard, graveyard, scry, peek, pile-choice, player-target (~320 lines)
- resolve-spell-with-selection (~130 lines)
- Trigger selection builders (~35 lines)
- resolve-activated-ability-with-selection (~165 lines)
- Core selection event handlers (~105 lines)
- All confirmation handlers: peek, graveyard, tutor, pile-choice, scry, player-target, x-cost (~775 lines)
- Cast-time targeting: build + confirm + event handlers (~95 lines)

Also moves from game.cljs:
- ::confirm-cast-time-target event handler (currently in game.cljs from abilities extraction)
- ::select-cast-time-object-target event handler

## What Stays in game.cljs
- Game init
- Spell casting (initiate-cast-with-mode, cast-spell)
- resolve-top-of-stack (calls into selection.cljs)
- Phase/turn management
- Cleanup step
- Land play + tap permanent

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Inventory exact functions and line numbers
Read game.cljs, map all functions/handlers that move. Identify exact boundaries.

### Step 2: Create events/selection.cljs
- Set up namespace with requires from game.cljs (datascript, re-frame, engine modules)
- Order functions so NO forward declarations are needed (define before use)
- Move all selection functions and handlers

### Step 3: Update events/game.cljs
- Remove all moved functions/handlers
- Remove all 13 forward declarations
- Add bare require for fizzle.events.selection
- game.cljs now requires selection.cljs for resolve-spell-with-selection etc.
- Remove unused engine requires (linter will flag them)

### Step 4: Update core.cljs dispatches
- Add require for fizzle.events.selection
- Update all selection-related dispatches (toggle, confirm, cancel, etc.)
- Keep game dispatches (cast, resolve, phase, land, etc.)

### Step 5: Update test file imports
- Find all test files referencing moved functions
- Add requires for fizzle.events.selection
- Update function call prefixes
- Remove unused game requires where applicable

### Step 6: Verify
- make validate (lint + format + tests)
- Verify zero forward declarations in all files
- Verify game.cljs under 800 lines
- Verify no circular dependencies

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO forward declarations in selection.cljs (order functions correctly)
- ❌ NO circular namespace dependencies (selection dispatches events, doesn't require game)
- ❌ NO changing function signatures or behavior
- ❌ NO modifying test assertions
- ❌ NO splitting selection into sub-namespaces

## Success Criteria
- [ ] events/selection.cljs exists with all selection logic
- [ ] events/game.cljs under 800 lines
- [ ] Zero forward declarations across all 3 files (game, selection, abilities)
- [ ] No circular namespace dependencies
- [ ] core.cljs dispatches updated for selection events
- [ ] All test files updated
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
close_reason: Extracted events/selection.cljs (1,608 lines) from game.cljs. game.cljs now 728 lines (under 800 target). Zero forward declarations across all 3 files. All 741 tests pass. make validate clean.

title: Extract selection/resolution.cljs and wire build-selection-for-effect multimethod
design:
## Goal

Extract resolution bridge functions from selection.cljs into events/selection/resolution.cljs. Replace the cond dispatch in resolve-spell-with-selection with calls to the build-selection-for-effect multimethod defined in core.cljs.

## Context
Completed fizzle-3v2f: core.cljs exists with both multimethods, data-driven validation, and mechanism functions. selection.cljs is bridged to core. All 1266 tests pass.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Create events/selection/resolution.cljs
Move these functions from selection.cljs:
- has-selection-effect? (selection.cljs:77-84)
- find-selection-effect-index (selection.cljs:87-107)
- split-effects-around-index (selection.cljs:110-117)
- resolve-effect-with-stored-target (selection.cljs:566-575)
- resolve-spell-with-selection (selection.cljs:578-709) — MODIFY to use multimethod
- resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection (selection.cljs:736-809)

Requires for resolution.cljs: core.cljs, engine/effects, engine/rules, engine/targeting, engine/zones, engine/stack, db/queries

### 2. Replace cond dispatch with multimethod
In resolve-spell-with-selection, replace the cond block (lines 670-709) with:
```clojure
pending-selection (core/build-selection-for-effect db-after-before player-id object-id
                                                    selection-effect (vec effects-after))
```

### 3. Register defmethods for build-selection-for-effect in selection.cljs

CRITICAL: Builder signatures must match multimethod: (db, player-id, object-id, effect, remaining-effects)

Builders that already match (take db as first arg):
- build-tutor-selection [game-db player-id object-id effect effects-after] -> :tutor
- build-scry-selection [game-db player-id object-id effect effects-after] -> :scry
- build-graveyard-selection [game-db player-id object-id effect effects-after] -> :return-from-graveyard
- build-peek-selection [game-db player-id object-id effect effects-after] -> :peek-and-select

Builders that DON'T match (missing db param):
- build-discard-selection [player-id object-id effect effects-after] -> :discard
  Wrapper: (defmethod core/build-selection-for-effect :discard [_db player-id object-id effect remaining] (build-discard-selection player-id object-id effect remaining))
- build-player-target-selection [player-id object-id effect effects-after] -> :player-target
  Wrapper: (defmethod core/build-selection-for-effect :player-target [_db player-id object-id effect remaining] (build-player-target-selection player-id object-id effect remaining))

Default method for unknown types:
```clojure
(defmethod core/build-selection-for-effect :default
  [_db player-id object-id effect remaining-effects]
  {:selection/zone :hand
   :selection/select-count (:effect/count effect)
   :selection/player-id player-id
   :selection/selected #{}
   :selection/spell-id object-id
   :selection/remaining-effects remaining-effects
   :selection/type (:effect/type effect)
   :selection/validation :exact
   :selection/auto-confirm? false})
```

### 4. Update selection.cljs
- Remove the 6 extracted functions
- Keep builders, execution functions, defmethods (extracted in later tasks)
- Remove the cond default branch inline map (now :default method)

### 5. Update game.cljs
Change require: selection -> selection.resolution for:
- resolve-spell-with-selection (called at game.cljs:359)
- resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection (called at game.cljs:367)

game.cljs does NOT call has-selection-effect?, find-selection-effect-index, or split-effects-around-index.

### 6. Update 10 test files that call resolve functions directly
These test files call selection/resolve-spell-with-selection:
- src/test/fizzle/cards/ill_gotten_gains_test.cljs
- src/test/fizzle/cards/flash_of_insight_test.cljs
- src/test/fizzle/cards/careful_study_test.cljs
- src/test/fizzle/cards/recoup_test.cljs
- src/test/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis_test.cljs
- src/test/fizzle/cards/polluted_delta_test.cljs
- src/test/fizzle/cards/cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs
- src/test/fizzle/cards/opt_test.cljs
- src/test/fizzle/cards/merchant_scroll_test.cljs
- src/test/fizzle/cards/intuition_test.cljs

Update: require selection.resolution instead of selection for resolve-* calls. Keep selection require if test also uses selection events (::toggle-selection, ::confirm-selection).

## Success Criteria
- [ ] events/selection/resolution.cljs exists with 6 extracted functions
- [ ] resolve-spell-with-selection uses core/build-selection-for-effect multimethod (no cond dispatch on effect type)
- [ ] resolution.cljs requires only core.cljs and engine/* (zero domain file imports)
- [ ] 6 build-selection-for-effect defmethods registered in selection.cljs (tutor, discard, scry, return-from-graveyard, peek-and-select, player-target) + :default
- [ ] game.cljs requires selection.resolution for resolve functions
- [ ] 10 test files updated to require selection.resolution for resolve-* calls
- [ ] All existing ~1266 tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] No circular dependencies

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Builder Signature Mismatch**
build-discard-selection and build-player-target-selection take 4 args (no db). The multimethod dispatches with 5 args (db, player-id, object-id, effect, remaining). Defmethod wrappers must accept 5 args and drop db when calling these builders. Forgetting this causes arity errors at runtime.

**Edge Case: Nil-Returning Builders**
build-scry-selection and build-peek-selection return nil when library is empty or amount <= 0. The calling code in resolve-spell-with-selection assigns the result to pending-selection, and if nil, returns {:db db-after-before :pending-selection nil}. The multimethod dispatch handles this naturally — nil return is valid.

**Edge Case: Default Dispatch**
The old cond has an :else branch creating a generic selection map. Must register a :default method on build-selection-for-effect. Without this, unknown effect types will throw 'No method' error instead of graceful fallback. The :default method MUST include :selection/validation and :selection/auto-confirm? fields.

**Edge Case: Test Import Updates**
10 test files call selection/resolve-spell-with-selection directly. Some also use selection events (::toggle-selection etc). For those, KEEP the selection require AND add resolution require. Don't accidentally remove the selection require when it's still used for events.

**Reference: Current cond dispatch at selection.cljs:670-709**
6 branches + :else default. Each branch calls a different builder. After extraction, these become 6 defmethods + :default.

## Anti-Patterns
- NO direct builder calls in resolution.cljs (must use multimethod — this is the key decoupling)
- NO domain file imports in resolution.cljs (engine/* and core.cljs only)
- NO forgetting :default method (would cause runtime error on unknown effect types)
- NO removing selection require from tests that still use selection events
- NO changing builder function signatures (wrap in defmethod instead)
close_reason: Closed

title: Remove RED tests (tautological/line-hitters)
design:
## Goal
Remove or rewrite 5 RED tests that provide false confidence by passing regardless of code correctness.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Tests to Address

### 1. test-shuffle-library-changes-positions (merchant_scroll_test.cljs:209-224)
**Problem:** Captures `_positions-before` but NEVER compares to after. Comment says "may occasionally fail" - flaky.
**Action:** Either add statistical comparison (chi-square test that positions differ) or delete entirely.
**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/merchant_scroll_test.cljs

### 2. test-tutor-effect-returns-db-unchanged (merchant_scroll_test.cljs:248-260)
**Problem:** Tests implementation detail (returns unchanged db) not behavior. Tutor selection happens via UI flow.
**Action:** Delete this test. The actual tutor behavior is tested through `resolve-spell-with-selection` tests.
**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/merchant_scroll_test.cljs

### 3. test-tutor-fail-to-find-shuffles (merchant_scroll_test.cljs:292-318)
**Problem:** Test name says "shuffles" but NO assertion that shuffle happened (comment at line 316-317 explicitly notes this). If shuffle was broken, test passes.
**Action:** Either add shuffle assertion comparing library positions before/after, OR rename test to `test-tutor-fail-to-find-preserves-cards` to accurately reflect what it tests.

### 4. can-cast-mode-empty-mana-cost-test (rules_test.cljs:695-711)
**Problem:** Claims to test `can-cast-mode?` but never calls it with assertion. Only verifies mode structure exists.
**Action:** Add `(is (true? (rules/can-cast-mode? db :player-1 obj-id alt-mode)))` assertion.
**File:** src/test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs

### 5. Pure card definition tests (lotus_petal_test.cljs:187-215, etc.)
**Problem:** Tests that DATA exists, not that production code USES it correctly.
**Decision:** These ARE somewhat valuable for documenting expected card structure. Keep but mark as YELLOW - could be strengthened by testing card lookup functions.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read merchant_scroll_test.cljs and verify test locations
- [ ] For test 1: Delete test-shuffle-library-changes-positions (flaky, captures but ignores positions)
- [ ] For test 2: Delete test-tutor-effect-returns-db-unchanged
- [ ] For test 3: Rename to test-tutor-fail-to-find-preserves-cards and remove shuffle claim from description
- [ ] For test 4: Add can-cast-mode? assertion with exact expected value
- [ ] Run `make test` to verify all tests still pass
- [ ] Run `make validate` to verify no lint/format issues

## Success Criteria
- [ ] 2 tests deleted (shuffle positions, tutor db unchanged)
- [ ] 1 test renamed to remove false shuffle claim
- [ ] 1 test strengthened with actual assertion
- [ ] `make test` passes
- [ ] `make validate` passes
- [ ] Test count reduced by 2 (from ~591 to ~589)

## Key Considerations

**Handling Test 1 (shuffle test):**
Shuffle randomness is hard to test deterministically. Options:
1. Delete entirely - shuffle is simple enough to trust (RECOMMENDED)
2. Add statistical test - but this is complex and still flaky
Decision: Delete. The implementation is trivial (just calls shuffle).

**Handling Test 3 (fail-to-find):**
The test DOES verify important behavior (cards preserved, hand unchanged). Only the shuffle claim is false.
Decision: Keep test, rename to accurately describe what it tests.

**Card Definition Tests:**
These catch copy-paste errors and document expected structure. Reclassify from RED to YELLOW.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Keeping tests "just in case" - if it catches no bugs, delete it
- ❌ Replacing tautological test with equally meaningless test
- ❌ Adding coverage-only tests to compensate for removal
- ❌ Changing test behavior without understanding what it originally intended
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 3: Create Intuition card definition
description: Create the actual Intuition card definition with the correct effect configuration to use the multi-select tutor and pile-choice systems.
design:
## Goal
Create the Intuition card definition (2U instant) that uses the multi-select tutor and pile-choice selection systems implemented in Tasks 1-2.

## Context
Completed fizzle-kd6: Multi-select tutor selection works with :effect/select-count
Completed fizzle-wti: Pile choice selection works with :selection/pile-choice

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing card patterns
- Review src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs for card definition structure
- Note: Cards use :card/id, :card/name, :card/cmc, :card/mana-cost, :card/colors, :card/types, :card/text, :card/effects
- Note: Mana cost uses {:colorless N :blue N} format (see merchant-scroll: {:colorless 1 :blue 1})
- Note: Cards are registered via all-cards vector (see bottom of iggy_pop.cljs)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Create tests in src/test/fizzle/cards/intuition_test.cljs:

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.intuition-test
  (:require
    [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.cards.iggy-pop :as cards]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
    [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
    [fizzle.events.game :as game]))

;; Use same test helpers from pile_choice_test.cljs

(deftest test-intuition-card-exists
  ;; Bug caught: Card not added to all-cards vector, won't be loaded
  (testing "Intuition is in all-cards"
    (let [intuition-card (some #(when (= "Intuition" (:card/name %)) %)
                               cards/all-cards)]
      (is (some? intuition-card)
          "Intuition must be in cards/all-cards")
      (is (= :intuition (:card/id intuition-card))
          "Card ID must be :intuition"))))

(deftest test-intuition-mana-cost
  ;; Bug caught: Wrong mana cost prevents casting
  (testing "Intuition has correct mana cost 2U"
    (let [intuition-card (some #(when (= :intuition (:card/id %)) %)
                               cards/all-cards)]
      (is (= {:colorless 2 :blue 1} (:card/mana-cost intuition-card))
          "Mana cost must be {2}{U}")
      (is (= 3 (:card/cmc intuition-card))
          "CMC must be 3")
      (is (= #{:blue} (:card/colors intuition-card))
          "Colors must be #{:blue}"))))

(deftest test-intuition-effect-structure
  ;; Bug caught: Missing parameters cause tutor/pile-choice to fail
  (testing "Intuition effect has required tutor parameters"
    (let [intuition-card (some #(when (= :intuition (:card/id %)) %)
                               cards/all-cards)
          effect (first (:card/effects intuition-card))]
      (is (= :tutor (:effect/type effect))
          "Effect type must be :tutor")
      (is (= 3 (:effect/select-count effect))
          "select-count must be 3")
      (is (= :hand (:effect/target-zone effect))
          "target-zone must be :hand")
      (is (= {:hand 1 :graveyard :rest} (:effect/pile-choice effect))
          "pile-choice must specify 1 to hand, rest to graveyard"))))

(deftest test-intuition-full-flow-integration
  ;; Bug caught: Card definition doesn't work with selection system
  (testing "Intuition triggers pile choice after tutor selection"
    (let [db (create-test-db)
          [db' card-ids] (add-cards-to-library db
                           [:dark-ritual :cabal-ritual :brain-freeze
                            :careful-study :mental-note]
                           :player-1)
          intuition-effect (first (:card/effects cards/intuition))
          selection (game/build-tutor-selection db' :player-1 (random-uuid)
                                                 intuition-effect [])]
      ;; Verify multi-select tutor
      (is (= 3 (:selection/select-count selection))
          "Intuition must require 3 cards")
      (is (= {:hand 1 :graveyard :rest} (:selection/pile-choice selection))
          "Selection must include pile-choice for second phase"))))
```

### 3. Implementation
Add to src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs (where other Iggy Pop deck cards live):

```clojure
;; Intuition - The power tutor
;; 2U -> Search for 3 cards, choose 1 for hand, rest to graveyard
(def intuition
  {:card/id :intuition
   :card/name "Intuition"
   :card/cmc 3
   :card/mana-cost {:colorless 2 :blue 1}
   :card/colors #{:blue}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/text "Search your library for three cards and reveal them. Target opponent chooses one. Put that card into your hand and the rest into your graveyard. Then shuffle."
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :tutor
                   :effect/select-count 3
                   :effect/target-zone :hand
                   :effect/pile-choice {:hand 1 :graveyard :rest}}]})
```

Also add to all-cards vector at bottom of file.

### 4. Verification
- Run: make test (all tests pass including new intuition_test.cljs)
- Run: make validate (lint, format, tests)
- Verify: cards/intuition is exported and accessible

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Intuition card definition added to src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs
- [ ] Card added to all-cards vector for registration
- [ ] Correct :card/id :intuition
- [ ] Correct mana cost {:colorless 2 :blue 1}
- [ ] Correct CMC: 3
- [ ] Correct colors: #{:blue}
- [ ] Type: #{:instant}
- [ ] Effect: :tutor with :effect/select-count 3
- [ ] Effect: :effect/target-zone :hand
- [ ] Effect: :effect/pile-choice {:hand 1 :graveyard :rest}
- [ ] 4 unit tests pass:
  - test-intuition-card-exists (catches missing registration)
  - test-intuition-mana-cost (catches casting failures)
  - test-intuition-effect-structure (catches selection failures)
  - test-intuition-full-flow-integration (catches end-to-end failures)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint, format, tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Card File Location**
- Add to iggy_pop.cljs (not separate file) - follows existing pattern where deck cards are grouped together
- Intuition is part of the Iggy Pop storm deck list

**Registration Pattern**
- Must add to all-cards vector at bottom of iggy_pop.cljs
- Without this, card won't be loaded into Datascript

**Effect Key Names**
- Use :effect/target-zone :hand (not :revealed)
- Cards are "revealed" conceptually during pile choice, but :target-zone is final destination
- :effect/pile-choice handles the intermediate reveal/choice flow

**Mana Cost Format**
- Use {:colorless 2 :blue 1} (matches existing Merchant Scroll pattern)
- NOT {:generic 2 :blue 1} or {2 :U} or similar

**No :effect/criteria Needed**
- Intuition searches for ANY 3 cards (no filter criteria)
- Omit :effect/criteria entirely (nil means no restriction)

**Test Helpers**
- Import create-test-db and add-cards-to-library from pile_choice_test.cljs pattern
- Or factor into shared test helper namespace if preferred

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO custom effect type (use :tutor with parameters)
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in effects.cljs or events/game.cljs (data-driven)
- ❌ NO separate card file (add to iggy_pop.cljs with other deck cards)
- ❌ NO forgetting to add to all-cards vector (card won't load)
- ❌ NO using :effect/criteria (Intuition has no filter - searches for any cards)
close_reason: Intuition card definition implemented with TDD. All 4 tests pass, make validate passes.

title: Remove SBA/bot interceptors, inline SBA calls, and fix auto-confirm dispatch
description: Now that the :db effect handler chokepoint is in place, remove all redundant mechanisms: SBA interceptor, bot interceptor, inline SBA calls in reduce-effects, and fix auto-confirm to dispatch instead of inlining.
design:
## Goal
Remove all redundant SBA/bot trigger mechanisms now that the :db effect handler handles everything. Fix auto-confirm to dispatch ::confirm-selection instead of calling confirm-selection-impl directly.

## Implementation

### 1. Study what exists (DO FIRST)
- events/db_effect.cljs — the new :db effect handler (from fizzle-ocqu)
- events/interceptors/sba.cljs — SBA interceptor to DELETE
- bots/interceptor.cljs — bot-action-interceptor + bot-trigger-events + register! to REMOVE (keep ::bot-decide handler, bot-should-act?, bot-decide-action, build-bot-dispatches, bot-decide-handler)
- core.cljs — remove sba-interceptor/register! and bot-interceptor/register! calls
- engine/effects.cljs:207,211 — inline SBA calls in reduce-effects to REMOVE
- engine/mana_activation.cljs — check for inline SBA call to REMOVE
- events/selection/core.cljs:443-448 — auto-confirm path to fix
- tests referencing interceptors: src/test/fizzle/engine/opponent_sba_test.cljs

### 2. Fix auto-confirm dispatch (events/selection/core.cljs)
In toggle-selection-impl (line ~443-448), change:
```clojure
;; BEFORE (synchronous inline call):
(if (and selected? (= select-count 1) (:selection/auto-confirm? selection))
  (confirm-selection-impl new-db)
  new-db)

;; AFTER (dispatch — goes through event system + :db effect handler):
(if (and selected? (= select-count 1) (:selection/auto-confirm? selection))
  (do (rf/dispatch [::confirm-selection])
      new-db)  ;; return toggled state, confirmation happens next tick
  new-db)
```

NOTE: The returned new-db has the item selected but NOT confirmed. The ::confirm-selection event fires on the next tick, picks up the pending selection from app-db, and confirms it. This is the correct async flow.

IMPORTANT: Need to require re-frame.core in core.cljs if not already. Also need the ::confirm-selection event keyword — it's in events/selection.cljs as :fizzle.events.selection/confirm-selection.

### 3. Delete SBA interceptor file
`rm src/main/fizzle/events/interceptors/sba.cljs`

### 4. Remove bot interceptor from bots/interceptor.cljs
DELETE these items from bots/interceptor.cljs:
- `bot-trigger-events` set (lines ~125-134)
- `bot-action-interceptor` def (lines ~142-174)
- `register!` function (lines ~246-249)
- `max-bot-actions` constant (lines ~137-139)

KEEP these items (still needed by :db effect handler and ::bot-decide):
- `bot-should-act?` function
- `bot-decide-action` function
- `build-bot-dispatches` function
- `bot-decide-handler` function
- `::bot-decide` event registration
- `find-tap-sequence` helper
- `find-bot-land-to-play` helper

### 5. Remove interceptor registrations from core.cljs
Remove from init function:
- `(sba-interceptor/register!)`
- `(bot-interceptor/register!)`
Keep: `(history-interceptor/register!)` and `(db-effect/register!)`

Remove the requires for the deleted modules:
- `[fizzle.events.interceptors.sba :as sba-interceptor]`
- If bots/interceptor require was only for register!, update it

### 6. Remove inline SBA calls from engine/effects.cljs
In reduce-effects (lines 203-211), change:
```clojure
;; BEFORE:
(loop [db db
       [effect & remaining] (seq effects)]
  (if-not effect
    {:db (sba/check-and-execute-sbas db)}
    (let [result (execute-effect-checked db player-id effect object-id)]
      (if (:needs-selection result)
        (assoc result :remaining-effects (vec remaining))
        (recur (sba/check-and-execute-sbas (:db result)) remaining)))))

;; AFTER:
(loop [db db
       [effect & remaining] (seq effects)]
  (if-not effect
    {:db db}
    (let [result (execute-effect-checked db player-id effect object-id)]
      (if (:needs-selection result)
        (assoc result :remaining-effects (vec remaining))
        (recur (:db result) remaining)))))
```

Also remove the `[fizzle.engine.state-based :as sba]` require from effects.cljs if SBA is no longer used there.

### 7. Remove inline SBA call from mana_activation.cljs (if present)
Check engine/mana_activation.cljs for any `check-and-execute-sbas` calls and remove.

### 8. Update tests
- opponent_sba_test.cljs — tests that reference sba-interceptor directly need updating. Tests for interceptor behavior (like sba-interceptor-skips-non-trigger-events) should be DELETED (interceptor no longer exists). Tests for SBA logic itself (check-sba, execute-sba) stay.
- fling_test.cljs — remove unused `[fizzle.engine.state-based :as sba]` require (the test no longer calls SBAs manually)
- Any test importing `fizzle.events.interceptors.sba` — update or delete
- Any test importing bot-action-interceptor from bots/interceptor — update or delete
- Tests that use auto-confirm behavior may need adjustment for async dispatch

### 9. Run make validate

## Success Criteria
- [ ] events/interceptors/sba.cljs DELETED (file does not exist)
- [ ] bot-action-interceptor, bot-trigger-events, register!, max-bot-actions removed from bots/interceptor.cljs
- [ ] core.cljs init does NOT call sba-interceptor/register! or bot-interceptor/register!
- [ ] reduce-effects no longer calls check-and-execute-sbas (engine/effects.cljs)
- [ ] mana_activation.cljs no longer calls check-and-execute-sbas (if it did)
- [ ] toggle-selection-impl dispatches ::confirm-selection instead of calling confirm-selection-impl
- [ ] No references to sba-interceptor or bot-action-interceptor in codebase (grep returns 0)
- [ ] Fling lethal damage still triggers game-over (existing test)
- [ ] Bot still responds after player actions (existing bot tests pass)
- [ ] All tests passing (make validate)

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ DO NOT leave dead interceptor code — delete entirely
- ❌ DO NOT keep inline SBA calls 'as backup' — chokepoint handles everything
- ❌ DO NOT break bot-should-act? or ::bot-decide — only the TRIGGER mechanism changes
- ❌ DO NOT make auto-confirm synchronous via dispatch-sync — use rf/dispatch (async)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Auto-confirm async timing**
After this change, toggle-selection returns the toggled-but-not-confirmed state. The ::confirm-selection fires on the next tick. Tests that previously got the confirmed state from toggle-selection will need to dispatch-sync ::confirm-selection separately. Check all tests using auto-confirm selections.

**Edge Case: reduce-effects without SBAs**
Removing inline SBAs from reduce-effects means mid-resolution SBAs no longer fire between effects. Example: effect 1 kills a creature (lethal damage), effect 2 references that creature. Previously SBA would clean up between effects. NOW SBA only runs at the :db chokepoint (after all effects). Verify this doesn't break any existing behavior — the :lethal-damage SBA moves creatures to graveyard, which could affect subsequent effects that reference the creature.

THIS IS A CRITICAL EDGE CASE. Check if any card has multiple effects where an earlier effect deals damage and a later effect references the damaged creature. If so, the creature would still be on the battlefield during effect 2 (SBA hasn't run yet). In MTG rules, SBAs only fire when a player would receive priority — not between individual effects on the same resolution. So removing mid-resolution SBAs is actually MORE correct per MTG rules.

**Regression risk: bot tests**
Bot tests in test/fizzle/bots/ may reference the interceptor. Check all test files for imports.

## Effort Estimate
~5 hours
close_reason: Closed

title: Build composable view components: action-log, fork-controls, fork-tree
design:
## Goal
Build three composable, layout-agnostic Reagent view components in views/history.cljs that use the events and subscriptions from completed tasks fizzle-4r1d and fizzle-k1hy.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Completed:
- fizzle-4r1d: Data model functions (rename-fork, delete-fork, entries-by-turn, fork-tree) in history/core.cljs
- fizzle-k1hy: Events (jump-to, create-fork, rename-fork, delete-fork) and subscriptions (entries-by-turn, fork-tree) in history/events.cljs and subs/history.cljs

Existing views/history.cljs has POC components (action-log, fork-list, step-controls, history-sidebar) that need to be replaced/upgraded with composable versions.

## Available Subscriptions
- ::subs/entries-by-turn → [{:turn N :entries [...]}] grouped by turn
- ::subs/fork-tree → [{:fork/id ... :fork/name ... :children [...]}] hierarchical tree
- ::subs/position → current position index
- ::subs/current-branch → current branch fork-id (nil = main)
- ::subs/entries → flat entry list (for position math)
- ::subs/forks → flat fork list

## Available Events
- ::events/jump-to [position] → jump to specific entry
- ::events/create-fork [name] → create new named fork at current position
- ::events/rename-fork [fork-id new-name] → rename fork
- ::events/delete-fork [fork-id] → cascade delete fork
- ::events/switch-branch [fork-id] → switch to branch (nil = main)
- ::events/step-back / ::events/step-forward → step navigation

## Implementation

### 1. action-log component (replace existing defn-)
Replace current flat action-log with turn-grouped version. Make it a public defn (not defn-) since it's a composable component.

**Absolute index computation:**
entries-by-turn returns [{:turn N :entries [...]} ...]. To map each entry to its absolute position, accumulate a running offset across groups:
\`\`\`
group 0: offset=0, entries [a b c] → indices 0, 1, 2
group 1: offset=3, entries [d e] → indices 3, 4
group 2: offset=5, entries [f] → index 5
\`\`\`
Use reduce or loop to compute \`(mapv (fn [group] (assoc group :offset running-offset)) groups)\` then within each group, entry i has absolute index = offset + i.

**Component structure:**
- Subscribe to ::subs/entries-by-turn, ::subs/position, ::subs/entries (entries only for empty check)
- Create reagent/atom \`collapsed\` (set of collapsed turn numbers)
- For each turn group:
  - Turn header div: "Turn N" text, clickable to toggle collapsed state
  - If not collapsed, render entries:
    - Each entry row is clickable → dispatches [::events/jump-to absolute-index]
    - If absolute-index == position: highlighted (border-left #4A9BD9, background #1a2a3a)
    - If absolute-index > position: dimmed (opacity 0.4, color #666)
    - Otherwise: normal (color #eee)
- Empty state: when entries-by-turn is empty, show "No actions yet"

### 2. fork-controls component (new, public defn)
- Subscribe to ::subs/forks, ::subs/current-branch
- Create reagent/atom \`editing\` for inline rename state: nil or {:fork-id id :value "name"}

**"New Fork" button:**
- Label: "+ New Fork"
- On click: dispatch [::events/create-fork (str "Fork " (inc (count forks)))]
- Default name follows existing auto-fork naming pattern

**Fork list:**
- Always show "main" first (hardcoded, not from forks list)
  - Active styling if current-branch is nil
  - No delete button for main
- For each fork in forks:
  - If editing this fork: show input field with edit-value
    - Enter key → dispatch [::events/rename-fork fork-id value], reset editing atom to nil
    - Escape key → reset editing atom to nil (cancel)
    - Blur → dispatch rename (same as Enter)
    - Input should auto-focus when entering edit mode (use reagent :component-did-update or :ref with .focus)
  - If not editing: show fork name as clickable span
    - Double-click → enter edit mode (set editing atom)
    - Active fork gets highlighted styling
  - Delete button (X or trash icon):
    - On click: dispatch [::events/delete-fork fork-id]
    - No confirmation dialog needed (cascade delete is the model, undo via history is possible)

### 3. fork-tree component (new, public defn)
- Subscribe to ::subs/fork-tree, ::subs/current-branch

**Structure:**
- Always render "main" as root node at depth 0
  - Active if current-branch is nil
  - Click → dispatch [::events/switch-branch nil]
- Render tree nodes from ::subs/fork-tree recursively

**Recursive render-node helper (defn- render-tree-node):**
- Takes: node (fork map with :children), current-branch, depth
- Renders div with padding-left (* depth 16) px for indentation
- Fork name text: if active (fork-id == current-branch) → accent color #4A9BD9, bold; else → #ccc, pointer cursor
- Click → dispatch [::events/switch-branch fork-id]
- Recursively render each child with (inc depth)

### 4. Keep existing step-controls and history-sidebar
- step-controls stays as-is (already working, keep defn-)
- Update history-sidebar to use new action-log (already calls [action-log])
- fork-list can remain as-is (fork-tree is the new composable component)
- history-sidebar is the POC integration point — no changes needed beyond action-log already being referenced

### 5. Verification
- Run: make validate
- Manual check: no hardcoded widths in new components
- Manual check: no requires of history/core in views/history.cljs
- Manual check: only rf/subscribe and rf/dispatch in component bodies

## Success Criteria
- [ ] action-log renders entries grouped by turn with collapsible turn headers
- [ ] Clicking any entry dispatches [::events/jump-to absolute-index] with correct index
- [ ] Current position highlighted (border-left accent, background), future entries dimmed
- [ ] Empty entries shows "No actions yet" message
- [ ] fork-controls has "+ New Fork" button that dispatches create-fork
- [ ] fork-controls inline rename: double-click to edit, Enter/blur to save, Escape to cancel
- [ ] fork-controls delete button per fork dispatches delete-fork
- [ ] Cannot delete main branch (no delete button shown for main)
- [ ] fork-tree shows "main" as root + indented children from ::fork-tree subscription
- [ ] Clicking branch in fork-tree dispatches [::events/switch-branch fork-id]
- [ ] Active branch visually highlighted in both fork-controls and fork-tree
- [ ] All components are layout-agnostic: no hardcoded widths, no position:absolute, no flex parent assumptions
- [ ] No game logic in views: only rf/subscribe and rf/dispatch calls
- [ ] views/history.cljs does NOT require history/core directly
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty state**
- entries-by-turn returns [] when no entries → action-log shows "No actions yet"
- fork-tree returns [] when no forks → fork-tree shows only "main"
- forks list empty → fork-controls shows only "main" + New Fork button

**Edge Case: Inline rename with empty string**
- If user clears input and presses Enter, dispatch rename with empty string
- This is harmless — rename-fork in core.cljs accepts any string
- Could optionally prevent empty rename (cancel if blank), but not required

**Edge Case: Collapse state across re-renders**
- reagent/atom for collapsed turns persists across re-renders (Reagent local state)
- If entries change (new turn added), collapsed set may reference old turns — harmless, set just has extra keys
- No cleanup needed

**Edge Case: Fork deleted while editing its name**
- If editing atom references a fork-id that gets deleted, the fork disappears from the list
- Editing atom still holds stale fork-id — harmless, input just disappears
- No special handling needed

**Absolute Index Correctness**
- CRITICAL: Must compute absolute index correctly for jump-to
- entries-by-turn preserves original entry order within each turn group
- Absolute index must match the flat entries list index for the same entry
- Pattern: reduce over groups accumulating offset, each entry index = offset + local-index

**Reagent Form-2 Components**
- Components with local atoms MUST use Form-2 (fn [] (let [state (r/atom ...)] (fn [] [:div ...])))
- The outer fn runs once (setup), inner fn re-renders
- Forgetting Form-2 = atom recreated every render = state lost

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO layout assumptions (no hardcoded widths, no position:absolute, no flex parent assumptions)
- ❌ NO game logic in views (dispatch events, subscribe to data, nothing else)
- ❌ NO component-local atoms for state that belongs in app-db (collapse and editing state are UI-only, OK as local atoms)
- ❌ NO direct history/core.cljs requires from views — only go through events/subs
- ❌ NO defn- for the three new composable components (must be public for external composition)
- ❌ NO Form-1 components with local state (must use Form-2 pattern for reagent/atom)
close_reason: All 3 composable view components implemented: action-log (turn-grouped, collapsible, click-to-rewind), fork-controls (create, inline rename, delete), fork-tree (indented hierarchy, branch switching). All layout-agnostic. make validate passes (963 tests).

title: Integration tests + old event cleanup
description: End-to-end priority flow tests: cast spell → yield → bot passes → resolves. Yield on empty stack → advances phase. Yield All resolving: resolves full stack. Yield All F6: advances through phases, stops at stops. Stop toggling persists. Cast-and-yield uses priority. Remove ::advance-phase as public event (keep internal helper). Remove ::resolve-all event (replaced by ::yield-all). Clean up any dead code. Run make validate.

title: Extract events/abilities.cljs from game.cljs
design:
## Goal
Extract mana ability activation (~120 lines) and non-mana ability activation (~200 lines) from events/game.cljs into a new events/abilities.cljs namespace. This is the smallest and least-coupled extraction, serving as a safe first step.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## CRITICAL: Event Keyword Namespace Change
Moving re-frame event handlers from game.cljs to abilities.cljs changes the registered keyword namespace:
- OLD: `::activate-mana-ability` in game.cljs → `:fizzle.events.game/activate-mana-ability`
- NEW: `::activate-mana-ability` in abilities.cljs → `:fizzle.events.abilities/activate-mana-ability`

This means ALL dispatch sites (core.cljs UI) and ALL test files calling these functions must be updated.

## Exact Functions to Move

**Move to abilities.cljs:**
- `activate-mana-ability` (lines 734-840, public fn)
- `::activate-mana-ability` (lines 843-847, event handler)
- `build-ability-target-selection` (lines 852-872, private fn)
- `confirm-ability-target` (lines 875-915, public fn)
- `activate-ability` (lines 918-980, public fn)
- `::activate-ability` (lines 983-992, event handler)
- `::confirm-ability-target` (lines 995-1004, event handler)
- `::select-ability-object-target` (lines 1035-1048, event handler)

**DO NOT move (cast-time targeting → stays in game.cljs, moves to selection.cljs later):**
- `::confirm-cast-time-target` (lines 1007-1016) — calls forward-declared `confirm-cast-time-target` at line ~2581
- `::select-cast-time-object-target` (lines 1019-1032)

## Implementation

### Step 1: Create events/abilities.cljs
Create `src/main/fizzle/events/abilities.cljs` with namespace:
```clojure
(ns fizzle.events.abilities
  (:require
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as queries]
    [fizzle.engine.abilities :as abilities]
    [fizzle.engine.effects :as effects]
    [fizzle.engine.events :as game-events]
    [fizzle.engine.state-based :as state-based]
    [fizzle.engine.targeting :as targeting]
    [fizzle.engine.trigger-dispatch :as dispatch]
    [fizzle.engine.triggers :as triggers]
    [re-frame.core :as rf]))
```

Move the 8 functions/handlers listed above. Keep exact same function signatures and behavior.

### Step 2: Update events/game.cljs
- Remove the 8 moved functions/handlers (lines 732-1004 and 1035-1048)
- Add require: `[fizzle.events.abilities]` (ensures event registration at startup)
- Cast-time targeting handlers (lines 1007-1032) remain in game.cljs
- Verify remaining code has no broken references to moved functions

### Step 3: Update core.cljs dispatches
File: `src/main/fizzle/core.cljs`
- Add require: `[fizzle.events.abilities :as ability-events]`
- Update dispatches:
  - Line 97: `::events/activate-mana-ability` → `::ability-events/activate-mana-ability`
  - Line 148: `::events/activate-ability` → `::ability-events/activate-ability`
  - Line 555: `::events/confirm-ability-target` → `::ability-events/confirm-ability-target`
  - Line 619: `::events/select-ability-object-target` → `::ability-events/select-ability-object-target`
  - Line 635: `::events/confirm-ability-target` → `::ability-events/confirm-ability-target`

### Step 4: Update test file imports
These test files call `game/activate-mana-ability` or `game/activate-ability` as functions. They need:
- Add require: `[fizzle.events.abilities :as abilities]` (or similar alias)
- Change function call prefixes from `game/` to `abilities/`

**Files requiring update:**
1. `src/test/fizzle/events/tap_land_test.cljs` — ~20 calls to `game/activate-mana-ability`
2. `src/test/fizzle/cards/basic_lands_test.cljs` — ~6 calls to `game/activate-mana-ability`
3. `src/test/fizzle/cards/led_test.cljs` — 6 calls to `game/activate-mana-ability`
4. `src/test/fizzle/cards/lotus_petal_test.cljs` — 8 calls to `game/activate-mana-ability`
5. `src/test/fizzle/cards/city_of_traitors_test.cljs` — 2 calls to `game/activate-mana-ability`
6. `src/test/fizzle/cards/cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs` — calls to `game/activate-mana-ability`, `game/activate-ability`, `game/confirm-ability-target`
7. `src/test/fizzle/cards/polluted_delta_test.cljs` — calls to `game/activate-ability`, `game/activate-mana-ability`
8. `src/test/fizzle/engine/cob_event_trigger_test.cljs` — ~6 calls to `game/activate-mana-ability`

**NOT affected (these reference confirm-cast-time-target which stays in game.cljs):**
- `src/test/fizzle/cards/recoup_test.cljs`
- `src/test/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis_test.cljs`

### Step 5: Verify
- `make test` (all tests pass)
- `make lint` (no linter warnings)
- `make fmt-check` (formatting correct)
- Verify no forward declarations in abilities.cljs
- Verify no circular dependencies (abilities.cljs does NOT require game.cljs)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Boundary Precision:**
The line range 850-1050 contains cast-time targeting handlers (lines 1007-1032) that belong in selection.cljs. These MUST stay in game.cljs during this task. Only move the 8 items explicitly listed above.

**Event Keyword Namespace Change:**
This is the highest-risk aspect. Moving event handlers changes their registered keyword. If even one dispatch site is missed, that UI action silently fails (re-frame dispatches to unregistered events are no-ops). Run the app and click through ability activation to verify.

**Namespace Loading Order:**
Re-frame events must be registered before dispatch. Adding `[fizzle.events.abilities]` to game.cljs ns requires ensures the namespace loads and events register at startup.

**Function Name Collision:**
Both `fizzle.engine.abilities` and `fizzle.events.abilities` exist. Test files will need different aliases (e.g., `ability-events` for events, `abilities` for engine). Check each test file's existing aliases.

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO changing function signatures or behavior (pure structural move)
- ❌ NO modifying test assertions (only import paths and call prefixes change)
- ❌ NO hardcoding old keyword namespaces in new file (e.g., don't register as `:fizzle.events.game/activate-ability` in abilities.cljs)
- ❌ NO leaving stale forward declarations in game.cljs for moved functions
- ❌ NO moving cast-time targeting handlers (lines 1007-1032) to abilities.cljs

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `src/main/fizzle/events/abilities.cljs` exists with all 8 listed functions/handlers
- [ ] events/game.cljs no longer contains the 8 moved items
- [ ] Cast-time targeting handlers (::confirm-cast-time-target, ::select-cast-time-object-target) remain in game.cljs
- [ ] core.cljs dispatches updated to use `::ability-events/*` keywords
- [ ] All 8 test files updated with new import and function call prefixes
- [ ] No forward declarations needed in abilities.cljs
- [ ] No circular dependencies (abilities.cljs does NOT require game.cljs)
- [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] Manual smoke test: ability activation works in browser
close_reason: Extracted 8 functions/handlers (302 lines) into events/abilities.cljs. Updated core.cljs dispatches and 8 test files. Zero forward declarations, no circular deps. make validate passes (741 tests, 0 failures).

title: Add mana pool + object creation boundary specs with validation chokepoints
description: Add HIGH-risk boundary specs: mana pool validation at engine/mana.cljs and object creation validation at sharing/restorer.cljs + engine/zones.cljs. Uses goog.DEBUG pattern matching existing chokepoints.
design:
## Goal
Add spec validation chokepoints for the 3 HIGH-risk unvalidated boundaries: mana pool operations and object creation during restore/zone entry. These catch silent failures (missing mana color keys producing nil, malformed creature stats).

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing chokepoint patterns
Read these files for the established validation pattern:
- src/main/fizzle/engine/spec_util.cljs — validate-at-chokepoint! function, goog.DEBUG gate
- src/main/fizzle/engine/stack.cljs — create-stack-item uses validate-stack-item! (example of chokepoint)
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection/core.cljs — set-pending-selection uses console.error (different pattern)

### 2. Study mana boundaries
- src/main/fizzle/engine/mana.cljs — find add-mana, spend-mana, empty-pool functions
- src/main/fizzle/db/game_state.cljs:30-32 — initial mana pool creation {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
- Understand what mana maps flow through these functions

### 3. Study object creation boundaries  
- src/main/fizzle/sharing/restorer.cljs:77-111 — snapshot restore creates object maps
- src/main/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs:96-104 — zone entry creates/moves objects
- Understand what fields objects have (creature power/toughness, zone, owner, etc.)

### 4. Write tests first (TDD)

**Mana spec tests** (src/test/fizzle/engine/mana_spec_test.cljs):
- Valid mana pool {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0} passes :game/mana-pool
- Mana pool missing a color key fails (catches the merge-with + nil bug)
- Mana pool with :generic key fails (catches typo that causes silent failure)
- Mana pool with negative value fails
- Partial mana map {:black 3} passes :game/mana-map but fails :game/mana-pool
- add-mana with valid map does NOT trigger chokepoint error
- add-mana with invalid map (bad key) triggers chokepoint error in dev mode

**Object creation spec tests** (if new spec file created):
- Valid object map passes spec
- Object missing required field fails
- Creature without power/toughness fails (if creature type)
- Object with invalid zone keyword fails

### 5. Add mana validation chokepoint
In engine/mana.cljs, add dev-only validation at add-mana and spend-mana entry points:
- Import spec_util
- Use validate-at-chokepoint! or similar goog.DEBUG-gated pattern
- Validate the mana map argument against :game/mana-map (NOT :game/mana-pool — add-mana receives partial maps)
- The mana pool in the db should be validated against :game/mana-pool at creation time (game_state.cljs)

### 6. Add object creation validation chokepoint
Investigate restorer.cljs and zones.cljs to determine:
- What spec to validate against (may need a new :game/object spec in spec_common or its own file)
- Where exactly to insert validation (at the point portable maps are transacted into Datascript)
- Use goog.DEBUG pattern matching existing chokepoints

### 7. Add generators for new specs
Update spec_generators.cljs with generators for any new specs created in this task.
These generators will be used by the property tests in the next task.

### 8. Verify
- make validate passes
- All 3243+ tests pass
- New chokepoints fire in dev mode for invalid data
- New chokepoints are dead-code eliminated in production (:advanced compilation)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Mana map validation exists at engine/mana.cljs boundary (add-mana/spend-mana)
- [ ] Mana pool validation exists at initial pool creation (game_state.cljs)
- [ ] Object creation validation exists at restorer.cljs and/or zones.cljs boundaries
- [ ] All new chokepoints use goog.DEBUG pattern (dev-only, production dead-code eliminated)
- [ ] Tests verify valid data passes and invalid data triggers chokepoint errors
- [ ] Generators added for any new specs
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] All existing tests pass (no regressions)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: add-mana receives partial maps, not full pools**
add-mana is called with {:black 3} (partial mana to add), not a full 6-key pool. Validate against :game/mana-map (partial OK) not :game/mana-pool (requires all 6). The pool in the db is the full pool — validate THAT against :game/mana-pool at creation time.

**Edge Case: Object specs must be DESCRIPTIVE**
Per epic anti-pattern: NO production code changes to satisfy specs. If restorer.cljs creates objects that dont match a spec, the SPEC is wrong, not the code. Read actual object creation sites first, then write specs that describe what they produce.

**Edge Case: Zone entry may not create new objects**
zones.cljs move-to-zone might just update an existing entity, not create a new one. Investigate whether validation should be at creation or at move. The chokepoint should be where new data enters the system.

**Edge Case: Test isolation with goog.DEBUG**
Existing tests rely on goog.DEBUG being true (spec validation runs during tests). New chokepoints will ALSO fire during tests. If existing tests create invalid mana maps or objects, they will now fail. This is DESIRED BEHAVIOR — it catches real bugs.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO production code changes to satisfy specs (descriptive: specs describe existing shapes)
- ❌ NO skipping dev-only guards (pattern: goog.DEBUG matching existing chokepoints)
- ❌ NO validating add-mana against :game/mana-pool (it receives partial maps, use :game/mana-map)
- ❌ NO mana specs accepting arbitrary keys (only the 6 valid mana colors)
close_reason: Mana + object boundary specs with chokepoints, 3266 tests pass, generators added

title: Update implement-card skill with view-layer verification step
description: The implement-card skill (Phase 5 verification) has no view-layer checks. When a card introduces a new selection type or card-source value, nothing prompts verifying that: (1) render-selection-modal has a defmethod for the new type, (2) ::selection-cards subscription handles the new card-source, (3) the modal correctly renders valid-targets filtering. This gap caused Duress hand-reveal and Chain of Vapor chain-bounce selection types to ship without view support. Add a View Layer Verification section to Phase 5 of the skill.
