title: Update City of Brass and Gemstone Mine card definitions
design:
## Goal
Update City of Brass and Gemstone Mine to use data-driven :card/triggers, :card/abilities, and :card/etb-effects fields.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## File Path
src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

## Implementation

### 1. Update City of Brass

Current definition (text-only):
```clojure
(def city-of-brass
  {:card/id :city-of-brass
   :card/name "City of Brass"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/text "Whenever City of Brass becomes tapped, it deals 1 damage to you. {T}: Add one mana of any color."})
```

New definition (data-driven):
```clojure
(def city-of-brass
  {:card/id :city-of-brass
   :card/name "City of Brass"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/text "Whenever City of Brass becomes tapped, it deals 1 damage to you. {T}: Add one mana of any color."
   
   ;; Trigger: fires when this permanent becomes tapped (ANY tap, not just mana ability)
   :card/triggers [{:trigger/type :becomes-tapped
                    :trigger/effects [{:effect/type :deal-damage
                                       :effect/amount 1
                                       :effect/target :controller}]}]
   
   ;; Mana ability: T: Add one mana of any color
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true}
                     :ability/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana
                                        :effect/mana {:any 1}}]}]})
```

**Key decisions:**
- :effect/target :controller (not :self) - damage goes to player, not land
- :trigger/type :becomes-tapped - matches engine/triggers.cljs implementation
- :ability/type :mana - identifies as mana ability (doesn't use stack)
- :ability/cost {:tap true} - uses costs.cljs :tap multimethod

### 2. Update Gemstone Mine

Current definition (text-only):
```clojure
(def gemstone-mine
  {:card/id :gemstone-mine
   :card/name "Gemstone Mine"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/text "Gemstone Mine enters the battlefield with three mining counters on it. {T}, Remove a mining counter from Gemstone Mine: Add one mana of any color. If there are no mining counters on Gemstone Mine, sacrifice it."})
```

New definition (data-driven):
```clojure
(def gemstone-mine
  {:card/id :gemstone-mine
   :card/name "Gemstone Mine"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/text "Gemstone Mine enters the battlefield with three mining counters on it. {T}, Remove a mining counter from Gemstone Mine: Add one mana of any color. If there are no mining counters on Gemstone Mine, sacrifice it."
   
   ;; ETB effect: add 3 mining counters to this permanent
   :card/etb-effects [{:effect/type :add-counters
                       :effect/counters {:mining 3}
                       :effect/target :self}]
   
   ;; Mana ability: T, remove mining counter: Add one mana of any color
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true
                                    :remove-counter {:mining 1}}
                     :ability/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana
                                        :effect/mana {:any 1}}]}]})
   ;; Note: Sacrifice when 0 counters is handled by state-based action, not card ability
```

**Key decisions:**
- :card/etb-effects is separate from :card/abilities (ETB fires on entry, not activation)
- :effect/target :self for ETB - counters go on the permanent entering
- :remove-counter {:mining 1} in ability cost - uses costs.cljs multimethod
- Sacrifice at 0 counters NOT in card data - handled by SBA system

## Success Criteria
- [ ] City of Brass has :card/triggers field with :becomes-tapped trigger
- [ ] City of Brass has :card/abilities field with :tap cost
- [ ] City of Brass trigger effect uses :deal-damage (not :lose-life)
- [ ] City of Brass trigger target is :controller (player, not permanent)
- [ ] Gemstone Mine has :card/etb-effects with :add-counters
- [ ] Gemstone Mine has :card/abilities with {:tap true :remove-counter {:mining 1}}
- [ ] Gemstone Mine ETB target is :self
- [ ] Both cards still in all-cards vector
- [ ] Existing tests still pass (card definitions backward compatible)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO hardcoded sacrifice logic in Gemstone Mine (use SBA)
- NO damage logic in City of Brass abilities (use trigger)
- NO :effect/target :self for damage (damage goes to controller, not land)
- NO mixing ETB effects in :card/abilities

## Edge Cases Handled
- :effect/target :controller vs :self - damage to player, counters to permanent
- Empty :card/triggers on Gemstone Mine - no triggered abilities
- Empty :card/etb-effects on City of Brass - no ETB effects
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 4: Implement Mode Selection UI for alternate casting modes
design:
## Goal
Implement the mode selection UI component that appears when a card has multiple valid casting modes from the same zone (e.g., a future Gush card with both normal and alternate cost from hand).

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Context
Tasks 1-3 completed the alternate casting engine infrastructure:
- get-casting-modes returns all valid modes for a card
- cast-spell-mode pays costs and tracks mode
- resolve-spell and move-spell-off-stack use mode's on-resolve

Deep Analysis has been added to the test deck (completed in prior session).

Missing:
- UI popover for selecting between modes (needed for future Gush-style cards)
- Event handler updates to support mode selection flow

## Implementation

### 1. Update cast event for mode selection

File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

Modify the ::cast-spell event handler:
```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::cast-spell
  (fn [db _]
    (let [game-db (:game/db db)
          selected (:game/selected-card db)]
      (if (and selected (rules/can-cast? game-db :player-1 selected))
        (let [modes (rules/get-casting-modes game-db :player-1 selected)]
          (if (= 1 (count modes))
            ;; Single mode: auto-cast
            (-> db
                (assoc :game/db (rules/cast-spell-mode game-db :player-1 selected (first modes)))
                (dissoc :game/selected-card))
            ;; Multiple modes: show selector
            (assoc db :game/pending-mode-selection
                   {:object-id selected
                    :modes modes})))
        db))))
```

### 2. Add mode selection confirmation event

File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::select-casting-mode
  (fn [db [_ mode]]
    (let [game-db (:game/db db)
          pending (:game/pending-mode-selection db)
          object-id (:object-id pending)]
      (-> db
          (assoc :game/db (rules/cast-spell-mode game-db :player-1 object-id mode))
          (dissoc :game/selected-card)
          (dissoc :game/pending-mode-selection)))))

(rf/reg-event-db
  ::cancel-mode-selection
  (fn [db _]
    (dissoc db :game/pending-mode-selection)))
```

### 3. Add subscription for pending mode selection

File: src/main/fizzle/subs/game.cljs

```clojure
(rf/reg-sub
  ::pending-mode-selection
  (fn [db _]
    (:game/pending-mode-selection db)))
```

### 4. Create mode selector UI component

File: src/main/fizzle/core.cljs (in render-game-ui function)

```clojure
(defn mode-selector
  "Popover showing available casting modes for selection."
  []
  (let [pending @(rf/subscribe [::subs/pending-mode-selection])]
    (when pending
      [:div.mode-selector.fixed.inset-0.bg-black.bg-opacity-50.flex.items-center.justify-center
       {:on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/cancel-mode-selection])}
       [:div.bg-white.rounded-lg.p-4.shadow-lg
        {:on-click #(.stopPropagation %)} ; Prevent closing when clicking inside
        [:h3.font-bold.mb-2 "Choose casting mode:"]
        [:div.space-y-2
         (for [mode (:modes pending)]
           ^{:key (:mode/id mode)}
           [:button.w-full.p-2.border.rounded.hover:bg-gray-100
            {:on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/select-casting-mode mode])}
            [:div.font-medium (name (:mode/id mode))]
            [:div.text-sm.text-gray-600
             (format-mana-cost (:mode/mana-cost mode))
             (when-let [additional (:mode/additional-costs mode)]
               (str " + " (format-additional-costs additional)))]])]]])))
```

### 5. Write tests

File: src/test/fizzle/events/mode_selection_test.cljs

```clojure
;; Test: Single-mode card auto-casts without showing UI
;; Bug caught: Regression where single-mode cards incorrectly show selector
(deftest single-mode-card-auto-casts-test
  (testing "Card with single mode auto-casts without showing selector"
    ;; Setup: Dark Ritual in hand (no alternate costs)
    ;; Action: Dispatch ::cast-spell
    ;; Assert: :game/pending-mode-selection is nil (no selector shown)
    ;; Assert: Card is on stack (was cast)
    ))

;; Test: Multi-mode card shows selector
;; Bug caught: Missing mode selector for cards with alternate costs from same zone
(deftest multi-mode-card-shows-selector-test
  (testing "Card with multiple modes from same zone shows selector"
    ;; Setup: Gush-like card in hand with primary + alternate mode
    ;; Action: Dispatch ::cast-spell
    ;; Assert: :game/pending-mode-selection contains modes
    ;; Assert: Card NOT on stack yet (waiting for selection)
    ))

;; Test: Selecting mode casts spell
;; Bug caught: Mode selection not dispatching cast-spell-mode correctly
(deftest select-mode-casts-spell-test
  (testing "Selecting a mode casts the spell with that mode"
    ;; Setup: Card with pending mode selection
    ;; Action: Dispatch ::select-casting-mode with specific mode
    ;; Assert: :game/pending-mode-selection cleared
    ;; Assert: Card is on stack with correct :object/cast-mode
    ))

;; Test: Cancel mode selection
;; Bug caught: UI stuck in selection state after cancel
(deftest cancel-mode-selection-test
  (testing "Canceling mode selection clears pending state"
    ;; Setup: Card with pending mode selection
    ;; Action: Dispatch ::cancel-mode-selection
    ;; Assert: :game/pending-mode-selection is nil
    ;; Assert: Card still in hand (not cast)
    ))
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] ::cast-spell event shows mode selector when card has 2+ valid modes from same zone
- [ ] ::cast-spell event auto-casts when card has only 1 valid mode (no selector shown)
- [ ] ::select-casting-mode event casts spell with selected mode and clears selector state
- [ ] ::cancel-mode-selection event clears selector state without casting
- [ ] Mode selector UI displays mode name, mana cost, and additional costs for each mode
- [ ] Clicking outside modal cancels selection (defensive UX)
- [ ] 4+ unit tests pass covering auto-cast, selector show, mode select, and cancel scenarios
- [ ] All existing tests pass: make test
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass: make validate

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Card has 0 modes**
- Shouldn't happen if can-cast? returns true, but defensive check
- If 0 modes, do nothing (same as can't cast)

**Edge Case: Mode becomes invalid after selector shown**
- User has mode selector open, mana pool changes (opponent effect?)
- Simplest: re-validate mode on selection, reject if invalid
- Future enhancement: re-check and update selector

**Edge Case: Click outside modal**
- Should cancel selection (implemented via overlay on-click)
- Prevents stuck UI state

**Edge Case: Keyboard accessibility**
- Out of scope for initial implementation
- Future: Add Escape key to cancel, Enter to confirm

**Test Card for Mode Selection**
- Deep Analysis doesn't test this (never 2 modes from same zone)
- Need a Gush-like test card for proper testing:
```clojure
(def test-gush-like
  {:card/id :test-gush-like
   :card/name "Test Gush-Like"
   :card/mana-cost {:colorless 4 :blue 1}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :draw :effect/amount 2}]
   :card/alternate-costs [{:alternate/id :return-islands
                           :alternate/zone :hand  ; Same zone as primary!
                           :alternate/mana-cost {}
                           :alternate/additional-costs [{:cost/type :return-lands
                                                         :cost/subtype :island
                                                         :cost/count 2}]}]})
```

**Note:** The :return-lands cost type doesn't exist yet - tests should mock or use a simpler alternate for now.

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO hardcoding specific cards in UI - use generic mode selection
- ❌ NO blocking UI for single-mode cards - auto-cast those
- ❌ NO skipping mode tracking - must use cast-spell-mode
- ❌ NO leaving selector open after cast - always clear pending state
- ❌ NO modal without click-outside-to-close - prevents stuck UI
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement :grant-mana-ability effect + granted ability discovery
description: New effect type :grant-mana-ability that grants a mana ability to all controlled lands until cleanup. Pioneer :grant/type :ability. Update ability discovery in views/battlefield.cljs and mana_activation.cljs to check object grants. Separate button row for granted abilities in UI. Granted abilities work on tapped lands.
design:
## Implement :grant-mana-ability effect + granted ability discovery

### Goal
New effect type granting mana abilities to permanents. Pioneer :grant/type :ability. Update views + engine for granted ability discovery. Separate UI button row.

### Codebase Verification
- ✓ Grant system: engine/grants.cljs with add-grant, get-grants, expire-grants
- ✓ :object/grants stored as vector of grant maps (schema.cljs:42)
- ✓ get-producible-colors at battlefield.cljs:28-44 only checks :card/abilities
- ✓ query-zone-by-criteria at queries.cljs:276-293 for finding lands
- ⚠ Mana activation file: engine/mana-activation.cljs (hyphen not underscore)
- ✓ can-activate? in engine/abilities.cljs handles :sacrifice-self cost
- ✓ grant-flashback pattern available as reference

### Step 1: Add :grant-mana-ability to card_spec

File: src/main/fizzle/engine/card_spec.cljs (line 15-22 area)
Add :grant-mana-ability to valid effect types set.

### Step 2: Implement :grant-mana-ability effect

File: src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs

```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect-impl :grant-mana-ability
  [db player-id effect object-id]
  (let [target-filter (:effect/target effect)
        ability-data (:effect/ability effect)
        game-state (q/get-game-state db)
        current-turn (or (:game/turn game-state) 1)]
    (if (= target-filter :controlled-lands)
      (let [lands (q/query-zone-by-criteria db player-id :battlefield
                    {:match/types #{:land}})
            land-ids (map :object/id lands)]
        (reduce (fn [d land-id]
                  (grants/add-grant d land-id
                    {:grant/id (random-uuid)
                     :grant/type :ability
                     :grant/source object-id
                     :grant/expires {:expires/turn current-turn
                                     :expires/phase :cleanup}
                     :grant/data ability-data}))
                db land-ids))
      db)))
```

### Step 3: Update ability discovery for grants

File: src/main/fizzle/views/battlefield.cljs

Update get-producible-colors to check grants:
```clojure
(let [card-abilities (get-in obj [:object/card :card/abilities])
      granted-abilities (->> (or (:object/grants obj) [])
                             (filter #(= :ability (:grant/type %)))
                             (map :grant/data))
      all-abilities (concat card-abilities granted-abilities)
      mana-abilities (filter #(= :mana (:ability/type %)) all-abilities)]
```

Add helper:
```clojure
(defn get-granted-mana-abilities [obj]
  (->> (or (:object/grants obj) [])
       (filter #(= :ability (:grant/type %)))
       (filter #(= :mana (:ability/type (:grant/data %))))))
```

### Step 4: Add ::activate-granted-mana-ability event

File: src/main/fizzle/events/abilities.cljs

New event handler activating granted ability by grant-id:
- Looks up grant on object via :object/grants
- Validates can-activate? for grant's costs
- Pays costs (sacrifice-self moves to graveyard)
- Executes effects (add-mana)
- Mana ability = no stack, resolves immediately

### Step 5: Add granted ability UI (separate button row)

File: src/main/fizzle/views/battlefield.cljs

New component for granted ability buttons:
- Rendered below native ability buttons
- Distinct style (amber border/bg to indicate temporary grant)
- NOT disabled when tapped (no :tap cost)
- Label shows cost + production (e.g. 'Sac: B')
- Dispatches ::activate-granted-mana-ability with object-id + grant-id

```clojure
(defn- granted-ability-button [object-id grant]
  (let [ability (:grant/data grant)
        produces (:ability/produces ability)
        color (first (keys produces))
        symbol (get mana-symbols color (name color))]
    [:button {:class "py-0.5 px-1.5 mx-0.5 border border-amber-500 rounded text-[11px] font-bold cursor-pointer bg-amber-100 text-amber-800"
              :on-click #(rf/dispatch [::ability-events/activate-granted-mana-ability
                                       object-id (:grant/id grant)])}
     (str "Sac: " symbol)]))
```

### Step 6: Write tests

Engine tests:
- :grant-mana-ability adds grants to all controlled lands
- Grants expire at cleanup (use grants/expire-grants)
- Granted ability activation: sacrifice land -> get {B}
- Granted ability works on tapped lands (no :tap cost)
- Only lands at resolution get grant (not later lands)
- No lands = no-op

Run: make validate

title: Phase 4 Epic 4: Hand Sculpting + London Mulligan
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Must-contain card picker on setup screen: select 0-N cards from decklist guaranteed in opening hand
- Must-contain picker uses toggleable chips UI, click to increment count (cycles 0 → max copies)
- Must-contain settings persisted in presets via localStorage
- Maximum must-contain cards: 7
- Sculpted cards are part of the 7-card hand (not extra) — if 3 sculpted, 4 random
- New :active-screen :opening-hand between setup and game
- Opening hand screen shows 7-card hand with sculpted cards visually marked
- Opening hand screen shows library card count
- Keep and Mulligan buttons on opening hand screen
- London mulligan: on mulligan, return all hand cards to library, shuffle, re-deal (sculpted cards guaranteed)
- Mulligan count tracked and displayed
- After keeping with mulligan-count > 0: bottom-card picker sub-phase
- Bottom-card picker: select exactly mulligan-count cards to put on bottom of library
- Bottom-card picker marks sculpted cards to discourage bottoming them
- New Game always goes through opening hand screen
- History tracking starts only when transitioning to :game (mulligans are pre-history)
- All tests passing
- make validate passing

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Setup screen has must-contain chip picker showing all unique deck cards
- [ ] Must-contain settings saved/loaded in presets
- [ ] Start Game goes to :opening-hand screen (not directly to :game)
- [ ] Opening hand shows 7 cards with sculpted cards marked
- [ ] Opening hand shows library count
- [ ] Mulligan reshuffles, re-deals, preserves sculpted cards, increments count
- [ ] Can mulligan multiple times with increasing bottom count
- [ ] Keep with 0 mulligans goes straight to :game
- [ ] Keep with N mulligans shows bottom-card picker for N cards
- [ ] Bottom-card picker marks sculpted cards
- [ ] Bottom-card selection moves cards to bottom of library
- [ ] New Game from game screen goes through opening hand
- [ ] History starts clean when entering :game (no mulligan entries)
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] make validate passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO skipping the opening hand screen (flow: always show it, even if no sculpting desired)
- NO auto-keeping hands (interaction: player always explicitly keeps)
- NO mulligan without bottom selection when mulligan-count > 0 (rules: London mulligan requires choosing cards to bottom)
- NO sculpted cards as extra beyond 7 (rules: sculpted cards fill slots in the 7-card hand)
- NO history entries for mulligans (architecture: history starts at game transition, not before)
- NO opening hand logic in events/game.cljs (architecture: separate events/opening_hand.cljs namespace)
- NO opening hand logic in events/setup.cljs (architecture: opening hand requires live :game/db, setup is pre-game)

## Approach
Create a new :active-screen :opening-hand phase between setup and game. Must-contain picker lives on setup screen as toggleable chips, persisted in presets. Game init modified to accept :must-contain config, place sculpted cards in hand, draw remaining slots randomly, and return :opening-hand screen. New events/opening_hand.cljs namespace handles mulligan/keep/bottom events. Sculpted cards marked in both hand display and bottom-card picker. History tracking deferred until :game transition.

## Architecture
New files:
- events/opening_hand.cljs — mulligan, keep, bottom-card events (~150 lines)
- views/opening_hand.cljs — opening hand screen view (~100 lines)
- subs/opening_hand.cljs — opening hand subscriptions (~50 lines)

Modified files:
- events/game.cljs — init-game-state accepts :must-contain, returns :opening-hand
- events/setup.cljs — must-contain state management, presets extension
- subs/setup.cljs — must-contain subscriptions
- views/setup.cljs — must-contain chip picker section
- core.cljs — :opening-hand routing case, new requires

State keys:
- :setup/must-contain — map of {card-id count} (setup phase)
- :opening-hand/mulligan-count — integer (opening hand phase)
- :opening-hand/sculpted-ids — set of object-ids (opening hand phase)
- :opening-hand/phase — :viewing or :bottoming (opening hand phase)

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Currently the game starts with a random 7-card hand with no mulligan option. Storm combo practice requires specific opening hands (e.g., must have Dark Ritual + Lion's Eye Diamond) and London mulligan decisions are a key skill to practice.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- events/setup.cljs:150-161 — start-game-handler validates deck and calls game/init-game-state
- events/game.cljs:35-116 — init-game-state shuffles deck, draws 7, returns {:active-screen :game}
- events/game.cljs:25-32 — deck-to-card-ids expands deck config to shuffled card-id vector
- views/modals.cljs — rich modal/selection patterns with builder/confirm
- storage.cljs — EDN localStorage persistence, presets are arbitrary config maps
- engine/zones.cljs — shuffle-library, move-to-zone pure functions
- core.cljs:95-97 — routing via (case screen :setup [...] [game-screen])

**External:**
- London mulligan rules: draw 7, decide keep/mulligan. On keep after N mulligans, put N cards on bottom in any order.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. New :active-screen :opening-hand ✓
**What it is:** Dedicated screen between setup and game. Own events/views/subs namespaces. History starts at game transition.
**Investigation:**
- core.cljs routing is a simple case statement — adding :opening-hand is trivial
- init-game-state already returns {:active-screen :game} — changing to :opening-hand is clean
- No history/rules complications since mulligans are pre-history
**Pros:** Clean separation, lightest touch on existing systems, testable handlers
**Cons:** Adds third screen state, three new files
**Chosen because:** Opening hand is conceptually setup-adjacent, not gameplay. Avoids history/rules complications.

#### 2. Modal overlay on game screen ❌
**What it is:** Game screen loads with overlay modal for mulligan decisions.
**Why we looked at this:** Rich modal precedent in modals.cljs (12+ modal types)
**Investigation:** Reviewed modals.cljs patterns. Modals work well for single-step decisions, but mulligan is multi-step (view → mulligan/keep → maybe bottom cards).
**Pros:** No routing changes, follows existing modal patterns
**Cons:** Mixes concerns (game UI visible but non-functional), history interceptor would record mulligan events, visual clutter
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Multi-step flow doesn't fit single-modal pattern. History contamination.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Mulligan becomes a single-step decision.

#### 3. Game phase :opening-hand in Datascript ❌
**What it is:** Add :opening-hand as a :game/phase value in Datascript.
**Why we looked at this:** Most rules-accurate representation of MTG opening hand procedure
**Investigation:** Reviewed phase system (advance-phase, next-phase, start-turn). Phase transitions are tied to turn system. Opening hand is pre-turn-1.
**Pros:** Rules-accurate, integrates with phase bar
**Cons:** Heaviest change, rules engine needs casting guards, history records mulligans, phase system not designed for pre-turn phases
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Opening hand doesn't fit the turn-based phase model. Excessive blast radius on existing phase/rules code.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Major phase system rewrite is already planned.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Must-contain picker on setup screen
- Opening hand screen with mulligan/keep/bottom flow
- Preset persistence for must-contain settings
- Sculpted card marking in hand and bottom picker

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Deck selection changes (Epic 2 scope)
- Opponent mulligan AI (practice tool, not needed)
- Partial Paris mulligan (London only)
- Scry after mulligan (not in Premodern rules)

### Open Questions
- Exact chip UI styling (resolve during implementation)
- Whether must-contain picker should be collapsible (resolve during implementation)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Where should must-contain picker live? | Setup screen | Persists in presets, part of deck config flow |
| Architecture for opening hand? | New :active-screen | Separate concern, no history complications |
| Sculpted cards part of 7 or extra? | Part of 7 | If 3 sculpted, 4 random. Matches real MTG |
| Mark sculpted in bottom picker? | Yes | Visual indicator helps informed decisions |
| Must-contain picker UI? | Toggleable chips | Compact, fits setup screen style |
| New Game behavior? | Always show opening hand | Consistent flow, good for practice reps |
| Show deck count? | Yes | Display library count on opening hand screen |

### Research Deep-Dives
#### Game Initialization Flow
**Question explored:** How does init-game-state work and where to intercept for mulligan?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/game.cljs:35-116 — full init-game-state implementation
- events/setup.cljs:150-161 — start-game-handler entry point
**Findings:**
- init-game-state takes {:main-deck [...] :clock-turns 4}
- Shuffles deck via deck-to-card-ids, creates library objects, draws 7 via effects/execute-effect :draw
- Returns {:game/db ... :active-screen :game} merged with history/init-history
**Conclusion:** Modify to accept :must-contain, separate sculpted cards before shuffle, return :opening-hand

#### Selection System Patterns
**Question explored:** Can we reuse selection modals for bottom-card picker?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/selection.cljs — builder/confirm pattern
- views/modals.cljs — modal components
**Findings:**
- Selection system is tightly coupled to spell resolution flow (remaining-effects, spell-id)
- Bottom-card picker is simpler — fixed count, no spell context
- Better to build a dedicated bottom picker in views/opening_hand.cljs using shared modal components
**Conclusion:** Use shared components (selectable-card-class, confirm-button) but not the selection event machinery

### Dead-End Paths
#### Reuse selection system for bottom-card picker
**Why explored:** Rich selection UI already exists
**Investigation:** Selection events require spell-id, remaining-effects context. Opening hand bottom picker has no spell.
**Why abandoned:** Selection system assumes spell resolution context. Simpler to build dedicated picker.

### Open Concerns Raised
- None raised during design
close_reason: All 15 success criteria verified. Review-implementation approved. Manual validation complete. 1094 tests passing.

title: Create opening hand subscriptions (subs/opening_hand.cljs)
design:
## Goal
Create subs/opening_hand.cljs with subscriptions for the opening hand view. These provide reactive access to the opening hand state keys (mulligan-count, sculpted-ids, phase, bottom-selection) and derived data (hand objects enriched with sculpted status, library count, bottom selection valid).

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours (simple layer 2/3 subscriptions following established patterns)

## Context
- Events exist in events/opening_hand.cljs — handlers manage :opening-hand/* keys in app-db
- Game state lives in :game/db (Datascript), opening-hand state lives at top-level app-db keys
- Subscription pattern: layer 2 = direct db extraction, layer 3 = derived via :<- arrows
- Reference: subs/game.cljs and subs/setup.cljs for patterns

## Implementation

### File: src/main/fizzle/subs/opening_hand.cljs

```clojure
(ns fizzle.subs.opening-hand
  (:require
    [fizzle.db.queries :as queries]
    [fizzle.subs.game :as game-subs]
    [re-frame.core :as rf]))

;; Layer 2: extraction subscriptions
(rf/reg-sub ::mulligan-count (fn [db _] (:opening-hand/mulligan-count db)))
(rf/reg-sub ::sculpted-ids (fn [db _] (:opening-hand/sculpted-ids db)))
(rf/reg-sub ::phase (fn [db _] (:opening-hand/phase db)))
(rf/reg-sub ::bottom-selection (fn [db _] (:opening-hand/bottom-selection db)))

;; Layer 3: derived subscriptions

;; Library count for display on opening hand screen
(rf/reg-sub
  ::library-count
  :<- [::game-subs/game-db]
  (fn [game-db _]
    (when game-db
      (count (queries/get-objects-in-zone game-db :player-1 :library)))))

;; Whether confirm-bottom is valid (selected count == mulligan count)
(rf/reg-sub
  ::bottom-selection-valid?
  :<- [::bottom-selection]
  :<- [::mulligan-count]
  (fn [[selection mulligan-count] _]
    (and selection mulligan-count
         (= (count selection) mulligan-count))))
```

### File: src/main/fizzle/core.cljs (add bare require)
Add to require vector:
```clojure
[fizzle.subs.opening-hand]
```

### Subscriptions Summary

| Subscription | Layer | Returns | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| ::mulligan-count | 2 | integer or nil | Display mulligan count |
| ::sculpted-ids | 2 | set of UUIDs or nil | Mark sculpted cards in view |
| ::phase | 2 | :viewing or :bottoming or nil | Determine which sub-view to show |
| ::bottom-selection | 2 | set of UUIDs or nil | Track selected bottom cards |
| ::library-count | 3 | integer or nil | Display library card count |
| ::bottom-selection-valid? | 3 | boolean | Enable/disable confirm button |

### Notes on Hand Objects
The view will use the existing ::game-subs/hand subscription from subs/game.cljs (line 17-21). No need to duplicate. The view component will combine ::hand with ::sculpted-ids and ::bottom-selection locally.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] subs/opening_hand.cljs exists with 6 subscriptions (4 layer 2, 2 layer 3)
- [ ] ::mulligan-count returns (:opening-hand/mulligan-count db)
- [ ] ::sculpted-ids returns (:opening-hand/sculpted-ids db)
- [ ] ::phase returns (:opening-hand/phase db)
- [ ] ::bottom-selection returns (:opening-hand/bottom-selection db)
- [ ] ::library-count returns count of library zone objects (derived from game-db)
- [ ] ::bottom-selection-valid? returns true when (count selection) == mulligan-count
- [ ] core.cljs has bare require [fizzle.subs.opening-hand]
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + fmt + tests)

## Key Considerations

**No Test File Needed**
These are trivial layer 2 extractions and simple layer 3 derivations. The event tests in opening_hand_test.cljs already verify the state keys are set correctly. Writing subscription tests would be tautological (testing that (fn [db _] (:key db)) returns db's :key value). The view integration test will validate subscriptions work end-to-end.

**Reuse ::game-subs/hand**
Don't create a separate ::hand subscription. The existing ::game-subs/hand (subs/game.cljs:17-21) queries objects in :hand zone. The view will combine this with ::sculpted-ids client-side.

**Nil Safety**
All subscriptions return nil when opening-hand state isn't active (pre-game or post-keep). The view must handle nil gracefully. The layer 3 subs use (when ...) guards matching the pattern in subs/game.cljs.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO duplicating the ::hand subscription from subs/game.cljs
- NO complex derived subscriptions that combine game-db queries with opening-hand state (keep layer 3 simple, combine in view)
- NO subscription tests for trivial extractions (tautological)
- NO requiring events/opening_hand.cljs from subs (subs never require events)
close_reason: 6 subscriptions created (4 layer 2, 2 layer 3). core.cljs bare require added. make validate passes: 1094 tests, 0 failures.

title: Create opening hand view + must-contain chip picker + core.cljs routing
design:
## Goal
Create the opening hand view screen, add must-contain chip picker to setup screen, and wire routing in core.cljs. This is the visual layer that connects the already-implemented events and subscriptions to the user.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (3 components: opening hand view, must-contain picker, routing)

## Context
- Events: events/opening_hand.cljs — ::mulligan, ::keep, ::toggle-bottom, ::confirm-bottom
- Events: events/setup.cljs — ::toggle-must-contain, ::clear-must-contain
- Subscriptions: subs/opening_hand.cljs — ::mulligan-count, ::sculpted-ids, ::phase, ::bottom-selection, ::library-count, ::bottom-selection-valid?
- Subscriptions: subs/game.cljs — ::hand (hand objects), ::game-db
- Subscriptions: subs/setup.cljs — ::must-contain-cards (enriched card data for picker)
- Card data: (get-in obj [:object/card :card/name]) for display name
- Object ID: (:object/id obj) for click handlers
- Tailwind classes: bg-surface, bg-surface-raised, border-border, border-border-selected, text-text, text-text-label, text-text-muted

## Implementation

### 1. File: src/main/fizzle/views/opening_hand.cljs (~100 lines)

```clojure
(ns fizzle.views.opening-hand
  (:require
    [fizzle.events.opening-hand :as oh-events]
    [fizzle.subs.game :as game-subs]
    [fizzle.subs.opening-hand :as oh-subs]
    [re-frame.core :as rf]))
```

**Component: card-view [obj sculpted? selected? on-click]**
Renders a single card in the opening hand. Shows card name with visual indicators.
- Base classes: same as hand.cljs card-view (border-2 rounded-md px-3.5 py-2.5 mx-1.5 min-w-[100px] text-center select-none text-text)
- Sculpted indicator: if sculpted?, add a small marker (e.g., ring-2 ring-accent or border-accent)
- Selected state (for bottoming): border-border-selected bg-surface-hover
- Default state: border-border bg-surface-raised
- On click: calls on-click with object-id

**Component: viewing-phase []**
The main opening hand display with Keep/Mulligan buttons.
- Subscribe to: ::game-subs/hand, ::oh-subs/sculpted-ids, ::oh-subs/mulligan-count, ::oh-subs/library-count
- Layout: centered content, max-w-2xl mx-auto p-8
- Header: "Opening Hand" with mulligan count display (e.g., "Mulligans: 2")
- Library count: "Library: 53 cards"
- Card display: flex flex-wrap justify-center, render card-view for each hand object
  - sculpted? = (contains? sculpted-ids (:object/id obj))
  - on-click = nil (no selection in viewing phase)
- Buttons: flex gap-4 justify-center mt-6
  - Keep button: bg-btn-enabled-bg text-white, dispatches [::oh-events/keep]
  - Mulligan button: bg-surface-raised text-text border border-border, dispatches [::oh-events/mulligan]

**Component: bottoming-phase []**
Card selection UI for choosing cards to put on bottom of library.
- Subscribe to: ::game-subs/hand, ::oh-subs/sculpted-ids, ::oh-subs/mulligan-count, ::oh-subs/bottom-selection, ::oh-subs/bottom-selection-valid?
- Header: "Put N cards on the bottom of your library" (N = mulligan-count)
- Selection counter: "Selected: M / N" (M = count of bottom-selection)
- Card display: flex flex-wrap justify-center, render card-view for each hand object
  - sculpted? = (contains? sculpted-ids (:object/id obj))
  - selected? = (contains? bottom-selection (:object/id obj))
  - on-click = #(rf/dispatch [::oh-events/toggle-bottom (:object/id obj)])
- Confirm button: enabled when bottom-selection-valid? is true
  - Enabled: bg-btn-enabled-bg text-white cursor-pointer
  - Disabled: bg-btn-disabled-bg text-border cursor-default opacity-50
  - Dispatches [::oh-events/confirm-bottom]

**Component: opening-hand-screen []**
Top-level component that switches between viewing and bottoming phases.
- Subscribe to ::oh-subs/phase
- (case phase :bottoming [bottoming-phase] [viewing-phase])

### 2. File: src/main/fizzle/views/setup.cljs (add ~30 lines)

**Component: must-contain-picker []**
Chip picker showing all unique deck cards for must-contain selection.
- Subscribe to: ::setup-subs/must-contain-cards, ::setup-subs/current-main
- Section label: "Hand Sculpting" (text-text-label text-xs font-bold uppercase tracking-wider)
- Total count display: "N / 7 cards sculpted"
- Chip grid: flex flex-wrap gap-1.5
- For each card in current-main (ALL unique deck cards, not just selected ones):
  - Compute must-contain count for this card-id from must-contain-cards
  - Chip: px-2 py-0.5 text-xs rounded-full border cursor-pointer select-none
  - Active chip (count > 0): border-accent bg-accent/20 text-text, show "Card Name xN"
  - Inactive chip (count 0): border-border bg-surface-raised text-text-muted, show "Card Name"
  - On click: rf/dispatch [::setup/toggle-must-contain card-id]
- Clear button (when any must-contain selected): text-xs text-text-muted cursor-pointer underline, dispatches [::setup/clear-must-contain]

**Integration into setup-screen:**
Add [must-contain-picker] between [opponent-config] and [action-buttons] in setup-screen component (line 155 of setup.cljs).

### 3. File: src/main/fizzle/core.cljs (3 lines changed)

Add require:
```clojure
[fizzle.views.opening-hand :as opening-hand]
```

Replace placeholder (lines 98-99):
```clojure
:opening-hand [opening-hand/opening-hand-screen]
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Opening hand screen shows 7 cards with card names visible
- [ ] Sculpted cards have distinct visual marker (border-accent or similar)
- [ ] Library count displayed on opening hand screen
- [ ] Mulligan count displayed and updates after each mulligan
- [ ] Keep button dispatches ::oh-events/keep
- [ ] Mulligan button dispatches ::oh-events/mulligan
- [ ] After mulligan, hand re-renders with new cards (sculpted preserved)
- [ ] Keep with mulligans shows bottoming phase
- [ ] Bottoming phase shows "Put N cards on bottom" header
- [ ] Clicking cards toggles them in/out of bottom selection
- [ ] Confirm button disabled until exactly N cards selected
- [ ] Confirm button dispatches ::oh-events/confirm-bottom and transitions to game
- [ ] Setup screen shows must-contain chip picker with all deck cards
- [ ] Clicking chip cycles count (0->1->...->max->0)
- [ ] Active chips show count, inactive chips show card name only
- [ ] Clear button resets must-contain
- [ ] Total sculpted count shown (N / 7)
- [ ] core.cljs routes :opening-hand to opening-hand-screen component
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + fmt + tests)

## Key Considerations

**Sculpted Card Visual Marker**
Use border-accent (accent color from theme) to distinguish sculpted cards. In viewing phase, sculpted cards get ring-2 ring-accent. In bottoming phase, sculpted cards get the same ring but can still be selected for bottoming (player's choice per epic anti-patterns).

**Card Ordering in Hand**
The ::game-subs/hand subscription returns objects as-is from Datascript query. Objects don't have guaranteed order. Consider sorting by card name for consistent display. This is a UI-only concern — no event changes needed.

**Must-Contain Picker: Show ALL Deck Cards**
The chip picker shows every unique card in the current main deck, not just those currently in must-contain. This lets the user see what's available to sculpt. Subscribe to ::setup-subs/current-main for the full card list and ::setup-subs/must-contain for counts.

**No View Tests**
Reagent component tests are not part of this project's test suite. Validation is visual (dev server) + make validate for lint/fmt/compilation.

**Phase Nil Handling**
When ::oh-subs/phase is nil (not on opening hand screen), the opening-hand-screen component won't render (routed away). No nil guard needed in the component itself since core.cljs only renders it when :active-screen is :opening-hand.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO creating new subscriptions in the view file (use subs/opening_hand.cljs and subs/game.cljs)
- NO dispatching game events from opening hand (only dispatch opening-hand events)
- NO blocking sculpted cards from bottom selection (player's choice per epic spec)
- NO auto-keeping hands (player must explicitly click Keep)
- NO complex state logic in view components (events handle all logic)
- NO adding opening hand logic to events/game.cljs or events/setup.cljs
close_reason: Closed

Add to validate target. Handle false positives by excluding comments and th/ helper references.
