title: Make move-resolved-spell idempotent with zone guard
description: Engine-side change for spell disposition unification. See epic fizzle-5388.
design:
## Goal
Add a zone guard to move-resolved-spell so it returns unchanged db when the spell is not on :stack. Migrate console.warn from cleanup-selection-source.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (zone guard + tests + verification)

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - engine/resolution.cljs:106-152 — move-resolved-spell definition
   - engine/resolution.cljs:195-220 — resolve-spell-type (DIRECT caller, pre-fetches obj BEFORE effects)
   - events/selection/core.cljs:263-304 — cleanup-selection-source (SELECTION caller)
   - events/selection/core.cljs:297-303 — console.warn for unexpected zones (will migrate)
   - engine/effects/zones.cljs:51-57 — :exile-self effect (read-only, no changes)
   - src/test/fizzle/engine/resolution_test.cljs — existing 234-line test file

2. Write tests first (TDD)
   Add tests in engine/resolution_test.cljs. Use th/create-test-db + th/add-card-to-zone to set up objects in specific zones.

   **Existing behavior tests (regression prevention):**
   - test_move_resolved_spell_on_stack_goes_to_graveyard: spell with zone :stack, no cast-mode → moves to :graveyard
   - test_move_resolved_spell_flashback_goes_to_exile: spell with zone :stack, :mode/on-resolve :exile → moves to :exile
   - test_move_resolved_spell_copy_on_stack_removed: copy with zone :stack → removed from db entirely
   - test_move_resolved_spell_permanent_goes_to_battlefield: creature/enchantment with zone :stack → :battlefield + triggers registered

   **New idempotent behavior tests:**
   - test_move_resolved_spell_in_exile_noop: spell with zone :exile → db unchanged (catches exile-self case)
   - test_move_resolved_spell_in_graveyard_noop: spell with zone :graveyard → db unchanged (catches double-move)
   - test_move_resolved_spell_copy_in_exile_noop: copy with zone :exile → db unchanged (copy exile-self'd before cleanup)
   - test_move_resolved_spell_unexpected_zone_warns: spell with zone :hand → db unchanged + console.warn emitted

   **CRITICAL test — stale obj parameter:**
   - test_move_resolved_spell_refetches_zone: Pass a stale obj with zone :stack, but db has object in :exile → must return db unchanged (proves zone is read from db, not stale obj)

3. Implementation checklist
   - [ ] engine/resolution.cljs:move-resolved-spell — CRITICAL: re-fetch object zone from db parameter, do NOT trust obj parameter's zone. Insert zone guard BEFORE the copy check (line 124). Guard: (let [current-zone (:object/zone (queries/get-object db object-id))] (if (not= current-zone :stack) db ...))
   - [ ] engine/resolution.cljs:move-resolved-spell — add console.warn for unexpected zones: if current-zone is not in #{:stack :exile :graveyard}, warn (migrated from cleanup-selection-source)
   - [ ] Verify resolve-spell-type path (line 220) works: this caller passes stale obj, so the re-fetch in the guard is essential
   - [ ] Verify cleanup-selection-source path works: cleanup passes fresh obj from queries/get-object, so both paths are covered
   - [ ] Run make validate — all 3134+ tests pass, lint clean, format clean

## Success Criteria
- [ ] move-resolved-spell returns unchanged db when spell's CURRENT zone (re-fetched from db) is not :stack
- [ ] Console.warn emitted for unexpected zones (not in #{:stack :exile :graveyard})
- [ ] Stale obj parameter does not bypass zone guard (critical: test with obj.zone=:stack but db has :exile)
- [ ] Copy handling preserved (copies on :stack removed, copies off :stack are no-op)
- [ ] Flashback disposition preserved (:mode/on-resolve :exile respected when spell is on :stack)
- [ ] All existing card tests pass unchanged (make test)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**CRITICAL: Stale obj parameter at resolution.cljs:220**
resolve-spell-type pre-fetches obj BEFORE running effects. If :exile-self runs during resolve-spell-effects, the obj still has :object/zone :stack. The zone guard MUST re-fetch the object's current zone from the db parameter (which reflects post-effect state), NOT trust the obj parameter. This is the primary correctness risk in this task.

**Edge Case: Copy already removed**
If a copy ceased to exist (removed by effects), queries/get-object returns nil. The zone guard (which calls get-object) must handle nil — treat as already-handled (return db unchanged). This aligns with cleanup-selection-source's nil spell-obj branch (core.cljs:283-285).

**Edge Case: Spell in :hand zone**
A bounce effect during resolution could put the spell back in hand. The console.warn should fire for :hand (unexpected zone for post-resolution spell). The guard returns db unchanged — do NOT try to move a spell that was bounced.

**Edge Case: Permanent entering battlefield**
The zone guard must NOT prevent permanents from entering the battlefield. Permanents have :object/zone :stack when move-resolved-spell is called (they were cast onto the stack). The guard only blocks non-:stack zones.

**Reference Implementation**
- The pattern of re-fetching state from db rather than trusting parameters is established in this codebase (e.g., engine/effects/zones.cljs:55 checks get-object-eid before moving)

## Anti-patterns
- NO modifying :exile-self effect (engine/effects/zones.cljs:51-57 must remain unchanged)
- NO trusting the obj parameter's zone field — MUST re-fetch from db
- NO adding new parameters to move-resolved-spell signature (keep backward compatible)
- NO removing the copy check — copies must still be handled (removed when on stack)

title: Add :target/type :any targeting and creature damage for Fling
description: Implement mixed player+creature targeting and extend :deal-damage to handle creature targets. This completes the remaining epic success criteria for Fling's any-target capability.
design:
## Goal
Add :target/type :any to the targeting system (allowing both players and creatures as targets) and extend :deal-damage to mark damage on creatures. Update Fling to use :any targeting.

## Existing Infrastructure (study first)
- engine/targeting.cljs — find-valid-targets, target validation
- engine/effects/zones.cljs or similar — :deal-damage effect executor (currently player-only)
- engine/creatures.cljs — effective-power, effective-toughness (NO mark-damage function exists — must create or use :object/damage-marked directly)
- engine/combat.cljs — deal-damage-to-player (for reference)
- db/schema.cljs — :object/damage-marked attribute exists (line 49)
- cards/red/fling.cljs — currently has :target/type :player, needs :target/type :any
- cards/green/crumble.cljs — example of :target/type :object with match criteria

## Implementation

### 1. Add :target/type :any to targeting system (engine/targeting.cljs)
- Extend find-valid-targets to handle :target/type :any
- Return BOTH valid player targets AND valid creature/object targets on the battlefield
- UI selection should show players and creatures in one modal
- Study how :target/type :object (Crumble) and :target/type :player (Abeyance) work separately

### 2. Extend :deal-damage effect for creatures
- The :deal-damage effect executor currently only handles player targets
- Check if target is an object (creature on battlefield) or player
- For player targets: existing deal-damage-to-player behavior
- For creature targets: set :object/damage-marked on the creature (add damage, don't replace)
- NOTE: mark-damage does NOT exist as a function — either create it in creatures.cljs or use d/db-with directly
- SBA for lethal damage is OUT OF SCOPE (per epic scope boundaries)

### 3. Update Fling card definition
- Change :target/type :player to :target/type :any in cards/red/fling.cljs
- Remove any TODO comment about targeting

### 4. Add targeting view support
- The selection UI needs to handle :target/type :any — showing both players and creatures
- Study existing targeting views in views/selection/

### 5. Tests (TDD)
- test: Fling can target a creature, deals damage equal to sacrificed power
- test: Fling can target a player (existing behavior preserved)
- test: :target/type :any shows both players and creatures as valid targets
- test: :deal-damage marks damage on creature (:object/damage-marked)
- test: :deal-damage still works on player targets (no regression)
- test: Fling with 0-power sacrifice deals 0 damage to creature
- test: Cannot target creature not on battlefield

## Success Criteria
- [ ] :target/type :any returns both players and battlefield creatures as valid targets
- [ ] :deal-damage correctly marks damage on creatures via :object/damage-marked
- [ ] :deal-damage still works on players (no regression)
- [ ] Fling card uses :target/type :any targeting
- [ ] Fling tests cover creature targeting (mandatory category F)
- [ ] All existing tests pass (make validate)
- [ ] No separate :deal-damage-to-creature effect created (extend existing)

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ DO NOT create separate :deal-damage-to-creature effect — extend existing :deal-damage
- ❌ DO NOT hardcode Fling-specific logic in the engine — :target/type :any is generic
- ❌ DO NOT implement SBA for lethal damage — out of scope per epic
- ❌ DO NOT use tautological assertions in tests

## Effort Estimate
~4-5 hours
close_reason: Closed

title: Create engine/state_based.cljs with registry pattern
design:
## Goal
Create the state-based actions module with registry pattern for extensibility.

## Effort Estimate
4 hours

## File Path
src/main/fizzle/engine/state_based.cljs
src/test/fizzle/engine/state_based_test.cljs

## Implementation

### 1. Create state_based.cljs with registry pattern

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.state-based
  "State-based actions for Fizzle.
   
   SBAs are checked after every game action. Uses registry pattern
   for extensibility - add new SBA types without modifying existing code.
   
   All functions are pure: (db, args) -> db"
  (:require
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
    [fizzle.engine.zones :as zones]))

;; Registry of SBA check functions
;; Each check-fn: (db) -> seq of {:sba/type keyword :sba/target object-id}
(defonce sba-registry (atom {}))

(defn register-sba!
  "Register a state-based action check function.
   
   Arguments:
     sba-type - Keyword identifying the SBA (e.g. :zero-counters)
     check-fn - Function (db) -> seq of SBA events to execute
   
   Returns:
     nil (side effect: updates registry)"
  [sba-type check-fn]
  (swap! sba-registry assoc sba-type check-fn)
  nil)

(defn check-all-sbas
  "Run all registered SBA checks.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
   
   Returns:
     Seq of SBA events: {:sba/type keyword :sba/target object-id}"
  [db]
  (mapcat (fn [[_type check-fn]] (check-fn db))
          @sba-registry))

(defmulti execute-sba
  "Execute a single state-based action.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     sba - Map with :sba/type and :sba/target
   
   Returns:
     New db after SBA executed."
  (fn [_db sba] (:sba/type sba)))

(defmethod execute-sba :default [db _sba] db)

;; === Zero Counters SBA (Gemstone Mine) ===

(defn- check-zero-counters
  "Find permanents that have counter requirements but 0 counters.
   
   Checks :object/counters map - if any counter type has 0 or negative value
   and the card requires counters (has :card/abilities with :remove-counter cost),
   the permanent should be sacrificed."
  [db]
  (let [battlefield-objects (d/q '[:find [(pull ?obj [* {:object/card [*]}]) ...]
                                   :where [?obj :object/zone :battlefield]]
                                 db)]
    (for [obj battlefield-objects
          :let [counters (:object/counters obj)
                card (:object/card obj)
                abilities (:card/abilities card)
                ;; Check if any ability has remove-counter cost
                has-counter-cost? (some #(get-in % [:ability/cost :remove-counter]) abilities)]
          ;; Only sacrifice if: has counter-using ability AND all counters depleted
          :when (and has-counter-cost?
                     counters
                     (every? (fn [[_type amt]] (<= amt 0)) counters))]
      {:sba/type :zero-counters
       :sba/target (:object/id obj)})))

(defmethod execute-sba :zero-counters
  [db sba]
  (let [object-id (:sba/target sba)]
    ;; Move to graveyard (sacrifice)
    (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :graveyard)))

;; Register built-in SBAs
(register-sba! :zero-counters check-zero-counters)

(defn check-and-execute-sbas
  "Check all SBAs and execute any that apply.
   
   Runs checks repeatedly until no more SBAs fire (loop handles cascading SBAs).
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
   
   Returns:
     New db after all SBAs executed."
  [db]
  (loop [db' db]
    (let [sbas (check-all-sbas db')]
      (if (empty? sbas)
        db'
        (recur (reduce execute-sba db' sbas))))))
```

### 2. Create tests

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.state-based-test
  (:require
    [cljs.test :refer [deftest testing is]]
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.engine.state-based :as sba]))

(deftest test-register-sba
  (testing "can register custom SBA check"
    (let [called (atom false)
          check-fn (fn [_db] (reset! called true) [])]
      (sba/register-sba! :test-sba check-fn)
      (sba/check-all-sbas {})
      (is @called "Custom SBA check should be called"))))

(deftest test-check-all-sbas-empty
  (testing "returns empty when no SBAs apply"
    ;; Create db with permanent that has counters
    (let [db (d/db-with (d/empty-db)
               [{:object/id (random-uuid)
                 :object/zone :battlefield
                 :object/counters {:mining 3}
                 :object/card {:card/abilities [{:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}}]}}])
          sbas (sba/check-all-sbas db)]
      (is (empty? sbas) "No SBAs when counters > 0"))))

(deftest test-zero-counters-detected
  (testing "finds permanents with 0 counters that require counters"
    (let [obj-id (random-uuid)
          db (d/db-with (d/empty-db)
               [{:object/id obj-id
                 :object/zone :battlefield
                 :object/counters {:mining 0}
                 :object/card {:card/abilities [{:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}}]}}])
          sbas (sba/check-all-sbas db)]
      (is (= 1 (count sbas)))
      (is (= :zero-counters (:sba/type (first sbas))))
      (is (= obj-id (:sba/target (first sbas)))))))

(deftest test-zero-counters-sacrifice
  (testing "executes sacrifice for zero-counter permanent"
    (let [obj-id (random-uuid)
          db (d/db-with (d/empty-db)
               [{:object/id obj-id
                 :object/zone :battlefield
                 :object/counters {:mining 0}
                 :object/card {:card/abilities [{:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}}]}}])
          db-after (sba/check-and-execute-sbas db)
          obj-after (d/q '[:find ?zone .
                           :in $ ?oid
                           :where [?e :object/id ?oid]
                                  [?e :object/zone ?zone]]
                         db-after obj-id)]
      (is (= :graveyard obj-after) "Object should be in graveyard after sacrifice"))))

(deftest test-non-zero-counters-ignored
  (testing "permanents with counters > 0 not sacrificed"
    (let [obj-id (random-uuid)
          db (d/db-with (d/empty-db)
               [{:object/id obj-id
                 :object/zone :battlefield
                 :object/counters {:mining 1}
                 :object/card {:card/abilities [{:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}}]}}])
          db-after (sba/check-and-execute-sbas db)
          obj-zone (d/q '[:find ?zone .
                          :in $ ?oid
                          :where [?e :object/id ?oid]
                                 [?e :object/zone ?zone]]
                        db-after obj-id)]
      (is (= :battlefield obj-zone) "Object should still be on battlefield"))))
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] src/main/fizzle/engine/state_based.cljs exists
- [ ] register-sba! function works (verified by test)
- [ ] check-all-sbas returns seq of SBA events
- [ ] execute-sba multimethod dispatches on :sba/type
- [ ] :zero-counters SBA detects permanents with 0 counters
- [ ] :zero-counters SBA moves permanent to graveyard
- [ ] check-and-execute-sbas loops until no more SBAs
- [ ] 5 tests in state_based_test.cljs all pass
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO card ID checks (check :card/abilities data, not :card/id)
- NO hardcoded Gemstone Mine references
- NO mutable state outside sba-registry atom
- NO blocking/looping without termination condition

## Edge Cases Handled
- Empty counters map: Treated as 0 for all counter types
- Negative counters: Treated same as 0 (sacrifice)
- No counter-using abilities: Not sacrificed even if counters exist
- Cascading SBAs: Loop handles multiple SBAs firing
close_reason: Closed

title: Decompose events/game.cljs into casting, resolution, and priority sub-modules
description: events/game.cljs (903 lines, 152/416 commits) braids three independent concerns. Extract into events/casting.cljs (~250 lines), events/resolution.cljs (~100 lines), events/priority_flow.cljs (~420 lines). game.cljs becomes a re-export facade. Follows existing pattern from phases/cleanup/lands extractions. See ADR-016.
design: ADR-016. Extract order: casting (zero deps) → resolution (one dep) → priority (orchestrator). Each extraction is a separate commit with tests green between each step. Re-export from game.cljs preserves all downstream requires.
close_reason: Superseded by epic fizzle-h5ss (full namespace normalization, broader scope)

title: Priority engine core + schema
description: Create engine/priority.cljs with pure state machine functions. Add schema fields: :game/passed, :game/auto-mode, :game/stops, :player/bot-archetype. Functions: yield-priority (add player to passed set, check if both passed), transfer-priority (move priority to other player), reset-passes (clear passed set), check-stop (does player have stop at phase), should-auto-advance (check auto-mode + stops). All pure functions operating on game-db.
close_reason: Premature decomposition

title: Creature data model and effective P/T engine
description: Foundation task: Add creature fields to schema, create engine/creatures.cljs with effective-power/effective-toughness computation, and summoning sickness tracking. Pure engine work — no combat flow, no UI, no tokens yet.
design:
## Goal
Establish the creature data model in Datascript, create the core engine module for creature stat computation, extend static abilities with :pt-modifier, and implement Nimble Mongoose as the first creature card. After this task, creatures exist on the battlefield with correct P/T (including conditional modifiers), summoning sickness, and shroud.

## Effort Estimate
8-10 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Schema additions (db/schema.cljs, lines 30-44)
Add after existing `:object/` fields:
```clojure
:object/power           {}  ; integer — base power (from card definition)
:object/toughness       {}  ; integer — base toughness (from card definition)
:object/damage-marked   {}  ; integer — damage taken this turn (default 0)
:object/summoning-sick  {}  ; boolean — entered battlefield this turn
:object/attacking       {}  ; boolean — declared as attacker this combat
:object/blocking        {}  ; ref — eid of attacker being blocked
:object/is-token        {}  ; boolean — token creature
```

### 2. Card spec update (engine/card_spec.cljs)
- Add specs for creature fields:
  ```clojure
  (s/def :card/power int?)        ; 0 is valid (Xantid Swarm, Nimble Mongoose base)
  (s/def :card/toughness pos-int?) ; toughness >= 1 on card definitions
  ```
- Add `:card/power :card/toughness` to `::card` spec's `:opt` keys (line 281-286)
- Add cross-field validation: if `:creature` in `:card/types`, then `:card/power` and `:card/toughness` required
- Extend `valid-static-types` (line 225-226): add `:pt-modifier`
- Extend `valid-applies-to` (line 233-234): add `:self`
- Add specs for P/T modifier fields:
  ```clojure
  (s/def :modifier/power int?)
  (s/def :modifier/toughness int?)
  ```
- Add `:modifier/power :modifier/toughness` to `::static-ability` spec `:opt` keys (line 245-247)

### 3. Static ability extension (engine/static_abilities.cljs)
Add parallel to existing `get-cost-modifiers`:
```clojure
(defn get-self-pt-modifiers
  "Get :pt-modifier static abilities on a specific object that apply to :self.
   Checks conditions (e.g., threshold) against the object's controller.
   Returns vector of {:power N :toughness N} maps for applicable modifiers."
  [db object-eid]
  ;; 1. Pull the object's card static-abilities
  ;; 2. Filter for :pt-modifier with :applies-to :self
  ;; 3. Check :modifier/condition via conditions/check-condition
  ;; 4. Return applicable {:power N :toughness N} maps
  )
```
This function is called by effective-power/effective-toughness in creatures.cljs.

### 4. Zone transition update (engine/zones.cljs, lines 43-84, `move-to-zone`)
When `new-zone` is `:battlefield`:
- Look up card definition via `:object/card` ref: `(d/pull db '[:card/types :card/power :card/toughness] (:db/id (:object/card (d/pull db '[{:object/card [:card/types :card/power :card/toughness]}] obj-eid))))`
- If card has `:creature` in `:card/types`:
  - Add to txs: `[:db/add obj-eid :object/power (:card/power card)]`
  - Add to txs: `[:db/add obj-eid :object/toughness (:card/toughness card)]`
  - Add to txs: `[:db/add obj-eid :object/summoning-sick true]`
  - Add to txs: `[:db/add obj-eid :object/damage-marked 0]`
- If card is NOT a creature, do NOT set any creature fields
When `current-zone` is `:battlefield` (leaving):
- If the object has creature fields, retract them to prevent stale state on re-entry

### 5. Create engine/creatures.cljs (NEW FILE)
```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.creatures
  "Pure functions for creature stat computation and predicates."
  (:require [datascript.core :as d]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
            [fizzle.engine.grants :as grants]
            [fizzle.engine.static-abilities :as static]))

(defn creature?
  "Check if object's card has :creature in :card/types."
  [game-db object-eid]
  ;; Pull :object/card ref -> check :card/types for :creature
  )

(defn effective-power
  "Compute effective power: base + counters + grants + self-static-abilities.
   Returns nil for non-creatures."
  [game-db object-eid]
  ;; Guard: (when (creature? ...) ...)
  ;; base: (:object/power entity)
  ;; counters: (- (get counters :+1/+1 0) (get counters :-1/-1 0))
  ;; grants: (reduce + 0 (map :grant/power (grants/get-grants-by-type db obj-id :pt-modifier)))
  ;; static: (reduce + 0 (map :power (static/get-self-pt-modifiers db object-eid)))
  ;; Result can be negative — do not clamp
  )

(defn effective-toughness
  "Same as effective-power but for toughness."
  [game-db object-eid]
  ;; Same structure, uses :object/toughness, :grant/toughness, :toughness
  )

(defn has-keyword?
  "Check if creature has keyword from card definition OR granted keywords."
  [game-db object-eid kw]
  ;; Union of :card/keywords and :keyword grants
  )

(defn summoning-sick?
  "True if creature with :object/summoning-sick and no haste."
  [game-db object-eid]
  ;; (and (creature?) (:object/summoning-sick) (not (has-keyword? :haste)))
  )

(defn can-attack? [game-db object-eid]
  ;; creature? AND not summoning-sick? AND not tapped AND not defender
  )

(defn can-block? [game-db object-eid attacker-eid]
  ;; creature? AND not tapped AND flying/reach rules
  )
```

### 6. Summoning sickness clearing
Find the untap-step handler in events/game.cljs (where untap triggers fire). Add clearing of `:object/summoning-sick` for active player's creatures at untap step.

### 7. Damage clearing
Find cleanup step handler in events/game.cljs (near `grants/expire-grants` call). Add clearing of `:object/damage-marked` for all creatures at cleanup.

### 8. Shroud targeting restriction
Find targeting validation in engine/targeting.cljs. Add check: if target object has `:shroud` in its keywords (via `has-keyword?`), targeting is invalid.

### 9. Nimble Mongoose card (cards/green/nimble_mongoose.cljs — NEW FILE)
```clojure
(def card
  {:card/id :nimble-mongoose
   :card/name "Nimble Mongoose"
   :card/cmc 1
   :card/mana-cost {:green 1}
   :card/colors #{:green}
   :card/types #{:creature}
   :card/subtypes #{:mongoose}
   :card/power 1
   :card/toughness 1
   :card/keywords #{:shroud}
   :card/text "Shroud\nThreshold — Nimble Mongoose gets +2/+2 as long as there are seven or more cards in your graveyard."
   :card/static-abilities [{:static/type :pt-modifier
                            :modifier/power 2
                            :modifier/toughness 2
                            :modifier/condition {:condition/type :threshold}
                            :modifier/applies-to :self}]})
```

### 10. Registry update (cards/registry.cljs)
Require `fizzle.cards.green.nimble-mongoose` and add to card collection.

### 11. Card test file (test/fizzle/cards/green/nimble_mongoose_test.cljs — NEW FILE)
Follow mandatory card testing categories from CLAUDE.md:

**A. Card definition** — verify ALL fields with exact values
**B. Cast-resolve happy path** — cast Nimble Mongoose, verify it enters battlefield with P/T 1/1
**C. Cannot-cast guards** — insufficient mana (no green), wrong zone
**D. Storm count** — casting increments storm
**E. Threshold P/T** — with 7+ cards in graveyard, effective P/T is 3/3; without threshold, 1/1
**F. Shroud** — creature cannot be targeted by spells or abilities
**G. Edge cases**:
  - Threshold gained mid-game (put 7th card in GY, P/T updates)
  - Threshold lost (card removed from GY below 7, P/T drops back)
  - Summoning sickness on entry, cleared after untap
  - Cannot attack first turn (summoning sickness)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Schema includes all 7 creature fields
- [ ] Card spec validates :card/power (int?) and :card/toughness (pos-int?) on creature cards
- [ ] Card spec enforces: creature type requires power+toughness
- [ ] valid-static-types includes :pt-modifier
- [ ] valid-applies-to includes :self
- [ ] static-ability spec accepts :modifier/power and :modifier/toughness
- [ ] Creatures entering battlefield get :object/power, :object/toughness, :object/summoning-sick, :object/damage-marked
- [ ] Non-creature permanents entering battlefield do NOT get creature fields
- [ ] effective-power returns nil for non-creatures
- [ ] effective-power computes: base + counters + grants + self-static-abilities
- [ ] Nimble Mongoose effective P/T is 1/1 without threshold, 3/3 with threshold
- [ ] Threshold change dynamically updates effective P/T (no stale state)
- [ ] summoning-sick? returns true for new creatures without haste, false for haste
- [ ] summoning-sick? returns false for non-creatures
- [ ] can-attack? respects: creature type, summoning sickness, tapped, defender
- [ ] can-block? respects: creature type, tapped, flying/reach
- [ ] Summoning sickness clears at untap step for active player only
- [ ] Damage marks clear at cleanup step
- [ ] Creature leaving battlefield has creature fields retracted
- [ ] Shroud prevents targeting in engine/targeting.cljs
- [ ] Nimble Mongoose card definition passes card spec validation
- [ ] Nimble Mongoose card test file covers categories A-G (8+ tests)
- [ ] All existing tests pass (no regression): `make validate`

## Specific Test Scenarios (Bug-Catching)

**effective-power / effective-toughness:**
- test_base_power_only — creature with no modifiers returns card P/T (catches: base lookup broken)
- test_zero_power_creature — Nimble Mongoose without threshold returns 0... wait, 1/1 base. Test with a 0-power creature fixture (catches: nil vs 0)
- test_plus_counters — +1/+1 counter adds correctly (catches: counter contribution ignored)
- test_minus_counters — -1/-1 counter subtracts correctly (catches: wrong sign)
- test_pt_grant — temporary +3/+3 grant works (catches: grant type filter wrong)
- test_multiple_grants_additive — two grants stack (catches: only first grant read)
- test_self_static_ability — Nimble Mongoose threshold P/T is 3/3 (catches: static ability scan missing)
- test_static_condition_not_met — Mongoose without threshold is 1/1 (catches: condition check skipped)
- test_non_creature_returns_nil — calling on land returns nil (catches: missing guard)
- test_nil_damage_treated_as_zero — creature without damage-marked attr (catches: nil arithmetic)

**Nimble Mongoose specific:**
- test_mongoose_card_definition — all fields match exact values (catches: typos, wrong values)
- test_mongoose_enters_bf_1_1 — enters as 1/1 with summoning sickness (catches: entry logic)
- test_mongoose_threshold_3_3 — 7+ cards in GY makes it 3/3 (catches: threshold not checked)
- test_mongoose_threshold_dynamic — add 7th card to GY, check P/T updates (catches: stale computation)
- test_mongoose_lose_threshold — remove card from GY below 7, back to 1/1 (catches: cached value)
- test_mongoose_shroud_blocks_targeting — can't target with spells (catches: shroud check missing)
- test_mongoose_summoning_sick_on_entry — can't attack turn it enters (catches: missing flag)
- test_mongoose_attacks_after_untap — can attack after your untap (catches: sickness not cleared)
- test_mongoose_cast_increments_storm (catches: creature casting not counted)

**summoning-sick? / can-attack? / can-block?:**
- test_new_creature_is_sick (catches: missing flag on entry)
- test_after_untap_not_sick (catches: missing clear)
- test_haste_overrides (catches: haste not checked)
- test_granted_haste_overrides (catches: only checking card keywords, not grants)
- test_non_creature_not_sick (catches: applying to all permanents)
- test_opponent_creature_not_cleared_on_your_untap (catches: clearing all players)
- test_tapped_cannot_attack (catches: missing tap check)
- test_defender_cannot_attack (catches: missing keyword check)
- test_flying_blocked_only_by_flying_or_reach (catches: evasion check missing)
- test_non_creature_cannot_attack (catches: missing creature guard)

**Zone transitions:**
- test_creature_entering_bf_gets_pt (catches: missing copy logic)
- test_creature_entering_bf_gets_summoning_sick (catches: missing flag)
- test_land_entering_bf_no_creature_fields (catches: applying to non-creatures)
- test_creature_leaving_bf_clears_fields (catches: stale data on re-entry)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: nil vs 0 for damage-marked**
Datascript attributes that are never set return nil, not 0. Always use `(or (:object/damage-marked entity) 0)`.

**Edge Case: Non-creature queries**
effective-power/effective-toughness MUST guard with creature? and return nil for non-creatures.

**Edge Case: Negative effective toughness**
-1/-1 counters can reduce toughness below 0. Return as-is. SBAs (later task) handle consequences.

**Edge Case: Summoning sickness clearing scope**
Only clear for active player's creatures at THEIR untap step.

**Edge Case: Leaving battlefield cleanup**
Retract creature-specific fields to prevent stale state on re-entry.

**Edge Case: Threshold dynamic update**
Nimble Mongoose P/T must reflect current threshold state at query time, not at entry time. This is guaranteed by the scan-at-check-time pattern in static_abilities.cljs.

**Edge Case: Shroud vs targeting validation**
Shroud check must happen in targeting validation (engine/targeting.cljs), not in individual card/effect code. Study existing targeting validation for where to add the check.

**Reference Patterns:**
- Static abilities: engine/static_abilities.cljs (get-cost-modifiers, modifier-applies?, get-static-abilities-from-battlefield)
- Conditions: engine/conditions.cljs:12-24 (threshold? function)
- Grant querying: grants.cljs:23-28 (get-grants-by-type)
- Zone transitions: zones.cljs:43-84 (move-to-zone, :object/tapped reset)
- Card definition pattern: cards/black/cabal_ritual.cljs (threshold conditional)
- Card test pattern: any test file in test/fizzle/cards/
- Card spec static abilities: card_spec.cljs:225-250

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO storing modified P/T on objects (correctness: compute dynamically)
- ❌ NO applying creature fields to non-creature permanents (scope: only :creature type)
- ❌ NO clamping effective P/T to 0 (accuracy: negative is valid in MTG)
- ❌ NO treating nil damage-marked as anything other than 0 (safety: use (or val 0))
- ❌ NO clearing summoning sickness for all players at untap (scope: active player only)
- ❌ NO using atoms or mutable state (immutability: all state in Datascript)
- ❌ NO adding :card/conditional-pt field (consistency: use static abilities)
- ❌ NO reimplementing production handlers in tests (testing: use th/ helpers)
- ❌ NO tautological assertions like (is (some? x)) (testing: exact value comparisons)

title: Game director model: decomplect engine from UX orchestration
description: Replace dual-orchestrator yield/bot-decide architecture with a game director loop that presents game state to player agents. Engine becomes purely game rules (no stops, no auto-mode). Eliminates cascade concept, dispatch-later races, and 6 pieces of coordination state.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- R1: Engine layer (engine/*.cljs) contains ZERO references to auto-mode, stops, or any UX/orchestration concept. Engine inputs are player actions and game state only.
- R2: A single game director loop runs after every action: check priority holder → ask their agent → apply response → repeat. No competing async loops.
- R3: Human player agent is a pure function: (game-db, player-id, stops, yield-all?) → :auto-pass | :await-input. Stops are a player concern, not engine.
- R4: Bot player agent is a pure function: (game-db, player-id) → action-map. Already exists — extract from interceptor wrapper.
- R5: History entries are generated through ONE path for all actions (human and bot). No separate history mechanisms for cascades vs normal play.
- R6: The game director is a pure function testable without re-frame. Test helpers call it directly.
- R7: All existing game behavior preserved — phase advancement, priority passing, stack resolution, SBAs, selections, cleanup discard, turn transitions, bot land play, bot casting.
- R8: The epoch fix (yield/epoch staleness guard) is removed as part of this work — the architecture it patches is replaced.

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] engine/priority.cljs has zero auto-mode functions (get/set/clear-auto-mode removed)
- [ ] engine/priority.cljs has zero stop functions (check-stop, set-player-stops, check-opponent-stop, set-opponent-stops removed)
- [ ] :game/auto-mode removed from datascript schema
- [ ] No dispatch-later in yield/cascade code path
- [ ] No :yield/epoch, :yield/step-count, :bot/action-pending? in app-db
- [ ] advance-with-stops deleted
- [ ] yield-handler replaced by game director
- [ ] db_effect does NOT dispatch ::bot-decide
- [ ] Game director is a pure function with unit tests
- [ ] Human player agent is a pure function with unit tests
- [ ] F6 (yield-all) works: cascades through opponent turn, stops at human's next stop
- [ ] Regular yield works: passes priority once, advances one stop if both pass
- [ ] Bot plays lands and casts spells during their turn
- [ ] Selections (cleanup discard, tutor, targeting) pause director and resume after confirmation
- [ ] History entries generated for all actions through one path
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO UX concepts in engine layer (auto-mode, stops, animation timing — engine is pure game rules)
- ❌ NO dispatch-later for game flow advancement (animation timing must not affect game logic)
- ❌ NO multiple competing async loops driving game advancement (one director, not two orchestrators)
- ❌ NO coordination state between orchestrators (epoch, action-pending — if you need coordination state, you have multiple orchestrators)
- ❌ NO bot-decide dispatch from db_effect (director owns bot action scheduling)
- ❌ NO separate cascade mode / auto-mode concept (F6 is a player agent behavior, not a game state)
- ❌ NO test helpers that simulate async interleaving (test the pure director function directly)

## Approach

Replace the dual-orchestrator architecture (yield-handler dispatch-later chain + db_effect bot-decide chain) with a game director model:

1. Game director: pure function that runs the game loop synchronously until a human decision point. Checks priority holder → asks player agent → applies action → runs SBAs → generates history → repeats. Returns when human agent says :await-input or when selection needed.

2. Human player agent: pure function. Given game state, stops, and yield-all flag, returns :auto-pass or :await-input. Stops and F6 are player-side concerns.

3. Bot player agent: already exists as pure functions (bot-decide-action, find-bot-land-to-play). Extract from re-frame wrapper.

4. Engine cleanup: remove auto-mode and stop accessors from engine/priority.cljs. Engine becomes purely game rules.

5. db_effect simplification: remove bot-decide dispatch. Director owns bot actions. db_effect keeps SBA execution for non-director mutations (or SBAs move into director).

6. History: director generates entries inline via descriptions.cljs (already pure). One path for all history — human and bot actions.

## Architecture

Components affected:
- engine/priority.cljs — REMOVE: 10 UX accessor functions (~60 lines)
- events/priority_flow.cljs — REWRITE: replace yield-handler/yield-impl/advance-with-stops with director
- events/db_effect.cljs — SIMPLIFY: remove bot-decide dispatch
- bots/interceptor.cljs — SIMPLIFY: remove event handlers, keep pure functions
- NEW: game director module (pure function, ~100-150 lines)
- NEW: human player agent (pure function, ~30 lines)

## Architecture Context

Model components: Engine (domain layer), Game Events (orchestration layer), Opponent AI (orchestration layer)
All components at stability_state: exploring
Co-change evidence: priority_flow + bots/interceptor changed together in 8 of last 16 commits (tight coupling)
engine/priority is stable (1 change in 16 commits) — clean shearing layer
Relevant ADRs: None directly (ADR-016, ADR-017 are tangential)

## Design Rationale

### Problem
The yield cascade and bot-decide loop are two independent async control flows that share game state but have no explicit coordination mechanism. A dispatch-later timer (100ms animation delay) races with db_effect's bot-decide dispatch, causing F6 to skip entire bot turns. The epoch fix patches the symptom but 3051 tests couldn't detect the race because test helpers simulate the cascade synchronously. UX concerns (animation timing, stops, auto-mode) are complected with the engine.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- engine/priority.cljs — contains 10 UX accessor functions (auto-mode, stops) despite being domain layer
- events/priority_flow.cljs — yield-handler has 5-branch cond, 3 branches exist for dual-orchestrator coordination
- events/db_effect.cljs — dispatches ::bot-decide after every game-db mutation, racing with cascade
- bots/interceptor.cljs — pure decision functions wrapped in re-frame event handlers
- 4 workaround-state fields (:yield/epoch, :yield/step-count, :bot/action-pending?, :bot/action-count) exist to coordinate dual orchestrators

**Architecture model:**
- Engine marked as domain layer, events marked as orchestration layer — boundary is declared but violated by UX state in engine
- bots -> events_game relationship hides the dual-path yield/bot-decide loop

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Game Director Model ✓

**What it is:** Replace cascading with a game loop that presents state to player agents. Each player (human or bot) decides via a pure function. Director runs synchronously until human decision point.

**Investigation:**
- Mapped all engine pure functions — already exist as single-step primitives
- Bot decision is already a pure function (bot-decide-action)
- Current ~300 lines of orchestration reduces to ~180 lines of pure loop + agent

**Pros:**
- Eliminates all coordination state (epoch, action-pending, step-count, action-count)
- Single loop — no races possible
- Fully testable as pure function
- Matches how MTG actually works (priority → decision → apply → repeat)

**Cons:**
- Significant event layer rewrite
- Test suite needs updating (but tests get simpler)
- No per-step animation (can add as rendering concern later)

**Chosen because:** Addresses root cause (dual orchestrator) rather than symptoms. Dramatically simpler architecture.

#### 2. Synchronous Cascade Function ❌

**What it is:** cascade-to-decision-point(app-db) runs entire cascade in one call. Similar to director but framed as 'cascade' rather than 'game loop'.

**Why we looked at this:** Natural evolution of current cascade concept

**Investigation:**
- Would require manual history generation (same as director)
- Would require inline bot actions (same as director)
- Still has 'cascade' as a concept — F6 triggers a special mode

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Still frames F6 as a special cascade mode rather than normal game loop with auto-passing player agent. Preserves conceptual complexity.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Director model proves too difficult to implement.

#### 3. Single Async Orchestrator ❌

**What it is:** Keep event-driven cascade but add :cascade/active flag to prevent db_effect from dispatching bot-decide during cascades.

**Why we looked at this:** Minimal change from current architecture

**Investigation:**
- Introduces another coordination flag (:cascade/active)
- Doesn't solve the fundamental dual-orchestrator problem
- Bot action history still needs manual generation

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Adds ANOTHER coordination mechanism to an architecture that already has too many. Treats the symptom.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Scope of director rewrite proves unacceptable.

#### 4. Epoch Staleness Guard (Current Fix) ❌

**What it is:** Add yield/epoch counter. dispatch-later includes epoch. Bot pause increments epoch. Stale timers check epoch and skip.

**Why we looked at this:** Quick fix for the immediate race condition bug

**Investigation:**
- Implemented and shipped — 20 lines, all tests pass
- Doesn't address root cause (dual orchestrator)
- 3051 existing tests didn't detect the race condition — testing a fiction

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Patches the symptom. UX timing (epoch) is now part of game flow control. Architecture that produced the bug remains.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Director model fails and we need a tactical fallback.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Engine boundary cleanup (remove UX concepts)
- Game director loop (pure function)
- Player agent interface (human + bot)
- db_effect simplification
- History generation through director
- Removing epoch fix and all coordination state
- Test rewrites

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Animation replay of director steps (future UX concern)
- New bot archetypes (separate epic)
- Selection system changes (works as-is)
- Card implementation changes (engine unchanged)

### Open Questions

- Should SBAs run inside the director loop or remain in db_effect? (Decide during implementation — both work, director-inline is simpler)
- Where does the human player agent store F6 state? app-db flag? (Decide during implementation — it's an orchestration concern)
- Does the director need to handle the ::yield event (single pass) or only ::yield-all? (Both — regular yield triggers one director step)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| What's the scariest test gap? | Architecture made it invisible — tests simulate a fiction | Test helpers must test the real mechanism (director function), not a simulation |
| Where is the complection? | Deeper — separate engine from orchestration | Engine returns 'what', orchestrator handles 'when'. Two different concerns. |
| Should advance-one-phase be the primitive? | Yes — decompose advance-with-stops | Engine provides single-step. Orchestrator composes. |
| Where should auto-mode live? | Eliminate — or defer to orchestration | Engine does NOT store auto-mode. It's a cascade concept or parameter. |
| Are stops engine or orchestration? | Orchestration — remove from engine | Engine doesn't check stops. Human agent checks stops. |
| How to resolve dual-orchestrator? | Director model — game loop with player agents | Not a cascade function, not async orchestrator. Game loop presenting state to players. |
| Animation concern? | Solve engine/orchestration first, animate later if needed | Animation is rendering, not game logic. Can replay director steps later. |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Intuition Audit: Engine/UX Boundary
12 tensions found. Root: T12 (workaround cascade) — 4 state fields exist because two async loops share state without coordination.
Secondary: T1-T3 (UX concepts in engine) — auto-mode and stops stored in game-db via engine accessors.
Co-change evidence: priority_flow + bots/interceptor change together 50% of the time (tight coupling = one system split across two files).
engine/priority is stable (1 change in 16 commits) — healthy shearing layer.

### Dead-End Paths

#### Epoch Fix (Tactical Patch)
**Why explored:** Immediate bug fix for F6 skipping turns
**Investigation:** Implemented — 20 lines, stale dispatch-later detection via epoch counter
**Why abandoned:** Patches symptom. Architecture that produced the bug remains. Tests can't verify it. User: 'revert it, do it right.'

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'The tests didn't catch the race — scary' → Director is a pure function, testable directly. No async simulation needed.
- 'This is a hard problem — our original design was a huge failure' → Director model is simpler than the original. ~180 lines replaces ~300 lines + 6 state fields.
- 'Why aren't we just presenting gamestate to each player?' → This IS the director model. The simplest possible design.
close_reason: Complete — Game director model implemented. 4 commits: a90dea0 (engine boundary), d42b200 (shared invariants), becd4b3 (scenario helper), 7dfefab (director). All success criteria met.

title: Task 4: Create Flash of Insight card definition
design:
## Goal
Create the Flash of Insight card definition file using the generic systems implemented in Tasks 1-3.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Context
- Task 1: X cost system implemented in mana.cljs (resolve-x-cost, can-pay?, pay-mana with x-value)
- Task 2: :peek-and-select effect implemented in effects.cljs + game.cljs
- Task 3: :exile-cards cost type implemented in rules.cljs

Flash of Insight is now ready to be defined as pure EDN data.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing card patterns (CRITICAL - reference these exactly)
- src/main/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis.cljs - flashback uses :card/alternate-costs NOT :card/flashback
- src/test/fizzle/engine/exile_cost_test.cljs:97-120 - has correct Flash of Insight card definition for tests

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

**Test file:** src/test/fizzle/cards/flash_of_insight_test.cljs

Specific tests with bugs each catches:

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.flash-of-insight-test
  (:require [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest is testing]]
            [fizzle.cards.flash-of-insight :as flash-of-insight]
            [fizzle.test-helpers :as h]))

(deftest flash-of-insight-card-structure-test
  ;; Bug caught: card not exported or malformed
  (testing "Flash of Insight card has required fields"
    (let [card flash-of-insight/flash-of-insight]
      (is (= :flash-of-insight (:card/id card))
          "Card ID must be :flash-of-insight")
      (is (= "Flash of Insight" (:card/name card))
          "Card name must match oracle")
      (is (= #{:instant} (:card/types card))
          "Must be instant")
      (is (= #{:blue} (:card/colors card))
          "Must be blue")
      (is (true? (:x (:card/mana-cost card)))
          "Mana cost must have :x true for X cost"))))

(deftest flash-of-insight-normal-cast-effect-test
  ;; Bug caught: effect misconfigured, wrong effect type
  (testing "Normal cast uses :peek-and-select effect"
    (let [effect (first (:card/effects flash-of-insight/flash-of-insight))]
      (is (= :peek-and-select (:effect/type effect))
          "Effect must be :peek-and-select")
      (is (= :x (:effect/count effect))
          "Effect count must be :x to use X value")
      (is (= 1 (:effect/select-count effect))
          "Must select exactly 1 card")
      (is (= :hand (:effect/selected-zone effect))
          "Selected card goes to hand")
      (is (= :bottom-of-library (:effect/remainder-zone effect))
          "Non-selected go to bottom")
      (is (true? (:effect/shuffle-remainder? effect))
          "Remainder should be shuffled (random order)"))))

(deftest flash-of-insight-flashback-cost-test
  ;; Bug caught: missing flashback, wrong mana cost, missing exile cost
  (testing "Flashback has correct costs"
    (let [alternate (first (:card/alternate-costs flash-of-insight/flash-of-insight))]
      (is (= :flashback (:alternate/id alternate))
          "Must have :alternate/id :flashback")
      (is (= :graveyard (:alternate/zone alternate))
          "Flashback casts from graveyard")
      (is (= {:colorless 1 :blue 1} (:alternate/mana-cost alternate))
          "Flashback mana cost is {1}{U}")
      (is (= :exile (:alternate/on-resolve alternate))
          "Spell exiles after flashback resolution"))))

(deftest flash-of-insight-exile-additional-cost-test
  ;; Bug caught: exile cost missing or misconfigured
  (testing "Flashback has exile blue cards additional cost"
    (let [alternate (first (:card/alternate-costs flash-of-insight/flash-of-insight))
          exile-cost (first (:alternate/additional-costs alternate))]
      (is (= :exile-cards (:cost/type exile-cost))
          "Additional cost type must be :exile-cards")
      (is (= :graveyard (:cost/zone exile-cost))
          "Exile from graveyard")
      (is (= #{:blue} (:card/colors (:cost/criteria exile-cost)))
          "Must exile blue cards")
      (is (= :x (:cost/count exile-cost))
          "Exile count is X (player chooses)"))))
```

### 3. Card definition

**File:** src/main/fizzle/cards/flash_of_insight.cljs

CRITICAL: Use :card/alternate-costs pattern (NOT :card/flashback)
CRITICAL: Use {:x true} in mana-cost (NOT {:x 1})

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.flash-of-insight
  "Flash of Insight card definition.

   Oracle text: Look at the top X cards of your library. Put one of them into
   your hand and the rest on the bottom of your library in any order.
   Flashback—{1}{U}, Exile X blue cards from your graveyard.

   Note: 'in any order' simplified to 'random order' per epic anti-pattern.")


(def flash-of-insight
  {:card/id :flash-of-insight
   :card/name "Flash of Insight"
   :card/cmc 2  ; X + 1U where X=0 gives CMC 2
   :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :blue 1 :x true}  ; {X}{1}{U}
   :card/colors #{:blue}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/text "Look at the top X cards of your library. Put one into your hand. Flashback {1}{U}—exile X blue cards from your graveyard."

   ;; Normal cast effect: peek X, select 1 for hand
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :peek-and-select
                   :effect/count :x  ; Resolved from spell's X value
                   :effect/select-count 1
                   :effect/selected-zone :hand
                   :effect/remainder-zone :bottom-of-library
                   :effect/shuffle-remainder? true}]

   ;; Flashback alternate cost
   :card/alternate-costs [{:alternate/id :flashback
                           :alternate/zone :graveyard
                           :alternate/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :blue 1}
                           :alternate/additional-costs [{:cost/type :exile-cards
                                                         :cost/zone :graveyard
                                                         :cost/criteria {:card/colors #{:blue}}
                                                         :cost/count :x}]
                           :alternate/on-resolve :exile}]})
```

### 4. Add to Iggy Pop deck pool

**File:** src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

Add require:
```clojure
[fizzle.cards.flash-of-insight :as flash-of-insight]
```

Add to all-cards vector:
```clojure
flash-of-insight/flash-of-insight
```

### 5. Verification
- Run: `make test` - All 715+ tests pass (4 new + 711 existing)
- Run: `make validate` - No lint or format errors

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Flash of Insight card file exists at src/main/fizzle/cards/flash_of_insight.cljs
- [ ] Card has mana cost {:colorless 1 :blue 1 :x true} (NOT {:x 1})
- [ ] Card uses :card/alternate-costs (NOT :card/flashback)
- [ ] Card has :peek-and-select effect with :effect/count :x
- [ ] Flashback has {1}{U} mana cost + :exile-cards additional cost
- [ ] Exile cost has :cost/criteria {:card/colors #{:blue}}
- [ ] Card added to iggy_pop.cljs all-cards vector
- [ ] 4 new structure tests pass: make test
- [ ] All 711+ existing tests pass: make test
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**CRITICAL: Use correct codebase patterns**
- Flashback uses :card/alternate-costs array, NOT :card/flashback
- X mana cost uses {:x true}, NOT {:x 1}
- Reference: deep_analysis.cljs:26-30, exile_cost_test.cljs:97-120

**Edge Case: :effect/count :x resolution**
- When effect has :effect/count :x, the engine resolves X from spell's stored X value
- Normal cast: X = mana paid
- Flashback: X = number of blue cards exiled
- Verify this works correctly with existing peek-and-select tests

**Edge Case: No blue cards in graveyard**
- Can't cast flashback if 0 blue cards available (can-pay-additional-cost? returns false)
- Already handled by Task 3's :exile-cards implementation

**Edge Case: X=0**
- Normal cast with X=0 is legal but useless (peek 0 cards)
- Flashback with X=0 should NOT be allowed (must exile at least 1)
- :cost/count :x in rules.cljs:140-141 enforces X >= 1 for exile costs

**Test already exists in exile_cost_test.cljs**
- Integration tests for flashback with exile cost already pass
- This task adds card structure tests and adds card to deck pool

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO :card/flashback key (use :card/alternate-costs array)
- ❌ NO {:x 1} in mana-cost (use {:x true})
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in engine files (card is pure data)
- ❌ NO skipping flashback mana cost (must pay {1}{U} PLUS exile)
- ❌ NO player-ordered bottom of library (use shuffle-remainder? true per epic)
close_reason: Flash of Insight card implemented with correct structure, added to Iggy Pop deck pool. 4 new tests pass, 715 total tests pass, make validate passes.
