title: Add history sidebar view and integrate into app layout
design:
## Goal
Create views/history.cljs with sidebar components (action log, step controls, fork list) and modify core.cljs layout to render the sidebar alongside the game area in a flex row.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-3xmp: events (history/events.cljs) and subscriptions (subs/history.cljs) are in place.

## Implementation

### 1. Create views/history.cljs

Namespace: fizzle.views.history
Requires: fizzle.history.events, fizzle.subs.history, re-frame.core

**Components:**

a) `history-sidebar` — Top-level sidebar component
   - Container div: fixed width 250px, height 100%, background #1a1a2a, border-left 1px solid #444, padding 12px
   - display flex, flex-direction column (header + controls at top, log fills middle, forks at bottom)
   - Contains: header, step-controls, action-log, fork-list

b) `step-controls` — Back/Forward buttons
   - Subscribe to `::subs/can-step-back?` and `::subs/can-step-forward?`
   - Two buttons: [Back] and [Forward] (or unicode arrows)
   - Enabled style: background #2a6a2a, color #fff, cursor pointer (matches core.cljs:396-405 btn-style pattern)
   - Disabled style: background #222, color #555, cursor default
   - Dispatch `::events/step-back` / `::events/step-forward` on click
   - Flex row with gap 8px, margin-bottom 12px

c) `action-log` — Scrollable list of history entries
   - Subscribe to `::subs/entries` and `::subs/position`
   - Render each entry as div with: "T{turn}: {description}"
   - Current entry (index == position): border-left 3px solid #4A9BD9, background #1a2a3a
   - Entries after position (index > position): opacity 0.4
   - Entries at/before position: normal opacity
   - Container: overflow-y auto, flex 1 (takes remaining vertical space)
   - Font: monospace, 12px, color #eee, padding 4px 8px per entry

d) `fork-list` — Inline list of branches
   - Subscribe to `::subs/forks` and `::subs/current-branch`
   - Always show "main" as first entry (current-branch nil means main is active)
   - Show each fork by :fork/name
   - Current branch: bold text, blue color (#4A9BD9)
   - Other branches: clickable (#ccc), dispatch `::events/switch-branch` with fork-id (or nil for main)
   - Section divider: border-top 1px solid #444, padding-top 8px, margin-top 8px
   - Header: "Branches" in #999, font-size 11px, text-transform uppercase, letter-spacing 1px

### 2. Modify core.cljs app function (lines 423-443)

Current: single [:div] with padding 20px, all panels inside.
Modals use position:fixed (overlay-style in modals.cljs:14) so they float above everything regardless of DOM position.

Change to:
```clojure
(defn app []
  [:div {:style {:display "flex"
                 :min-height "100vh"
                 :background "#0d0d1a"
                 :font-family "monospace"
                 :color "#eee"}}
   [:div {:style {:flex 1 :padding "20px" :overflow-y "auto"}}
    [:h1 ...] [turn-bar] [life-view] ... [stack-view]]
   [history/history-sidebar]
   [modals/selection-modal]
   [modals/mode-selector-modal]])
```

Key changes:
- Outer div gets display:flex, loses padding (moves to inner wrapper)
- New inner div wraps all game panels with flex:1 and padding:20px
- history-sidebar added after game area div
- Modals stay at same level (position:fixed means they work anywhere)

Import: add `[fizzle.views.history :as history]` to core.cljs requires.

### 3. No new tests for views
View components are pure Reagent rendering — tested implicitly via manual browser verification. Underlying events and subscriptions already have 16 tests.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] views/history.cljs exists with history-sidebar, step-controls, action-log, fork-list components
- [ ] Sidebar renders to the right of game area (fixed 250px width)
- [ ] Action log displays entries with T{turn}: {description} format
- [ ] Current entry highlighted with blue left border and brighter background
- [ ] Entries past position are dimmed (opacity 0.4)
- [ ] Step buttons dispatch events and disable at boundaries (correct styling)
- [ ] Fork list shows main + all forks, current branch highlighted blue
- [ ] Clicking a non-current branch dispatches switch-branch
- [ ] core.cljs layout updated to flex row (outer div display:flex)
- [ ] All inline JS styles matching existing conventions (dark theme, monospace, color palette)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO CSS files or Tailwind (inline JS style objects only)
- NO direct app-db reads (use re-frame subscriptions only)
- NO history logic in views (dispatch events only, no calls to history/core)
- NO click-to-jump on action log entries (deferred to Complete UI epic)
- NO turn grouping or collapsible sections (deferred to Complete UI epic)
- NO manual fork creation button (auto-fork only per epic requirement)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Layout change safety**: The core.cljs app function is the root component (lines 423-443). Modals use position:fixed (modals.cljs:14 overlay-style) so moving them in the DOM tree won't affect their rendering. The flex wrapper is safe.

**Empty state**: Before game init, entries will be empty and position will be nil. Action log should render empty (no entries to show). Step buttons will both be disabled (can-step-back? and can-step-forward? return false/nil). Fork list shows just main.

**Overflow**: Action log needs overflow-y:auto to handle long game sessions. The sidebar itself should not overflow — it fills the viewport height.

## Reference Implementations
- Button enabled/disabled pattern: core.cljs:396-405 (btn-style with enabled? param)
- Color palette: #0d0d1a (page bg), #1a1a2a (container bg), #4A9BD9 (blue accent), #eee (text), #999 (dim), #555 (border)
- Modal overlay pattern: views/modals.cljs:14 (position:fixed, z-index:1000)
- Subscription usage: core.cljs:37-46 (hand-view subscribing and rendering)
close_reason: views/history.cljs created with history-sidebar, step-controls, action-log, fork-list. core.cljs updated to flex row layout. make validate passes (934 tests, 2131 assertions). Committed.

title: Task 1: Create event bus and trigger registry infrastructure
design:
## Goal
Build the foundational event bus and trigger registry that all phase/card triggers will use.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns
- src/main/fizzle/engine/state-based.cljs:13-42 - Registry pattern with atom
- src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs:54-94 - Multimethod dispatch pattern
- src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs:82-113 - resolve-trigger multimethod (reuse for trigger resolution)

### 2. Create src/main/fizzle/engine/events.cljs
```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.events
  "Event creation helpers for the event-driven trigger system.
   Events are immutable maps describing game state changes.")

;; Event creation helpers
(defn phase-entered-event [phase turn player-id]
  {:event/type :phase-entered
   :event/phase phase
   :event/turn turn
   :event/player player-id})

(defn permanent-tapped-event [object-id controller-id]
  {:event/type :permanent-tapped
   :event/object-id object-id
   :event/controller controller-id})

(defn zone-change-event [object-id from-zone to-zone]
  {:event/type :zone-change
   :event/object-id object-id
   :event/from-zone from-zone
   :event/to-zone to-zone})
```

### 3. Create src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger-registry.cljs
```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.trigger-registry
  "Dynamic trigger registration for event-driven trigger system.
   Triggers register when cards enter active zones, unregister when leaving.")

;; Registry: {trigger-id -> trigger-def}
(defonce registry (atom {}))

(defn register-trigger!
  "Register a trigger. Idempotent - registering same ID overwrites.
   Returns the trigger-id."
  [trigger]
  (let [id (:trigger/id trigger)]
    (swap! registry assoc id trigger)
    id))

(defn unregister-trigger!
  "Remove a trigger by ID. No-op if ID doesn't exist."
  [trigger-id]
  (swap! registry dissoc trigger-id)
  nil)

(defn unregister-by-source!
  "Remove all triggers from a source (when card leaves zone).
   No-op if source has no triggers."
  [source-id]
  (swap! registry (fn [r] (->> r (remove (fn [[_ t]] (= source-id (:trigger/source t)))) (into {}))))
  nil)

(defn get-triggers-for-event
  "Find all registered triggers that match this event type.
   Returns empty seq if no matches."
  [event]
  (->> @registry vals (filter #(= (:event/type event) (:trigger/event-type %)))))

(defn get-all-triggers
  "Return all registered triggers. For debugging/testing."
  []
  (vals @registry))

(defn clear-registry!
  "Remove all triggers. Use in tests and game reset."
  []
  (reset! registry {})
  nil)
```

### 4. Create src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger-dispatch.cljs
```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.trigger-dispatch
  "Event dispatch and trigger matching.
   Handles both immediate (turn-based) and stacked (card) triggers."
  (:require [fizzle.engine.trigger-registry :as registry]
            [fizzle.engine.triggers :as triggers]))

(defn matches-filter?
  "Check if event matches trigger's filter criteria.
   :self in filter value matches trigger's source object.
   Returns true if no filter (matches all events of type)."
  [trigger event]
  (let [f (:trigger/filter trigger)]
    (if (and f (seq f))
      (every? (fn [[k v]]
                (cond
                  (= v :self) (= (get event k) (:trigger/source trigger))
                  :else (= (get event k) v)))
              f)
      true)))

(defn dispatch-event
  "Dispatch event to all matching triggers. Returns new db.
   Turn-based actions (uses-stack? false) execute immediately.
   Card triggers (uses-stack? true) go on stack.
   Order: immediate triggers first, then stacked triggers."
  [db event]
  (let [all-triggers (registry/get-triggers-for-event event)
        matching (filter #(matches-filter? % event) all-triggers)
        ;; Group by uses-stack? - note: false = immediate, true = stacked
        {stacked true, immediate false} (group-by #(get % :trigger/uses-stack? true) matching)]
    (as-> db db'
      ;; Execute immediate triggers first (turn-based actions)
      (reduce (fn [d t] (triggers/resolve-trigger d t)) db' (or immediate []))
      ;; Add stacked triggers to stack (card triggers)
      (reduce (fn [d t] (triggers/add-trigger-to-stack d t)) db' (or stacked [])))))
```

### 5. Write tests in src/test/fizzle/engine/trigger_registry_test.cljs

### 6. Write tests in src/test/fizzle/engine/trigger_dispatch_test.cljs

## Success Criteria
- [ ] events.cljs created with 3 event constructor functions (phase-entered, permanent-tapped, zone-change)
- [ ] trigger-registry.cljs created with register/unregister/query functions
- [ ] trigger-dispatch.cljs created with matches-filter? and dispatch-event
- [ ] 8+ registry tests pass covering:
  - test_register_trigger_adds_to_registry
  - test_register_trigger_idempotent_same_id_overwrites
  - test_unregister_trigger_removes_from_registry
  - test_unregister_nonexistent_trigger_no_op
  - test_unregister_by_source_removes_all_from_source
  - test_unregister_by_source_nonexistent_no_op
  - test_get_triggers_for_event_filters_by_type
  - test_get_triggers_for_event_empty_registry_returns_empty
- [ ] 8+ dispatch tests pass covering:
  - test_matches_filter_no_filter_matches_all
  - test_matches_filter_exact_match
  - test_matches_filter_self_matches_source
  - test_matches_filter_mismatch_returns_false
  - test_dispatch_event_immediate_executes_first
  - test_dispatch_event_stacked_goes_to_stack
  - test_dispatch_event_no_matching_triggers_returns_db_unchanged
  - test_dispatch_event_order_immediate_before_stacked
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Idempotent Registration:**
- Same trigger ID registered twice should overwrite, not duplicate
- This is intentional - allows updating trigger definition

**Graceful No-Ops:**
- Unregistering non-existent trigger: return nil, no error
- Unregistering by non-existent source: return nil, no error
- Get triggers for event with empty registry: return empty seq

**Default uses-stack? Value:**
- If trigger doesn't specify :trigger/uses-stack?, default to true (card triggers use stack)
- Turn-based actions must explicitly set :trigger/uses-stack? false

**Dispatch Order:**
- MUST execute immediate triggers before adding stacked triggers
- This matches MTG rules: turn-based actions happen, THEN triggered abilities go on stack

**Empty/Nil Handling:**
- Empty filter map should match all events of that type
- Nil filter should match all events of that type
- Empty matching list should return db unchanged

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO throwing on missing trigger (graceful no-op)
- ❌ NO mutating event maps (events are immutable)
- ❌ NO direct atom access outside registry module (use functions)
- ❌ NO assuming triggers exist (always handle empty case)
- ❌ NO changing dispatch order (immediate MUST come before stacked)
close_reason: Closed

title: Reactive bot priority: consult bot protocol before auto-passing in negotiate-priority
description:
Currently negotiate-priority unconditionally auto-passes bots when a human yields (game.cljs:896-897). This prevents bots from ever responding on the human's turn — fine for goldfish/burn, but blocks any reactive bot archetype (control with counterspells, discard with instant-speed interaction).

The fix: before auto-passing, check if the bot wants to act. If yes, transfer priority instead of auto-passing. This breaks the current isolation between negotiate-priority (which only reads :player/bot-archetype from DB) and the bot protocol (which makes decisions). Needs careful design — full brainstorm when picked up.

Adjacent changes this enables but doesn't include:
- Stack item targeting (:target/type :stack-item)
- Counter-spell effect type
- Control bot archetype
- Counterspell card definition

See conversation notes: this was identified during bot architecture review on 2026-02-19.
close_reason: Implemented: negotiate-priority now consults bot protocol before auto-passing. Commit cfa6e9c.

title: Land type change mechanic: selection + grant-based override
description: Implement Vision Charm Mode 2 engine infrastructure: land type selection, grant-based type/mana override, EOT cleanup.
design:
## Goal
Implement the land type change mechanic: player chooses a source land type and a target basic land type, all lands with the source subtype get their mana ability overridden until end of turn via grants.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (new selection type + grant logic + mana ability override + tests)

## Implementation

### Overview
1. New effect type `:change-land-types` — interactive, returns `:needs-selection`
2. Two-step chained selection: source land type → target basic land type
3. Grant applied to each matching land: overrides mana production
4. Mana activation reads grants before card abilities
5. EOT cleanup via existing expire-grants

### Canonical Land Type Data
Define ONCE in src/main/fizzle/engine/land_types.cljs (new file):

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.land-types
  "Canonical land type definitions. Single source of truth for
   basic land subtypes and their mana production.")

(def basic-land-types
  "The 5 basic land types with their subtype keyword and mana color."
  {:plains   {:subtype :plains   :mana-color :white :produces {:white 1}}
   :island   {:subtype :island   :mana-color :blue  :produces {:blue 1}}
   :swamp    {:subtype :swamp    :mana-color :black :produces {:black 1}}
   :mountain {:subtype :mountain :mana-color :red   :produces {:red 1}}
   :forest   {:subtype :forest   :mana-color :green :produces {:green 1}}})

(def basic-land-type-keys
  "Ordered vector of basic land type keywords for selection UI."
  [:plains :island :swamp :mountain :forest])
```

### 1. :change-land-types Effect Type
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/effects/zones.cljs (or new file if cleaner)

```clojure
(defmethod effects/execute-effect-impl :change-land-types
  [db _player-id effect _object-id]
  ;; Interactive: returns needs-selection for source land type choice
  {:db db :needs-selection effect})
```

File: src/main/fizzle/engine/card_spec.cljs — add :change-land-types to valid-effect-types

### 2. Selection System — Two-Step Chained

**Step 1: Source land type selection**
Selection type: `:land-type-source`

Build function (in events/selection/ — new file or extend existing):
```clojure
;; build-selection-for-effect :change-land-types
;; Returns selection state for choosing source land type
{:selection/type :land-type-source
 :selection/lifecycle :chaining
 :selection/options land-types/basic-land-type-keys  ; [:plains :island :swamp :mountain :forest]
 :selection/selected nil
 :selection/select-count 1
 :selection/exact? true
 :selection/validation :exact
 :selection/player-id player-id
 :selection/spell-id object-id
 :selection/remaining-effects remaining}
```

Executor (execute-confirmed-selection :land-type-source):
- Stores the chosen source type on the selection state
- Returns {:db db} with :chaining lifecycle to trigger build-chain-selection

**Step 2: Target land type selection (chained)**
Selection type: `:land-type-target`

build-chain-selection for :land-type-source returns:
```clojure
{:selection/type :land-type-target
 :selection/lifecycle :standard
 :selection/options (remove #{source-type} land-types/basic-land-type-keys)
 :selection/selected nil
 :selection/select-count 1
 :selection/exact? true
 :selection/validation :exact
 :selection/source-type source-type  ; from step 1
 :selection/player-id player-id
 :selection/spell-id spell-id
 :selection/remaining-effects remaining}
```

Executor (execute-confirmed-selection :land-type-target):
- Reads source-type from selection state
- Reads target-type from :selection/selected
- Finds all lands on battlefield with source subtype (:card/subtypes contains source-type)
- Applies :land-type-override grant to each
- Returns {:db db-with-grants}

### 3. Grant Structure
```clojure
{:grant/id (random-uuid)
 :grant/type :land-type-override
 :grant/source spell-object-id
 :grant/data {:original-subtype :island
              :new-subtype :mountain
              :new-produces {:red 1}}
 :grant/expires {:expires/turn current-turn :expires/phase :cleanup}}
```

### 4. Mana Ability Override in mana_activation.cljs
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/mana_activation.cljs

At line ~47-49, after getting the object and card abilities, check for land-type-override grants:

```clojure
;; Current code reads abilities from card:
card-abilities (:card/abilities card)

;; Add override logic:
;; Check if object has land-type-override grants
override-grants (filter #(= :land-type-override (:grant/type %))
                        (or (:object/grants obj) []))
;; If override exists, replace the mana ability's produces with override
effective-abilities (if (seq override-grants)
                      (let [override (first override-grants)  ; one override per land
                            new-produces (get-in override [:grant/data :new-produces])]
                        (mapv (fn [ability]
                                (if (= :mana (:ability/type ability))
                                  (assoc ability :ability/produces new-produces)
                                  ability))
                              card-abilities))
                      card-abilities)
```

Then use `effective-abilities` instead of `card-abilities` for the rest of the function.

### 5. View Support
The selection UI needs to present land type keywords as readable labels. Check how existing keyword-based selections are displayed in the views. The selection state has :selection/options which the view reads. May need a display-name map:

```clojure
{:plains "Plains" :island "Island" :swamp "Swamp" :mountain "Mountain" :forest "Forest"}
```

### Tests (12+)

**Selection flow:**
1. :change-land-types effect returns :needs-selection
2. Source type selection presents 5 options
3. Target type selection presents 4 options (source excluded)
4. Full flow: select :island as source, :mountain as target → grants applied to all Islands

**Grant application:**
5. Grant applied to each land with matching subtype
6. Non-matching lands (e.g., Swamps when source=Island) get no grants
7. No matching lands on battlefield → no grants applied (no crash)
8. Grant has correct expires structure (current turn + :cleanup phase)

**Mana ability override:**
9. Land with override grant produces new mana type when tapped (Island→Mountain produces red)
10. Land WITHOUT override still produces original mana (Swamp still produces black)
11. After EOT cleanup (expire-grants), land reverts to original production

**Edge cases:**
12. Multiple lands of same type all get overridden
13. Dual land (e.g., Tundra = Plains+Island) with source=Island gets overridden (has :island in subtypes)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] :change-land-types effect type in card_spec.cljs
- [ ] Two-step chained selection (source → target land type)
- [ ] Grants applied to all lands with matching subtype
- [ ] Mana activation reads override grants (mana_activation.cljs modified)
- [ ] After expire-grants, lands revert to original production
- [ ] Canonical land type data in engine/land_types.cljs (defined once)
- [ ] 12+ tests all passing
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO hardcoded land type lists in multiple files — use engine/land_types.cljs
- ❌ NO modifying card data (:object/card is immutable) — use grants
- ❌ NO skipping the selection chaining — two-step selection required per oracle text
- ❌ NO tautological assertions

## Key Considerations

**Mana Activation Override Location:**
src/main/fizzle/engine/mana_activation.cljs line 47-49 reads card abilities. The override must intercept HERE, before the ability matching logic at line 56-72. The object is already loaded with grants at line 39 (q/get-object pulls [* {:object/card [*]}] which includes :object/grants).

**Selection Pattern:**
Look at existing chained selections for the pattern. The key is :selection/lifecycle :chaining on the first selection, which triggers build-chain-selection to produce the second selection. Study events/selection/core.cljs for the multimethod registration pattern.

**Dual Lands:**
Pain lands (e.g., Underground River) have subtypes like #{:island :swamp}. If source type is :island, this land should be affected (it contains :island). The subtype check should be `(contains? (:card/subtypes card) source-type)`, not equality.
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: Fork/Replay Engine
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

1. Global re-frame interceptor captures a snapshot of :game/db whenever it changes, excluding history navigation events
2. Each history entry stores: immutable Datascript db reference, event type keyword, human-readable description, turn number
3. Free stepping: player can move position forward/backward through any branch without creating forks; :game/db is swapped to the snapshot at that position
4. Auto-fork on divergence: taking a game action from a non-tip position automatically creates a named fork, preserving the original timeline
5. Branch switching: player can switch between main timeline and any fork
6. Nested forks: forks from forks supported via parent chain; effective entry list computed by walking parent chain
7. All history operations (step, fork, switch-branch, effective-entries) are pure functions on app-db
8. Description generator maps event types to human-readable strings using event args and game state

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] Playing a normal game records history entries for each player action
- [ ] Stepping to any position swaps game-db to that snapshot
- [ ] Taking action from rewound position creates auto-fork preserving original timeline
- [ ] Can switch between main and fork branches
- [ ] Nested forks work (fork from a fork)
- [ ] History navigation events (step, switch) do not create new entries
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (interceptor is transparent)
- [ ] History operations have comprehensive tests
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- NO replay engine / event sourcing (we use snapshots via structural sharing, not event replay — replay adds complexity with zero benefit given immutable Datascript)
- NO modifying existing event handlers (interceptor is transparent, zero changes to game.cljs/selection.cljs/abilities.cljs)
- NO serializing game-db in history entries (store the Datascript db reference for structural sharing, not EDN strings)
- NO single-level fork limitation (parent chain supports arbitrary nesting)
- NO interceptor on history navigation events (step/switch must not record themselves as new entries)
- NO UI components in this epic (engine only, UI is separate epics)
- NO description generation in the interceptor itself (delegate to a separate description generator function for testability)

## Approach

Build a history/snapshot layer that sits alongside the existing game state in app-db. A global re-frame interceptor transparently captures immutable Datascript db snapshots whenever game state changes. History is stored as a vector of entries per branch, with forks forming a tree via parent references. All operations (stepping, forking, branch switching) are pure functions.

Structural sharing in ClojureScript persistent data structures means storing a snapshot per action is nearly free — only the changed nodes are allocated. This gives instant rewind (swap db reference) with minimal memory overhead.

## Architecture

### Data Model
```clojure
;; New keys in app-db:
{:history/main []              ;; Vector of entries on main timeline
 :history/forks {}             ;; Map of fork-id -> fork descriptor
 :history/current-branch nil   ;; nil = main, or fork-id
 :history/position -1}         ;; Index in effective entry list

;; Entry:
{:entry/snapshot <datascript-db>   ;; Immutable db value (structural sharing)
 :entry/event-type :cast-spell     ;; Re-frame event keyword
 :entry/description "Cast Dark Ritual"
 :entry/turn 1}

;; Fork:
{:fork/id <uuid>
 :fork/name "Auto-fork at step 5"
 :fork/branch-point 5          ;; Index in parent where we diverged
 :fork/parent nil              ;; nil = from main, or another fork-id
 :fork/entries []}             ;; Entries specific to this fork
```

### Components
- history/core.cljs — Data model, pure operations (init, append, step, fork, switch, effective-entries)
- history/interceptor.cljs — Global re-frame interceptor for snapshot capture
- history/descriptions.cljs — Event type -> human-readable description mapping

### Integration Points
- core.cljs: Register global interceptor during init, add history keys to initial app-db
- No changes to events/game.cljs, events/selection.cljs, or events/abilities.cljs

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Players need to rewind to earlier game states and try different lines. The current architecture has no history tracking — each re-frame event overwrites the previous game state.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- All events use rf/reg-event-db (pure functions, no interceptors)
- App-db has :game/db (Datascript immutable value), :game/selected-card, :game/pending-selection, :game/pending-mode-selection
- No existing interceptors — clean slate for global interceptor
- Events dispatched from core.cljs: cast-spell, play-land, resolve-top, advance-phase, start-turn, activate-mana-ability, activate-ability
- Subscriptions use 3-layer pattern with ::game-db as source

**External:**
- ClojureScript persistent data structures use structural sharing (HAMTs) — storing old references is nearly free
- Datascript db values are immutable — d/db-with returns new value, old unchanged
- re-frame supports rf/reg-global-interceptor for transparent event interception

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Hybrid snapshots + event metadata (CHOSEN)
Store immutable Datascript db references at each action. Event metadata (type, description) alongside for display. No replay engine.

Chosen because: Instant rewind (swap reference), near-zero memory overhead (structural sharing), simplest implementation, no replay logic needed.

#### 2. Full event sourcing with replay engine (REJECTED)
Store event log as source of truth, replay events to reconstruct state.

REJECTED BECAUSE: Adds replay engine complexity with no benefit — Datascript immutability already gives us free snapshots. Replay would be slow for rewinding to early states. Event format would need to be stable across code changes.

DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS: We need cross-session persistence where snapshots cannot be serialized (but they can be — Datascript db values are serializable as EDN).

#### 3. Snapshot only at key points (periodic checkpoints) (REJECTED)
Store snapshots every N events, replay between checkpoints.

REJECTED BECAUSE: Still requires a replay engine for the gaps. More complex than snapshotting everything, without saving meaningful memory (structural sharing already handles this).

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** History data model, snapshot capture, stepping, forking, branch switching, description generation
**Out of scope:** UI components (separate epics), puzzle export (Phase 6), action log display (UI epic)

### Open Questions
- Exact description format for selection events (e.g., Intuition pile choices) — resolve during implementation
- Whether to auto-name forks with timestamp or sequential number — resolve during implementation

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Snapshot vs replay vs hybrid? | Hybrid (snapshots + metadata) | No replay engine; store db references |
| Puzzle dependencies? | Depends on card registry | Puzzles don't embed card definitions |
| Puzzle history? | Board state only | Puzzles are snapshot exports, no event log |
| Log granularity? | Player actions only | Snapshot on every game-db change (= player decisions) |
| Rewind behavior? | Auto-fork on divergence | Original timeline always preserved |
| Stepping within branch? | Free stepping | Position cursor moves without forking |
| Scope? | 3 epics: engine, minimal UI, complete UI | Engine epic first |
| Capture mechanism? | Global interceptor | Zero changes to existing handlers |

### Dead-End Paths

#### Full Event Sourcing
Why explored: Design doc originally described event replay approach
Investigation: Reviewed codebase — Datascript db values are already immutable with structural sharing
Why abandoned: Replay engine adds complexity with no benefit; snapshots are free
close_reason: All subtasks complete. 918 tests pass, 0 failures. History data model (core.cljs), description generator (descriptions.cljs), global interceptor (interceptor.cljs) all implemented. core.cljs and game.cljs integrated. make validate clean.

title: Implement history description generator and global interceptor
design:
## Goal

Build two modules that complete the Fork/Replay Engine epic:
1. **history/descriptions.cljs** — Maps re-frame event types to human-readable description strings
2. **history/interceptor.cljs** — Global re-frame interceptor that captures Datascript snapshots whenever :game/db changes

Together these enable transparent history recording during normal gameplay with zero changes to existing event handlers.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Create src/main/fizzle/history/descriptions.cljs

A single pure function that takes a re-frame event vector and returns a human-readable string.

```clojure
(ns fizzle.history.descriptions)

(defn describe-event
  "Generate a human-readable description for a re-frame event.
   Takes event vector [event-id & args]. Returns string or nil.
   Returns nil for events that should not generate descriptions (UI-only events)."
  [[event-id & args]]
  (case event-id
    ;; Game events
    :fizzle.events.game/cast-spell        "Cast spell"
    :fizzle.events.game/resolve-top       "Resolve top of stack"
    :fizzle.events.game/advance-phase     "Advance phase"
    :fizzle.events.game/start-turn        "Start new turn"
    :fizzle.events.game/play-land         (let [object-id (first args)]
                                            (if object-id
                                              "Play land"
                                              "Play land"))
    :fizzle.events.game/select-casting-mode "Select casting mode"

    ;; Ability events
    :fizzle.events.abilities/activate-mana-ability "Activate mana ability"
    :fizzle.events.abilities/activate-ability       "Activate ability"
    :fizzle.events.abilities/confirm-ability-target "Confirm ability target"

    ;; Selection confirmations (these change game state)
    :fizzle.events.selection/confirm-selection        "Confirm selection"
    :fizzle.events.selection/confirm-peek-selection   "Confirm peek selection"
    :fizzle.events.selection/confirm-graveyard-selection "Confirm graveyard selection"
    :fizzle.events.selection/confirm-tutor-selection  "Confirm tutor selection"
    :fizzle.events.selection/confirm-pile-choice-selection "Confirm pile choice"
    :fizzle.events.selection/confirm-scry-selection   "Confirm scry"
    :fizzle.events.selection/confirm-player-target-selection "Confirm player target"
    :fizzle.events.selection/confirm-exile-cards-selection "Confirm exile cards"
    :fizzle.events.selection/confirm-x-mana-selection "Confirm X mana"
    :fizzle.events.selection/confirm-cast-time-target "Confirm cast-time target"

    ;; Default: return nil for unknown/UI-only events
    nil))
```

Key decisions:
- Returns nil for unknown events — interceptor uses this to decide whether to record
- Simple static descriptions initially (not card-name-aware) — card names can be added later by reading game-db, but the epic design says "resolve during implementation" for selection event descriptions
- NO dependency on game state — descriptions are derived purely from event type and args
- This function is intentionally simple; richer descriptions (including card names) can be added incrementally without changing the interceptor

### 2. Create src/main/fizzle/history/interceptor.cljs

A global re-frame interceptor that:
1. Runs as an :after interceptor (sees db after event handler ran)
2. Compares :game/db before and after — if changed, appends a history entry
3. Skips history navigation events (identified by event-id namespace)
4. Uses history/core functions to append or auto-fork

```clojure
(ns fizzle.history.interceptor
  (:require
    [fizzle.history.core :as history]
    [fizzle.history.descriptions :as descriptions]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as queries]
    [re-frame.core :as rf]))

(def ^:private history-event-ns
  "Namespace prefix for history navigation events that should NOT be recorded."
  "fizzle.history")

(defn- history-event?
  "Returns true if this event is a history navigation event (step, switch, etc.)
   that should not create new history entries."
  [event-id]
  (and (keyword? event-id)
       (let [ns (namespace event-id)]
         (and ns (clojure.string/starts-with? ns history-event-ns)))))

(defn- get-turn
  "Get current turn number from game-db. Returns 0 if not available."
  [game-db]
  (if game-db
    (let [game-state (queries/get-game-state game-db)]
      (or (:game/turn game-state) 0))
    0))

(def history-interceptor
  "Global re-frame interceptor that captures Datascript db snapshots.
   Appends history entry whenever :game/db changes (excluding history events).
   Auto-forks when taking action from a rewound position."
  (rf/->interceptor
    :id :history/snapshot
    :before (fn [context]
              ;; Store :game/db before event runs (for comparison in :after)
              (let [db (get-in context [:coeffects :db])
                    game-db (:game/db db)]
                (assoc-in context [:coeffects :history/pre-game-db] game-db)))
    :after (fn [context]
             (let [event (get-in context [:coeffects :event])
                   event-id (first event)]
               ;; Skip history navigation events
               (if (history-event? event-id)
                 context
                 (let [pre-game-db (get-in context [:coeffects :history/pre-game-db])
                       db-after (get-in context [:effects :db])
                       game-db-after (:game/db db-after)]
                   ;; Only record if :game/db actually changed
                   (if (and db-after
                            game-db-after
                            (not (identical? pre-game-db game-db-after)))
                     (let [description (or (descriptions/describe-event event)
                                           (str (name event-id)))
                           turn (get-turn game-db-after)
                           entry (history/make-entry game-db-after event-id description turn)]
                       ;; Check if at tip — if so, append; if not, auto-fork
                       (if (or (= -1 (:history/position db-after))
                               (history/at-tip? db-after))
                         (assoc-in context [:effects :db] (history/append-entry db-after entry))
                         (assoc-in context [:effects :db] (history/auto-fork db-after entry))))
                     context)))))))

(defn register!
  "Register the history interceptor globally. Call once during app initialization."
  []
  (rf/reg-global-interceptor history-interceptor))
```

Key decisions:
- Uses `identical?` for game-db comparison (reference equality, O(1)) — this is correct because Datascript returns new db values only when data changes
- Stores pre-game-db in coeffects (not a separate atom) for purity
- history-event? checks namespace prefix — all future history events will be in `fizzle.history.*` namespace
- Falls back to `(name event-id)` for description if describe-event returns nil
- Auto-fork logic: checks at-tip? and uses auto-fork when not at tip, append-entry when at tip
- init-game is NOT excluded — the initial game state IS the first history entry (position goes from -1 to 0)

### 3. Modify src/main/fizzle/core.cljs

Add two changes:
1. Require the interceptor namespace
2. Register the interceptor before dispatching init-game
3. Merge init-history into the init-game result

#### core.cljs changes:
Add to requires:
```clojure
[fizzle.history.interceptor :as history-interceptor]
[fizzle.history.core :as history]
```

In `init` function, add before dispatch:
```clojure
(history-interceptor/register!)
```

#### events/game.cljs changes (init-game ONLY):
Merge history initial state into init-game result:
```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::init-game
  (fn [_ _]
    (merge (init-game-state) (history/init-history))))
```

This is the ONLY change to existing event handlers — adding history keys to initial app-db. The interceptor is transparent to all other events.

### 4. Create src/test/fizzle/history/descriptions_test.cljs

Test the description generator.

#### Test cases (8 tests):

1. **test-game-events-have-descriptions** — cast-spell, resolve-top, advance-phase, start-turn, play-land all return non-nil strings
2. **test-ability-events-have-descriptions** — activate-mana-ability, activate-ability, confirm-ability-target return non-nil strings
3. **test-selection-confirm-events-have-descriptions** — all confirm-* selection events return non-nil strings
4. **test-unknown-event-returns-nil** — :some.unknown/event returns nil
5. **test-ui-only-events-return-nil** — select-card, toggle-selection, cancel-selection return nil (these don't change game state or are handled by interceptor's identical? check anyway)
6. **test-descriptions-are-strings** — all non-nil results are strings
7. **test-init-game-returns-nil** — init-game returns nil (interceptor records it anyway based on game-db change)
8. **test-cancel-mode-selection-returns-nil** — cancel-mode-selection is UI-only, returns nil

### 5. Create src/test/fizzle/history/interceptor_test.cljs

Test the interceptor integration with simulated re-frame contexts. These tests use the interceptor's :before and :after functions directly with mock contexts (no actual re-frame dispatch).

#### Test cases (10 tests):

1. **test-before-stores-pre-game-db** — :before handler stores :game/db in coeffects under :history/pre-game-db
2. **test-after-appends-entry-on-game-db-change** — When :game/db differs (not identical?), history entry is appended
3. **test-after-skips-when-game-db-unchanged** — When :game/db is identical? (same reference), no entry appended
4. **test-after-skips-history-events** — Events with namespace starting with "fizzle.history" are not recorded
5. **test-after-auto-forks-when-not-at-tip** — When position < tip and game-db changes, auto-fork is called instead of append-entry
6. **test-entry-has-correct-fields** — Appended entry has :entry/snapshot, :entry/event-type, :entry/description, :entry/turn
7. **test-description-from-describe-event** — Entry uses description from descriptions/describe-event when available
8. **test-fallback-description-for-unknown-event** — Entry uses (name event-id) as fallback when describe-event returns nil
9. **test-init-game-creates-first-entry** — init-game event creates entry (position goes from -1 to 0)
10. **test-after-skips-when-no-db-in-effects** — If event handler returns no :db in effects (fx handler), interceptor is a no-op

#### How to test interceptor without re-frame:

```clojure
;; Build mock context
(def mock-context
  {:coeffects {:db (merge {:game/db :old-db} (history/init-history))
               :event [:fizzle.events.game/cast-spell]}
   :effects {:db (merge {:game/db :new-db} (history/init-history))}})

;; Run :before then :after
(let [ctx-after-before ((:before interceptor/history-interceptor) mock-context)
      ctx-after-after ((:after interceptor/history-interceptor) ctx-after-before)]
  ;; Assert on ctx-after-after
  ...)
```

But note: the interceptor accesses `:before` and `:after` keys from the interceptor map. re-frame interceptors created via `rf/->interceptor` store these as functions in the map. We test by calling these functions directly with constructed contexts.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] descriptions/describe-event returns non-nil strings for all game-state-changing events (cast-spell, resolve-top, advance-phase, start-turn, play-land, activate-mana-ability, activate-ability, confirm-ability-target, all confirm-* selection events)
- [ ] descriptions/describe-event returns nil for UI-only events (select-card, toggle-selection, etc.)
- [ ] Interceptor records history entry when :game/db changes (verified via mock context test)
- [ ] Interceptor does NOT record when :game/db is unchanged (identical? check)
- [ ] Interceptor does NOT record history navigation events (namespace check)
- [ ] Interceptor auto-forks when not at tip (verified via mock context test)
- [ ] Interceptor falls back to (name event-id) when describe-event returns nil
- [ ] init-game merges history/init-history into app-db
- [ ] history-interceptor/register! is called in core.cljs init before dispatch
- [ ] 18+ tests pass (8 description + 10 interceptor)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (interceptor is transparent)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Event handler returns nil effects**
- Some event handlers might not modify db (guard conditions return db unchanged)
- Interceptor checks `(not (identical? pre-game-db game-db-after))` — handles this correctly
- Also checks `db-after` and `game-db-after` are non-nil

**Edge Case: init-game (first event)**
- Position starts at -1, init-history has empty main vector
- append-entry handles position -1 correctly (increments to 0)
- This creates the first history entry with the initial game state

**Edge Case: Multiple game-db changes in one event**
- re-frame events are synchronous — each event produces one final db
- Interceptor only sees the final db, which is correct (we snapshot the result)

**Edge Case: Selection events that DON'T change game-db**
- toggle-selection, set-pending-selection only modify :game/pending-selection (app-db level)
- :game/db is unchanged → identical? is true → no recording
- This is correct behavior (UI-intermediate steps)

**Edge Case: confirm-selection changes game-db**
- confirm-selection dispatches back to game events which change game-db
- But if confirm-selection itself modifies game-db, the interceptor captures it
- Dual-dispatch scenario: both events would be captured separately (each changes game-db)

**Performance: identical? check**
- O(1) reference comparison, not structural comparison
- Datascript db values are immutable — same reference means same data
- No performance concern even at high event rates

**Integration: re-frame interceptor ordering**
- Global interceptors run in registration order
- Our interceptor should be registered first (before init-game dispatch)
- :before runs before event handler, :after runs after
- coeffects and effects are standard re-frame context keys

**Testing: Accessing interceptor functions**
- rf/->interceptor returns a map with :before and :after keys (functions)
- Can call these directly with constructed context maps
- No need to run full re-frame dispatch in tests

**clojure.string requirement**
- history-event? uses clojure.string/starts-with?
- Must require [clojure.string] in interceptor namespace

## Anti-Patterns (Task-Level)

- NO changes to existing event handlers (except init-game adding history keys)
- NO explicit event allowlist/denylist in interceptor (use :game/db change detection)
- NO structural comparison of game-db (use identical? reference check)
- NO atoms or mutable state in interceptor (use coeffects for pre-event state)
- NO throwing exceptions for unexpected events (return context unchanged)
- NO description generation that requires game-db queries (keep descriptions pure and fast — card-name enrichment is a future enhancement)
close_reason: All 19 tests pass (918 total, 2103 assertions). descriptions.cljs + interceptor.cljs + core.cljs integration. make validate clean. Zero changes to existing event handlers except init-game adding history keys.
