title: Task 4: Migrate scry-modal, x-mana-selection-modal, mode-selector-modal, and selection-modal router to modals.cljs
description:
## Goal
Migrate the remaining 4 modal functions and their helpers from core.cljs to modals.cljs:

### Move to modals.cljs:
1. scry-card-view (core.cljs:422-439) - helper used only by scry-modal
2. scry-modal (core.cljs:442-508) - scry selection with 3 piles
3. x-mana-selection-modal (core.cljs:511-604) - X value selector
4. format-mana-cost (core.cljs:683-700) - helper for mode-selector
5. format-additional-costs (core.cljs:703-714) - helper for mode-selector
6. mode-button (core.cljs:717-751) - helper for mode-selector
7. mode-selector-modal (core.cljs:754-801) - casting mode selection
8. selection-modal (core.cljs:607-677) - router that dispatches to all modals

### Key details:
- scry-modal uses overlay-style and container-style (blue border, 600px)
- scry-modal needs ::selection-events/scry-unassign, scry-assign-top, scry-assign-bottom, confirm-scry-selection
- scry-modal uses ::subs/scry-cards subscription
- x-mana-selection-modal uses purple border (#6a4a8a), 400px max-width
- x-mana-selection-modal needs ::selection-events/decrement-x-value, increment-x-value, cancel-x-mana-selection, confirm-x-mana-selection
- mode-selector-modal uses blue border, 400px, dispatches ::events/select-casting-mode and ::events/cancel-mode-selection
- mode-selector-modal uses ::subs/pending-mode-selection subscription
- selection-modal router needs ::subs/pending-selection, ::subs/selection-cards, and references all other modals

### Changes to core.cljs:
- Delete scry-card-view, scry-modal, x-mana-selection-modal, format-mana-cost, format-additional-costs, mode-button, mode-selector-modal, selection-modal (lines 422-801)
- Update app component to use modals/selection-modal and modals/mode-selector-modal
- core.cljs should drop from 836 to ~440 lines

### New requires in modals.cljs:
- [fizzle.subs.game :as subs] may need ::subs/scry-cards, ::subs/pending-mode-selection, ::subs/pending-selection, ::subs/selection-cards
- [fizzle.events.game :as events] for ::events/select-casting-mode, ::events/cancel-mode-selection
- clojure.string (for str/join in format-additional-costs)

### Verification:
- make fmt then make validate
- core.cljs should be ~440 lines
- All 741 tests pass
close_reason: Migrated scry-modal, x-mana-selection-modal, mode-selector-modal (+ helpers format-mana-cost, format-additional-costs, mode-button, scry-card-view), and selection-modal router to modals.cljs. core.cljs reduced from 836 to 449 lines. All 741 tests pass, lint + format clean.

title: T2: Trigger expansion — ETB, sacrifice, mana-ability + weak assertion cleanup
description:
## Goal
Expand trigger test coverage beyond draw-step/untap-step to ETB, sacrifice, mana-ability triggers. Also fix weak assertions in trigger_db_test.cljs and priority_test.cljs (merged from T4 since same files, small scope).

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours. ~15-20 new trigger tests + ~5 assertion fixes.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- src/test/fizzle/engine/triggers_test.cljs (207 lines) — 8 tests: draw-step (2), untap-step (2), create-spell-copy (4). Only tests resolve-trigger multimethod.
- src/test/fizzle/engine/trigger_db_test.cljs (471 lines) — tests trigger entity creation/querying. Has 2 contains? assertions at lines 92-93 to fix.
- src/test/fizzle/engine/trigger_db_integration_test.cljs (98 lines) — tests trigger creation for cards with triggers.
- src/test/fizzle/engine/priority_test.cljs (100 lines) — 3 contains? assertions at lines 26, 35-36 to fix.
- src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs — resolve-trigger multimethod (3 defmethods: default, draw-step, untap-step)
- src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger_db.cljs — trigger entity CRUD: create-trigger-tx, create-triggers-for-card-tx, get-triggers-for-event, matches-filter?
- Trigger types mapped: becomes-tapped→permanent-tapped, land-entered→land-entered, creature-attacks→creature-attacked, enters-battlefield→permanent-entered

### 2. New trigger tests to add in triggers_test.cljs

**A. ETB triggers (enters-battlefield / permanent-entered)**
- Card with :card/triggers [{:trigger/type :enters-battlefield :trigger/effects [...]}] enters battlefield → trigger entity created → get-triggers-for-event finds it with {:event/type :permanent-entered}
- Filter: :event/object-id :self → only fires for source permanent
- Trigger with effects: verify effects data stored on trigger entity

**B. Sacrifice triggers**
- Card with :card/triggers [{:trigger/type :sacrifice ...}] → trigger registered
- get-triggers-for-event with {:event/type :sacrifice} matches trigger

**C. Mana-ability triggers (becomes-tapped / permanent-tapped)**
- Card with :card/triggers [{:trigger/type :becomes-tapped ...}] → maps to :permanent-tapped event
- Filter matching: :self filter means only own tap triggers

**D. trigger-type->event-type mapping tests**
- :becomes-tapped → :permanent-tapped
- :land-entered → :land-entered
- :creature-attacks → :creature-attacked
- :enters-battlefield → :permanent-entered
- :unknown-type → :unknown-type (default identity)

**E. matches-filter? tests**
- No filter → matches everything
- :self filter → matches only source entity
- :exclude-self → matches everything except source
- Multiple conditions → all must match

**F. create-triggers-for-card-tx tests**
- Creates trigger entity linked to source object
- Default filter applied when none specified
- :self-controller resolved to player-id

### 3. Weak assertion fixes

**trigger_db_test.cljs (2 assertions):**
Line 92: (is (not (contains? m :trigger/description))) → verify m has no :trigger/description key by checking = nil or checking exact keys
Line 93: (is (not (contains? m :trigger/filter))) → same pattern

**priority_test.cljs (3 assertions):**
Line 26: (is (contains? (priority/get-passed-eids db') p1-eid)) → (is (= #{p1-eid} (priority/get-passed-eids db')))
Lines 35-36: same pattern for both-players-passed → (is (= #{p1-eid p2-eid} (priority/get-passed-eids db')))

### 4. Anti-patterns
- NO d/conn-from-db in new tests
- NO some?/contains? assertions
- NO production code changes
- NO hand-built trigger entities — use create-trigger-tx or create-triggers-for-card-tx

## Success Criteria
- [ ] ETB trigger tests: card with enters-battlefield trigger → trigger entity created → event matching works
- [ ] Sacrifice trigger tests: trigger registered and event-matched
- [ ] Mana-ability trigger tests: becomes-tapped→permanent-tapped mapping + filter matching
- [ ] trigger-type->event-type mapping tested for all 4 registered types + default
- [ ] matches-filter? tested for no-filter, self, exclude-self, multi-condition
- [ ] trigger_db_test.cljs: zero contains? assertions (currently 2)
- [ ] priority_test.cljs: zero contains? assertions (currently 3)
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] No production code changes
close_reason: 15 trigger tests added, 5 weak assertions fixed, make validate green, commit 096269a

title: Migrate deferred-entry event handlers
description: Move cast-spell, cast-and-yield, activate-ability to use :history/deferred-entry. Add deferred-entry processing to confirm-selection-impl and resolve-one-and-stop continuation. Remove priority-selection-types. Fix the original cast-and-yield bug.
design:
## Migrate deferred-entry handlers

### New mechanism
- cast-spell/cast-and-yield/activate-ability set :history/deferred-entry when selection needed
- deferred-entry holds: type, object-id, pre-game-db, principal, event-type
- confirm-selection-impl processes deferred when chain completes (no pending-selection, no continuation)
- resolve-one-and-stop continuation processes deferred for cast-and-yield

### descriptions.cljs changes  
- Make describe-cast-spell, describe-cast-and-yield, describe-activate-ability public

### Handler changes
- cast-spell: immediate entry when no selection, deferred when selection needed
- cast-and-yield: immediate entry when no selection (after resolve), deferred when selection needed
- activate-ability: immediate entry when no selection, deferred when targeting needed

### Selection system changes
- Add deferred-entry processing to confirm-selection-handler (when no pending-selection after impl)
- Add deferred-entry processing to apply-continuation :resolve-one-and-stop

### Result: priority-events empty, priority-selection-types unused
close_reason: Closed

title: Unify modal spell selection under selection system
description: Replace :game/pending-spell-mode-selection with :selection/type :spell-mode. Modal becomes a selection type with mode objects as candidates, :selection/select-count 1, :selection/auto-confirm? true. Eliminate ::select-spell-mode event handler. Move per-mode targeting evaluation from game.cljs to spell-mode builder. Update views to render mode-type candidates. ADR-014 reference. Touches events/game.cljs, events/selection/ (new builder+executor), views/modals.cljs.
design:
## Goal
Eliminate :game/pending-spell-mode-selection parallel mechanism. Modal spell selection becomes :selection/type :spell-mode under :game/pending-selection, using standard selection system (builder + executor + lifecycle).

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### Step 1: Add spell-mode selection type (builder + executor)

Create builder and executor in a new module or in game.cljs:

**Builder** (called from cast-spell-handler when modal card detected):
- Receives game-db, player-id, object-id, valid-spell-modes
- Returns selection map:
  - :selection/type :spell-mode
  - :selection/lifecycle :finalized
  - :selection/player-id player-id
  - :selection/object-id object-id
  - :selection/candidates valid-spell-modes (vector of mode maps)
  - :selection/selected #{}
  - :selection/select-count 1
  - :selection/auto-confirm? true
  - :selection/validation :exact
  - :selection/on-complete continuation to trigger initiate-cast-with-mode

**Executor** (execute-confirmed-selection :spell-mode):
- Reads selected mode from :selection/selected (first element is index or mode)
- Stores :object/chosen-mode on game object via d/db-with
- Gets casting mode (primary) for the card
- Returns {:db db-with-mode}

**Continuation** (apply-continuation :cast-after-spell-mode):
- Reads object-id, casting-mode from continuation data
- Calls initiate-cast-with-mode on app-db
- Returns updated app-db

### Step 2: Update cast-spell-handler

Replace the modal branch:
```clojure
;; OLD: (assoc app-db :game/pending-spell-mode-selection {:object-id :modes})
;; NEW: (assoc app-db :game/pending-selection (build-spell-mode-selection ...))
```

### Step 3: Update view rendering

The standard selection-modal needs to handle spell-mode candidates. Options:
a) Add a spell-mode branch to selection-modal that renders mode labels
b) Or render spell-mode selection through existing candidate rendering

Mode candidates have :mode/label — the selection modal needs to display this.

### Step 4: Remove old mechanism

- Remove select-spell-mode-handler function
- Remove ::select-spell-mode event registration
- Remove cancel-spell-mode-selection-handler function
- Remove ::cancel-spell-mode-selection event registration
- Remove ::pending-spell-mode-selection subscription
- Remove spell-mode-selector-modal view
- Remove spell-mode-button view helper
- Remove :game/pending-spell-mode-selection from core.cljs
- Remove :game/pending-spell-mode-selection from history/core.cljs clear-stale-ui-state
- Update resolve-all check at game.cljs:1091-1092 (remove spell-mode check)

### Step 5: Update tests

- Rewrite spell_mode_selection_test.cljs to use :game/pending-selection instead of :game/pending-spell-mode-selection
- Update test-helpers cast-mode-with-target if needed
- Verify all modal card tests still pass (BEB, REB, Hydroblast, Pyroblast)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Modal spells flow through :game/pending-selection as :selection/type :spell-mode
- [ ] Zero references to :game/pending-spell-mode-selection in codebase
- [ ] Zero references to ::select-spell-mode event handler in codebase
- [ ] spell-mode-selector-modal view removed
- [ ] Mode objects are candidates with select-count 1 and auto-confirm true
- [ ] Per-mode targeting evaluation (get-valid-spell-modes) runs in builder, not in event handler
- [ ] All existing tests pass (no regressions)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations

**Candidate identity**: Standard selection uses entity IDs in :selection/selected. Spell modes are maps, not entities. Need to use mode index or mode map identity. Since modes have unique :mode/label, can use index into the candidates vector.

**Selection rendering**: The selection-modal view currently renders cards (with names, zones). Spell modes need different rendering (just the label text). The view needs a branch for :spell-mode selection type.

**Continuation data**: The continuation must carry enough data to call initiate-cast-with-mode: object-id plus the casting mode. The casting mode is derived from the card after chosen-mode is stored, so continuation only needs object-id.

**resolve-all interaction**: game.cljs:1091-1092 checks for pending-spell-mode-selection to stop auto-resolve. With the new approach, this check is already covered by :game/pending-selection being set.

## Anti-Patterns
- NO functions stored in selection maps
- NO new app-db keys
- NO card definition changes
- NO modifying confirm-selection-impl
close_reason: Phase 2 complete: modal spell selection unified under standard selection system. Zero references to :game/pending-spell-mode-selection, ::select-spell-mode, spell-mode-selector-modal. 2085 tests pass.

title: Task 2: Implement :peek-and-select effect
design:
## Goal
Implement the :peek-and-select effect type that looks at top N cards of library, lets player select some for hand, and puts remainder to a destination zone.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Context
Completed Task 1: X cost system working. Now need the core effect for Flash of Insight.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:58-103 - execute-effect-impl multimethod dispatch
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs:947-977 - build-tutor-selection pattern
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs:1597-1647 - execute-tutor-selection pattern
- src/test/fizzle/cards/merchant_scroll_test.cljs - tutor test patterns

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

**Test file:** src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs

Add these specific test functions:

```clojure
;; === peek-and-select effect tests ===

(deftest peek-and-select-basic-test
  ;; Bug it catches: effect doesn't peek correct number of cards
  (testing "peek-and-select with X=3 shows top 3 cards"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (add-cards-to-library :player-1 [:card-a :card-b :card-c :card-d]))
          effect {:effect/type :peek-and-select
                  :effect/count 3
                  :effect/select-count 1
                  :effect/selected-zone :hand
                  :effect/remainder-zone :bottom-of-library
                  :effect/shuffle-remainder? true}
          selection (build-peek-selection db :player-1 :spell-1 effect [])]
      (is (= 3 (count (:selection/candidates selection)))
          "Should have 3 candidates from top of library")
      (is (= 1 (:selection/select-count selection))
          "Should select exactly 1 card"))))

(deftest peek-and-select-x-zero-test
  ;; Bug it catches: X=0 creates empty selection causing errors
  (testing "peek-and-select with X=0 is a no-op"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (add-cards-to-library :player-1 [:card-a :card-b]))
          effect {:effect/type :peek-and-select
                  :effect/count 0
                  :effect/select-count 1}
          selection (build-peek-selection db :player-1 :spell-1 effect [])]
      (is (nil? selection)
          "Should return nil for X=0 (no selection needed)"))))

(deftest peek-and-select-library-smaller-than-x-test
  ;; Bug it catches: crashes when library has fewer cards than X
  (testing "peek-and-select peeks available cards when library < X"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (add-cards-to-library :player-1 [:card-a :card-b]))  ; only 2 cards
          effect {:effect/type :peek-and-select
                  :effect/count 5  ; asking for 5
                  :effect/select-count 1}
          selection (build-peek-selection db :player-1 :spell-1 effect [])]
      (is (= 2 (count (:selection/candidates selection)))
          "Should peek all 2 available cards"))))

(deftest execute-peek-selection-moves-cards-test
  ;; Bug it catches: cards not moved to correct zones
  (testing "execute-peek-selection moves selected to hand, rest to bottom"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (add-cards-to-library :player-1 [:card-a :card-b :card-c]))
          selection {:selection/effect-type :peek-and-select
                     :selection/selected #{:obj-a}
                     :selection/candidates #{:obj-a :obj-b :obj-c}
                     :selection/selected-zone :hand
                     :selection/remainder-zone :bottom-of-library
                     :selection/shuffle-remainder? true
                     :selection/player-id :player-1}
          db' (execute-peek-selection db selection)]
      (is (= :hand (get-zone db' :obj-a))
          "Selected card should be in hand")
      (is (= :library (get-zone db' :obj-b))
          "Remainder should stay in library")
      (is (> (get-position db' :obj-b) 0)
          "Remainder should be at bottom (position > 0)"))))

(deftest peek-and-select-fail-to-find-test
  ;; Bug it catches: player forced to select when they want to fail-to-find
  (testing "peek-and-select allows fail-to-find (select 0)"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          effect {:effect/type :peek-and-select
                  :effect/count 3
                  :effect/select-count 1}
          selection (build-peek-selection db :player-1 :spell-1 effect [])]
      (is (true? (:selection/allow-fail-to-find selection))
          "Must allow player to select 0 cards"))))

(deftest peek-and-select-single-card-shows-ui-test
  ;; Bug it catches: auto-selecting when only 1 card (violates anti-pattern)
  (testing "peek-and-select with 1 card still shows selection UI"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (add-cards-to-library :player-1 [:card-a]))
          effect {:effect/type :peek-and-select
                  :effect/count 1
                  :effect/select-count 1}
          selection (build-peek-selection db :player-1 :spell-1 effect [])]
      (is (= 1 (count (:selection/candidates selection)))
          "Should have 1 candidate")
      (is (empty? (:selection/selected selection))
          "Should NOT auto-select - player must choose"))))
```

### 3. Implementation checklist

- [ ] effects.cljs - Add :peek-and-select multimethod:
  ```clojure
  (defmethod execute-effect-impl :peek-and-select
    [db _player-id _effect _object-id]
    ;; Returns db unchanged - selection handled at app-db level
    db)
  ```

- [ ] game.cljs - Add build-peek-selection function (follow build-tutor-selection pattern):
  ```clojure
  (defn build-peek-selection
    "Build pending selection state for peek-and-select effect.
     Returns nil if peek-count is 0 (no selection needed)."
    [db player-id object-id effect effects-after]
    (let [peek-count (or (:effect/count effect) 0)]
      (when (pos? peek-count)
        (let [library-cards (q/get-library-cards db player-id)
              ;; Sort by position (0 = top), take top N
              top-cards (->> library-cards
                             (sort-by :object/position)
                             (take peek-count))
              candidate-ids (set (map :object/id top-cards))
              select-count (or (:effect/select-count effect) 1)]
          {:selection/zone :peek  ; Distinct from :library
           :selection/effect-type :peek-and-select
           :selection/candidates candidate-ids
           :selection/select-count (min select-count (count candidate-ids))
           :selection/exact? false  ; Can select fewer (fail-to-find)
           :selection/allow-fail-to-find true
           :selection/selected #{}
           :selection/player-id player-id
           :selection/spell-id object-id
           :selection/remaining-effects effects-after
           :selection/selected-zone (or (:effect/selected-zone effect) :hand)
           :selection/remainder-zone (or (:effect/remainder-zone effect) :bottom-of-library)
           :selection/shuffle-remainder? (:effect/shuffle-remainder? effect)}))))
  ```

- [ ] game.cljs - Add execute-peek-selection function:
  ```clojure
  (defn execute-peek-selection
    "Execute peek selection: move selected to selected-zone, rest to remainder-zone."
    [db selection]
    (let [selected (:selection/selected selection)
          candidates (:selection/candidates selection)
          remainder (clojure.set/difference candidates selected)
          selected-zone (:selection/selected-zone selection)
          remainder-zone (:selection/remainder-zone selection)
          shuffle? (:selection/shuffle-remainder? selection)
          player-id (:selection/player-id selection)]
      (-> db
          ;; Move selected cards to selected-zone (hand)
          (move-cards-to-zone selected selected-zone)
          ;; Move remainder to bottom of library
          (move-cards-to-bottom remainder player-id shuffle?))))
  ```

- [ ] game.cljs - Add helper move-cards-to-bottom:
  ```clojure
  (defn move-cards-to-bottom
    "Move cards to bottom of library. If shuffle? true, randomize their order."
    [db card-ids player-id shuffle?]
    (let [library-max-pos (q/get-max-library-position db player-id)
          cards-to-move (if shuffle?
                          (shuffle (seq card-ids))
                          (seq card-ids))]
      (reduce-kv
        (fn [db' idx card-id]
          (zones/set-position db' card-id (+ library-max-pos 1 idx)))
        db
        (vec cards-to-move))))
  ```

- [ ] Verify all 6 new peek-and-select tests pass
- [ ] Verify all existing tests still pass (688 tests)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] :peek-and-select multimethod registered in effects.cljs
- [ ] build-peek-selection returns correct selection state
- [ ] build-peek-selection returns nil for count=0
- [ ] build-peek-selection handles library < count gracefully
- [ ] execute-peek-selection moves cards to correct zones
- [ ] Remainder shuffled when :shuffle-remainder? true
- [ ] Selection allows fail-to-find (select 0)
- [ ] Single card does NOT auto-select (shows UI)
- [ ] 6 new tests pass
- [ ] All 688+ existing tests pass
- [ ] make validate passing

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: X=0**
- build-peek-selection returns nil (no selection state)
- Calling code must handle nil return
- Don't create empty selection which could cause UI errors

**Edge Case: Library has fewer cards than X**
- Peek min(X, library-size) cards
- `(take peek-count library-cards)` naturally handles this
- Test: peek-and-select-library-smaller-than-x-test

**Edge Case: Fail-to-find**
- Player can always choose 0 cards (select nothing)
- :selection/allow-fail-to-find must be true
- :selection/exact? must be false (can select fewer)
- Anti-pattern forbids auto-selecting even with 1 card

**Edge Case: Empty library**
- peek-count > 0 but library empty -> 0 candidates
- Return nil (same as X=0 case)

**Position Management:**
- Library cards have :object/position (0 = top)
- get-max-library-position finds highest current position
- New bottom cards get positions: max+1, max+2, etc.
- If shuffle?, randomize order before assigning positions

**Reference Implementation:**
- Follow build-tutor-selection at game.cljs:947-977
- Follow execute-tutor-selection at game.cljs:1597-1647
- Key difference: :selection/zone is :peek not :library

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN for this task)
- ❌ NO auto-selecting when only 1 card peeked (always show UI, always allow fail-to-find)
- ❌ NO card-specific logic (generic effect type only)
- ❌ NO player-ordered bottom (shuffle only, per user decision)
- ❌ NO modifying effect system dispatch (just add new multimethod)
close_reason: Closed

title: Remove confirm step from selection dialogs: accept choice immediately
description: Most selection dialogs (tutor, discard, etc.) require the player to make a selection and then click confirm. For a practice tool where speed matters, the extra confirm click adds friction. Change dialogs to accept the user's choice immediately on click when only one selection is needed. Consider: multi-select dialogs (discard N cards) still need confirm since player builds a set, but single-select (tutor for one card, choose one mode) should close on click. Optional: setting to re-enable confirm for players who want the safety net.
close_reason: Auto-confirm implemented in ::toggle-selection for single-select types (tutor/1, cast-time-targeting, player-target, ability-targeting). 11 tests added covering positive, negative, deselect, and edge cases.

title: Epic: Unit tests for 12 untested effect types
description: effects_test.cljs covers 27 of 39 effect types. 12 types have no isolated unit tests: :bounce, :phase-out, :tap-all, :untap-all, :welder-swap, :create-token, :apply-pt-modifier, :peek-random-hand, :exile-zone, :counter-spell, :counter-ability, :gain-life-equal-to-cmc. These are tested through card tests but lack isolated coverage. 3 tasks by complexity tier: simple (4) → filter/multi (5) → complex (3). Blocks fizzle-rcaw (integration tests).
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- All 12 untested effect types have isolated unit tests in effects_test.cljs
- Effect types: :bounce, :phase-out, :tap-all, :untap-all, :welder-swap, :create-token, :apply-pt-modifier, :peek-random-hand, :exile-zone, :counter-spell, :counter-ability, :gain-life-equal-to-cmc
- Each type has: happy path, nil/invalid target guard, and at least 1 boundary case
- :counter-spell unless-pay path tests tagged return shape {:db :needs-selection} with correct controller-id (NOT the full selection flow)
- All tests use fx/execute-effect as entry point (production path, not direct defmethod calls)
- Tests follow existing effects_test.cljs patterns and conventions
- All tests appended to existing effects_test.cljs (no new test files)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] 12 effect types have dedicated test groups in effects_test.cljs
- [ ] Each type has minimum 3 tests (happy path + nil target + boundary)
- [ ] :counter-spell has tests for: direct counter, nil target, not-on-stack, unless-pay tagged return
- [ ] :tap-all/:untap-all test filter logic (permanent-type, skip already-tapped/untapped)
- [ ] :welder-swap tests dual-target validation (both must be valid)
- [ ] :create-token verifies card entity + object + P/T + summoning sickness
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + fmt + all tests)
- [ ] No pre-existing test failures introduced

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO direct defmethod calls (use fx/execute-effect — production entry point; reason: tests must exercise condition checking)
- ❌ NO mutable conn pattern (use d/db-with not d/conn-from-db; reason: established convention, see audit learnings)
- ❌ NO tautological assertions like (is (some? x)) (reason: CLAUDE.md explicit prohibition; use exact value checks)
- ❌ NO hand-built selection maps for interactive effects (reason: test tagged return shape, not selection internals)
- ❌ NO new test files (reason: single-file-per-concern convention for effects)
- ❌ NO copy-paste test variants differing only by parameter (reason: use doseq per CLAUDE.md)

## Approach
3 tasks batched by complexity tier. Tier 1 (simple): :bounce, :phase-out, :peek-random-hand, :gain-life-equal-to-cmc. Tier 2 (filter/multi): :tap-all, :untap-all, :exile-zone, :create-token, :apply-pt-modifier. Tier 3 (complex): :counter-spell, :counter-ability, :welder-swap. Each task appends to effects_test.cljs following existing patterns. Iterative task creation — only first task created upfront.

## Architecture
- Test file: src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs (append)
- Production code under test: engine/effects/zones.cljs, stack.cljs, tokens.cljs, pt_modifier.cljs, simple.cljs, life.cljs
- Entry point: fx/execute-effect (engine/effects.cljs)
- Helpers: fizzle.test-helpers (th/create-test-db, th/add-card-to-zone, th/add-test-creature)

## Design Rationale
### Problem
effects_test.cljs covers 27 of 39 effect types. 12 types have no isolated unit tests, only indirect coverage through card tests. This gap means regressions in effect logic (especially :counter-spell branching, :tap-all/:untap-all filtering) could go undetected.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- effects_test.cljs:1-2238 — 27 tested types with consistent pattern: fx/execute-effect entry, happy+nil+boundary
- effects/stack.cljs:10-25 — :counter-spell has 3-way branch (nil, direct counter, unless-pay interactive)
- effects/zones.cljs:177-216 — :tap-all/:untap-all filter by :effect/permanent-type, skip already-tapped/untapped
- effects/zones.cljs:219-240 — :welder-swap dual-target with sacrifice+return
- effects/tokens.cljs:14-43 — :create-token builds card entity + object with full attributes

**Patterns observed:**
- Guard clauses return db unchanged on invalid input (never throw)
- Interactive effects return {:db db :needs-selection effect}
- Zone helpers handle all transitions (zones/move-to-zone, zones/phase-out)

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. By complexity tier ✓
**What it is:** 3 tasks: simple (4) → filter/multi (5) → complex (3). Natural learning curve.
**Chosen because:** Each tier builds on patterns from previous. No single task too large. Catches pattern issues early on simple types.

#### 2. By source file ❌
**Why explored:** Natural grouping alongside production code.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Uneven batches (zones.cljs has 6 types, others have 1). Tasks would be lopsided.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We need to split effects_test.cljs by production source file.

#### 3. Single task ❌
**Why explored:** Minimal task overhead.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Too large for single executor context. Hard to track progress through 12 implementations.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Executor context limits increase significantly.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** Isolated unit tests for 12 effect types via fx/execute-effect
**Out of scope:** Integration tests (resolve-stack-item→reduce-effects→SBA chain — that's fizzle-rcaw), full selection flow for :counter-spell unless-pay, card-level tests

### Open Questions
- :peek-random-hand uses randomness — test that a card is returned (not which card)
- :create-token may need new local helper for token setup

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Counter-spell interactive depth? | Tagged return only | Test {:db :needs-selection} shape + controller-id; defer selection flow to integration epic |
| Scope — all 12 or subset? | All 12 | Complete coverage gap in one pass |
| Organization — new file or append? | Append to existing | Follow single-file-per-concern convention |
| Batching approach? | By complexity tier | 3 tasks: simple→filter/multi→complex |

### Research Deep-Dives
#### Effect implementation survey
**Question explored:** What do the 12 untested types look like in production?
**Sources:** effects/zones.cljs, stack.cljs, tokens.cljs, pt_modifier.cljs, simple.cljs, life.cljs
**Findings:** All follow guard-clause-first pattern. :counter-spell has 3-way branch. :tap-all/:untap-all filter by permanent-type. :create-token builds both card entity and object.
**Conclusion:** Tests can follow existing effects_test.cljs patterns uniformly; only :counter-spell needs special handling for tagged return.

### Dead-End Paths
None — straightforward test addition task.

### Open Concerns Raised
- :peek-random-hand randomness → test that a card is returned, not which specific card
close_reason: All 12 effect types have isolated unit tests. 53 new tests, 3484 total, 0 failures.

title: Bug: History logs every bot phase transition — too noisy
description: yield-loop creates a history entry for every game-db change during bot turns. Each phase advance (untap/upkeep/draw/main1/combat/main2/end/cleanup) produces a separate entry, making history unusable. Bot turns should produce minimal/consolidated history entries.
design:
## Goal
Reduce history noise during bot turns. Currently ~8 history entries are created per bot turn (one per phase: untap, upkeep, draw, main1, combat, main2, end, cleanup). This makes the history panel unusable.

## Root Cause
History entries for bot phases are created in TWO places:

### Path 1: yield-loop (events/game.cljs:765-794)
Called by ::yield-all (F6). Creates an entry whenever game-db changes:
    result-adb (if (and post-db (not (identical? pre-db post-db)))
                 (let [... entry (history/make-entry post-db ::yield desc turn)]
                   (history/append-entry result-adb entry))
                 result-adb)

### Path 2: history interceptor (history/interceptor.cljs:52-111)
Global interceptor runs on every ::yield dispatch. When the user clicks single "Yield", ::yield re-dispatches itself for each bot phase (continue-yield? triggers [:dispatch [::yield]]). Each dispatch goes through the interceptor, which creates an entry for game-db changes.

**Both paths create per-phase entries during bot turns.** The fix must address BOTH.

## Fix
**Filter logic (same for both paths):** Skip the history entry when ALL of these are true:
1. Active player in post-db is a bot (bot/get-bot-archetype returns non-nil)
2. Stack is empty in BOTH pre-db and post-db (no spell cast or resolution)
3. Turn number did NOT change between pre-db and post-db (not a turn boundary)

This preserves:
- All human player entries (condition 1 fails — human is active)
- Bot casting entries (condition 2 fails — stack goes empty → non-empty)
- Bot spell resolution entries (condition 2 fails — stack was non-empty in pre-db)
- Turn boundary entries (condition 3 fails — turn number changed)
- Human actions during bot's turn with priority (condition 1 fails — human has priority after fizzle-vxlz fix, and the human player takes the action)

Filters out:
- Bot phase advances (bot active, stack empty→empty, same turn)
- Bot land plays (bot active, stack empty→empty, same turn)
- Bot draw step (bot active, stack empty→empty, same turn)

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Bot turn via ::yield-all produces at most 2-3 history entries (bolt cast + resolution + turn transition), not 8+
- [ ] Bot turn via single ::yield clicks also produces minimal entries (interceptor path filtered too)
- [ ] Bot casting Lightning Bolt still creates a visible history entry
- [ ] Bolt resolution (damage dealt) still creates a history entry
- [ ] Turn boundary transitions (start-turn) still create history entries
- [ ] Human player actions are completely unaffected (all entries preserved)
- [ ] History stepping (back/forward) works correctly through filtered bot turns
- [ ] Fork at any point during bot turns still works
- [ ] 6+ new tests covering both yield-loop and interceptor paths
- [ ] All existing tests pass (make validate clean)

## Implementation Checklist

### yield-loop fix (events/game.cljs:780-790)
- [ ] Extract a helper function (e.g., should-create-history-entry?) that takes pre-db and post-db and returns boolean:
    (defn- should-create-history-entry?
      "Returns true if a history entry should be created for this game-db change.
       Filters out bot phase advances (bot active, empty stack, same turn)."
      [pre-db post-db]
      (let [active-pid (queries/get-active-player-id post-db)
            is-bot (boolean (bot/get-bot-archetype post-db active-pid))
            pre-stack-empty (queries/stack-empty? pre-db)
            post-stack-empty (queries/stack-empty? post-db)
            pre-turn (:game/turn (queries/get-game-state pre-db))
            post-turn (:game/turn (queries/get-game-state post-db))]
        (not (and is-bot pre-stack-empty post-stack-empty (= pre-turn post-turn)))))
- [ ] Use helper in yield-loop: change condition from (not (identical? pre-db post-db)) to (and (not (identical? pre-db post-db)) (should-create-history-entry? pre-db post-db))

### history interceptor fix (history/interceptor.cljs:96-97)
- [ ] Import bot/get-bot-archetype and queries/stack-empty? into interceptor namespace
- [ ] In the :after fn, after the existing game-db-changed check (line 96), add the same filter:
    Before creating the entry, check should-create-history-entry? (or inline the logic)
    If the filter says skip, return context unchanged (same as when game-db didn't change)
- [ ] Note: the interceptor has pre-game-db (line 78) and game-db-after (line 80) available — use these

### Shared helper location
- [ ] Place should-create-history-entry? in a location accessible to both events/game.cljs and history/interceptor.cljs. Options:
  - history/core.cljs (history module, but would need bot dependency — bad)
  - engine/game_loop.cljs (already imports bot and queries — natural fit)
  - New small namespace like history/filters.cljs
  - SIMPLEST: inline the logic in both places (3 lines each, avoid new deps)
  Decision: inline in both places is simplest. The logic is 3-4 lines. Extracting to a shared namespace adds a dependency that doesn't exist yet (interceptor.cljs doesn't import bots).

### Tests
- [ ] In test/fizzle/bots/burn_integration_test.cljs, add yield-loop path tests:
  - yield-loop-skips-bot-phase-advance-entries:
    Setup: create game with burn bot, run yield-loop through a bot turn with no bolts (just phase advances)
    Assert: no history entries created for bot phase advances (count entries before and after)
  - yield-loop-keeps-bot-cast-entry:
    Setup: create game with burn bot with 1 mountain + 1 bolt, run yield-loop
    Assert: at least 1 entry exists for the bolt cast (entry description mentions Lightning Bolt or stack changed)
  - yield-loop-keeps-turn-boundary-entry:
    Setup: run yield-loop through bot turn to completion (cleanup → start-turn)
    Assert: entry exists for the turn transition (turn number changed)
- [ ] In test/fizzle/engine/game_loop_test.cljs or new file, add interceptor path tests:
  - yield-dispatch-skips-bot-phase-entries:
    Setup: create game with burn bot active, dispatch ::yield, check history entries
    Assert: bot phase advance does not create a history entry
  - yield-dispatch-keeps-human-entries:
    Setup: create game with human active, dispatch ::yield
    Assert: human phase advance DOES create a history entry (no false filtering)
  - history-stepping-works-with-filtered-entries:
    Setup: create game, run through human turn + bot turn, check history
    Assert: step-back from bot turn goes to last human action (no gap/crash)
- [ ] Run make validate

## Key Considerations

**Two Entry Creation Paths (CRITICAL):**
The yield-loop path handles ::yield-all (F6). The interceptor path handles single ::yield. Both MUST be filtered. Missing either path means bot phase noise persists for that interaction mode.

**Stack-Empty Check Approach:**
Using stack-empty? on both pre-db and post-db naturally captures all cases:
- Bot casts: pre-db stack empty, post-db stack non-empty → entry created (condition 2 fails)
- Bolt resolves: pre-db stack non-empty, post-db stack empty → entry created (condition 2 fails because pre-db had stack)
- Phase advance: both stacks empty → entry skipped

**Interceptor Dependency Concern:**
history/interceptor.cljs currently imports only queries and history. Adding bot import creates a new dependency: History → Opponent AI. This is a minor architectural concern but acceptable since:
- The filter is 3-4 lines of inline code
- The alternative (shared helper namespace) adds more complexity
- The dependency is read-only (just checking archetype, not calling bot actions)

**History Position Tracking:**
Filtered entries are simply not appended. The history position counter only advances on append. So fewer entries = fewer positions = correct behavior. No gaps, no off-by-one.

**Fork Safety:**
Fork-at-position and step-to use absolute entry indices. Fewer entries means fewer positions. step-back from a post-bot-turn position will land on the last human action entry. This is the desired UX.

**yield-loop Docstring Update:**
The yield-loop docstring (line 767-768) says "bot turns produce separate entries for each phase (ADR-004 compliant)." This docstring must be updated to reflect the new filtering behavior.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Do NOT batch/collapse bot entries into a single summary entry — just skip them entirely (simpler, fewer edge cases)
- ❌ Do NOT modify handle-loop-state :bot-phase to advance multiple phases at once — one-phase-at-a-time is correct for stops and stack checks
- ❌ Do NOT add a suppress-history flag or mode — use natural game state to decide
- ❌ Do NOT add bot dependency to history/core.cljs — inline the filter logic instead
- ❌ Do NOT forget to update the yield-loop docstring — it currently promises per-phase entries for bots
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 1: Create Cephalid Coliseum card definition
design:
## Goal
Create the Cephalid Coliseum card definition with both abilities (mana + threshold).

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours (single card definition + comprehensive tests)

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - cards/iggy_pop.cljs:171-182 - Lotus Petal (mana ability with sacrifice, :ability/effects pattern)
   - cards/iggy_pop.cljs:204-218 - Careful Study (draw then discard with :effect/selection :player)
   - cards/deep_analysis.cljs - Player targeting with :effect/target :any-player
   - engine/conditions.cljs - Threshold condition check
   - cards/iggy_pop.cljs:58-78 - City of Brass (for contrast - uses trigger, NOT what we want)

2. Write tests first (TDD)
   Follow test patterns from lotus_petal_test.cljs and careful_study_test.cljs

3. Implementation checklist
   - [ ] Create src/main/fizzle/cards/cephalid_coliseum.cljs
   - [ ] Define card structure:
         ```clojure
         {:card/id :cephalid-coliseum
          :card/name "Cephalid Coliseum"
          :card/types #{:land}
          :card/colors #{}
          :card/text "{T}: Add {U}. Cephalid Coliseum deals 1 damage to you.\nThreshold — {U}, {T}, Sacrifice Cephalid Coliseum: Target player draws three cards, then discards three cards. Activate only if seven or more cards are in your graveyard."
          :card/keywords #{:threshold}
          :card/abilities [...]}
         ```
   - [ ] Add mana ability:
         ```clojure
         {:ability/type :mana
          :ability/cost {:tap true}
          :ability/produces {:blue 1}
          :ability/effects [{:effect/type :deal-damage
                             :effect/amount 1
                             :effect/target :self}]}
         ```
   - [ ] Add threshold ability:
         ```clojure
         {:ability/type :activated
          :ability/cost {:tap true :sacrifice-self true :mana {:blue 1}}
          :ability/condition {:condition/type :threshold}
          :ability/effects [{:effect/type :draw
                             :effect/amount 3
                             :effect/target :any-player}
                            {:effect/type :discard
                             :effect/count 3
                             :effect/selection :player}]}
         ```
   - [ ] Register in all-cards vector in iggy_pop.cljs
   - [ ] Create test file src/test/fizzle/cards/cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Card definition compiles: `make dev` starts without errors
- [ ] 10+ unit tests pass covering all scenarios below
- [ ] Card appears in all-cards: verify in REPL `(count cards/all-cards)` increases
- [ ] All project tests pass: `make test`
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass: `make validate`

## Test Specifications (TDD - write these FIRST)

### Card Definition Tests
- `test-cephalid-coliseum-is-land-type` - Verify :card/types contains :land (catches wrong card type)
- `test-cephalid-coliseum-has-two-abilities` - Verify exactly 2 abilities exist (catches missing ability)
- `test-cephalid-coliseum-has-threshold-keyword` - Verify :card/keywords contains :threshold

### Mana Ability Tests
- `test-coliseum-tap-produces-blue-mana` - Activate mana ability, verify {:blue 1} added to pool (catches wrong mana production)
- `test-coliseum-tap-deals-one-damage-to-controller` - Activate mana ability, verify player life reduced by 1 (catches missing :ability/effects)
- `test-coliseum-tap-mana-and-damage-happen-together` - Single activation produces mana AND deals damage (catches trigger vs effect bug)
- `test-coliseum-cannot-tap-when-already-tapped` - Verify tapped land cannot activate (catches missing tap cost check)

### Threshold Ability Tests
- `test-coliseum-threshold-blocked-with-six-cards-in-graveyard` - With exactly 6 cards in graveyard, ability should not be activatable (catches off-by-one in threshold check)
- `test-coliseum-threshold-activatable-with-seven-cards-in-graveyard` - With exactly 7 cards in graveyard, ability should be activatable (catches wrong threshold number)
- `test-coliseum-threshold-requires-blue-mana` - Without {U} in pool, ability cannot be activated (catches missing mana cost)
- `test-coliseum-threshold-sacrifices-land` - After activation, land should be in graveyard (catches missing sacrifice cost)
- `test-coliseum-threshold-draws-three-cards` - Targeted player draws 3 cards (catches wrong draw count)
- `test-coliseum-threshold-target-discards-three` - After draw, selection modal requires 3 cards (catches wrong discard count)

### Edge Case Tests
- `test-coliseum-threshold-targeting-self` - Can select self as target for draw/discard
- `test-coliseum-threshold-targeting-opponent` - Can select opponent as target for draw/discard
- `test-coliseum-in-graveyard-cannot-activate` - Card in graveyard cannot use either ability

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

### Edge Case: Threshold Timing
- Threshold is checked on ACTIVATION only, not on resolution
- Per MTG rules, if graveyard drops below 7 after activation, ability still resolves
- Test: Activate with 7 cards, then somehow remove cards before resolution → ability still works
- Implementation: :ability/condition is checked in can-activate?, not during resolution

### Edge Case: Discard Count Mismatch
- What if target player has fewer than 3 cards after drawing?
- Per MTG rules: Discard as many as possible (min of hand size and 3)
- Codebase pattern: Careful Study test shows min(hand_count, required) logic
- Test: Target opponent with empty hand → draws 3, discards 3 (exactly what they have)

### Edge Case: Self-Damage at Low Life
- Player at 1 life tapping Coliseum → life becomes 0
- State-based actions check for 0 life
- This is correct MTG behavior, no special handling needed

### Edge Case: Mana Ability vs Trigger Distinction (CRITICAL)
- City of Brass uses :trigger/type :becomes-tapped (fires on ANY tap)
- Coliseum damage is part of mana ability (only fires when activated for mana)
- If opponent taps your Coliseum with some effect, City of Brass deals damage, Coliseum does NOT
- Implementation: Use :ability/effects, NOT :card/triggers
- Anti-pattern explicitly forbids trigger approach

### Reference Implementation
- Study src/test/fizzle/cards/lotus_petal_test.cljs for test helper patterns
- Study src/test/fizzle/cards/careful_study_test.cljs for selection/discard test patterns
- Follow exact test structure: create-test-db, add-card-to-zone, assertions

## Anti-Patterns (from Epic + Task-Specific)
- ❌ NO trigger for damage (use :ability/effects, not :trigger/type :becomes-tapped)
- ❌ NO auto-selecting discard targets (must use :effect/selection :player)
- ❌ NO skipping player target selection (must use :effect/target :any-player)
- ❌ NO hardcoding threshold check (must use :condition/type :threshold)
- ❌ NO skipping test cases (all 10+ tests must be implemented)
- ❌ NO tautological tests (each test must catch a specific bug, documented in test name)
close_reason: Cephalid Coliseum card implemented with all tests passing

title: Composable continuation chaining (ADR-020)
description: Change apply-continuation to return {:app-db ... :then continuation-or-nil} instead of plain app-db. Fixes circular dep between casting.cljs and priority_flow.cljs (fizzle-81fi), unifies deferred-entry processing ownership, and eliminates the rf/dispatch escape hatch. See docs/adr/adr-020-composable-continuation-chaining.md for full rationale.
close_reason: All 3 tasks complete, reviewer APPROVED, ADR-020 accepted, 3138 tests green

title: Add setup events, subscriptions, and localStorage persistence
design:
## Goal
Create the events/setup.cljs namespace with setup state management (deck selection, sideboard swaps, preset management), subs/setup.cljs with derived subscriptions, and storage.cljs for localStorage EDN persistence. This builds the data layer that the setup screen view will consume.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation Checklist

### Step 1: Create storage.cljs localStorage module

File: src/main/fizzle/storage.cljs

Functions needed:
- (save-presets! presets-map) — Serializes map to localStorage key \"fizzle-presets\" via pr-str
- (load-presets) — Reads and deserializes from localStorage via cljs.reader/read-string, returns {} on missing/corrupt
- (save-last-preset! preset-name) — Saves MRU preset name to localStorage key \"fizzle-last-preset\"
- (load-last-preset) — Reads MRU preset name, returns nil if missing

Use js/localStorage.setItem and js/localStorage.getItem directly.
Use cljs.reader/read-string for deserialization (safe for EDN).
Wrap cljs.reader/read-string in try/catch — return default value on parse failure.

### Step 2: Create events/setup.cljs namespace

File: src/main/fizzle/events/setup.cljs

Requires: [fizzle.cards.iggy-pop :as cards], [fizzle.events.game :as game], [fizzle.storage :as storage], [re-frame.core :as rf]

Events needed:

1. ::init-setup — Load presets from localStorage, set default deck config in app-db:
   - Load presets via storage/load-presets
   - Load last-preset name via storage/load-last-preset
   - Set :setup/selected-deck to :iggy-pop
   - Set :setup/main-deck to (:deck/main cards/iggy-pop-decklist)
   - Set :setup/sideboard to (:deck/side cards/iggy-pop-decklist)
   - Set :setup/clock-turns to 4
   - Set :setup/presets to loaded presets
   - Set :setup/last-preset to loaded last-preset name
   - Set :active-screen to :setup

2. ::select-deck [deck-id] — Load deck config into setup state:
   - Currently only :iggy-pop is available
   - Set :setup/selected-deck, :setup/main-deck, :setup/sideboard from cards/iggy-pop-decklist

3. ::move-to-side [card-id] — Move 1 copy from main to side:
   - Check total side count first — no-op if already at 15
   - Decrement count in :setup/main-deck for card-id (remove entry entirely if count reaches 0)
   - Increment count in :setup/sideboard for card-id (add new {:card/id X :count 1} entry if not present)

4. ::move-to-main [card-id] — Move 1 copy from side to main:
   - Decrement count in :setup/sideboard for card-id (remove entry entirely if count reaches 0)
   - Increment count in :setup/main-deck for card-id (add new {:card/id X :count 1} entry if not present)

5. ::set-clock-turns [n] — Set :setup/clock-turns to n (clamp to range 1-20)

6. ::save-preset [name] — Save current config as named preset:
   - If name is empty/blank string, no-op
   - Build config map from current setup state {:main-deck :sideboard :clock-turns :selected-deck}
   - Add to :setup/presets map under name
   - Persist via storage/save-presets!
   - Set :setup/last-preset to name and persist via storage/save-last-preset!

7. ::load-preset [name] — Load named preset:
   - Get config from :setup/presets map — no-op if name not found
   - Set :setup/main-deck, :setup/sideboard, :setup/clock-turns, :setup/selected-deck from config
   - Set :setup/last-preset to name and persist

8. ::delete-preset [name] — Remove named preset:
   - Remove from :setup/presets map
   - Persist via storage/save-presets!
   - Clear :setup/last-preset if it was the deleted preset

9. ::start-game — Validate and start game:
   - Compute main-count (sum of all :count in :setup/main-deck) and side-count
   - No-op if main-count != 60 or side-count > 15
   - No-op if :setup/main-deck is nil or empty
   - Dispatch [::game/init-game {:main-deck (:setup/main-deck db) :clock-turns (:setup/clock-turns db)}]
   - Return value of init-game-state (replaces entire app-db, so just call game/init-game-state directly)

NOTE: ::start-game should call game/init-game-state directly (not rf/dispatch) because it needs to replace the entire app-db. Pattern: (fn [db _] (if valid? (game/init-game-state config) db))

### Step 3: Create subs/setup.cljs namespace

File: src/main/fizzle/subs/setup.cljs

Subscriptions needed:

1. ::setup-config — Layer 2 extraction of all :setup/* keys (select-keys db [:setup/selected-deck ...])
2. ::available-decks — Returns [{:deck/id :iggy-pop :deck/name \"Iggy Pop\"}] (hardcoded for now, no input signals)
3. ::selected-deck — Layer 2: returns (:setup/selected-deck db)
4. ::current-main — Layer 2: returns (:setup/main-deck db)
5. ::current-main-grouped — Layer 3 sub: groups main deck by card type using all-cards definitions
   - Build card-type-lookup at namespace load: (into {} (map (juxt :card/id identity) cards/all-cards))
   - Input signal: ::current-main
   - Group entries by card type, output: {:land [...] :instant [...] :sorcery [...] :artifact [...]}
   - Each entry has {:card/id :card/name :count :card/types}
6. ::current-side — Layer 2: returns (:setup/sideboard db)
7. ::deck-valid? — Layer 3 sub with inputs ::main-count and ::side-count
   - Returns (and (= 60 main-count) (<= side-count 15))
8. ::main-count — Layer 2: (reduce + 0 (map :count (:setup/main-deck db)))
9. ::side-count — Layer 2: (reduce + 0 (map :count (:setup/sideboard db)))
10. ::saved-presets — Layer 2: returns (keys (:setup/presets db))
11. ::clock-turns — Layer 2: returns (:setup/clock-turns db)
12. ::last-preset — Layer 2: returns (:setup/last-preset db)

### Step 4: Write tests

Test file: src/test/fizzle/events/setup_test.cljs

Tests for:
- test-init-setup-populates-defaults: ::init-setup sets :setup/selected-deck, :setup/main-deck, :setup/sideboard, :setup/clock-turns, :active-screen. Catches: missing initialization fields.
- test-select-deck-loads-config: ::select-deck :iggy-pop sets correct main/side. Catches: deck loading bug.
- test-move-to-side-transfers-one-copy: start with 4x Dark Ritual in main, move-to-side, verify 3 remain. Catches: wrong decrement logic.
- test-move-to-side-blocked-at-15: set side to 15 cards total, try move-to-side, verify no change. Catches: missing validation.
- test-move-to-side-removes-entry-at-zero: start with 1x card in main, move-to-side, verify entry removed from main (not present with count 0). Catches: stale zero-count entries.
- test-move-to-main-transfers-one-copy: mirror of move-to-side test. Catches: wrong decrement on side.
- test-move-to-main-adds-new-entry: move card from side that isn't in main yet, verify new entry created in main. Catches: missing entry creation.
- test-set-clock-turns: set to 6, verify stored. Catches: wrong key update.
- test-save-preset-stores-config: save preset \"Test\", verify in :setup/presets. Catches: persistence bug.
- test-save-preset-empty-name-noop: save preset with empty name, verify no change. Catches: missing input validation.
- test-load-preset-restores-config: save then load preset, verify config matches. Catches: restore bug.
- test-load-preset-unknown-name-noop: load nonexistent preset, verify no change. Catches: missing guard.
- test-delete-preset-removes: save then delete, verify removed. Catches: deletion bug.
- test-delete-preset-clears-last-if-match: delete the MRU preset, verify :setup/last-preset cleared. Catches: stale MRU reference.
- test-start-game-with-valid-deck: start game with valid 60/15, verify :active-screen :game and :game/db exists. Catches: game init not triggered.
- test-start-game-invalid-deck-noop: set main to 59 cards, try start, verify still on setup screen. Catches: missing validation.

Test file: src/test/fizzle/subs/setup_test.cljs

Tests for:
- test-deck-valid-true-for-60-15: valid deck returns true. Catches: wrong comparison.
- test-deck-valid-false-for-59: main at 59 returns false. Catches: off-by-one.
- test-deck-valid-false-for-side-16: side at 16 returns false. Catches: side validation missing.
- test-main-count-sums-correctly: verify sum matches expected. Catches: reduce bug.
- test-current-main-grouped-groups-by-type: verify lands grouped separately from instants. Catches: grouping logic bug.

Test file: src/test/fizzle/storage_test.cljs

Tests for:
- test-presets-round-trip: save and load, verify equal. Catches: serialization bug.
- test-load-presets-empty-returns-default: clear localStorage, load, verify {}. Catches: nil handling.
- test-load-presets-corrupt-returns-default: set corrupt string in localStorage, load, verify {} not crash. Catches: parse error handling.
- test-last-preset-round-trip: save and load MRU name. Catches: wrong key.

### Step 5: Update core.cljs

Change init function:
- Add require for [fizzle.events.setup :as setup]
- Change (rf/dispatch-sync [::events/init-game]) to (rf/dispatch-sync [::setup/init-setup])
- This makes setup screen the landing page
- Remove the old dispatch — do NOT keep both

## Success Criteria
- [ ] storage.cljs handles localStorage EDN round-trip with corrupt-data resilience
- [ ] events/setup.cljs has all 9 event handlers
- [ ] subs/setup.cljs has all 12 subscriptions
- [ ] ::init-setup sets complete default config and loads presets from localStorage
- [ ] ::move-to-side blocked when side total = 15 (no-op, no crash)
- [ ] ::move-to-side and ::move-to-main remove entries at count 0 (no stale entries)
- [ ] ::save-preset with empty name is no-op
- [ ] ::load-preset with unknown name is no-op
- [ ] ::start-game validates deck (60 main, <=15 side) before starting
- [ ] ::start-game is no-op when deck invalid (stays on setup screen)
- [ ] core.cljs dispatches ::init-setup on app init (setup screen is landing page)
- [ ] All tests pass (new + existing 1007)
- [ ] make validate passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO game state in localStorage (only presets/settings)
- NO Datascript schema changes (setup state is app-db maps only)
- NO direct js/localStorage calls outside storage.cljs module
- NO circular dependencies (setup -> game events: call game/init-game-state directly for ::start-game, not rf/dispatch)
- NO breaking existing game initialization (::init-game still works independently when called with config)
- NO leaving zero-count entries in deck vectors (remove entry when count reaches 0)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Uninitialized setup state**
If ::start-game is dispatched before ::init-setup (e.g., via REPL), :setup/main-deck could be nil. Guard with nil check: (when (seq main-deck) ...).

**Edge Case: Empty preset name**
::save-preset with \"\" or \"   \" should be no-op. Use (when-not (str/blank? name) ...).

**Edge Case: Stale preset referencing removed cards**
If a future update removes a card-id from all-cards, presets saved with that card-id would reference invalid cards. For v1 this is acceptable — deck validation in ::start-game will catch invalid decks (card won't exist in Datascript). Future: add preset migration.

**Edge Case: clock-turns validation**
::set-clock-turns should clamp to reasonable range (1-20). Negative or zero clock-turns are nonsensical.

**localStorage availability**
In rare cases (private browsing, storage quota), localStorage may throw. Wrap setItem in try/catch, silently degrade — presets are nice-to-have, not critical.

**Subscription dependencies**
::deck-valid? should use ::main-count and ::side-count as input signals (Layer 3), not recompute from db directly. This ensures re-frame's signal graph is efficient.

**::start-game implementation pattern**
Since ::start-game needs to completely replace app-db (game/init-game-state returns a fresh app-db), it should call game/init-game-state directly in the event handler, NOT use rf/dispatch. Pattern:
(rf/reg-event-db ::start-game (fn [db _] (let [config {...}] (if (valid? db) (game/init-game-state config) db))))
This avoids async dispatch ordering issues.
close_reason: Closed

title: Wave 1: Testing Strategy Documentation
design:
## Goal
Document the card testing strategy at two levels: a concise summary in CLAUDE.md for always-visible requirements, and a full guide in docs/testing-strategy.md with examples, exemplar references, and rationale. Establishes the standard that all future card tests must follow.

## Epic Context
**Epic:** fizzle-8tl0 — Test Infrastructure & Card Testing Strategy

**Requirements (IMMUTABLE):**
- Card testing strategy documented in both CLAUDE.md (summary) and docs/testing-strategy.md (full guide)
- Every card definition has at least the mandatory test categories defined by the strategy

**Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN):**
- ❌ NO reimplementing production event handlers in tests (test through rf/dispatch)
- ❌ NO copy-pasted test variants that differ only by a parameter (use doseq/parameterization)
- ❌ NO test-only methods on production code
- ❌ NO mocking re-frame dispatch in card tests

## File Ownership
**This task OWNS (exclusive):**
- docs/testing-strategy.md (NEW file)
- CLAUDE.md — Card Testing Requirements section ONLY (append new section, do not modify existing content)

**This task MUST NOT modify:**
- Any test files (owned by Stream 1: Test Helpers)
- src/main/fizzle/subs/game.cljs (owned by Stream 3: Selection Boilerplate)
- src/main/fizzle/views/modals.cljs (owned by Stream 3: Selection Boilerplate)

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (2-3 hours studying exemplars and writing full guide, 1-2 hours CLAUDE.md section, 1 hour review and cross-referencing)

## Implementation

### 1. Study exemplar tests to extract patterns

Read these files to understand what good tests look like:
- src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs (1569 lines) — engine gold standard: exhaustive, edge cases, boundary conditions. Rating: 10/10
- src/test/fizzle/cards/brain_freeze_test.cljs (484 lines) — complex integration: full storm pipeline. Rating: 9/10
- src/test/fizzle/cards/recoup_test.cljs (586 lines) — thorough boundary testing: targeting, flashback lifecycle. Rating: 9/10
- src/test/fizzle/engine/mana_test.cljs — comprehensive mana operations. Rating: 9/10

Also study anti-pattern examples:
- src/test/fizzle/cards/careful_study_test.cljs:322-395 — reimplements production handler (~73 lines of logic duplication)
- src/test/fizzle/cards/lotus_petal_test.cljs — 5 near-identical color tests varying only by keyword

### 2. Study card types and mechanics in the codebase
- src/fizzle/cards/ — all card definition files (8 card def files, ~58 cards)
- src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs — effect types: add-mana, draw, discard, mill, tutor, return-from-graveyard, exile-self, sacrifice, drain, each-player
- src/fizzle/events/selection.cljs — selection types for interactive effects
- src/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs — flashback, alternate costs, additional costs

### 3. Write docs/testing-strategy.md (full guide)

Structure with these exact sections:
- **Overview**: Purpose, link to CLAUDE.md summary, philosophy (test through real event dispatch, not mocks)
- **Mandatory Test Categories (A-D)**: Required for EVERY card regardless of type
  A. Card definition test — verify ALL fields: card/id, card/name, card/cmc, card/mana-cost, card/types, card/colors, card/effects (full effect list with all keys), card/abilities, card/keywords, card/targeting, card/alternate-costs. NOT just "card exists" — verify the COMPLETE data structure matches expected values. This catches typos in effect types, missing fields, wrong mana costs.
  B. Cast-resolve happy path — full cycle: create-test-db, add mana, add to hand, cast, verify stack item, resolve, verify end state (card in correct zone, effects applied, mana pool changed). Tests the actual game flow.
  C. Cannot-cast guards — at minimum: insufficient mana (have 1, need 2), wrong phase (sorcery in combat), no valid targets (targeted spell). Each should verify the cast attempt is blocked.
  D. Storm count — casting MUST increment storm count. Simple but critical for combo deck practice tool.
- **Conditional Test Categories (E-I)**: Required when card has matching mechanic
  E. Selection/modal tests — when card requires player choice (discard, tutor, scry). Test: selection state has correct params, confirm with valid selection produces correct outcome, edge case: minimum and maximum selection counts.
  F. Targeting tests — when card targets. Test: valid targets accepted, invalid targets rejected, fizzle when target removed before resolution, targeting with empty valid set.
  G. Edge case tests (always 2+ per card) — empty zones (draw from empty library), partial resources (not enough cards to discard), already-tapped permanents, threshold active/inactive.
  H. Flashback tests — when card has flashback. Test: cast from graveyard works, correct (alternate) cost paid, exiled after resolution (not returned to graveyard), cannot flashback from hand.
  I. Trigger/ability tests — when permanent has triggers/abilities. Test: trigger registers on ETB, trigger fires on correct event, trigger uses stack, trigger doesn't fire on its own entry.
- **Minimum Test Counts** (with rationale):
  - Simple instant/sorcery: 4-5 (A + B + C + D = 4 base)
  - Selection spell: 6-8 (base + E selections)
  - Targeted spell: 8-12 (base + F targeting variations)
  - Flashback spell: 12-20 (base + F + H, both cast modes)
  - Permanent with abilities: 6-9 (base + I triggers)
  - Complex multi-mechanic (storm+targeting): 12-15 (base + E + F + combined interactions)
- **Exemplar Test Files** (with commentary on WHY they're good):
  - effects_test.cljs: One concern per test, exhaustive edge cases, clear naming
  - brain_freeze_test.cljs: Full integration flow, tests real event dispatch
  - recoup_test.cljs: Tests both cast modes, boundary conditions, flashback lifecycle
  - mana_test.cljs: Parameterized across colors, invariant testing
- **Anti-Pattern Examples** (with commentary on WHY they're bad):
  - careful_study_test.cljs:322-395: Reimplements confirm-fn manually — if production handler changes, test still passes while app is broken
  - lotus_petal_test.cljs: 5 copy-paste tests for sacrifice-for-color — use doseq [:white :blue :black :red :green]
- **Test Helper Usage**: Reference fizzle.test-helpers namespace (being created by concurrent Stream 1). Document expected helpers: create-test-db, add-card-to-zone, etc.
- **Parameterization Patterns**: When to use doseq (same test shape, different data) vs separate tests (different test shapes)

### 4. Add Card Testing Requirements section to CLAUDE.md

Append to the Conventions section. Keep concise (15-25 lines):
- Mandatory categories A-D (one line each)
- Conditional categories E-I (trigger condition + one line)
- Minimum test count table (compact)
- Link to docs/testing-strategy.md
- Top 3 anti-patterns (one line each)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] docs/testing-strategy.md exists with all sections listed above
- [ ] CLAUDE.md has Card Testing Requirements section appended to Conventions
- [ ] Strategy references actual exemplar files with approximate line ranges
- [ ] Anti-pattern examples reference actual code with specific file:line
- [ ] Minimum test counts specified for each card type with rationale
- [ ] All mandatory categories (A-D) described with concrete what-to-verify details
- [ ] All conditional categories (E-I) described with trigger conditions and examples
- [ ] Category A explicitly requires verifying FULL card definition (not just existence)
- [ ] No modifications to any test files or production code
- [ ] make validate passes (no broken formatting in CLAUDE.md)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Category A (card definition test) must NOT be tautological**
- BAD: (is (some? (:card/id card))) — tautological, compiler catches this
- GOOD: (is (= :dark-ritual (:card/id card))) (is (= {:black 3} (:effect/mana (first (:card/effects card))))) — verifies actual data
- Strategy doc MUST explicitly warn against tautological definition tests
- Include a "good vs bad" example in the Category A section

**Edge Case: Test helper namespace name may differ**
- Stream 1 creates test_helpers.cljs concurrently
- Reference it generically as "the shared test helpers namespace (fizzle.test-helpers)"
- If name changes, only one line in the doc needs updating

**Edge Case: Card types not yet in the codebase**
- Strategy should be generic enough for future card types (creatures, enchantments, planeswalkers)
- Use conditional categories (E-I) to handle "if card has X mechanic, test Y"
- Don't over-specify for card types that don't exist yet

**Edge Case: Premodern format specifics**
- Some categories (like creature combat) may be irrelevant for current Premodern combo focus
- Note in the docs that categories are format-aware and some may not apply to current card pool
- Keep categories generic enough for future format expansion

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO vague test descriptions ("test it works") — every category must specify WHAT to verify with concrete examples
- ❌ NO test counts without rationale — explain WHY a targeted spell needs 8-12 tests
- ❌ NO referencing files that don't exist — verify all file paths before referencing
- ❌ NO modifying existing CLAUDE.md content — only APPEND new section
- ❌ NO prescribing tautological tests — Category A must verify actual values, not just existence
- ❌ NO prescribing tests that verify mocks — all examples must use real event dispatch
close_reason: Superseded: epic restructured from wave-based to linear execution model

title: Implement Rain of Filth card
description: Black instant {B}: Until end of turn, lands you control gain 'Sacrifice this land: Add {B}.' Only grants to lands controlled at resolution time. Card def + full test suite.
design:
## Implement Rain of Filth card

### Goal
Black instant {B}: Until end of turn, lands you control gain 'Sacrifice this land: Add {B}.'

### Codebase Verification
- ✓ Black cards: src/main/fizzle/cards/black/
- ✓ Template: dark_ritual.cljs for simple black instant
- ✓ Query: query-zone-by-criteria db player-id :battlefield {:match/types #{:land}}

### Step 1: Create card file

File: src/main/fizzle/cards/black/rain_of_filth.cljs

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.black.rain-of-filth
  "Rain of Filth card definition.

   Rain of Filth: B - Instant
   Until end of turn, lands you control gain
   'Sacrifice this land: Add {B}.'")

(def card
  {:card/id :rain-of-filth
   :card/name "Rain of Filth"
   :card/cmc 1
   :card/mana-cost {:black 1}
   :card/colors #{:black}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/text "Until end of turn, lands you control gain 'Sacrifice this land: Add {B}.'"
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :grant-mana-ability
                   :effect/target :controlled-lands
                   :effect/ability {:ability/type :mana
                                    :ability/cost {:sacrifice-self true}
                                    :ability/produces {:black 1}
                                    :ability/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana
                                                       :effect/mana {:black 1}}]}}]})
```

### Step 2: Register in registry

File: src/main/fizzle/cards/registry.cljs
- Add require: [fizzle.cards.black.rain-of-filth :as rain-of-filth]
- Add to all-cards vector: rain-of-filth/card

### Step 3: Write test file

File: src/test/fizzle/cards/black/rain_of_filth_test.cljs

Mandatory categories:
- A. Card definition — all fields exact
- B. Cast-resolve: grants sacrifice-for-B to all controlled lands
  - Verify each land has :ability grant in :object/grants
  - Verify grant has correct ability data
- C. Cannot-cast: no black mana, wrong zone
- D. Storm count
- G. Edge cases:
  - No lands on battlefield = no-op
  - Tapped lands get grant and can activate (no :tap cost)
  - Activate granted ability: sacrifice land -> get {B} mana
  - Multiple lands: each gets independent grant
  - Grant expires at cleanup phase
  - Lands entering AFTER resolution don't get ability
- I. Integration test: Cast Rain of Filth, then sacrifice lands for mana, use mana

### Step 4: Verify
Run: make validate

title: Blocker assignment: serial selection with flying/reach restrictions
description:
Implement declare-blockers resolution with serial blocker assignment (one attacker at a time using selection chaining). Flying/reach blocking restriction enforced. Player controls all blocking for both sides.

## Scope
- :declare-blockers resolution builds chaining selection for each attacker
- Each attacker gets one selection round: pick 0+ eligible blockers
- Flying creatures can only be blocked by creatures with flying or reach
- Selection uses :selection/lifecycle :chaining pattern
- Player controls blocking for both their creatures and opponent's creatures (no bot blocker AI per epic anti-pattern)

## Files
- engine/combat.cljs — can-block?, get-eligible-blockers
- engine/resolution.cljs — replace :declare-blockers stub with real implementation
- events/selection/combat.cljs — :assign-blockers selection type with chaining
close_reason: Closed

title: Abilities integration + unit tests
description:
## Goal
Create src/test/fizzle/events/abilities_test.cljs with integration (dispatch-sync) and unit tests for events/abilities.cljs. Focus on guard conditions and SBA verification — card tests already cover happy paths.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### Setup (same pattern as phases_test.cljs)
File: src/test/fizzle/events/abilities_test.cljs

Copy setup from phases_test.cljs:
- interceptor/register! at top level
- setup-app-db via h/create-game-scenario
- dispatch-event wrapper (reset!/dispatch-sync/deref)

Additional requires: fizzle.events.abilities, fizzle.engine.abilities, fizzle.engine.priority

### Context
abilities.cljs has 3 handlers:
- ::activate-mana-ability — simple: calls engine activate-mana-ability, creates history entry
- ::activate-ability — complex: calls activate-ability pure fn, may create pending-selection (targeting/sacrifice), creates history or deferred entry
- ::activate-granted-mana-ability — calls activate-granted-mana-ability pure fn

Key pure fns: activate-ability (4-way dispatch: sacrifice/targeting/generic-mana/direct), build-ability-target-selection, confirm-ability-target, activate-granted-mana-ability

### Integration Tests (dispatch-sync)
1. **activate-mana-ability-adds-mana**: Add a land to battlefield (e.g., Swamp via add-card-to-zone :battlefield). Dispatch ::activate-mana-ability with object-id and :black. Assert mana pool has 1 black. Bug: event handler wiring broken.

2. **activate-mana-ability-creates-history-entry**: Same as above. Assert :history/main has entry with ::activate-mana-ability event-type (interceptor consumes pending-entry before dispatch returns — check :history/main not :history/pending-entry).

3. **activate-ability-creates-pending-selection-for-targeting**: Add a permanent with a targeted activated ability to battlefield (e.g., fetchland like Polluted Delta). Dispatch ::activate-ability. Assert :game/pending-selection with :selection/type :ability-targeting. Bug: targeting selection wiring broken.

4. **activate-ability-clears-selected-card**: After dispatch, verify :game/selected-card is dissoc'd from app-db. Bug: stale selection after activation (abilities.cljs:299).

### Unit Tests (direct function calls on game-db)
5. **activate-ability-wrong-zone**: Call activate-ability with object in :hand (not :battlefield). Assert returns {:db db :pending-selection nil} unchanged. Bug: zone check at abilities.cljs:279 broken.

6. **activate-ability-wrong-controller**: Call activate-ability with object controlled by opponent. Assert returns unchanged. Bug: controller check at abilities.cljs:280 broken.

7. **activate-ability-not-in-priority-phase**: Set game phase to :untap (not priority). Call activate-ability. Assert returns unchanged. Bug: priority check at abilities.cljs:270 broken.

8. **activate-ability-with-restriction**: Add :cannot-activate-non-mana-abilities restriction to player. Call activate-ability. Assert returns unchanged. Bug: restriction check at abilities.cljs:273 broken.

9. **activate-ability-invalid-ability-index**: Call activate-ability with ability-index out of bounds (e.g., 99). Assert returns unchanged. Bug: nth with nil guard at abilities.cljs:282 broken.

10. **activate-ability-can-activate-false**: Call with ability that can't be activated (e.g., already tapped for tap-cost ability). Assert returns unchanged. Bug: can-activate? check at abilities.cljs:285 broken.

11. **activate-granted-mana-ability-wrong-zone**: Call activate-granted-mana-ability with object not on battlefield. Assert returns db unchanged. Bug: zone check broken.

12. **activate-granted-mana-ability-not-priority**: Set phase to non-priority phase. Call. Assert returns unchanged. Bug: priority check broken.

### SBA Verification Tests
13. **activate-ability-sacrifice-cost-moves-to-graveyard**: Setup permanent with sacrifice-cost ability. Call activate-ability. Verify the sacrificed permanent moved to graveyard (not still on battlefield). Bug: sacrifice cost SBA not verified.

14. **activate-mana-ability-tap-cost-taps-permanent**: Dispatch ::activate-mana-ability on untapped land. Verify the land is now tapped after activation. Bug: tap cost not applied.

### Key Learnings from phases_test.cljs (APPLY)
- Check :history/main not :history/pending-entry (interceptor consumes it)
- Use (merge (setup-app-db) {:game/db modified-db}) to inject test state into app-db
- Run make fmt before make validate (Datalog query indentation)
- Destructure as [db _] when obj-id unused (clj-kondo warning)
- Check if existing unit tests exist (abilities may have pure fn tests already)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] File exists at src/test/fizzle/events/abilities_test.cljs
- [ ] 14 tests specified above all pass
- [ ] Integration tests (1-4) all use dispatch-sync pattern
- [ ] Unit tests (5-12) call pure functions directly
- [ ] SBA tests (13-14) verify state changes after ability activation
- [ ] Guard conditions tested: wrong zone, wrong controller, no priority, restriction, invalid index, can-activate false
- [ ] History entry tested with exact event-type assertion (not some?)
- [ ] make validate passes (all existing + new tests green, lint clean, fmt clean)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO mocking re-frame dispatch or interceptors
- ❌ NO hand-building app-db from scratch — use h/create-game-scenario
- ❌ NO some?/string? assertions — assert exact values
- ❌ NO duplicating card test happy paths (fetchland/altar tests exist — focus on guards + SBAs)
- ❌ NO testing pure functions via dispatch-sync or vice versa — keep layers separate
close_reason: Closed

description:
