title: Stifle ability targeting not implemented in find-valid-targets
description: find-valid-targets in engine/targeting.cljs only handles :player and :object target types. :ability falls through to :else [] returning empty targets. Need to add :ability clause that queries stack items of type :activated-ability/:triggered-ability, excludes mana abilities, returns EIDs. Also need target-still-legal? for :ability type.
close_reason: Closed

title: Peek-and-reorder selection has no render modal (shows hand fallback)
description:
## Problem

peek-and-reorder selection type has no defmethod in render-selection-modal. Falls through to :default which renders card-selection-modal. Since peek-and-reorder doesn't set :selection/card-source, the subscription falls back to showing hand cards.

## Root cause

The executor implemented the peek-and-reorder engine mechanic and selection builder but never added the UI render method. The ordering infrastructure exists (order-card/unorder-card events, :selection/ordered, :selection/candidates) — reused from order-bottom. Just needs a modal component and subscription.

## Fix

1. Add ::peek-and-reorder-cards subscription in subs/game.cljs (parallel to ::order-bottom-cards)
2. Add peek-and-reorder-modal component in views/modals.cljs (similar to order-bottom-modal with different labels)
3. Register render-selection-modal :peek-and-reorder method
close_reason: Closed

title: fire-delayed-effects only checks active player grants (should check all)
description:
## Problem

fire-delayed-effects in turn_based.cljs is called with the active player's ID during advance-phase. It only checks that player's grants. Portent's delayed draw grant is stored on the caster, but the 'next turn' upkeep is the opponent's turn. The caster's grants are never checked during the opponent's upkeep, so the draw fires one turn late.

## MTG Rules

Portent: 'Draw a card at the beginning of the next turn's upkeep.' This creates a delayed triggered ability that fires at the next upkeep regardless of whose turn it is. The controller (caster) draws.

## Fix

advance-phase should fire delayed effects for ALL players when entering upkeep, not just the active player. fire-delayed-effects already passes the grant holder's player-id to execute-effect, so the draw correctly goes to the grant holder.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 2: Rewrite Anti-Pattern Tests
design:
## Goal
Rewrite three test files to eliminate anti-patterns: careful_study reimplements production handler, lotus_petal/led have copy-pasted color variants.

## Pre-conditions
- Task 1 (migration) complete — all files use shared test helpers
- make validate passes (1412 tests, 3153 assertions)

## Effort Estimate
3-5 hours (mechanical rewrites with verification between each file)

## Implementation

### 1. Fix careful_study_test.cljs — Replace handler reimplementation with production code path

**Problem:** test-careful-study-discard-via-production-handler (line 227) defines a manual confirm-fn (lines 262-286) that reimplements the discard confirm logic. This anti-pattern means the test passes even if the real confirm-selection-impl is broken.

**Production code path (reference):**
- selection.core/confirm-selection-impl (core.cljs:143) takes app-db, calls execute-confirmed-selection multimethod
- execute-confirmed-selection :discard (zone_ops.cljs:76) moves selected cards to graveyard
- confirm-selection-impl then processes remaining-effects and calls cleanup-selection-source
- cleanup-selection-source moves spell from stack to graveyard AND removes stack-item entity

**Fix:**
a. Add require: [fizzle.events.selection.core :as core] (if not present)
b. Ensure zone_ops namespace is loaded (bare require [fizzle.events.selection.zone-ops] — needed for :discard defmethod registration)
c. Replace the manual confirm-fn call (lines 262-287) with: (core/confirm-selection-impl app-db)
d. The test already builds proper app-db with {:game/db db-after-draw :game/pending-selection sel-with-choice}
e. Extract result: result-app-db = (core/confirm-selection-impl app-db), final-db = (:game/db result-app-db)
f. Keep ALL existing assertions: empty hand, 3 cards in graveyard, CS in graveyard, no pending selection
g. Run make validate after this change

**CRITICAL edge case:** The production cleanup-selection-source also removes the stack-item entity (via remove-spell-stack-item). The manual confirm-fn did NOT do this. After switching to production code, the stack-item count will differ. This is correct behavior — the test now exercises the real cleanup path.

### 2. Fix lotus_petal_test.cljs — Parameterize 5 identical color tests

**Problem:** 5 separate deftest blocks (lines 13-77) test the same behavior for :black, :blue, :white, :red, :green. They differ ONLY in the color keyword and pool access key.

**Current assertion count:** 5 tests × 2 core assertions (sacrifice + mana) + ~5 precondition assertions = ~15 assertions total

**Fix:**
a. Replace 5 deftests with a single deftest containing doseq:
```clojure
(deftest test-lotus-petal-sacrifice-for-any-color
  (doseq [color [:black :blue :white :red :green]]
    (testing (str "Lotus Petal sacrifices for " (name color) " mana")
      (let [db (th/create-test-db)
            [db' obj-id] (th/add-card-to-zone db :lotus-petal :battlefield :player-1)
            initial-pool (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)
            _ (is (= 0 (get initial-pool color)) (str "Precondition: " (name color) " mana is 0"))
            db'' (ability-events/activate-mana-ability db' :player-1 obj-id color)]
        (is (= :graveyard (th/get-object-zone db'' obj-id))
            "Lotus Petal should be in graveyard after sacrifice")
        (is (= 1 (get (q/get-mana-pool db'' :player-1) color))
            (str (name color) " mana should be added to pool"))))))
```
b. Keep edge case tests (graveyard, hand, definition) completely unchanged
c. Final assertion count: 5 × 3 = 15 (precondition + sacrifice + mana) — same coverage
d. Run make validate

### 3. Fix led_test.cljs — Parameterize 3 color tests into comprehensive parameterized test

**Problem:** 3 separate deftests (lines 14-63) test black, blue, white. Black is detailed (4 assertions), blue/white are minimal (2 assertions each). Also missing :red and :green coverage.

**Current assertion count:** black(4 core + 3 preconditions) + blue(2 core + 1 precondition) + white(2 core + 1 precondition) = 13 total

**Fix:**
a. Replace 3 deftests with one parameterized test using doseq over ALL 5 colors:
```clojure
(deftest test-led-sacrifice-and-discard-for-any-color
  (doseq [color [:black :blue :white :red :green]]
    (testing (str "LED sacrifices and discards hand for 3 " (name color) " mana")
      (let [db (th/create-test-db)
            [db' led-id] (th/add-card-to-zone db :lions-eye-diamond :battlefield :player-1)
            [db'' _] (th/add-card-to-zone db' :dark-ritual :hand :player-1)
            [db''' _] (th/add-card-to-zone db'' :dark-ritual :hand :player-1)
            [db'''' _] (th/add-card-to-zone db''' :dark-ritual :hand :player-1)
            _ (is (= :battlefield (th/get-object-zone db'''' led-id))
                  "Precondition: LED starts on battlefield")
            _ (is (= 3 (th/get-zone-count db'''' :hand :player-1))
                  "Precondition: 3 cards in hand")
            initial-pool (q/get-mana-pool db'''' :player-1)
            _ (is (= 0 (get initial-pool color))
                  (str "Precondition: " (name color) " mana is 0"))
            db-after (ability-events/activate-mana-ability db'''' :player-1 led-id color)]
        (is (= :graveyard (th/get-object-zone db-after led-id))
            "LED should be in graveyard after sacrifice")
        (is (= 3 (get (q/get-mana-pool db-after :player-1) color))
            (str (name color) " mana should be 3"))
        (is (= 0 (th/get-zone-count db-after :hand :player-1))
            "Hand should be empty after discard")
        (is (= 4 (th/get-zone-count db-after :graveyard :player-1))
            "Graveyard should have 4 objects (3 hand cards + LED)")))))
```
b. Keep ALL edge case tests unchanged (empty hand, graveyard, hand, tapped, definition)
c. Final assertion count: 5 × 7 = 35 (3 preconditions + 4 core) — INCREASED from 13
d. Run make validate

### 4. Verify and commit
- Run make validate
- Record assertion count (should be >= 3153)
- No lint/format warnings
- Commit changes

## Success Criteria
- [ ] careful_study_test.cljs test-careful-study-discard-via-production-handler calls core/confirm-selection-impl (zero manual handler reimplementation)
- [ ] careful_study_test.cljs requires zone_ops namespace (ensures :discard defmethod loaded)
- [ ] lotus_petal_test.cljs has single parameterized deftest with doseq over 5 colors (replacing 5 separate deftests)
- [ ] led_test.cljs has single parameterized deftest with doseq over 5 colors (replacing 3 separate deftests)
- [ ] All existing tests pass (make validate green)
- [ ] No lint or format warnings
- [ ] Total assertion count >= 3153 (no coverage loss, LED gains coverage for :red/:green)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: zone_ops namespace loading**
- The :discard defmethod is registered in zone_ops.cljs, not core.cljs
- If zone_ops is not loaded, execute-confirmed-selection will dispatch to the default method (likely an error)
- MUST add bare require [fizzle.events.selection.zone-ops] to careful_study_test.cljs
- Verify: check if resolution.cljs already requires zone_ops transitively — if yes, bare require is defensive but not strictly needed

**Edge Case: stack-item cleanup difference**
- Current manual confirm-fn does NOT call remove-spell-stack-item
- Production confirm-selection-impl DOES call it via cleanup-selection-source
- This means after switching to production code, stack-item entity will be properly cleaned up
- If any assertion checks stack-item count, it may need updating (currently none do)

**Edge Case: cleanup? flag in selection**
- The :discard multimethod checks :selection/cleanup? flag — if true, takes cleanup path (expire grants, finalized)
- resolve-spell-with-selection creates a standard selection (not cleanup), so this flag should be false/nil
- No action needed, but be aware cleanup discard behaves differently

**Edge Case: LED produces :any mana**
- LED's ability/produces is {:any 3}, meaning it produces 3 mana of chosen color
- Parameterized test should cover all 5 colors to verify the :any production logic works for each
- Current tests only cover 3 colors — this is a coverage improvement

**Edge Case: doseq assertion reporting**
- cljs.test with doseq: failures report line number of the deftest, not the specific doseq iteration
- MUST include the color name in assertion messages (e.g., str "Black mana should be 3") so failures identify which color failed
- All assertion messages must be dynamically constructed with (str (name color) ...)

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO changing test BEHAVIOR — only change test STRUCTURE (parameterize, use production code)
- ❌ NO weakening assertions — each parameterized variant must assert the same things as the original
- ❌ NO mocking or stubbing production code — use real confirm-selection-impl
- ❌ NO removing edge case tests — only touch the duplicated/reimplemented tests
- ❌ NO removing unused requires without verifying they're truly unused (e.g., datascript.core may be needed elsewhere)
- ❌ NO hardcoding color lists — use a vector constant so new colors can be added later
close_reason: All three anti-pattern rewrites complete: careful_study uses production confirm-selection-impl, lotus_petal and led use doseq parameterization. make validate passes (1406 tests, 3174 assertions). Net -95 lines.

title: Tier 3: Unit tests for complex effects (counter-spell, counter-ability, welder-swap)
description: Add unit tests for 3 complex effect types to effects_test.cljs. These involve stack interaction, dual-target validation, and interactive branching. Final tier — completes epic fizzle-b6wd.
design:
## Goal
Add isolated unit tests for 3 complex effect types: :counter-spell, :counter-ability, :welder-swap. This is the FINAL task — completing it closes the epic.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

1. Study production code + prior tier patterns
   - effects_test.cljs lines 2249-2842 (Tier 1+2 patterns)
   - effects/stack.cljs:10-25 (:counter-spell impl — 3-way branch)
   - effects/stack.cljs:28-46 (:counter-ability impl)
   - effects/zones.cljs:219-240 (:welder-swap impl)
   - Read memory: epic-fizzle-b6wd-task-fizzle-wkg5-learnings.md and epic-fizzle-b6wd-task-fizzle-hs9b-learnings.md

2. Write tests (TDD — run make test between each type)

### :counter-spell (~5 tests)
Production (stack.cljs:10-25): 3-way branch:
  a) nil target → db (guard)
  b) target on stack + no unless-pay → counter via counter-target-spell (moves to graveyard)
  c) target on stack + has unless-pay → returns {:db db :needs-selection (assoc effect :unless-pay/controller controller-id)} (interactive)
Also guards: target not on stack → db, target doesn't exist → db.

Tests:
- Direct counter (no unless-pay): spell on stack → spell moved to graveyard (catches: counter-target-spell not called)
- Nil target: nil :effect/target → db unchanged (catches: missing nil guard)
- Not on stack: target exists but in :battlefield zone → db unchanged (catches: missing zone check)
- Nonexistent target: random-uuid → db unchanged (catches: missing existence guard)
- Unless-pay tagged return: spell on stack + :effect/unless-pay present → returns map with :db and :needs-selection keys, :needs-selection has :unless-pay/controller matching target's controller player-id (catches: interactive path broken, wrong controller resolution)
  NOTE: Test the SHAPE of the tagged return only, NOT the full selection flow.

### :counter-ability (~4 tests)
Production (stack.cljs:28-46): counters activated/triggered abilities on stack. Guards: nil target, nonexistent target, target not on stack.
- Happy path: ability on stack → removed from stack (catches: stack item not removed)
- Nil target: nil :effect/target → db unchanged
- Nonexistent target: random-uuid → db unchanged
- Target not on stack: target exists but wrong zone → db unchanged

### :welder-swap (~5 tests)
Production (zones.cljs:219-240): dual-target swap. Sacrifices artifact on battlefield (:effect/target), returns artifact from graveyard (:effect/graveyard-id) to battlefield.
- Happy path: artifact on battlefield + artifact in graveyard → battlefield art goes to graveyard, graveyard art comes to battlefield (catches: wrong zone transitions)
- Missing battlefield target: :effect/target nil or nonexistent → db unchanged (catches: missing guard on first target)
- Missing graveyard target: :effect/graveyard-id nil or nonexistent → db unchanged (catches: missing guard on second target)
- Both invalid: both targets invalid → db unchanged
- Battlefield target not on battlefield: target exists but wrong zone → behavior depends on production guards

## Success Criteria
- [ ] 3 effect types have dedicated test groups appended to effects_test.cljs
- [ ] :counter-spell has 5 tests: direct counter, nil target, not-on-stack, nonexistent target, unless-pay tagged return
- [ ] :counter-ability has 4 tests: happy path, nil target, nonexistent target, not-on-stack
- [ ] :welder-swap has 5 tests: happy path swap, missing bf target, missing gy target, both invalid, zone guard
- [ ] :counter-spell unless-pay test verifies {:db :needs-selection} shape + :unless-pay/controller value (NOT full selection flow)
- [ ] All tests use fx/execute-effect as entry point
- [ ] No tautological assertions (exact values only)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + fmt-check + all tests green)
- [ ] Commit with hash, close fizzle-hs9b in bd, run bd sync

## Key Considerations

**:counter-spell requires a spell ON THE STACK**
Must use stack/push-to-stack or equivalent to set up a spell object in the :stack zone with a corresponding stack-item entity. Study existing stack test setup in effects_test.cljs or stack_test.cljs for patterns.

**:counter-spell unless-pay returns a TAGGED MAP, not plain db**
Use fx/execute-effect-checked (or verify the map return from execute-effect-impl). The map has {:db db :needs-selection effect-with-controller}. Assert map?, assert :db key, assert :needs-selection key, assert :unless-pay/controller = correct player-id.

**:counter-ability targets STACK ITEMS, not zone objects**
The target is a stack-item entity ID, not an object-id. Set up an ability on the stack to test this.

**:welder-swap has TWO targets that must both be validated**
:effect/target = battlefield artifact (to sacrifice), :effect/graveyard-id = graveyard artifact (to return). Both must exist and be in correct zones.

**add-object-with-card returns [obj-id db] NOT [db obj-id]**
Critical destructuring order from Tier 1 learnings.

**:card/types/:card/colors are cardinality-many**
Use (set ...) for comparison — from Tier 2 learnings.

## Anti-patterns (inherited from epic)
- ❌ NO direct defmethod calls (use fx/execute-effect)
- ❌ NO mutable conn pattern in NEW code
- ❌ NO tautological assertions (exact values only)
- ❌ NO hand-built selection maps for :counter-spell unless-pay (test tagged return shape only)
- ❌ NO testing full selection flow for unless-pay (that's integration scope — fizzle-rcaw)
close_reason: Closed

title: Add pay-mana-with-allocation to engine/mana.cljs
description: Engine foundation for manual mana allocation. Add a new function pay-mana-with-allocation that accepts an explicit allocation map for generic costs instead of using the greedy algorithm. Colored costs still auto-deducted. This is the foundation that selection events and integration will build on.
design:
## Goal
Add pay-mana-with-allocation function to engine/mana.cljs that deducts colored costs normally, then applies an explicit generic allocation map instead of greedy sorting.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours (one function + 10-12 tests)

## Key Design Decision
The function takes a ALREADY-RESOLVED cost (no :x key). The caller (selection confirm handler) calls resolve-x-cost first, then passes the resolved cost + allocation map. This keeps the function simple and matches the casting flow where X selection completes before mana allocation.

## Function Signature
```clojure
(defn pay-mana-with-allocation
  "Remove mana from player's pool using explicit allocation for generic costs.

   cost: Mana cost map with :colorless for generic, color keys for colored.
         MUST NOT contain :x — caller resolves X before calling.
   allocation: Map of {color amount} specifying how to pay generic portion.
               e.g., {:black 2 :blue 1} means 'pay 2 generic from black, 1 from blue'
               Sum of allocation values MUST equal (:colorless cost 0).

   IMPORTANT: Caller MUST verify can-pay? first AND that allocation is valid.
   Invalid allocation (wrong sum, exceeds pool) results in undefined behavior."
  [db player-id cost allocation]
  ...)
```

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - engine/mana.cljs:75-114 — current pay-mana with greedy allocation
   - engine/mana.cljs:42-62 — can-pay? validation
   - engine/mana.cljs:28-39 — add-mana (merge-with pattern for pool updates)
   - src/test/fizzle/engine/mana_test.cljs — all existing tests

2. Write tests first (TDD)

   **Core behavior tests:**
   - test-pay-allocated-single-color: pool {:black 5 :blue 0 ...}, cost {:colorless 2 :black 1}, allocation {:black 2} → pool {:black 2} (1 colored + 2 allocated from black)
   - test-pay-allocated-multi-color: pool {:black 3 :blue 2}, cost {:colorless 2 :black 1}, allocation {:black 1 :blue 1} → pool {:black 1 :blue 1}
   - test-pay-allocated-preserves-unused-colors: pool {:black 3 :blue 2 :red 1}, cost {:colorless 2}, allocation {:black 2} → red stays 1, blue stays 2
   - test-pay-allocated-zero-generic: cost {:black 2}, allocation {} → just deducts colored, pool updated correctly
   - test-pay-allocated-only-generic: cost {:colorless 3}, allocation {:black 2 :blue 1} → no colored deduction, just allocation

   **Edge case tests (SRE additions):**
   - test-pay-allocated-colorless-mana-in-allocation: pool {:colorless 2 :black 1}, cost {:colorless 2}, allocation {:colorless 1 :black 1} → uses actual colorless mana toward generic
   - test-pay-allocated-all-from-one-color: pool {:black 5}, cost {:colorless 3 :black 2}, allocation {:black 3} → pool {:black 0}
   - test-pay-allocated-empty-cost-empty-allocation: cost {}, allocation {} → pool unchanged (no-op)
   - test-pay-allocated-large-pool-values: pool {:black 100}, cost {:colorless 50}, allocation {:black 50} → pool {:black 50} (no precision issues)

   **Contract documentation tests:**
   - test-pay-allocated-without-can-pay-goes-negative: documents that calling without can-pay? results in negative pool (matches pay-mana behavior, caller responsibility)

3. Implementation checklist
   - [ ] engine/mana.cljs — Add pay-mana-with-allocation function with signature above
   - [ ] Implementation: separate colored-cost (dissoc :colorless) from cost, deduct colored via (merge-with - pool colored-cost), then deduct allocation via (merge-with - pool-after-colored allocation)
   - [ ] Handle empty cost as no-op (return db unchanged, matching pay-mana pattern)
   - [ ] Handle empty allocation (zero generic) — just deduct colored costs
   - [ ] src/test/fizzle/engine/mana_test.cljs — Add all test cases above in a new '=== pay-mana-with-allocation tests ===' section

## Success Criteria
- [ ] pay-mana-with-allocation deducts colored costs from specific color pools (e.g., {:black 1} cost removes 1 from :black)
- [ ] pay-mana-with-allocation applies allocation map to deduct generic (e.g., allocation {:black 2} removes 2 from :black)
- [ ] Untouched colors remain unchanged (pool {:red 1} stays {:red 1} when red not in cost or allocation)
- [ ] Colorless mana in pool can be allocated toward generic ({:colorless 2} in pool, {:colorless 2} in allocation works)
- [ ] Empty cost with empty allocation is a no-op
- [ ] Existing pay-mana function unchanged: grep for pay-mana calls shows no modifications needed
- [ ] All 10+ new tests pass
- [ ] All existing 25+ mana tests pass unchanged
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO modifying existing pay-mana function (preserve greedy behavior for internal use)
- ❌ NO validation inside pay-mana-with-allocation (matches pay-mana contract: caller MUST check can-pay? first)
- ❌ NO :x handling inside pay-mana-with-allocation (caller resolves X before calling — separation of concerns)
- ❌ NO atoms or mutable state (pure function: db in, db out)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Colorless mana in pool:**
- The pool has a :colorless key (from cards that add colorless mana or Eldrazi lands)
- Colorless mana in the pool CAN be used to pay generic costs via allocation
- Allocation map {:colorless 2} means 'use 2 colorless mana from pool for generic'
- This is distinct from :colorless in cost (which means generic requirement)

**Allocation map validity (caller responsibility):**
- Sum of allocation values must equal (:colorless cost 0)
- Each color in allocation must not exceed remaining pool after colored deduction
- These are enforced by the selection system (auto-confirm only when remaining=0, only clickable colors shown)
- Engine function trusts caller, matching pay-mana's contract pattern

**Shared code with pay-mana:**
- Both functions share: resolve empty cost, separate colored/generic, deduct colored via merge-with
- Do NOT extract a shared helper yet — wait to see if the pattern stabilizes across the full feature
- Duplication of 3-4 lines is acceptable; premature abstraction is not

**Reference implementation:**
- Follow pay-mana at mana.cljs:75-114 as the structural template
- Same let-binding pattern: player-eid, current-pool, colored-cost, pool-after-colored
- Different only in the generic deduction step: allocation map instead of greedy loop
close_reason: Closed

title: Add turn structure with phase advancement
design:
## Goal

Implement turn structure and phase advancement so players can take multiple turns. This is foundational for the 'Player can take multiple turns' success criterion.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-c69 (draw effect) and fizzle-yxj (lose/gain-life effects). The engine can now draw cards and track life. Next we need turn progression so players can:
- Advance through phases (untap, upkeep, draw, main1, combat, main2, end)
- Start new turns (which resets storm count, untaps permanents)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code:
- src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs:54-57 - :game/turn, :game/phase, :game/step already exist
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs - existing re-frame event patterns (see ::init-game, ::cast-spell)
- src/main/fizzle/engine/mana.cljs - has empty-pool function to clear mana

### 2. Write tests first (TDD):

**Test file:** src/test/fizzle/events/turn_test.cljs (new file)

| Test Name | Bug It Catches |
|-----------|----------------|
| test-advance-phase-from-main1-to-combat | Basic phase advancement wrong |
| test-advance-phase-from-combat-to-main2 | Phase sequence ordering wrong |
| test-advance-phase-from-cleanup-stays-cleanup | Auto-advancing to next turn (should require explicit start-turn) |
| test-advance-phase-clears-mana-pool | Mana persisting across phases |
| test-start-turn-increments-turn-number | Turn counter not incrementing |
| test-start-turn-resets-storm-count | Storm count carrying over between turns |
| test-start-turn-resets-land-plays-to-one | Land plays not resetting to 1 |
| test-start-turn-sets-phase-to-untap | Turn not starting at untap phase |
| test-start-turn-clears-mana-pool | Mana persisting from previous turn |

### 3. Implementation checklist:
- [ ] Define phase sequence constant in events/game.cljs:
  ```clojure
  (def phases [:untap :upkeep :draw :main1 :combat :main2 :end :cleanup])
  ```
- [ ] Add helper function to get next phase:
  ```clojure
  (defn next-phase [current-phase]
    (let [idx (.indexOf phases current-phase)]
      (if (= idx (dec (count phases)))
        current-phase  ;; Stay at cleanup, don't auto-advance
        (nth phases (inc idx)))))
  ```
- [ ] Add ::advance-phase event:
  - Get current phase from :game/phase
  - Calculate next phase using next-phase helper
  - Clear mana pool using (mana/empty-pool db player-id)
  - Update :game/phase in datascript
- [ ] Add ::start-turn event:
  - Increment :game/turn
  - Set :game/phase to :untap
  - Reset :player/storm-count to 0 for active player
  - Reset :player/land-plays-left to 1 for active player
  - Clear mana pool using (mana/empty-pool db player-id)
- [ ] Add subscriptions in subs/game.cljs:
  ```clojure
  (rf/reg-sub ::current-phase
    (fn [db] (some-> (:game/db db) queries/get-game-state :game/phase)))
  
  (rf/reg-sub ::current-turn
    (fn [db] (some-> (:game/db db) queries/get-game-state :game/turn)))
  ```

### 4. Reference patterns:
- Follow ::cast-spell pattern for event structure (events/game.cljs:86-95)
- Use d/db-with for datascript updates (see effects.cljs:68-69)
- Get game entity ID pattern (events/game.cljs:70-74)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] test-advance-phase-from-main1-to-combat passes: phase main1 → combat
- [ ] test-advance-phase-from-combat-to-main2 passes: phase combat → main2
- [ ] test-advance-phase-from-cleanup-stays-cleanup passes: cleanup → cleanup (NOT untap)
- [ ] test-advance-phase-clears-mana-pool passes: mana pool all zeros after advance
- [ ] test-start-turn-increments-turn-number passes: turn 1 → turn 2
- [ ] test-start-turn-resets-storm-count passes: storm count → 0
- [ ] test-start-turn-resets-land-plays-to-one passes: land-plays-left → 1
- [ ] test-start-turn-sets-phase-to-untap passes: phase → :untap
- [ ] test-start-turn-clears-mana-pool passes: mana pool all zeros
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (make test)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing (make validate)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Cleanup Phase Boundary**
- Advancing from :cleanup MUST NOT auto-start next turn
- User must explicitly call ::start-turn to begin new turn
- Why: Allows UI to prompt "End turn?" or handle end-of-turn effects
- Test: test-advance-phase-from-cleanup-stays-cleanup verifies this

**Edge Case: Mana Pool Clearing**
- MUST clear mana pool at EVERY phase transition (not just end of turn)
- Per modern MTG rules, mana empties at each phase/step transition
- Use existing mana/empty-pool function (check it exists, may need to add)

**Edge Case: Land Plays Reset**
- Default land plays per turn is 1 (not 0, not unlimited)
- MUST reset to 1 at turn start, not at end of previous turn
- Why: Effects like "play an additional land" need to know base value

**Edge Case: Storm Count Scope**
- Storm count ONLY resets at turn start
- Does NOT reset at phase transitions
- Why: Storm triggers care about spells THIS TURN across all phases

**Reference Implementation**
- Study mana/empty-pool or mana/add-mana for pool manipulation pattern
- Study ::cast-spell event for re-frame event structure

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO infinite mana pools (mana empties at phase transitions)
- ❌ NO skipping untap step (turns MUST start at untap)
- ❌ NO auto-advancing from cleanup to next turn (must be explicit)
- ❌ NO hardcoded player-id :player-1 in events (use db to get active player)
- ❌ NO resetting storm count at phase transitions (only at turn start)
close_reason: Closed

Add styling variation based on card properties: color identity (black/blue/white border or background tint), card type (instant/sorcery/land/artifact with icons or distinct shapes). Helps with at-a-glance board reading during fast combo practice. title: Feature: Add Cephalid Coliseum card
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Card has two abilities as per oracle text
- Mana ability: {T} produces {U} and deals 1 damage to controller as part of ability resolution
- Threshold ability: {U}, {T}, Sacrifice - target player draws 3, then discards 3
- Threshold condition: 7+ cards in graveyard (use existing threshold check)
- Player targeting: user selects self or opponent for draw/discard effect
- Discard selection: user chooses which 3 cards to discard from hand

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Card definition follows existing patterns in cards/iggy_pop.cljs
- [ ] Mana ability produces {U} and deals 1 damage to self
- [ ] Threshold ability only activatable when 7+ cards in graveyard
- [ ] Threshold ability costs {U}, tap, and sacrifice
- [ ] Player targeting modal appears for draw effect
- [ ] Card selection modal appears for discard (player chooses 3)
- [ ] Card registered in all-cards vector
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO trigger for damage (damage is part of mana ability via :ability/effects, not a :becomes-tapped trigger like City of Brass)
- ❌ NO auto-selecting discard targets (must use :effect/selection :player for user choice)
- ❌ NO skipping player target selection (must use :effect/target :any-player)
- ❌ NO hardcoding threshold check (must use existing :condition/type :threshold)

## Approach
Define Cephalid Coliseum as a land with two abilities following existing codebase patterns. The mana ability uses :ability/effects to deal damage as part of resolution. The threshold ability uses :ability/condition to gate activation, with effects that leverage existing player targeting and card selection systems.

## Architecture
- src/fizzle/cards/cephalid_coliseum.cljs - New card definition file
- src/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Register in all-cards vector
- src/test/fizzle/cards/cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs - Test coverage

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Cephalid Coliseum is a key card in Iggy Pop storm for graveyard filling and card selection. Currently missing from the card pool.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:58-78 - City of Brass pattern (trigger-based damage, NOT what we want)
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:171-182 - Lotus Petal pattern (mana ability with sacrifice)
- cards/deep_analysis.cljs - Player targeting with :effect/target :any-player
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:204-218 - Careful Study pattern (draw then discard with selection)
- engine/conditions.cljs - Threshold check implementation

**External:**
- Scryfall oracle: {T}: Add {U}. Deals 1 damage. Threshold: {U}, {T}, Sacrifice: Target player draws 3, discards 3.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Two Separate Abilities with :ability/effects for damage ✓

**What it is:** Mana ability produces {U} with :ability/effects dealing damage. Separate activated ability for threshold effect using existing player targeting and card selection.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed mana abilities - can have :ability/effects for side effects
- Confirmed threshold conditions work on abilities
- Player targeting and discard selection already implemented

**Pros:**
- Matches oracle text exactly (damage part of mana ability)
- Leverages all existing systems
- Clean separation of concerns

**Cons:**
- None significant

**Chosen because:** Accurately implements oracle text using existing patterns

#### 2. City of Brass trigger pattern for damage ❌

**What it is:** Use :trigger/type :becomes-tapped like City of Brass to deal damage whenever land taps.

**Why we looked at this:** City of Brass is another painland, seemed similar

**Investigation:**
- Compared oracle texts - City of Brass triggers on ANY tap, Coliseum damage is part of mana ability only

**Pros:**
- Existing pattern in codebase

**Cons:**
- Incorrect rules implementation - trigger would fire even if tapped by other effects

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Oracle text specifies damage as part of mana ability, not a trigger

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Oracle text changes

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Mana ability with damage
- Threshold activated ability
- Player targeting for draw
- Card selection for discard

**Out of scope:**
- Opponent AI using the ability (goldfish mode sufficient)

### Open Questions
- None - design is straightforward using existing patterns

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Target player for threshold ability? | Choice: self or opponent | Use :effect/target :any-player |
| Discard selection mode? | Player chooses | Use :effect/selection :player |
| Damage implementation? | Part of mana ability, not trigger | Use :ability/effects, not trigger |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Damage Implementation Pattern
**Question explored:** How to implement 'deals 1 damage to you' on mana ability?
**Sources consulted:**
- City of Brass (trigger pattern - rejected)
- Mana ability :ability/effects field

**Findings:**
- City of Brass uses trigger because it fires on ANY tap
- Coliseum damage is specifically part of mana ability text
- :ability/effects allows side effects during ability resolution

**Conclusion:** Use :ability/effects for damage, not trigger

### Dead-End Paths

#### City of Brass Trigger Pattern
**Why explored:** Both are painlands, seemed like same pattern
**Investigation:** Compared oracle texts carefully
**Why abandoned:** Different rules meaning - trigger vs ability effect

### Open Concerns Raised
- None
close_reason: All success criteria met - Cephalid Coliseum card implemented with full test coverage

title: Extract shared game state invariants module
description: Extract the shared invariant parts of player/game entity construction from events/init.cljs into a shared module. Refactor all three consumers (init, test_helpers, restorer) to use it. First task of the ADR-017 epic.
design:
## Goal

Create a unified function that constructs a complete, valid player (entity + turn-based triggers) in one call. All four consumers call it instead of independently calling create-player-tx + create-turn-based-triggers-tx.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study the four consumers

- **events/init.cljs:117-144** — production: create-player-tx + create-turn-based-triggers-tx for both players + create-game-entity-tx
- **test_helpers.cljs:create-test-db (line 28)** — tests: create-player-tx + create-turn-based-triggers-tx + create-game-entity-tx
- **test_helpers.cljs:add-opponent (line 183)** — tests: create-player-tx + create-turn-based-triggers-tx (no game entity — already exists)
- **sharing/restorer.cljs:54,223** — snapshot: create-player-tx + create-turn-based-triggers-tx (game entity reconstructed separately)
- **db/init.cljs:16** — legacy goldfish init: create-player-tx only, NO triggers, hardcoded game entity. Fourth consumer, most divergent.

### 2. Identify invariants vs policy

**Invariants (shared function provides):**
- Player entity with required attributes (life, mana-pool, storm-count, land-plays-left)
- Turn-based trigger registration (draw-step + untap-step)
- Player-id comes from game-state constants

**Policy (consumers provide via overrides):**
- :player/name, :player/mana-pool, :player/life (test customization)
- :player/bot-archetype, :player/is-opponent (opponent setup)
- :player/stops, :player/opponent-stops (UI preferences)
- Deck contents (not part of player entity)

### 3. Create unified function

**Location**: db/game_state.cljs (already has create-player-tx and player-id constants)

**New function**: `create-complete-player`
```clojure
(defn create-complete-player
  "Create a complete player: entity + turn-based triggers.
   Transacts both into conn. Returns player-eid.
   overrides: map merged into player defaults (life, mana, name, etc.)"
  [conn player-id overrides]
  (d/transact! conn (create-player-tx player-id overrides))
  (let [player-eid (get-player-eid @conn player-id)]
    (d/transact! conn (turn-based/create-turn-based-triggers-tx player-eid player-id))
    player-eid))
```

**Note**: This function takes a conn (mutable), transacts, and returns the player eid. This matches how all four consumers currently work — they all transact into a conn.

### 4. Refactor four consumers

- **events/init.cljs**: Replace 4 lines (2x create-player-tx + 2x create-turn-based-triggers-tx) with 2x create-complete-player
- **test_helpers.cljs:create-test-db**: Replace create-player-tx + triggers with create-complete-player
- **test_helpers.cljs:add-opponent**: Replace create-player-tx + triggers with create-complete-player
- **sharing/restorer.cljs**: Replace create-player-tx + triggers with create-complete-player
- **db/init.cljs**: Replace manual create-player-tx + hardcoded game entity with create-complete-player + create-game-entity-tx. This fixes the most divergent consumer.

### 5. Verify: grep for independent trigger registration

```bash
grep -r 'create-turn-based-triggers-tx' src/main/ src/test/test_helpers.cljs
```
Should return zero matches (only the definition in engine/turn_based.cljs and direct test files that test the trigger system itself).

### 6. Verify: make validate

## Success Criteria

- [ ] create-complete-player function exists in db/game_state.cljs
- [ ] events/init.cljs uses create-complete-player (not independent tx + triggers)
- [ ] test_helpers.cljs:create-test-db uses create-complete-player
- [ ] test_helpers.cljs:add-opponent uses create-complete-player
- [ ] sharing/restorer.cljs uses create-complete-player
- [ ] db/init.cljs uses create-complete-player + create-game-entity-tx (no more hardcoded game entity)
- [ ] grep -r 'create-turn-based-triggers-tx' src/main/ src/test/test_helpers.cljs returns zero matches (only engine/turn_based.cljs definition and trigger-specific test files)
- [ ] make validate passes (3055+ tests, 0 failures)
- [ ] No production behavior changes — game starts identically

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**db/game_state.cljs needs turn-based import**: The shared module must import engine/turn_based for trigger registration. Currently db/game_state.cljs only imports datascript. This adds a dependency: db -> engine. Check that this doesn't create a circular import (engine -> db exists, so db -> engine would be circular).

**Circular import risk mitigation**: If db/game_state.cljs can't import engine/turn_based (circular), create the unified function in a NEW module (e.g., db/player_init.cljs) that imports both db/game_state and engine/turn_based. The consumers import from the new module.

**add-opponent creates second player on existing conn**: create-complete-player must work when called on a conn that already has one player entity and a game entity. Verify the second transact doesn't conflict with existing entities.

**Restorer has snapshot-specific needs**: sharing/restorer.cljs reconstructs players from snapshot data (object zones, counters, etc.). create-complete-player provides the base entity + triggers. The restorer adds snapshot-specific data on top. Verify the override mechanism supports this.

**db/init.cljs is the most divergent**: It doesn't register triggers and hardcodes the game entity. After refactoring, it should be the simplest consumer — just call create-complete-player + create-game-entity-tx.

## Tests for the shared module

- **test: create-complete-player registers draw-step trigger** — catches the missing-trigger bug (what broke tests when director cascaded through draw phase)
- **test: create-complete-player registers untap-step trigger** — catches missing untap
- **test: create-complete-player with overrides merges correctly** — catches override clobbering defaults
- **test: create-complete-player called twice (two players) on same conn** — catches entity collision
- **test: player-id comes from game-state constants** — catches the :opponent vs :player-2 divergence class

## Anti-patterns

- ❌ NO changing what production init produces (pure extraction, not modification)
- ❌ NO breaking existing narrow test patterns
- ❌ NO adding library cards as part of invariants (that's policy — scenario helper handles it later)
- ❌ NO independent turn-based trigger registration outside the shared function (consumers must not call create-turn-based-triggers-tx separately)
- ❌ NO circular imports between db/ and engine/ (use intermediate module if needed)
close_reason: Complete — commit d42b200. create-complete-player unifies player construction across all 4 consumers.

title: Bug: Storm copies go to graveyard instead of ceasing to exist
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 1: Write comprehensive tests for db/queries.cljs
design:
## Goal
Create queries_test.cljs covering all 19 public query functions in db/queries.cljs with positive, negative, and edge cases.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Pre-existing Coverage
schema_test.cljs (lines 47-71) already tests 3 query functions as edge cases:
- get-mana-pool nil for nonexistent player
- get-hand empty returns []
- get-card by object-id
DO NOT duplicate these. The new queries_test.cljs should test DIFFERENT scenarios for these functions (e.g., get-mana-pool with valid player, get-hand with multiple cards). Reference schema_test.cljs for shared context.

## Implementation

1. Study existing test patterns
   - src/test/fizzle/cards/led_test.cljs:14-35 - create-test-db pattern with all-cards
   - src/test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs:1-11 - imports and db/init usage
   - src/test/fizzle/db/schema_test.cljs:47-71 - existing query edge case tests (DO NOT duplicate)
   - Naming convention: function-name-scenario-test (e.g., get-hand-returns-objects-with-card-data-test)

2. Test DB setup helpers (define in queries_test.cljs)

   Basic setup - use init-game-state for tests needing just player + hand:
   \`\`\`clojure
   (let [db (init-game-state)]
     ;; Has: player-1 with 20 life, empty mana pool, 0 storm, 1 Dark Ritual in hand
     ;; Has: game state at turn 1, main1 phase
     ...)
   \`\`\`

   Trigger setup - transact trigger entities directly:
   \`\`\`clojure
   (let [db (init-game-state)
         hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
         source-id (:object/id (first hand))
         trigger-id (random-uuid)
         conn (d/conn-from-db db)]
     (d/transact! conn [{:trigger/id trigger-id
                         :trigger/type :storm
                         :trigger/source source-id
                         :trigger/controller :player-1
                         :trigger/stack-order 0
                         :trigger/description "Storm copy"}])
     @conn)
   \`\`\`

   Library with positions - add objects with :object/position:
   \`\`\`clojure
   (let [db (create-test-db) ;; LED test helper loads all-cards
         conn (d/conn-from-db db)
         player-eid (q/get-player-eid db :player-1)]
     (doseq [[idx card-id] (map-indexed vector [:dark-ritual :dark-ritual :brain-freeze])]
       (let [card-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] @conn card-id)]
         (d/transact! conn [{:object/id (random-uuid)
                             :object/card card-eid
                             :object/zone :library
                             :object/owner player-eid
                             :object/controller player-eid
                             :object/position idx
                             :object/tapped false}])))
     @conn)
   \`\`\`

   Opponent setup - add second player with :player/is-opponent:
   \`\`\`clojure
   (let [db (init-game-state)
         conn (d/conn-from-db db)]
     (d/transact! conn [{:player/id :opponent
                         :player/name "Opponent"
                         :player/life 20
                         :player/is-opponent true
                         :player/mana-pool {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
                         :player/storm-count 0}])
     @conn)
   \`\`\`

3. Tests for all 19 functions (organized by group):

   **Player queries:**
   - get-player-eid: valid player-id returns integer eid; :nonexistent returns nil
   - get-mana-pool: valid player returns pool map (schema_test covers nil case)
   - get-storm-count: returns 0 initially, returns updated value after transact
   - get-life-total: returns 20 initially; nil for nonexistent player
   - get-max-hand-size: defaults to 7 when attribute not set; returns custom value when set
   - get-opponent-id: returns :opponent when opponent exists; nil when no opponent

   **Object queries:**
   - get-hand: returns vector of objects with :object/card pulled as map (not ref); (schema_test covers empty and nil)
   - get-card: returns card definition map with :card/name etc.; nil for nonexistent object-id
   - get-object: returns full object with card pulled in; nil for random-uuid (nonexistent)
   - get-objects-in-zone: returns vec for :hand, :graveyard, :battlefield; empty vec for zone with no objects; nil for nonexistent player

   **Game state queries:**
   - get-game-state: returns map with :game/turn, :game/phase; :game/active-player is PULLED (a map with :player/id and :player/name, not an integer ref)

   **Stack queries:**
   - get-trigger: returns trigger map by id; nil for nonexistent trigger-id
   - get-next-stack-order: returns 0 for empty stack; returns max(trigger-orders, object-positions)+1 when BOTH triggers and objects exist on stack
   - get-stack-items: returns triggers sorted by stack-order descending (LIFO); empty vec for empty stack; does NOT include spell objects (only triggers)
   - stack-empty?: true when no triggers AND no spells on stack; false when only triggers; false when only spells; false when both

   **Library queries:**
   - get-top-n-library: returns top N object-ids sorted by position ascending; returns all available when n > library size; returns [] when n=0; nil for nonexistent player

   **Trigger queries:**
   - get-trigger-source-card-name: returns card name string from trigger's source object; nil when trigger has no :trigger/source; nil when source object doesn't exist (removed)

   **Criteria queries:**
   - query-zone-by-criteria: types uses AND (must have ALL); colors uses OR (any match); subtypes uses OR; empty criteria {} matches all objects in zone; criteria matching nothing returns []; nil for nonexistent player
   - query-library-by-criteria: correctly delegates to query-zone-by-criteria with :library zone

4. Test setup approach
   - Use db/init/init-game-state for basic tests
   - Use led_test.cljs create-test-db pattern (loads all-cards) for tests needing multiple card types
   - Use d/conn-from-db + d/transact! for additional state
   - Pure Datascript - no re-frame

## Success Criteria
- [ ] src/test/fizzle/db/queries_test.cljs exists with 35+ test assertions
- [ ] All 19 public functions have at least one positive test AND at least one edge case test
- [ ] No duplication with existing schema_test.cljs tests (lines 47-71)
- [ ] Trigger setup tests verify get-trigger, get-stack-items, get-next-stack-order, stack-empty?
- [ ] get-next-stack-order tested with BOTH triggers and objects on stack simultaneously
- [ ] stack-empty? tested with 4 scenarios: empty/triggers-only/spells-only/both
- [ ] query-zone-by-criteria tested with AND logic (types), OR logic (colors, subtypes), empty criteria, and no-match criteria
- [ ] get-top-n-library tested with n=0, n < library size, n > library size
- [ ] get-game-state asserts :game/active-player is a pulled map (not integer ref)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: get-top-n-library with n=0**
- Docstring says returns [] for fewer than N cards
- n=0 should return [] (take 0 of anything)
- Verify this works, don't assume

**Edge Case: get-next-stack-order with mixed stack**
- Must check BOTH trigger stack-orders AND object positions
- Test: trigger at order 2, object at position 5 → should return 6
- This catches the bug where only triggers OR only objects are checked

**Edge Case: get-trigger-source-card-name with removed source**
- Trigger can reference source object-id that no longer exists (removed via remove-object)
- Should return nil gracefully, not throw

**Edge Case: query-zone-by-criteria with empty criteria {}**
- Empty criteria map should match ALL objects in zone (no filters = everything passes)
- Catches bug where empty set check fails

**Existing Test Overlap**
- schema_test.cljs already covers: mana-pool nil, hand empty, get-card by id
- DO NOT write tests for these exact same scenarios
- Write complementary tests (valid cases, different edge cases)

## Anti-patterns
- NO mocking Datascript (pure function tests against real DB)
- NO test-only methods in production code
- NO duplicating tests already in schema_test.cljs
- NO testing private functions directly (test matches-criteria? through query-zone-by-criteria)
- NO using (is (some? result)) as sole assertion - always assert specific values
close_reason: All 19 query functions tested with 51 tests/90 assertions. make validate passes (792 tests, 1677 assertions).

title: Test Quality Improvement: Fizzle Game Engine
design:
## Goal
Improve test effectiveness by removing tautological tests, strengthening weak tests, and adding missing corner case coverage.

## Analysis Summary
- Total tests analyzed: 591
- GREEN (keep): 487 (82%)
- YELLOW (strengthen): 89 (15%)
- RED (remove/replace): 15 (3%)
- Missing corner cases: 47

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 15 RED tests removed or replaced with meaningful tests
- [ ] All YELLOW tests with weak assertions strengthened
- [ ] P0 missing corner cases covered (engine/effects, engine/mana, engine/rules)
- [ ] No new tautological tests introduced

## Scope
Modules analyzed:
- engine/ (287 tests) - Core game logic
- cards/ (185 tests) - Card implementations
- events/ (107 tests) - Re-frame event handlers
- db/ (8 tests) - Schema

## Anti-patterns to Avoid
- Adding tests that only check `!= nil` or `some?`
- Adding tests that verify mock/helper behavior instead of production code
- Adding happy-path-only tests
- Keeping tautological tests "for coverage"
- Testing implementation details rather than behavior
close_reason: All success criteria met: RED tests removed/replaced, YELLOW tests strengthened, P0 corner cases covered, no tautological tests added. Test suite: 602 tests, 1321 assertions passing.

title: Data-Driven Ability System
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- Card abilities defined as EDN data in :card/abilities field
- Card triggers defined as EDN data in :card/triggers field
- Card ETB effects defined as EDN data in :card/etb-effects field
- Cost payment uses multimethod dispatch on cost type (extensible)
- Ability activation uses generic engine, no card-specific checks in events
- State-based actions use registry pattern (extensible)
- City of Brass uses :card/triggers with :becomes-tapped trigger type
- Gemstone Mine uses :card/abilities with :remove-counter cost and :card/etb-effects
- :deal-damage effect type distinct from :lose-life (damage vs life loss)
- :add-counters effect type for ETB counter initialization
- No card IDs checked in engine code (data over code principle)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] City of Brass defined with :card/triggers and :card/abilities (no hardcoded logic)
- [ ] Gemstone Mine defined with :card/abilities and :card/etb-effects (no hardcoded logic)
- [ ] engine/costs.cljs exists with multimethod for :tap and :remove-counter
- [ ] engine/abilities.cljs exists with generic mana ability activation
- [ ] engine/state_based.cljs exists with registry pattern
- [ ] :becomes-tapped trigger type implemented in engine/triggers.cljs
- [ ] :deal-damage and :add-counters effect types in engine/effects.cljs
- [ ] events/game.cljs activate-mana-ability has NO card ID checks
- [ ] Gemstone Mine sacrifices when counters reach 0 (via state-based action)
- [ ] City of Brass deals damage when tapped (via trigger, not ability effect)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
- [ ] New tests cover ability/trigger/cost/SBA systems
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO card ID checks in engine code (violates data-over-code: engine interprets data generically)
- ❌ NO hardcoded cost handling in cond-> blocks (use multimethod dispatch for extensibility)
- ❌ NO hardcoded SBA checks in single function (use registry for extensibility)
- ❌ NO conflating damage with life loss (MTG rules: damage can be prevented, life loss cannot)
- ❌ NO ability effects for tap triggers (City of Brass damage is trigger, not mana ability effect)
- ❌ NO modifying existing methods to add new cost types (add new multimethod instead)

## Approach

Extend the existing multimethod pattern (used for effects and triggers) to abilities and costs. Create three new engine modules: costs.cljs for cost payment dispatch, abilities.cljs for generic ability activation, state_based.cljs for SBA registry. Update card definitions to use pure data, refactor events/game.cljs to read card data and delegate to engine.

## Architecture

**New Modules:**
- engine/costs.cljs - Multimethod dispatch on cost type (:tap, :remove-counter)
- engine/abilities.cljs - Generic ability activation, can-activate?, pay-all-costs
- engine/state_based.cljs - Registry of SBA check functions

**Updated Modules:**
- engine/effects.cljs - Add :deal-damage, :add-counters effect types
- engine/triggers.cljs - Add :becomes-tapped trigger type
- db/schema.cljs - Add :card/etb-effects, :card/triggers fields
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Update City of Brass, Gemstone Mine definitions
- events/game.cljs - Refactor activate-mana-ability to use generic engine

**Data Flow:**
1. Player activates mana ability on land
2. events/game.cljs reads :card/abilities from card data
3. engine/abilities.cljs checks can-activate? using engine/costs.cljs
4. engine/costs.cljs pays each cost via multimethod dispatch
5. Mana added to pool
6. engine/triggers.cljs fires :becomes-tapped triggers
7. engine/state_based.cljs checks all registered SBAs

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Mana abilities currently hardcode card-specific logic in events/game.cljs (line 382: checking card-id for City of Brass). This violates the 'data over code' principle. Every new land requires code changes instead of just data.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- engine/effects.cljs:13-154 - Multimethod pattern for effects, works well
- engine/triggers.cljs:53-126 - Same pattern for triggers
- db/schema.cljs:25 - :card/abilities field exists but unused
- events/game.cljs:382 - Hardcoded City of Brass check (architecture violation)

**MTG Rules:**
- Damage vs life loss are distinct (damage preventable, life loss not)
- City of Brass trigger fires on ANY tap, not just mana ability
- State-based actions checked after any game action

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Multimethod Pattern for Costs and Abilities ✓

**What it is:** Apply same multimethod dispatch pattern used for effects/triggers to costs and abilities. Registry pattern for state-based actions.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed engine/effects.cljs - multimethod pattern extensible, tested
- Pattern allows adding new types without modifying existing code

**Pros:**
- Consistent with existing architecture
- Extensible without code modification
- Each cost/SBA type independently testable

**Cons:**
- More files/indirection than hardcoded approach

**Chosen because:** Matches existing patterns, truly extensible, testable in isolation.

#### 2. Conditional Cost Handling ❌

**What it is:** Use cond-> in pay-costs function to handle each cost type.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler initial implementation.

**Investigation:**
- Would require modifying pay-costs for every new cost type
- Same problem as current hardcoded approach

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Not extensible - adding :pay-life, :sacrifice, :discard would require modifying existing code.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We decide extensibility doesn't matter (it does for MTG).

#### 3. Keep Mana Abilities Special ❌

**What it is:** Only data-drive non-mana abilities, keep mana abilities as event handlers.

**Why we looked at this:** Less refactoring effort.

**Investigation:**
- Every new land still requires code changes
- Violates stated architecture principle

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Violates 'data over code' principle explicitly stated in CLAUDE.md.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We abandon the data-over-code architecture entirely.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Mana abilities with :tap and :remove-counter costs
- :becomes-tapped trigger type
- ETB effects for counter initialization
- State-based action for 0-counter sacrifice
- City of Brass and Gemstone Mine refactored

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Non-mana activated abilities (no cards need them yet)
- Other trigger types (no cards need them yet)
- Other cost types beyond :tap/:remove-counter (no cards need them yet)
- Damage prevention mechanics (Phase 2+)

### Open Questions
- Should triggers use the stack? (default: no for mana ability triggers, matches MTG)
- SBA check frequency? (default: after every game action that modifies state)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Tap cost explicit or implicit? | Explicit in ability data | {:ability/cost {:tap true}} |
| Mana color choice | Player chooses at activation | {:ability/mana {:any 1}}, color passed as param |
| ETB effects location | Separate :card/etb-effects field | Not part of :card/abilities |
| Counter costs | Generic cost system | {:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}} |
| Sacrifice timing | State-based action | Engine checks after resolution, not ability-specific |
| City of Brass damage | Triggered ability, not mana ability effect | Separate :card/triggers field |
| Damage vs life loss | Distinct effect types | :deal-damage preventable, :lose-life not |
| Extensibility pattern | Multimethods for costs, registry for SBAs | No cond-> blocks that need modification |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Effect System Pattern
**Question explored:** How does current effect system work?
**Sources consulted:**
- engine/effects.cljs:13-154 - multimethod implementation
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - effect data structures

**Findings:**
- Multimethod dispatches on :effect/type
- Pure functions: (db, player-id, effect) -> db
- Unknown types return db unchanged (graceful degradation)

**Conclusion:** Apply same pattern to costs and abilities

### Dead-End Paths

#### Ability Effects for Tap Triggers
**Why explored:** Initially modeled City of Brass damage as part of mana ability
**Investigation:** User pointed out card text says 'whenever becomes tapped' - separate trigger

**Why abandoned:** Incorrect MTG rules modeling - damage should fire on ANY tap, not just mana ability

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Feels like a smell to keep adding to methods' → Multimethod pattern solves this
- 'Damage vs life loss distinction' → Added :deal-damage as separate effect type
close_reason: All success criteria met. 207 tests pass. Implementation verified via code review and automated tests. Manual UI validation deferred (UI doesn't support phases/turns yet).

title: ::yield-all + ::cast-and-yield update
description: ::yield-all: if stack non-empty, set :game/auto-mode :resolving and dispatch ::yield. If stack empty, set :game/auto-mode :f6 and dispatch ::yield. In ::yield handler check auto-mode: :resolving auto-yields for active player, breaks on stack empty/bot acts/selection needed. :f6 auto-yields for both, breaks on stop hit/turn boundary/selection needed. ::cast-and-yield: cast spell then dispatch ::yield instead of directly resolving.
close_reason: Premature decomposition

title: Add land cards and play-land mechanics
design:
## Goal
Implement land card definitions (City of Brass, Gemstone Mine) and the play-land mechanics so players can play lands from hand to battlefield, tracked by :player/land-plays-left.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Completed:
- fizzle-c69: Draw effect (library → hand) works
- fizzle-dbg: Turn structure with phase advancement works (including land-plays reset in start-turn)
- fizzle-yxj: Lose-life and gain-life effects work

Schema already has:
- :player/land-plays-left (reset to 1 each turn in start-turn)
- :game/phase and :game/step fields
- :object/zone for :battlefield

Existing patterns to follow:
- zones.cljs:move-to-zone for zone transitions
- events/game.cljs:start-turn pattern for pure functions wrapped by re-frame events
- turn_test.cljs pattern for helper functions and test structure
- iggy_pop.cljs card definition format

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Write tests for play-land pure function
File: src/test/fizzle/events/play_land_test.cljs

Create test namespace with helpers similar to turn_test.cljs:
- add-card-to-hand helper (creates object in :hand zone)
- get-object-zone helper (query object's current zone)
- get-land-plays helper (reuse from turn_test.cljs)

Tests to write (follow existing test naming conventions):

```clojure
(deftest test-play-land-moves-to-battlefield
  ;; Bug caught: Zone transition doesn't happen
  ;; Setup: Land in hand, land-plays-left = 1
  ;; Assert: After play-land, object zone is :battlefield)

(deftest test-play-land-decrements-land-plays
  ;; Bug caught: Land plays not tracked
  ;; Setup: land-plays-left = 1
  ;; Assert: After play-land, land-plays-left = 0)

(deftest test-play-land-fails-when-no-plays-left
  ;; Bug caught: Can play unlimited lands
  ;; Setup: land-plays-left = 0
  ;; Assert: Returns unchanged db, card still in hand)

(deftest test-play-land-fails-for-non-land
  ;; Bug caught: Can play any card as land
  ;; Setup: Dark Ritual in hand (type :instant)
  ;; Assert: Returns unchanged db, card still in hand)

(deftest test-play-land-fails-for-card-not-in-hand
  ;; Bug caught: Can play cards from graveyard/library
  ;; Setup: Land in graveyard, not hand
  ;; Assert: Returns unchanged db, card still in graveyard)

(deftest test-play-land-fails-outside-main-phase
  ;; Bug caught: Can play lands during combat
  ;; Setup: Phase is :combat
  ;; Assert: Returns unchanged db, card still in hand)
```

### Step 2: Run tests RED
Command: make test
Expected: All 6 tests fail (play-land function doesn't exist)

### Step 3: Add land card definitions
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

Add cards following existing pattern (no mana abilities yet - just card data):

```clojure
(def city-of-brass
  {:card/id :city-of-brass
   :card/name "City of Brass"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/text "Whenever City of Brass becomes tapped, it deals 1 damage to you. {T}: Add one mana of any color."})

(def gemstone-mine
  {:card/id :gemstone-mine
   :card/name "Gemstone Mine"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/text "Gemstone Mine enters the battlefield with 3 mining counters. {T}, Remove a mining counter: Add one mana of any color. If there are no mining counters on Gemstone Mine, sacrifice it."})

;; Update all-cards vector
(def all-cards
  [dark-ritual brain-freeze cabal-ritual city-of-brass gemstone-mine])
```

### Step 4: Implement play-land pure function
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

Add helper function to check if card is a land:
```clojure
(defn land-card?
  "Check if a card entity has :land in its types."
  [db object-id]
  (let [obj (queries/get-object db object-id)
        card-eid (:object/card obj)
        card (d/pull db '[*] card-eid)]
    (contains? (:card/types card) :land)))
```

Add play-land pure function (following advance-phase pattern):
```clojure
(defn play-land
  "Play a land from hand to battlefield.
   Pure function: (db, player-id, object-id) -> db

   Validation:
   - Player must have land-plays-left > 0
   - Object must be in player's hand
   - Card must be a land type
   - Phase must be :main1 or :main2

   Returns unchanged db if any validation fails."
  [db player-id object-id]
  (let [game-state (queries/get-game-state db)
        phase (:game/phase game-state)
        player-eid (queries/get-player-eid db player-id)
        land-plays (d/q '[:find ?plays .
                          :in $ ?pid
                          :where [?e :player/id ?pid]
                          [?e :player/land-plays-left ?plays]]
                        db player-id)
        obj (queries/get-object db object-id)]
    (if (and (pos? land-plays)
             (= (:object/zone obj) :hand)
             (= (:object/owner obj) player-eid)
             (land-card? db object-id)
             (#{:main1 :main2} phase))
      (-> db
          (zones/move-to-zone object-id :battlefield)
          (d/db-with [[:db/add player-eid :player/land-plays-left (dec land-plays)]]))
      db)))
```

Add re-frame event wrapper:
```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::play-land
  (fn [db [_ object-id]]
    (let [game-db (:game/db db)]
      (assoc db :game/db (play-land game-db :player-1 object-id)))))
```

### Step 5: Run tests GREEN
Command: make test
Expected: All 6 play-land tests pass

### Step 6: Run full validation
Command: make validate
Expected: lint + fmt-check + all tests pass

### Step 7: Commit changes
Command: git add -A && git commit -m "Add land cards and play-land mechanics"

## Success Criteria
- [ ] city-of-brass and gemstone-mine defined in cards/iggy_pop.cljs with :types #{:land}
- [ ] play-land pure function in events/game.cljs moves land from :hand to :battlefield
- [ ] play-land decrements :player/land-plays-left by 1
- [ ] play-land returns unchanged db when land-plays-left = 0 (no land left to play)
- [ ] play-land returns unchanged db for non-land cards (prevents exploit)
- [ ] play-land returns unchanged db for cards not in hand (prevents cheating)
- [ ] play-land returns unchanged db outside :main1/:main2 phases (MTG rule)
- [ ] 6+ unit tests pass with meaningful assertions
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + fmt + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO playing lands from zones other than hand
- ❌ NO allowing land plays when land-plays-left = 0
- ❌ NO silently ignoring errors - return unchanged db for invalid plays
- ❌ NO mocking in tests - use real game state
- ❌ NO implementing mana abilities in this task (separate task)
- ❌ NO hardcoding player-id (:player-1) in pure function - accept as parameter
- ❌ NO tautological tests - each test must catch a specific bug

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Object ID doesn't exist**
- queries/get-object returns nil for non-existent object
- play-land should handle gracefully (check obj is not nil)
- Add test: test-play-land-handles-invalid-object-id

**Edge Case: Player doesn't exist**
- queries/get-player-eid returns nil for invalid player
- play-land should check player-eid is not nil
- Test: test-play-land-handles-invalid-player-id

**Phase Restriction (MTG Rules)**
- Lands can only be played during main phases (not untap, draw, combat, etc.)
- MUST check phase before allowing play-land
- Test: test-play-land-fails-outside-main-phase

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow zones.cljs:move-to-zone for zone transition
- Follow events/game.cljs:start-turn for pure function + re-frame event pattern
- Follow turn_test.cljs for test helper patterns

## Notes
- This task does NOT implement mana abilities (tap to produce mana) - next task
- This task does NOT implement land counters (Gemstone Mine) - part of mana abilities task
- This task does NOT implement untap logic - separate concern
- Lands bypass the stack per MTG rules (hand → battlefield directly)
close_reason: Closed
