title: Feature: Implement Intuition card with multi-select tutor and pile choice
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Intuition card (2U instant) is playable and resolves correctly
- Player must select exactly 3 cards from library (or all cards if fewer than 3 exist)
- After selection, player chooses 1 card for hand, rest go to graveyard
- Random button available during pile choice phase
- Library shuffles after resolution
- Fail-to-find allowed only when library is empty

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Intuition card definition exists with correct mana cost, types, and effect data
- [ ] Multi-select tutor selection works (exact count required based on library size)
- [ ] Pile choice selection works (choose 1 for hand from revealed)
- [ ] Random button randomly selects 1 card for hand
- [ ] Cards distributed correctly: 1 to hand, rest to graveyard
- [ ] Library shuffles after all cards moved
- [ ] Edge cases handled: 0/1/2/3+ cards in library
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing (make validate)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO allowing fewer than 3 selections when library has 3+ cards (rules: Intuition requires exactly 3)
- ❌ NO cast-time opponent targeting (simplification: goldfish mode, player makes all choices)
- ❌ NO shuffling before cards moved (consistency: existing tutor pattern shuffles after)
- ❌ NO new effect type (design: extend existing tutor with parameters)
- ❌ NO skipping pile choice when 1 card selected (UX: still show choice UI for consistency, auto-confirm allowed)

## Approach
Extend the existing tutor effect system with multi-select capability and a new pile-choice selection phase. The tutor effect gains :effect/select-count and :effect/pile-choice parameters. After multi-select tutor confirmation, if pile-choice is present, a second selection phase begins where player picks which card(s) go to hand. A Random button provides quick selection. This approach follows the data-over-code principle and will be reusable for similar cards (Gifts Ungiven, etc).

## Architecture
### Card Definition (intuition.cljs)
- Standard card structure with :tutor effect
- New parameters: :effect/select-count 3, :effect/pile-choice {:hand 1 :graveyard :rest}

### Selection System (events/game.cljs)
- build-tutor-selection: Extended for :selection/select-count and :selection/exact? true
- build-pile-choice-selection: NEW - shows revealed cards, player picks N for hand
- confirm-pile-choice-selection: NEW - handles Random button, distributes cards
- execute-pile-choice: Moves cards to hand/graveyard, triggers shuffle

### Data Flow
1. Cast Intuition → resolve-spell-with-selection
2. Detect tutor with select-count → build-tutor-selection (multi-select mode)
3. Player selects exactly 3 (or fewer if library smaller) → confirm-tutor-selection
4. Detect pile-choice → build-pile-choice-selection
5. Player picks 1 for hand (or Random) → confirm-pile-choice-selection
6. Execute: move to hand, move rest to graveyard, shuffle library

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Intuition is a key Premodern combo card that searches for 3 cards with opponent choosing which goes to hand. Need to implement for practice reps.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- events/game.cljs:911-930 - build-tutor-selection creates single-select state
- events/game.cljs:1385-1428 - execute-tutor-selection moves card and shuffles
- events/game.cljs:951-960 - build-discard-selection pattern for multi-card selection
- cards/deep_analysis.cljs - cast-time targeting pattern (not needed here)

**External:**
- Intuition Oracle: Search for 3, opponent chooses 1 for hand, rest to graveyard, shuffle
- Rules: Must find exactly 3 if 3+ exist in library

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Extend tutor with multi-select + pile choice ✓

**What it is:** Add :effect/select-count and :effect/pile-choice to tutor effects. Two-phase selection: pick cards, then pick distribution.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed build-tutor-selection - straightforward to add select-count
- Reviewed discard selection - similar multi-select pattern exists
- Pile choice is new selection type but follows existing patterns

**Pros:**
- Extends existing pattern minimally
- Reusable for Gifts Ungiven, Fact or Fiction
- Data-driven (card definition, not card-specific code)

**Cons:**
- Two-phase selection adds complexity

**Chosen because:** Follows data-over-code principle, minimal new concepts, reusable

#### 2. New dedicated :intuition effect type ❌

**What it is:** Single effect type handling entire Intuition flow.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler mental model, self-contained

**Investigation:**
- Would require new effect handler in effects.cljs
- Card-specific logic violates data-over-code principle

**Pros:**
- Self-contained

**Cons:**
- Not reusable
- Violates design principles

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Violates data-over-code principle. Tutor extension is more consistent.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We find tutor extension creates unacceptable complexity.

#### 3. Compose separate effects with state passing ❌

**What it is:** Chain of effects: search-reveal, pile-split, shuffle-library

**Why we looked at this:** Maximum composability

**Investigation:**
- Would require new state-passing mechanism between effects
- Significant infrastructure change

**Pros:**
- Maximum composability

**Cons:**
- Major infrastructure change
- Over-engineered for current needs

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Over-engineered. Would require state-passing mechanism that doesn't exist.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We have 5+ cards needing complex effect composition.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Intuition card implementation
- Multi-select tutor extension
- Pile choice selection system
- Random button for pile choice

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Actual opponent AI choosing (deferred - player makes choice in goldfish mode)
- Other pile-choice cards like Gifts Ungiven (future epic)
- Reveal animation/UI (not needed for practice tool)

### Open Questions
- Should pile choice auto-confirm when only 1 card selected? (default: yes, for UX)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Who makes opponent choice? | Player + random button | No opponent targeting, add Random button to pile choice |
| Allow <3 cards if library small? | Yes, select all available | :select-count is max, actual count = min(3, library-size) |
| Must select 3 if 3+ exist? | Yes, mandatory | :exact? true in selection state, no partial selection allowed |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Tutor Selection System
**Question explored:** How do existing tutors work?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/game.cljs:911-930 - build-tutor-selection
- events/game.cljs:1385-1428 - execute-tutor-selection

**Findings:**
- Tutor effect returns db unchanged in effects.cljs
- Selection built at event layer with candidates from library
- Fail-to-find always allowed
- Shuffle happens AFTER card moved

**Conclusion:** Extend existing pattern, don't create new effect type

#### Multi-Select Patterns
**Question explored:** How does multi-select work elsewhere?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/game.cljs:951-960 - build-discard-selection

**Findings:**
- Discard selection uses :selection/count for required selections
- :selection/selected is a set of card IDs
- Confirm button enables when count reached

**Conclusion:** Follow same pattern for multi-select tutor

### Dead-End Paths

#### Cast-Time Opponent Targeting
**Why explored:** Oracle text says 'target opponent'
**Investigation:**
- Reviewed deep_analysis.cljs cast-time targeting
- Considered goldfish mode implications

**Why abandoned:** In goldfish/practice mode, no opponent to target. Player makes all choices. Targeting adds complexity with no benefit.

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'What if only 1 card selected?' → Still show pile choice for consistency, but can auto-confirm
close_reason: All success criteria met. Intuition card fully implemented with multi-select tutor, pile choice UI, and Random button. User confirmed working in manual testing.

title: Modify init-game-state to accept must-contain and return :opening-hand screen
design:
## Goal
Modify game initialization to accept :must-contain config, place sculpted cards in the opening hand, draw remaining slots randomly, and return :opening-hand screen instead of :game. This is the critical flow change: start-game now goes to :opening-hand, not :game.

## Context
Completed fizzle-jxi8: must-contain state management in setup events/subs is done. The must-contain map lives at :setup/must-contain in db. Now we need init-game-state to consume it.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (modify init, update callers, fix existing tests)

## Implementation

1. Study init-game-state in events/game.cljs:
   - events/game.cljs:35-116 — current init-game-state
   - events/game.cljs:25-32 — deck-to-card-ids shuffles and expands
   - It takes {:main-deck [...] :clock-turns 4}
   - Shuffles via deck-to-card-ids, creates library objects with random-uuid, draws 7 via effects/execute-effect :draw
   - Returns {:active-screen :game :game/db db} merged with history/init-history

2. Write tests first (TDD) in events/init_game_test.cljs (extend existing):
   - init-game-state with :must-contain {} — hand has 7 cards, lib has 53, :active-screen :opening-hand
   - init-game-state with :must-contain {:dark-ritual 2} — hand contains exactly 2 dark-rituals + 5 random, lib has 53
   - init-game-state with :must-contain {:dark-ritual 4 :cabal-ritual 3} (7 total) — hand has 4 DR + 3 CR, lib has 53, 0 random draws
   - init-game-state with :must-contain {:orims-chant 1} (single-copy card) — hand has 1 orims-chant + 6 random
   - :opening-hand/mulligan-count initialized to 0
   - :opening-hand/sculpted-ids is set of exactly N UUIDs matching sculpted hand objects
   - :opening-hand/phase initialized to :viewing
   - Sculpted cards are NOT in library (verify by checking library card-ids)
   - Library count = deck-count - 7 (same as before)
   - Must-contain with small custom deck (7 cards, 4 of one + 3 of another, must-contain 2) — works correctly

3. Update existing tests that assert :active-screen :game (they will break):
   - init_game_test.cljs:181-185 — test-init-game-state-includes-active-screen: change :game to :opening-hand
   - setup_test.cljs:232 — test-start-game-with-valid-deck: change :game to :opening-hand
   - setup_test.cljs:245 — test-start-game-with-oversized-deck: change :game to :opening-hand
   - setup_test.cljs:268 — test-quick-start-with-saved-preset: change :game to :opening-hand
   - setup_test.cljs:339 — test-new-game-redeals-same-config: change :game to :opening-hand
   - routing_test.cljs:22 — test-set-active-screen-switches-back-to-game: this test switches TO :game manually, should still pass

4. Algorithm for sculpted hand (modify init-game-state):
   - [ ] Accept :must-contain key in config (default to {})
   - [ ] Calculate sculpted-count = sum of must-contain values
   - [ ] Call deck-to-card-ids as before to get shuffled flat card-id list
   - [ ] Extract sculpted cards: for each {card-id count} in must-contain, take count occurrences of card-id from the shuffled list, removing them from the pool. Use a loop that finds and removes matching card-ids one at a time.
   - [ ] Take (7 - sculpted-count) random cards from remaining pool for random hand slots
   - [ ] Remaining pool cards become library
   - [ ] Create library objects from remaining pool (same as current: map-indexed with random-uuid, :object/zone :library)
   - [ ] Create hand objects from sculpted + random cards (same pattern but :object/zone :hand)
   - [ ] Track sculpted object-ids: the UUIDs assigned to sculpted card objects
   - [ ] Do NOT use effects/execute-effect :draw anymore (we build hand directly)
   - [ ] Return merged map with :active-screen :opening-hand, :opening-hand/mulligan-count 0, :opening-hand/sculpted-ids (set of uuids), :opening-hand/phase :viewing

5. Modify start-game-handler in events/setup.cljs:
   - [ ] Pass :must-contain (:setup/must-contain db) to init-game-state config

6. Modify new-game-handler in events/setup.cljs:
   - [ ] Pass :must-contain (:setup/must-contain config) to init-game-state config

7. Modify core.cljs:
   - [ ] Add :opening-hand case to screen routing (placeholder div for now — actual view is a separate task)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] init-game-state with empty must-contain returns :active-screen :opening-hand (not :game)
- [ ] init-game-state with :must-contain {:dark-ritual 2} places exactly 2 dark-rituals in hand
- [ ] init-game-state with :must-contain totaling 7 places all 7 sculpted cards, 0 random
- [ ] init-game-state with :must-contain {:orims-chant 1} places 1 orims-chant in hand
- [ ] Hand always has exactly 7 cards regardless of must-contain count
- [ ] Library count = deck-count - 7
- [ ] :opening-hand/mulligan-count starts at 0
- [ ] :opening-hand/sculpted-ids is a set of UUIDs matching sculpted hand card objects
- [ ] :opening-hand/phase starts at :viewing
- [ ] Sculpted cards not present in library (verified by card-id check)
- [ ] start-game-handler passes must-contain from db
- [ ] new-game-handler passes must-contain from stashed config
- [ ] core.cljs handles :opening-hand screen routing
- [ ] All 8 existing :active-screen tests updated and passing
- [ ] make validate passing (lint + fmt + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Algorithmic Change: No More effects/execute-effect :draw**
The current init-game-state uses effects/execute-effect :draw to move 7 cards from library to hand. With sculpting, we need to build hand and library directly from the card-id list BEFORE transacting objects. This means:
- Don't transact all cards as library then draw
- Instead, split card-ids into hand-ids + library-ids, then transact each group with the correct :object/zone
- This is a structural change to the init flow, not just adding a parameter

**Edge Case: Must-Contain Exceeds Deck Copies**
If must-contain says {:dark-ritual 4} but deck only has 3 (e.g., after sideboarding), take min(must-contain-count, available-in-deck). The setup toggle handler already validates this, but defensive coding prevents crashes.

**Edge Case: Must-Contain 7 = All Sculpted**
When all 7 slots are sculpted, random-draw count = 0. The (take 0 ...) should return empty — verify this works and doesn't error.

**Edge Case: Must-Contain 0 = Backward Compat**
With empty must-contain, sculpted-count = 0, all 7 cards are random. Should produce same hand/library distribution as before (modulo the draw-effect path change). :opening-hand/sculpted-ids should be #{} (empty set).

**Existing Test Breakage**
8 tests across 3 files assert :active-screen :game. These must be updated to :opening-hand:
- init_game_test.cljs: 1 test
- setup_test.cljs: 4 tests  
- routing_test.cljs: 0 need changes (it manually sets screen, not checking init output)
Additionally, setup_test.cljs tests that create {:active-screen :game} as input fixtures (restore-setup, new-game-without-stash) are fine — they're testing the handler's behavior with arbitrary input, not init-game-state output.

**Object ID Tracking**
:opening-hand/sculpted-ids must contain the actual random-uuid values assigned to the hand objects during transact. This requires generating UUIDs first, creating objects, then recording which UUIDs were sculpted.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO drawing sculpted cards via the normal draw path (must be separated before shuffle)
- NO :active-screen :game from init-game-state (always :opening-hand now)
- NO modifying the opening hand events here (separate namespace, separate task)
- NO transacting all cards as library then drawing (build hand/library zones directly)
- NO mutating deck-to-card-ids function signature (create new helper if needed)
close_reason: Closed

title: Make interactive effects declare themselves explicitly
description:
## The Problem

Several effects — `:discard`, `:tutor`, `:scry`, `:peek-and-select` — return `db` unchanged in the engine. The *real* work happens in the event layer, which detects these effect types and sets up UI selection state. This is a protocol that exists in comments and conventions, not in code.

### How it works today

1. Engine's `execute-effect-impl` for `:tutor` (effects.cljs:498-500): literally just `db`
2. `resolve-spell-with-selection` in selection.cljs scans effects *before* executing them using `has-selection-effect?` (line 78) and `find-selection-effect-index` (line 90)
3. These helpers check for specific effect types: `:discard`, `:tutor`, `:scry`, `:peek-and-select`, `:return-from-graveyard`, plus `:effect/selection :player`
4. If found, the event layer pauses execution, builds selection state, and waits for UI confirmation

### What's wrong

This is two parallel dispatch systems that must stay in sync:
- The engine dispatches on `:effect/type` via multimethods
- The event layer dispatches on the same `:effect/type` via `cond`/`case` in helper functions

If you add a new interactive effect type, you must update both. There's no compiler enforcement. The contract is implicit.

### The Rich Hickey question

"Can we make the protocol explicit? Can the data tell us what it needs?" Right now you have to *know* that `:tutor` needs player interaction. The effect map doesn't say so. It just... returns db unchanged and hopes someone upstream noticed.

### Proposed direction (needs brainstorming)

Effects that need player interaction should declare it in the effect data itself:

Option A: Marker key on effect maps
```clojure
{:effect/type :tutor
 :effect/interactive true   ;; <-- engine can check this
 :effect/criteria {...}
 :effect/target-zone :hand}
```

Option B: Engine returns a richer result
```clojure
;; Instead of just returning db:
{:db db :awaiting {:selection-type :tutor :context {...}}}
```

Option C: Effect metadata registry (less invasive)
```clojure
(def interactive-effects #{:tutor :discard :scry :peek-and-select ...})
```
At minimum, the set of interactive effects should be defined once, not reconstructed by pattern-matching in `has-selection-effect?` and `find-selection-effect-index`.

### Files involved

- `engine/effects.cljs` — effect implementations that return db unchanged (lines ~456-551)
- `events/selection.cljs` — `has-selection-effect?`, `find-selection-effect-index`, `resolve-spell-with-selection`
- Card definitions — would gain `:effect/interactive` if Option A
- `events/game.cljs` — `resolve-one-item` delegates to selection-aware resolution

### Risks

- Option A changes card data shape (many card defs to update, but mechanical)
- Option B changes engine return type contract (bigger change)
- Option C is lowest risk but least principled
close_reason: Replaced by epic fizzle-igid with full brainstormed design

title: Vision Charm card + tests
description: Implement Vision Charm ({U} Instant) modal card with 3 modes using all infrastructure built in prior tasks.
design:
## Goal
Implement Vision Charm ({U} Instant) modal card with 3 modes: mill 4, change land types, phase out artifact.

## Effort Estimate
3-5 hours (modal card definition + 12 tests covering all 3 modes + registry)

## Implementation

### Card Definition
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/blue/vision_charm.cljs

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.blue.vision-charm)


(def card
  {:card/id :vision-charm
   :card/name "Vision Charm"
   :card/cmc 1
   :card/mana-cost {:blue 1}
   :card/colors #{:blue}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/text "Choose one — • Target player mills four cards. • Choose a land type and a basic land type. Each land of the first chosen type becomes the second chosen type until end of turn. • Target artifact phases out."
   :card/modes [{:mode/label "Target player mills four cards"
                 :mode/targeting [{:target/id :mill-player
                                   :target/type :player
                                   :target/options #{:any-player}
                                   :target/required true}]
                 :mode/effects [{:effect/type :mill
                                 :effect/amount 4
                                 :effect/target-ref :mill-player}]}
                {:mode/label "Change land types until end of turn"
                 :mode/effects [{:effect/type :change-land-types}]}
                {:mode/label "Target artifact phases out"
                 :mode/targeting [{:target/id :target-artifact
                                   :target/type :object
                                   :target/zone :battlefield
                                   :target/controller :any
                                   :target/criteria {:match/types #{:artifact}}
                                   :target/required true}]
                 :mode/effects [{:effect/type :phase-out
                                 :effect/target-ref :target-artifact}]}]})
```

### Registry
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/registry.cljs
- Add require for `fizzle.cards.blue.vision-charm`
- APPEND `vision-charm/card` to END of all-cards

### Test File
File: src/test/fizzle/cards/blue/vision_charm_test.cljs

**CRITICAL: Mode 2 test flow pattern**
Mode 2 has NO target — it uses the selection system (returns :needs-selection). You CANNOT use th/cast-mode-with-target for Mode 2. Instead:

```clojure
;; Mode 2 test pattern:
;; 1. Cast Vision Charm with mode index 1 (second mode)
;; 2. th/resolve-top → returns {:db db :selection source-selection}
;; 3. th/confirm-selection with source type → returns {:db db :selection target-selection}
;; 4. th/confirm-selection with target type → returns {:db db-final}
;; 5. Assert grants on matching lands
```

Study how other selection-returning effects are tested. Look at tests for :untap-lands or :scry effects for the resolve-top → confirm-selection pattern.

For Modes 1 and 3 (targeted): use th/cast-mode-with-target.

**Tests (12+ minimum — modal + targeted):**

A. Card definition — all fields exact:
   - :card/id = :vision-charm
   - :card/name = "Vision Charm"
   - :card/cmc = 1
   - :card/mana-cost = {:blue 1}
   - :card/colors = #{:blue}
   - :card/types = #{:instant}
   - 3 modes with correct labels and effect types

B1. Mode 1 happy path — mills 4 from target player's library (catches wrong :effect/amount)
B2. Mode 1 mills opponent — target :player-2 mills opponent library (catches player targeting)

C. Cannot cast — no blue mana

D. Storm count increments on cast

**Mode 3 (Phase out):**
E1. Mode 3 happy path — artifact moves to :phased-out zone (catches :phase-out wiring)
E2. Mode 3 preserves tapped state — tapped artifact stays tapped after phase-in at untap (catches phasing semantics violation — KEY correctness property)

**Mode 2 (Land type change):**
F1. Mode 2 returns needs-selection on resolve (catches missing interactive return)
F2. Mode 2 full flow: Island→Mountain, grants applied (catches selection chain wiring)
F3. Mode 2 overridden Island produces red mana when tapped (catches mana_activation override regression)

**Edge cases:**
G1. Mode 1 with fewer than 4 cards in library — mills all available (no crash)
G2. Mode 3 cannot target non-artifacts (targeting criteria)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Card definition: 3 modes with correct targeting and effects for each
- [ ] Mode 1: mills exactly 4 from target player; player targeting works
- [ ] Mode 2: full 2-step selection flow; grants applied; mana override produces correct color
- [ ] Mode 3: artifact phases out; tapped state preserved across phase-in
- [ ] 12+ tests all passing
- [ ] Card registered in registry.cljs (appended to end)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO (is (some? x)) — use exact value checks
- ❌ NO cast-mode-with-target for Mode 2 — it has no target, use resolve-top + confirm-selection
- ❌ NO :colorless in mana cost (Vision Charm is just {:blue 1})
- ❌ NO reimplementing production handlers in tests
- ❌ APPEND to end of all-cards in registry

title: Task 2: Update stack-item-view to display card name + description
design:
## Goal
Update the stack-item-view component to display triggers with format: 'Trigger: [Card Name] [description]'

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-032: Added :trigger/description to schema and create-trigger. Description now flows from card definition to trigger on stack.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- core.cljs:172-186 - stack-item-view currently shows 'Trigger: ' + type name
- subs/game.cljs:36-49 - ::stack subscription combines triggers and spells
- db/queries.cljs:130-139 - get-stack-items returns triggers with all attributes

### 2. Data Flow Understanding
Current: Trigger has :trigger/source (object ID) and :trigger/description (string)
Need: Card name from the source object's card

Path: :trigger/source → look up object → :object/card → :card/name

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] db/queries.cljs - Update get-stack-items to join source object and get card name:
      ```clojure
      ;; Add helper function
      (defn get-trigger-source-card-name [db trigger]
        (when-let [source-id (:trigger/source trigger)]
          (when-let [obj (get-object db source-id)]
            (get-in obj [:object/card :card/name]))))
      ```
- [ ] subs/game.cljs - Update ::stack subscription to enrich triggers with :trigger/card-name:
      ```clojure
      (let [triggers (queries/get-stack-items game-db)
            enriched-triggers (mapv (fn [t]
                                      (assoc t :trigger/card-name
                                             (queries/get-trigger-source-card-name game-db t)))
                                    triggers)
            ...]
      ```
- [ ] core.cljs:172-186 - Update stack-item-view to display new format:
      ```clojure
      (if (:trigger/type item)
        (if-let [desc (:trigger/description item)]
          (str "Trigger: " (:trigger/card-name item) " " desc)
          (str "Trigger: " (name (:trigger/type item))))
        label)
      ```
- [ ] Manual test: Run dev server, tap City of Brass, verify stack display
- [ ] Run `make test` - all tests passing
- [ ] Run `make validate` - lint/format pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Triggers with description show 'Trigger: City of Brass deals 1 damage to you'
- [ ] Triggers without description show 'Trigger: becomes-tapped' (backwards compatible)
- [ ] Source object in graveyard still shows card name (handles moved objects)
- [ ] All tests passing (`make test`)
- [ ] `make validate` passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Data Access Pattern:**
- Subscription enriches triggers with card name (view stays pure)
- get-object handles objects that moved zones (graveyard) - still returns object
- Card name lookup happens once per render, not per item

**Edge Case: Source Object Moved/Destroyed:**
- :trigger/source is an object ID, not a ref
- Object may have moved to graveyard after trigger created
- get-object should still find it (objects persist across zones)
- If object somehow doesn't exist, :trigger/card-name will be nil
- View should handle nil card-name gracefully (fall back to type)

**Edge Case: No Description:**
- Must check for :trigger/description before accessing
- Fall back to current 'Trigger: type' format when nil
- Backwards compatible with existing triggers

**Performance:**
- Enrichment happens in subscription (cached by re-frame)
- Only re-computes when game-db changes
- N lookups for N triggers (acceptable for small stacks)

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO auto-generating descriptions (explicit only)
- ❌ NO changing the 'Trigger:' prefix
- ❌ NO breaking triggers without descriptions
- ❌ NO doing db lookups in view component (keep views pure)
close_reason: Closed

title: Extract events/priority_flow.cljs from game.cljs
description: Third extraction: move priority/yield system (~385 lines) from game.cljs into events/priority_flow.cljs. This is the orchestrator — depends on casting, resolution, phases, cleanup. After this, only cycling + re-exports remain in game.cljs.
design:
## Goal
Create events/priority_flow.cljs with the priority system extracted from game.cljs lines 98-484. Register events with :: (native namespace :fizzle.events.priority-flow/). Tests green after this task.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours (largest extraction, most dependencies)

## Implementation

### 1. Current game.cljs state (post-casting + resolution extraction)
- Lines 1-96: ns declaration + re-exports + old keyword forwarding (casting + resolution)
- Lines 98-484: priority flow (THIS EXTRACTION)
- Lines 487-532: cycling (next task)

### 2. Create src/main/fizzle/events/priority_flow.cljs

Namespace requires:
```clojure
(ns fizzle.events.priority-flow
  (:require
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.bots.protocol :as bot-protocol]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as queries]
    [fizzle.engine.priority :as priority]
    [fizzle.engine.stack :as stack]
    [fizzle.events.casting :as casting]
    [fizzle.events.cleanup :as cleanup]
    [fizzle.events.phases :as phases]
    [fizzle.events.resolution :as resolution]
    [fizzle.events.selection.core :as sel-core]
    [re-frame.core :as rf]))
```

CRITICAL: Import from casting and resolution directly — NOT via game.cljs re-exports. Replace:
- `resolve-one-item` → `resolution/resolve-one-item`
- `cast-spell-handler` → `casting/cast-spell-handler`

Move these items (CUT from game.cljs):

**Public functions:**
- advance-with-stops
- negotiate-priority
- yield-impl

**Private functions:**
- yield-resolve-stack
- player-is-bot?
- bot-would-pass?
- bot-turn-advance-one-phase
- yield-advance-phase
- resolve-one-and-stop
- max-yield-steps (private def)

**Selection multimethod:**
- sel-core/apply-continuation :resolve-one-and-stop

**Event handlers (4):**
- rf/reg-event-fx ::yield
- rf/reg-event-fx ::yield-all
- rf/reg-event-fx ::cast-and-yield
- rf/reg-event-db ::cast-and-yield-resolve

### 3. Update game.cljs with re-exports + old keyword forwarding

Add require:
```clojure
[fizzle.events.priority-flow :as priority-flow]
```

Add re-exports (check which are called downstream):
```clojure
(def advance-with-stops priority-flow/advance-with-stops)
(def negotiate-priority priority-flow/negotiate-priority)
(def yield-impl priority-flow/yield-impl)
```

Add old keyword forwarding (4 events):
```clojure
(rf/reg-event-fx :fizzle.events.game/yield ...)
(rf/reg-event-fx :fizzle.events.game/yield-all ...)
(rf/reg-event-fx :fizzle.events.game/cast-and-yield ...)
(rf/reg-event-db :fizzle.events.game/cast-and-yield-resolve ...)
```

NOTE: ::yield dispatches ::yield recursively via :fx. In priority_flow.cljs this becomes :fizzle.events.priority-flow/yield which is correct. But the old forwarding handler in game.cljs needs to dispatch to the new namespace.

NOTE: ::yield-all dispatches [::yield] — in priority_flow.cljs this dispatches :fizzle.events.priority-flow/yield. The old forwarding handler for yield-all should dispatch [:fizzle.events.priority-flow/yield].

SIMPLEST APPROACH: priority_flow.cljs registers its own :: events. game.cljs forwarding handlers re-dispatch to the new keywords: (rf/dispatch [:fizzle.events.priority-flow/yield]).

### 4. Remove unused imports from game.cljs
After extraction, game.cljs should no longer need:
- datascript.core (verify: only used by priority code)
- fizzle.bots.protocol (only used by priority code)
- fizzle.engine.priority (only used by priority code)
- fizzle.engine.stack (check: used by resolve-all forwarding?)
- fizzle.events.selection.core (check: used by any remaining code?)

CAREFUL: The resolve-all forwarding handler (lines 77-95) uses stack/get-top-stack-item and queries/get-all-stack-items. If we keep that forwarding handler, we need those imports. Alternative: simplify forwarding handlers to just re-dispatch.

### 5. Verification
```bash
npx shadow-cljs compile test
make validate
grep -n 'advance-with-stops\|negotiate-priority\|yield-impl\|yield-resolve-stack\|resolve-one-and-stop\|player-is-bot?\|bot-would-pass?' src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs | grep -v 'def \|require\|priority-flow/'
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] events/priority_flow.cljs exists at src/main/fizzle/events/priority_flow.cljs
- [ ] Contains: advance-with-stops, negotiate-priority, yield-impl (public)
- [ ] Contains: yield-resolve-stack, player-is-bot?, bot-would-pass?, bot-turn-advance-one-phase, yield-advance-phase, resolve-one-and-stop (private)
- [ ] Contains: sel-core/apply-continuation :resolve-one-and-stop defmethod
- [ ] Contains: ::yield, ::yield-all, ::cast-and-yield, ::cast-and-yield-resolve event handlers
- [ ] Events registered with :: (native namespace :fizzle.events.priority-flow/)
- [ ] Calls casting/cast-spell-handler and resolution/resolve-one-item directly (NOT via game.cljs re-exports)
- [ ] game.cljs re-exports advance-with-stops, negotiate-priority, yield-impl
- [ ] game.cljs forwards old :fizzle.events.game/ keywords for yield, yield-all, cast-and-yield, cast-and-yield-resolve
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)
- [ ] No priority flow code remains in game.cljs (except re-exports and forwarding)

## Anti-Patterns (task-specific)
- ❌ NO importing resolve-one-item or cast-spell-handler via game.cljs re-exports (import from casting/resolution directly)
- ❌ NO changing the negotiate-priority algorithm or bot auto-pass logic
- ❌ NO changing the yield cascade safety limit (max-yield-steps = 200)
- ❌ NO extracting cycling in this task (separate task)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Self-referential dispatch in ::yield:**
The ::yield handler dispatches [::yield] for cascading. In priority_flow.cljs, this becomes [:fizzle.events.priority-flow/yield]. The old forwarding handler must dispatch to the new keyword, not recurse on the old one.

**::yield-all dispatches [::yield]:**
Both must be in the same namespace for :: to resolve correctly. Since both move to priority_flow.cljs, this works naturally.

**::cast-and-yield calls cast-spell-handler:**
Currently calls the game.cljs local var (which is a re-export). In priority_flow.cljs, it should call casting/cast-spell-handler directly.

**resolve-all forwarding handler complexity:**
The resolve-all forwarding (lines 77-95) contains inline logic and self-dispatches. Rather than duplicating this logic in the forwarding handler, consider: resolution.cljs already has ::resolve-all. The forwarding handler can simply re-dispatch to [:fizzle.events.resolution/resolve-all initial-ids]. BUT the internal recursion in resolve-all dispatches [:fizzle.events.game/resolve-all] — this needs updating in resolution.cljs to dispatch [::resolve-all] (its own namespace). Check if resolution.cljs already does this correctly.
close_reason: Extracted priority_flow into events/priority_flow.cljs. Commit 71bedc8. 2939 tests pass.

title: Fix snapshot sharing: encode player metadata in EDN trailer
description: Snapshot binary format drops player/bot-archetype and player/is-opponent, causing restored games to break — director can't identify bots, game freezes after first yield
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Snapshot encode/decode round-trip preserves all player metadata (bot-archetype, is-opponent, max-hand-size)
- Restored games allow full game advancement (multiple yields, bot actions, phase progression)
- Backward-compatible: v1 snapshots without metadata still decode and restore correctly
- No bot-specific code in encoder/decoder — generic per-player property maps

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] restore-yield-test: restored game can yield twice with game-db changing each time
- [ ] restore-yield-test: yield-all on restored game completes full turn cycle
- [ ] Existing snapshot tests still pass
- [ ] Encoder/decoder round-trip preserves player/bot-archetype and player/is-opponent
- [ ] Old snapshots (without flag bit 2) decode without error
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO bot-specific encoding logic (generalize: the encoder should not know about bots — it encodes player properties)
- ❌ NO version bump (use reserved flag bit 2 for backward compatibility)
- ❌ NO changes to restorer.cljs (it already reads these keys from player maps)
- ❌ NO encoding of transient state (stops from localStorage, drew-from-empty)

## Approach
Use reserved flag bit 2 (0x4) in the existing v1 header to signal 'has player metadata.' After the grants section, encode one EDN blob per player containing non-binary properties. Decoder checks the flag, reads the blobs, and merges into player maps. The restorer already reads these keys — no restorer changes needed.

## Architecture
- sharing/encoder.cljs: extract player metadata, set flag bit, write EDN blobs after grants
- sharing/decoder.cljs: check flag bit, read EDN blobs, merge into player maps
- sharing/extractor.cljs: no changes (already captures all player attributes)
- sharing/restorer.cljs: no changes (already reads bot-archetype and is-opponent)

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Snapshot binary format only encodes numeric player data (life, mana, storm, land-plays). Non-numeric attributes like bot-archetype and is-opponent are silently dropped. After restore, the director can't identify player-2 as a bot, so it returns await-human whenever the bot has priority — game freezes after one yield.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- encoder.cljs: binary header has 4-bit flags field, only bit 1 used (has-player-grants), bits 2-3 reserved
- decoder.cljs:229: checks flags for grants, ignores other bits — safe to add bit 2
- extractor.cljs:109-118: already pulls bot-archetype and is-opponent from datascript
- restorer.cljs:42-52: already reads bot-archetype from player map and conditionally adds it
- director.cljs:340-351: current-holder-player-id returns nil when bot-archetype missing

**Root cause trace:**
1. extractor captures bot-archetype ✓
2. encoder drops it (not in binary format) ✗
3. decoder produces player map without it
4. restorer creates player without bot-archetype
5. director/get-bot-archetype returns nil
6. director falls to :else → :await-human without advancing

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. EDN player metadata trailer ✓
**What it is:** Use flag bit 2 to signal 'has player metadata', encode per-player property maps as EDN blobs after grants.

**Pros:**
- Generic: no bot-specific code, any future player property works
- Backward-compatible: old snapshots without flag work fine
- Self-describing: EDN is human-readable for debugging

**Cons:**
- Slightly larger (~50 bytes per snapshot vs 3 bits for header approach)

**Chosen because:** User requirement for no bot-specific code. Extensible for future player properties.

#### 2. Header bits for bot-archetype ❌
**What it is:** 2 bits for bot-archetype index + 1 bit for is-opponent in binary header.

**Why we looked at this:** Most compact encoding.

**Pros:** Minimal size impact (3 bits)
**Cons:** Bot-specific encoding, requires updating if new archetypes added

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Violates 'no bot-specific code' requirement.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** EDN overhead causes URL length issues.

#### 3. Version bump to v2 ❌
**What it is:** New binary format version with player metadata fields.

**Why we looked at this:** Cleanest separation of format versions.

**Pros:** Clean versioning
**Cons:** Requires decoder fallback logic for v1

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Unnecessary complexity when reserved flag bits are available.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** v1 format needs other incompatible changes.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Encode/decode player metadata (bot-archetype, is-opponent, max-hand-size)
- Backward compatibility with existing v1 snapshots
- Fix the restore-yield test

**Out of scope:**
- Encoding player stops (loaded from localStorage)
- Encoding transient game state (drew-from-empty)
- Changes to the restorer
- Initial director run after restore (separate concern)

### Open Questions
- Should max-hand-size be encoded? (default 7, but could vary with grants — yes, encode it)
close_reason: All tasks complete - player metadata EDN encoding via flag bit 2

title: Modal Counter/Destroy: REB, BEB, Pyroblast, Hydroblast
description: Third epic in the counterspell chain. Builds on Epic 1 (fizzle-plg3) counter foundation to add modal spells and color-based targeting.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Modal spell mechanic: choose one mode at cast time, each mode has different targeting and effects
- Color-based targeting criteria: target only spells/permanents of a specific color
- Resolution-time color check: Pyroblast/Hydroblast can target anything but only counter/destroy if correct color
- Red Elemental Blast: {R} instant, choose one — counter target blue spell OR destroy target blue permanent
- Blue Elemental Blast: {U} instant, choose one — counter target red spell OR destroy target red permanent
- Pyroblast: {R} instant, choose one — counter target spell if blue OR destroy target permanent if blue
- Hydroblast: {U} instant, choose one — counter target spell if red OR destroy target permanent if red
- REB/BEB restrict targets at cast time (must be blue/red)
- Pyroblast/Hydroblast can target anything but effect is conditional on color at resolution
- Both player and bot can cast these cards

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Modal spell mechanic: player chooses mode at cast time
- [ ] Mode selection determines targeting requirements
- [ ] Color-based targeting criteria works (match/colors)
- [ ] REB correctly counters blue spells and destroys blue permanents
- [ ] BEB correctly counters red spells and destroys red permanents
- [ ] Pyroblast can target any spell/permanent, only resolves if blue
- [ ] Hydroblast can target any spell/permanent, only resolves if red
- [ ] All 4 cards defined with correct oracle text
- [ ] Full test suites per CLAUDE.md requirements
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO collapsing REB/BEB with Pyroblast/Hydroblast (correctness: they have different targeting rules — REB restricts at cast time, Pyroblast checks at resolution)
- ❌ NO hardcoding card-specific mode logic in engine (architecture: modal mechanic must be generic)
- ❌ NO skipping the modal selection step (interaction: player must choose mode before targeting)
- ❌ NO special-casing card names in engine code (architecture: cards are data)
- ❌ NO tautological test assertions (testing: assert exact values)

## Approach
Add a modal spell mechanic where cards define multiple modes, each with its own targeting requirements and effects. At cast time, player selects a mode (new selection type), then proceeds to target based on the chosen mode. For Pyroblast/Hydroblast, use a resolution-time condition check (:condition/type :target-is-color) that allows targeting anything but only applies the effect if the target matches.

## Architecture
- Card definitions: modal cards define :card/modes vector, each mode has targeting + effects
- events/selection/ — New mode selection handler at cast time
- engine/effects.cljs — Resolution-time color condition check
- cards/red/red_elemental_blast.cljs, cards/red/pyroblast.cljs
- cards/blue/blue_elemental_blast.cljs, cards/blue/hydroblast.cljs
- cards/registry.cljs — Register new cards

## Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** Modal spells, color targeting, resolution-time color checks
**Out of scope:** Other modal cards beyond these 4 (but mechanic should be generic)
**Depends on:** Epic 1 (fizzle-plg3) counter foundation
close_reason: Closed

title: Split battlefield into land and non-land rows with sorting
design:
## Goal
Modify the battlefield subscription to return cards grouped into lands and non-lands, each sorted by CMC+name. Update the battlefield view to render two distinct rows.

## Context
Completed fizzle-x19c: sort-cards utility exists at engine/sorting.cljs, already imported in subs/game.cljs.

## Implementation

1. Modify ::battlefield subscription in subs/game.cljs (line 132-136)
   - Current: returns flat vector from queries/get-objects-in-zone
   - New: return map {:lands [...] :non-lands [...]} where each is sorted
   - Land detection: check if :card/types contains :land
   - Use sorting/sort-cards on each group

   Change from:
     (when game-db (queries/get-objects-in-zone game-db :player-1 :battlefield))
   To:
     (when game-db
       (let [all (queries/get-objects-in-zone game-db :player-1 :battlefield)
             {:keys [lands non-lands]} (group-by-land all)]
         {:lands (sorting/sort-cards lands)
          :non-lands (sorting/sort-cards non-lands)}))

   Helper (inline or in sorting.cljs):
     (defn- land? [obj] (contains? (get-in obj [:object/card :card/types]) :land))

2. Write tests first (TDD) in src/test/fizzle/engine/sorting_test.cljs (add to existing file)
   - test-group-lands-and-non-lands: mixed battlefield -> lands separate from non-lands
   - test-empty-battlefield-returns-empty-groups: no cards -> {:lands [] :non-lands []}
   - test-all-lands-battlefield: only lands -> {:lands [sorted] :non-lands []}
   - test-each-group-sorted: within each group, CMC+name order maintained

3. Update battlefield-view in views/battlefield.cljs (line 115-125)
   - Current: subscribes to flat list, renders single flex-wrap div
   - New: destructure {:keys [lands non-lands]} from subscription
   - Render non-lands row first (spells/artifacts), then lands row below
   - Each row has a subtle label (NON-LANDS, LANDS) or just visual separation
   - Keep 'No permanents' message when both groups empty

   Change from:
     (let [battlefield @(rf/subscribe [::subs/battlefield])]
       [:div ...
        (for [obj battlefield] ...)])
   To:
     (let [{:keys [lands non-lands]} @(rf/subscribe [::subs/battlefield])]
       [:div ...
        (when (seq non-lands)
          [:div ... (for [obj non-lands] [permanent-view obj])])
        (when (seq lands)
          [:div ... (for [obj lands] [permanent-view obj])])
        (when (and (empty? lands) (empty? non-lands))
          [:div ... 'No permanents'])])

4. Verify
   - make validate passes
   - Visually confirm in browser: lands in separate row from non-lands

## Success Criteria
- [ ] ::battlefield subscription returns {:lands [...] :non-lands [...]} map
- [ ] Each group sorted by CMC then name
- [ ] Battlefield view renders non-lands row above lands row
- [ ] Empty battlefield still shows 'No permanents'
- [ ] 4 new tests pass in sorting_test.cljs
- [ ] make validate passes with zero failures

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty groups**
- Battlefield with only lands should show lands row only
- Battlefield with only non-lands should show non-lands row only
- Both empty -> 'No permanents'

**Edge Case: Subscription API change**
- This changes ::battlefield from vector to map — any other subscriber will break
- Check: views/battlefield.cljs is the ONLY consumer (confirmed via codebase search)
- No other file subscribes to ::battlefield

**Performance**
- group-by + two sort-cards calls vs single flat list
- Battlefield typically has 3-8 cards, negligible performance impact

## Anti-Patterns (task-level)
- ❌ NO sorting in battlefield view (sort in subscription only)
- ❌ NO land detection by card name (use :card/types #{:land})

title: Extract selection/zone_ops.cljs with discard unification and graveyard-return
design:
## Goal

Extract zone operations (discard, cleanup-discard, graveyard-return) from selection.cljs into events/selection/zone_ops.cljs. Also unify :discard and :cleanup-discard into a single :discard type with :selection/cleanup? flag (epic requirement #6).

## Context
Completed fizzle-0b5g (costs.cljs extraction). selection.cljs now has ~176 lines with only zone-ops code (discard/cleanup-discard/graveyard-return builders, defmethods, build-selection-for-effect registrations) plus shared re-frame event handlers (set-pending, toggle, confirm, cancel). After this extraction, the shared event handlers remain in selection.cljs as a thin shim.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Move :default defmethod to core.cljs
The build-selection-for-effect :default defmethod is a mechanism fallback, not domain-specific. Move it from selection.cljs to core.cljs next to the multimethod definition.

File: src/main/fizzle/events/selection/core.cljs
- Add defmethod core/build-selection-for-effect :default after the defmulti

### 2. Create events/selection/zone_ops.cljs
Move from selection.cljs:

**Builders:**
- build-discard-selection (was private, make public)
- build-graveyard-selection

**Defmethods on core/execute-confirmed-selection:**
- :discard (unified — checks :selection/cleanup? flag)
- :graveyard-return

**Defmethods on core/build-selection-for-effect:**
- :discard
- :return-from-graveyard

**Discard unification (epic requirement #6):**
- Remove :cleanup-discard type entirely
- Add optional :selection/cleanup? flag to build-discard-selection
- Single :discard defmethod checks flag:
  - true → discard + expire-grants + return {:db :finalized? true}
  - false → discard + return {:db} (standard path)
- The builder signature becomes: (build-discard-selection player-id object-id discard-effect effects-after & [{:keys [cleanup?]}])
  Or simply add an arity: (build-discard-selection player-id object-id discard-effect effects-after) for normal discard, and (build-cleanup-discard-selection player-id discard-count) as a separate builder that sets cleanup? true.

Requires for zone_ops.cljs:
- fizzle.db.queries :as queries (for build-graveyard-selection, expire-grants lookup)
- fizzle.engine.grants :as grants (for cleanup discard grant expiration)
- fizzle.engine.zones :as zones (for move-to-zone)
- fizzle.events.selection.core :as core (for defmethod registrations)

Does NOT need: datascript.core, rules, mana, effects

### 3. Update selection.cljs → thin event handler shim
After zone_ops extraction, selection.cljs will have ONLY the 4 shared re-frame event handlers:
- ::set-pending-selection
- ::toggle-selection
- ::confirm-selection
- ::cancel-selection

Plus require of core.cljs (for toggle-selection-impl, confirm-selection-handler).

Keep selection.cljs as thin shim — other namespaces (views, tests) reference ::selection/toggle-selection keyword namespace. Moving events to core.cljs would break all dispatch keyword references.

Remove requires no longer needed: queries, grants, zones (only used by zone-ops code).

### 4. Update game.cljs
- build-cleanup-discard-selection (line 392): change :selection/type from :cleanup-discard to :discard, add :selection/cleanup? true
- Add bare require [fizzle.events.selection.zone-ops] to ensure defmethod registration
- KEEP [fizzle.events.selection] bare require — still needed for event handler registrations (set-pending, toggle, confirm, cancel)
- NOTE: complete-cleanup-discard function (line 430) is a SEPARATE code path from the selection system. It is called directly by tests and does NOT use selection types. No changes needed to this function.

### 5. Update core.cljs comment
core.cljs line 148 comment says "cleanup-discard" — update to reference ":discard with :selection/cleanup?" instead.

### 6. Update test files
Specific changes needed:

**turn_test.cljs:**
- Line 338: Change (= :cleanup-discard ...) to (= :discard ...)
- Line 339: Update test description string
- Line 415: Change (= :cleanup-discard ...) to (= :discard ...)
- Also verify the selection has :selection/cleanup? true in these assertions

**auto_confirm_test.cljs:**
- Line 16: Comment mentions cleanup-discard — update to "discard (with cleanup?)"

**NOTE: history tests do NOT reference :cleanup-discard — no changes needed there.**
**NOTE: interceptor priority-selection-types does NOT include :cleanup-discard — no changes needed.**

## Success Criteria
- [ ] events/selection/zone_ops.cljs exists with discard (unified), graveyard-return builders and defmethods
- [ ] build-selection-for-effect :default defmethod lives in core.cljs (mechanism, not domain)
- [ ] :cleanup-discard type removed everywhere, unified into :discard with :selection/cleanup? flag
- [ ] zone_ops.cljs requires only core.cljs and engine/* (no other domain files)
- [ ] selection.cljs reduced to thin event handler shim (4 handlers + core require only)
- [ ] game.cljs build-cleanup-discard-selection uses :discard with :selection/cleanup? true
- [ ] game.cljs keeps both bare requires: selection (event handlers) and zone-ops (defmethods)
- [ ] turn_test.cljs assertions updated from :cleanup-discard to :discard
- [ ] All ~1266 tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] No circular dependencies

## Anti-Patterns
- NO type-specific logic leaking into core.cljs (except :default fallback, which is type-agnostic mechanism)
- NO leaving orphaned defmethods in selection.cljs
- NO removing selection.cljs bare require from game.cljs (event handlers still need loading)
- NO modifying complete-cleanup-discard function (separate code path, out of scope)
- NO modifying history interceptor or descriptions (they don't reference :cleanup-discard)
- NO putting :default defmethod in a domain file (it's mechanism fallback)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Discard unification approach:**
The unified :discard defmethod must check :selection/cleanup? to decide the return shape:
- false/nil → {:db updated-db} (standard, wrapper handles remaining-effects + cleanup)
- true → {:db final-db :finalized? true} (cleanup path, grant expiration included)
This flag-based dispatch inside a single defmethod is simple and correct.

**complete-cleanup-discard is a separate path:**
game.cljs has complete-cleanup-discard as a direct function (not selection system). Tests call it directly. It does the same thing as the :cleanup-discard defmethod. This duplication is pre-existing and out of scope for this task. Do not modify or remove it.

**Event handler keyword stability:**
selection.cljs must remain as a thin shim because ::selection/toggle-selection expands to :fizzle.events.selection/toggle-selection. Views and tests dispatch these keywords. Moving handlers to core.cljs would change all keyword namespaces.

**:default defmethod in core.cljs:**
The :default method on build-selection-for-effect is a mechanism fallback (creates generic selection for unknown effect types). It belongs in core.cljs with the defmulti, not in any domain file. This is consistent with Clojure convention (default near defmulti).
close_reason: Closed

title: Add :entry/principal to history data model and interceptor
description:
## Goal
Add `:entry/principal` field to history entries. The history interceptor determines which player caused each event and passes that to `make-entry`.

## Changes

### 1. `src/main/fizzle/history/core.cljs` — make-entry (line 12)

Add optional 5th parameter `principal`. Use multi-arity for backward compat:

```clojure
(defn make-entry
  ([game-db event-type description turn]
   (make-entry game-db event-type description turn nil))
  ([game-db event-type description turn principal]
   (cond-> {:entry/snapshot game-db
            :entry/event-type event-type
            :entry/description description
            :entry/turn turn}
     principal (assoc :entry/principal principal))))
```

### 2. `src/main/fizzle/history/interceptor.cljs`

Add `[fizzle.engine.priority :as priority]` to requires.

Add helper to resolve priority holder to player-id:
```clojure
(defn- get-priority-holder-pid
  "Get the :player/id keyword of the player currently holding priority.
   Returns nil if no priority holder."
  [game-db]
  (when-let [holder-eid (priority/get-priority-holder-eid game-db)]
    (queries/get-player-id game-db holder-eid)))
```

**NOTE:** Check if `queries/get-player-id` exists (EID → player-id). If not, add it to `db/queries.cljs`:
```clojure
(defn get-player-id
  "Get the :player/id keyword for a player entity ID."
  [db eid]
  (:player/id (d/pull db [:player/id] eid)))
```

Add principal determination function:
```clojure
(defn- determine-principal
  "Determine which player caused this event. Returns player-id keyword or nil.
   System events (init-game, start-turn) return nil."
  [event game-db]
  (let [event-id (first event)
        args (rest event)]
    (case event-id
      ;; Cast with optional opts map
      :fizzle.events.game/cast-spell
      (let [opts (first args)]
        (or (:player-id opts) (queries/get-human-player-id game-db)))

      ;; Always human-initiated
      (:fizzle.events.game/cast-and-yield
       :fizzle.events.abilities/activate-ability)
      (queries/get-human-player-id game-db)

      ;; Explicit player-id in args: [_ object-id player-id]
      :fizzle.events.game/play-land
      (or (second args) (queries/get-human-player-id game-db))

      ;; Explicit player-id: [_ object-id color player-id]
      :fizzle.events.abilities/activate-mana-ability
      (or (nth args 2 nil) (queries/get-human-player-id game-db))

      ;; Priority holder is the actor
      (:fizzle.events.game/yield
       :fizzle.events.game/yield-all)
      (get-priority-holder-pid game-db)

      ;; Confirm selection: use selection player-id
      :fizzle.events.selection/confirm-selection
      nil  ;; handled separately below

      ;; System events: no principal
      nil)))
```

Modify `:before` phase (around line 62) to capture principal:
```clojure
;; Add after existing captures:
(assoc-in [:coeffects :history/principal]
          (or (determine-principal event game-db)
              ;; For confirm-selection, use selection's player-id
              (get-in db [:game/pending-selection :selection/player-id])))
```

Modify `:after` phase (around line 107) to pass principal to make-entry:
```clojure
(let [principal (get-in context [:coeffects :history/principal])
      entry (history/make-entry snapshot-db event-id description turn principal)]
```

### 3. `src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs` — start-turn handler (line 666)

The `start-turn` handler creates entries manually. Pass `nil` as principal (system event):
```clojure
entry (history/make-entry db-after-turn ::start-turn
                          (str "Start Turn " turn) turn nil)
```
This is already backward compatible via the new multi-arity, but explicit nil makes intent clear.

### 4. Tests

Create `src/test/fizzle/history/principal_test.cljs`:

**Tests to write (RED first):**
1. `make-entry-with-principal` — entry includes `:entry/principal` when provided
2. `make-entry-without-principal` — entry has no `:entry/principal` key when nil
3. `make-entry-backward-compat` — 4-arg call still works (no principal)
4. `determine-principal-cast-spell-human` — returns human player-id
5. `determine-principal-cast-spell-bot` — returns bot player-id from opts
6. `determine-principal-yield` — returns priority holder
7. `determine-principal-activate-mana-human` — returns human (no player-id arg)
8. `determine-principal-activate-mana-bot` — returns bot player-id from args
9. `determine-principal-play-land` — returns player-id from args
10. `determine-principal-init-game` — returns nil
11. `determine-principal-start-turn` — entry has nil principal

**Integration test:**
12. Full event dispatch → verify entry has correct principal (using test-helpers setup)

## Verification
- `make validate` passes
- All existing history tests still pass (backward compatible make-entry)
close_reason: Closed

title: Necrologia card implementation
description: Implement Necrologia (3BB Instant): 'Cast this spell only during your end step. As an additional cost to cast this spell, pay X life. Draw X cards.' Requires three new infrastructure pieces: (1) general cast-restriction check in can-cast?, (2) X-life additional cost in pre-cast pipeline with accumulator selection, (3) :chosen-x dynamic value type. Design decisions: life payment is an additional COST (not effect) for accurate counterspell interaction. X stored on stack-item as :stack-item/chosen-x, read at resolution via resolve-dynamic-value. Cast restriction is data-driven and general-purpose.
