title: Decompose events/game.cljs with full namespace normalization
description: Supersedes fizzle-9gfv. Full decomposition of events/game.cljs (903 lines) into focused sub-modules WITH event keyword namespace normalization across all extracted modules (including previously-extracted phases, lands, init). Single PR, atomic change.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Extract casting pipeline (~280 lines) into events/casting.cljs
- Extract stack resolution (~130 lines) into events/resolution.cljs
- Extract priority/yield system (~420 lines) into events/priority_flow.cljs
- Extract cycling (~45 lines) into events/cycling.cljs
- Normalize ALL event keywords: previously-extracted modules (phases, lands, init) change from :fizzle.events.game/X to ::X (their own namespace)
- Update ALL downstream files (~107) to import directly from sub-modules, not game.cljs
- Update ALL event keyword literals in history, bots, SBA interceptors, views, and tests
- Delete events/game.cljs entirely (no facade)
- All 2100+ existing tests pass after completion
- Zero logic changes — pure code motion + rename

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] events/casting.cljs exists with ::cast-spell, ::select-casting-mode, ::cancel-mode-selection, evaluate-pre-cast-step multimethod + 8 defmethods
- [ ] events/resolution.cljs exists with ::resolve-top, ::resolve-all, resolve-one-item
- [ ] events/priority_flow.cljs exists with ::yield, ::yield-all, ::cast-and-yield, ::cast-and-yield-resolve, negotiate-priority, advance-with-stops, yield-impl
- [ ] events/cycling.cljs exists with ::cycle-card
- [ ] events/phases.cljs registers ::advance-phase, ::start-turn (not :fizzle.events.game/ prefix)
- [ ] events/lands.cljs registers ::play-land, ::tap-permanent (not :fizzle.events.game/ prefix)
- [ ] events/init.cljs registers ::init-game (not :fizzle.events.game/ prefix)
- [ ] events/game.cljs is deleted
- [ ] No file imports fizzle.events.game
- [ ] No literal :fizzle.events.game/ keyword appears anywhere in src/
- [ ] history/interceptor.cljs references new keyword namespaces
- [ ] history/descriptions.cljs references new keyword namespaces
- [ ] bots/interceptor.cljs references new keyword namespaces
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO re-export facade in game.cljs (decision: delete entirely, not keep as aggregator)
- ❌ NO logic changes during extraction (this is pure code motion + rename, not refactoring behavior)
- ❌ NO leaving any :fizzle.events.game/ keyword literal in src/ (full normalization, not partial)
- ❌ NO updating imports incrementally across multiple PRs (decision: one atomic PR)
- ❌ NO keeping old keyword aliases or backward-compat shims (clean break, not gradual migration)
- ❌ NO changing the evaluate-pre-cast-step multimethod dispatch contract (moves to casting.cljs intact)
- ❌ NO splitting pre-cast pipeline defmethods away from the multimethod definition (all move together to casting.cljs)

## Approach
Extract game.cljs into 4 new modules following the proven pattern from phases/cleanup/lands, then normalize ALL event keywords to use native namespace (::) instead of the :fizzle.events.game/ prefix. Update every downstream reference atomically. Delete game.cljs.

Extraction order: casting (zero deps) → resolution (one dep) → priority_flow (orchestrator) → cycling (standalone). Keyword normalization applies to all 9 event sub-modules simultaneously.

Dependency graph after extraction:
- casting.cljs → engine/*, selection/* (no deps on other event modules)
- resolution.cljs → engine/*, selection/* (no deps on other event modules)
- priority_flow.cljs → casting, resolution, phases, cleanup, engine/*, bots/protocol
- cycling.cljs → engine/* (standalone)

## Architecture

### New files
- src/main/fizzle/events/casting.cljs (~280 lines)
  - evaluate-pre-cast-step multimethod + 8 defmethods
  - pre-cast-pipeline vector
  - initiate-cast-with-mode, get-valid-spell-modes, build-spell-mode-selection
  - cast-spell-handler, select-casting-mode-handler, cancel-mode-selection-handler
  - execute-confirmed-selection :spell-mode defmethod
  - apply-continuation :cast-after-spell-mode defmethod
  - Events: ::cast-spell, ::select-casting-mode, ::cancel-mode-selection

- src/main/fizzle/events/resolution.cljs (~130 lines)
  - get-source-id, build-selection-from-result, clear-peek-result
  - resolve-one-item (public)
  - Events: ::resolve-top, ::resolve-all

- src/main/fizzle/events/priority_flow.cljs (~420 lines)
  - advance-with-stops, yield-resolve-stack, yield-advance-phase
  - negotiate-priority, yield-impl (public)
  - player-is-bot?, bot-would-pass?, bot-turn-advance-one-phase (private)
  - resolve-one-and-stop, apply-continuation :resolve-one-and-stop
  - Events: ::yield, ::yield-all, ::cast-and-yield, ::cast-and-yield-resolve

- src/main/fizzle/events/cycling.cljs (~45 lines)
  - cycle-card (public)
  - Event: ::cycle-card

### Event keyword mapping (15 keywords)
| Old (:fizzle.events.game/) | New (native ::) |
|---|---|
| cast-spell | :fizzle.events.casting/cast-spell |
| select-casting-mode | :fizzle.events.casting/select-casting-mode |
| cancel-mode-selection | :fizzle.events.casting/cancel-mode-selection |
| resolve-top | :fizzle.events.resolution/resolve-top |
| resolve-all | :fizzle.events.resolution/resolve-all |
| yield | :fizzle.events.priority-flow/yield |
| yield-all | :fizzle.events.priority-flow/yield-all |
| cast-and-yield | :fizzle.events.priority-flow/cast-and-yield |
| cast-and-yield-resolve | :fizzle.events.priority-flow/cast-and-yield-resolve |
| advance-phase | :fizzle.events.phases/advance-phase |
| start-turn | :fizzle.events.phases/start-turn |
| play-land | :fizzle.events.lands/play-land |
| tap-permanent | :fizzle.events.lands/tap-permanent |
| cycle-card | :fizzle.events.cycling/cycle-card |
| init-game | :fizzle.events.init/init-game |

### Files requiring keyword updates (~24 source files)
- history/interceptor.cljs (17 refs)
- history/descriptions.cljs (10 refs)
- bots/interceptor.cljs (13 refs)
- events/interceptors/sba.cljs (8 refs)
- events/ui.cljs (8 refs)
- views/controls.cljs, views/hand.cljs, views/battlefield.cljs, views/phase_bar.cljs, views/graveyard.cljs, views/game_over.cljs
- events/setup.cljs (imports game/init-game-state)
- core.cljs (requires game.cljs)

### Test files (~85 files)
All test files importing fizzle.events.game need :require updates + dispatch keyword updates.

## Architecture Update Checklist
After inner-loop work completes, review-implementation runs these 5 questions:
1. Did this work change a component's public interface? → No (same functions, new namespaces)
2. Did this work add or remove a dependency between components? → No (same deps, different require paths)
3. Did this work create a new component or remove an existing one? → Removed game.cljs facade, created 4 focused modules
4. Did this work move responsibility from one component to another? → Yes, from game.cljs to 4 sub-modules
5. Did this work create a new request path through 2+ components? → No

## Design Rationale
### Problem
events/game.cljs (903 lines, 37% of all commits) braids three independent concerns: casting, resolution, and priority orchestration. It's the highest-churn file. Additionally, 6 previously-extracted modules (phases, cleanup, lands, init, ui, abilities) register their events under :fizzle.events.game/ prefix for backward compat, creating a lie: the keyword namespace doesn't match the code namespace. ADR-016 accepted the decomposition.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- game.cljs:49-296 — casting pipeline (pre-cast multimethod + 8 defmethods + handlers)
- game.cljs:304-430 — resolution (resolve-one-item + event handlers)
- game.cljs:433-820 — priority flow (yield, negotiate, advance, bot helpers)
- game.cljs:862-903 — cycling (standalone mechanic)
- phases.cljs, cleanup.cljs, lands.cljs — proven extraction pattern (extract + re-export facade)
- 107 files reference fizzle.events.game — mostly test files + views
- 24 files contain literal :fizzle.events.game/ keyword strings — history, bots, SBA, views
- Casting has zero deps on resolution/priority. Resolution called by priority only. Priority orchestrates all.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Full normalization in one PR ✓
**What it is:** Extract 4 new modules + normalize all event keywords + update all imports + delete facade. One atomic PR.

**Investigation:**
- Verified casting has zero deps on other concerns (safe to extract first)
- Verified event keywords are code-only (not persisted in snapshots)
- Counted 154 keyword literal occurrences across 24 files — mechanical replacement
- Confirmed phases/lands pattern works (re-export + full keyword prefix)

**Pros:**
- Honest namespaces everywhere (keyword matches code location)
- No stale facade or backward-compat debt
- Single bisectable PR

**Cons:**
- Large diff (~107 files touched)
- All-or-nothing (can't merge partial)

**Chosen because:** User explicitly chose full normalization + one PR. The changes are mechanical (find-replace), not behavioral. Risk is manageable with make validate.

#### 2. Extract with facade only (original ADR-016) ❌
**What it is:** Extract 3 modules, keep game.cljs as facade, no keyword changes. Zero downstream changes.

**Why we looked at this:** Minimal blast radius, proven pattern from phases/lands.

**Investigation:**
- Would leave :fizzle.events.game/ keyword lie in place
- 107 files would still import game.cljs
- Facade would grow with each extraction (already 15 re-exports)

**Pros:**
- Zero downstream changes
- Lowest risk

**Cons:**
- Keywords don't match code location (lie persists)
- Facade grows forever
- Imports still centralized on one file

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** User chose honest namespaces over minimal blast radius.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** The diff turns out to be truly unmanageable (>200 files) and we need to stage it.

#### 3. Staged PRs (extract then normalize) ❌
**What it is:** PR1: extract with facade. PR2: normalize keywords + update imports.

**Why we looked at this:** Lower risk per PR, easier to review.

**Investigation:**
- Would require temporary state where both old and new keywords work
- PR1 alone adds value (decoupled concerns)

**Pros:**
- Smaller PRs, easier review
- PR1 is independently valuable

**Cons:**
- Temporary dual-keyword state between PRs
- Two rounds of test updates

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** User explicitly chose one PR. Mechanical changes don't benefit from staging.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** PR review shows the single diff is too large to review effectively.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- 4 new event modules (casting, resolution, priority_flow, cycling)
- Event keyword normalization for all 9 sub-modules
- All downstream import updates
- game.cljs deletion
- ADR-016 update to reflect expanded scope

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Selection system refactoring (separate concern)
- Splitting test files to match new modules (test files keep existing organization)
- Normalizing selection event keywords (already use ::)
- Normalizing ability event keywords (already use ::)

### Open Questions
- Should ADR-016 be updated in-place or superseded by ADR-017? (decide during implementation)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Where does cycle-card go? | New events/cycling.cljs | 4th extraction target, not 3 |
| Update test imports? | All at once | ~85 test files change imports + dispatch keywords |
| Pre-cast pipeline location? | All move to casting.cljs | Multimethod + 8 defmethods stay together |
| Event keyword namespaces? | New namespaces + normalize phases/lands too | 15 keywords change, full normalization |
| game.cljs fate? | Delete entirely | No facade, no aggregator |
| priority_flow dependency fan-out? | Accept it | Orchestrator naturally has many deps |
| Risk tolerance? | One PR, all at once | Atomic change, large diff |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Dependency Analysis
**Question explored:** Can the three concerns be cleanly separated?
**Sources consulted:**
- game.cljs line-by-line analysis
- ADR-016 coupling analysis
- Existing extraction pattern (phases, cleanup, lands)

**Findings:**
- Casting: zero deps on resolution or priority (lines 49-296, 822-855)
- Resolution: resolve-one-item called only by priority_flow (line 491)
- Priority: orchestrates casting (cast-spell-handler) + resolution (resolve-one-item) + phases + cleanup
- cycle-card: standalone, no deps on any concern

**Conclusion:** Clean seams exist. Extraction order: casting → resolution → priority_flow → cycling.

#### Event Keyword Persistence
**Question explored:** Are event keywords stored as data anywhere?
**Sources consulted:**
- history/interceptor.cljs — uses keywords as filter set + case dispatch
- snapshot/events.cljs — encodes game-db, not event history
- history/core.cljs — stores DB snapshots, not event replay

**Findings:**
- Event keywords are code-only (filter sets, case statements, descriptions)
- Snapshots encode Datascript db state, not event stream
- No persistent storage of keyword strings

**Conclusion:** Safe to rename atomically. No migration needed.

### Dead-End Paths
None — the approaches were all viable, user preference drove the choice.

### Open Concerns Raised
- 'Large blast radius (107 files)' → Mitigated by mechanical nature of changes + make validate
- 'Event keywords as data in history' → Verified: code-only, not persisted

title: Card Test Quality Audit — contributor-ready patterns and coverage gaps
description: Audit card test files for effectiveness and contributor readiness. Cards are the primary contribution surface — tests need to be so well-patterned that outsiders can write them correctly by copying the template. Audit goals: (1) verify existing card tests use production-path helpers consistently (cast-and-resolve, cast-with-target, confirm-selection) vs hand-rolling, (2) check CLAUDE.md mandatory category compliance across all 47 cards, (3) identify a gold-standard reference card test for each card type, (4) assess test-helpers themselves for correctness. Use analyze-tests skill on representative sample: 1 simple spell, 1 targeted spell, 1 modal spell, 1 flashback/storm spell, 1 land with ability.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- All 72 card test files audited with analyze-tests skill (SRE-level scrutiny)
- All ~20 legacy tests migrated from rules/cast-spell to production-path helpers (th/cast-and-resolve, th/cast-with-target, th/cast-mode-with-target)
- All card tests comply with CLAUDE.md mandatory categories: (A) exact card definition fields, (B) cast-resolve happy path via production code, (C) cannot-cast guards, (D) storm count
- Gold-standard reference tests designated in CLAUDE.md for each card type (simple, targeted, modal, land, flashback/storm)
- Template skeleton added to docs/testing-strategy.md showing mandatory structure with commented sections
- lions_eye_diamond test file written (currently missing)
- Conditional categories (E-I) assessed and gaps filled where applicable
- test_helpers.cljs assessed for correctness

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] All 72 test files reviewed by analyze-tests (batched by directory)
- [ ] Zero card tests using rules/cast-spell or rules/resolve-spell directly (all use th/ helpers)
- [ ] Every card test has: (A) exact field assertions, (B) production-path happy path, (C) cannot-cast guard, (D) storm count test
- [ ] CLAUDE.md updated with gold-standard reference test for each card type
- [ ] docs/testing-strategy.md has template skeleton with mandatory categories
- [ ] lions_eye_diamond_test.cljs exists and meets all mandatory categories
- [ ] make validate passes after every task
- [ ] No existing tests broken (test count can only increase)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO tautological assertions (some?, string?, any?) — exact values only (per CLAUDE.md)
- NO reimplementing production handlers in tests — use th/ helpers (per CLAUDE.md)
- NO copy-pasted test variants differing only by parameter — use doseq (per CLAUDE.md)
- NO hand-built selection maps — use production builders + th/confirm-selection
- NO reducing test count — fixes must preserve or add tests, never remove passing tests
- NO skipping make validate between tasks — every task must leave the build green
- NO mocking production code paths — tests must exercise real engine

## Approach
Rolling audit+fix by card directory. Each task runs analyze-tests on one directory batch, then fixes all issues found (legacy migration, missing mandatory categories, weak assertions, missing edge cases). Blue/ (28 tests) splits into 2-3 sub-batches. After all directories audited and fixed: designate gold-standard tests, add template skeleton to docs, write lions_eye_diamond test.

Estimated task batches:
1. black/ (9 tests) — establish audit+fix pattern
2. artifacts/ (10 tests)
3. blue/ batch 1 (~10 tests)
4. blue/ batch 2 (~10 tests)  
5. blue/ batch 3 (~8 tests)
6. red/ (7 tests)
7. lands/ (7 tests)
8. green/ + white/ + multicolor/ (9 tests combined)
9. Template skeleton + CLAUDE.md gold-standard references
10. lions_eye_diamond test

## Architecture
No architectural changes. This epic modifies only test files and documentation:
- src/test/fizzle/cards/**/*_test.cljs (72 existing + 1 new)
- src/test/fizzle/test_helpers.cljs (review only, fix if issues found)
- CLAUDE.md (add gold-standard test references)
- docs/testing-strategy.md (add template skeleton)

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Cards are the primary contribution surface. Test quality is uneven — 28% use legacy APIs, mandatory categories (cannot-cast, storm) frequently skipped, no designated reference tests. Contributors copying the wrong test propagate bad patterns.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- 72 card test files, 1 missing (lions_eye_diamond)
- ~20 tests use legacy rules/cast-spell instead of th/cast-and-resolve
- Production-path helpers well-designed at test_helpers.cljs:239-346
- Good exemplars exist: lightning_bolt (targeted), vision_charm (modal), city_of_brass (land)
- SBA integration almost never tested outside lightning_bolt
- CLAUDE.md has clear mandatory categories but compliance is inconsistent

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Rolling audit+fix by directory ✓
**What it is:** Go directory by directory, run analyze-tests on each batch, fix all issues immediately while context is fresh.
**Chosen because:** Natural grouping, manageable batch sizes, fixes happen while context fresh. Blue/ splits into sub-batches.

#### 2. Audit-first, then batch-fix by issue type ❌
**What it is:** Full audit pass first producing gap report, then fix by category (migrate legacy, add cannot-cast, etc.).
**Why abandoned:** Two passes over each file doubles context loading. Fixes lose context from audit.
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Audit reveals cross-cutting issues that can't be fixed per-directory.

#### 3. Per-card audit+fix ❌
**What it is:** One task per card test. 72+ tasks.
**Why abandoned:** Massive overhead — task creation/teardown dominates implementation time.
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Directory batches prove too large and need further splitting.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- All 72 existing card test files
- New lions_eye_diamond test
- Legacy API migration
- Mandatory category compliance
- Gold-standard designation + template skeleton
- test_helpers.cljs correctness review

**Out of scope:**
- New card implementations (only tests)
- Engine/production code changes (unless test_helpers has a bug)
- Selection system tests (not card tests)
- Performance optimization of tests

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Deliver audit or fix? | Audit + Fix | Ship better tests, not just a report |
| Legacy migration scope | All 20 | Full consistency, no legacy API usage remaining |
| Audit depth | All 72 files | Comprehensive audit, batched by directory |
| Gold-standard approach | Designate existing + template skeleton | CLAUDE.md references + docs/testing-strategy.md |
| LED missing test | In scope | Write new test file for lions_eye_diamond |

### Research Deep-Dives
#### Production-Path Helper Adoption
**Sources:** test_helpers.cljs:239-346, grep across all test files
**Findings:** 52/72 (72%) use helpers; 20/72 (28%) use legacy rules/cast-spell
**Conclusion:** Migration is mechanical — each legacy test needs cast-spell→th/cast-and-resolve swap

#### Mandatory Category Compliance
**Sources:** Sample audit of dark_ritual, lightning_bolt, vision_charm, city_of_brass
**Findings:** A & B well-covered; C (cannot-cast) and D (storm) frequently missing; SBA integration rare
**Conclusion:** Most gaps are missing tests, not wrong tests — additive fixes
close_reason: All 72 card tests audited+fixed. Gold-standard refs in CLAUDE.md. Template in testing-strategy.md. LED test written. Zero legacy rules/resolve-spell. 3339 tests.

title: Tier 2: Unit tests for filter/multi-object effects (tap-all, untap-all, exile-zone, create-token, apply-pt-modifier)
description: Add unit tests for 5 medium-complexity effect types to effects_test.cljs. These involve filtering objects, mass operations, and entity creation. Follow Tier 1 patterns from commit 67d8a6c.
design:
## Goal
Add isolated unit tests for 5 filter/multi-object effect types: :tap-all, :untap-all, :exile-zone, :create-token, :apply-pt-modifier

## Effort Estimate
4-8 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing patterns + Tier 1 tests
   - effects_test.cljs lines 2249-2509 (Tier 1 patterns from fizzle-wkg5)
   - effects/zones.cljs:177-216 (:tap-all/:untap-all impl)
   - effects/zones.cljs:113-123 (:exile-zone impl)
   - effects/tokens.cljs:14-43 (:create-token impl)
   - effects/pt_modifier.cljs:14-30 (:apply-pt-modifier impl)

2. Write tests (TDD — run make test between each type)

### :tap-all (~4 tests)
Production (zones.cljs:177-195): filters by :effect/permanent-type, taps all matching UNTAPPED objects on target player's battlefield.
- Happy path: 2 untapped creatures + 1 tapped creature → only 2 get tapped (catches: filter not checking tapped state)
- Empty battlefield: no objects → db unchanged (catches: crash on empty seq)
- Nil/no matching type: no creatures matching permanent-type → db unchanged (catches: wrong filter)
- Already all tapped: all matching objects tapped → db unchanged (catches: redundant state changes)

### :untap-all (~4 tests)
Production (zones.cljs:198-216): mirror of tap-all — filters TAPPED objects matching permanent-type, untaps them.
- Happy path: 2 tapped creatures + 1 untapped → only 2 get untapped (catches: filter not checking untapped state)
- Empty battlefield: no objects → db unchanged
- No tapped matching: all matching already untapped → db unchanged
- Consider doseq for tap-all/untap-all symmetric test pairs if clean

### :exile-zone (~4 tests)
Production (zones.cljs:113-123): exiles ALL objects in a specified zone for target player.
- Happy path: player has 3 cards in graveyard → all 3 moved to :exile (catches: zone move not applied to all objects)
- Empty zone: target zone has 0 objects → db unchanged (catches: crash on empty)
- Nil target player: nil :effect/target → db unchanged (catches: missing nil guard)
- Specific zone: :effect/zone :hand → exiles hand not graveyard (catches: wrong zone read)

### :create-token (~5 tests)
Production (tokens.cljs:14-43): creates a synthetic card entity AND object on battlefield with summoning sickness. Token def has :token/types, :token/name, :token/power, :token/toughness, :token/subtypes, :token/colors, :token/keywords.
- Happy path: create 1/1 white Soldier token → verify card entity exists, object on battlefield, power=1, toughness=1 (catches: entity creation missing, wrong zone)
- Summoning sickness: newly created token has :object/summoning-sick true (catches: missing summoning sickness flag)
- Token types: token has correct :card/types (catches: type not propagated)
- Token colors: token has correct :card/colors (catches: color not propagated)
- Nil token def: nil :effect/token → db unchanged (catches: crash on nil token)

### :apply-pt-modifier (~4 tests)
Production (pt_modifier.cljs:14-30): creates a PT grant with power/toughness deltas that expires at cleanup.
- Happy path: creature with P/T 2/2, apply +1/+1 modifier → P/T becomes 3/3 (catches: grant not applied, wrong delta)
- Negative modifier: apply -1/-1 → P/T becomes 1/1 (catches: sign handling)
- Nil target: nil :effect/target → db unchanged (catches: missing nil guard)
- Nonexistent target: random-uuid → db unchanged (catches: missing existence guard)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] 5 effect types have dedicated test groups appended to effects_test.cljs
- [ ] :tap-all has 4 tests including filter logic (skip already-tapped)
- [ ] :untap-all has 4 tests including filter logic (skip already-untapped)
- [ ] :exile-zone has 4 tests including empty zone and specific zone targeting
- [ ] :create-token has 5 tests including card entity + object + P/T + summoning sickness
- [ ] :apply-pt-modifier has 4 tests including positive and negative deltas
- [ ] All tests use fx/execute-effect as entry point
- [ ] No tautological assertions (exact values only)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + fmt-check + all tests green)

## Key Considerations

**:tap-all/:untap-all need board setup with multiple objects**
Use add-permanent or add-object-with-card (returns [obj-id db] NOT [db obj-id] — see Tier 1 learnings). Create 2-3 objects to test filtering. Set :object/tapped true via d/db-with on specific objects before testing.

**:create-token creates BOTH card entity AND object**
Token creation is more complex than zone moves. Verify both the card definition entity and the game object exist after execution. Check :card/types, :card/colors on the card entity. Check :object/zone, :object/power, :object/toughness, :object/summoning-sick on the object.

**:exile-zone uses :effect/zone to specify WHICH zone to exile**
Not just graveyard — test with different zone keywords to verify the zone parameter is respected.

**:apply-pt-modifier creates a grant, not a direct mutation**
The modifier creates a grant entity that adjusts P/T. Test the final P/T values on the object, not the grant internals.

**Use existing add-opponent helper for multi-player tests**
effects_test.cljs line 69 — uses d/conn-from-db (grandfathered).

## Anti-patterns (inherited from epic)
- ❌ NO direct defmethod calls (use fx/execute-effect)
- ❌ NO mutable conn pattern in NEW code (d/db-with; existing add-opponent grandfathered)
- ❌ NO tautological assertions (exact values only)
- ❌ NO copy-paste test variants (use doseq where parameter is the only difference)
close_reason: Closed

title: Investigate selection hierarchy long-term value
description: The selection-hierarchy in events/selection/core.cljs derives types into parent groups (zone-pick, accumulator, reorder, etc.) for multimethod dispatch. Executor layer gets minimal leverage (only :graveyard-return uses parent defmethod, all others override). Builder and chain layers get more value. Worth auditing whether the hierarchy is pulling its weight or adding indirection for little benefit. Emerged during fizzle-5xze spec brainstorm.

title: Separate player-id from effect target keywords (ADR-016)
description: The keyword :opponent is used as both a player-id and an effect target directive, causing silent collision bugs. ADR-016 decides to separate these namespaces. Choose a new opponent player-id that cannot collide with effect target keywords. Update all 26+ production files and 75 test files.

title: Task 1: Extend casting rules with alternate cost modes
design:
## Goal
Extend the casting system to support alternate casting modes. Cards can define `:card/alternate-costs` and the engine finds all valid modes based on card's current zone.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- `src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:16-29` - Current `can-cast?` implementation
- `src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:76-94` - Current `cast-spell` implementation  
- `src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs` - Cost checking/payment patterns (`:pay-life` already exists)
- `src/main/fizzle/engine/mana.cljs` - Mana payment patterns
- `src/test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs` - Existing test patterns to follow

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Add to `src/test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs`:

```clojure
;; === Alternate casting modes tests ===

;; get-casting-modes tests
(deftest get-casting-modes-primary-only-hand-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes returns primary mode for card in hand without alternates"
    ;; Setup: Dark Ritual in hand (no alternate costs)
    ;; Assert: Returns vector with one mode {:mode/id :primary ...}
    ;; Bug caught: If returns empty, cards can't be cast at all
    ))

(deftest get-casting-modes-no-modes-wrong-zone-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes returns empty for card in graveyard without flashback"
    ;; Setup: Dark Ritual in graveyard
    ;; Assert: Returns empty vector
    ;; Bug caught: If returns mode, all cards could be cast from graveyard
    ))

(deftest get-casting-modes-flashback-in-graveyard-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes returns flashback mode for flashback card in graveyard"
    ;; Setup: Deep Analysis (with flashback) in graveyard
    ;; Assert: Returns vector with flashback mode {:mode/id :flashback ...}
    ;; Bug caught: If returns empty, flashback cards can't be cast from GY
    ))

(deftest get-casting-modes-flashback-not-in-hand-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes does NOT return flashback mode when card in hand"
    ;; Setup: Deep Analysis in hand
    ;; Assert: Returns only primary mode, not flashback
    ;; Bug caught: If returns flashback, cards could pay flashback cost from hand
    ))

(deftest get-casting-modes-multiple-modes-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes returns both modes for card with hand-alternate"
    ;; Setup: Gush-like card with {:alternate/zone :hand} in hand
    ;; Assert: Returns two modes (primary + alternate)
    ;; Bug caught: If returns only one, player can't choose casting mode
    ))

;; can-cast-mode? tests
(deftest can-cast-mode-sufficient-mana-test
  (testing "can-cast-mode? returns true when player has sufficient mana"
    ;; Setup: Player has {B}, mode requires {B}
    ;; Assert: Returns true
    ;; Bug caught: Basic mana check regression
    ))

(deftest can-cast-mode-insufficient-mana-test
  (testing "can-cast-mode? returns false when player lacks mana"
    ;; Setup: Player has {}, mode requires {B}
    ;; Assert: Returns false
    ;; Bug caught: If true, spells cast without mana
    ))

(deftest can-cast-mode-with-life-cost-test
  (testing "can-cast-mode? checks additional life cost"
    ;; Setup: Player has 3+ life, mode has :pay-life 3
    ;; Assert: Returns true
    ;; Bug caught: If additional costs ignored, flashback is free
    ))

(deftest can-cast-mode-insufficient-life-test
  (testing "can-cast-mode? returns false when can't pay life cost"
    ;; Setup: Player has 2 life, mode has :pay-life 3
    ;; Assert: Returns false
    ;; Bug caught: If true, player can pay life they don't have
    ))

;; Edge case tests
(deftest get-casting-modes-nil-alternate-costs-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes handles nil alternate-costs gracefully"
    ;; Setup: Card without :card/alternate-costs key
    ;; Assert: Returns primary mode only, no error
    ;; Bug caught: NPE on cards without alternate costs
    ))

(deftest get-casting-modes-empty-alternate-costs-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes handles empty alternate-costs vector"
    ;; Setup: Card with :card/alternate-costs []
    ;; Assert: Returns primary mode only
    ;; Bug caught: Empty vector treated differently than nil
    ))

(deftest can-cast-mode-empty-mana-cost-test
  (testing "can-cast-mode? returns true for zero mana cost mode"
    ;; Setup: Mode with :mode/mana-cost {}
    ;; Assert: Returns true (free spell)
    ;; Bug caught: Empty cost map treated as unpayable
    ))

;; Backwards compatibility
(deftest can-cast-unchanged-for-existing-cards-test
  (testing "can-cast? behavior unchanged for cards without alternate costs"
    ;; Run same tests as existing can-cast tests
    ;; Bug caught: Regression in existing functionality
    ))
```

### 3. Implementation checklist

- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Add `get-primary-mode` helper (returns mode map for normal casting)
- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Add `get-alternate-modes` helper (filters alternates by current zone)
- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Add `get-casting-modes` function:
  ```clojure
  (defn get-casting-modes
    "Returns all valid casting modes for a card based on its current zone.
     Each mode is a map with :mode/id, :mode/mana-cost, :mode/additional-costs, :mode/on-resolve"
    [db player-id object-id]
    ...)
  ```
- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Add `can-cast-mode?` function:
  ```clojure
  (defn can-cast-mode?
    "Check if player can pay all costs for a specific casting mode.
     Checks mana cost AND all additional costs."
    [db player-id object-id mode]
    ...)
  ```
- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Update `can-cast?` to use `get-casting-modes`:
  ```clojure
  ;; Backwards compatible: true if ANY mode is castable
  (defn can-cast? [db player-id object-id]
    (let [modes (get-casting-modes db player-id object-id)]
      (some #(can-cast-mode? db player-id object-id %) modes)))
  ```
- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Add `get-castable-cards` function:
  ```clojure
  (defn get-castable-cards
    "Returns all cards (from hand and graveyard) with at least one valid casting mode."
    [db player-id]
    ...)
  ```
- [ ] `rules_test.cljs` - All 12+ tests listed above

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `get-casting-modes` returns primary mode for hand cards
- [ ] `get-casting-modes` returns flashback mode for graveyard cards with flashback
- [ ] `get-casting-modes` returns empty vector for cards in wrong zone without alternates
- [ ] `get-casting-modes` returns multiple modes when applicable (Gush pattern)
- [ ] `can-cast-mode?` validates mana cost via `mana/can-pay?`
- [ ] `can-cast-mode?` validates additional costs via `costs/can-pay?`
- [ ] `can-cast-mode?` returns false when any cost is unpayable
- [ ] Existing `can-cast?` tests pass unchanged (backwards compatible)
- [ ] All 12+ new tests pass: `make test`
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass: `make validate`

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Card without mana cost**
- Some cards may have nil or missing `:card/mana-cost`
- Treat as zero cost `{}` (e.g., Lotus Petal pattern)
- Test: `get-casting-modes-nil-mana-cost-test`

**Edge Case: Empty alternate-costs**
- Card may have `:card/alternate-costs []` (empty vector)
- Must return primary mode only, not fail
- Treat same as nil

**Edge Case: Unknown additional cost type**
- If `:cost/type` not recognized by `costs/can-pay?`
- Current costs.cljs throws - let it bubble up (fail-fast)
- Document this behavior, fix in future task if needed

**Edge Case: Invalid player-id**
- If player-id doesn't exist, return empty modes
- Follow pattern from existing `can-cast?` which returns nil/false

**Backwards Compatibility**
- Existing cards have no `:card/alternate-costs` key
- `get-casting-modes` must handle missing key gracefully
- All existing `can-cast?` tests must continue passing

**Reference Implementation**
- Study `rules.cljs:16-29` for `can-cast?` pattern
- Study `costs.cljs:167-206` for `:pay-life` pattern

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO hardcoding specific cards (Deep Analysis, Gush) - use generic alternate-costs structure
- ❌ NO breaking existing can-cast? tests - backwards compatibility required
- ❌ NO adding new effect types - this task is casting rules only
- ❌ NO modifying stack resolution - that's a separate task
- ❌ NO modifying UI - this task is engine only
close_reason: Implemented get-casting-modes, can-cast-mode?, get-castable-cards. All 15 new tests pass. Pre-commit hooks pass.

title: Separate bot action stops from human opponent-turn stops — data model brainstorm needed
description: Bot-derived stops (from phase-actions) and human opponent-turn stops are conflated on the same :player/stops attribute on the bot entity. The toggle-stop UI handler overwrites the whole set, wiping bot-derived stops when the user modifies their opponent-turn preferences. Needs a brainstorm to design the right data model before implementation.
notes: REQUIRES /brainstorm before implementation. Key tension: :player/stops on the bot entity serves two purposes — (1) where advance-with-stops pauses for the bot to act, and (2) the human's opponent-turn stop preferences for UI display. toggle-stop in events/ui.cljs:66-81 replaces the whole set. Possible approaches: separate attributes (:player/bot-stops vs :player/opponent-stops on human entity), check both players' stops in advance-with-stops, or protect bot-derived stops from toggle. Each has trade-offs for the schema, UI subscriptions, storage, and sharing/restorer.
close_reason: Brainstorm complete — design captured in epic fizzle-53sy, now implemented.

title: Add property-based tests: chokepoint acceptance + rejection
description: Add property-based tests using test.check generators: chokepoint acceptance (generated valid data passes through actual validation chokepoints) and rejection (generated data with required keys removed fails validation). Final task to complete epic.
design:
## Goal
Complete the property-based testing suite. Roundtrip tests already exist (s/exercise produces valid data). Add chokepoint acceptance tests (generated data passes through production chokepoints without error) and rejection tests (intentionally broken data is caught by specs).

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing generative tests
Read src/test/fizzle/engine/spec_generative_test.cljs — has 4 roundtrip tests.
Read src/test/fizzle/engine/spec_generators.cljs — has generators for all specs including new mana/object specs.

### 2. Study chokepoint functions
- src/main/fizzle/engine/spec_util.cljs — validate-at-chokepoint! (throws on invalid, gated by goog.DEBUG)
- src/main/fizzle/engine/stack.cljs — create-stack-item calls validate-stack-item!
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection/core.cljs — set-pending-selection (console.error pattern)
- src/main/fizzle/engine/mana_spec.cljs — validate-mana-add-arg!, validate-mana-pay-arg!
- src/main/fizzle/engine/object_spec.cljs — validate-object-tx!
- src/main/fizzle/bots/decisions.cljs — validate-bot-action!

### 3. Write chokepoint acceptance tests
For each chokepoint, generate valid data using spec generators and pass it through the ACTUAL validation function:

- Generate valid stack-item maps -> pass through validate-stack-item! -> no throw
- Generate valid mana-add maps -> pass through validate-mana-add-arg! -> no throw
- Generate valid mana-pay maps -> pass through validate-mana-pay-arg! -> no throw
- Generate valid object-tx maps -> pass through validate-object-tx! -> no throw
- Generate valid bot-action maps -> pass through validate-bot-action! -> no throw
- Generate valid selection maps -> pass through set-pending-selection validation -> no error

These tests catch the bug where a generator and spec agree but the chokepoint function has additional validation logic beyond the spec.

### 4. Write rejection tests
For each multi-spec, generate valid data then systematically break it:

- Remove a required key -> s/valid? returns false
- Change dispatch key to invalid value -> s/valid? returns false
- Replace a typed field with wrong type (string where int expected) -> s/valid? returns false

Pattern:
(deftest test-effect-rejects-missing-type
  (let [[valid _] (first (s/exercise :fizzle.engine.card-spec/effect 1))
        broken (dissoc valid :effect/type)]
    (is (not (s/valid? :fizzle.engine.card-spec/effect broken)))))

These tests catch vacuously true specs that accept everything.

### 5. Verify
- make validate passes
- All property tests pass
- Rejection tests actually reject (not vacuously passing)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Chokepoint acceptance tests for all 6 validation chokepoints (stack, selection, mana-add, mana-pay, object-tx, bot-action)
- [ ] Rejection tests for all 4 multi-specs (effects, selections, stack items, bot actions) + 3 boundary specs (mana-add, mana-pay, object-tx)
- [ ] Rejection tests verify: missing required key, wrong dispatch type, wrong field type
- [ ] No vacuously true specs (rejection tests actually fail when spec is broken)
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] All existing tests pass (no regressions)

## Key Considerations

**Edge Case: Selection chokepoint uses console.error not throw**
set-pending-selection logs a console.error instead of throwing. The acceptance test may need to verify no error was logged (or just verify the selection was set on the db). Check the actual validation pattern.

**Edge Case: Chokepoint functions may need a db argument**
create-stack-item needs a Datascript db. validate-mana-add-arg! and validate-object-tx! may just need the data map. Check each function's signature to determine what test setup is needed.

**Edge Case: Generated data may need db context**
Some chokepoints validate data in context of a game database (e.g., create-stack-item transacts into db). Tests may need a minimal test db. Use test helper patterns from existing tests.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO vacuously true specs (rejection tests MUST actually fail when given broken data)
- ❌ NO testing mocks instead of production chokepoints (acceptance tests call REAL validation functions)
- ❌ NO skipping any chokepoint (all 6 must have acceptance tests)
close_reason: 20 property-based tests: 6 chokepoint acceptance + 14 rejection, 3286 tests pass

title: Fix :draw effect to resolve :opponent target
description: The :draw effect in effects.cljs doesn't resolve symbolic :opponent target (unlike :mill, :discard-hand). Add :opponent resolution so draw can target the opponent player. Required by Words of Wisdom.
design:
## Fix :draw effect to resolve :opponent target

### Goal
Add :opponent target resolution to :draw effect (matching :mill pattern at effects.cljs:195-198).

### Codebase Verification
- ✓ :draw at effects.cljs:315-355 does NOT resolve :opponent
