title: Feature: Add view-layer verification to implement-card skill
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Add a View-Layer Verification subsection to Phase 5 of the implement-card skill
- The section MUST be conditional: only triggers when a card introduces a new selection type
- The section MUST check three things: render-selection-modal defmethod, ::selection-cards subscription card-source, and modal rendering
- Include explicit file paths (views/modals.cljs, subs/game.cljs) so implementers know where to look
- Include common selection-type-to-modal mappings as quick reference

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Phase 5 of implement-card skill contains a View-Layer Verification subsection
- [ ] The subsection has a clear trigger condition (new selection type not in existing defmethod list)
- [ ] Checklist includes: defmethod exists, card-source handled, modal renders correctly
- [ ] File paths reference views/modals.cljs and subs/game.cljs
- [ ] Common selection-type-to-modal mapping table included
- [ ] Skill reads naturally and flows with existing Phase 5 content
- [ ] make validate passes (no broken references)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO always-run checklist items for all cards (noise: most cards don't introduce selection types, conditional only)
- NO restructuring existing phases (scope: this is an additive change to Phase 5 only)
- NO removing or modifying existing verification checklist items (preservation: existing checks are correct)
- NO view-layer checks beyond selection types (scope: broader view checks are out of scope per design decision)

## Approach
Add a conditional View-Layer Verification subsection to Phase 5 of the implement-card skill at .claude/skills/implement-card/SKILL.md. Place it after the existing verification checklist, before the Final Validation section. The subsection triggers only when a card introduces a new :selection/type value. It checks three things: (1) render-selection-modal defmethod exists in views/modals.cljs, (2) ::selection-cards subscription handles the card-source in subs/game.cljs, (3) modal renders correctly with valid-targets filtering. Include a mapping table of common selection types to their expected modal components.

## Architecture
Single file change: .claude/skills/implement-card/SKILL.md
No code changes — this is a skill/documentation update only.

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The implement-card skill has no view-layer checks in Phase 5 verification. When a card introduces a new selection type (e.g., :hand-reveal-discard for Duress, :chain-bounce for Chain of Vapor), nothing prompts verifying that the view layer supports it. This caused Chain of Vapor's chain-bounce selection to ship without a proper modal defmethod, falling through to the generic default.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- views/modals.cljs:823-896 — render-selection-modal multimethod with 15 defmethods
- subs/game.cljs:379-422 — ::selection-cards subscription with 6 card-source cases
- Duress (:hand-reveal-discard) — HAS full view support (defmethod + subscription + modal)
- Chain of Vapor (:chain-bounce, :chain-bounce-target) — MISSING defmethods, falls to :default

**Pattern:**
Each new selection type needs: effect type → selection builder → execute handler → view defmethod → subscription handler (if new card-source)

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Conditional subsection in Phase 5 ✓
**What it is:** Add view-layer verification as conditional checklist items in Phase 5, triggered only when card introduces a new selection type.
**Chosen because:** Matches natural verification flow, minimal skill restructuring, conditional avoids noise for simple cards.

#### 2. View-layer in Phase 3 TDD loop ❌
**What it is:** Add view-layer checks during implementation phase.
**Why explored:** Catches issues earlier.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Phase 3 is already the longest section; selection types are rare; view rendering isn't easily unit-tested in this project.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** The project adds view-layer unit testing infrastructure.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Selection type defmethod verification
- Card-source subscription handling
- Modal rendering verification
- Common mapping reference table

**Out of scope:**
- Broader view-layer concerns (card type rendering, zone display)
- View-layer unit tests
- Fixing existing Chain of Vapor view-layer gap (separate issue)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Selection types only or broader view-layer? | Selection types only | Checklist limited to defmethods + card-source |
| Conditional or always-run? | Conditional section | Trigger condition needed, skip for simple cards |

### Research Deep-Dives
#### View-Layer Selection Architecture
**Question explored:** How does the selection modal system work end-to-end?
**Sources consulted:**
- views/modals.cljs — 15 defmethods for render-selection-modal
- subs/game.cljs — 6 card-source cases in ::selection-cards
- events/selection/zone_ops.cljs — selection builders

**Findings:**
- Pattern: card effect → selection builder → selection state → subscription → view defmethod
- modal-dispatch-key function handles special cases (player targeting alias)
- :default fallback renders card-selection-modal (generic grid)

**Conclusion:** Three checkpoints needed: defmethod, card-source, modal rendering

### Dead-End Paths
None — straightforward additive change.

### Open Concerns Raised
None raised.
close_reason: Closed

title: Reactive bot priority: consult bot protocol before auto-passing in negotiate-priority
description: Reactive bot priority epic: consult bot protocol before auto-passing in negotiate-priority. Enables future reactive archetypes (counterspells, instant-speed interaction) without implementing them. Single change point: negotiate-priority condition at game.cljs:896-897.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
1. negotiate-priority must consult bot-priority-decision before auto-passing bot opponents
2. If bot protocol returns :pass, auto-pass bot (preserves current behavior for goldfish/burn)
3. If bot protocol returns an action, transfer priority to bot instead of auto-passing (enables future reactive archetypes)
4. Existing goldfish behavior unchanged (empty rules → always :pass → auto-passed)
5. Existing burn bot behavior unchanged (stack-empty condition means only acts on own turn empty stack)
6. No changes to the interceptor action dispatch flow — it already handles bot-has-priority correctly

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Bot with reactive priority rules (e.g. :stack-has :owner :opponent condition) gets priority transferred when human yields with items on stack
- [ ] Bot with empty priority rules (goldfish) is still auto-passed
- [ ] Bot with rules that don't match current state is still auto-passed
- [ ] Existing burn bot behavior unchanged on bot's turn (stack-empty means only acts on empty stack)
- [ ] negotiate-priority calls bots/protocol for decision (gameOrchestration → opponentAi dependency, already declared in model.c4)
- [ ] interceptor.cljs header comment updated (no longer fully bot-unaware)
- [ ] All existing tests pass — zero regressions
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO duplicating action dispatch logic in negotiate-priority (it only checks pass/act — interceptor dispatches actions via build-bot-dispatches)
- ❌ NO new bot archetypes in this epic (just the priority mechanism change)
- ❌ NO stack-item targeting (out of scope — future epic for control archetype)
- ❌ NO counter-spell effect type (out of scope — future epic)
- ❌ NO modifying the interceptor's action dispatch flow (it already handles bot-has-priority correctly)
- ❌ NO mocking bot-priority-decision in tests (test through real bot specs with real condition evaluation)
- ❌ NO adding bot protocol calls anywhere except negotiate-priority (single point of integration)

## Approach
Add a bot protocol check inside negotiate-priority before auto-passing bot opponents. When the human yields and the opponent is a bot, call bot-priority-decision with current game state. If the protocol returns :pass, auto-pass as before. If it returns an action map, transfer priority to the bot instead — the existing interceptor will fire after the yield event, detect the bot has priority, and handle action dispatch through the standard pipeline.

This is the minimal change that enables future reactive bot archetypes (counterspells, instant-speed discard) without implementing any of those archetypes. The double-evaluation (negotiate-priority checks once, interceptor checks again) is cheap and maintains independence between the two code paths.

## Architecture
### Files changed
- events/game.cljs: add require for bots/protocol, add bot-would-pass? helper, modify negotiate-priority condition at L896-897
- interceptor.cljs: update header comment about bot-unaware principle

### New dependency
- events/game.cljs → bots/protocol.cljs (first bot import in event layer)
- Corresponds to declared model.c4 edge: gameOrchestration → opponentAi

### Unchanged
- bots/interceptor.cljs logic (already handles bot-has-priority)
- bots/protocol.cljs (no API changes)
- bots/rules.cljs (no changes)
- bots/definitions.cljs (no changes)
- engine/priority.cljs (no changes)

## Architecture Context
- Component: Opponent AI (engine layer, stability: exploring)
- Component: Game Orchestration (manager layer, stability: exploring)
- Dependency: gameOrchestration -[blocks]-> opponentAi (declared in model.c4)
- Relevant ADR: ADR-008 (split Opponent AI as independent engine component)
- Key data flow: human yield → negotiate-priority → bot protocol check → auto-pass or transfer
- Proposed dynamic view: None (modifies existing yield flow, doesn't create new cross-component path)

## Architecture Update Checklist
1. Did this work change a component's public interface? → No (bots/protocol API unchanged)
2. Did this work add or remove a dependency between components? → No (dependency already declared)
3. Did this work create a new component or remove an existing one? → No
4. Did this work move responsibility from one component to another? → Partially (negotiate-priority now calls into Opponent AI, but the dependency was designed for this)
5. Did this work create a new request path through 2+ components? → No (modifies existing yield flow)

## Design Rationale
### Problem
negotiate-priority (game.cljs:896-897) unconditionally auto-passes bots when a human yields. The condition `(player-is-bot? gdb opp-eid)` at L897 means bots never get priority to respond to human spells on the stack. This is fine for goldfish and burn (which never respond), but blocks any future reactive bot archetype (control with counterspells, discard with instant-speed interaction).

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- events/game.cljs:864-916 — negotiate-priority pure function with 4-condition auto-pass logic
- events/game.cljs:896-897 — the specific unconditional bot auto-pass line
- bots/protocol.cljs:26-34 — bot-priority-decision: archetype + context → :pass or action map
- bots/rules.cljs:147-158 — match-priority-rule: iterate rules, evaluate conditions, resolve first match
- bots/interceptor.cljs:140-172 — bot-action-interceptor fires after game events, checks bot-should-act?
- bots/interceptor.cljs:191-235 — bot-decide-handler: phase action → priority decision → dispatch
- bots/definitions.cljs:9-17 — goldfish-spec: empty priority-rules (always passes)
- bots/definitions.cljs:20-30 — burn-spec: priority-rules with stack-empty condition (only acts on empty stack)
- bots/rules.cljs:93-104 — :stack-has condition evaluator already exists (checks stack for item type + owner)

**Architecture:**
- model.c4: gameOrchestration -[blocks]-> opponentAi (dependency declared)
- arch/components/opponent-ai.c4: engine layer, stability exploring
- ADR-008: Opponent AI split out as independent engine component

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Full protocol eval in negotiate-priority ✓
**What it is:** Call bot-priority-decision with current game state before auto-passing. Use the actual protocol result (:pass or action) to decide.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed bot-priority-decision API: takes archetype + {:db :player-id} → :pass or action map
- Confirmed dependency declared in model.c4
- Verified interceptor handles bot-has-priority correctly (no changes needed)
- Confirmed double-evaluation (negotiate + interceptor) is cheap (iterate EDN, check datascript)

**Pros:**
- Correct: uses same decision logic as interceptor (same rules, same evaluation)
- Simple: one condition change + one helper function
- Architecturally sound: dependency already declared

**Cons:**
- Breaks 'game events remain bot-unaware' comment (needs update)
- Double-evaluates when bot wants to act (cheap, acceptable)

**Chosen because:** Most correct approach — same protocol, same rules. Architecture supports it.

#### 2. Rule existence check ❌
**What it is:** Check if bot spec has non-empty :bot/priority-rules. Non-empty → transfer priority. Empty → auto-pass.

**Why we looked at this:** Cheaper than full evaluation — no condition matching needed.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed burn-spec: has priority-rules with :stack-empty condition
- Burn only acts on empty stack — would get priority when stack isn't empty (unnecessary overhead)
- Goldfish still optimized (empty rules)

**Pros:**
- Cheaper evaluation (just check seq of rules)
- Goldfish perfectly optimized

**Cons:**
- Too coarse: burn bot gets priority every time human yields, only to pass back
- Extra yield dispatches for burn bot on every human spell

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Coarse check causes unnecessary priority transfers for burn bot. Full eval is cheap enough.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Performance profiling shows bot-priority-decision is a bottleneck (extremely unlikely with EDN rule evaluation).

#### 3. No auto-pass for bots at all ❌
**What it is:** Remove all bot auto-pass logic. Always transfer priority. Let interceptor handle everything.

**Why we looked at this:** Cleanest separation — negotiate-priority stays fully bot-unaware.

**Investigation:**
- Goldfish: empty rules → interceptor fires → bot-decide → :pass → dispatches ::yield
- This happens on EVERY priority pass during human's turn
- Extra events in history, more dispatches per turn

**Pros:**
- negotiate-priority remains fully bot-unaware (no bot import)
- Simplest code change (just remove bot conditions)

**Cons:**
- Goldfish generates extra yield dispatches on every priority pass
- More events in history log
- Slower for the common case (goldfish is default opponent)

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Adds overhead to the most common case (goldfish). Full protocol eval in negotiate-priority avoids unnecessary dispatches for bots that always pass.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** All bot archetypes are reactive (no goldfish equivalent), making the optimization unnecessary.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- negotiate-priority bot protocol check
- Tests with reactive bot specs
- Comment updates

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Stack item targeting (:target/type :stack-item) — future epic
- Counter-spell effect type — future epic
- Control bot archetype — future epic
- Counterspell card definition — future epic
- New bot archetypes — future epics

### Open Questions
- Should bot-would-pass? be a private helper in game.cljs or a public function in protocol.cljs? (decide during implementation — leaning toward game.cljs private since it's the only caller)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| How should negotiate-priority decide whether to auto-pass a bot? | Full protocol eval | Call bot-priority-decision, check :pass vs action. Import bots/protocol in game.cljs. |
| Any concerns with design? | Looks good, create the epic | Proceed with implementation. |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### negotiate-priority Auto-Pass Logic
**Question explored:** How does the current auto-pass work and where exactly is the problem?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/game.cljs:864-916 — full negotiate-priority function
- events/game.cljs:896-897 — the unconditional bot auto-pass condition

**Findings:**
- 4-condition OR for should-auto-pass-opponent?
- Condition at L897: (player-is-bot? gdb opp-eid) — unconditional, no game state check
- Bot never gets priority transferred during human's turn
- Interceptor fires after events but priority already resolved

**Conclusion:** Single condition change at L897 fixes the problem.

#### Bot Interceptor Already Handles Bot Priority
**Question explored:** Does the interceptor need changes to handle reactive bots?
**Sources consulted:**
- bots/interceptor.cljs:140-172 — bot-action-interceptor
- bots/interceptor.cljs:191-235 — bot-decide-handler

**Findings:**
- Interceptor fires after ::yield event in :after phase
- Checks bot-should-act? (is priority holder a bot?)
- If yes, dispatches ::bot-decide which runs full decision logic
- Already handles phase actions + priority decisions + tap sequences
- No changes needed for reactive bots

**Conclusion:** Interceptor already supports the reactive flow. Only negotiate-priority needs fixing.

#### :stack-has Condition Already Exists
**Question explored:** Can bot rules already express 'opponent has a spell on the stack'?
**Sources consulted:**
- bots/rules.cljs:93-104 — :stack-has condition evaluator

**Findings:**
- :stack-has checks for stack items by type and owner
- Format: {:check :stack-has :type :spell :owner :opponent}
- Already resolves :opponent to actual player-id via context
- Complete — no changes needed for reactive bot specs

**Conclusion:** The condition system already supports reactive rules. Only the priority mechanism blocks them.

### Dead-End Paths
None — all three approaches were viable, just with different tradeoffs.

### Open Concerns Raised
- 'Bot-unaware principle' in interceptor.cljs → Acknowledged tension, updating comment. The principle served its purpose when bots only acted on their own turn. Reactive bots require the orchestration layer to consult bot decisions.
close_reason: Implemented: negotiate-priority now consults bot protocol before auto-passing. Commit cfa6e9c.

title: Add must-contain state management to setup events and subscriptions
design:
## Goal
Add must-contain card selection state to the setup system: events for toggling/clearing must-contain cards, subscriptions for the UI, and preset persistence. No UI changes yet — this task delivers the pure event handlers and subscriptions that the chip picker UI will consume.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (pure handler functions + subscriptions + tests)

## Implementation

1. Study existing setup patterns:
   - events/setup.cljs:38-49 — init-setup-handler pattern (db -> db)
   - events/setup.cljs:65-75 — move-to-side-handler pattern (db, card-id -> db, reads deck from db)
   - events/setup.cljs:97-111 — save-preset-handler (preset config structure)
   - events/setup.cljs:114-124 — load-preset-handler
   - subs/setup.cljs:68-88 — derived subscription pattern (current-main-grouped)

2. Write tests first (TDD) in src/test/fizzle/events/setup_test.cljs (extend existing):
   - toggle-must-contain-handler: card not in must-contain -> adds with count 1
   - toggle-must-contain-handler: card at count 1 -> increments to 2
   - toggle-must-contain-handler: card at max copies (e.g., 4 Dark Rituals) -> cycles to 0 (removes)
   - toggle-must-contain-handler: card-id not in main deck -> no-op
   - toggle-must-contain-handler: total must-contain already 7 and adding new card -> no-op
   - toggle-must-contain-handler: total must-contain 6, card at count 1 -> allows increment to 2 only if total stays <= 7
   - clear-must-contain-handler: resets to empty map
   - init-setup-handler: result includes :setup/must-contain {}
   - save-preset-handler: saved config map includes :must-contain key
   - load-preset-handler: restores must-contain from preset config
   - load-preset-handler: defaults to {} when preset has no :must-contain key (backward compat with old presets)
   - move-to-side-handler: when card moved to sideboard reduces main count below must-contain count, must-contain count is clamped (e.g., 4 DR in main, must-contain 3, move 2 to side -> must-contain clamped to 2)
   - Subscription test: must-contain-cards returns vec of maps with shape {:card/id :dark-ritual :card/name "Dark Ritual" :count 2 :max-count 4}

3. Implementation checklist:
   - [ ] events/setup.cljs — add toggle-must-contain-handler (db, card-id) -> db
         Reads :setup/main-deck and :setup/must-contain from db
         Finds max copies of card-id in main deck
         If card-id not in main deck: return db unchanged
         If not in must-contain: add with count 1 (if total < 7)
         If count < max AND total < 7: increment count
         If count = max OR would exceed 7: remove from must-contain (cycle to 0)
   - [ ] events/setup.cljs — add clear-must-contain-handler (db) -> db
         Returns (assoc db :setup/must-contain {})
   - [ ] events/setup.cljs — extend init-setup-handler to include :setup/must-contain {}
   - [ ] events/setup.cljs — extend stash-setup-config to include :setup/must-contain
   - [ ] events/setup.cljs — extend save-preset-handler config map: add :must-contain key
   - [ ] events/setup.cljs — extend load-preset-handler: read :must-contain from config, default to {}
   - [ ] events/setup.cljs — extend move-to-side-handler: after moving card, clamp must-contain count if it exceeds new main-deck count for that card
   - [ ] events/setup.cljs — register ::toggle-must-contain and ::clear-must-contain rf/reg-event-db
   - [ ] subs/setup.cljs — add ::must-contain subscription: (fn [db _] (:setup/must-contain db))
   - [ ] subs/setup.cljs — add ::must-contain-cards derived subscription:
         Input signals: ::must-contain, ::current-main
         Returns: vec of {:card/id kw, :card/name str, :count int, :max-count int}
         for each entry in must-contain map, enriched with card name from card-lookup
   - [ ] subs/setup.cljs — extend ::setup-config select-keys to include :setup/must-contain

## Success Criteria
- [ ] toggle-must-contain cycles count 0->1->...->max->0 for a card
- [ ] toggle-must-contain is no-op for card-id not in main deck
- [ ] Total must-contain capped at 7 (global cap across all cards)
- [ ] move-to-side clamps must-contain count when main deck copies decrease
- [ ] Must-contain persists in presets (save includes, load restores)
- [ ] Loading old presets without must-contain key defaults to {} (backward compat)
- [ ] Init includes :setup/must-contain {}
- [ ] ::must-contain-cards subscription returns vec of {:card/id :card/name :count :max-count}
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (no regressions)
- [ ] make validate passing (lint + fmt + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Sideboard Swap Invalidation**
When move-to-side reduces main deck copies of a card below its must-contain count, the must-contain count MUST be clamped. Example: 4 Dark Rituals in main, must-contain 3, move 2 to side -> main has 2, must-contain must clamp to 2. The move-to-side-handler already exists and must be extended — NOT a separate event.

**Edge Case: Card Not In Deck**
toggle-must-contain with a card-id not in :setup/main-deck must be a no-op. This prevents stale state if UI and deck state get out of sync.

**Edge Case: Backward Compatible Preset Loading**
Old presets saved before this feature won't have a :must-contain key. load-preset-handler must default to {} when key is missing. Use (get config :must-contain {}).

**Edge Case: Global Cap Interaction with Increment**
When total must-contain is 6 and player clicks a card already at count 1, increment to 2 would bring total to 7 (allowed). But if total is 7, clicking any card should cycle it to 0 (remove), not try to add. The toggle logic must check global cap BEFORE incrementing.

**Edge Case: Deck Change Resets Must-Contain**
When select-deck-handler changes the deck (e.g., from iggy-pop to another), must-contain should reset to {} since card availability changes entirely. Currently only iggy-pop exists, but design for correctness.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO storing must-contain as a set (must be map of card-id -> count to support multi-copy sculpting)
- NO separate must-contain validation pass (inline validation in toggle handler — keep it simple)
- NO must-contain state outside app-db (no atoms, no separate storage — lives in re-frame db)
- NO breaking existing preset save/load format (additive only — old presets must still load)
close_reason: Closed

title: Migrate protocol from multimethods to spec-based functions
description:
Replace the 3 multimethods in protocol.cljs with regular functions that look up bot specs and delegate to the rules engine. Public API signatures stay the same so interceptor needs zero changes.

## Modify: src/main/fizzle/bots/protocol.cljs

### Changes:
- Add requires: fizzle.bots.rules, fizzle.bots.definitions
- Remove defmulti/defmethod for bot-priority-decision → replace with defn:
  (defn bot-priority-decision [archetype context]
    (let [spec (definitions/get-spec archetype)]
      (if spec
        (rules/match-priority-rule (:bot/priority-rules spec) context)
        :pass)))

- Remove defmulti/defmethod for bot-phase-action → replace with defn:
  (defn bot-phase-action [archetype phase _db _player-id]
    (let [spec (definitions/get-spec archetype)]
      (if spec
        {:action (rules/get-phase-action spec phase)}
        {:action :pass})))

- Remove defmulti/defmethod for bot-deck → replace with defn:
  (defn bot-deck [archetype]
    (or (definitions/get-deck archetype)
        (definitions/get-deck :goldfish)))  ;; default fallback

- Keep get-bot-archetype unchanged (still reads from Datascript)
- Delete has-untapped-red-source? (moved to rules.cljs condition evaluator)
- Delete get-other-player-id (action resolver handles this)

## Modify: src/test/fizzle/bots/protocol_test.cljs
- All existing tests should pass WITHOUT changes since public API is identical
- If any test directly tests defmethod dispatch, update to test function call instead
- The test inputs/outputs are the same — only internals changed

## Modify: src/main/fizzle/bots/interceptor.cljs
- Should require zero changes (calls bot/bot-priority-decision which has same signature)
- Verify: interceptor_test.cljs and burn_integration_test.cljs pass unchanged

## Delete: src/main/fizzle/bots/actions.cljs (unused auto-tap utility)
## Delete: src/test/fizzle/bots/actions_test.cljs (tests for deleted file)

## Update: src/main/fizzle/events/setup.cljs
- Replace bot/bot-deck call with definitions/get-deck
- Or keep using bot/bot-deck since it now delegates to definitions

## Verification
make validate — ALL existing bot tests must pass
close_reason: Closed

title: Selection system refactoring: lifecycle, modal unification, pre-cast pipeline
description: Three-phase refactoring of the selection system implementing ADR-013 (builder-declared lifecycle), ADR-014 (unify modal spell selection), and ADR-015 (data-driven pre-cast pipeline). Executed in dependency order: lifecycle first, then modal, then pipeline.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

### Phase 1: ADR-013 — Builder-declared selection lifecycle
1. Selection builders declare lifecycle via `:selection/lifecycle` key with values `:standard`, `:finalized`, or `:chaining`
2. `:standard` is the default lifecycle — omitting `:selection/lifecycle` means standard
3. All executors return only `{:db db}` — no `:finalized?` or `:pending-selection` in return maps
4. `confirm-selection-impl` reads lifecycle from the selection map, not from executor return shape
5. A `build-chain-selection` multimethod dispatching on `:selection/type` handles chaining — framework calls it after `:chaining` executors complete, it returns next selection map or nil

### Phase 2: ADR-014 — Unify modal spell selection
6. Modal spell selection becomes `:selection/type :spell-mode` under the standard selection system
7. `:game/pending-spell-mode-selection` app-db key is eliminated
8. `::select-spell-mode` event handler is eliminated
9. Mode objects are candidates with `:selection/select-count 1` and `:selection/auto-confirm? true`
10. Per-mode targeting evaluation moves from game.cljs to the spell-mode builder

### Phase 3: ADR-015 — Data-driven pre-cast pipeline
11. `initiate-cast-with-mode` conditional cascade replaced by pipeline loop over ordered keyword vector
12. Each pre-cast step is a `defmethod` on `evaluate-pre-cast-step` multimethod
13. Pipeline ordering is an explicit vector of step keywords
14. Steps return nil (skip), `{:selection sel}` (pause for input), or `{:db db}` (continue)
15. Pre-cast chaining (cost → targeting → mana-allocation) replaced by pipeline resumption

### Cross-cutting
16. Card definitions (EDN data) are unaffected by all changes
17. Post-resolution chaining (tutor → pile-choice, peek-and-select → order-bottom) uses `build-chain-selection`

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] All 21+ executors return only `{:db db}`
- [ ] All builders with non-standard lifecycle declare `:selection/lifecycle`
- [ ] `confirm-selection-impl` has zero executor return-shape inspection
- [ ] `build-chain-selection` handles tutor → pile-choice and peek-and-select → order-bottom chaining
- [ ] Modal spells flow through `:game/pending-selection` as `:selection/type :spell-mode`
- [ ] Zero references to `:game/pending-spell-mode-selection` in codebase
- [ ] Zero references to `::select-spell-mode` event handler in codebase
- [ ] `initiate-cast-with-mode` is a pipeline loop (~15 lines), not 110-line cascade
- [ ] Each pre-cast step is independently testable as a `defmethod`
- [ ] Pre-cast pipeline ordering is a data vector, not branch placement
- [ ] All existing tests pass (no regressions)
- [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO executor return-shape inspection in confirm-selection-impl (ADR-013: lifecycle is builder-declared, not executor-signaled — the whole point of the refactor)
- ❌ NO parallel mechanisms for player input (ADR-014: everything flows through `:game/pending-selection`, no second key)
- ❌ NO conditional cascade in pre-cast pipeline (ADR-015: use multimethod dispatch, not if/when branches)
- ❌ NO functions stored in selection maps (data over code principle — use multimethods for chain building, not embedded fns)
- ❌ NO card definition changes (these are orchestration-internal refactors — card EDN is stable)
- ❌ NO new app-db keys for selection state (single `:game/pending-selection` key for all player input)
- ❌ NO big-bang refactoring (migrate executors incrementally — each migration is independently testable and commitable)

## Approach

Three-phase refactoring of the selection system, executed in dependency order:

**Phase 1 (ADR-013):** Add `:selection/lifecycle` metadata to selection maps. Introduce `build-chain-selection` multimethod for chaining. Migrate 6 finalized executors and 7 chaining executors to return only `{:db db}`. Remove return-shape inspection from `confirm-selection-impl`. Standard executors (9) need no changes.

**Phase 2 (ADR-014):** Create `:spell-mode` selection type with builder and executor. Mode objects as candidates, select-count 1, auto-confirm true. Lifecycle is `:finalized` with continuation to trigger `initiate-cast-with-mode`. Eliminate `:game/pending-spell-mode-selection`, `::select-spell-mode` event handler, and `spell-mode-selector-modal` view. Update selection rendering to handle mode-type candidates.

**Phase 3 (ADR-015):** Define pre-cast pipeline as ordered keyword vector. Implement `evaluate-pre-cast-step` multimethod with methods for each step (exile-cards-cost, return-land-cost, discard-specific-cost, x-mana-cost, targeting, mana-allocation). Replace 110-line cascade with ~15-line pipeline loop. Store pipeline position for resumption after selection completion. Pre-cast chaining from Phase 1 becomes pipeline resumption. Spell-mode from Phase 2 becomes first pipeline step.

## Architecture

**Files modified (primary):**
- `events/selection/core.cljs` — `confirm-selection-impl` rewrite, `build-chain-selection` multimethod
- `events/selection/costs.cljs` — 4 builders + 4 executors (lifecycle metadata, return simplification)
- `events/selection/targeting.cljs` — 2 builders + 2 executors
- `events/selection/library.cljs` — 3 builders + 3 executors
- `events/selection/storm.cljs` — 1 builder + 1 executor
- `events/game.cljs` — spell-mode flow, `initiate-cast-with-mode` pipeline rewrite
- `views/modals.cljs` — remove `spell-mode-selector-modal`, update selection rendering

**New constructs:**
- `build-chain-selection` multimethod (core.cljs or new file)
- `:spell-mode` builder + executor (new selection module or game.cljs)
- `evaluate-pre-cast-step` multimethod (game.cljs or new file)
- `pre-cast-pipeline` vector (game.cljs)

**Unchanged:**
- `events/selection/zone_ops.cljs` — all standard lifecycle, no changes needed
- All card definition files — EDN data unaffected
- `engine/` — resolution, effects, rules unaffected

## Design Rationale

### Problem
The selection system has three structural tensions identified by intuition audit: (1) lifecycle semantics encoded in executor return values rather than declared by builders, (2) parallel mechanism for modal spell input that duplicates selection system concerns, (3) conditional cascade for pre-cast flow that doesn't match the multimethod extension model used post-cast. These tensions increase the cost of adding new selection types and pre-cast mechanics.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- events/selection/core.cljs:129-176 — `confirm-selection-impl` inspects executor returns for `:pending-selection` and `:finalized?`
- events/game.cljs:245-351 — `initiate-cast-with-mode` 110-line conditional cascade
- events/game.cljs:1258-1287 — `select-spell-mode-handler` separate from selection system
- views/modals.cljs:776-812 — `spell-mode-selector-modal` separate rendering
- 21 executor implementations across 5 domain modules: 9 standard, 6 finalized, 7 chaining (note: ADR-013 estimated 5+3, actual count is 6+7)

**Patterns:**
- Post-cast interactive effects use multimethod dispatch (symmetric with proposed pre-cast pipeline)
- Continuation system exists (`:continuation/type` multimethod) — usable for spell-mode → cast flow
- All builders construct selection maps with consistent base keys

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Three-phase incremental refactoring (013 → 014 → 015) ✓
**What it is:** Stabilize executor contract first, then unify modal, then restructure pipeline. Each phase independently testable and commitable.

**Chosen because:** Dependency order is natural — clean executor contract (013) before adding new selection type (014), then pipeline (015) simplifies what 013 established for pre-cast chaining.

#### 2. Pipeline first (015 → 014 → 013) ❌
**Why we looked at this:** Pipeline replaces pre-cast chaining, so doing it first would avoid refactoring 5 pre-cast chaining executors in 013 only to have 015 replace them.

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Pipeline depends on understanding the full selection lifecycle (standard/finalized/chaining) to handle resumption correctly. Stabilizing the lifecycle first (013) makes pipeline implementation clearer.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We discover the lifecycle refactoring has zero interaction with the pipeline.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- All three ADRs (013, 014, 015) as one atomic improvement
- Executor migration, modal unification, pipeline extraction
- View updates for mode-type candidates
- Test updates for changed selection flows

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- New pre-cast mechanics (Convoke, Kicker, etc.) — these are the *payoff* of the pipeline, not part of this epic
- cast-resolve view drift (deferred tension from intuition audit)
- game.cljs rate-of-change mismatch (reassess after these refactors)

### Open Questions
- Exact location for `evaluate-pre-cast-step` multimethod (game.cljs vs new file) — decide during implementation
- Pipeline position storage (on selection map vs app-db) — decide during Phase 3 task
- Whether spell-mode view rendering needs a new component or can reuse existing selection rendering with data-driven differences — decide during Phase 2 task

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| One epic or three? | One combined epic | Phases ordered 013→014→015, tracked as single refactoring |
| Ordering? | 013→014→015 | Stabilize lifecycle, then modal, then pipeline |
| Chaining mechanism? | Multimethod (build-chain-selection) | Framework calls after executor, clean returns, domain-colocated |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Selection Executor Return Pattern Census
**Question explored:** How many executors use each lifecycle pattern?
**Sources consulted:** All 5 domain selection modules
**Findings:**
- 9 standard (zone_ops mainly) — return `{:db db}`, no changes needed
- 6 finalized (costs: exile-cards, mana-allocation; library: scry, pile-choice, peek-and-reorder; storm: storm-split) — return `{:db db :finalized? true}`
- 7 chaining (costs: discard-specific, return-land, x-mana; targeting: cast-time, ability-cast; library: tutor, peek-and-select) — return `{:db db :pending-selection next-sel}`
- ADR-013 estimated 5 finalized + 3 chaining; actual is 6 + 7

#### Pre-cast Chaining vs Post-resolution Chaining
**Question explored:** Which chaining executors will be replaced by pipeline vs remain?
**Findings:**
- Pre-cast (replaced by ADR-015 pipeline): discard-specific-cost, return-land-cost, x-mana-cost, cast-time-targeting, ability-cast-targeting (5)
- Post-resolution (permanent, ADR-013 mechanism): tutor, peek-and-select (2)
- Finalized but chains internally: exile-cards-cost (casts directly) (1)

### Dead-End Paths
None — the ADRs were already accepted, approaches pre-validated.

### Open Concerns Raised
- Pre-cast chaining executors will be refactored in Phase 1 then replaced by pipeline in Phase 3 — acceptable intermediate step, each phase is independently correct
- Modal executor uses `:finalized` lifecycle with continuation in Phase 2, then becomes pipeline step in Phase 3 — natural evolution
close_reason: All three phases complete: ADR-013 lifecycle, ADR-014 modal unification, ADR-015 pre-cast pipeline. Reviewer approved.

title: Implement Chain of Vapor with bounce effect and chain mechanic
description: Chain of Vapor: U - Instant. Return target nonland permanent to its owner's hand. Then that permanent's controller may sacrifice a land. If the player does, they may copy this spell and may choose a new target for the copy. Requires: new :bounce effect type, nonland permanent targeting via :match/not-types, chain/copy mechanic (post-resolution selection for sacrifice-land-to-copy). Scryfall verified, no rulings.
close_reason: Chain of Vapor implemented with bounce effect, 16 tests passing, Scryfall verified

title: Card Library Reorganization
description: Restructure the card library from deck-centric aggregation (iggy_pop.cljs with 17 cards) to per-card file organization with color-identity subdirectories. Create namespace-convention registry, fix ADR-010 violations, update documentation and implement-card skill. Optimized for LLM authorship at scale (871+ Premodern cards).
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Every card definition lives in its own file (color variant cycles may share a file)
- Card files organized in subdirectories by color identity: black/, blue/, white/, red/, green/, multicolor/, lands/, artifacts/
- Each card file exports a def called `card` (or `cards` for cycles, as a vector)
- A single `cards/registry.cljs` requires all card namespaces and exports `all-cards` vector
- `engine/cards.cljs` (Interpretation Core) imports ONLY from registry — no direct card file imports anywhere else
- All events and subscriptions access cards through `engine/cards.cljs` only (ADR-010 compliance)
- Every card has a dedicated test file mirroring its source path (cycles share a test file)
- Decklists are NOT part of the card library — hard-coded iggy-pop-decklist replaced with localStorage/import mechanism
- The implement-card skill and docs/testing-strategy.md updated to reflect new conventions
- CLAUDE.md Source Layout section updated

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Zero card definitions remain in iggy_pop.cljs (file removed or repurposed)
- [ ] All 32 cards exist as individual files in color-identity subdirectories
- [ ] cards/registry.cljs requires all card namespaces and exports all-cards
- [ ] engine/cards.cljs imports only from cards/registry (not from any card file directly)
- [ ] No file outside cards/ and engine/cards.cljs imports from any cards/ namespace
- [ ] Every card file has a matching test file in the mirrored test directory
- [ ] All 1779+ existing tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] implement-card skill updated with new directory conventions
- [ ] docs/testing-strategy.md updated with new organization rules
- [ ] CLAUDE.md updated with new Source Layout

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO auto-discovery macros or build-time filesystem scanning (reliability: simple requires over compile-time magic)
- ❌ NO direct card file imports from events, subs, or views (ADR-010: all access through Interpretation Core)
- ❌ NO hard-coded decklist definitions in the card library (separation: decklists are external data, not card library concerns)
- ❌ NO card files exporting multiple unrelated cards (isolation: each card is independently editable without touching other cards)
- ❌ NO test files covering multiple unrelated cards (enforcement: 1:1 card-to-test mapping, cycles excepted)
- ❌ NO human-browsability optimizations that hurt LLM workflow (audience: cards are LLM-authored, optimize for isolation and grep-ability)

## Approach
Restructure the cards/ directory from deck-centric aggregation (iggy_pop.cljs with 17 cards) to a per-card file organization with color-identity subdirectories. Create a registry.cljs that requires all card namespaces via explicit requires (no build magic). Update engine/cards.cljs to import from registry instead of directly from card files. Fix ADR-010 violations in events/game.cljs and events/setup.cljs. Mirror the directory structure in the test path. Update the implement-card skill and documentation to reflect the new conventions.

## Architecture
Card data flow: individual card files → cards/registry.cljs → engine/cards.cljs → consumers (events, subs, views)

Directory structure:
  cards/black/, cards/blue/, cards/white/, cards/red/, cards/green/,
  cards/multicolor/, cards/lands/, cards/artifacts/
  cards/registry.cljs

Each card file exports `card` def. Cycles (basic lands) export `cards` vector.
Registry requires all card namespaces, builds all-cards vector.
engine/cards.cljs builds card-by-id lookup map from registry's all-cards.

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Current card library has 17 of 32 cards in a single iggy_pop.cljs file. As the card pool grows toward 871+ Premodern cards, this monolithic aggregation breaks down. Cards are LLM-authored, so organization must optimize for isolation (safe edits) and discoverability (grep/glob), not human file browsing.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs — 17 card defs + decklist + all-cards aggregation in one file
- engine/cards.cljs — Interpretation Core exists, re-exports from iggy-pop
- events/game.cljs:5, events/setup.cljs:5 — ADR-010 violations (direct card imports)
- subs/setup.cljs — correctly routes through engine/cards (ADR-010 compliant)
- 8 newer cards already follow 1-card-per-file pattern

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Per-card files with namespace convention registry ✓
**What it is:** Each card in its own file exporting `card`. Registry.cljs requires all card namespaces explicitly. Color-identity subdirectories for grouping.

**Chosen because:** Optimizes for LLM authorship — isolation means editing card #150 can't break card #1. Namespace convention is simple and reliable. At 871 cards, 8 subdirectories keep each bucket manageable (~100 files).

#### 2. Auto-discovery via build-time macros ❌
**Why explored:** Zero-registration seemed ideal — just create a file and it's discovered.

**Investigation:** ClojureScript macros run in Clojure JVM, filesystem scanning at compile time has shadow-cljs caching gotchas. Build-time directory scanning is fragile.

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Magic that silently breaks is worse than a simple require line. LLM authorship makes forgetting registration a non-issue.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** shadow-cljs adds native directory-scanning support.

#### 3. Grouped card files (by type, color, etc.) ❌
**Why explored:** Concern about having 1000 files.

**Investigation:** File count is a human UX concern. LLMs search with glob/grep, not directory browsing. Grouping cards into shared files sacrifices edit isolation.

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Contradicts LLM-optimized design. Multiple cards per file means editing one card risks breaking others.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We find that compile times degrade significantly with 1000+ individual files.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Restructure card files to per-card organization
- Create registry with namespace convention
- Fix ADR-010 violations
- Update implement-card skill and documentation
- Remove hard-coded iggy-pop-decklist from card library

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Deck import/export from Moxfield/MTGGoldfish (separate feature)
- localStorage deck storage UX (separate feature)
- Adding new cards beyond the current 32 (future work using the new structure)

### Open Questions
- What replaces iggy-pop-decklist for development bootstrapping? (resolve during implementation — likely a default deck in localStorage or a minimal bootstrap in setup events)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Card organization granularity? | 1 card per file, cycles can share | Each card independently editable |
| Directory grouping? | Color identity + lands/ + artifacts/ | 8 subdirs, natural MTG taxonomy |
| Registration mechanism? | Namespace convention (require in registry) | Simple, no build magic |
| Who authors cards? | LLM (Claude) | Optimize for isolation and grep, not human browsing |
| Where do decklists live? | External (Moxfield/MTGGoldfish/localStorage) | Not part of card library |
| Test organization? | 1:1 mirroring of card directory structure | Strict enforcement, cycles excepted |
| What documentation? | Update existing implement-card skill + docs it references | No new doc files |
| Git/tooling at 1000 cards? | Acceptable with subdirectories | Color-identity subdirs keep buckets manageable |

### Open Concerns Raised

- '1000 files in flat directory rough for human tooling' → Resolved with color-identity subdirectories
- 'Hard-coded iggy-pop-decklist is bootstrap artifact' → Deferred: resolve during implementation
- 'Current test organization inconsistent' → Addressed: strict 1:1 rule with directory mirroring
close_reason: All 29 cards migrated to per-card files in color-identity subdirectories. Registry, ADR-010 fixes, docs all complete. Reviewer approved.

title: Add events and subscriptions for fork/replay UI components
design:
## Goal
Add re-frame events and subscriptions that wire the new data model functions to the UI layer. Events handle user actions (jump-to, create/rename/delete fork). Subscriptions derive turn-grouped entries and hierarchical fork tree for the view components.

## Context
Completed fizzle-4r1d: Four pure functions now exist in history/core.cljs:
- rename-fork, delete-fork, entries-by-turn, fork-tree
- All tested and working (21 tests)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- history/events.cljs — existing events: ::step-back, ::step-forward, ::switch-branch
- subs/history.cljs — existing subs: ::entries, ::position, ::current-branch, ::forks, ::can-step-back?, ::can-step-forward?, ::at-tip?, ::entry-count
- history/core.cljs:67-75 — step-to (used by ::jump-to)
- history/core.cljs:105-115 — create-named-fork (used by ::create-fork)
- history/core.cljs:130-134 — rename-fork (used by ::rename-fork)
- history/core.cljs:138-167 — delete-fork (used by ::delete-fork)
- history/core.cljs:175-183 — fork-tree (used by ::fork-tree sub)
- history/core.cljs:186-191 — entries-by-turn (used by ::entries-by-turn sub)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

**Event tests (in history/events_test.cljs):**
- test-jump-to-event: dispatching ::jump-to with position updates position and game/db
- test-jump-to-event-out-of-bounds: out-of-bounds position leaves db unchanged
- test-create-fork-event: dispatching ::create-fork adds fork to :history/forks
- test-rename-fork-event: dispatching ::rename-fork updates fork name
- test-delete-fork-event: dispatching ::delete-fork removes fork from map
- test-delete-fork-event-cascade: deleting parent fork cascades to children

**Subscription tests (in subs/history_test.cljs):**
- test-entries-by-turn-sub: subscribe to ::entries-by-turn returns grouped structure
- test-fork-tree-sub: subscribe to ::fork-tree returns hierarchical tree

### 3. Implementation checklist

**Events (history/events.cljs):**
- [ ] ::jump-to [db [_ position]] — calls history/step-to
- [ ] ::create-fork [db [_ name]] — calls history/create-named-fork
- [ ] ::rename-fork [db [_ fork-id new-name]] — calls history/rename-fork
- [ ] ::delete-fork [db [_ fork-id]] — calls history/delete-fork

**Subscriptions (subs/history.cljs):**
- [ ] ::entries-by-turn — Layer 3 sub, derives from ::entries, calls history/entries-by-turn
- [ ] ::fork-tree — Layer 3 sub, derives from ::forks (the raw forks map from db), calls history/fork-tree

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 4 events work correctly (6 event tests)
- [ ] Both subscriptions return correct derived data (2 sub tests)
- [ ] Events delegate to history/core.cljs functions (no logic duplication)
- [ ] Subscriptions are Layer 3 (derived from existing Layer 2 subs)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations

**::forks subscription shape**: The existing ::forks sub returns `(vec (vals (:history/forks db)))` — a vector of fork maps. But fork-tree needs the forks MAP (fork-id → fork). May need a new Layer 2 sub `::forks-map` that returns `:history/forks` directly, or adjust fork-tree to accept a vector. Check existing sub shape before implementing.

**create-named-fork does NOT switch branch**: Per existing behavior (core.cljs:105-115), create-named-fork creates the fork but stays on current branch. The UI may want to switch after creation — handle that in the view layer (dispatch switch-branch after create), not in the event.

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO game logic in events (delegate to history/core.cljs functions)
- ❌ NO direct db manipulation — events call pure functions
- ❌ NO subscription that queries app-db directly for derived data (use Layer 2 → Layer 3 pattern)
close_reason: All 4 events (jump-to, create-fork, rename-fork, delete-fork) and 2 subscriptions (entries-by-turn, fork-tree) implemented. 8 new tests, all passing. make validate clean.

title: Bug: Bot casts spells during untap step (no priority in untap)
description: Bot priority decision is consulted regardless of phase, allowing casts during untap where MTG rules give no player priority
design:
## Goal
Bot priority decisions must only be consulted during phases where MTG rules give players priority. Currently, bot-priority-decision is called regardless of phase, allowing the burn bot to cast Lightning Bolt during untap (where no player gets priority).

## Root Cause
After fizzle-ipoo fix, bot priority decisions move to handle-loop-state :bot-phase. The bot priority loop there must be guarded by a phase check.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## MTG Priority Rules
Players receive priority at the beginning of most steps and phases. Exceptions:
- Untap step: No player receives priority (CR 502.3)
- Cleanup step: No player receives priority unless a state-based action or triggered ability occurs (CR 514.3a)

Phases WITH priority: upkeep, draw, main1, combat (all sub-steps), main2, end step

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Bot never casts during untap or cleanup phases
- [ ] Bot still casts normally during main1, main2, upkeep, draw, combat, end
- [ ] Phase guard is a def constant named priority-phases in game_loop.cljs
- [ ] The constant value is #{:upkeep :draw :main1 :combat :main2 :end}
- [ ] Bot priority loop in handle-loop-state :bot-phase wraps decision call with (when (priority-phases current-phase) ...)
- [ ] 3+ new tests: bot-does-not-cast-in-untap, bot-does-not-cast-in-cleanup, bot-casts-in-main1
- [ ] make validate clean

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] In game_loop.cljs, add constant at top of file (after requires):
  ```clojure
  (def priority-phases
    "Phases where players receive priority per MTG rules.
     Untap and cleanup do not grant priority."
    #{:upkeep :draw :main1 :combat :main2 :end})
  ```
- [ ] In handle-loop-state :bot-phase :else branch, wrap the bot priority loop (added by fizzle-ipoo) with phase check:
  ```clojure
  (let [final-db (if (priority-phases current-phase)
                   (loop [db acted-db, n 20] ...)  ;; existing loop from fizzle-ipoo
                   acted-db)  ;; no priority in this phase, skip bot cast
        ...]
  ```
- [ ] Add unit tests in game_loop_test.cljs that test the phase guard directly

## Key Considerations
- This fix coordinates with fizzle-ipoo: both modify handle-loop-state :bot-phase :else branch. If implemented by the same executor, apply both in one pass.
- Combat is modeled as single :combat phase (no sub-steps). Using :combat in the set is sufficient.
- The constant is in game_loop.cljs (game loop concern), NOT in protocol.cljs (bot concern) or priority.cljs (priority mechanism). The game loop decides WHEN to consult bots; bots decide WHAT to do.

## Edge Cases (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Cleanup with state-based actions**:
- MTG CR 514.3a: players get priority during cleanup if state-based actions happen (e.g., creature dies). Current model doesn't have state-based actions during cleanup, so excluding cleanup entirely is correct for now. If this changes, add cleanup to the set conditionally.

**Bot phase action still runs in all phases**:
- execute-bot-phase-action (play land in main1) runs BEFORE the priority guard. The phase guard only affects the priority loop (cast spells). This is correct: phase actions are not priority-dependent.

## Anti-patterns
- Do NOT add phase awareness to burn bot protocol (phase is a game loop concern)
- Do NOT use inline #{:untap :cleanup} exclusion checks (use the named constant)
- Do NOT put the constant in protocol.cljs or priority.cljs (it belongs in game_loop.cljs)
close_reason: Closed

title: Create events/opening_hand.cljs with mulligan, keep, and bottom-card events
design:
## Goal
Create the events/opening_hand.cljs namespace with pure handler functions for:
1. Mulligan: return hand to library, shuffle, re-deal 7 (sculpted cards guaranteed), increment mulligan-count
2. Keep with 0 mulligans: transition to :game, clear opening-hand keys
3. Keep with N>0 mulligans: switch opening-hand/phase to :bottoming
4. Toggle bottom-card selection: add/remove card from bottom-selection set
5. Confirm bottom selection: move selected cards to bottom of library, transition to :game, clear opening-hand keys

## Context
Completed fizzle-f935: init-game-state now returns :opening-hand with mulligan-count 0, sculpted-ids set, phase :viewing. The game/db is live with hand and library objects. This namespace operates on the app-db (which contains :game/db, :opening-hand/* keys).

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (5 handlers + tests + registration)

## Architecture Constraints
- Anti-pattern: NO opening hand logic in events/game.cljs or events/setup.cljs
- Anti-pattern: NO history entries for mulligans (history starts at :game transition)
- Anti-pattern: NO auto-keeping (player must explicitly keep)
- Anti-pattern: NO mulligan without bottom selection when mulligan-count > 0

## State Keys Used
- :game/db — Datascript db with hand/library objects
- :opening-hand/mulligan-count — integer, incremented on each mulligan
- :opening-hand/sculpted-ids — set of UUIDs for sculpted objects (regenerated on each mulligan)
- :opening-hand/must-contain — map of {card-id count} (REQUIRED: stored in app-db for mulligan re-sculpting)
- :opening-hand/phase — :viewing or :bottoming
- :opening-hand/bottom-selection — set of object-ids selected for bottoming (new key, only during :bottoming)
- :active-screen — :opening-hand during this phase, :game after keep+bottom

## PREREQUISITE: Store :opening-hand/must-contain in init-game-state output
The mulligan handler needs the must-contain config ({card-id count}) to re-sculpt hands. Currently init-game-state only stores :opening-hand/sculpted-ids (UUIDs). Add :opening-hand/must-contain to the init-game-state output map.

Modify events/game.cljs init-game-state to add:
  :opening-hand/must-contain (or must-contain {})
to the merge map alongside :opening-hand/mulligan-count, :opening-hand/sculpted-ids, :opening-hand/phase.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Add :opening-hand/must-contain to init-game-state (prerequisite)
- Edit events/game.cljs: add :opening-hand/must-contain to the merge map in init-game-state
- Add test in init_game_test.cljs: verify :opening-hand/must-contain matches input config
- Add test: verify empty must-contain stored as {}
- Run tests RED then GREEN

### Step 2: Write tests for mulligan-handler (TDD RED)
File: src/test/fizzle/events/opening_hand_test.cljs

Test helper: create-opening-hand-app-db that calls game/init-game-state with configurable must-contain.

Tests (each catches a specific bug):
- test-mulligan-returns-seven-cards: After mulligan, hand has exactly 7 cards. Catches: hand size mismatch after re-deal.
- test-mulligan-preserves-library-count: Library count unchanged after mulligan (cards returned then re-drawn). Catches: card duplication or loss.
- test-mulligan-increments-count: mulligan-count goes from 0 to 1 to 2. Catches: counter not incrementing.
- test-mulligan-preserves-sculpted-card-ids: After mulligan with must-contain {:dark-ritual 2}, hand contains exactly 2 dark-rituals (by card-id). Catches: sculpting not re-applied.
- test-mulligan-generates-new-sculpted-uuids: After mulligan, sculpted-ids are different UUIDs than before (old objects gone, new ones created). Catches: stale UUID tracking.
- test-mulligan-with-empty-must-contain: Mulligan with no sculpting still returns 7 random cards. Catches: nil handling.
- test-mulligan-with-all-seven-sculpted: must-contain totaling 7 — mulligan returns same 7 card-ids. Catches: zero-random-draw edge case.
- test-mulligan-stays-on-opening-hand: :active-screen remains :opening-hand, :opening-hand/phase stays :viewing. Catches: premature screen transition.

### Step 3: Implement mulligan-handler (TDD GREEN)
File: src/main/fizzle/events/opening_hand.cljs

Algorithm:
1. Get all hand objects from game-db via queries/get-objects-in-zone
2. Move each hand object to :library via zones/move-to-zone (loop/reduce)
3. Shuffle library via zones/shuffle-library
4. Read :opening-hand/must-contain from app-db
5. Get all library objects, extract card-ids
6. Use sculpting algorithm: for each {card-id count} in must-contain, find library objects with that card-id, take min(count, available)
7. Take (7 - sculpted-count) random library objects for remaining hand slots
8. Move selected objects from :library to :hand via zones/move-to-zone
9. Update app-db: increment :opening-hand/mulligan-count, set :opening-hand/sculpted-ids to new set of sculpted object UUIDs

Key difference from init-game-state: mulligan works on existing Datascript objects (move between zones), not creating new objects.

### Step 4: Write tests for keep-handler (TDD RED)
- test-keep-zero-mulligans-transitions-to-game: :active-screen becomes :game, :opening-hand/* keys dissoc'd. Catches: screen not transitioning.
- test-keep-zero-mulligans-preserves-game-db: :game/db unchanged. Catches: accidental db mutation.
- test-keep-with-mulligans-enters-bottoming: mulligan-count 2 → :opening-hand/phase :bottoming, :opening-hand/bottom-selection #{}. Catches: skip to :game without bottom selection.
- test-keep-preserves-hand: hand cards unchanged during keep. Catches: card loss.
- test-keep-clears-opening-hand-keys: After keep with 0 mulligans, :opening-hand/mulligan-count, :opening-hand/sculpted-ids, :opening-hand/phase, :opening-hand/must-contain, :opening-hand/bottom-selection all nil. Catches: key leakage.

### Step 5: Implement keep-handler (TDD GREEN)
- If mulligan-count is 0: dissoc all :opening-hand/* keys, set :active-screen :game
- If mulligan-count > 0: set :opening-hand/phase :bottoming, assoc :opening-hand/bottom-selection #{}

### Step 6: Write tests for toggle-bottom-selection (TDD RED)
- test-toggle-adds-to-selection: Adds object-id to :opening-hand/bottom-selection. Catches: selection not updating.
- test-toggle-removes-if-selected: Toggling already-selected id removes it. Catches: toggle not working.
- test-toggle-noop-when-not-bottoming: If :opening-hand/phase is :viewing, toggle is no-op. Catches: selection in wrong phase.

### Step 7: Implement toggle-bottom-selection (TDD GREEN)
- Check phase is :bottoming, otherwise no-op
- If object-id in bottom-selection: disj; else: conj

### Step 8: Write tests for confirm-bottom-handler (TDD RED)
- test-confirm-bottom-moves-cards: Selected cards move from hand to library. Hand count drops by N, library grows by N. Catches: cards not moving.
- test-confirm-bottom-noop-wrong-count: If selected count != mulligan-count, no-op. Catches: allowing invalid selection.
- test-confirm-bottom-transitions-to-game: :active-screen becomes :game. Catches: stuck on opening-hand.
- test-confirm-bottom-clears-keys: All :opening-hand/* keys dissoc'd. Catches: key leakage.
- test-confirm-bottom-specific-cards: Verify the specific selected cards are in library and NOT in hand. Catches: wrong cards moved.

### Step 9: Implement confirm-bottom-handler (TDD GREEN)
- Validate count(bottom-selection) == mulligan-count, else no-op
- Move each selected object to :library via zones/move-to-zone
- Dissoc all :opening-hand/* keys
- Set :active-screen :game

### Step 10: Register re-frame events and core.cljs require
- Register all 4 events: ::mulligan, ::keep, ::toggle-bottom, ::confirm-bottom
- Add bare require [fizzle.events.opening-hand] in core.cljs
- Run make validate

## History Safety (Verified)
The history interceptor (history/interceptor.cljs) only records events in the priority-events set. Opening-hand events (namespace fizzle.events.opening-hand) are NOT in that set. Therefore:
- Mulligan events will NOT create history entries (correct)
- Keep/bottom events will NOT create history entries (correct)
- History arrays stay empty until first game action after :game transition
- No explicit history code needed in this namespace

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Mulligan Sculpting on Existing Objects**
Unlike init-game-state which creates new objects, mulligan operates on existing Datascript entities. Must find library objects by card-id, not create new ones. Algorithm:
1. Query library objects: (queries/get-objects-in-zone db :player-1 :library)
2. Group by card-id: (group-by #(get-in % [:object/card :card/id]) lib-objects)
3. For each {card-id count} in must-contain, take N objects from that group
4. Remaining hand slots filled from remaining library objects (random selection)
5. Move selected objects hand→library via zones/move-to-zone

**Edge Case: Mulligan with Empty Must-Contain**
sculpted-count = 0, all 7 are random. :opening-hand/sculpted-ids = #{}.

**Edge Case: Mulligan with All 7 Sculpted**
random-draw-count = 0. (take 0 ...) returns empty, no random draws. Verify this path works.

**Edge Case: Must-Contain Exceeds Available After Shuffle**
Defensive: take min(requested, available). Setup toggle already validates, but after sideboarding edge cases are possible.

**Edge Case: Multiple Sequential Mulligans**
Must work for mulligan 1, 2, 3+ in sequence. Each time: return hand, shuffle entire library, re-sculpt, re-draw 7. mulligan-count increments correctly each time.

**Edge Case: Bottom Selection with Sculpted Cards**
Player CAN select sculpted cards for bottoming (it's their choice). The view will mark sculpted cards to discourage it, but the handler must allow it. Don't filter sculpted cards from bottom selection.

**Edge Case: Confirm Bottom with Exact Count**
Must validate count(bottom-selection) == mulligan-count. If player selected 1 card but mulligan-count is 2, no-op. This prevents partial bottoming.

**Cleanup Completeness**
Both keep (0 mulligans) and confirm-bottom must dissoc ALL opening-hand keys:
- :opening-hand/mulligan-count
- :opening-hand/sculpted-ids
- :opening-hand/must-contain
- :opening-hand/phase
- :opening-hand/bottom-selection

## Success Criteria
- [ ] init-game-state stores :opening-hand/must-contain in app-db output
- [ ] mulligan-handler: hand has 7 cards after mulligan, library count unchanged
- [ ] mulligan-handler: mulligan-count increments correctly (0→1→2→...)
- [ ] mulligan-handler: sculpted cards preserved in hand (verified by card-id)
- [ ] mulligan-handler: new sculpted-ids generated (old UUIDs gone)
- [ ] mulligan-handler: works with empty must-contain (7 random cards)
- [ ] mulligan-handler: works with all-7-sculpted must-contain
- [ ] keep-handler: 0 mulligans → :active-screen :game, opening-hand keys cleared
- [ ] keep-handler: N>0 mulligans → :opening-hand/phase :bottoming
- [ ] toggle-bottom-selection: adds/removes from set, no-op if not bottoming
- [ ] confirm-bottom: moves exactly N cards, no-op if count wrong
- [ ] confirm-bottom: transitions to :game, clears opening-hand keys
- [ ] history arrays remain empty during entire opening-hand phase
- [ ] re-frame events registered, core.cljs has bare require
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] make validate passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO calling history functions during mulligan/keep (interceptor already excludes these events)
- NO modifying game.cljs or setup.cljs for opening hand logic (separate namespace)
- NO allowing keep without bottom selection when mulligan-count > 0
- NO filtering sculpted cards from bottom selection (player's choice)
- NO creating new Datascript objects during mulligan (reuse existing by moving zones)
- NO using effects/execute-effect :draw for mulligan re-deal (build hand directly from zone moves)
close_reason: All 4 handlers implemented with TDD (21 tests). make validate passes: 1094 tests, 0 failures.

title: Task 1: Extend tutor selection for multi-card select
design:
## Goal
Extend the existing tutor selection system to support selecting multiple cards (up to N) instead of just 1. This is the foundational change needed for Intuition.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing tutor selection
- events/game.cljs:911-930 - build-tutor-selection (current single-select, uses :selection/count 1)
- events/game.cljs:1385-1428 - execute-tutor-selection (moves card via `(first selected)`, shuffles)
- events/game.cljs:1431+ - confirm-tutor-selection event handler
- events/game.cljs:951-960 - build-discard-selection (example of multi-count selection)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Create src/test/fizzle/events/tutor_multi_select_test.cljs:

```clojure
;; Test: Multi-select tutor selects exactly 3 from large library
;; Setup: Library with 10 cards, tutor effect with :effect/select-count 3
;; Action: Build selection, select 3 cards, confirm
;; Assert: All 3 cards moved to target zone, library shuffled, 7 cards remain

;; Test: Multi-select tutor with exact library size
;; Setup: Library with 2 cards, tutor effect with :effect/select-count 3
;; Action: Build selection
;; Assert: :selection/select-count is 2 (min of effect count and library size)
;; Assert: Selection requires exactly 2 (cannot confirm with 1)

;; Test: Multi-select tutor with smaller library
;; Setup: Library with 1 card, tutor effect with :effect/select-count 3
;; Action: Build selection, select 1 card, confirm
;; Assert: 1 card moved to target zone, library shuffled

;; Test: Multi-select tutor fail-to-find (empty library)
;; Setup: Empty library, tutor effect with :effect/select-count 3
;; Action: Build selection
;; Assert: :selection/allow-fail-to-find true, can confirm with 0 selected
;; Assert: Library shuffled, no cards moved

;; Test: Backwards compat - existing single-select tutors work
;; Setup: Library with 5 cards, tutor effect WITHOUT :effect/select-count
;; Action: Build selection
;; Assert: :selection/select-count defaults to 1
;; Assert: Can select exactly 1 card, confirm moves 1 card
```

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] events/game.cljs:911-930 - Modify build-tutor-selection:
      - Add parameter extraction: `(or (:effect/select-count tutor-effect) 1)`
      - Compute actual count: `(min select-count (count candidate-ids))`
      - Add to selection state: `:selection/select-count actual-count`
      - Add to selection state: `:selection/exact? true` (must select exactly this many)
- [ ] events/game.cljs:1385-1428 - Modify execute-tutor-selection:
      - Change `(first selected)` to iterate over all selected cards
      - Move each card to target-zone in sequence
      - Shuffle once at end (not per card)
- [ ] events/game.cljs:1431+ - Verify confirm-tutor-selection handles set correctly (already uses set)
- [ ] Backwards compat: `(or (:effect/select-count tutor-effect) 1)` ensures default is 1

### 4. Verification
- Run: make test (all tests pass)
- Run: make validate (lint, format, tests)
- Manual test: Cast Merchant Scroll, verify still selects 1 card

## Success Criteria
- [ ] build-tutor-selection extracts :effect/select-count (default 1)
- [ ] :selection/select-count = min(effect-count, library-size)
- [ ] :selection/exact? = true in selection state
- [ ] execute-tutor-selection iterates over all selected cards (not just first)
- [ ] All selected cards moved to target-zone before shuffle
- [ ] Shuffle happens once after all moves (not per card)
- [ ] Existing tutors (no :select-count) default to 1 card
- [ ] 5+ unit tests pass with specific assertions
- [ ] make validate passes (lint, format, tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: select-count <= 0**
- If :effect/select-count is 0 or negative, treat as 1 (defensive)
- Use: `(max 1 (or (:effect/select-count tutor-effect) 1))`

**Edge Case: Empty candidates but select-count > 0**
- If no matching cards in library, :selection/select-count = 0
- :selection/allow-fail-to-find = true allows confirming with 0 selected
- This is existing behavior, preserve it

**Edge Case: Selection cancelled**
- User closes modal without confirming
- Existing behavior: selection state cleared, spell remains on stack
- No change needed, but verify tests cover this

**Performance: Multiple card moves**
- Moving 3 cards sequentially is fine (not hot path)
- Shuffle once at end, not per move

**Reference Implementation**
- build-discard-selection (line 951-960) uses :selection/count for multi-select
- Follow same pattern for consistency

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO shuffling between card moves (shuffle once at end)
- ❌ NO changing fail-to-find behavior (must always be allowed per existing anti-pattern)
- ❌ NO breaking single-select default (backwards compat required)
- ❌ NO using (first selected) when multiple cards possible (iterate over all)
close_reason: Multi-select tutor selection implemented with TDD. All tests passing, make validate passes.

title: Create create-game-scenario test helper for loop/director tests
description: Build a test helper that creates valid game state for tests that run the game director loop. Both players get library cards, stops, and turn-based triggers. Built on top of the create-complete-player shared invariant.
design:
## Goal

Create a create-game-scenario test helper that produces game state valid for running the game director loop (multiple turn cycles without game-over from empty library draws).

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Implementation

Add to test_helpers.cljs:

```clojure
(defn create-game-scenario
  "Create valid game state for tests that run the game loop.
   Both players have libraries, stops, turn-based triggers.
   Returns app-db (not just game-db) with history initialized.
   opts: {:bot-archetype :goldfish  ;; default :goldfish
          :stops #{:main1 :main2}   ;; human stops, default #{:main1 :main2}
          :mana {:black 3}          ;; human mana pool
          :life 20                  ;; human life
          :human-library 10         ;; number of library cards per player, default 10
          :bot-library 10}"
  [opts]
  ...)
```

Implementation:
- Call create-test-db with opts (mana, life, stops)
- Call add-opponent with bot-archetype
- Add library cards for BOTH players (default 10 islands each)
- Return app-db with history initialized (merge (history/init-history) {:game/db db})

## Success Criteria

- [ ] create-game-scenario exists in test_helpers.cljs
- [ ] Produces state that survives 3+ turn cycles via director (no game-over from empty library)
- [ ] Default stops #{:main1 :main2} — director stops at human's main1 after F6 cascade
- [ ] Both players have library cards (configurable count, default 10)
- [ ] Returns app-db with history (not just game-db)
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] No changes to existing tests (additive only — new helper)

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO modifying create-test-db or add-opponent (this is additive)
- ❌ NO making create-game-scenario the default for narrow tests (it's for scenario/loop tests only)
close_reason: Complete — commit becd4b3. create-game-scenario helper added.

title: Merge Island and Swamp into basic_lands.cljs
description: Merge the separate island.cljs and swamp.cljs definitions into basic_lands.cljs, update the registry and tests, delete the old files.
design:
## Goal
Consolidate all 5 basic lands into a single basic_lands.cljs file. Currently Island and Swamp are in separate files while Plains, Mountain, Forest share basic_lands.cljs.

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - basic_lands.cljs:1-57 — current 3-card cycle (Plains, Mountain, Forest)
   - island.cljs:1-21 — Island definition to merge
   - swamp.cljs:1-21 — Swamp definition to merge
   - registry.cljs:25-27 — separate island/swamp requires to remove
   - basic_lands_test.cljs:1-231 — tests that reference island/card and swamp/card
