title: Extract shared game state invariants into db/game_state.cljs
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- R1: A single module (db/game_state.cljs) defines player-id constants, player entity factory, and game entity factory
- R2: All consumers (events/init.cljs, test_helpers.cljs, sharing/restorer.cljs) use the shared module for player-id constants and entity construction
- R3: Opponent player-id is :player-2 everywhere (ADR-016) — no production code uses :opponent as a player-id
- R4: Card definitions and effect target keywords (:opponent, :self, :any-player, :controller) remain unchanged — these are directives, not player-ids
- R5: All player entities get identical base defaults: life 20, empty mana pool, storm-count 0, land-plays-left 1, max-hand-size 7
- R6: The 3 remaining vulnerable (get effect :effect/target player-id) patterns are fixed to use the safe explicit contains? pattern
- R7: The bots/interceptor.cljs compensating pattern [:player-1 :player-2 :opponent] uses only the shared constants
- R8: All existing tests pass without modification (test files already use :player-2)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] db/game_state.cljs exists with: human-player-id, opponent-player-id, empty-mana-pool, create-player-tx, create-game-entity-tx
- [ ] grep for :opponent as player-id in production code returns zero hits (card definitions and effect directives excluded)
- [ ] events/init.cljs uses db.game-state/create-player-tx and create-game-entity-tx
- [ ] test_helpers.cljs uses db.game-state/create-player-tx and create-game-entity-tx
- [ ] sharing/restorer.cljs uses db.game-state/opponent-player-id
- [ ] subs/game.cljs uses db.game-state/opponent-player-id (6 references)
- [ ] bots/interceptor.cljs uses [db.game-state/human-player-id db.game-state/opponent-player-id]
- [ ] events/selection/targeting.cljs:34 uses shared constants
- [ ] engine/effects/life.cljs uses safe (contains?) pattern (2 sites)
- [ ] events/selection/core.cljs:139 uses safe pattern
- [ ] Opponent gets land-plays-left 1 (was 0 — Turn 1 land-play bug fixed)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] All tests pass without modification to test files

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO using :opponent as a player-id anywhere in production code (ADR-016: keyword collision causes silent bugs)
- ❌ NO role-based dispatch in create-player-tx (complects identity with behavior — bot-archetype is an override, not a role)
- ❌ NO duplicating empty-mana-pool, player defaults, or player-id constants outside db/game_state.cljs (duplication is the root cause)
- ❌ NO modifying test files to change :player-2 references (they are already correct)
- ❌ NO changing card definitions — :effect/target :opponent and :target/options :opponent are semantic directives, not player-ids
- ❌ NO (get effect :effect/target player-id) pattern — always use explicit (contains?) check then resolve (commit 1605323 established this)

## Approach
Create db/game_state.cljs as the single source of truth for game state entity construction. The module exports identity constants (human-player-id = :player-1, opponent-player-id = :player-2) and flat factory functions (create-player-tx, create-game-entity-tx) with universal defaults. No role dispatch.

Update all consumers (events/init.cljs, test_helpers.cljs, sharing/restorer.cljs) to import from the shared module. Update all production code that hardcodes :opponent as a player-id to use the shared constant. Fix the 3 remaining vulnerable effect target resolution patterns.

The opponent player-id changes from :opponent to :player-2 (ADR-016). Test files already use :player-2 so they need no changes. Production files (~26) change from hardcoded :opponent to the shared constant.

## Architecture
- NEW: db/game_state.cljs — identity constants, create-player-tx, create-game-entity-tx
- MODIFIED: events/init.cljs — uses shared factories, removes local player-tx and empty-mana-pool
- MODIFIED: test_helpers.cljs — uses shared factories, removes local add-opponent construction
- MODIFIED: sharing/restorer.cljs — uses opponent-player-id constant
- MODIFIED: subs/game.cljs — uses opponent-player-id constant (6 refs)
- MODIFIED: bots/interceptor.cljs — uses shared constants (3 refs)
- MODIFIED: events/selection/targeting.cljs — uses shared constants
- MODIFIED: engine/effects/life.cljs — fixes 2 vulnerable patterns
- MODIFIED: events/selection/core.cljs — fixes 1 vulnerable pattern
- MODIFIED: events/ui.cljs — uses shared constants

## Architecture Context
- Components affected: Data Foundation (new module), Game Events (init), Engine (effects), Selection Events (targeting), Opponent AI (interceptor), Subscriptions (queries)
- Layers: data (db/game_state.cljs), orchestration (events/init, selection, bots), domain (engine effects)
- ADRs: ADR-016 (separate player-id from effect target keywords), ADR-017 (extract shared game state invariants)

## Architecture Update Checklist
1. Did this work change a component's public interface? YES — Data Foundation gains new public module
2. Did this work add or remove a dependency between components? YES — multiple components now depend on db/game_state
3. Did this work create a new component or remove an existing one? Possibly — db/game_state could be modeled as a new sub-component
4. Did this work move responsibility from one component to another? YES — player entity construction moves from events/init to db/
5. Did this work create a new request path through 2+ components? NO

If any yes: invoke /ponder update with description of what changed.

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Three consumers (events/init.cljs, test_helpers.cljs, sharing/restorer.cljs) independently construct game state entities with no shared source of truth. This caused a 3-week silent production regression where opponent draw was broken but 2939 tests passed. The divergence also hid a Turn 1 land-play bug (opponent got land-plays-left: 0). The :opponent keyword is overloaded as both a player-id and an effect target directive, creating a collision class that requires special disambiguation in every effect handler.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- events/init.cljs:131-134 — production player construction with :opponent id, land-plays-left 0
- test_helpers.cljs:44-49,194-215 — test player construction with :player-2 id, land-plays-left 1
- sharing/restorer.cljs:205 — hardcodes :opponent for stops restoration
- engine/effects/life.cljs:12,25 — 2 unfixed vulnerable (get effect :effect/target player-id) patterns
- events/selection/core.cljs:139 — 1 unfixed vulnerable pattern
- bots/interceptor.cljs:35,80,211 — compensating [:player-1 :player-2 :opponent] pattern
- subs/game.cljs:241,299,392-394 — hardcoded :opponent player-id lookups
- events/selection/targeting.cljs:34 — hardcoded #{:player-1 :opponent} valid targets
- db/queries.cljs:219-228 — get-opponent-id queries by :player/is-opponent flag (safe)
- engine/rules.cljs:562 — can-play-land? requires (pos? land-plays) — blocks opponent Turn 1

**Investigation findings:**
- Opponent land-plays-left: 0 is a BUG — bot calls can-play-land? which requires pos? check, blocking Turn 1 land play
- Missing max-hand-size on opponent: NOT a bug — get-max-hand-size defaults to 7
- Turn start resets land-plays-left to 1 (phases.cljs:117) — bug only affects Turn 1

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Compositional shared module (constants + factories) ✓
**What it is:** db/game_state.cljs exports identity constants and flat factory functions. Consumers compose what they need — init adds deck policy, tests add manual cards, restorer uses constants.

**Pros:** Eliminates entity drift, minimal coupling, compositional, YAGNI-compliant
**Cons:** New module, all consumers must adopt

**Chosen because:** Matches Rich Hickey principle of composition over complecting. Factory is data-in-data-out. Consumers stay independent.

#### 2. Base game state function ❌
**What it is:** Single create-base-game-state function that creates conn, loads cards, creates players, creates game entity, registers triggers — returns a db ready for policy additions.

**Why we looked at this:** Maximum sharing of init logic
**Pros:** Test helpers become very thin
**Cons:** Inflexible — tests often don't need an opponent, or need custom game phase. Over-couples consumers to a single init sequence.

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Over-constrains test setup. Tests legitimately need surgical control over which entities exist.
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Test helpers grow to 5+ consumers that all need the same base setup.

#### 3. Constants only (minimal) ❌
**What it is:** Only export player-id constants. Each consumer still constructs entities independently.

**Why we looked at this:** Least structural change
**Pros:** Fixes player-id collision with minimal effort
**Cons:** Does NOT fix entity shape drift — land-plays-left bug and missing attributes would recur.

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Doesn't address root cause. The user explicitly identified duplicated logic as the endemic issue.
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We decide entity shape drift is acceptable.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Create db/game_state.cljs with constants and factories
- Update all consumers to use shared module
- Change opponent player-id from :opponent to :player-2 (ADR-016)
- Fix 3 remaining vulnerable effect target patterns
- Fix opponent Turn 1 land-play bug (automatic with shared defaults)

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Modifying card definitions (these use :opponent as a directive, not a player-id)
- Modifying bot decision keywords (:target :opponent in bots/rules.cljs is a bot directive)
- Adding runtime validation/spec for entity shape (Approach 3 from earlier brainstorm)
- Changing the (get effect :effect/target player-id) pattern in grants.cljs:82-86 — already uses safe (:effect/target effect) pattern

### Open Questions
- Should create-player-tx also register turn-based triggers, or leave that to callers? (Defer to implementation)
- Should events/ui.cljs stop checks use the constant? (Yes, include in scope)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Constraint level? | Constants + factory (after investigation) | No role dispatch; flat factory with universal defaults |
| Is :role comingling with :bot-archetype? | Yes — role is wrong abstraction | Factory takes player-id + overrides, no role parameter |
| Module location? | db/game_state.cljs | Data Foundation layer, adjacent to schema and queries |
| Opponent land-plays-left: 0 bug? | Investigate, fix if real | Confirmed bug: opponent can't play lands Turn 1. Fixed by shared defaults. |
| New opponent player-id? | :player-2 | Zero test file churn. ~26 production files change. |
| Module name? | db/game_state.cljs | Broader than player.cljs since it includes game entity factory |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Opponent Land-Play Bug
**Question explored:** Does opponent's land-plays-left: 0 actually block land plays?
**Sources:** engine/rules.cljs:562 (can-play-land?), bots/interceptor.cljs:187 (find-bot-land-to-play), events/phases.cljs:117 (turn start reset)
**Findings:** Yes — can-play-land? requires (pos? land-plays). Bot calls this. Turn start resets to 1, so only Turn 1 is affected.
**Conclusion:** Bug confirmed. Shared factory with land-plays-left: 1 default fixes it.

#### Max Hand Size Investigation
**Question explored:** Does missing :player/max-hand-size on opponent break cleanup discard?
**Sources:** db/queries.cljs:244-253 (get-max-hand-size), events/cleanup.cljs:47-72 (begin-cleanup)
**Findings:** No — get-max-hand-size returns default 7 when attribute missing.
**Conclusion:** Not a bug. But shared factory sets it explicitly for clarity.

### Dead-End Paths
None — all investigated approaches were viable; selection was based on design principles rather than dead ends.

### Open Concerns Raised
- 'Is :role comingling with :bot-archetype?' → Yes — eliminated role dispatch, factory is flat with overrides
- 'Is db/player.cljs still the right name?' → No — renamed to db/game_state.cljs since it includes game entity factory
close_reason: All success criteria met, reviewer approved

title: Implement selection hierarchy and generic zone-pick builder/executor for :discard
design:
## Goal
Prove the hierarchy pattern on the event layer by converting :discard from a custom builder to the generic zone-pick builder. The :discard EXECUTOR stays custom (cleanup-discard path requires it). View layer unchanged.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - events/selection/core.cljs:20-53 — build-selection-for-effect and execute-confirmed-selection multimethods, existing :default builder
   - events/selection/zone_ops.cljs:25-37 — build-discard-selection (current custom builder)
   - events/selection/zone_ops.cljs:116-130 — execute-confirmed-selection :discard (handles cleanup path via :selection/cleanup? flag — DO NOT REMOVE)
   - events/game.cljs:546-559 — build-cleanup-discard-selection (built directly, bypasses build-selection-for-effect — unaffected by this change)
   - engine/effects.cljs — execute-effect-impl :discard (returns {:db :needs-selection})

2. Write tests first (TDD — RED phase)
   - Test generic zone-pick builder output shape:
     - Given effect {:effect/type :discard :effect/count 2 :effect/selection :player}, assert builder returns selection with :selection/type :discard (NOT :zone-pick), :selection/pattern :zone-pick, :selection/select-count 2, :selection/zone :hand, :selection/card-source :hand, :selection/validation :exact, :selection/selected #{}
   - Test generic zone-pick executor:
     - Given db with 2 cards in hand and selection {:selection/selected #{card1 card2} :selection/target-zone :graveyard}, assert both cards moved to graveyard
   - Test hierarchy routing:
     - Call build-selection-for-effect with :discard effect → assert :selection/pattern :zone-pick on result
     - Call build-selection-for-effect with :chain-bounce effect → assert existing custom builder still handles it (hierarchy precedence)
   - Test cleanup-discard still works:
     - Build cleanup discard selection via game.cljs path → execute → assert grants expired (regression test)

3. Define the selection hierarchy in core.cljs
   - Create: (def selection-hierarchy (-> (make-hierarchy) (derive :discard :zone-pick)))
   - Update build-selection-for-effect multimethod: add :hierarchy #'selection-hierarchy to defmulti
   - Update execute-confirmed-selection multimethod: add :hierarchy #'selection-hierarchy to defmulti
   - Update build-chain-selection multimethod: add :hierarchy #'selection-hierarchy to defmulti
   - IMPORTANT: Existing defmethods on specific keywords (e.g., :chain-bounce, :storm-split) take precedence over parent patterns automatically — no changes needed to custom handlers

4. Implement generic zone-pick builder
   - Register as (defmethod build-selection-for-effect :zone-pick ...)
   - CRITICAL: Set :selection/type to (:effect/type effect), NOT :zone-pick — preserves view dispatch and executor dispatch for types with custom executors
   - Set :selection/pattern :zone-pick — new field for future view migration
   - Read :effect/count or :effect/select-count for :selection/select-count
   - Default :selection/zone to :hand (correct for :discard; future zone-pick types will override via effect fields or custom builder)
   - Default :selection/card-source to :hand
   - Default :selection/target-zone to :graveyard (new field on selection, used by generic executor)
   - Default :selection/validation to :exact
   - Set :selection/auto-confirm? false
   - Pass through player-id, object-id, remaining-effects as usual

5. Implement generic zone-pick executor
   - Register as (defmethod execute-confirmed-selection :zone-pick ...)
   - Read :selection/selected → reduce over them with zones/move-to-zone to :selection/target-zone
   - Support optional :selection/shuffle-after? flag
   - NOTE: :discard will NOT use this executor in this task because the specific :discard defmethod takes precedence in the hierarchy. This executor is tested directly and will be used by future zone-pick types (e.g., :graveyard-return) that don't have custom executors.

6. Migrate :discard builder only
   - Add (derive :discard :zone-pick) to hierarchy (already done in step 3)
   - Remove the defmethod registration at zone_ops.cljs:97-99 (the build-selection-for-effect :discard defmethod)
   - Remove build-discard-selection function at zone_ops.cljs:25-37 (no longer called)
   - KEEP execute-confirmed-selection :discard at zone_ops.cljs:116-130 (cleanup path needs it)
   - Verify: effect-triggered discards (Careful Study, etc.) route via hierarchy to zone-pick builder → produce correct selection → route to :discard executor (specific takes precedence)

7. GREEN phase — make tests pass

8. Verification
   - make validate passes
   - All existing discard card tests pass (Careful Study, etc.)
   - Cleanup discard tests pass (turn_test.cljs:341)
   - No new selection-type-specific builder code written for :discard

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Selection hierarchy defined in core.cljs with derive declarations
- [ ] build-selection-for-effect, execute-confirmed-selection, and build-chain-selection multimethods all use :hierarchy #'selection-hierarchy
- [ ] Generic zone-pick builder function registered on :zone-pick pattern
- [ ] Generic zone-pick builder sets :selection/type to original effect type (NOT :zone-pick) — view backward compatibility
- [ ] Generic zone-pick builder sets :selection/pattern :zone-pick — future view dispatch
- [ ] Generic zone-pick executor function registered on :zone-pick pattern
- [ ] Custom build-discard-selection function and its defmethod removed from zone_ops.cljs
- [ ] Custom execute-confirmed-selection :discard defmethod KEPT (cleanup path)
- [ ] Cleanup discard still works — turn_test.cljs:341 passes
- [ ] All existing discard card tests pass unchanged (Careful Study, etc.)
- [ ] Existing custom builders (chain-bounce, tutor, etc.) unaffected — hierarchy precedence verified
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Cleanup Discard**
The discard executor handles two paths via :selection/cleanup? flag:
- false/nil: standard discard (move to graveyard, lifecycle :standard handles remaining-effects)
- true: cleanup discard (move to graveyard, expire grants, lifecycle :finalized)
Cleanup discard is built directly in game.cljs:546-559, NOT through build-selection-for-effect. It manually sets :selection/type :discard. So the builder migration doesn't affect it. But the executor MUST stay because it checks :selection/cleanup?.
Migration path for executor: future task can either (a) make cleanup a separate selection type, or (b) add generic post-execution hooks to zone-pick.

**Edge Case: Hierarchy Precedence**
When both :zone-pick (parent) and :discard (child) have defmethods on execute-confirmed-selection, Clojure multimethods prefer the more specific child. This is correct for our design:
- Builder: only :zone-pick defmethod exists → :discard routes to it via hierarchy ✅
- Executor: both :zone-pick AND :discard defmethods exist → :discard takes precedence ✅
Test this explicitly to prevent regressions if defmethods are added/removed.

**Edge Case: Non-Derived Effect Types**
Effect types without derive declarations (e.g., :chain-bounce, :storm-split) are NOT affected. They either have their own defmethods (which fire directly) or fall through to :default. The hierarchy only applies to types that explicitly derive from a pattern. Verified safe — no change in behavior for non-derived types.

**Edge Case: :selection/target-zone Field**
This is a NEW field on selection state. Currently, executors hardcode the target zone (discard → :graveyard). The generic executor reads it from the selection. The builder must set it. For :discard, the default is :graveyard. Future zone-pick types will set different values (e.g., graveyard-return sets :hand).

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO setting :selection/type to :zone-pick on the selection state (view backward compat: must stay as original effect type, e.g., :discard)
- ❌ NO removing execute-confirmed-selection :discard (cleanup path requires it until separately migrated)
- ❌ NO separate routing table or config map alongside the hierarchy (single dispatch mechanism)
- ❌ NO adding :effect/zone or :effect/target-zone to existing card definitions in this task (defaults handle :discard; field additions are a future migration concern)
- ❌ NO modifying cleanup-discard builder in game.cljs (out of scope, works independently)
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 4 Epic 2: Setup Screen + Deck/Sideboard
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Setup screen is the app landing page (game no longer auto-starts)
- Deck selection UI via dropdown (Iggy Pop as built-in default, architected for more)
- Side-by-side maindeck/sideboard panels with click-to-swap interaction
- Card list grouped by card type (Lands, Instants, Sorceries, Artifacts) showing Nx format
- Sideboard swaps validate 60 main / ≤15 side with inline error display
- Opponent configuration: goldfish clock turns (numeric, default 4)
- Named presets saved to localStorage for configuration management
- Quick Start button loads most-recently-used preset and starts game immediately
- Save As / Delete preset management via dropdown UI
- Confirmation dialog before leaving game to return to setup
- Start Game button dispatches init-game with full config payload
- init-game-state refactored to accept config map (no more hardcoded make-test-deck)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] App opens to setup screen, not directly to game
- [ ] Can select deck from dropdown and see its contents grouped by type
- [ ] Maindeck and sideboard displayed side-by-side (grid-cols-[2fr_1fr])
- [ ] Can click-to-swap cards between main and side panels
- [ ] Validation shows red when main ≠ 60 or side > 15
- [ ] Start Game disabled when deck invalid
- [ ] Clicking Start Game creates properly initialized game with chosen config
- [ ] Goldfish clock turns configurable (default 4)
- [ ] Can save current config as named preset
- [ ] Can load/delete saved presets
- [ ] Quick Start loads MRU preset and starts game immediately
- [ ] Settings persist across browser refreshes via localStorage
- [ ] Confirmation dialog when navigating from game back to setup
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (no regressions)
- [ ] make validate passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoded deck in init-game-state (configurability: deck comes from setup config)
- ❌ NO game state in localStorage (complexity: only settings/presets, not live game state)
- ❌ NO blocking API calls (performance: all data is local, no network needed)
- ❌ NO Datascript schema changes for setup state (simplicity: setup config lives in app-db maps, not entities)
- ❌ NO drag-and-drop for sideboard (scope: click-to-swap is sufficient for v1)
- ❌ NO bot archetype selection (scope: deferred to Epic 5, only clock turns for now)
- ❌ NO deck editing beyond sideboard swaps (scope: card counts within a deck are fixed, only main↔side movement)

## Approach
Add :active-screen :setup as default. New events/setup.cljs namespace handles setup state management (deck selection, sideboard swaps, presets, clock config). New views/setup.cljs renders the setup screen with stacked sections: preset bar, deck dropdown, side-by-side main/side panels, opponent config, action buttons. New storage.cljs module handles localStorage EDN serialization for presets. Refactor init-game-state to accept a config map. Built-in deck definitions stay as constants (iggy-pop-decklist map with :deck/main and :deck/side).

## Architecture

### Data Model (app-db)
Setup state stored as plain maps in re-frame app-db:
- :setup/selected-deck — keyword id of chosen deck
- :setup/main-deck — vec of {:card/id :count} maps (mutable via swaps)
- :setup/sideboard — vec of {:card/id :count} maps
- :setup/clock-turns — integer (default 4)
- :setup/presets — map of preset-name → config (loaded from localStorage)
- :setup/last-preset — string name of MRU preset

### New Files
- events/setup.cljs — Setup event handlers
- subs/setup.cljs — Setup subscriptions
- views/setup.cljs — Setup screen components
- storage.cljs — localStorage EDN persistence

### Modified Files
- core.cljs — Change init dispatch, update root component routing
- events/game.cljs — Refactor init-game-state to accept config, remove make-test-deck
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs — Add iggy-pop-decklist constant with :deck/main and :deck/side

### Event Flow
1. App init → ::init-setup (load presets from localStorage, set defaults)
2. User selects deck → ::select-deck (populate main/side from built-in deck)
3. User swaps cards → ::move-to-side / ::move-to-main (validate counts)
4. User sets clock → ::set-clock-turns
5. User saves preset → ::save-preset (persist to localStorage)
6. User clicks Start Game → ::start-game (validate → dispatch ::init-game with config)
7. ::init-game receives {:main-deck [...] :clock-turns N} → builds game state
8. In-game New Game → confirm dialog → ::set-active-screen :setup

### Subscriptions
- ::setup-config, ::available-decks, ::current-main (grouped by type), ::current-side
- ::deck-valid? (main=60, side≤15), ::saved-presets, ::clock-turns

### UI Layout
Side-by-side grid layout (2fr 1fr):
- Left panel: Maindeck grouped by card type, each card has → button
- Right panel: Sideboard flat list, each card has ← button
- Header counts turn red on validation failure
- Preset dropdown + Save As at top
- Opponent clock + Start Game / Quick Start at bottom

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Game currently auto-starts with a hardcoded Iggy Pop deck. No way to configure the deck, swap sideboard cards, or persist settings. This blocks hand sculpting (Epic 4), deck import (Epic 3), and goldfish bots (Epic 5).

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- core.cljs:50-53 — Placeholder setup screen ("Coming Soon")
- core.cljs:80-96 — Screen routing already works (:setup/:game via :active-screen)
- events/game.cljs:25-65 — make-test-deck hardcodes Iggy Pop 60-card list
- events/game.cljs:68-151 — init-game-state takes no config, always uses make-test-deck
- events/game.cljs:154-157 — ::init-game event takes no parameters
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:344-351 — all-cards vector collects card definitions
- subs/game.cljs:13 — :active-screen subscription already exists

**External:**
- No external APIs needed — all data is local
- localStorage API is synchronous, suitable for EDN blob storage
- Tailwind CSS grid already available for side-by-side layout

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. App-db config with built-in deck constants ✓

**What it is:** Deck config lives in re-frame app-db as plain maps. Built-in decks defined as ClojureScript constants. localStorage stores only presets (named configs).

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed app-db structure — already uses flat maps for game state
- Checked re-frame patterns — app-db maps are idiomatic for UI state
- Verified localStorage works with pr-str/reader for EDN

**Pros:**
- Consistent with existing app-db patterns
- Simple — no schema changes needed
- Presets are just EDN blobs, trivial to serialize

**Cons:**
- No Datascript query power for deck data (but we don't need it)

**Chosen because:** Simplest approach that meets all requirements. Deck config is UI state, not game entity state.

#### 2. Deck entities in Datascript ❌

**What it is:** Add :deck/* and :sideboard/* entity types to Datascript schema. Query decks with Datalog.

**Why we looked at this:** Datascript is the existing data store, might be natural fit.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed db/schema.cljs — schema is for game entities (cards, players, objects)
- Deck config is setup-time data, not game-time data
- Would need schema migration and new entity types

**Pros:**
- Could use Datalog queries for deck filtering

**Cons:**
- Overengineered — deck config is simple key-value, not relational
- Schema changes for non-game data muddies the data model
- Datascript DB gets recreated on each game init

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Deck config is UI/setup state, not game entity state. Adding it to Datascript conflates two concerns.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We need complex deck queries (e.g., "find all decks containing Brain Freeze").

#### 3. Separate deck registry namespace ❌

**What it is:** Create a decks/ namespace with deck definitions as separate files, a registry, and a loader.

**Why we looked at this:** Might scale better for many decks.

**Investigation:**
- Only one deck (Iggy Pop) exists now
- Design says "architected for more" but doesn't require multi-deck infrastructure yet

**Pros:**
- Clean separation of deck data
- Easy to add new decks

**Cons:**
- Overengineered for one deck
- Extra files and indirection for no current benefit

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** YAGNI — one deck constant in cards/iggy_pop.cljs is sufficient. When more decks arrive (via deck import Epic 3), that epic can establish the pattern.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We're adding 3+ built-in decks and need organization.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Setup screen as landing page
- Deck selection dropdown (one built-in deck)
- Side-by-side sideboard swap UI with click-to-swap
- Goldfish clock turns config
- Named presets in localStorage
- Quick Start from MRU preset
- Confirmation dialog for game→setup navigation
- Refactoring init-game-state to accept config

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Hand sculpting / mulligan (Epic 4 — depends on this)
- Deck import from Moxfield/MTGGoldfish (Epic 3 — depends on this)
- Bot archetype selection (Epic 5 — depends on this)
- Card art or images (not in any current epic)
- Deck editing beyond sideboard swaps (not needed for practice tool)

### Open Questions
- Should Quick Start skip the setup screen entirely or show it briefly? (Default: skip entirely)
- Exact Tailwind color classes for validation errors? (Resolve during implementation, match existing theme)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Deck data model? | App-db only | No separate namespace, config in re-frame app-db maps |
| Sideboard UI level? | Click-to-swap | Per-card → buttons, validate 60/15, no drag-and-drop |
| Opponent config? | Goldfish clock only | Simple number input, defer bot archetypes to Epic 5 |
| Setup screen layout? | Single page, stacked sections | Preset bar → deck dropdown → deck panels → opponent → buttons |
| Deck picker style? | Dropdown with preview | Dropdown selects deck, card list shows below grouped by type |
| localStorage scope? | Named presets | Multiple saved configs, not just last-used. Save As + Delete UI |
| Nav safety? | Confirm dialog | "Leave current game?" before navigating back to setup |
| Preset UX? | Dropdown + Save As | Preset dropdown at top of setup, Save As prompts for name, X to delete |
| Card display format? | Names + quantities grouped by type | "4x Dark Ritual" format, grouped as Lands/Instants/Sorceries/Artifacts |
| Main/Side layout? | Side-by-side | grid-cols-[2fr_1fr], main left, side right, both scrollable |

### Open Concerns Raised
- Side-by-side vs stacked sideboard → Resolved: side-by-side for better swap UX
- Named presets vs last-used-only → User chose named presets, adds Save As/Delete UI
close_reason: All child tasks complete. Setup screen is landing page with deck panels, presets, sideboard swaps, clock config, new game, and restore-setup.
