title: Add bot protocol check in negotiate-priority before auto-passing
description: Add bot-priority-decision check in negotiate-priority so bots can respond to human spells on the stack. When a human yields and the opponent is a bot, call bot-priority-decision. If :pass → auto-pass as before. If action → transfer priority to bot.
design:
## Goal
negotiate-priority consults the bot protocol before auto-passing bot opponents. Enables future reactive archetypes without implementing any.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- events/game.cljs:864-916 — negotiate-priority function (pure fn on app-db)
- events/game.cljs:896-897 — the unconditional bot auto-pass: `(player-is-bot? gdb opp-eid)`
- bots/protocol.cljs:15-23 — get-bot-archetype: (db, player-id) → keyword | nil
- bots/protocol.cljs:26-34 — bot-priority-decision: (archetype, {:db :player-id}) → :pass | action-map
- bots/interceptor.cljs:1-15 — header comment to update
- Existing negotiate-priority tests: src/test/fizzle/events/priority_test.cljs (L700+)
- Existing interceptor tests: src/test/fizzle/bots/interceptor_test.cljs

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
New tests go in src/test/fizzle/events/priority_test.cljs alongside existing negotiate-priority tests.

**Test setup pattern** (from existing tests at L115-121):
```clojure
(let [db (-> (h/create-test-db {:mana {:black 1} :stops #{:main1}})
             (h/add-opponent {:bot-archetype :goldfish}))  ;; or :burn
      [db' obj-id] (h/add-card-to-zone db :dark-ritual :hand :player-1)
      game-db (rules/cast-spell db' :player-1 obj-id)  ;; puts spell on stack
      app-db {:game/db game-db}
      result (game/negotiate-priority app-db)]  ;; returns {:all-passed? bool}
  ...)
```

**For reactive bot test**: Create a test-only bot archetype. Two approaches:
- Option A: Use `with-redefs` on `definitions/get-spec` to temporarily register a reactive spec
- Option B: Register a new archetype in the test via `(d/transact\! conn [{:player/id :player-2 :player/bot-archetype :reactive-test}])` and add spec to registry

Recommend Option A — cleaner, no permanent registry changes:
```clojure
(def reactive-test-spec
  {:bot/name "Reactive Test"
   :bot/priority-rules
   [{:rule/mode :auto
     :rule/conditions [{:check :stack-has :type :spell :owner :opponent}
                       {:check :zone-contains :zone :hand :player :self :card-id :lightning-bolt}
                       {:check :has-untapped-source :color :red}]
     :rule/action {:action :cast-spell :card-id :lightning-bolt :target :opponent}}]})

(with-redefs [definitions/get-spec
              (fn [arch] (case arch
                           :reactive-test reactive-test-spec
                           (definitions/get-spec arch)))]
  ...)
```

Wait — `with-redefs` creates recursion since the fallback calls the original. Use a let binding:
```clojure
(let [original-get-spec definitions/get-spec]
  (with-redefs [definitions/get-spec
                (fn [arch] (if (= :reactive-test arch)
                             reactive-test-spec
                             (original-get-spec arch)))]
    ...))
```

**Test scenarios:**

a. **Reactive bot gets priority (core behavior change)**
   - Setup: human is active, opponent is :reactive-test bot with bolt in hand + untapped mountain
   - Human casts Dark Ritual → puts spell on stack
   - Call negotiate-priority
   - Assert: {:all-passed? false} — priority transferred to bot (not auto-passed)
   - Bug caught: the main bug — unconditional bot auto-pass

b. **Goldfish still auto-passed (regression)**
   - Setup: human is active, opponent is :goldfish
   - Human casts Dark Ritual → puts spell on stack
   - Call negotiate-priority
   - Assert: {:all-passed? true} — goldfish auto-passed (empty rules → :pass)
   - Bug caught: accidentally breaking goldfish by always transferring priority

c. **Burn with non-matching conditions auto-passed**
   - Setup: human is active, opponent is :burn, stack NOT empty (human spell on stack)
   - Burn's rules have :stack-empty condition which won't match
   - Call negotiate-priority
   - Assert: {:all-passed? true} — burn auto-passed (conditions don't match → :pass)
   - Bug caught: transferring priority to bots whose rules don't match

d. **Reactive bot with empty stack — auto-passed (conditions don't match)**
   - Setup: human is active, opponent is :reactive-test, stack is EMPTY
   - Call negotiate-priority
   - Assert: {:all-passed? true} — reactive bot's :stack-has condition doesn't match
   - Bug caught: giving priority to reactive bots when there's nothing to react to

e. **Auto-mode bypasses bot protocol check**
   - Setup: same as test (a) but set auto-mode to :resolving
   - Call negotiate-priority
   - Assert: {:all-passed? true} — auto-mode takes priority over bot check
   - Bug caught: bot protocol interfering with auto-mode (F6/resolving)

f. **Reactive bot conditions match but card not in hand (resolve-action returns :pass)**
   - Setup: opponent is :reactive-test but NO bolt in hand (only condition :stack-has matches)
   - Human casts spell → stack not empty
   - Call negotiate-priority
   - Assert: {:all-passed? true} — resolve-action can't find card → :pass → auto-pass
   - Bug caught: transferring priority when bot can't actually act

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] events/game.cljs: add require `[fizzle.bots.protocol :as bot-protocol]`
- [ ] events/game.cljs: add private helper:
      ```clojure
      (defn- bot-would-pass?
        "Check if bot player would pass priority in current game state.
         Returns true if bot has no action (should auto-pass).
         Pure function: (game-db, opponent-player-id) -> boolean"
        [game-db opponent-player-id]
        (let [archetype (bot-protocol/get-bot-archetype game-db opponent-player-id)]
          (if archetype
            (= :pass (bot-protocol/bot-priority-decision
                       archetype {:db game-db :player-id opponent-player-id}))
            true)))  ;; no archetype = not a bot = pass
      ```
      NOTE: takes player-id (not eid) because get-bot-archetype and bot-priority-decision both use player-id. The negotiate-priority scope already has `opponent-player-id` available (L890).
- [ ] events/game.cljs:896-897: change condition from:
      `(player-is-bot? gdb opp-eid)`
      to:
      `(and (player-is-bot? gdb opp-eid) (bot-would-pass? gdb opponent-player-id))`
- [ ] bots/interceptor.cljs:1-15: update header comment — change "All game events remain bot-unaware" to note that negotiate-priority now consults bot protocol for priority decisions
- [ ] Run make validate — all tests pass, lint clean, format clean

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Test: reactive bot spec with matching conditions → negotiate-priority returns {:all-passed? false}
- [ ] Test: goldfish (empty rules) → negotiate-priority returns {:all-passed? true}
- [ ] Test: burn with non-matching conditions (stack not empty) → negotiate-priority returns {:all-passed? true}
- [ ] Test: reactive bot with empty stack → negotiate-priority returns {:all-passed? true}
- [ ] Test: auto-mode (:resolving) bypasses bot protocol → {:all-passed? true} regardless of bot rules
- [ ] Test: reactive bot conditions match but card not in hand → {:all-passed? true}
- [ ] All existing tests pass (zero regressions) — specifically existing negotiate-priority tests at priority_test.cljs:700+
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Parameter confusion: player-id vs eid**
- negotiate-priority works with both eids and player-ids
- `player-is-bot?` takes eid (reads :player/bot-archetype from entity)
- `get-bot-archetype` and `bot-priority-decision` take player-id
- `bot-would-pass?` MUST take player-id, not eid
- The scope has `opponent-player-id` at L890 — use that

**Evaluation order matters**
- The auto-pass condition is an `or` chain (L895-903)
- `auto-mode` check is FIRST — if :resolving/:f6, bot protocol is never called
- `(player-is-bot? gdb opp-eid)` is checked BEFORE `bot-would-pass?` (short-circuit AND)
- If opponent is human, bot-would-pass? is never called — correct

**with-redefs for test-only bot spec**
- Use with-redefs on definitions/get-spec to avoid modifying the registry
- Must capture original fn to avoid recursion
- Alternative: add a :reactive-test spec to definitions.cljs (less clean, pollutes production)

**Double evaluation is acceptable**
- negotiate-priority evaluates bot-priority-decision once (to check pass/act)
- If not pass, interceptor evaluates again after yield event
- Same game state → same result. Cheap evaluation (iterate EDN, check datascript).

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO duplicating action dispatch logic in negotiate-priority (only check pass/act)
- ❌ NO modifying interceptor logic (it already handles bot-has-priority)
- ❌ NO mocking bot-priority-decision in tests (test through real specs + real evaluation)
- ❌ NO using opp-eid where player-id is needed (type confusion causes silent wrong behavior)
- ❌ NO adding test-only bot specs to production definitions.cljs
close_reason: Closed

title: Audit+fix black/ card tests (9 files)
description: First batch of card test audit. Run analyze-tests on all 9 black card test files, then fix all issues found: migrate legacy API usage, add missing mandatory categories, strengthen weak assertions.
design:
## Goal
Audit all 9 black card test files with analyze-tests skill, then fix every issue found. Establish the audit+fix pattern for subsequent directory batches.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (2 legacy migrations, ~5 missing tests to add, verify remaining 7 files)

## Files to Audit
1. src/test/fizzle/cards/black/cabal_ritual_test.cljs (138 lines, 8 tests)
2. src/test/fizzle/cards/black/crippling_fatigue_test.cljs (539 lines, 21 tests)
3. src/test/fizzle/cards/black/dark_ritual_test.cljs (78 lines, 4 tests)
4. src/test/fizzle/cards/black/duress_test.cljs (278 lines, 14 tests)
5. src/test/fizzle/cards/black/ill_gotten_gains_test.cljs (255 lines, 9 tests)
6. src/test/fizzle/cards/black/infernal_contract_test.cljs (156 lines, 11 tests)
7. src/test/fizzle/cards/black/necrologia_test.cljs (255 lines, 18 tests)
8. src/test/fizzle/cards/black/rain_of_filth_test.cljs (196 lines, 11 tests)
9. src/test/fizzle/cards/black/vendetta_test.cljs (231 lines, 11 tests)

## Pre-Audit State
| File | A | B | C | D | Tautological | Status |
|------|---|---|---|---|-------------|--------|
| cabal_ritual | EXACT | LEGACY | FULL | YES | NONE | Needs B migration |
| crippling_fatigue | EXACT | MODERN | FULL | YES | NONE | GOOD |
| dark_ritual | EXACT | LEGACY | PARTIAL | YES | NONE | Needs B migration + C fix |
| duress | EXACT | MODERN | FULL | YES | NONE | GOOD |
| ill_gotten_gains | EXACT | MODERN | FULL | NO | NONE | Needs D (storm) |
| infernal_contract | EXACT | MODERN | FULL | YES | NONE | GOOD |
| necrologia | EXACT | MODERN | FULL | YES | NONE | GOOD |
| rain_of_filth | EXACT | MODERN | PARTIAL | YES | NONE | Needs C fix (minor) |
| vendetta | EXACT | MODERN | FULL | YES | NONE | GOOD |

## Implementation

### Step 1: Run analyze-tests on all 9 files
Use hyperpowers:analyze-tests skill on src/test/fizzle/cards/black/.
Capture audit report. Compare findings against the Pre-Audit State table above.
If analyze-tests finds additional issues beyond those listed, fix them too.

### Step 2: Migrate legacy tests (2 files)

**cabal_ritual_test.cljs** — Replace rules/cast-spell + rules/resolve-spell with th/cast-and-resolve:
- Lines using legacy: ~70, 84, 100, 117, 134 (5 call sites)
- Cabal Ritual is a simple mana spell (no targets, no selection)
- Replace pattern: `(rules/cast-spell db :player-1 obj-id)` + `(rules/resolve-spell db-cast :player-1 obj-id)` → `(th/cast-and-resolve db :player-1 obj-id)`
- EXCEPTION: Threshold tests (lines ~100-137) may need to keep separate cast/resolve if they check intermediate state between cast and resolve. If so, use rules/cast-spell for cast + th/resolve-top for resolve (still modern resolution path).
- Update require: add fizzle.test-helpers :as th, remove rules require if no longer needed

**dark_ritual_test.cljs** — Same migration:
- Lines using legacy: ~36-37 (1 call site, used in multiple tests via let binding)
- Simple mana spell, straightforward th/cast-and-resolve replacement
- Update require: add fizzle.test-helpers :as th, remove rules require if no longer needed

### Step 3: Add missing mandatory category tests

**dark_ritual_test.cljs** — Add Category C zone guard:
- Add test: `test-cannot-cast-from-graveyard` — put Dark Ritual in graveyard, verify can-cast? returns false
- Pattern: `(let [[db obj-id] (th/add-card-to-zone (th/create-test-db) :player-1 dark-ritual/card :graveyard)] (is (not (rules/can-cast? db :player-1 obj-id))))`

**ill_gotten_gains_test.cljs** — Add Category D storm count:
- Add test: `test-casting-increments-storm-count` — cast IGG, verify storm count increments by 1
- Pattern: follow cabal_ritual_test storm count pattern but with modern helpers
- IGG needs {2}{B}{B} mana — ensure test db has sufficient mana

**rain_of_filth_test.cljs** — Assess Category C completeness:
- Currently has: without mana, from graveyard
- Rain of Filth CAN be cast with no lands (it just does nothing) — no additional guard needed
- Verdict: ACCEPTABLE as-is. Rain of Filth has no targets and no zone requirements beyond hand.

### Step 4: Verify 5 already-good files
Read each file, confirm analyze-tests found no additional issues:
- crippling_fatigue_test.cljs (21 tests, comprehensive)
- duress_test.cljs (14 tests, comprehensive)
- infernal_contract_test.cljs (11 tests, good edge cases)
- necrologia_test.cljs (18 tests, complex pay-x pattern)
- vendetta_test.cljs (11 tests, good targeting + edge cases)

### Step 5: Run make validate
Ensure all tests pass, lint clean, format clean.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 9 black test files reviewed by analyze-tests (audit report captured)
- [ ] cabal_ritual_test.cljs migrated: zero rules/cast-spell or rules/resolve-spell calls
- [ ] dark_ritual_test.cljs migrated: zero rules/cast-spell or rules/resolve-spell calls
- [ ] dark_ritual_test.cljs has cannot-cast-from-graveyard test (Category C)
- [ ] ill_gotten_gains_test.cljs has storm count increment test (Category D)
- [ ] Verification: `grep -r 'rules/cast-spell\|rules/resolve-spell' src/test/fizzle/cards/black/` returns empty
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] Test count >= 117 (current ~117 across 9 files; expect ~119+ after additions)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Legacy migration changes test behavior**
Cabal Ritual's threshold tests check intermediate state between cast and resolve (graveyard card count at threshold=7). The th/cast-and-resolve helper combines both steps. If a test needs to inspect state AFTER cast but BEFORE resolve, keep the two-step pattern but upgrade resolve to th/resolve-top instead of rules/resolve-spell. Read the test carefully before blindly replacing.

**Edge Case: Cabal Ritual threshold conditional**
Cabal Ritual has :card/conditional-effects with threshold condition. The cast+resolve must test both paths: (1) graveyard has 7+ cards → produces {B}{B}{B}{B}{B}, (2) graveyard has <7 → produces {B}{B}{B}. Ensure threshold tests still exercise both paths after migration.

**Edge Case: IGG partial resolution**
Ill-Gotten Gains uses resolution/resolve-one-item (partial resolution) because it has 5 effects with interactive selections between them. The storm count test must cast the spell but does NOT need to fully resolve it — storm increments on cast, not resolve. Test can cast and check storm count immediately.

**Reference Implementation: How th/cast-and-resolve works**
See test_helpers.cljs:~280-300. It: (1) asserts can-cast? true, (2) calls rules/cast-spell, (3) calls resolution/resolve-one-item. Returns the post-resolve db. For spells that produce selections on resolve, it returns {:db :selection} — but cabal_ritual and dark_ritual are non-interactive, so it returns just db.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO removing existing passing tests — only add or strengthen
- NO using rules/cast-spell in new test code — always th/cast-and-resolve or th/cast-with-target
- NO tautological assertions (some?, string?, any?) — exact values only
- NO hand-built selection maps — use production builders + th/confirm-selection
- NO changing card definitions to make tests pass — tests must match production cards
- NO skipping make validate before declaring done
close_reason: Audited+fixed all 9 black card tests. Commit 3baa0e6. 3319 tests, 0 failures.

title: Add selection lifecycle metadata support to confirm-selection-impl
description: Foundation task: add :selection/lifecycle metadata support to confirm-selection-impl with backward compatibility. Introduces build-chain-selection multimethod. Existing executors continue working via return-shape fallback while migration proceeds.
design:
## Goal
Add `:selection/lifecycle` metadata support to `confirm-selection-impl` and introduce the `build-chain-selection` multimethod. This establishes the new mechanism WITH backward compatibility — existing executors continue working via return-shape inspection as fallback, while new/migrated executors use builder-declared lifecycle.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - `events/selection/core.cljs:129-176` — current `confirm-selection-impl` with return-shape inspection (3-way cond on `:pending-selection`, `:finalized?`, and `:else`)
   - `events/selection/core.cljs:61-78` — continuation system (`apply-continuation` multimethod)
   - `events/selection/costs.cljs` — finalized executors that return `:clear-selected-card? true` (7 occurrences at lines 297, 330, 348, 370, 373, 399, 419)
   - `events/selection/library.cljs` — finalized executors (scry, pile-choice, peek-and-reorder) that do NOT return `:clear-selected-card?`

2. Write tests first (TDD)
   Test scenarios with concrete assertions:

   **Standard lifecycle:**
   - Given selection with `:selection/lifecycle :standard`, `:selection/remaining-effects [effect1]`, and executor returns `{:db db}`
   - Assert: remaining-effects are applied to db (effect1 executed)
   - Assert: `cleanup-selection-source` is called (spell moves off stack)
   - Assert: `:game/pending-selection` is dissoc'd from app-db
   - Assert: continuation applied if `:selection/on-complete` present

   **Finalized lifecycle:**
   - Given selection with `:selection/lifecycle :finalized` and executor returns `{:db db}`
   - Assert: remaining-effects are NOT applied
   - Assert: `:game/pending-selection` is dissoc'd from app-db
   - Assert: continuation applied if `:selection/on-complete` present

   **Finalized lifecycle with clear-selected-card:**
   - Given selection with `:selection/lifecycle :finalized` and `:selection/clear-selected-card? true`
   - Assert: `:game/selected-card` is dissoc'd from app-db

   **Chaining lifecycle:**
   - Given selection with `:selection/lifecycle :chaining`, register a test `build-chain-selection` defmethod that returns a next-selection map
   - Assert: `build-chain-selection` is called with post-execution db and selection
   - Assert: app-db has `:game/pending-selection` set to the chain-built selection
   - Assert: `:selection/on-complete` propagated to chained selection

   **Chaining lifecycle with nil chain (conditional chaining):**
   - Given selection with `:selection/lifecycle :chaining`, register `build-chain-selection` that returns nil
   - Assert: falls through to standard behavior (remaining-effects applied, cleanup called)

   **No lifecycle (backward compat default):**
   - Given selection WITHOUT `:selection/lifecycle` and executor returns `{:db db}`
   - Assert: standard path (same as `:standard`)

   **Old return shape backward compat — finalized:**
   - Given selection WITHOUT `:selection/lifecycle` and executor returns `{:db db :finalized? true :clear-selected-card? true}`
   - Assert: finalized path still works (no remaining-effects, card cleared)

   **Old return shape backward compat — chaining:**
   - Given selection WITHOUT `:selection/lifecycle` and executor returns `{:db db :pending-selection next-sel}`
   - Assert: chaining path still works (next selection set on app-db)

3. Implementation checklist
   - [ ] `events/selection/core.cljs` — Add `build-chain-selection` multimethod:
     ```clojure
     (defmulti build-chain-selection
       "Build the next selection for a chaining lifecycle.
        Dispatches on :selection/type of the completed selection.
        Returns next selection map, or nil for conditional chaining
        (no chain needed, fall through to standard)."
       (fn [_db selection] (:selection/type selection)))

     (defmethod build-chain-selection :default [_db _selection] nil)
     ```
   - [ ] `events/selection/core.cljs` — Rewrite `confirm-selection-impl` to check `:selection/lifecycle` FIRST:
     ```clojure
     (let [lifecycle (or (:selection/lifecycle selection) :legacy)]
       (case lifecycle
         :chaining  (let [next-sel (build-chain-selection (:db result) selection)]
                      (if next-sel
                        ;; chain to next selection
                        ...
                        ;; nil = conditional, fall to standard
                        (standard-path ...)))
         :finalized (finalized-path ... (read :selection/clear-selected-card? from selection))
         :standard  (standard-path ...)
         :legacy    (legacy-return-shape-inspection ...)))
     ```
   - [ ] Handle `:selection/clear-selected-card?` on selection map for finalized lifecycle (costs builders will set this in migration tasks)
   - [ ] Preserve full backward compat: when `:selection/lifecycle` is absent, use existing cond on `:pending-selection`/`:finalized?` return keys (the `:legacy` path)
   - [ ] Update `execute-confirmed-selection` docstring to note that lifecycle is now builder-declared, return shape no longer drives framework behavior when `:selection/lifecycle` is present

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `confirm-selection-impl` reads `:selection/lifecycle` from selection map when present
- [ ] When `:selection/lifecycle` absent, falls back to return-shape inspection (zero regressions)
- [ ] `build-chain-selection` multimethod exists with `:default` method returning nil
- [ ] `:standard` lifecycle: remaining-effects applied, cleanup called, continuation applied
- [ ] `:finalized` lifecycle: no remaining-effects, selection cleared, continuation applied
- [ ] `:finalized` lifecycle reads `:selection/clear-selected-card?` from selection map
- [ ] `:chaining` lifecycle calls `build-chain-selection`, chains to result, propagates `on-complete`
- [ ] `:chaining` with nil from `build-chain-selection` falls through to standard behavior
- [ ] All existing tests pass (zero regressions) — `make test` green
- [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: `:clear-selected-card?` migration**
- 7 finalized executors in costs.cljs return `{:finalized? true :clear-selected-card? true}`
- 3 finalized executors in library.cljs and 1 in storm.cljs return `{:finalized? true}` WITHOUT `:clear-selected-card?`
- With lifecycle-declared finalized, executor returns only `{:db db}` — flag must come from builder
- Solution: `confirm-selection-impl` reads `:selection/clear-selected-card?` from selection map for lifecycle-declared finalized; reads from executor return for legacy path
- Builders will set this flag in subsequent migration tasks

**Edge Case: Conditional finalized/chaining executors**
- Several executors (`:discard-specific-cost`, `:return-land-cost`, `:x-mana-cost`, `:cast-time-targeting`, `:ability-cast-targeting`) have paths that either chain or finalize depending on game state (e.g., chain to mana-allocation if generic cost, finalize if not)
- With ADR-013, the builder determines lifecycle at creation time based on effect data
- This is a MIGRATION concern for subsequent tasks, not this task
- This task just ensures the mechanism handles all three lifecycle values correctly

**Edge Case: Chaining nil fallthrough**
- When `build-chain-selection` returns nil for a `:chaining` selection, this is conditional chaining (expected behavior, not an error)
- Example: tutor with no pile-choice config — builder sets `:chaining`, chain builder returns nil, standard path applies
- Must NOT treat nil as an error — fall through to standard behavior

**Edge Case: Lifecycle and old return shapes both present**
- During migration, a builder may set `:selection/lifecycle :finalized` while the executor still returns `{:db db :finalized? true}`
- Lifecycle metadata takes precedence — if `:selection/lifecycle` is present, ignore executor return flags
- Old `:pending-selection` and `:finalized?` keys on executor return are simply ignored when lifecycle is declared

**Reference Implementation**
- Pattern to follow: `apply-continuation` multimethod at core.cljs:61-78 — same dispatch-on-keyword pattern
- Study `confirm-selection-impl` at core.cljs:129-176 for current 3-way cond structure

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO modifying any executor implementations in this task (migration is subsequent tasks — this task only changes the framework)
- ❌ NO removing old return-shape inspection (backward compatibility must be preserved until all executors migrated)
- ❌ NO functions stored in selection maps (use `build-chain-selection` multimethod, not embedded fns)
- ❌ NO changing card definitions (orchestration-internal only)
- ❌ NO treating nil from `build-chain-selection` as an error (it means conditional chaining — fall through to standard)
close_reason: All three phases complete: ADR-013 lifecycle, ADR-014 modal unification, ADR-015 pre-cast pipeline. Reviewer approved.

title: Test gap: modal spell selection pipeline untested end-to-end
description: The validate-selection contains? bug on vector candidates shipped because test helpers (cast-mode-with-target) bypass the selection→validation→confirmation pipeline entirely. This is either a missing integration test or a deeper testing strategy problem.
design:
## Problem
cast-mode-with-target sets :object/chosen-mode directly via d/db-with (test_helpers.cljs:295), skipping:
1. toggle-selection-impl (selection dispatch)
2. validate-selection (candidate membership check)
3. confirm-selection-handler (lifecycle routing)
4. execute-confirmed-selection :spell-mode (mode storage)
5. apply-continuation :cast-after-spell-mode (cast initiation)

This allowed a contains?-on-vector bug in validate-selection to ship undetected. Blue Elemental Blast has the same latent gap.

## Scope Options
**Option A (narrow):** Add integration tests that exercise the full app-db selection pipeline for modal spells — toggle, validate, confirm, continue. Covers the specific gap.

**Option B (broader):** Audit all test helpers for pipeline bypass patterns. cast-with-target, cast-and-resolve, confirm-selection — do any skip production validation steps that could hide bugs?

## Evidence
- Bug: validation.cljs:70 used contains? on vector (checks indices not values)
- Fix: commit c6e1003 coerces to set
- All 3122 tests passed WITH the bug present
- Blue Elemental Blast (existing modal card) has the same gap — its tests also use cast-mode-with-target
close_reason: ADR-019 implemented: confirm-selection-impl validates internally + processes deferred entries, toggle-selection-impl is pure (returns data, no dispatch), cast-mode-with-target uses production pipeline. 12 new tests. 3134 total tests passing.

title: Flash of Insight stuck on stack when cast from flashback — loops back to selection
description: When Flash of Insight is cast via flashback, it gets stuck on the stack. Each attempt to resolve it pops back into a selection dialog instead of completing resolution. Reported during live play 2026-04-06.
design:
## Bug Description
Flash of Insight stuck on stack when cast from flashback — loops back to selection instead of completing resolution.

## Root Cause
`finalized-path` in `events/selection/core.cljs:381-392` does NOT call `cleanup-selection-source`. When Flash of Insight resolves:
1. `:peek-and-select` (lifecycle `:chaining`) — user picks 1 card for hand
2. Chains to `:order-bottom` (lifecycle `:finalized`) — user orders remainder on bottom
3. `finalized-path` clears selection but never moves spell off stack
4. Spell stays on stack → player tries to resolve again → peek-and-select loop

Affects ANY spell with `:effect/order-remainder? true` that chains to `:order-bottom`/`:order-top`. Flashback makes it worse because spell should exile, not just go to graveyard.

## Fix
`finalized-path` must call `cleanup-selection-source` when selection has `:selection/spell-id`, same as `standard-path` does at line 372.

## Regression Test
Full flashback resolution: cast from graveyard → resolve → peek-and-select → order-bottom → verify spell exiled and off stack.
notes:
## Fix Status: FIXED

**Root Cause:** `finalized-path` in `events/selection/core.cljs` never called `cleanup-selection-source`. When a chaining selection (e.g. `:peek-and-select`) chains to a finalized selection (e.g. `:order-bottom`), the spell stays on the stack permanently — the player can keep trying to resolve it, creating an infinite selection loop.

**Fix:** `finalized-path` now calls `cleanup-selection-source` when the selection carries `:selection/remaining-effects` (indicating a resolution-phase selection). Pre-cast finalized selections (targeting, mana-allocation, spell-mode) never carry this key, so they're unaffected.

**Evidence:**
- 2 regression tests added: flashback full resolution + normal cast full resolution with order-bottom chain
- 3389/3389 tests pass (0 failures)
- make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

**Files changed:**
- `src/main/fizzle/events/selection/core.cljs` — finalized-path gains selection param + conditional cleanup
- `src/test/fizzle/cards/blue/flash_of_insight_test.cljs` — 2 regression tests
close_reason: Fixed: finalized-path now calls cleanup-selection-source for resolution-phase selections

title: Test Quality Improvement: Fizzle Test Suite
design:
## Goal
Improve test effectiveness by removing 20 RED tests, strengthening 164 YELLOW tests, and adding missing corner case coverage for the highest-risk modules.

## Analysis Summary
- 1110 tests analyzed across engine, events, cards, history
- 20 RED (2%) - provide false confidence, must remove/replace
- 164 YELLOW (15%) - weak assertions or partial coverage, must strengthen
- 926 GREEN (83%) - genuine quality
- 38+ missing corner cases identified

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 20 RED tests removed or replaced with meaningful tests
- [ ] Top YELLOW patterns addressed (some? removal, card def consolidation)
- [ ] HIGH priority missing corner cases covered
- [ ] make validate passes after all changes

## Anti-patterns
- Adding tests that only check != nil
- Adding tests that verify mock behavior (none in this codebase anyway)
- Keeping tautological tests for coverage
- Adding happy-path-only tests
close_reason: All 4 success criteria met: 20 RED tests removed, YELLOW patterns strengthened, 7 corner cases covered (10 new tests), make validate passes (1237 tests, 0 failures). Bug fix: grant-expired? nil handling.

title: Consistent card sorting and grouping across all zones and dialogs
description: Cards should be sorted and grouped consistently in most zones: hand, battlefield, selection dialogs (tutor, discard), setup deck lists. Sorting order: group by card type (Lands, Instants, Sorceries, Artifacts), then alphabetical within each group. EXCEPTIONS: Stack must preserve resolution order (top-to-bottom). Graveyard needs a toggle — default to sorted/grouped for easy scanning, but toggle to chronological order when graveyard order matters (e.g. checking what was milled recently, Ill-Gotten Gains choices). Consider: toggle button on graveyard panel header, extract setup screen's grouping logic into shared utility, apply to hand view, tutor/selection modals, battlefield, discard selection.
close_reason: Epic fizzle-d4ib completed: consistent CMC+name sorting across hand, battlefield (land/non-land split), graveyard (castable partition + toggle), all selection dialogs, opening hand, and scry. Sort utility in engine/sorting.cljs.

title: Feature: Turn Structure UI
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Turn counter displays current turn number
- Phase bar shows all 8 phases (untap, upkeep, draw, main1, combat, main2, end, cleanup)
- Current phase is visually highlighted
- 'Next Phase' button advances to next phase
- Button changes to 'New Turn' when at cleanup phase
- Clicking 'New Turn' starts the next turn (calls start-turn event)
- UI positioned at top of screen, above battlefield

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Turn number displays and updates when new turn starts
- [ ] All 8 phases visible in horizontal strip
- [ ] Current phase visually distinct from others
- [ ] Next Phase button dispatches ::advance-phase event
- [ ] Button text changes to 'New Turn' at cleanup phase
- [ ] New Turn button dispatches ::start-turn event
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO auto-advance of phases (user wants manual control for practice)
- ❌ NO complex animations/transitions (POC - keep simple)
- ❌ NO new subscriptions when existing ones work (use ::current-phase, ::current-turn)
- ❌ NO separate component files (POC - add to core.cljs like other components)

## Approach
Add a turn-bar component to core.cljs following existing patterns (mana-pool-view, life-view). Uses existing subscriptions and events - no engine changes needed.

## Architecture
- src/fizzle/core.cljs - Add turn-bar component, insert at top of app
- Uses: subs/game.cljs ::current-phase, ::current-turn (existing)
- Dispatches: events/game.cljs ::advance-phase, ::start-turn (existing)

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Turn/phase structure exists in engine but is invisible to user. Cannot practice turn sequencing or test phase-dependent behavior without UI.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- src/fizzle/subs/game.cljs:73-83 - ::current-phase and ::current-turn subscriptions exist
- src/fizzle/events/game.cljs:173-175 - phases vector defines order
- src/fizzle/events/game.cljs:240-250 - ::advance-phase and ::start-turn events exist
- src/fizzle/core.cljs - UI components follow Reagent + Tailwind pattern

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Display turn counter
- Display phase bar with current phase highlighted
- Next Phase / New Turn button

**Out of scope:**
- Opponent turn support (single player for now)
- Phase-specific styling/theming
- Keyboard shortcuts
close_reason: All success criteria met. Turn-bar component implemented with turn counter, 8-phase strip, current phase highlighting, and Next Phase/New Turn button. Commit a861b40.

title: Decompose selection.cljs — separate mechanism from policy
description:
## The Problem

`selection.cljs` is ~1440 lines handling 11 selection types, 11 `defmethod execute-confirmed-selection` implementations, and 12 event registrations. It does the work of a subsystem while wearing the clothes of a single namespace.

### Why size alone isn't the issue

The real problem is that different *concerns* share one space:
- **Library operations:** tutor, peek-and-select, pile-choice, scry
- **Hand operations:** discard, cleanup-discard
- **Targeting:** cast-time-targeting, ability-targeting, player-target
- **Cost selection:** x-mana-cost, exile-cards-cost
- **Zone operations:** graveyard-return

Each type has a builder, a confirm handler, and specific execution logic. The builder/confirm pattern is consistent — that's good — but consistency of *shape* doesn't mean unity of *concern*.

### Growth problem

When you add a new card that needs a new selection type, you modify the largest file in the codebase. Plus modals.cljs for the UI, subs.cljs for the cards subscription, and game.cljs for initiation. That's 4 files for every new player-choice type.

### Specific overlaps that beg for unification

- `:discard` and `:cleanup-discard` differ only in what happens after (continue resolution vs expire grants). That's a flag, not a type.
- `:cast-time-targeting` and `:ability-targeting` both select targets; they differ in what happens with the result. Context, not type.

### The Rich Hickey question

"Are we complecting mechanism and policy?" Selection is a *mechanism* (show cards, let player pick, return choices). What you do with the selected items is *policy* (move to graveyard, return to hand, set as target). Right now mechanism and policy are tangled together in every defmethod.

### Proposed direction (needs brainstorming)

Several possible approaches:

**Approach A: Subdirectory decomposition**
```
events/selection/
  core.cljs       — shared helpers, multimethod, confirm-selection-impl
  targeting.cljs   — cast-time + ability targeting
  library.cljs     — tutor, peek-and-select, pile-choice, scry
  discard.cljs     — discard, cleanup-discard
  costs.cljs       — x-mana-cost, exile-cards-cost
  graveyard.cljs   — graveyard-return
```

**Approach B: Mechanism/policy split**
```
events/selection.cljs     — the mechanism (toggle, confirm, cancel, state machine)
events/selection_types.cljs — the policy (what each confirmed selection does)
```

**Approach C: Data-driven selection registry**
```clojure
(def selection-types
  {:tutor    {:builder build-tutor-selection
              :executor execute-tutor-selection}
   :discard  {:builder build-discard-selection
              :executor execute-discard-selection}
   ...})
```

### Also consider

- `resolve-spell-with-selection` (107 lines) conflates fizzle checking, target resolution, and selection building. Could be decomposed into phases.
- `reorder-library-for-scry` is pure zone position manipulation — belongs in `engine/zones.cljs`.
- The `confirm-selection-impl` wrapper handles 3 different return shapes (`{:db}`, `{:db :pending-selection}`, `{:db :finalized?}`). Could standardize.

### Files involved

- `events/selection.cljs` — the 1440-line file being decomposed
- `views/modals.cljs` — `selection-modal` router has parallel `cond` dispatch (lines 605-674)
- `subs/game.cljs` — `::selection-cards` subscription routes on selection type
- `events/game.cljs` — `resolve-one-item` calls into selection resolution functions

### Risks

- Namespace extraction risks breaking requires (learned this lesson before — check ALL requires)
- `defn-` private functions become public when moved (known pattern)
- The multimethod must remain in one namespace for open dispatch
- Test files import selection events — keyword namespaces change
close_reason: All 6 extraction tasks completed — selection.cljs fully decomposed into events/selection/ subdirectory

title: Task 1: Migrate All Test Files to Shared Helpers
design:
## Goal
Migrate all 29 test files that define their own create-test-db to use the shared fizzle.test-helpers namespace. test_helpers.cljs and test_helpers_test.cljs already exist and are validated. This task is purely mechanical migration + commit.

## Pre-conditions
- src/test/fizzle/test_helpers.cljs EXISTS (uncommitted) with: create-test-db, add-card-to-zone, add-cards-to-library, add-cards-to-graveyard, get-zone-count, get-object-zone, get-hand-count, add-opponent
- src/test/fizzle/test_helpers_test.cljs EXISTS (uncommitted) with 11 tests

## Effort Estimate
8-12 hours (mechanical migration across 29 files in batches of ~5, with make validate between batches)

## Implementation

### 1. Validate existing helpers work
- Run: make test (confirm test_helpers_test.cljs passes)
- If any failures, fix before proceeding

### 2. Migrate test files in batches of ~5

For each file:
a. Add [fizzle.test-helpers :as th] to requires
b. Remove local create-test-db, add-card-to-zone, add-cards-to-library, add-cards-to-graveyard, get-zone-count, get-object-zone, get-hand-count, add-opponent definitions
c. Replace calls: create-test-db -> th/create-test-db, add-card-to-zone -> th/add-card-to-zone, etc.
d. For files with custom mana (careful_study, intuition): change (create-test-db) to (th/create-test-db {:mana {:blue 1}})
e. Remove zone-specific helpers (add-artifact-to-battlefield, add-land-to-battlefield) — replace with (th/add-card-to-zone db card-id :battlefield player-id)
f. Preserve any file-specific helpers that are NOT duplicated elsewhere
g. Run make validate after each batch of 5 files

Complete file list (29 files):
1. src/test/fizzle/cards/basic_lands_test.cljs
2. src/test/fizzle/cards/brain_freeze_test.cljs
3. src/test/fizzle/cards/cabal_ritual_test.cljs
4. src/test/fizzle/cards/careful_study_test.cljs
5. src/test/fizzle/cards/cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs
6. src/test/fizzle/cards/city_of_traitors_test.cljs
7. src/test/fizzle/cards/dark_ritual_test.cljs
8. src/test/fizzle/cards/flash_of_insight_test.cljs
9. src/test/fizzle/cards/ill_gotten_gains_test.cljs
10. src/test/fizzle/cards/intuition_test.cljs
11. src/test/fizzle/cards/led_test.cljs
12. src/test/fizzle/cards/lotus_petal_test.cljs
13. src/test/fizzle/cards/mental_note_test.cljs
14. src/test/fizzle/cards/merchant_scroll_test.cljs
15. src/test/fizzle/cards/opt_test.cljs
16. src/test/fizzle/cards/orims_chant_test.cljs
17. src/test/fizzle/cards/polluted_delta_test.cljs
18. src/test/fizzle/cards/recoup_test.cljs
19. src/test/fizzle/cards/underground_river_test.cljs
20. src/test/fizzle/db/queries_test.cljs
21. src/test/fizzle/engine/cob_event_trigger_test.cljs
22. src/test/fizzle/engine/trigger_db_integration_test.cljs
23. src/test/fizzle/events/integration/mana_allocation_test.cljs
24. src/test/fizzle/events/pile_choice_test.cljs
25. src/test/fizzle/events/play_land_test.cljs
26. src/test/fizzle/events/selection/costs_allocation_test.cljs
27. src/test/fizzle/events/selection/storm_split_test.cljs
28. src/test/fizzle/events/tap_land_test.cljs
29. src/test/fizzle/events/tutor_multi_select_test.cljs

### 3. Verify duplication eliminated in migrated files
- grep -r 'defn create-test-db' src/test/ should return ONLY test_helpers.cljs
- grep -r 'defn add-card-to-zone' src/test/ should return test_helpers.cljs + 11 unmigrated files (see Key Considerations)

### 4. Files NOT migrated in this task

**32 init-game-state files (DO NOT TOUCH):**
These use fizzle.db.init/init-game-state and have an INCOMPATIBLE add-card-to-zone signature:
- Signature: [db player-id card zone] — takes card MAP (not keyword), transacts def inline
- Returns: [obj-id db] (reversed from shared helper's [db obj-id])
- Cannot use shared th/add-card-to-zone because their db doesn't pre-load card defs

Files with incompatible add-card-to-zone that remain after this migration:
1. src/test/fizzle/engine/stack_test.cljs (own create-game-db, standard-ish signature)
2. src/test/fizzle/engine/grants_test.cljs (init-game-state, reversed tuple)
3. src/test/fizzle/engine/targeting_test.cljs (init-game-state, reversed tuple)
4. src/test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs (init-game-state, reversed tuple)
5. src/test/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis_test.cljs (init-game-state, reversed tuple)
6. src/test/fizzle/cards/ray_of_revelation_test.cljs (init-game-state, reversed tuple)
7. src/test/fizzle/events/mode_selection_test.cljs (init-game-state, reversed tuple)
8. src/test/fizzle/cards/seal_of_cleansing_test.cljs (init-game-state, reversed tuple)
9. src/test/fizzle/events/resolve_all_test.cljs (init-game-state, reversed tuple)
10. src/test/fizzle/events/auto_confirm_test.cljs (own create-game-db, defn-, standard signature)
11. src/test/fizzle/history/interceptor_test.cljs (own create-game-db, defn-, standard signature)

These 11 files need a FUTURE task that either:
a. Adds a card-map variant to test_helpers (th/add-card-def-to-zone), or
b. Migrates them to create-test-db (scope change for those tests)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] src/test/fizzle/test_helpers_test.cljs passes (all 11 tests green)
- [ ] Zero test files define their own create-test-db (verify: grep returns only test_helpers.cljs)
- [ ] All 29 migrated files use th/add-card-to-zone instead of local definition
- [ ] All return types in migrated files use [db result] tuple convention
- [ ] All existing tests pass identically (make validate green)
- [ ] No lint or format warnings
- [ ] Remaining add-card-to-zone definitions are ONLY in the 11 documented unmigrated files

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**CRITICAL: Two incompatible add-card-to-zone signatures exist**
- Standard (29 create-test-db files): [db card-id zone player-id] returns [db obj-id] — card-id is keyword, card defs pre-loaded
- Init-game-state variant (9 files): [db player-id card zone] returns [obj-id db] — card is full map, transacted inline
- Shared helper uses standard signature. Init-game-state files CANNOT use it without either adding a new helper variant or migrating their DB setup.
- DO NOT attempt to migrate init-game-state files in this task — different scope, different risk.

**Edge Case: Custom mana files**
- careful_study_test.cljs and intuition_test.cljs initialize with non-zero mana
- MUST use opts: (th/create-test-db {:mana {:blue 1}})
- Verify exact mana values match each file's original setup

**Edge Case: queries_test.cljs also defines add-card-to-zone-with-position**
- queries_test.cljs:58 defines add-card-to-zone-with-position (separate from add-card-to-zone)
- This is a unique helper, NOT duplicated elsewhere — PRESERVE it in queries_test.cljs
- Only migrate create-test-db and add-card-to-zone from this file

**Edge Case: Zone-specific helpers**
- add-artifact-to-battlefield / add-land-to-battlefield are just add-card-to-zone with hardcoded :battlefield
- Replace with (th/add-card-to-zone db card-id :battlefield player-id)
- May need to update variable names in let bindings

**Edge Case: Library position**
- Shared add-card-to-zone sets :object/position 0 when zone is :library
- Verify files that add cards to library don't rely on position being ABSENT

**Edge Case: Invalid card-id**
- Shared helper throws informative error on nil card-eid
- Existing local helpers may silently create nil-card objects — migration may surface latent bugs
- If a test fails after migration, check whether the card-id keyword matches a card def in iggy-pop

**Batch strategy**
- Migrate ~5 files at a time, run make validate between batches
- If a batch fails, narrow to individual file to find the issue
- Suggested batches:
  - Batch 1: basic_lands, brain_freeze, cabal_ritual, careful_study, cephalid_coliseum
  - Batch 2: city_of_traitors, dark_ritual, flash_of_insight, ill_gotten_gains, intuition
  - Batch 3: led, lotus_petal, mental_note, merchant_scroll, opt
  - Batch 4: orims_chant, polluted_delta, recoup, underground_river, queries_test
  - Batch 5: cob_event_trigger, trigger_db_integration, mana_allocation, pile_choice, play_land
  - Batch 6: costs_allocation, storm_split, tap_land, tutor_multi_select

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in shared helpers (helpers must be card-agnostic)
- ❌ NO breaking existing test assertions during migration (pure extraction)
- ❌ NO changing test logic — only change helper imports and call sites
- ❌ NO mutable conn leaking from helpers (return immutable db values, not connections)
- ❌ NO touching files that use init-game-state (incompatible signature, separate scope)
- ❌ NO migrating private (defn-) helpers from auto_confirm_test or interceptor_test (separate scope)
- ❌ NO removing queries_test.cljs add-card-to-zone-with-position (unique helper, not duplicated)
close_reason: All 29 files migrated to shared test_helpers. Zero create-test-db duplication. 1412 tests, 3153 assertions pass. No lint/format warnings. Remaining add-card-to-zone in 11 documented unmigrated files (incompatible init-game-state signature).

title: Hurkyl's Recall card + tests
description: Implement Hurkyl's Recall card definition and full test suite using new :bounce-all effect.
design:
## Goal
Implement Hurkyl's Recall ({1}{U} Instant) card definition and test file using the :bounce-all effect from fizzle-y6op.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours (card definition + 8 tests + registry entry)

## Implementation

### Card Definition
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/blue/hurkyls_recall.cljs

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.blue.hurkyls-recall)

(def card
  {:card/id :hurkyls-recall
   :card/name "Hurkyl's Recall"
   :card/cmc 2
   :card/mana-cost {:generic 1 :blue 1}
   :card/colors #{:blue}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/text "Return all artifacts target player controls to their hand."
   :card/targeting [{:target/id :target-player
                     :target/type :player
                     :target/required true}]
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :bounce-all
                   :effect/target-ref :target-player
                   :effect/criteria {:match/types #{:artifact}}}]})
```

### Registry
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/registry.cljs
- Add `[fizzle.cards.blue.hurkyls-recall :as hurkyls-recall]` to requires
- Add `hurkyls-recall/card` to the all-cards concat vector
- Follow existing blue card pattern (e.g., brain-freeze, chain-of-vapor)

### Test File
File: src/test/fizzle/cards/blue/hurkyls_recall_test.cljs

**Tests (8 minimum — targeted spell):**

A. Card definition — all fields exact values:
   - :card/id = :hurkyls-recall
   - :card/name = "Hurkyl's Recall"
   - :card/cmc = 2
   - :card/mana-cost = {:generic 1 :blue 1}
   - :card/colors = #{:blue}
   - :card/types = #{:instant}
   - :card/targeting vector with :target/type :player
   - :card/effects vector with :effect/type :bounce-all

B. Cast-resolve happy path — opponent has 3 artifacts on battlefield → th/cast-with-target targeting :player-2 → all 3 artifacts return to opponent's hand
   - IMPORTANT: Use th/add-opponent to add :player-2 before testing
   - Use th/cast-with-target (targeted spell helper)

C1. Cannot cast — only 1 blue mana (missing generic mana for {1}{U})

C2. Cannot cast — only 2 black mana (wrong color)

D. Storm count — casting increments storm by 1

G1. Target player has no artifacts — spell resolves cleanly, no crash, no side effects

G2. Mixed battlefield — target player has land + creature + artifact → only artifact bounces, land and creature stay

G3. Caster targets themselves — valid play, own artifacts return to caster's hand

**Key testing notes:**
- th/add-opponent MUST be called before targeting :player-2
- Use th/cast-with-target for player-targeted spells
- Verify artifacts moved to :hand zone, not just removed from :battlefield
- Verify non-artifacts still on :battlefield after resolve

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Card definition with all 8 required fields, exact values
- [ ] 8+ tests all passing
- [ ] Card registered in registry.cljs (all-cards count increases by 1)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ No (is (some? x)) assertions — use exact value checks
- ❌ No reimplementing production handlers — use th/cast-with-target
- ❌ No hardcoded UUIDs in card data — use :effect/target-ref
- ❌ Do NOT forget th/add-opponent before targeting :player-2
close_reason: Closed

title: Heuristic Burn Bot
description:
First bot that creates real clock pressure. Follows simple heuristics: play a land, cast bolts at face. Proof that bots can cast spells using the existing engine.

## Vision
- Semi-random clock bot: 20 Mountains + 40 Lightning Bolts (or similar ratio)
- Heuristic: play a land each turn, cast bolts targeting opponent (always face)
- First version: bolt always goes face, no targeting decisions
- Uses hold priority at end of player turn to cast bolts (plugs into priority system)
- Random hand/deck order (unlike scripted bots)

## What Changes
- Bot deck configuration system (card ratios, not hardcoded)
- Bot spell casting: use existing cast infrastructure with opponent player-id
- Bot heuristic: play land → cast available spells → pass
- Clock becomes real — bolts deal actual damage, game can end from bolt pressure
- May need Lightning Bolt card implementation if not already present

## Design Notes
- First bot that exercises the 'bot casts spells' path
- Validates that engine operations work correctly for opponent-initiated casts
- No targeting decisions needed (always face) keeps scope manageable
- More sophisticated burn (Gear 2 burn with targeting choices) deferred to later
close_reason: Duplicate of fizzle-26c4 which is now complete.

### 2. subs/game.cljs — ::selection-cards subscription (lines 406-449)
