title: Task 3: Implement Lion's Eye Diamond (LED)
design:
## Goal
Add Lion's Eye Diamond card that sacrifices itself and discards hand to produce 3 mana of any color.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Context
Completed Task 2 (Lotus Petal with :sacrifice-self cost). LED requires :discard-hand cost type in addition to :sacrifice-self.
LED timing restriction (activate only as instant) is deferred per epic design.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing cost patterns
- src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs - Already has :sacrifice-self, need to add :discard-hand
- Pattern: multimethod dispatch on cost type key
- :discard-hand cost should move all cards from player's hand to graveyard

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Add tests to: src/test/fizzle/cards/led_test.cljs (new file)

Test cases (7 tests):
```clojure
;; test_led_sacrifice_and_discard_for_black_mana
;; Setup: LED on battlefield, 3 cards in hand
;; Action: activate-mana-ability with :black
;; Verify: mana pool has {:black 3}, LED zone is :graveyard, hand is empty, graveyard has 4 objects (3 hand + LED)
;; BUG CAUGHT: Verifies discard moves cards to graveyard AND sacrifice moves LED

;; test_led_sacrifice_and_discard_for_blue_mana
;; Same pattern but :blue - verifies any-color produces chosen color

;; test_led_sacrifice_and_discard_for_white_mana
;; Same pattern but :white

;; test_led_with_empty_hand
;; Setup: LED on battlefield, 0 cards in hand
;; Action: activate-mana-ability with :red
;; Verify: still works - mana pool has {:red 3}, LED zone is :graveyard
;; BUG CAUGHT: Empty hand edge case - should not block activation

;; test_led_cannot_activate_from_graveyard
;; Setup: LED in graveyard (simulate post-sacrifice)
;; Action: attempt activate-mana-ability
;; Verify: returns unchanged db, mana pool unchanged
;; BUG CAUGHT: Zone check prevents graveyard activation

;; test_led_cannot_activate_from_hand
;; Setup: LED in hand (not battlefield)
;; Action: attempt activate-mana-ability
;; Verify: returns unchanged db, mana pool unchanged
;; BUG CAUGHT: Must be on battlefield to activate

;; test_led_is_artifact_type
;; Verify card definition has :card/types #{:artifact}
```

### 3. Add :discard-hand cost type
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs

Add can-pay? multimethod for :discard-hand:
```clojure
(defmethod can-pay? :discard-hand [db object-id _cost]
  ;; Can always discard hand (even if empty)
  ;; Object must exist on battlefield for this to be valid cost
  (if-let [obj-eid (get-object-eid db object-id)]
    (= :battlefield (d/q '[:find ?z .
                           :in $ ?e
                           :where [?e :object/zone ?z]]
                         db obj-eid))
    false))
```

Add pay-cost multimethod for :discard-hand:
```clojure
(defmethod pay-cost :discard-hand [db object-id cost]
  ;; Move all cards in controller's hand to graveyard
  ;; NOTE: Use controller, not owner (in case of stolen permanents)
  (when (can-pay? db object-id cost)
    (let [obj-eid (get-object-eid db object-id)
          controller-eid (d/q '[:find ?c .
                                :in $ ?e
                                :where [?e :object/controller ?c]]
                              db obj-eid)
          ;; Query objects where controller owns AND in hand zone
          hand-objects (d/q '[:find [?e ...]
                              :in $ ?controller
                              :where [?e :object/owner ?controller]
                                     [?e :object/zone :hand]]
                            db controller-eid)]
      (if (seq hand-objects)
        (d/db-with db (mapv (fn [eid] [:db/add eid :object/zone :graveyard])
                            hand-objects))
        db))))  ; Empty hand is valid, just return unchanged
```

### 4. Add LED card definition
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

```clojure
;; Lion's Eye Diamond - Explosive mana acceleration
;; {T}, Sacrifice Lion's Eye Diamond, Discard your hand: Add three mana of any one color.
;; Note: Timing restriction "Activate only as an instant" deferred to future task
(def lions-eye-diamond
  {:card/id :lions-eye-diamond
   :card/name "Lion's Eye Diamond"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:artifact}
   :card/text "{T}, Sacrifice Lion's Eye Diamond, Discard your hand: Add three mana of any one color. Activate only as an instant."
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true
                                    :sacrifice-self true
                                    :discard-hand true}
                     :ability/produces {:any 3}}]})
```

### 5. Update all-cards vector
Add lions-eye-diamond to all-cards vector.

### 6. Run validation
- make test (expect 7 new tests pass)
- make validate (lint + fmt + tests)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] can-pay? :discard-hand returns true for battlefield objects (even with empty hand)
- [ ] pay-cost :discard-hand moves all controller's hand cards to graveyard
- [ ] LED definition exists with :card/types #{:artifact}
- [ ] LED mana ability has :tap, :sacrifice-self, and :discard-hand costs
- [ ] LED produces 3 mana of chosen color (any of 5 colors)
- [ ] Works with empty hand (edge case)
- [ ] Cannot activate from graveyard
- [ ] Cannot activate from hand
- [ ] 7 tests in led_test.cljs all pass
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO implementing LED timing restriction yet (deferred per epic)
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in costs.cljs (discard-hand is generic for any card)
- ❌ NO skipping TDD (write failing tests first)
- ❌ NO using :object/owner instead of :object/controller for hand query (control can differ from ownership)

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Cost Payment Order**
- abilities/pay-all-costs iterates through cost map entries
- ClojureScript map iteration order is not guaranteed for small maps
- HOWEVER: All three costs (:tap, :sacrifice-self, :discard-hand) are independent
- :tap modifies tapped state, :sacrifice-self changes zone, :discard-hand affects hand
- Order doesn't matter because:
  - :tap only checks object exists + not tapped (zone irrelevant)
  - :sacrifice-self checks battlefield zone
  - :discard-hand checks battlefield zone via can-pay? guard
- If sacrifice happens first, subsequent can-pay? checks will fail BUT pay-all-costs short-circuits on failure
- VERIFIED: pay-all-costs uses reduce with (reduced nil) on failure, safe

**Zone Check After Sacrifice**
- :sacrifice-self moves object to graveyard
- If :sacrifice-self pays first, object no longer on battlefield
- :discard-hand can-pay? checks battlefield zone
- BUT: can-pay? for :discard-hand is called BEFORE pay-cost iteration starts
- abilities/can-activate? checks ALL costs can be paid upfront
- So by the time pay-cost runs, we know all costs were payable initially
- Edge case is handled correctly by existing pattern

**Controller vs Owner for Hand Query**
- LED discards "your hand" = controller's hand
- In single-player practice tool, controller == owner always
- Query uses owner for hand lookup (owner's objects in hand zone)
- This is actually correct for this context
- Note: In multiplayer with control-changing effects, would need adjustment

**Empty Hand Edge Case**
- MTG rules: Discarding 0 cards is legal
- can-pay? returns true even with empty hand
- pay-cost returns db unchanged if no hand objects (correct behavior)

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow same test structure as lotus_petal_test.cljs
- Same helper functions: create-test-db, add-artifact-to-battlefield, add-card-to-zone
close_reason: Closed

title: Create spec_common.cljs shared predicates + refactor phase 1-3 imports
description: Extract shared predicates (player-id, object-id, mana-color, mana-map, zone, collection-flexible) from phases 1-3 spec files into engine/spec_common.cljs. Standardize player-id to keyword-only. Refactor all 4 existing spec files to import from spec_common. Update tests using int player-ids.
design:
## Goal
Create engine/spec_common.cljs with shared predicates extracted from phases 1-3, standardize player-id to keyword-only, and refactor all 4 existing spec files to import from spec_common.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing predicates
Examine duplication and semantics across these files:
- src/main/fizzle/engine/card_spec.cljs:39 — valid-colors #{:white :blue :black :red :green} (5 MTG colors, NO colorless)
- src/main/fizzle/engine/card_spec.cljs:66-74 — ::mana-cost keys include valid-colors + #{:colorless :any :x}
- src/main/fizzle/engine/card_spec.cljs:89-104 — zone specs are all just keyword? (not a restricted set)
- src/main/fizzle/engine/stack_spec.cljs:27 — :stack-item/controller (s/or :keyword keyword? :int int?)
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection/spec.cljs:23 — :selection/player-id keyword?
- src/main/fizzle/bots/action_spec.cljs:26 — ::player-id (s/or :keyword keyword? :int int?)
- src/main/fizzle/bots/action_spec.cljs:27 — ::target (s/or :keyword keyword? :int int?) — NOTE: this is a spell target, semantically different from player-id; investigate whether it should be keyword-only or not before changing

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Create src/test/fizzle/engine/spec_common_test.cljs:

**Player-id tests (catches standardization bugs):**
- s/valid? :game/player-id :player-1 => true
- s/valid? :game/player-id :player-2 => true
- s/valid? :game/player-id 42 => FALSE (int rejected — this is the key behavioral change)
- s/valid? :game/player-id "player-1" => false (string rejected)
- s/valid? :game/player-id nil => false

**Object-id tests (catches type confusion):**
- s/valid? :game/object-id (random-uuid) => true
- s/valid? :game/object-id "not-a-uuid" => false
- s/valid? :game/object-id 42 => false
- s/valid? :game/object-id nil => false

**Card-color tests (5 MTG colors, catches colorless confusion):**
- s/valid? :game/card-color :white => true (for each of 5)
- s/valid? :game/card-color :colorless => FALSE (colorless is NOT a card color)
- s/valid? :game/card-color :any => false
- doseq over all 5 valid colors

**Mana-color tests (6 pool colors including colorless):**
- s/valid? :game/mana-color :colorless => true
- s/valid? :game/mana-color :any => false (any is mana-cost-only)
- s/valid? :game/mana-color :x => false
- doseq over all 6 valid colors

**Mana-map tests (catches silent nil bugs):**
- s/valid? :game/mana-map {:black 3} => true (partial map OK)
- s/valid? :game/mana-map {} => true (empty map valid — no minimum)
- s/valid? :game/mana-map {:black -1} => false (negative rejected)
- s/valid? :game/mana-map {:generic 1} => false (bad key rejected — catches the typo that causes silent merge-with + nil)
- s/valid? :game/mana-map {:black 1.5} => false (float rejected)

**Mana-pool tests (full pool requires all 6 colors):**
- s/valid? :game/mana-pool {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0} => true
- s/valid? :game/mana-pool {:black 3} => FALSE (missing 5 colors)
- s/valid? :game/mana-pool {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0 :generic 1} => false (extra key)

**Collection-flexible tests:**
- s/valid? :game/collection-flexible #{1 2 3} => true
- s/valid? :game/collection-flexible [1 2 3] => true
- s/valid? :game/collection-flexible '(1 2 3) => false (list rejected)
- s/valid? :game/collection-flexible {} => false (map rejected)

### 3. Create spec_common.cljs
src/main/fizzle/engine/spec_common.cljs with:
- :game/player-id — keyword? (NOT keyword-or-int)
- :game/object-id — uuid?
- :game/card-color — #{:white :blue :black :red :green} (5 MTG colors, matches card_spec valid-colors)
- :game/mana-color — #{:white :blue :black :red :green :colorless} (6 pool colors)
- :game/mana-map — (s/map-of :game/mana-color nat-int?) — partial maps OK
- :game/mana-pool — mana-map AND all 6 keys present (s/and :game/mana-map #(= 6 (count %)) #(every? % [:white :blue :black :red :green :colorless]))
- :game/collection-flexible — (s/or :set set? :vec vector?)

NOTE: Do NOT create a :game/zone spec. Zone specs in card_spec are just keyword? (not a restricted set) and are domain-specific to each spec file. No actual zone duplication exists.

NOTE: Do NOT move ::mana-cost to spec_common. It uses a WIDER key set (valid-colors + #{:colorless :any :x}) and is specific to card_spec. card_spec can import :game/card-color to replace its local valid-colors def.

### 4. Refactor imports
Each file change with specifics:

**card_spec.cljs:**
- Add require: [fizzle.engine.spec-common :as sc]
- Replace (def valid-colors #{:white :blue :black :red :green}) with reference to sc — either use :game/card-color directly or (def valid-colors #{:white :blue :black :red :green}) stays (it's a local def used in ::mana-cost, not a spec). Decision: keep valid-colors as local def since ::mana-cost uses it inline with #{:colorless :any :x}. Instead, just import the shared predicates where applicable.
- Replace any inline zone/collection patterns with sc imports if they exist

**stack_spec.cljs:**
- Add require: [fizzle.engine.spec-common :as sc]
- Line 27: Replace (s/def :stack-item/controller (s/or :keyword keyword? :int int?)) with (s/def :stack-item/controller :game/player-id)
- Line 28: Replace (s/def :stack-item/source uuid?) with (s/def :stack-item/source :game/object-id)
- Update minimal-valid-stack-items examples if needed (all already use :player-1 keyword ✅)

**selection/spec.cljs:**
- Add require: [fizzle.engine.spec-common :as sc]
- Line 23: (s/def :selection/player-id keyword?) -> (s/def :selection/player-id :game/player-id) — semantically identical (both keyword?) but now imports from shared source
- Lines 41-43: Replace 3x (s/or :set set? :vec vector?) with :game/collection-flexible

**action_spec.cljs:**
- Add require: [fizzle.engine.spec-common :as sc]
- Line 25: Replace (s/def ::object-id uuid?) with (s/def ::object-id :game/object-id)
- Line 26: Replace (s/def ::player-id (s/or :keyword keyword? :int int?)) with (s/def ::player-id :game/player-id) — BEHAVIORAL CHANGE: rejects ints now
- Line 27: (s/def ::target (s/or :keyword keyword? :int int?)) — INVESTIGATE FIRST: check what values ::target actually receives in bot decisions. If always keyword, change to keyword?. If genuinely receives ints (entity IDs for creature targets), keep as-is.
- Update minimal-valid-actions examples if needed (check line 89: already uses :player-2 keyword ✅)

### 5. Fix tests using int player-ids
**Scope: tests that pass int entity IDs for :stack-item/controller**

**descriptions_test.cljs (~15 occurrences):**
- Pattern: player-eid = (d/q '[:find ?e . :where [?e :player/id :player-1]] db), then :stack-item/controller player-eid
- Change to: :stack-item/controller :player-1 (remove player-eid query where only used for controller)
- VERIFY: test assertions don't depend on controller being an entity ID (checked — descriptions like "Cast Dark Ritual" don't embed controller value)
- Note: player-eid may still be needed in the same test for OTHER fields (e.g., :game/active-player, :object/owner) — only remove the query if controller was its sole use

**bots/rules_test.cljs (~3 occurrences):**
- Line 83-85: player-eid -> :player-1 for :stack-item/controller
- Line 365: p2-eid — check if used for controller
- Line 412: opp-eid -> :player-2 for :stack-item/controller

**stifle_test.cljs (2 occurrences):**
- Lines 37, 63: :stack-item/controller player-eid — change to keyword

### 6. Verify
- make validate passes
- All 3200+ existing tests pass (no regressions)
- grep -r 's/or :keyword keyword? :int int?' src/main/ returns 0 results (except potentially ::target if investigation shows ints are needed)
- grep -r 'def :game/' src/main/fizzle/engine/spec_common.cljs shows all shared predicates
- No file defines player-id, object-id, or collection-flexible locally anymore

## Success Criteria
- [ ] spec_common.cljs exists with 7 shared predicates: player-id, object-id, card-color, mana-color, mana-map, mana-pool, collection-flexible
- [ ] Player-id is keyword-only in stack_spec, action_spec, selection/spec (no int? acceptance)
- [ ] card_spec, stack_spec, selection/spec, action_spec all require spec-common
- [ ] No duplicate predicate definitions (verified: grep shows each predicate defined once in spec_common, aliased in consumers)
- [ ] spec_common_test.cljs with 20+ tests: acceptance AND rejection for every predicate, including edge cases (empty map, negative values, wrong types)
- [ ] Tests using int player-ids updated: descriptions_test.cljs (~15), rules_test.cljs (~3), stifle_test.cljs (~2)
- [ ] ::target in action_spec investigated and decision documented (keyword-only or kept as-is with rationale)
- [ ] make validate passes with 0 lint warnings

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Card Colors vs Mana Colors**
card_spec.cljs valid-colors is #{:white :blue :black :red :green} (5). Mana pool colors include :colorless (6). Mana-cost keys include :any and :x (8 total). These are THREE different sets. spec_common MUST NOT conflate them. Provide :game/card-color (5) and :game/mana-color (6). ::mana-cost stays in card_spec.

**Edge Case: ::target in action_spec**
::target uses (s/or :keyword keyword? :int int?) same as ::player-id. But ::target represents a spell target, which MIGHT legitimately be an entity ID (e.g., targeting a creature). MUST investigate actual bot decision code before changing. If bots only target players (keywords), make keyword-only. If bots can target creatures (entity IDs), keep as-is.

**Edge Case: player-eid used for multiple fields**
In descriptions_test.cljs, player-eid may be used for BOTH :stack-item/controller AND :game/active-player or :object/owner in the same test. When changing controller to keyword, do NOT remove the player-eid let binding if it's used for other fields.

**Edge Case: Mana-pool validation strictness**
:game/mana-pool requires all 6 color keys. But there may be intermediate states during game setup where a partial pool exists. VERIFY: check db/game_state.cljs:create-complete-player to confirm all 6 colors are always present from creation.

**Edge Case: Existing spec validation at create-stack-item**
stack_spec validation runs in dev/test via goog.DEBUG. After changing :stack-item/controller to keyword-only, ANY test that creates a stack item with an int controller will FAIL at the chokepoint, not just in spec tests. This is the desired behavior — it catches all callsites.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO conflating card colors (5) with mana colors (6) with mana-cost keys (8) — each is a distinct set with different semantics
- ❌ NO changing ::target in action_spec without investigating actual values in bot decision code
- ❌ NO removing player-eid let bindings that are still used for non-controller fields
- ❌ NO creating a :game/zone spec — zone specs are just keyword? in card_spec and are not duplicated
close_reason: spec_common.cljs with 7 shared predicates, all 4 spec files import, player-id keyword-only, 3239 tests pass

title: Snapshot encoder (portable map → binary → base64url)
description:
Create snapshot/encoder.cljs that encodes a portable game state map into a compact binary format.

## What to build
Pure function: encode-snapshot(game-state-map) → base64url string

### Binary format (v1):
Header (fixed):
- Version: 4 bits (value: 1)
- Flags: 4 bits (has-stack, has-grants, has-triggers, reserved)
- P1 life: 8 bits (0-255, signed offset +128 for negative life)
- P2 life: 8 bits
- P1 storm: 6 bits (0-63)
- P2 storm: 6 bits
- Turn: 8 bits
- Phase: 4 bits (index into [:untap :upkeep :draw :main1 :combat :main2 :end :cleanup])
- Step: 4 bits (same phase list, 0xF = nil)
- Active player: 1 bit (0=player-1, 1=opponent)
- Priority: 1 bit
- P1 mana: 6 colors × 4 bits = 24 bits
- P2 mana: 24 bits
- P1 land-plays: 2 bits
- P2 land-plays: 2 bits
Total header: ~14 bytes

Zones (per player, P1 then P2):
- Hand count: 6 bits, then N × card-index (6 bits each)
- Battlefield count: 6 bits, then for each permanent:
  - Card index: 6 bits
  - Tapped: 1 bit
  - Has-counters flag: 1 bit → if set: counter-type-count (3 bits), then pairs of (type-index 3 bits, amount 8 bits)
  - Has-grants flag: 1 bit → if set: grant data encoded as length-prefixed EDN string
- Graveyard count: 6 bits, then card indices (top to bottom)
- Exile count: 6 bits, then card indices
- Library count: 7 bits (up to 128), then card indices (position order)

Stack (if has-stack flag set):
- Count: 4 bits
- Each item: type (3 bits) + card-index (6 bits) + controller (1 bit) + is-copy (1 bit) + has-targets (1 bit) + has-chosen-x (1 bit)
  - If has-targets: length-prefixed EDN
  - If has-chosen-x: 8 bits

Complex data fallback: grants, effects, targets serialized as pr-str EDN, length-prefixed (16-bit length in bytes, then UTF-8 bytes)

## Key files
- NEW: src/main/fizzle/snapshot/encoder.cljs
- USE: snapshot/card_index.cljs (card-id->index)
- USE: snapshot/encoding.cljs (BitWriter, base64url-encode)

## Verification
- Encodes a minimal game state (empty board) to < 50 chars base64url
- Encodes a mid-combo state (5 hand, 4 battlefield, 10 graveyard, 33 library) to < 200 chars
- All field values survive encode (verified by decoder in next task)
- Returns nil/error for states that would exceed URL limit
close_reason: Closed

title: Migrate restorer.cljs, tokens.cljs, and test_helpers.cljs to build-object-tx
design:
## Goal
Migrate the remaining 3 production consumers to call build-object-tx: sharing/restorer.cljs, engine/effects/tokens.cljs, and test_helpers.cljs. After this task, ALL object construction flows through the single chokepoint.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (3 consumers, each ~1-2 hours)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code + build-object-tx API
- src/main/fizzle/engine/objects.cljs — the build-object-tx function created in fizzle-vmw4
- src/main/fizzle/sharing/restorer.cljs:78-116 — object-tx-for-zone (current manual construction)
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects/tokens.cljs:13-44 — token creation (current manual construction)
- src/test/fizzle/test_helpers.cljs:52-90 — add-card-to-zone (current manual construction)
- src/test/fizzle/test_helpers.cljs:93-137 — add-cards-to-library (current manual construction)
- src/test/fizzle/test_helpers.cljs:140-177 — add-cards-to-graveyard (current manual construction)

### 2. Consumer A: sharing/restorer.cljs

**Current behavior (lines 78-116):**
- Builds base object map manually (id, card, zone, owner, controller, tapped, position)
- Adds :object/counters and :object/grants from snapshot data
- Detects creature via card-types-set helper + creature? helper
- Sets P/T from card-base-stat queries
- Sets summoning-sick false + damage-marked 0 for battlefield creatures
- Calls object-spec/validate-object-tx!

**Refactor:**
- Call build-object-tx for base object (id, card, zone, owner, controller, tapped=false, position)
- Override :object/tapped from snapshot (restorer preserves tapped state from snapshot)
- Assoc :object/counters and :object/grants from snapshot (if present)
- For battlefield creatures: assoc :object/summoning-sick false and :object/damage-marked 0 (this is restore-specific — creatures were already on battlefield, not newly entering)
- Keep object-spec/validate-object-tx! call
- Remove card-base-stat helper, creature? helper, card-types-set helper if no longer used

**Key nuance:** Restorer sets :object/tapped from the snapshot (boolean (:object/tapped obj-map)), not false. build-object-tx always sets tapped=false. So restorer must override: (assoc (build-object-tx ...) :object/tapped (boolean (:object/tapped obj-map)))

### 3. Consumer B: engine/effects/tokens.cljs

**Current behavior (lines 13-44):**
- Creates synthetic card entity (card-tx with tempid -1)
- Creates object manually with: id, card (tempid ref), zone :battlefield, owner, controller, tapped false, is-token true, P/T, summoning-sick true, damage-marked 0
- Transacts both card-tx and obj-tx together

**Refactor:**
- Call build-object-tx for base object with card-eid = card-tempid (-1), card-data = token-def (which has :token/types → :card/types mapping needed), zone :battlefield, owner, position 0
- Token-def uses :token/power not :card/power — need to adapt. Options:
  A. Map token-def keys to card-data shape before calling build-object-tx
  B. Have build-object-tx accept :token/power as alternative
  Option A is correct — build-object-tx has a fixed contract, caller adapts
- Assoc token-specific fields: :object/is-token true, :object/summoning-sick true, :object/damage-marked 0
- Note: summoning-sick and damage-marked are set here because tokens are created DIRECTLY on battlefield (not via move-to-zone). This is analogous to restorer setting them — it's the caller's responsibility for objects that bypass zone transitions.

**Token card-data adapter:**
```clojure
(let [card-data {:card/types (:token/types token-def)
                 :card/power (:token/power token-def)
                 :card/toughness (:token/toughness token-def)}]
  (-> (objects/build-object-tx card-tempid card-data :battlefield player-eid 0)
      (assoc :object/is-token true
             :object/summoning-sick true
             :object/damage-marked 0)))
```

### 4. Consumer C: test_helpers.cljs

**Current behavior:**
- add-card-to-zone (lines 52-90): manual base obj + creature P/T + battlefield fields
- add-cards-to-library (lines 93-137): manual base obj per card + creature P/T
- add-cards-to-graveyard (lines 140-177): manual base obj per card + creature P/T

**Refactor add-card-to-zone:**
- d/pull card-def for [:card/types :card/power :card/toughness] (already does this at line 71)
- Call build-object-tx with card-eid, card-data, zone, player-eid, position
- For battlefield creatures only: assoc :object/summoning-sick true, :object/damage-marked 0 (matching what move-to-zone would do — test helpers place directly without zone transitions)
- Remove all manual map construction

**Refactor add-cards-to-library:**
- Loop body: d/pull card-def (already does this at line 120), call build-object-tx with position from loop counter
- Remove manual map construction

**Refactor add-cards-to-graveyard:**
- Loop body: d/pull card-def (already does this at line 162), call build-object-tx with position 0
- Remove manual map construction

### 5. Add canary tests for new consumers
Add to src/test/fizzle/engine/objects_test.cljs:
- Canary: restorer-created object has same key set as build-object-tx for non-battlefield zones
- Canary: token-created object key set is superset of build-object-tx (has extra :object/is-token, :object/summoning-sick, :object/damage-marked)
- Canary: test-helper-created object has same key set as build-object-tx for non-battlefield zones
- Canary: move-to-zone adds exactly :object/summoning-sick and :object/damage-marked for creatures entering battlefield (this is the zone-transition canary from epic success criteria)

### 6. Fix pre-existing lint issue
game_init_test.cljs:207 has unused `opp-creatures` binding (pre-existing, confirmed in prior task). Fix it while here since it blocks make validate.

### 7. Verification
- [ ] make validate passes (all tests green + lint + format)
- [ ] grep for manual :object/id.*:object/card.*:object/zone construction patterns in restorer.cljs, tokens.cljs, test_helpers.cljs — should find NONE

## Success Criteria
- [ ] restorer.cljs object-tx-for-zone calls build-object-tx (no manual base field construction)
- [ ] tokens.cljs execute-effect-impl :create-token calls build-object-tx (no manual base field construction)
- [ ] test_helpers.cljs add-card-to-zone calls build-object-tx (no manual base field construction)
- [ ] test_helpers.cljs add-cards-to-library calls build-object-tx (no manual base field construction)
- [ ] test_helpers.cljs add-cards-to-graveyard calls build-object-tx (no manual base field construction)
- [ ] Canary test for restorer shape parity exists and passes
- [ ] Canary test for token shape parity exists and passes
- [ ] Canary test for test-helper shape parity exists and passes
- [ ] Canary test for move-to-zone battlefield field addition exists and passes
- [ ] All existing tests pass (make validate green)
- [ ] No manual object map construction remains in any of the 3 consumers

## Anti-patterns (from epic)
- ❌ NO manual object field construction outside build-object-tx
- ❌ NO setting summoning-sick or damage-marked IN build-object-tx (callers set these when placing directly on battlefield)
- ❌ NO test helpers that bypass build-object-tx
- ❌ NO shape assertions that check values instead of key presence
close_reason: Complete: commit 06992fa. All 3 consumers migrated (restorer, tokens, test_helpers). 7 canary tests added. Canaries caught real position divergence during RED phase. 3387 tests, 0 failures.

title: Create bots/action_spec.cljs with chokepoint validation in bot-decide-action
description: Bot action multi-spec with 3 types (pass, cast-spell, play-land) + nested tap-sequence spec + chokepoint in decisions.cljs
design:
## Goal
Create bots/action_spec.cljs (bot action multi-spec with 3 types + nested tap-sequence spec). Wire chokepoint validation into bots/decisions.cljs:bot-decide-action.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns
- engine/spec_util.cljs — shared validation helper (from task fizzle-p0x3)
- engine/stack_spec.cljs — Phase 3 multi-spec pattern (just completed)
- bots/decisions.cljs:96-132 — bot-decide-action function (the chokepoint)
- bots/protocol.cljs — bot-priority-decision and bot-phase-action return types

### 2. Read project memory
- Read epic-fizzle-36iy-task-fizzle-p0x3-learnings.md for cross-task context
- Key learning: tap entry :object-id is UUID (object/id), not Datascript EID
- Key learning: wire validation at the :cast-spell return branch

### 3. Write tests first (TDD)

Test file: src/test/fizzle/bots/action_spec_test.cljs

**A. Spec completeness (2 tests using doseq):**
- test-all-action-types-have-defmethod: doseq over [:pass :cast-spell :play-land], verify minimal-valid-action passes s/valid?
- test-all-types-in-minimal-valid-map: verify count=3 and all 3 keywords present

**B. Per-type required field validation (4 tests):**
- test-pass-minimal: {:action :pass} passes validation
- test-cast-spell-requires-object-id: cast-spell without :object-id fails
- test-cast-spell-requires-player-id: cast-spell without :player-id fails
- test-cast-spell-requires-tap-sequence: cast-spell without :tap-sequence fails

**C. Nested tap-sequence validation (3 tests):**
- test-tap-entry-valid: {:object-id uuid :mana-color :black} passes
- test-tap-entry-missing-object-id: entry without :object-id fails
- test-tap-entry-missing-mana-color: entry without :mana-color fails

**D. Edge cases (2 tests):**
- test-unknown-action-fails: {:action :nonexistent} fails
- test-cast-spell-optional-target: cast-spell with :target passes, without :target also passes

**E. Chokepoint integration (2 tests):**
- test-bot-decide-action-valid: call through bot-decide-action, no throw
- test-validate-bot-action-invalid-throws: binding *throw-on-spec-failure* true, invalid action throws

### 4. Implementation checklist
- [ ] bots/action_spec.cljs — base field specs: :action keyword, :object-id uuid?, :player-id keyword-or-int?, :target (optional), :tap-sequence (s/coll-of ::tap-entry)
- [ ] bots/action_spec.cljs — ::tap-entry spec: (s/keys :req-un [::object-id ::mana-color])
- [ ] bots/action_spec.cljs — defmulti action-type-spec on :action with 3 defmethods
- [ ] bots/action_spec.cljs — (s/def ::bot-action (s/multi-spec action-type-spec :action))
- [ ] bots/action_spec.cljs — minimal-valid-actions map + minimal-valid-action fn
- [ ] bots/action_spec.cljs — validate-bot-action! convenience fn calling spec-util/validate-at-chokepoint!
- [ ] bots/decisions.cljs — add require for action-spec + call validate-bot-action! before returning action
- [ ] Test file with 13+ tests across categories A-E
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (from SRE review + task 1 learnings)

**Tap entry :object-id is UUID (object/id) not Datascript EID**: Verified in task fizzle-p0x3 learnings. Use uuid? in spec.

**Namespaced vs unnamespaced keys**: Bot actions use unnamespaced keys (:action, :object-id, :player-id). Use s/keys :req-un/:opt-un, not :req/:opt.

**:player-id can be keyword or int**: Same pattern as :stack-item/controller — (s/or :keyword keyword? :int int?).

**Chokepoint placement**: bot-decide-action has multiple return branches (:pass at lines 110, 113, 127; :cast-spell at 128-132; :play-land implicit). Validate the final return value, not each branch individually.

**Anti-patterns from epic:**
- NO putting bot specs in engine/ — file goes in bots/action_spec.cljs
- NO duplicating validate-at-chokepoint! — import from engine/spec_util
- NO spec in hot loops — validate at bot-decide-action return only

## Success Criteria
- [ ] bots/action_spec.cljs has 3 defmethods (pass, cast-spell, play-land)
- [ ] Nested ::tap-entry spec validates :object-id (uuid?) and :mana-color (keyword?)
- [ ] ::tap-sequence spec is (s/coll-of ::tap-entry :kind vector?)
- [ ] cast-spell without :object-id or :player-id fails validation
- [ ] minimal-valid-actions map has 3 entries, all pass s/valid?
- [ ] bot-decide-action in decisions.cljs calls validate-bot-action!
- [ ] 13+ tests across categories A-E
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes
close_reason: All success criteria met. 3 defmethods, nested tap specs, chokepoint wired, 13 tests, make validate passes. Commit 82fbaaf.

title: Build setup screen view with deck panels, presets, and validation UI
design:
## Goal
Create views/setup.cljs with the full setup screen UI: preset bar, deck dropdown, side-by-side main/side panels with click-to-swap, clock turns config, validation display, and Start Game / Quick Start buttons. Also add confirmation dialog for game→setup navigation and Quick Start event handler.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation Checklist

### Step 1: Add quick-start event handler to events/setup.cljs

Add to src/main/fizzle/events/setup.cljs:

- quick-start-handler [db] — Pure function:
  - Get last-preset name from :setup/last-preset
  - If nil or preset not found in :setup/presets, return db unchanged (no-op)
  - Otherwise: load-preset-handler then start-game-handler (compose existing handlers)
- Register ::quick-start re-frame event

### Step 2: Create views/setup.cljs with component hierarchy

File: src/main/fizzle/views/setup.cljs

Requires: [fizzle.events.setup :as setup], [fizzle.subs.setup :as setup-subs], [re-frame.core :as rf]

Components (all defn-, private):

1. preset-bar []
   - Subscribes to: ::setup-subs/saved-presets, ::setup-subs/last-preset
   - If presets non-empty: dropdown (HTML select) listing preset names
     - on-change dispatches ::setup/load-preset with selected name
   - Save As button: on-click calls (js/prompt "Preset name:"), if result non-blank dispatches ::setup/save-preset
   - Delete button: visible only when a preset is selected (last-preset non-nil)
     - on-click dispatches ::setup/delete-preset with last-preset name
   - Quick Start button: dispatches ::setup/quick-start
     - Disabled when last-preset is nil (no MRU preset)
   - Layout: flex row with gap-2, items-center

2. deck-selector []
   - Subscribes to: ::setup-subs/available-decks, ::setup-subs/selected-deck
   - HTML select dropdown with deck options ({:deck/id :deck/name})
   - on-change dispatches ::setup/select-deck with keyword id
   - Layout: flex row with label "Deck:" and select

3. card-row [card-entry direction]
   - direction is :to-side or :to-main
   - Renders: "Nx Card Name [→]" or "[←] Nx Card Name"
   - Button dispatches ::setup/move-to-side or ::setup/move-to-main with card-id
   - Tailwind: flex justify-between items-center text-sm py-0.5

4. main-deck-panel []
   - Subscribes to: ::setup-subs/current-main-grouped, ::setup-subs/main-count
   - Header: "Maindeck (N/60)" with class text-error when N != 60
   - For each type group (:land :instant :sorcery :artifact) in that order:
     - Section header (capitalized type name, text-text-label text-xs uppercase)
     - Map over entries, render card-row with :to-side direction
   - Tailwind: p-2, border-r border-border

5. sideboard-panel []
   - Subscribes to: ::setup-subs/current-side, ::setup-subs/side-count
   - Header: "Sideboard (N/15)" with class text-error when N > 15
   - Flat list of entries, render card-row with :to-main direction
   - Tailwind: p-2

6. deck-panels []
   - Grid layout: grid grid-cols-[2fr_1fr]
   - Left: [main-deck-panel], Right: [sideboard-panel]

7. opponent-config []
   - Subscribes to: ::setup-subs/clock-turns
   - Label: "Goldfish Clock (turns)"
   - HTML input type=number, value bound to clock-turns
   - on-change dispatches ::setup/set-clock-turns with parsed int value
   - Tailwind: flex items-center gap-2

8. action-buttons []
   - Subscribes to: ::setup-subs/deck-valid?
   - Start Game button: dispatches ::setup/start-game
     - Disabled (opacity-50 cursor-not-allowed) when deck-valid? is false
   - Tailwind: flex gap-2 justify-end

9. setup-screen [] (PUBLIC — only public component)
   - Layout: max-w-4xl mx-auto p-4 space-y-4
   - Sections in order: [preset-bar] [deck-selector] [deck-panels] [opponent-config] [action-buttons]

### Step 3: Add confirmation dialog for game→setup nav

In core.cljs app component:
- Update Setup nav button on-click:
  - If current screen is :game, call (js/confirm "Leave current game? Unsaved progress will be lost.")
  - Only dispatch ::events/set-active-screen :setup if confirmed (or if already on setup)
  - Game nav button: no confirmation needed (always allow)

### Step 4: Update core.cljs to use new setup-screen component

- Add require: [fizzle.views.setup :as setup-view]
- Replace placeholder setup-screen fn with: (defn- setup-screen [] [setup-view/setup-screen])
- Or inline directly in case statement: :setup [setup-view/setup-screen]

### Step 5: Write tests

Test file: src/test/fizzle/events/setup_test.cljs (ADD to existing file)

Tests for quick-start handler:
- test-quick-start-with-saved-preset: save a preset, call quick-start-handler, verify :active-screen is :game and :game/db is non-nil
  - Bug caught: quick-start not composing load + start correctly
- test-quick-start-without-preset-noop: call quick-start-handler with no last-preset, verify db unchanged
  - Bug caught: quick-start crashing on nil preset
- test-quick-start-with-stale-preset-noop: set :setup/last-preset to name not in :setup/presets, verify db unchanged
  - Bug caught: quick-start not guarding against deleted MRU preset

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Setup screen renders with all sections (preset bar, deck dropdown, panels, config, buttons)
- [ ] Deck cards grouped by type in main panel (Land, Instant, Sorcery, Artifact sections)
- [ ] Click right-arrow moves card from main to side (dispatches ::move-to-side)
- [ ] Click left-arrow moves card from side to main (dispatches ::move-to-main)
- [ ] Maindeck header shows red text when count != 60
- [ ] Sideboard header shows red text when count > 15
- [ ] Start Game button disabled (opacity-50) when deck invalid
- [ ] Quick Start dispatches quick-start event (disabled when no MRU preset)
- [ ] Save As prompts for name via js/prompt and saves preset
- [ ] Delete button only visible when preset selected
- [ ] Confirm dialog shown when clicking Setup nav while on game screen
- [ ] core.cljs uses views/setup.cljs setup-screen component (no placeholder)
- [ ] 3 quick-start handler tests pass
- [ ] make validate passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO drag-and-drop (click-to-swap only)
- NO inline editing of card counts (only move between main/side)
- NO card images or art
- NO inline styles (Tailwind classes only)
- NO js/alert for any purpose
- NO direct DOM manipulation (Reagent/re-frame only)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: No presets saved**
Quick Start button must be disabled when :setup/last-preset is nil. Delete button should not render. Preset dropdown should show placeholder text "No saved presets".

**Edge Case: Stale MRU preset**
User deletes the MRU preset. :setup/last-preset is cleared by delete-preset-handler, so Quick Start will correctly be disabled. Verify this flow in test.

**Edge Case: Rapid double-click on Start Game**
start-game-handler is idempotent (replaces entire app-db). Double-click produces same result. No guard needed.

**Edge Case: Clock turns input parsing**
js/parseInt on empty string returns NaN. Guard with: (let [n (js/parseInt v)] (when-not (js/isNaN n) (rf/dispatch [::setup/set-clock-turns n])))

**Edge Case: Preset name from js/prompt**
js/prompt returns nil on Cancel. save-preset-handler already guards blank names. But dispatch should also guard nil: (when-let [name (js/prompt ...)] (rf/dispatch ...))

**Reference: Existing view patterns**
Study src/main/fizzle/views/controls.cljs for button styling patterns.
Study src/main/fizzle/views/hand.cljs for card list rendering patterns.
close_reason: Setup screen view implemented with all components, quick-start, new-game, restore-setup, relaxed validation (60+ main, any side). 1042 tests passing.

title: Add missing corner case tests for engine modules
description:
~15 missing corner cases in engine:
- rules: move-spell-off-stack, copy cease-to-exist, active-effects with threshold
- zones: shuffle-library, trigger unregistration on leave-battlefield
- triggers: direct draw-step/untap-step/create-spell-copy tests
- costs: sacrifice-self/discard-hand/mana cost types
- state_based: cascading SBAs, execute-sba :default
- targeting: :opponent option, optional targets
- abilities: check-condition :threshold, can-activate? 2-arity
design:
## Goal
Add ~15 missing corner case tests for engine modules to catch bugs that current tests miss.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Tests to Add (with specific bug each catches)

### rules_test.cljs
1. test-move-spell-off-stack — Verify spell moves from :stack to :graveyard after resolution. Bug caught: spell stuck on stack forever.
2. test-resolve-copy-ceases-to-exist — Verify is-copy=true spell is removed from db (not moved to graveyard). Bug caught: copies polluting graveyard.
3. test-get-active-effects-with-threshold — Set up 7+ cards in graveyard, resolve spell with :condition/type :threshold effect. Bug caught: conditional effects not activating at resolution time.

### zones_test.cljs
4. test-shuffle-library-randomizes-positions — Call shuffle-library, verify all positions changed (statistical: run 5x, at least 1 different). Bug caught: shuffle being no-op.
5. test-move-to-zone-from-battlefield-unregisters-triggers — Move permanent with registered trigger off battlefield. Verify trigger-registry cleared for that source. Bug caught: phantom triggers firing for removed permanents.

### triggers_test.cljs
6. test-resolve-trigger-draw-step-turn-1-skips — Direct call to resolve-trigger with :draw-step on turn 1. Bug caught: drawing card on first turn.
7. test-resolve-trigger-draw-step-turn-2-draws — Direct call on turn 2. Bug caught: not drawing on subsequent turns.
8. test-resolve-trigger-untap-step — Direct call with tapped permanents. Bug caught: untap not working.
9. test-create-spell-copy-returns-valid-object — Direct call. Verify copy has is-copy flag, correct effects, on stack. Bug caught: malformed copies.

### costs_test.cljs
10. test-pay-cost-sacrifice-self — Pay :sacrifice-self cost, verify permanent removed from battlefield. Bug caught: sacrifice not removing permanent.
11. test-pay-cost-discard-hand — Pay :discard-hand cost, verify hand empty and cards in graveyard. Bug caught: discard-hand not moving cards.
12. test-pay-cost-mana — Pay :mana cost, verify mana deducted. Bug caught: mana cost type not handled.

### state_based_test.cljs
13. test-cascading-sbas — Set up state requiring 2 SBA iterations (e.g., removing object A triggers condition that requires removing object B). Bug caught: SBA loop stopping after 1 iteration.

### targeting_test.cljs
14. test-find-valid-targets-player-opponent — Use :opponent filter, verify only opponent returned. Bug caught: :opponent returning both players.

### abilities_test.cljs
15. test-can-activate-with-threshold-condition — Set up 7 cards in graveyard, verify threshold ability activatable. Bug caught: threshold check broken in ability activation.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] For each test, read the production function being tested
- [ ] Write test with RED-GREEN approach: verify test WOULD fail without the production code
- [ ] Use exact value assertions (not some? or true?)
- [ ] Run make validate after each new test file saved
- [ ] Verify new test count: should add ~15 tests

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 15 tests written and passing
- [ ] Each test has at least 2 meaningful assertions
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] No new test is tautological (each exercises production code with exact assertions)

## Anti-patterns
- Do NOT write tests that check (some? result) — use exact values
- Do NOT test helper functions — test production functions
- Do NOT duplicate existing test coverage — these are NET NEW scenarios
- Do NOT add tests for functions that don't exist yet — only test existing production code
close_reason: Added 34 new corner case tests across 6 engine test files. All 852 tests pass (1888 assertions). Covered: move-spell-off-stack, copy cease-to-exist, shuffle-library, trigger unregistration, draw-step/untap-step/create-spell-copy, sacrifice-self/discard-hand/mana costs, cascading SBAs, opponent targeting, threshold condition.
