title: Unified state-based actions via re-frame interceptor
description: Replace scattered inline loss-condition checks with a unified SBA system using a re-frame interceptor. Currently, life-zero is checked inline in :lose-life, :deal-damage effects but NOT in :pay-life/:pay-x-life costs — a bug factory that grows with every new life-modifying effect. Empty-library is checked inline in :draw.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- All life-zero detection goes through one SBA check, not inline in effects/costs
- All empty-library detection goes through one SBA check, not inline in draw effect
- SBA interceptor runs after ::resolve, ::confirm-selection, ::pass-priority events
- New SBA types can be added with ONLY a defmethod registration (no call-site changes)
- Existing loss-condition behavior preserved: game-over modal, continue-playing option
- Paying life to 0 via costs (pay-life, pay-x-life) triggers loss condition

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] :life-zero SBA fires when any player reaches 0 or negative life, regardless of source (effect, cost, mana ability)
- [ ] :empty-library SBA fires when a player attempts to draw from empty library
- [ ] No inline set-loss-condition calls remain in effects.cljs
- [ ] SBA interceptor attached to ::resolve, ::confirm-selection, ::pass-priority
- [ ] Extensibility test proves new SBA type works with just a defmethod
- [ ] Existing tests still pass (game-over detection, Lightning Bolt kills, etc.)
- [ ] New test: pay-life cost reducing to 0 triggers loss
- [ ] New test: pay-x-life (Necrologia) reducing to 0 triggers loss
- [ ] All tests passing, make validate clean

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO inline life-zero or empty-library checks in individual effects (whole point of this epic)
- NO modifying individual effect implementations to call SBAs — the interceptor handles it
- NO SBA checks inside engine-layer pure functions — interceptor is event-layer only
- NO breaking the existing check-and-execute-sbas loop contract (must loop until no SBAs fire)
- NO flag mutation without cleanup — :player/drew-from-empty must be cleared after SBA processes it

## Approach
Re-frame interceptor pattern (same as existing bot interceptor). The interceptor runs in the :after stage of game events, calls check-and-execute-sbas on game-db. SBA checks are defmethods on check-sba dispatching on SBA type keyword. Two checks registered: :life-zero (scan all players for life <= 0) and :empty-library (check :player/drew-from-empty flag). The :draw effect sets the flag instead of checking inline; the SBA clears it after processing.

## Architecture
- engine/state_based.cljs — Register :life-zero and :empty-library defmethods on existing check-sba multimethod
- New: events/interceptors/sba.cljs (or similar) — Re-frame interceptor that calls check-and-execute-sbas
- events/game.cljs — Add SBA interceptor to ::resolve, ::confirm-selection, ::pass-priority registrations
- engine/effects.cljs — Remove inline set-loss-condition from :lose-life, :deal-damage, :draw; draw sets :player/drew-from-empty flag instead
- db/schema.cljs — Add :player/drew-from-empty to schema

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Life-zero detection is scattered across individual effects (lose-life, deal-damage) but missing from cost payment paths (pay-life, pay-x-life). Every new effect or cost that modifies life is another place to forget the check. This is a growing surface area bug.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Re-frame interceptor on game events ✓
**What it is:** A re-frame interceptor (same pattern as bot interceptor) that runs check-and-execute-sbas after event handlers modify game-db. Attached declaratively to specific events.

**Chosen because:** One code path. Follows existing interceptor pattern. New events opt-in declaratively. No modifications to engine-layer pure functions.

#### 2. Wire into priority negotiation ❌
**What it is:** Put SBA check inside negotiate-priority function.

**Why we looked at this:** Closest to MTG rules (SBAs fire when priority would be given).

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Priority negotiation doesn't cover all state-changing paths (e.g., cleanup discard). Tighter coupling between priority and SBA systems.

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Priority system is redesigned to be the sole entry point for all game actions.

#### 3. Explicit call sites (7 sites) ❌
**What it is:** Add check-and-execute-sbas at each of 7 identified mutation points.

**Why we looked at this:** Most precise about when SBAs fire.

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Same surface-area problem we're trying to solve. Every new state-changing code path needs to remember to add the call.

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Interceptor approach proves to have unacceptable performance or ordering issues.

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | Answer | Implication |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| SBA call site approach | Interceptor on game events | One code path, declarative opt-in |
| Which SBAs to register | life-zero + empty-library + extensibility test | Replace all 3 inline checks |
| Empty library detection | Flag on player entity | :player/drew-from-empty set by draw, read/cleared by SBA |

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** life-zero SBA, empty-library SBA, interceptor infrastructure, remove inline checks, extensibility test
**Out of scope:** creature SBAs (zero toughness, legend rule) — deferred to creatures epic
close_reason: All success criteria met and reviewer approved. Unified SBA system replaces inline loss checks with re-frame interceptor. 6 commits, 2201 tests passing.

title: Create SBA re-frame interceptor, wire to game events, remove inline loss checks
description:
Create a re-frame interceptor that runs check-and-execute-sbas after game events, attach it to ::resolve-top, ::confirm-selection, and ::yield, then incrementally remove all inline set-loss-condition calls from effects.cljs.

## Goal
Complete the epic: all loss detection flows through the unified SBA system via interceptor. No inline set-loss-condition calls remain in effects.cljs.

## Implementation Checklist (incremental, tests green between each step)

### Step 1: Create SBA interceptor and wire to events
- [ ] Create events/interceptors/sba.cljs with rf/->interceptor :after stage
- [ ] Pattern: read (get-in context [:effects :db]) for app-db, extract :game/db, call sba/check-and-execute-sbas, write updated game-db back
- [ ] Skip if game-db nil or game already has :game/loss-condition
- [ ] Wire interceptor to ::resolve-top in events/game.cljs
- [ ] Wire interceptor to ::confirm-selection in events/selection.cljs
- [ ] Wire interceptor to ::yield in events/game.cljs
- [ ] Verify: both interceptor AND inline checks fire -- all tests green
- [ ] Commit checkpoint

### Step 2: Update :draw to set flag instead of inline check
- [ ] In effects.cljs :draw, replace sba/set-loss-condition call with setting :player/drew-from-empty flag
- [ ] Fix any broken tests (tests calling execute-effect-impl :draw directly need sba/check-and-execute-sbas after)
- [ ] make validate clean
- [ ] Commit checkpoint

### Step 3: Remove inline check from :lose-life
- [ ] Remove sba/set-loss-condition call from :lose-life in effects.cljs
- [ ] Fix broken tests (add sba/check-and-execute-sbas to engine-layer tests)
- [ ] make validate clean
- [ ] Commit checkpoint

### Step 4: Remove inline check from :deal-damage
- [ ] Remove sba/set-loss-condition call from :deal-damage in effects.cljs
- [ ] Fix broken tests
- [ ] Remove state-based require from effects.cljs (should be unused now)
- [ ] make validate clean
- [ ] Commit checkpoint

### Step 5: Add epic-required new tests
- [ ] Test: pay-life cost reducing to 0 triggers loss (via SBA)
- [ ] Test: pay-x-life (Necrologia) reducing to 0 triggers loss (via SBA)
- [ ] Test: extensibility -- new SBA type works with just a defmethod registration
- [ ] make validate clean
- [ ] Commit checkpoint

## Success Criteria
- [ ] No inline set-loss-condition calls remain in effects.cljs
- [ ] SBA interceptor attached to ::resolve-top, ::confirm-selection, ::yield
- [ ] :draw sets :player/drew-from-empty flag, SBA detects and sets loss condition
- [ ] pay-life cost to 0 triggers loss via SBA
- [ ] pay-x-life to 0 triggers loss via SBA
- [ ] Extensibility test passes
- [ ] All existing tests pass, make validate clean

## Key Considerations
- Bot interceptor pattern in bots/interceptor.cljs is the reference implementation
- ::confirm-selection is in events/selection.cljs not game.cljs -- check reg-event-db vs reg-event-fx
- ::activate-mana-ability path may need auditing if pay-life abilities do not go through ::yield
- About 29 tests will break incrementally as inline checks are removed -- fix them per-step
- The SBA interceptor life-zero check is idempotent (skips when loss-condition already set), so step 1 is safe with dual-fire
notes: All 5 steps complete. SBA interceptor wired, inline checks removed, 18 new tests total across task. 2201 tests, 0 failures.

title: Create spec_util.cljs + stack_spec.cljs with chokepoint validation
description: Shared validation utility and stack item multi-spec — establishes Phase 3 pattern
design:
## Goal
Create engine/spec_util.cljs (shared validation helper) and engine/stack_spec.cljs (stack item multi-spec with 10 types). Wire chokepoint validation into engine/stack.cljs:create-stack-item.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns
- engine/card_spec.cljs — Phase 1 effect multi-spec pattern (defmulti + defmethods + minimal-valid helpers)
- events/selection/spec.cljs — Phase 2 selection multi-spec pattern (chokepoint with goog.DEBUG guard + console.error)
- engine/stack.cljs:29-40 — create-stack-item function (the chokepoint, auto-assigns :stack-item/position)
- engine/resolution.cljs:29 — resolve-stack-item defmethods (verify all 10 types exist)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Test file: src/test/fizzle/engine/stack_spec_test.cljs

**Testing approach**: All spec validation tests use `(binding [spec-util/*throw-on-spec-failure* true] ...)` to make validation throw. Do NOT try to test console.error directly — the throw path exercises the same validation logic. The console.error path is implicitly validated by existing tests still passing.

**A. spec_util tests (3 tests):**
- `test-validate-valid-data-no-throw`: binding *throw-on-spec-failure* true, pass valid data to validate-at-chokepoint! — no exception thrown. Catches: broken validation accepting invalid data.
- `test-validate-invalid-data-throws`: binding *throw-on-spec-failure* true, pass data missing required field — `(is (thrown? js/Error (validate-at-chokepoint! ...)))`. Catches: validation not detecting invalid data.
- `test-validate-default-no-throw`: without binding (default false), pass invalid data — no exception thrown (console.error only). Catches: default mode accidentally throwing.

**B. Stack item spec completeness (2 tests using doseq):**
- `test-all-types-have-defmethod`: doseq over all 10 type keywords, verify `(s/valid? ::stack-spec/stack-item (minimal-valid-stack-item type))` is true. Catches: missing defmethod for a type.
- `test-all-types-in-minimal-valid-map`: verify `(count minimal-valid-stack-items)` = 10 and all 10 keywords present. Catches: forgotten type in minimal-valid map.

**C. Per-type required field validation (6+ tests):**
- `test-spell-requires-object-ref`: spell without :stack-item/object-ref fails. Catches: spell on stack without card reference.
- `test-spell-requires-source`: spell without :stack-item/source fails. Catches: spell without object-id.
- `test-activated-ability-requires-effects`: ability without :stack-item/effects fails. Catches: ability with no effects to resolve.
- `test-delayed-trigger-requires-effects`: delayed trigger without effects fails.
- `test-combat-types-minimal`: :declare-attackers, :declare-blockers, :combat-damage pass with only type+controller. Catches: over-constraining combat markers.
- `test-state-check-trigger-requires-source`: state-check without :stack-item/source fails.

**D. Edge case validation (3 tests):**
- `test-nil-type-fails`: map with :stack-item/type nil fails validation. Catches: nil type slipping through.
- `test-unknown-type-fails`: map with :stack-item/type :nonexistent fails validation. Catches: typo in type keyword.
- `test-extra-fields-allowed`: valid spell with extra :foo/bar key still passes. Catches: s/keys over-constraining (s/keys allows extra keys by default, verify this).

**E. Chokepoint integration (2 tests):**
- `test-create-stack-item-valid-succeeds`: create-stack-item with valid :spell attrs produces item in db. Catches: validation blocking valid data.
- `test-create-stack-item-invalid-throws`: binding *throw-on-spec-failure* true, create-stack-item with :spell missing :object-ref throws. Catches: chokepoint not wired up.

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] engine/spec_util.cljs — `(def ^:dynamic *throw-on-spec-failure* false)` + `(defn validate-at-chokepoint! [spec-key data label] ...)` with goog.DEBUG guard
- [ ] engine/stack_spec.cljs — base field specs: :stack-item/type (keyword), :stack-item/controller (int, player-id), :stack-item/source (uuid, object-id), :stack-item/object-ref (int, datascript EID), :stack-item/effects (coll), :stack-item/targets (coll, optional), :stack-item/description (string, optional)
- [ ] engine/stack_spec.cljs — `(defmulti stack-item-type-spec :stack-item/type)` with 10 defmethods
- [ ] engine/stack_spec.cljs — Per-type defmethod specs (see Field Reference below)
- [ ] engine/stack_spec.cljs — `(s/def ::stack-item (s/multi-spec stack-item-type-spec :stack-item/type))`
- [ ] engine/stack_spec.cljs — `minimal-valid-stack-items` map (10 entries) + `(defn minimal-valid-stack-item [type] ...)`
- [ ] engine/stack.cljs — add `(spec-util/validate-at-chokepoint! ::stack-spec/stack-item attrs "create-stack-item")` in create-stack-item
- [ ] Test file with 16+ tests across categories A-E
- [ ] make validate passes (all existing tests + new tests)

## Field Reference (from codebase investigation)

**CRITICAL**: :stack-item/source is an Object ID (UUID). :stack-item/object-ref is a Datascript EID (integer). They are DIFFERENT fields. Spells have both; triggers may have only source; combat markers have neither.

| Type | Required | Optional |
|------|----------|----------|
| :spell | type, controller, source, object-ref | targets |
| :storm-copy | type, controller, source, object-ref | targets, description |
| :activated-ability | type, controller, source, effects | targets, description |
| :permanent-entered | type, controller, effects | source, description |
| :storm | type, controller, source, effects, description | targets |
| :declare-attackers | type, controller | description |
| :declare-blockers | type, controller | description |
| :combat-damage | type, controller | description |
| :delayed-trigger | type, controller, effects | description |
| :state-check-trigger | type, controller, source, effects | description |

**Verify each row** by reading the actual creation site before writing the defmethod. The codebase investigation found these, but always confirm against live code.

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: nil or missing :stack-item/type**
The s/multi-spec dispatch function will throw on nil type. This is correct behavior — a stack item without a type is a bug. The spec catches it at creation.

**Edge Case: Unknown :stack-item/type**
s/multi-spec throws if no defmethod matches the dispatch value. This is correct — an unknown type is a bug. No :default defmethod needed (unlike resolution.cljs which has a :default for generic effect execution).

**Edge Case: :stack-item/position (auto-assigned)**
create-stack-item assigns :stack-item/position after the attrs map is passed in. Do NOT include :stack-item/position in the spec — it's not part of the caller's input, it's assigned by create-stack-item internally. Validate BEFORE position assignment.

**Edge Case: Fields on object vs stack item**
:object/is-copy, :object/cast-mode, :object/chosen-x are on the Datascript OBJECT entity, not on the stack item attrs. Do NOT spec these — they're separate entities. The stack item only references the object via :stack-item/object-ref (EID).

**Edge Case: :sacrifice-info field**
Found in abilities.cljs:137 on :activated-ability items. Verify whether it flows through create-stack-item. If yes, add as optional field. If it's set separately via d/db-with, ignore it.

**Console.error testing**
Do NOT test console.error path directly. goog.DEBUG is true in test env (shadow-cljs :none optimization). The *throw-on-spec-failure* var converts console.error into throws for test assertions. This is cleaner than trying to mock js/console.error.

## Anti-Patterns (from epic)
- NO changing stack-item creation to fit spec — spec describes what IS, not what SHOULD BE
- NO spec in hot loops — validate at create-stack-item only
- NO :default defmethod on the multi-spec — unknown types should fail loudly
- NO speccing :stack-item/position — it's auto-assigned after validation
- NO speccing object-level fields (:object/is-copy, :object/cast-mode) — wrong entity

## Success Criteria
- [ ] engine/spec_util.cljs exists with *throw-on-spec-failure* and validate-at-chokepoint!
- [ ] validate-at-chokepoint! throws when *throw-on-spec-failure* bound true + invalid data
- [ ] validate-at-chokepoint! does NOT throw when *throw-on-spec-failure* is false (default)
- [ ] engine/stack_spec.cljs has 10 defmethods (one per type keyword)
- [ ] All 10 minimal-valid-stack-items pass s/valid? (doseq test)
- [ ] Spell without :stack-item/object-ref fails validation (thrown? test)
- [ ] Activated-ability without :stack-item/effects fails validation (thrown? test)
- [ ] nil :stack-item/type fails validation
- [ ] Unknown :stack-item/type fails validation
- [ ] create-stack-item in engine/stack.cljs calls validate-at-chokepoint!
- [ ] create-stack-item with invalid attrs + *throw-on-spec-failure* true throws
- [ ] 16+ tests in stack_spec_test.cljs across categories A-E
- [ ] All existing tests pass (no regression)
- [ ] make validate passes
close_reason: All success criteria met. 16 defmethods (10 core + 6 trigger types), spec_util shared helper, chokepoint wired, 16 tests pass, make validate passes. Commit d8bd9f3.

title: Engine infrastructure for static abilities + Sphere of Resistance
design:
## Goal
Build the static ability framework (`:cost-modifier` type) in the engine and implement Sphere of Resistance as the first card using it. This validates the full pipeline: card spec → static_abilities module → rules integration → card definition → tests.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- `engine/rules.cljs:158-166` — `can-cast-mode?` takes `[db player-id object-id mode]`, checks `mana/can-pay?` against `(:mode/mana-cost mode)`
- `engine/rules.cljs:336-355` — `cast-spell-mode` takes `[db player-id object-id mode]`, pays `(:mode/mana-cost mode)`
- `engine/rules.cljs:358-374` — `cast-spell-mode-with-allocation` takes `[db player-id object-id mode allocation]`, pays `(:mode/mana-cost mode)` with allocation
- `events/game.cljs:354-361` — `evaluate-pre-cast-step :mana-allocation` — checks `has-generic-mana-cost?` on raw mode cost, passes to builder
- `events/game.cljs:375-383` — `initiate-cast-with-mode` fallback at line 379 calls `cast-spell-mode` directly with raw mode
- `events/selection/costs.cljs:81-85` — `has-generic-mana-cost?` checks `(pos? (get cost :colorless 0))`
- `events/selection/costs.cljs:314-348` — `build-mana-allocation-selection` takes `resolved-cost` param, reads `:colorless` key for generic
- `events/selection/costs.cljs:468-491` — `confirm-spell-mana-allocation` calls `cast-spell-mode-with-allocation` with mode (uses `(:mode/mana-cost mode)`)
- `db/queries.cljs:80-87` — `get-object` pulls card data from object-id
- `db/queries.cljs:90-100` — `get-objects-in-zone` takes player-id + zone, returns objects with card data
- `cards/artifacts/lotus_petal.cljs` — pattern for artifact card definitions

### CRITICAL: Mana cost key naming
The codebase uses `:colorless` for generic mana in cost maps (e.g., `{:colorless 2 :blue 1}` for {2}{U}). The modifier amount should be a simple integer (always adds generic mana), and `apply-cost-modifiers` adds to the `:colorless` key of the cost map.

### CRITICAL: Pipeline has-generic-mana-cost? bug
`evaluate-pre-cast-step :mana-allocation` (game.cljs:356) checks `has-generic-mana-cost?` on the RAW `(:mode/mana-cost mode)`. If a spell has no generic in base cost (e.g., Dark Ritual `{:black 3}`), the check returns false, and the allocation step is SKIPPED. But with Sphere in play, the effective cost is `{:black 3 :colorless 1}` — there IS generic to allocate.

**Fix**: Compute effective cost at the top of `:mana-allocation` pre-cast step. Use effective cost for BOTH the `has-generic-mana-cost?` check AND passing to the builder. The `initiate-cast-with-mode` fallback (line 379) that calls `cast-spell-mode` directly also needs to pass the effective cost.

### CRITICAL: Need cross-player battlefield query
`get-objects-in-zone` requires a player-id. Static abilities must scan ALL players' battlefields. Need a new query in `db/queries.cljs` (or call for each player). A new Datalog query `get-all-objects-in-zone [db zone]` that omits the owner filter is cleanest.

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

**A. Card definition tests** (Sphere of Resistance):
- Assert `:card/name` = "Sphere of Resistance"
- Assert `:card/mana-cost` = `{:colorless 2}`
- Assert `:card/colors` = `#{}`
- Assert `:card/types` = `#{:artifact}`
- Assert `:card/text` = "Spells cost {1} more to cast."
- Assert `:card/cmc` = 2
- Assert `:card/static-abilities` = vector with exactly one entry
- Assert static ability `:static/type` = `:cost-modifier`, `:modifier/amount` = 1, `:modifier/direction` = `:increase`

**B. Engine tests** (static_abilities module):
- `get-cost-modifiers` returns empty vec when no permanents on battlefield
- `get-cost-modifiers` returns empty vec when permanents have no static abilities (e.g., Lotus Petal on battlefield)
- `get-cost-modifiers` returns Sphere's modifier when Sphere is on battlefield
- `apply-cost-modifiers` returns `{:blue 2}` unchanged when no modifiers (Counterspell base cost)
- `apply-cost-modifiers` returns `{:blue 2 :colorless 1}` when Sphere on battlefield (Counterspell + Sphere)
- `apply-cost-modifiers` returns `{:black 3 :colorless 1}` when Sphere on battlefield (Dark Ritual + Sphere — spell with NO base generic)
- `apply-cost-modifiers` returns `{:colorless 3}` when Sphere on battlefield (Sphere itself has `{:colorless 2}`, becomes `{:colorless 3}`)
- Multiple Spheres: `apply-cost-modifiers` returns `{:blue 2 :colorless 2}` with 2 Spheres (Counterspell)
- Sphere in graveyard/hand does NOT modify costs — only battlefield objects scanned
- Sphere on stack does NOT modify costs — being cast doesn't affect its own casting

**C. Integration tests** (full cast flow):
- Cast Dark Ritual with Sphere on battlefield: player needs {B}{B}{B} + {1} = 4 total mana. With pool `{:black 4}`, succeeds. With pool `{:black 3}`, fails `can-cast?`
- `can-cast?` returns false when player has exact base cost but not modified cost (e.g., 2 blue for Counterspell, but need 2 blue + 1 for Sphere)
- `can-cast?` returns true when player has enough for modified cost
- Cast + resolve Sphere: it enters battlefield as permanent, subsequent spells cost more
- Storm count incremented when casting with Sphere in play
- Mana allocation UI appears for spells that gain generic from Sphere (Dark Ritual with Sphere should trigger mana allocation, even though base cost has no generic)

**D. Flashback integration**:
- Cast Deep Analysis via flashback (`{:colorless 1 :blue 1}` flashback cost) with Sphere: effective flashback cost = `{:colorless 2 :blue 1}`

**G. Edge cases**:
- Zero-cost artifact (Lotus Petal, cost `{}`) with Sphere: effective cost = `{:colorless 1}`
- Sphere does NOT affect activated ability costs — only spells (test: Lotus Petal's mana ability still free with Sphere in play)
- Both players' spells cost more (symmetric): test with opponent casting via bot
- Multiple Spheres on different players' battlefields stack

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] `db/queries.cljs` — Add `get-all-objects-in-zone [db zone]` query (no owner filter, returns objects with card data)
- [ ] `engine/card_spec.cljs` — Add `:card/static-abilities` spec: vector of maps, each with `:static/type` keyword, plus type-specific keys. For `:cost-modifier`: `:modifier/amount` (pos-int), `:modifier/direction` (`:increase`), optional `:modifier/condition` map, optional `:modifier/criteria` map
- [ ] `engine/static_abilities.cljs` — NEW file with:
  - `get-cost-modifiers [db]` — queries battlefield for all permanents with `:cost-modifier` static abilities, returns vec of modifier maps with source metadata (`:source/controller`, `:source/object-id`)
  - `modifier-applies? [db caster-id spell-card modifier]` — checks criteria/condition (for Sphere: always true; criteria/condition support needed for future Defense Grid/Chill)
  - `apply-cost-modifiers [db caster-id spell-card base-cost]` — computes effective cost: sums applicable `:increase` modifiers, adds to `:colorless` key of base-cost. Returns modified cost map.
  - `get-effective-mana-cost [db player-id object-id mode]` — convenience function: looks up card from object-id, gets base cost from mode, applies modifiers. Used by rules functions.
- [ ] `engine/rules.cljs` — Modify `can-cast-mode?` (line 158-166): compute effective cost via `get-effective-mana-cost`, check `mana/can-pay?` against effective cost
- [ ] `engine/rules.cljs` — Modify `cast-spell-mode` (line 336-355): pay effective cost via `get-effective-mana-cost` instead of raw `(:mode/mana-cost mode)`
- [ ] `engine/rules.cljs` — Modify `cast-spell-mode-with-allocation` (line 358-374): pass effective cost to `pay-mana-with-allocation`
- [ ] `events/game.cljs:354-361` — Modify `evaluate-pre-cast-step :mana-allocation`: compute effective cost first, check `has-generic-mana-cost?` on EFFECTIVE cost, pass effective cost to `build-mana-allocation-selection`
- [ ] `events/game.cljs:379` — Modify `initiate-cast-with-mode` fallback: use effective cost when calling `cast-spell-mode`
- [ ] `events/selection/costs.cljs:468-491` — Modify `confirm-spell-mana-allocation`: pass effective cost to `cast-spell-mode-with-allocation` (or have the function compute internally)
- [ ] `cards/artifacts/sphere_of_resistance.cljs` — NEW card definition:
  ```clojure
  {:card/id :sphere-of-resistance
   :card/name "Sphere of Resistance"
   :card/cmc 2
   :card/mana-cost {:colorless 2}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:artifact}
   :card/text "Spells cost {1} more to cast."
   :card/static-abilities [{:static/type :cost-modifier
                            :modifier/amount 1
                            :modifier/direction :increase}]}
  ```
- [ ] `cards/registry.cljs` — Require `fizzle.cards.artifacts.sphere-of-resistance`
- [ ] `test/fizzle/cards/sphere_of_resistance_test.cljs` — Full test suite covering categories A, B, C, D, G

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `engine/static_abilities.cljs` exists with `get-cost-modifiers`, `modifier-applies?`, `apply-cost-modifiers`, `get-effective-mana-cost`
- [ ] New `get-all-objects-in-zone` query in `db/queries.cljs`
- [ ] `can-cast-mode?` accounts for battlefield cost modifiers
- [ ] `cast-spell-mode` and `cast-spell-mode-with-allocation` pay effective cost
- [ ] Pre-cast pipeline `:mana-allocation` step uses effective cost for BOTH `has-generic-mana-cost?` check AND builder
- [ ] `initiate-cast-with-mode` fallback uses effective cost
- [ ] Card spec validates `:card/static-abilities` field
- [ ] Sphere of Resistance: all card definition fields tested with exact values
- [ ] Sphere of Resistance: cast/resolve through production path (enters battlefield)
- [ ] Sphere of Resistance: `can-cast?` correctly reflects modified cost
- [ ] Dark Ritual + Sphere: triggers mana allocation (no-generic-base + modifier = generic)
- [ ] Flashback + Sphere: effective flashback cost tested
- [ ] Multiple Spheres stacking tested
- [ ] Sphere does NOT affect activated ability costs (only spells)
- [ ] Sphere in non-battlefield zones has no effect
- [ ] `make validate` passes

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Critical: `has-generic-mana-cost?` pipeline bypass**
The `:mana-allocation` pre-cast step checks `has-generic-mana-cost?` on the raw mode cost. Spells with no base generic (Dark Ritual `{:black 3}`, Counterspell `{:blue 2}`) will skip allocation even when Sphere adds generic. MUST compute effective cost BEFORE the `has-generic-mana-cost?` check. Test with Dark Ritual + Sphere specifically.

**Critical: Mana key is `:colorless`, not `:generic`**
The codebase uses `:colorless` for generic mana throughout (mana.cljs, costs.cljs, card definitions). The modifier amount should be a simple integer (not a map). `apply-cost-modifiers` adds to the `:colorless` key: `(update base-cost :colorless (fnil + 0) total-increase)`.

**Critical: Cross-player battlefield scan**
`get-objects-in-zone` requires player-id. Cost modifiers from ANY player's battlefield must be found. Need new `get-all-objects-in-zone [db zone]` that omits owner filter. Datalog query: `[:find [(pull ?obj [* {:object/card [*]}]) ...] :in $ ?zone :where [?obj :object/zone ?zone]]`

**Edge case: Sphere doesn't affect its own casting**
When Sphere is cast, it's on the stack (not battlefield). The battlefield scan correctly excludes it. MUST test this explicitly — cast Sphere with no other cost modifiers, verify no extra cost.

**Edge case: Activated abilities unaffected**
Sphere says "spells cost more", not "spells and abilities". `apply-cost-modifiers` is only called from spell-casting paths (`can-cast-mode?`, `cast-spell-mode`). Ability paths (`confirm-ability-mana-allocation`) must NOT call `apply-cost-modifiers`. MUST test that Lotus Petal's mana ability is unaffected.

**Edge case: confirm-spell-mana-allocation cost flow**
`confirm-spell-mana-allocation` (costs.cljs:468) calls `cast-spell-mode-with-allocation` passing `mode`. If `cast-spell-mode-with-allocation` computes effective cost internally, this works. If it takes effective cost as a param, the allocation executor needs to pass it. Recommend: have `cast-spell-mode-with-allocation` compute effective cost internally (same as `cast-spell-mode`), keeping function signatures unchanged.

**Reference patterns:**
- Card definition: follow `cards/artifacts/lotus_petal.cljs` structure
- Test helpers: `th/cast-and-resolve` for simple cast/resolve, `th/setup-game` for game state
- Place Sphere on battlefield for tests: use `th/resolve-permanent` or manually move to battlefield zone

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO using `:generic` key in mana cost maps (convention: use `:colorless` for generic mana)
- ❌ NO changing function signatures of `cast-spell-mode` / `cast-spell-mode-with-allocation` (compute effective cost internally)
- ❌ NO calling `apply-cost-modifiers` from ability activation paths (only spells)
- ❌ NO tautological assertions like `(is (some? x))` (testing: assert exact values)
- ❌ NO reimplementing production handlers in tests (use test-helpers)
close_reason: Static abilities engine + Sphere of Resistance implemented. Commit 405e87d. 2292 tests passing.

title: Add validation assertion to confirm-selection test helper
description: confirm-selection in test_helpers.cljs silently returns unchanged db when selection validation fails. Tests passing invalid selections (wrong count, items not in candidates) get no feedback — they pass while the selection was never actually executed.
design:
## Goal
Make confirm-selection throw an assertion error when the selection it submits fails validation, so tests that pass invalid selections break immediately instead of silently doing nothing.

## Effort Estimate
3-6 hours (1h implementation + 2-5h fixing broken tests)

## Implementation

1. Study existing code:
   - test_helpers.cljs:335-346 — current confirm-selection implementation
   - engine/validation.cljs:51-91 — validate-selection function (returns true/false)
   - selection/core.cljs:426-444 — confirm-selection-impl returns app-db unchanged on validation failure (line 428-429)

2. Write tests first (TDD) in test_helpers_test.cljs:
   - Test: confirm-selection throws when count doesn't match :selection/select-count
     Setup: build selection with {:selection/validation :exact :selection/select-count 1 :selection/candidates #{:a :b :c}}
     Act: call (th/confirm-selection db sel #{:a :b}) — 2 items, expects 1
     Assert: throws with ex-info containing {:selected #{:a :b} :validation :exact :select-count 1}
   - Test: confirm-selection throws when items not in :selection/candidates
     Setup: build selection with {:selection/candidates #{:a :b}}
     Act: call (th/confirm-selection db sel #{:x}) — :x not in candidates
     Assert: throws with ex-info
   - Test: confirm-selection throws when :selection/validation is nil (catches builder bugs)
     Setup: build selection without :selection/validation
     Act: call (th/confirm-selection db sel #{:a})
     Assert: throws (nil validation = safe reject per validate-selection line 83)
   - Test: confirm-selection works for valid :exact selection (regression)
     Setup: build selection with {:selection/validation :exact :selection/select-count 1 :selection/candidates #{:a :b}}
     Act: call (th/confirm-selection db sel #{:a})
     Assert: returns {:db ...} without error
   - Test: confirm-selection works for :always validation (no-op validation type)
     Setup: build selection with {:selection/validation :always}
     Act: call (th/confirm-selection db sel #{:a :b :c})
     Assert: returns {:db ...} without error

3. Implementation in test_helpers.cljs:
   - Add require: [fizzle.engine.validation :as validation]
   - In confirm-selection, BEFORE calling confirm-selection-impl, add:
     ```clojure
     (when-not (validation/validate-selection sel)
       (throw (ex-info "confirm-selection: validation failed"
                       {:selected selected-items
                        :validation (:selection/validation selection)
                        :select-count (:selection/select-count selection)
                        :candidates (or (:selection/candidates selection)
                                        (:selection/candidate-ids selection))})))
     ```
   - Use ex-info (not assert) so the diagnostic map is available in test error output

4. Fix broken tests:
   - Run: make test 2>&1 | grep -E "ExceptionInfo|confirm-selection.*validation failed" | head -30
   - For each broken test, categorize:
     a. Test passes wrong count → fix the selected-items to match :selection/select-count
     b. Test passes items not in candidates → fix items to be actual candidates
     c. Test setup creates selection with wrong validation type → fix the selection setup
     d. Test was testing rejection behavior → use (is (thrown? ...)) instead of confirm-selection
   - NEVER weaken validation logic to make tests pass
   - NEVER remove confirm-selection calls to avoid fixing tests

## Success Criteria
- [ ] confirm-selection calls validation/validate-selection before confirm-selection-impl
- [ ] confirm-selection throws ex-info with diagnostic map when validation fails
- [ ] 5+ TDD tests in test_helpers_test.cljs verify throw on invalid and pass on valid
- [ ] All 130 existing confirm-selection usages pass — broken tests fixed with correct selections (not weakened validation)
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] grep -r "confirm-selection" src/test | wc -l shows same or fewer count (no workaround wrappers added)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: nil :selection/validation**
validate-selection returns false for nil validation type (safe default, line 83). confirm-selection will throw. This is CORRECT behavior — catches builder bugs that forget to set validation type. Do not special-case nil to pass.

**Edge Case: :always validation type**
Selections with :selection/validation :always always pass validation. confirm-selection should work normally for these. Test this explicitly.

**Edge Case: chaining selections**
When confirm-selection returns {:db db :selection next-sel}, the next-sel is a NEW selection from the chain (not the failed original). The assert fires BEFORE confirm-selection-impl, so chaining is unaffected.

**Blast radius**
130 usages across 29 files. Most are card tests. Expect 0-10 failures (well-written tests pass valid selections). If >20 break, investigate whether validate-selection has an edge case, not whether the tests are all wrong.

**Reference: how confirm-selection-impl signals failure today**
selection/core.cljs:428-429 — returns app-db unchanged. No throw, no log, no error key. The test helper sees {:game/pending-selection sel} in the result and returns it as {:db db :selection sel}, making it look like a chaining selection. This is the silent failure being fixed.

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO weakening validate-selection to make tests pass — if a test fails, the test was wrong
- ❌ NO removing confirm-selection calls from tests to avoid fixing them
- ❌ NO adding a :skip-validation? parameter to confirm-selection
- ❌ NO using assert instead of ex-info (assert can be compiled out; ex-info is always active)
close_reason: Validation assertion added to confirm-selection. Commit cbbdb67. 0 existing tests broken.

title: Extract shared game state invariants (ADR-017)
description: Production init, test helpers, and snapshot restoration independently construct game state. Extract shared invariants into a common module so they cannot diverge. Design pending from brainstorm on decomposition boundary (what is invariant vs policy in init-game-state).
close_reason: Completed as part of epic fizzle-lvs0

title: Unify object/power — always set creature P/T in all zones (production + test helpers)
description: Creature objects should always have :object/power and :object/toughness set from card definition regardless of zone. Currently only set on battlefield. Production zones.cljs retracts P/T on battlefield exit — should reset to base instead. Test helpers add-card-to-zone, add-cards-to-library, add-cards-to-graveyard only set creature attrs for battlefield.
design:
## Goal
Set :object/power and :object/toughness on all creature objects at creation time regardless of zone. Reset (not retract) P/T to base values when leaving battlefield. Update test helpers to match.

## Effort Estimate
4-8 hours (production change + 3 test helper changes + fixing broken tests)

## Implementation

1. Study existing code:
   - engine/zones.cljs:75-117 — zone transition creature attr handling
   - engine/zones.cljs:96-104 — entering-battlefield adds P/T + summoning-sick + damage-marked
   - engine/zones.cljs:76-93 — leaving-battlefield retracts P/T + combat attrs
   - test_helpers.cljs:51-90 — add-card-to-zone (only sets creature attrs for battlefield)
   - test_helpers.cljs:93-129 — add-cards-to-library (no creature attrs)
   - test_helpers.cljs:132-161 — add-cards-to-graveyard (no creature attrs)
   - engine/creatures.cljs:27,46 — defensively defaults to (or :object/power 0)

2. Write tests first (TDD):
   - Test: creature added to graveyard has :object/power and :object/toughness
   - Test: creature added to hand has :object/power and :object/toughness
   - Test: creature added to library has :object/power and :object/toughness
   - Test: non-creature in graveyard does NOT get :object/power
   - Test: creature leaving battlefield resets P/T to card base values (not nil)
   - Test: creature entering battlefield keeps existing P/T, adds summoning-sick/damage-marked

3. Production changes in engine/zones.cljs:
   a. Leaving battlefield (lines 76-93):
      - KEEP retracting: :object/summoning-sick, :object/damage-marked, :object/attacking, :object/blocking
      - CHANGE: instead of retracting :object/power and :object/toughness, RESET them to :card/power and :card/toughness from card definition
      - Need to pull card data to get base values: (d/pull db [{:object/card [:card/power :card/toughness :card/types]}] obj-eid)
   b. Entering battlefield (lines 96-104):
      - REMOVE: :object/power and :object/toughness additions (already present on creature objects)
      - KEEP: :object/summoning-sick true, :object/damage-marked 0
      - Still need the creature type check for summoning-sick/damage-marked

4. Test helper changes:
   a. add-card-to-zone (lines 51-90):
      - Move creature attr setting OUT of the battlefield-only branch
      - If card has :creature in :card/types, ALWAYS set:
        :object/power (:card/power card-def)
        :object/toughness (:card/toughness card-def)
      - Battlefield-only: :object/summoning-sick true, :object/damage-marked 0
      - Library: :object/position 0 (keep existing)
   b. add-cards-to-library (lines 93-129):
      - Add card def lookup for each card
      - If creature, add :object/power and :object/toughness to the transaction
   c. add-cards-to-graveyard (lines 132-161):
      - Same pattern: lookup card def, add creature attrs if creature

5. Fix broken tests:
   - Tests that assert creatures in graveyard DON'T have :object/power will now fail (correct)
   - Tests that assert leaving battlefield removes :object/power will need to assert reset-to-base instead

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Creature objects in ALL zones have :object/power and :object/toughness from card definition
- [ ] engine/zones.cljs resets (not retracts) P/T when leaving battlefield
- [ ] engine/zones.cljs entering-battlefield only adds summoning-sick/damage-marked (P/T already present)
- [ ] add-card-to-zone always sets creature attrs regardless of zone
- [ ] add-cards-to-library always sets creature attrs for creatures
- [ ] add-cards-to-graveyard always sets creature attrs for creatures
- [ ] Non-creature cards do NOT get :object/power
- [ ] All existing tests pass (or fixed)
- [ ] 6+ TDD tests covering creature attrs in all zones + zone transitions
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Zone transition ordering**
When resetting P/T on battlefield exit, must pull card data BEFORE the zone change transaction. The card data lookup should use the current db state.

**Non-creature cards**
Only set :object/power/:object/toughness for cards with :creature in :card/types. Instants, sorceries, artifacts without creature type must NOT get these attributes.

**Summoning-sick and damage-marked remain battlefield-only**
These combat-related attributes are NOT zone-universal. Only P/T is unified. Keep summoning-sick/damage-marked in battlefield-only logic.

**add-cards-to-library performance**
This helper loops through cards and transacts one at a time. Adding a card def lookup per card adds a query. Should be fine for test sizes.

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO conditional creature attrs based on zone — always set for creatures (epic anti-pattern)
- ❌ NO retracting P/T on zone exit — reset to base values (epic anti-pattern)
- ❌ NO adding summoning-sick/damage-marked to non-battlefield zones — these stay battlefield-only
- ❌ NO skipping non-creature checks — verify :card/types contains :creature before setting
close_reason: Creature P/T unified across all zones. Commit b989d8d. Production zones.cljs + 3 test helpers updated. 0 tests broken.

title: Clean up app-db/game-db function signature boundary
description:
## Problem
Functions inconsistently handle the app-db vs game-db boundary. Some take both, some take one, no clear convention.

## Specifics
- initiate-cast-with-mode takes BOTH app-db and game-db as parameters — unclear contract
- Event handlers should take app-db, pure engine functions should take game-db only
- Some helpers cross the boundary within a single function
- Makes refactoring dangerous (easy to pass wrong db type to a Datascript query)

## Rule to Enforce
- re-frame event handlers: receive app-db, extract game-db internally
- Pure engine functions (in engine/ namespace): receive only game-db, return game-db
- Helper functions in events/: should clearly document which they take

## Success Criteria
- All engine functions take only game-db
- No function takes both app-db and game-db as parameters
- Clear convention documented
- All tests pass
close_reason: Superseded by epic fizzle-ag28 with full design and requirements

title: Always-visible graveyard and library card counts
description: Display card counts for graveyard and library for both players in a persistent, always-visible location (not behind collapsed panels). Critical for threshold checks (7+ cards in graveyard) and Brain Freeze lethality math (opponent library size). Consider: compact display near mana pool or phase bar area, color change when threshold is active (graveyard >= 7), both player counts visible.

Card-specific logic should NOT be in the engine - cards define effects/abilities as data, engine interprets generically. Check: events/game.cljs for hardcoded card checks, any other places where card IDs are checked directly in engine code.

Resolves top-to-bottom in order. Consider: button placement near Yield, keyboard shortcut (shift+space?), show results summary after bulk resolution, animation or brief display of each resolution, interaction with triggered abilities that might add new items to stack mid-resolution.

title: Extract hand, mana pool, and storm count components
description: Extract hand-view, card-view, mana-pool-view, and storm-count-view from core.cljs into views/hand.cljs and views/mana_pool.cljs. Migrate all inline styles to Tailwind utility classes using @theme tokens. Follow the extraction pattern established in opponent.cljs (fizzle-ryvi).
design:
## Goal
Extract hand-view, card-view, mana-pool-view, storm-count-view, and the mana-colors constant from core.cljs into views/hand.cljs and views/mana_pool.cljs. Migrate all inline :style maps to Tailwind utility classes using @theme tokens. Follow the extraction pattern established in opponent.cljs.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-ryvi established the extraction pattern: create view file, use Tailwind classes, subscribe to re-frame subs, no inline styles. views/common.cljs provides collapsible-zone wrapper. This task extracts the next batch of left-column zones.

## Implementation

### 1. Add missing @theme tokens to src/css/app.css
Add tokens needed by these components that don't already exist:
- --color-perm-border: #6a6 (untapped permanent border)
- --color-perm-border-tapped: #444 (tapped permanent border)  
- --color-perm-bg: #1a2a1a (untapped permanent background)
- --color-perm-bg-tapped: #1a1a1a (tapped permanent background)
- --color-perm-text-tapped: #666 (tapped permanent text)
- --color-ability-bg: #4a3a2a (activated ability button background)
- --color-ability-text: #dd44aa (activated ability button text)
- --color-btn-enabled-bg: #2a6a2a (enabled action button background — shared with controls, added here)

Note: Many colors already exist as @theme tokens (surface-raised, surface-hover, border, border-selected, gold, mana-*, text, text-muted, text-dim, text-label). Only add tokens that are genuinely new.

### 2. Create views/hand.cljs
New file: src/main/fizzle/views/hand.cljs
Namespace: fizzle.views.hand

Move from core.cljs:
- card-view (lines 21-38) — private helper, renders a single hand card
- hand-view (lines 41-50) — public, subscribes to ::subs/hand and ::subs/selected-card

Tailwind conversions:
- Card border: border-2 + conditional border-border-selected / border-border
- Card styling: rounded-md px-3.5 py-2.5 mx-1.5 cursor-pointer min-w-[100px] text-center select-none
- Card background: conditional bg-surface-hover (selected) / bg-surface-raised (default)
- Card text: text-text
- Hand label: text-text-label mb-1.5 text-xs (matches opponent pattern)
- Hand container: flex flex-wrap
- Hand wrapper: mb-4

Subscriptions needed:
- fizzle.subs.game/hand
- fizzle.subs.game/selected-card

Events dispatched:
- fizzle.events.game/select-card (on card click)

Requires:
- [fizzle.events.game :as events]
- [fizzle.subs.game :as subs]
- [re-frame.core :as rf]

### 3. Create views/mana_pool.cljs
New file: src/main/fizzle/views/mana_pool.cljs
Namespace: fizzle.views.mana-pool

Move from core.cljs:
- mana-colors constant (line 17-18) — letter symbols map
- mana-pool-view (lines 53-72) — subscribes to ::subs/mana-pool
- storm-count-view (lines 75-80) — subscribes to ::subs/storm-count

Tailwind conversions for mana-pool-view:
- Container: mb-4
- Label: text-text-label mb-1.5 text-xs
- Pool flex: flex gap-2.5
- Each mana span: font-bold text-lg + conditional mana color class
- Mana color mapping function: returns Tailwind class string based on keyword
  - :white -> "text-mana-white"
  - :blue -> "text-mana-blue"  
  - :black -> "text-mana-black"
  - :red -> "text-mana-red"
  - :green -> "text-mana-green"
  - :colorless -> "text-mana-colorless"
  - default -> "text-mana-colorless"

Tailwind conversions for storm-count-view:
- Container: mb-4
- Label: text-text-label mb-1.5 text-xs
- Count display: text-2xl font-bold text-gold

Subscriptions needed:
- fizzle.subs.game/mana-pool
- fizzle.subs.game/storm-count

No events dispatched (read-only components).

Requires:
- [fizzle.subs.game :as subs]
- [re-frame.core :as rf]

### 4. Update core.cljs
- Add requires: [fizzle.views.hand :as hand], [fizzle.views.mana-pool :as mana-pool]
- Replace [hand-view] with [hand/hand-view]
- Replace [mana-pool-view] with [mana-pool/mana-pool-view]
- Replace [storm-count-view] with [mana-pool/storm-count-view]
- Remove from core.cljs: mana-colors constant, card-view, hand-view, mana-pool-view, storm-count-view
- Keep all other components in core.cljs (battlefield, graveyard, stack, controls, turn-bar, etc.)

### 5. Create tests
Create test files following opponent_test.cljs pattern:

src/test/fizzle/views/hand_test.cljs - not needed (card-view/hand-view are pure Reagent subscriptions with no extractable logic)

src/test/fizzle/views/mana_pool_test.cljs - test the mana-color-class function:
- test each of 6 mana colors returns correct Tailwind class
- test unknown color returns default (text-mana-colorless)

The mana-color-class function is the only testable pure function being extracted. card-view and hand-view are pure Reagent components that subscribe to state — testing them requires re-frame test infrastructure which is out of scope.

### 6. Verify
- make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- No inline :style maps in hand.cljs or mana_pool.cljs
- No hardcoded hex colors in hand.cljs or mana_pool.cljs

## Success Criteria
- [ ] views/hand.cljs exists with card-view and hand-view components
- [ ] views/mana_pool.cljs exists with mana-pool-view, storm-count-view, and mana-color-class
- [ ] Zero inline :style maps in hand.cljs and mana_pool.cljs
- [ ] No hardcoded hex colors in hand.cljs or mana_pool.cljs (all @theme tokens)
- [ ] mana-color-class pure function maps each mana keyword to correct Tailwind class
- [ ] core.cljs requires and uses hand/hand-view, mana-pool/mana-pool-view, mana-pool/storm-count-view
- [ ] card-view, hand-view, mana-pool-view, storm-count-view, mana-colors removed from core.cljs
- [ ] New @theme tokens added to app.css for permanent and ability colors
- [ ] mana_pool_test.cljs has 7+ tests for mana-color-class (6 colors + unknown default)
- [ ] Components are layout-agnostic (no grid/position styling)
- [ ] No changes to event/subscription contracts
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO inline :style maps (use Tailwind utility classes)
- NO hardcoded hex colors (use @theme tokens)
- NO layout positioning in components (grid parent handles placement)
- NO game logic in views (subscribe and dispatch only)
- NO breaking existing subscription/event contracts
- NO mana color tokens for non-mana purposes (semantic correctness)
- NO Tailwind v3 patterns (tailwind.config.js, @tailwind directives)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Mana Color Class Selection:**
The mana-color-class function uses a case expression to map mana keywords to Tailwind class strings. Tailwind v4 scans compiled JS for string literals, so the full class names (e.g., "text-mana-blue") must appear as complete strings in the source — NOT constructed dynamically (e.g., (str "text-mana-" (name color)) would NOT work because Tailwind can't see the generated class names at build time).

**mana-colors constant scope:**
The mana-colors constant (W/U/B/R/G/C symbol map) is only used by mana-pool-view. Moving it to mana_pool.cljs is correct. Verify no other file references it (grep for mana-colors).

**card-view dispatch event namespace:**
card-view dispatches ::events/select-card. In core.cljs, :: resolves to fizzle.core. But the actual event is registered as :fizzle.events.game/select-card. Check how it's aliased — core.cljs requires [fizzle.events.game :as events], so ::events/select-card resolves to :fizzle.events.game/select-card. In hand.cljs, we need the same require and alias to keep the dispatch working.

**Arbitrary Tailwind values:**
min-w-[100px] uses Tailwind's arbitrary value syntax. This works in v4 but verify the compiled CSS includes it. Alternative: min-w-24 (96px) or min-w-28 (112px) — close but not exact. Arbitrary value [100px] is correct here.

**No test for card-view/hand-view:**
These are pure Reagent components with no extractable business logic. Testing them would require re-frame-test setup (mock subscriptions, render components, check hiccup). The opponent.cljs pattern tested the extracted pure function (health-class), not the Reagent component. Same pattern here: test mana-color-class, don't test Reagent rendering.
close_reason: All success criteria met. hand.cljs and mana_pool.cljs extracted with Tailwind classes, 7 new tests for mana-color-class, 8 @theme tokens added, make validate passes (980 tests).

title: Implement tutor effect type and Merchant Scroll
design:
## Goal

Implement the :tutor effect type with library search, criteria filtering, selection modal, and fail-to-find option. Validate by implementing Merchant Scroll card.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Phase 1 complete: City of Brass trigger migrated to event system, dead code removed.

Phase 2 of epic fizzle-wjl: Tutor Infrastructure.

## Implementation

### 1. Study Existing Patterns

**Selection modal (Careful Study pattern):**
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs:588-717 - pending-selection system
- src/test/fizzle/cards/careful_study_test.cljs - selection state tests
- Key: resolve-spell-with-selection handles selection-requiring effects

**Library access:**
- src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs:142-161 - get-top-n-library pattern
- Library uses :object/position for ordering (0 = top)

**Shuffle implementation needed:**
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs:28-30 - uses cljs `shuffle` at init
- Need new `shuffle-library` function in zones.cljs or queries.cljs

### 2. Add shuffle-library Function (NEW)

In src/main/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs:

```clojure
(defn shuffle-library
  "Randomize the order of all cards in a player's library.
   Updates :object/position for all library cards with new random positions.
   Returns updated db."
  [db player-id]
  (let [player-eid (q/get-player-eid db player-id)
        library-cards (d/q '[:find ?e
                              :in $ ?owner
                              :where [?e :object/owner ?owner]
                                     [?e :object/zone :library]]
                           db player-eid)
        shuffled-eids (vec (shuffle library-cards))
        position-txs (map-indexed (fn [pos [eid]]
                                    [:db/add eid :object/position pos])
                                  shuffled-eids)]
    (d/db-with db position-txs)))
```

### 3. Add query-library-by-criteria Function

In src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs:

```clojure
(defn query-library-by-criteria
  "Return objects in library matching criteria.
   Criteria map supports:
     :card/types    - Set of types, object must have ALL (AND)
     :card/colors   - Set of colors, object must have ANY (OR - blue OR white)
     :card/subtypes - Set of subtypes, object must have ANY (OR)
   Returns vector of objects with card data, or [] if no matches."
  [db player-id criteria]
  (let [player-eid (get-player-eid db player-id)
        library-objs (get-objects-in-zone db player-id :library)]
    (when player-eid
      (filterv
        (fn [obj]
          (let [card (:object/card obj)
                card-types (:card/types card #{})
                card-colors (:card/colors card #{})
                card-subtypes (:card/subtypes card #{})]
            (and
              ;; All required types must be present (AND logic)
              (every? card-types (get criteria :card/types #{}))
              ;; At least one required color (OR logic) - empty criteria = matches all
              (or (empty? (get criteria :card/colors #{}))
                  (some card-colors (get criteria :card/colors #{})))
              ;; At least one required subtype (OR logic)
              (or (empty? (get criteria :card/subtypes #{}))
                  (some card-subtypes (get criteria :card/subtypes #{}))))))
        library-objs))))
```

### 4. Add :tutor Effect Type to effects.cljs

In src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:

```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect-impl :tutor
  ;; Search library for a card matching criteria.
  ;; 
  ;; Effect keys:
  ;;   :effect/criteria - Map of card attributes to match (types, colors, subtypes)
  ;;   :effect/target-zone - Zone to move found card to (:hand for Merchant Scroll)
  ;;   :effect/shuffle? - Whether to shuffle after (default true)
  ;;
  ;; This effect returns db UNCHANGED. The actual tutor happens at the
  ;; app-db level via re-frame events when the player confirms their selection.
  ;; The calling code checks for :selection :tutor and sets up pending selection.
  [db _player-id _effect]
  ;; Return unchanged - selection handled at app-db level
  db)
```

### 5. Extend Selection System for Tutor

In src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs, extend resolve-spell-with-selection:

```clojure
;; In resolve-spell-with-selection, detect :tutor effect similar to :discard
;; Key differences from discard selection:
;;   :selection/zone :library (not :hand)
;;   :selection/count 1 (tutors find exactly 1)
;;   :selection/allow-fail-to-find true (always!)
;;   :selection/candidates <matching-object-ids>  ; Precomputed matches

;; Selection state for tutor:
{:selection/zone :library
 :selection/count 1
 :selection/player-id player-id
 :selection/selected #{}
 :selection/spell-id object-id
 :selection/remaining-effects effects-after
 :selection/effect-type :tutor
 :selection/target-zone :hand  ; Where card goes if found
 :selection/allow-fail-to-find true
 :selection/candidates matching-ids}  ; Pre-filtered valid choices
```

### 6. Add confirm-tutor-selection Event

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
 ::confirm-tutor-selection
 (fn [db _]
   (let [selection (:game/pending-selection db)
         selected (:selection/selected selection)  ; #{} or #{object-id}
         spell-id (:selection/spell-id selection)
         target-zone (:selection/target-zone selection)
         player-id (:selection/player-id selection)
         game-db (:game/db db)]
     (if (empty? selected)
       ;; Fail-to-find: just shuffle
       (-> db
           (assoc :game/db (zones/shuffle-library game-db player-id))
           (dissoc :game/pending-selection))
       ;; Found card: move to target zone, then shuffle
       (let [card-id (first selected)
             db-after-move (zones/move-to-zone game-db card-id target-zone)
             db-after-shuffle (zones/shuffle-library db-after-move player-id)]
         (-> db
             (assoc :game/db db-after-shuffle)
             (dissoc :game/pending-selection)))))))
```

### 7. Implement Merchant Scroll Card

In src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:

```clojure
(def merchant-scroll
  {:card/id :merchant-scroll
   :card/name "Merchant Scroll"
   :card/types #{:sorcery}
   :card/mana-cost {:blue 1 :generic 1}
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :tutor
                   :effect/criteria {:card/types #{:instant}
                                     :card/colors #{:blue}}
                   :effect/target-zone :hand
                   :effect/shuffle? true}]
   :card/text "Search your library for a blue instant card, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle."})
```

### 8. Add Tests

In src/test/fizzle/cards/merchant_scroll_test.cljs:

**Query function tests (what bugs they catch):**
- test-query-library-single-match: Catches regression if criteria matching broken
- test-query-library-multiple-matches: Catches bug where only first match returned
- test-query-library-no-matches: Catches crash on empty result set
- test-query-library-type-and-color: Catches AND/OR logic bugs

**Tutor effect tests:**
- test-tutor-shows-selection-modal: Catches missing UI interaction
- test-tutor-fail-to-find-shuffles: Catches shuffle skip on empty selection
- test-tutor-card-to-hand: Catches zone movement bugs
- test-tutor-shuffles-after-find: Catches shuffle order bugs (must shuffle after move)

**Merchant Scroll integration tests:**
- test-merchant-scroll-finds-blue-instant: Happy path, catches basic integration
- test-merchant-scroll-ignores-non-blue: Catches color filter bug
- test-merchant-scroll-ignores-non-instant: Catches type filter bug
- test-merchant-scroll-fail-to-find-works: Catches fail-to-find path regression
- test-merchant-scroll-with-empty-library: Catches crash on edge case

## Success Criteria

- [ ] query-library-by-criteria function exists in queries.cljs
- [ ] shuffle-library function exists in zones.cljs
- [ ] :tutor effect type defined in effects.cljs
- [ ] Selection modal appears for tutor with matching cards + "Find Nothing" option
- [ ] Selecting card moves it to target zone (hand for Merchant Scroll)
- [ ] Selecting "Find Nothing" shuffles library without moving card
- [ ] Library shuffled after BOTH find and fail-to-find
- [ ] Merchant Scroll card definition in iggy_pop.cljs
- [ ] 12+ tests passing: `make test` shows tutor tests green
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty Library**
- If library is empty when tutor resolves, modal shows only "Find Nothing" option
- Must not crash, must still shuffle (shuffle of empty is no-op)
- Test: test-merchant-scroll-with-empty-library

**Edge Case: No Matching Cards**
- If library has cards but none match criteria, modal shows only "Find Nothing"
- Player must be able to legally fail-to-find (MTG rules)
- Test: test-merchant-scroll-no-blue-instants-in-library

**Edge Case: Exactly One Match**
- If exactly one card matches, still show modal with that card + "Find Nothing"
- NEVER auto-select (player may want to fail-to-find for bluffing)
- Anti-pattern enforces this

**Shuffle Timing**
- CRITICAL: Move card BEFORE shuffle
- If shuffle first: card ends up at random position then moved (wastes entropy)
- Correct order: move to hand → shuffle remaining library
- Test: test-tutor-shuffles-after-find verifies shuffle happens after move

**Selection Count**
- Tutor selects exactly 0 or 1 card (not 2 like Careful Study)
- :selection/count should be 1 but allow 0 for fail-to-find
- Different from discard which requires exact count

**Reference Implementation**
- Study careful_study_test.cljs:275-305 for resolve-spell-with-selection pattern
- Tutor is similar but: zone=library, count=0-1, has candidates filter

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO auto-selecting when only one match (always show modal with fail-to-find)
- ❌ NO omitting fail-to-find option (even when matches exist)
- ❌ NO shuffling before moving card (shuffle AFTER move)
- ❌ NO revealing non-revealed tutors (Merchant Scroll reveals - text says "reveal it")
- ❌ NO skipping shuffle on fail-to-find (always shuffle, even if nothing found)
- ❌ NO crashing on empty library (handle gracefully)
- ❌ NO leaking card order pre-shuffle (don't iterate library in visible order then shuffle)
close_reason: Tutor infrastructure implemented with Merchant Scroll card. Tests passing, make validate passed.

title: Counter Foundation: Counterspell + Annul
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Counter effect type that removes a targeted spell from the stack
- Stack-zone targeting: spells can target other spells on the stack
- Type-restricted stack targeting: Annul targets only artifact/enchantment spells
- Zone transitions respect existing move-spell-off-stack behavior (graveyard for normal, exile for flashback, removal for copies)
- Both player and bot can cast counterspells
- Counterspell: {U}{U} instant, counter target spell
- Annul: {U} instant, counter target artifact or enchantment spell

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] New :counter-spell effect type in engine/effects.cljs
- [ ] Stack-zone targeting works in targeting system
- [ ] Counterspell card defined with correct oracle text, mana cost, types
- [ ] Annul card defined with correct oracle text, mana cost, types, type-restricted targeting
- [ ] Countered spells go to graveyard (normal), exile (flashback), removed (copies)
- [ ] Fizzling works when counter target leaves stack before resolution
- [ ] Both cards registered in registry.cljs
- [ ] Full test suites per CLAUDE.md requirements (8+ tests each as targeted spells)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoded graveyard destination for countered spells (correctness: move-spell-off-stack already handles zone transitions for flashback/copies)
- ❌ NO special-casing card names in engine code (architecture: cards are data, engine interprets data)
- ❌ NO new zones or zone-like concepts (simplicity: stack is already a zone, use it)
- ❌ NO reimplementing spell removal logic (reuse: move-spell-off-stack exists and is tested)
- ❌ NO tautological test assertions like (some? x) or (string? x) (testing: assert exact values)
- ❌ NO reimplementing production handlers in tests (testing: test through rules/cast-spell and game/resolve-one-item)

## Approach
Add a single new effect type :counter-spell to engine/effects.cljs that calls the existing rules/move-spell-off-stack function on the targeted spell. Cards use :target/zone :stack for targeting with optional :target/criteria for type restrictions (Annul). This follows the exact same pattern as :destroy for permanents. No new infrastructure needed — stack targeting, fizzling, and spell removal all exist.

## Architecture
- engine/effects.cljs — New :counter-spell effect handler (~15 lines)
- cards/blue/counterspell.cljs — Card definition with stack targeting
- cards/blue/annul.cljs — Card definition with type-restricted stack targeting
- cards/registry.cljs — Register both new cards
- engine/targeting.cljs — May need minor updates if stack targeting validation needs adjustment

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Fizzle has no counterspell mechanics. The bot system needs countermagic to create realistic practice scenarios (forcing through counters). The engine already supports stack-zone targeting and spell removal but lacks a :counter-spell effect type to connect them.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- engine/effects.cljs — 19 effect types, :destroy handles permanent removal
- engine/resolution.cljs — move-spell-off-stack handles all zone transitions
- engine/targeting.cljs — valid-targets supports :target/zone :stack
- Fizzling mechanism already works when targets become illegal

**External:**
- Counterspell oracle: Counter target spell. {U}{U} instant.
- Annul oracle: Counter target artifact or enchantment spell. {U} instant.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. New :counter-spell effect type ✓
**What it is:** Single new effect handler calling existing move-spell-off-stack.
**Chosen because:** Minimal change, follows :destroy pattern, correct semantics for future soft-counter extension.

#### 2. Reuse :destroy effect ❌
**Why explored:** Fewer new concepts.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Semantically wrong — destroy and counter are different MTG concepts. Would complicate soft counter extension in Epic 2.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We're collapsing all removal into a single effect type.

#### 3. Generic remove-from-zone effect ❌
**Why explored:** More general abstraction.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** YAGNI — over-engineering for current need.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We need 3+ zone-based removal effect types.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** Hard counters (unconditional), stack targeting, type-restricted targeting
**Out of scope:** Soft counters (unless-pay), alternate costs, modal spells, color targeting — covered by Epics 2 and 3

### Open Questions
- Does stack targeting need any UI changes to present stack items as targets? (resolve during implementation)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Who casts counterspells? | Both player and bot | No special bot-only logic needed |
| Scope approach? | Simple counters first, chain epics | Epic 1 = foundation only |
| Effect approach? | New :counter-spell type | Follows :destroy pattern |

### Research Deep-Dives
#### Stack Targeting Validation
**Question:** Does the targeting system already support :target/zone :stack?
**Sources:** engine/targeting.cljs, engine/resolution.cljs
**Findings:** Yes — valid-targets supports stack zone. move-spell-off-stack handles all zone transitions.
**Conclusion:** No new targeting infrastructure needed.

### Dead-End Paths
#### Reuse :destroy for countering
**Why explored:** Fewer effect types
**Why abandoned:** Semantically wrong, would complicate soft counters in Epic 2

### Open Concerns Raised
- Stack targeting UI presentation — deferred to implementation
close_reason: All success criteria met. Reviewer APPROVED. Counter-spell effect, Counterspell, and Annul implemented with 22 tests.

title: Replace hand-built selections with builder output in executor tests
description: 26 calls to execute-confirmed-selection use manually constructed selection maps. If builders produce different field names or defaults, executor tests pass with stale data while production breaks. Key files: costs_allocation_test.cljs (4 executor tests), storm_split_test.cljs (4 executor tests), hierarchy_test.cljs (5 executor tests), order_bottom_selection_test.cljs (4 chain tests). For each: call the production builder first, then pass its output to the executor or confirm-selection-impl.

title: Refactor: Unify target storage on stack-item instead of object
description:
Cast-time targeting stores targets on :object/targets while activated abilities store on :stack-item/targets. This inconsistency caused a bug where history descriptions couldn't find targets for targeted spells (Orim's Chant). A workaround is in place (get-target-info fallback in descriptions.cljs) but the root cause needs a brainstorm and refactor to unify on :stack-item/targets.

## Problem
- Activated abilities: targets stored on :stack-item/targets (correct)
- Cast-time targeting: targets stored on :object/targets (inconsistent)
- Targets conceptually belong to the spell-on-the-stack, not the card object

## Known consumers of :object/targets
- confirm-cast-time-target (selection.cljs:999) — writes it
- resolve-effect-with-stored-target (selection.cljs:565) — reads it
- resolve-targeted-effect (effects.cljs:438) — reads it
- resolve-grant-alternate-cost (effects.cljs:570) — reads it
- resolve-add-restriction (effects.cljs:614) — reads it
- target validation (targeting.cljs:137) — reads it

## Approach
Requires brainstorm to design the refactor:
- Move target storage from object to stack-item for cast-time targeting
- Update all effect resolution points to read from stack-item instead of object
- Remove get-target-info fallback in descriptions.cljs once unified
- Consider impact on target validation (fizzle check on resolution)
- Ensure storm copies inherit targets correctly
close_reason: Superseded and completed by epic fizzle-etdk (Unify target storage on stack-items)

title: Clean engine boundary: remove UX concepts from engine/priority
description: Remove auto-mode accessors (get/set/clear-auto-mode) and stop accessors (check-stop, set-player-stops, check-opponent-stop, set-opponent-stops) from engine/priority.cljs. Move stop accessors to events/priority_flow.cljs or db/queries.cljs. Remove :game/auto-mode from schema. Engine becomes purely game rules — no UX concepts.
design:
## Goal

Remove all UX/orchestration concepts from engine/priority.cljs. After this task, engine/priority contains ONLY priority bookkeeping (yield-priority, transfer-priority, reset-passes, set-priority-holder, get-priority-holder-eid, both-passed?, in-priority-phase?, priority-phases, get-passed-eids).

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## What Moves Where

**Auto-mode functions → events/priority_flow.cljs** (they're consumed only there):
- get-auto-mode (3 lines)
- set-auto-mode (3 lines)
- clear-auto-mode (5 lines)

**Stop functions → events/priority_flow.cljs** (check-stop/opponent-stop used only there; set-stops used only in events/ui.cljs which can import from priority_flow):
- check-stop (3 lines)
- set-player-stops (2 lines)
- check-opponent-stop (4 lines)
- set-opponent-stops (2 lines)

**:game/auto-mode in schema**: Keep for now — later task eliminates it entirely when the director model replaces auto-mode. Removing from schema now would break all auto-mode consumers before the replacement exists.

**:player/stops, :player/opponent-stops in schema**: Stay — these are persistent player preferences, not engine concepts. The data lives in game-db; only the accessor functions move.

**subs/game.cljs, events/init.cljs, sharing/restorer.cljs**: These read/write :player/stops via raw d/pull, NOT via priority functions. No code change needed — they don't import the functions being moved.

## Implementation Checklist

- [ ] Move 3 auto-mode functions from engine/priority.cljs to events/priority_flow.cljs (pure datascript operations, no re-frame dependency)
- [ ] Move 4 stop functions from engine/priority.cljs to events/priority_flow.cljs
- [ ] Update events/ui.cljs import: change `priority/set-player-stops` and `priority/set-opponent-stops` to import from events/priority_flow (or inline the 2-line functions)
- [ ] Move tests from engine/priority_test.cljs to events/priority_test.cljs: stop tests (lines 104-189), auto-mode tests (lines 126-155)
- [ ] Verify engine/mana_activation.cljs only uses priority bookkeeping (get-priority-holder-eid) — no stop/auto-mode calls
- [ ] Verify engine/rules.cljs only uses priority bookkeeping (in-priority-phase?) — no stop/auto-mode calls
- [ ] Verify bots/interceptor.cljs uses priority only for get-priority-holder-eid — no stop/auto-mode calls
- [ ] Grep verification: `grep -r 'auto-mode\|check-stop\|set-player-stops\|opponent-stop' src/main/fizzle/engine/` returns zero results
- [ ] Run make validate

## Success Criteria

- [ ] engine/priority.cljs exports only: yield-priority, transfer-priority, reset-passes, set-priority-holder, get-priority-holder-eid, both-passed?, in-priority-phase?, priority-phases, get-passed-eids (9 functions)
- [ ] All auto-mode functions (get/set/clear) live in events/priority_flow.cljs
- [ ] All stop functions (check-stop, set-player-stops, check-opponent-stop, set-opponent-stops) live in events/priority_flow.cljs
- [ ] events/ui.cljs updated to import stop setters from new location
- [ ] Grep `auto-mode\|check-stop\|set-player-stops\|opponent-stop` in src/main/fizzle/engine/ returns zero matches
- [ ] No engine/*.cljs file imports or calls auto-mode or stop functions
- [ ] Tests for moved functions relocated from engine/priority_test.cljs to events/priority_test.cljs
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] No behavior changes — all existing tests pass (after relocation)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Circular import risk**: events/priority_flow.cljs already imports engine/priority. Moving functions INTO priority_flow from priority creates no circular dependency — the arrow goes one way (events → engine). events/ui.cljs currently imports engine/priority for stop setters; it will import events/priority_flow instead (or inline the 2-line datascript operations).

**Test relocation**: engine/priority_test.cljs contains tests for both bookkeeping functions (stay) and stop/auto-mode functions (move). Split the test file — stop/auto-mode tests move to events/priority_test.cljs. Verify the test counts match before and after.

**bots/interceptor.cljs**: Currently imports engine/priority. Grep confirms it only uses get-priority-holder-eid (bookkeeping). No change needed, but verify explicitly.

**What NOT to change**: Do not modify :game/auto-mode schema, do not change auto-mode semantics, do not change stop behavior. This task is purely about where the accessor functions live — a module boundary change, not a behavior change.

## Anti-patterns

- ❌ NO changing auto-mode or stop behavior (pure relocation, no semantic changes)
- ❌ NO removing :game/auto-mode from schema (later task handles elimination)
- ❌ NO creating circular imports between engine/ and events/ layers
- ❌ NO leaving dead imports (if engine/priority no longer exports stops, remove the import from consumers)
close_reason: Complete — commit a90dea0. 7 UX accessors moved from engine/priority to events/priority_flow. Engine boundary clean.

title: Implement :apply-pt-modifier effect type and SBA for 0-toughness creatures
description: Build the new effect infrastructure needed for Crippling Fatigue and future PT-modifying cards.
design:
## Goal
Implement :apply-pt-modifier effect type that creates :pt-modifier grants via the existing grants system, and hook SBA (state-based action) checking into reduce-effects to destroy creatures with effective toughness <= 0 after each effect resolves.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing code:
   - engine/grants.cljs — grant structure: {:grant/id (random-uuid) :grant/type :pt-modifier :grant/source source-id :grant/expires {:expires/turn N :expires/phase :cleanup} :grant/data {:grant/power X :grant/toughness Y}}
   - engine/creatures.cljs:40-56 — effective-toughness reads base + counters + grants + static modifiers
   - engine/effects.cljs:150-175 — reduce-effects loop with [effect & remaining] destructuring and recur
   - engine/effects/tokens.cljs:13-44 — pattern for effect executor: defmethod execute-effect-impl :type [db player-id effect _object-id]
   - engine/effects/zones.cljs:99-106 — pattern for reading target: (:effect/target effect) (pre-resolved by stack.cljs:resolve-effect-target)
   - engine/zones.cljs:43-117 — zones/move-to-zone handles zone transitions, auto-retracts creature fields on leave

2. Write tests first (TDD):
   **PT Modifier Effect Tests (engine/effects/pt_modifier_test.cljs):**
   - test_apply_pt_modifier_creates_grant — set up creature on battlefield, execute :apply-pt-modifier effect, verify grant exists with correct :grant/type :pt-modifier
   - test_grant_has_correct_power_toughness_values — execute with {:effect/power -2 :effect/toughness -2}, verify grant data matches {:grant/power -2 :grant/toughness -2}
   - test_grant_has_until_eot_expiration — verify grant has :grant/expires with current turn and :cleanup phase
   - test_effective_toughness_reflects_modifier — 4/4 creature gets -2/-2, effective-toughness returns 2
