- ❌ NO counting opponent's graveyard for player's threshold
close_reason: Closed

title: Migrate 13 assoc :game/pending-selection sites to set-pending-selection + add lint to validate
description: Migrate all direct assoc :game/pending-selection call sites to use the set-pending-selection helper from events/selection/spec.cljs, then add lint-pending-selection to the Makefile validate target.
design:
## Goal
Replace all 13 direct (assoc ... :game/pending-selection ...) call sites with (sel-spec/set-pending-selection app-db selection). After migration, add lint-pending-selection to the Makefile validate target so future direct assocs are caught.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours (mechanical replacements + verify tests still pass)

## Implementation Checklist

### Step 1: Add require to each file
Each file needs: [fizzle.events.selection.spec :as sel-spec]

### Step 2: Replace call sites (13 total across 8 files)
Run make lint-pending-selection to find all sites, then replace each:

**casting.cljs** (2 sites):
- Line ~162: (assoc :game/pending-selection (:selection result)) → (sel-spec/set-pending-selection (:selection result))
- Line ~263: (assoc :game/pending-selection ...) → (sel-spec/set-pending-selection ...)

**cleanup.cljs** (1 site):
- Line ~108: (assoc :game/pending-selection (:pending-selection result)) → (sel-spec/set-pending-selection (:pending-selection result))

**director.cljs** (3 sites):
- Line ~188, ~256, ~274: same pattern

**selection/core.cljs** (2 sites):
- Line ~368: (assoc :game/pending-selection next-sel) → (sel-spec/set-pending-selection next-sel)
- Line ~403: (assoc :game/pending-selection chained-sel) → (sel-spec/set-pending-selection chained-sel)

**resolution.cljs** (2 sites):
- Line ~118, ~137: same pattern

**abilities.cljs** (1 site):
- Line ~299: same pattern

**phases.cljs** (1 site):
- Line ~141: same pattern

**selection.cljs** (1 site):
- Line ~23: same pattern

IMPORTANT: These are in threading macros (-> app-db ...). The set-pending-selection helper takes (app-db selection), so in a threading context it becomes (sel-spec/set-pending-selection selection) where app-db is threaded.

### Step 3: Verify lint passes
make lint-pending-selection should now PASS (0 direct assocs outside spec.cljs)

### Step 4: Add lint to validate target
Update Makefile validate target:
From: @$(MAKE) lint && $(MAKE) fmt-check && $(MAKE) test
To: @$(MAKE) lint && $(MAKE) fmt-check && $(MAKE) lint-pending-selection && $(MAKE) test

### Step 5: Run full validation
make validate must pass (lint + fmt-check + lint-pending-selection + test)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] 0 direct assoc :game/pending-selection calls outside spec.cljs (make lint-pending-selection passes)
- [ ] lint-pending-selection added to Makefile validate target
- [ ] make validate passes (all tests, lint, fmt-check, lint-pending-selection)
- [ ] All 13 sites use sel-spec/set-pending-selection
- [ ] No new require warnings from clj-kondo
- [ ] Test count unchanged (3184 tests, 0 failures)

## Anti-Patterns
- NO changing builder output shapes (if set-pending-selection logs a validation error in dev, fix the SPEC not the builder)
- NO modifying any logic — only replacing the assoc call with the helper
- NO adding set-pending-selection to assoc-in sites (those update existing validated selections)
- NO removing or modifying dissoc :game/pending-selection calls (those are cleanup, not creation)
close_reason: Committed as 48e17fd. All 13 sites migrated, lint-pending-selection passes and added to validate, 3184 tests pass.

title: Phase 5: Migrate Cephalid Coliseum threshold ability to cast-time targeting
description:
## Goal
Migrate Cephalid Coliseum threshold ability to use :ability/targeting for cast-time target declaration, matching the pattern established in Phase 4 (Deep Analysis).

## Context
Phases 1-4 complete:
- Grant system implemented (grants.cljs)
- Cleanup system implemented (expire-grants trigger)
- Targeting foundation implemented (targeting.cljs)
- Deep Analysis migrated to cast-time targeting

Cephalid Coliseum threshold ability currently uses :effect/target :any-player in its effects. Need to migrate to :ability/targeting.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Add :ability/targeting to threshold ability
Update cephalid_coliseum.cljs threshold ability to add targeting:
```clojure
{:ability/type :activated
 :ability/name "Threshold"
 :ability/targeting [{:target/id :player
                      :target/type :player
                      :target/options [:self :opponent :any-player]
                      :target/required true}]
 ...}
```

### Step 2: Extend ability activation flow for targeting
Modify ability activation to:
- Check if ability has :ability/targeting
- Prompt for target selection at activation time
- Store targets on ability stack item

### Step 3: Update ability resolution to use stored targets
- Read targets from stored ability state
- Pass to effect execution

### Step 4: Write tests
- Test ability targeting extraction
- Test activation with target selection
- Test resolution uses stored target

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Threshold ability has :ability/targeting attribute
- [ ] targeting/get-targeting-requirements works with abilities
- [ ] Activating threshold prompts for player target before stack
- [ ] Target stored and used at resolution
- [ ] Existing Cephalid Coliseum tests pass
- [ ] make test passes

## Anti-Patterns
- Do NOT break existing tests
- Do NOT duplicate targeting logic - reuse from targeting.cljs
design:
## Goal
Migrate Cephalid Coliseum threshold ability to use :ability/targeting for cast-time target declaration, matching the pattern established in Phase 4 (Deep Analysis).

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Context
Phases 1-4 complete:
- Grant system implemented (grants.cljs)
- Cleanup system implemented (expire-grants trigger)
- Targeting foundation implemented (targeting.cljs)
- Deep Analysis migrated to cast-time targeting

Cephalid Coliseum threshold ability currently uses :effect/target :any-player in its effects (lines 47-50 of cephalid_coliseum.cljs). Need to migrate to :ability/targeting for cast-time target declaration.

The targeting module already supports :ability/targeting extraction (targeting.cljs line 20-22).

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Add :ability/targeting to threshold ability (TDD)
**File:** src/main/fizzle/cards/cephalid_coliseum.cljs

Add targeting requirement to threshold ability (second ability, line 40):
\`\`\`clojure
{:ability/type :activated
 :ability/name "Threshold"
 :ability/targeting [{:target/id :player
                      :target/type :player
                      :target/options [:self :opponent :any-player]
                      :target/required true}]
 :ability/cost {:tap true
                :sacrifice-self true
                :mana {:blue 1}}
 :ability/condition {:condition/type :threshold}
 :ability/description "Target player draws 3 cards, then discards 3 cards"
 :ability/effects [{:effect/type :draw
                    :effect/amount 3
                    :effect/target :any-player}
                   {:effect/type :discard
                    :effect/count 3
                    :effect/selection :player}]}
\`\`\`

Keep :effect/target :any-player during migration for backwards compat.

**Test:** Write test verifying targeting/get-targeting-requirements returns player target for threshold ability.

### Step 2: Extend activate-ability for targeting prompts
**File:** src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

**Location:** activate-ability function (lines 672-726)

Modify flow to:
1. After checking can-activate?, check if ability has :ability/targeting via targeting/get-targeting-requirements
2. If targeting exists AND requires selection:
   - Build target selection state (similar to build-cast-time-target-selection but for abilities)
   - Return {:db db :pending-ability-target-selection selection-state}
3. When target selection confirmed (new function confirm-ability-target), continue with original flow:
   - Pay costs
   - Add ability trigger to stack WITH stored targets

**New helper function needed:**
\`\`\`clojure
(defn build-ability-target-selection
  "Build selection state for ability target selection."
  [db player-id object-id ability-index target-req]
  (let [valid-targets (targeting/find-valid-targets db player-id target-req)]
    {:selection/type :ability-targeting
     :selection/player-id player-id
     :selection/object-id object-id
     :selection/ability-index ability-index
     :selection/target-requirement target-req
     :selection/valid-targets valid-targets
     :selection/selected-target nil}))
\`\`\`

**Modified activate-ability return:**
- With targeting: {:db db :pending-ability-target-selection selection}
- Without targeting: {:db db-with-trigger :pending-ability-target-selection nil}

### Step 3: Add confirm-ability-target function
**File:** src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

New function to complete ability activation after target selection:
\`\`\`clojure
(defn confirm-ability-target
  "Complete ability activation after target selection.
   1. Pays costs
   2. Creates ability trigger with stored targets
   3. Adds trigger to stack"
  [db selection]
  (let [object-id (:selection/object-id selection)
        ability-index (:selection/ability-index selection)
        target-ref (get-in selection [:selection/target-requirement :target/id])
        target-id (:selection/selected-target selection)
        ;; Get ability and pay costs
        obj (queries/get-object db object-id)
        card (:object/card obj)
        ability (nth (:card/abilities card) ability-index)
        db-after-costs (abilities/pay-all-costs db object-id (:ability/cost ability))]
    (if db-after-costs
      (let [;; Create trigger with targets
            effects-list (:ability/effects ability [])
            ability-trigger (triggers/create-trigger
                              :activated-ability
                              object-id
                              (:selection/player-id selection)
                              {:effects effects-list
                               :description (:ability/description ability)
                               :targets {target-ref target-id}})
            db-with-trigger (triggers/add-trigger-to-stack db-after-costs ability-trigger)]
        {:db db-with-trigger
         :pending-ability-target-selection nil})
      {:db db :pending-ability-target-selection nil})))
\`\`\`

### Step 4: Update ability resolution to use stored targets
**File:** src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs or src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs

Modify resolve-trigger or resolve-activated-ability to:
1. Read targets from trigger data (:targets in trigger context)
2. When executing effects with :effect/target :any-player, use stored target instead of prompting

**Location to investigate:** resolve-activated-ability-with-selection function

### Step 5: Add re-frame event for confirming ability targets
**File:** src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

\`\`\`clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::confirm-ability-target
  (fn [db [_ target-id]]
    (let [selection (get-in db [:game/pending-selection])
          selection-with-target (assoc selection :selection/selected-target target-id)
          game-db (:game/db db)
          result (confirm-ability-target game-db selection-with-target)]
      (-> db
          (assoc :game/db (:db result))
          (dissoc :game/pending-selection)))))
\`\`\`

### Step 6: Write tests for ability targeting
**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs

New tests to add:
1. test_threshold_ability_has_targeting_requirement
2. test_threshold_activation_prompts_for_target
3. test_threshold_target_stored_on_resolution
4. test_threshold_activation_cancelled_on_empty_selection

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Threshold ability has :ability/targeting attribute with player target
- [ ] targeting/get-targeting-requirements returns 1 requirement for threshold ability
- [ ] Activating threshold ability returns :pending-ability-target-selection (not immediately on stack)
- [ ] confirm-ability-target completes activation with stored target
- [ ] :targets map stored in trigger context, used at resolution
- [ ] All 15 existing Cephalid Coliseum tests pass without modification
- [ ] 4 new tests pass confirming ability targeting
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + fmt + tests)

## Key Considerations

**Edge Case: No Valid Targets**
- If no players exist (edge case: game not fully initialized), find-valid-targets returns empty
- has-valid-targets? returns false
- can-activate? should be extended to check has-valid-targets? for abilities with targeting
- Test: Mock scenario where find-valid-targets returns empty

**Edge Case: Cancelled Target Selection**
- If player dismisses target selection UI (clicks away)
- Ability should NOT activate (costs not paid, nothing on stack)
- Current UI pattern: dissoc :game/pending-selection, return to normal game state
- Test: Verify no cost payment or stack addition if selection cancelled

**Edge Case: Ability Uses Target on Resolution**
- Current effects use :effect/target :any-player which prompts for selection
- With stored targets, resolution should use :targets from trigger context
- May need to update effect execution to check for stored targets first
- Keep :effect/target :any-player for backwards compat during migration

**Trigger Context Structure**
- Existing: {:effects [...] :description "..."}
- New: {:effects [...] :description "..." :targets {:player :player-1}}
- Resolution code must handle both old (no :targets) and new (with :targets) format

**Reference Implementation**
- Study cast-spell-with-targeting (game.cljs ~line 1464)
- Study confirm-cast-time-target (game.cljs ~line 1502)
- Study build-cast-time-target-selection (game.cljs ~line 1450)
- Follow same pattern for abilities

**Storm Copy Consideration (Future)**
- When abilities create copies on stack (not current), copies should inherit :targets
- Not blocking for this task, but design should allow it

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ Do NOT break existing Cephalid Coliseum tests (migration preserves behavior)
- ❌ Do NOT remove :effect/target :any-player yet (needed for backwards compat during resolution)
- ❌ Do NOT modify targeting.cljs (already supports :ability/targeting)
- ❌ Do NOT hardcode player IDs - use stored target from selection/trigger
- ❌ Do NOT duplicate targeting logic - reuse build-*-target-selection patterns
- ❌ Do NOT use unwrap/expect patterns - handle nil gracefully
- ❌ Do NOT leave TODO comments without issue numbers
- ❌ Do NOT skip testing cancelled selection edge case

## Specific Test Cases (must implement)

1. test_threshold_ability_has_targeting_requirement
   - Catches: Missing :ability/targeting attribute on ability definition
   - Verifies: targeting/get-targeting-requirements returns vector with 1 player target for threshold ability
   - Assert: (= 1 (count (targeting/get-targeting-requirements threshold-ability)))

2. test_threshold_activation_prompts_for_target
   - Catches: Target prompt not happening at activation time
   - Verifies: activate-ability returns :pending-ability-target-selection instead of immediately placing on stack
   - Assert: (some? (:pending-ability-target-selection result))
   - Assert: (empty? (q/get-stack-items (:db result))) ;; not on stack yet

3. test_threshold_target_stored_and_used
   - Catches: Target not stored or not used at resolution
   - Verifies: After confirm-ability-target, trigger has :targets in context
   - Assert: (= {:player target-id} (get-in trigger [:context :targets]))

4. test_threshold_activation_cancelled_preserves_state
   - Catches: Partial activation on cancellation
   - Verifies: If selection dismissed, costs not paid, stack empty
   - Assert: Land still on battlefield (not sacrificed)
   - Assert: Mana pool unchanged
   - Assert: Stack empty
close_reason: Implemented cast-time targeting for Cephalid Coliseum threshold ability. Added :ability/targeting, modified activate-ability to pause for target selection, added confirm-ability-target function, and wrote 4 new tests. All 524 tests pass.

title: Blue Combo Cards: Frantic Search, Cloud of Faeries, Turnabout
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Frantic Search: {2}{U} Instant. Draw 2, discard 2, untap up to 3 lands. Player selects which tapped lands to untap.
- Cloud of Faeries: {1}{U} 1/1 Creature — Faerie. Flying. ETB: untap up to 2 lands (player selects). Cycling {2} (activated from hand: pay {2}, discard self, draw a card).
- Turnabout: {2}{U}{U} Instant. 6 modes (tap/untap × artifact/creature/land). Targets a player. Taps all untapped or untaps all tapped of chosen type that player controls.
- All cards oracle-accurate per Scryfall
- All cards have dedicated test files meeting card testing requirements from CLAUDE.md
- Untap-lands selection uses zone-pick pattern with player choosing from tapped lands
- Cycling is a reusable keyword mechanic (not Cloud-specific hack)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Frantic Search: cast → draw 2 → discard 2 (selection) → untap up to 3 lands (selection) → all resolve correctly
- [ ] Cloud of Faeries: cast → enters battlefield → ETB untap up to 2 lands (selection) → works as creature with power/toughness
- [ ] Cloud of Faeries: cycling from hand works (pay {2}, discard self, draw 1)
- [ ] Turnabout: all 6 modes work (tap/untap × artifact/creature/land) with player targeting
- [ ] Untap selection shows only tapped lands, respects 'up to N' constraint
- [ ] Cycling mechanic is generic (not hardcoded to Cloud of Faeries)
- [ ] Each card has dedicated test file meeting CLAUDE.md minimums
- [ ] All tests passing (make test)
- [ ] All validation passing (make validate)
- [ ] Cards registered in registry.cljs

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoded cycling logic in Cloud of Faeries (reusability: cycling must work for any card with :card/cycling)
- ❌ NO reimplementing production handlers in tests (testing: use th/cast-and-resolve, th/resolve-top, th/confirm-selection)
- ❌ NO tautological assertions in tests (testing: assert exact values, not some?/string?)
- ❌ NO case statements on card names in engine code (architecture: cards are data, engine interprets data)
- ❌ NO mutable state outside Datascript db (architecture: all game state in immutable db)
- ❌ NO skipping player selection for untap (UX: player must choose which lands, not auto-untap)
- ❌ NO separate untap code paths for Frantic Search vs Cloud of Faeries (DRY: shared untap-lands selection)

## Approach
Build cards in order of increasing complexity: Frantic Search → Cloud of Faeries → Turnabout. Each card builds infrastructure reused by subsequent cards.

Frantic Search introduces the :untap effect type and untap-land selection (zone-pick pattern). Cloud of Faeries reuses the untap selection via ETB triggers and adds cycling as a new keyword mechanic. Turnabout adds mass tap/untap (no selection — affects all matching permanents) with 6 flat modes and player targeting.

Two distinct untap mechanisms:
1. Selective untap (Frantic Search, Cloud of Faeries): 'untap up to N lands' — zone-pick selection from tapped lands
2. Mass tap/untap (Turnabout): tap/untap ALL permanents of chosen type — no selection, iterates matching permanents

## Architecture
- New effect type: :untap in engine/effects/zones.cljs (selective) and :tap-all/:untap-all (mass, for Turnabout)
- New selection: untap-lands selection using zone-pick pattern (pick from tapped lands, up to N)
- New keyword: :card/cycling in card data, cycling ability registered as activated ability from hand zone
- Card files: cards/blue/frantic_search.cljs, cards/blue/cloud_of_faeries.cljs, cards/blue/turnabout.cljs
- Registry update: cards/registry.cljs
- Turnabout modes: 6 flat modes in :card/modes array, each with distinct effects

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The card pool needs more blue combo staples for High Tide practice. Frantic Search, Cloud of Faeries, and Turnabout are core to Premodern blue combo but require untap effects and cycling — mechanics not yet in the engine.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- Modal spells: Blue Elemental Blast uses :card/modes array pattern
- Creatures: Nimble Mongoose and Goblin Welder exist with :card/power/:card/toughness
- ETB triggers: Gemstone Mine uses :card/etb-effects array
- Draw+discard: Careful Study chains draw → discard selection
- Discard selection: zone-pick pattern in events/selection/zone_ops.cljs
- 33 effect types exist, :untap is missing
- Player targeting: player-target selection type exists

**External:**
- Scryfall oracle text confirmed for all three cards
- Turnabout: 'Choose artifact, creature, or land. Tap all untapped permanents of the chosen type target player controls, or untap all tapped permanents of that type that player controls.'
- Cloud of Faeries: 'Flying. When this creature enters, untap up to two lands. Cycling {2}'
- Frantic Search: 'Draw two cards, then discard two cards. Untap up to three lands.'

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Card-by-card, increasing complexity ✓
**What it is:** Implement Frantic Search first (builds untap infra), then Cloud of Faeries (reuses untap + adds cycling), then Turnabout (most complex).
**Chosen because:** Each card builds on previous infrastructure. Iterative learning — later tasks informed by earlier implementation. Follows YAGNI.

#### 2. Infrastructure-first ❌
**What it is:** Build all shared infrastructure (untap effect, cycling, mass tap/untap) first, then implement all three cards.
**Why we looked at this:** Front-loading infrastructure ensures clean boundaries.
**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Over-engineers infrastructure before knowing exact card needs. Risk of building wrong abstractions.
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We're building 10+ cards that all need these mechanics simultaneously.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Three card implementations with full oracle accuracy
- Untap effect type (selective and mass)
- Cycling keyword mechanic (generic/reusable)
- Untap-land selection UI

**Out of scope:**
- Flying keyword mechanics (no combat system)
- Cycling triggers (cards that trigger when cycled) — deferred
- Other cycling cards — just the mechanic infrastructure
- Creature combat

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Turnabout modes? | All 6 modes | Full modal: tap/untap × artifact/creature/land + player target |
| Implement cycling? | Yes | New keyword mechanic, reusable for future cycling cards |
| Untap UX? | Player chooses | Selection UI, zone-pick pattern for tapped lands |
| Card ordering? | Frantic → Cloud → Turnabout | Increasing complexity, infrastructure builds incrementally |

### Open Concerns Raised
- Turnabout mass tap/untap is a different mechanism than selective untap — need distinct effect types
- Cycling is activated from hand (unusual zone for abilities) — needs ability system support for non-battlefield activation
close_reason: All 3 cards implemented: Frantic Search, Cloud of Faeries, Turnabout. All children closed. Card files and test files verified.

title: Implement Cloud of Faeries with ETB untap trigger and generic cycling keyword
description:
Implement Cloud of Faeries (1U 1/1 Creature — Faerie. Flying. ETB: untap up to 2 lands. Cycling {2}).

## Implementation

### 1. ETB trigger mechanism for non-land permanents
move-resolved-spell (engine/resolution.cljs:104) moves creatures to :battlefield but does NOT fire :card/etb-effects (only play-land does this for lands).

Approach: Add ETB effect firing to move-resolved-spell when destination is :battlefield and card has :card/etb-effects. Use effects/reduce-effects (not execute-effect) to support interactive ETB effects (like untap-lands). This is the generic mechanism; Cloud of Faeries benefits automatically.

### 2. Untap ETB effect
ETB effects: [{:effect/type :untap-lands :effect/count 2}]
This reuses the untap infrastructure from Frantic Search — no new effect type needed.

### 3. Cycling keyword mechanic
Cycling is an activated ability from hand. The ability system currently only supports activation from :battlefield (events/abilities.cljs:226 checks :object/zone = :battlefield).

Approach: Add :card/cycling key to card data (e.g., {:card/cycling {:colorless 2}}). Register a cycling activation path that:
- Checks object is in :hand zone (not battlefield)
- Pays mana cost
- Moves card to graveyard (self-discard)
- Draws 1 card
This is DISTINCT from the regular ability system — cycling from hand bypasses the stack (per MTG rules 702.29b in Premodern), or can be simplified to instant-speed activation.

For Fizzle (practice tool): cycle as a re-frame event ::cycle-card that checks hand zone + mana + draws 1.

### 4. Card definition
{:card/id :cloud-of-faeries
 :card/name 'Cloud of Faeries'
 :card/cmc 2
 :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :blue 1}
 :card/colors #{:blue}
 :card/types #{:creature}
 :card/subtypes #{:faerie}
 :card/power 1
 :card/toughness 1
 :card/keywords #{:flying}
 :card/cycling {:colorless 2}
 :card/etb-effects [{:effect/type :untap-lands :effect/count 2}]
 :card/text 'Flying. When this creature enters, untap up to two lands. Cycling {2}'}

### 5. Tests (TDD)
- Card definition fields (all exact values)
- Cast → ETB untap selection → confirm → lands untapped
- Cannot cast guards
- Storm count
- Cycling: pay {2} from hand, discard self, draw 1
- Cycling: cannot cycle without mana
- Cycling: cannot cycle from non-hand zone
- ETB with 0 tapped lands: empty selection
- Cycling triggers draw correctly
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement Turnabout with tap-all/untap-all mass effects
description:
Implement Turnabout ({2}{U}{U} Instant, 6 modes: tap/untap × artifact/creature/land, targets a player).

## Implementation

### 1. Card definition
6 flat modes, player targeting, no spell effects:
{:card/id :turnabout
 :card/name 'Turnabout'
 :card/cmc 4
 :card/mana-cost {:colorless 2 :blue 2}
 :card/colors #{:blue}
 :card/types #{:instant}
 :card/text 'Choose artifact, creature, or land. Tap all untapped permanents of the chosen type target player controls, or untap all tapped permanents of that type that player controls.'
 :card/modes [
   {:mode/id :tap-artifacts ... :mode/targeting [...] :mode/effects [{:effect/type :tap-all :effect/permanent-type :artifact :effect/target :targeted-player}]}
   {:mode/id :untap-artifacts ...}
   {:mode/id :tap-creatures ...}
   {:mode/id :untap-creatures ...}
   {:mode/id :tap-lands ...}
   {:mode/id :untap-lands-all ...}]}

Note: :untap-lands-all mode id to avoid collision with :untap-lands selection type.

### 2. New effect types: :tap-all and :untap-all
File: engine/effects/zones.cljs
- :tap-all: iterate all untapped permanents of :effect/permanent-type controlled by target player, tap each
- :untap-all: iterate all tapped permanents of :effect/permanent-type controlled by target player, untap each
- Use events/lands/tap-permanent and events/lands/untap-permanent (pure functions)
- Add :tap-all and :untap-all to card_spec.cljs valid-effect-types

### 3. Player targeting via existing system
Turnabout uses the existing player-target selection (pre-cast targeting). Each mode has:
  :mode/targeting [{:target/id :player :target/type :player :target/required true :target/options [:self :opponent :any-player]}]
The target player is stored in :stack-item/targets, resolved at effect execution time via :effect/target :targeted-player.

### 4. Mode selection
Modal spell using existing :card/modes array pattern. The player selects one of 6 modes at cast time. Mode selection is already implemented in events/game.cljs (build-spell-mode-selection). No new mechanism needed.

### 5. Tests (TDD)
- Card definition: all fields including all 6 modes
- Each of the 6 modes: tap/untap artifacts, creatures, lands
- Player targeting: can target self or opponent
- Cannot-cast guards
- Storm count
- Edge: no untapped/tapped permanents of chosen type → no-op
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement generic accumulator pattern for :x-mana-cost and :storm-split
design:
Goal: Prove the accumulator interaction pattern by adding hierarchy entries and pattern tags.

Analysis: Accumulator builders CANNOT follow zone-picks derive->build-selection-for-effect pattern. Each accumulator enters through a different domain path (pre-cast pipeline for x-mana-cost and mana-allocation, storm resolution for storm-split). Executors are also genuinely different (store value vs create copies vs cast spell).

What we DO unify: (1) selection-hierarchy derive entries, (2) :selection/pattern :accumulator tag on all builders, (3) executor dispatch through hierarchy (custom executors take precedence).

Implementation:
1. Add derive :storm-split :accumulator, :x-mana-cost :accumulator, :mana-allocation :accumulator to selection-hierarchy
2. Tag all three builders with :selection/pattern :accumulator
3. Write hierarchy routing tests (isa? relationships, pattern tags, custom executor precedence)
4. Verify all existing tests pass unchanged (make validate)

Success Criteria:
- selection-hierarchy has derive entries for all three under :accumulator
- All three accumulator builders set :selection/pattern :accumulator
- New hierarchy routing tests verify isa? relationships
- Custom executors still dispatch correctly (hierarchy precedence)
- All existing tests pass unchanged (make validate)

Anti-Patterns:
- NO generic accumulator builder (each enters through different domain path)
- NO generic accumulator executor (genuinely different actions)
- NO breaking existing tests
close_reason: Accumulator hierarchy + pattern tags added, 8 new tests, all 2117 pass

title: Scripted Bot: Hate Piece Testing
description:
Scripted bots with deterministic hand/deck order. First target: bot that casts Seal of Cleansing on turn 2. Enables practicing against specific hate pieces.

## Vision
- Scripted bots have fixed hand and deck order (no randomness)
- Follow a script: specific actions on specific turns
- First scripted bot: cast Seal of Cleansing on turn 2
- Player activates opponent's permanents on their behalf (Seal's sac ability)
- Player practices sequencing around known threats

## Seal of Cleansing as First Target
- White enchantment (1W): Sacrifice ~ Destroy target artifact or enchantment
- Perfect first scripted bot target because:
  - It's a permanent (not instant-speed interaction)
  - Player activates it on opponent's behalf (matches interaction model)
  - Directly threatens combo pieces
  - No combat or creatures needed

## Interaction Model
- For permanent-based interaction: player activates opponent's abilities directly
- For spell-based interaction (future): player-directed — bot has cards, player decides when bot uses them
- UX TBD: could be interaction button or alert that opponent can respond

## Script Definition
- Should be data-driven (matching data-over-code principle)
- Fixed hand order, fixed deck order, action sequence by turn
- Example: Turn 1: Plains. Turn 2: Plains, cast Seal of Cleansing. Turn 3+: pass.

## Design Notes
- Scope is fuzzier than earlier epics — will be fully refined before development
- May need Seal of Cleansing card implementation
- Script format design is the key architectural decision
close_reason: Superseded by planned configurable heuristic bot approach

title: cleanup-selection-source silently succeeds on wrong cleanup path
description: cleanup-selection-source in events/selection/core.cljs has a silent no-op when the spell source isn't on the stack (line 274: returns game-db unchanged). This masked a real bug: ETB trigger stack-items were never removed because build-selection-from-result routed them to the spell cleanup path (which found the creature on battlefield, not stack, and silently did nothing). The trigger stayed on the stack forever, causing an infinite loop at runtime. Root cause: the API makes it impossible to distinguish 'nothing to clean up (correct)' from 'wrong cleanup path was chosen (bug)'. Fix should either assert/warn when the spell path is invoked but nothing is on the stack, or unify the two cleanup paths so the routing decision isn't needed.
close_reason: Fixed: cleanup-selection-source now distinguishes 3 cases: nil spell-id (no-op), spell already moved to exile/graveyard (no-op), spell on unexpected zone like battlefield (warns via console.warn + no-op). Added 3 regression tests.

title: Epic: Battlefield UX Redesign — 6-row layout, color coding, opponent zone
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Battlefield displays 3 horizontal rows per player: creatures, non-creature/non-lands, lands
- Row order is mirrored: opponent lands at top → opponent other → opponent creatures → phase bar → player creatures → player other → player lands at bottom
- Phase/step control bar sits between the two players' battlefields with life totals flanking it (opponent life left, player life right)
- Each card has a type-based border color: creatures green, artifacts gray/silver, enchantments purple, lands brown/tan
- Each card has an MTG color identity background tint: white=warm cream, blue=cool blue, black=dark purple, red=warm red, green=forest green, multicolor=gold, colorless=neutral gray
- Empty rows reserve minimum height (~24px) with faint type label so layout does not jump
- Opponent starts with hardcoded basic lands on the battlefield (visible in opponent zone)
- Tapped/untapped visual distinction preserved (rotation + dimming)
- Mana ability and activated ability buttons still visible and functional on player lands/permanents

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Player battlefield shows 3 distinct rows: creatures, non-creature/non-lands, lands
- [ ] Opponent battlefield shows 3 distinct rows in mirrored order
- [ ] Phase bar with life totals sits visually between the two battlefields
- [ ] Cards are color-coded by type (border) and MTG color identity (background tint)
- [ ] Empty rows show minimum-height placeholder with faint label
- [ ] Opponent starts game with basic lands visible on battlefield
- [ ] Existing mana abilities and activated abilities still work
- [ ] Tapped permanents remain visually distinct
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO grouping logic in view components (consistency: grouping belongs in subscriptions/engine, views render data)
- ❌ NO hardcoding player count assumptions beyond 2 players (future-proofing: keep player-id parameterized)
- ❌ NO breaking existing single-player goldfish flow (compatibility: opponent zone is additive, not replacement)
- ❌ NO inline CSS color values in view components (maintainability: use CSS custom properties or Tailwind classes for all colors)
- ❌ NO separate subscription per row (simplicity: one subscription per player returning grouped data, not 6 subscriptions)
- ❌ NO removing existing card interactivity (correctness: mana abilities, activated abilities, counters must still render and function)

## Approach
Subscription-driven layout. Upgrade the sorting module with a three-tier group-by-type function (creatures / non-creature-non-lands / lands). Rework the battlefield subscription to return three sorted groups per player. Create a new opponent battlefield subscription using the same grouping. Move life totals into the phase bar component. Restructure the battlefield view to render 6 rows in mirrored order with a central phase bar. Add CSS custom properties for type borders and color identity tints. Give opponent hardcoded basic lands during game initialization.

## Architecture
- engine/sorting.cljs — new group-by-type function returning {:creatures [] :other [] :lands []}
- subs/game.cljs — rework ::battlefield to return 3 groups; new ::opponent-battlefield subscription
- views/battlefield.cljs — restructured: opponent rows (top) + phase bar + player rows (bottom)
- views/opponent.cljs — life-view refactored, life totals move into phase bar
- events/game.cljs — opponent init adds basic lands to battlefield
- src/css/app.css — CSS custom properties for type borders and color identity tints

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The battlefield is visually flat — all permanents are identical dark green boxes in a flex-wrap layout with only a lands/non-lands split. This makes it hard to quickly scan game state during play. The opponent has no visible battlefield zone, limiting future hate pieces work.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- subs/game.cljs:176-183 — existing ::battlefield sub does two-tier grouping via sorting/group-by-land
- engine/sorting.cljs:13-20 — group-by-land checks :land in :card/types, returns {:lands :non-lands}
- db/queries.cljs:79-90 — get-objects-in-zone is player-agnostic, works for :opponent already
- events/game.cljs:135-136 — opponent entity created with :player/is-opponent true, land-plays 0
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:146-177 — Island and Swamp definitions exist with mana abilities
- views/battlefield.cljs:115-134 — current flex-wrap layout with permanent-view components

**External:**
- MTG Arena and MTGO use mirrored battlefield layouts with creatures facing center
- Type-based color coding is standard in digital MTG implementations

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Subscription-Driven Layout ✓
**What it is:** Upgrade sorting module with three-tier grouping. Subscriptions return grouped data. View renders rows from subscription output.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed existing group-by-land pattern — natural extension to three-tier
- Confirmed get-objects-in-zone works for :opponent without changes
- Subscription approach matches existing re-frame convention

**Pros:**
- Clean data flow, testable at subscription level
- View stays dumb — just maps over groups
- Consistent with existing pattern in subs/game.cljs

**Cons:**
- Slightly more subscription logic

**Chosen because:** Matches existing codebase convention, grouping logic is testable without rendering

#### 2. View-Side Grouping ❌
**What it is:** Keep current subscription returning raw objects. View handles grouping inline.

**Why we looked at this:** Fewer changes to subscription layer

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed re-frame docs on subscription vs view logic
- Checked existing codebase pattern — subscriptions do all data shaping

**Pros:**
- Simpler subscription changes

**Cons:**
- Logic in views, harder to test
- Violates re-frame convention used throughout codebase

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Breaks established pattern of data shaping in subscriptions, not views. Anti-pattern in re-frame.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** re-frame convention changes (it won't)

#### 3. Separate Subscriptions Per Row ❌
**What it is:** 6 subscriptions, one per battlefield row per player.

**Why we looked at this:** Fine-grained reactivity per row

**Investigation:**
- Counted: would need 6 subs (3 per player × 2 players)
- Checked subscription dependencies — all would derive from same base query

**Pros:**
- Each row re-renders independently

**Cons:**
- 6 subscriptions duplicating grouping logic
- Maintenance burden, harder to understand

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Subscription sprawl. Re-frame's efficient diffing makes per-row reactivity unnecessary.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Performance profiling shows battlefield as bottleneck (unlikely with <50 permanents)

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- 6-row mirrored battlefield layout
- Three-tier permanent grouping (creatures/other/lands)
- Type border colors and MTG color identity tinting
- Life totals in phase bar
- Opponent starting with hardcoded basic lands
- Empty row placeholders

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Setup screen config for opponent starting permanents (hate pieces epic)
- Card images from Scryfall (separate backlog item)
- Opponent AI or bot actions (future work)
- Additional basic land definitions beyond what exists (can add as needed)
- Keyboard shortcuts (separate backlog item fizzle-txiw)

### Open Questions
- Exact hex values for color identity tints — will tune during implementation
- Whether to add Mountain/Plains/Forest card definitions for opponent variety or just use existing Island/Swamp

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Primary pain point? | All of the above (lands hard to read, non-lands identical, layout flat) | Full visual rethink, not incremental |
| Card visual style? | Color-coded borders by type + MTG color identity tinting | Both type and color differentiation needed |
| Row order? | Creatures at center, lands at edges (mirrored) | Opponent: lands/other/creatures → phase → player: creatures/other/lands |
| Life totals? | Flanking the phase bar | Central, always-visible, part of phase bar component |
| Opponent battlefield? | Implement now with hardcoded lands | Need opponent init with battlefield objects |
| Opponent starting cards? | Hardcoded basics for now | Setup screen config deferred to hate pieces epic |
| Empty rows? | Reserve minimum height | Stable layout with thin placeholders, no jumping |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Opponent Entity Support
**Question explored:** Can the opponent own battlefield objects?
**Sources consulted:**
- db/schema.cljs — full player schema, :player/is-opponent flag
- db/queries.cljs — get-objects-in-zone is player-agnostic
- events/game.cljs — opponent init creates entity but no battlefield objects

**Findings:**
- Opponent entity fully supported in schema
- All zone queries work for :opponent parameter already
- Only initialization needs changing to put objects on battlefield

**Conclusion:** No engine changes needed for opponent ownership, just init code

#### Current Grouping System
**Question explored:** How does battlefield grouping work today?
**Sources consulted:**
- engine/sorting.cljs — group-by-land and sort-cards functions
- subs/game.cljs:176-183 — battlefield subscription

**Findings:**
- Two-tier: lands vs non-lands
- sort-cards sorts by CMC then name
- Natural extension to three-tier by adding :creature check

**Conclusion:** Extend group-by-land to group-by-type with three categories

### Dead-End Paths
None — requirements were clear from the start.

### Open Concerns Raised
- None raised during brainstorming.
close_reason: All 10 success criteria met. Reviewer APPROVED. 4 tasks completed, 38 new tests, 0 failures.

title: Task 2: Opponent starting with hardcoded basic lands on battlefield
description: During game initialization, give the opponent basic lands on the battlefield so the opponent zone has visible permanents from game start
design:
## Goal
During game initialization, give the opponent basic lands on the battlefield so the opponent zone has visible permanents from game start.

## Effort Estimate
3 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - events/game.cljs:135-136 — opponent entity creation
   - events/game.cljs opponent-library-tx — how opponent library is populated
   - cards/iggy_pop.cljs:146-177 — Island and Swamp card definitions
   - db/queries.cljs:79-90 — get-objects-in-zone (verify works for opponent)

2. Write tests first (TDD)
   - Test init-game-state creates 4 objects in opponent :battlefield zone
   - Test those objects are 2 Island and 2 Swamp (card names)
   - Test all 4 objects are untapped (:object/tapped false)
   - Test opponent library still populated correctly (opponent-library-tx unchanged)
   - Test ::opponent-battlefield subscription returns these lands in :lands group
   - Test player battlefield remains empty (isolation check)

3. Implementation checklist
   - [ ] events/game.cljs — add opponent-battlefield-tx function similar to opponent-library-tx
   - [ ] Create 4 objects: 2 Island, 2 Swamp
   - [ ] Set :object/zone :battlefield
   - [ ] Set :object/owner to opponent-eid
   - [ ] Set :object/controller to opponent-eid
   - [ ] Set :object/tapped false
   - [ ] Include opponent-battlefield-tx in init-game-state transaction
   - [ ] Verify opponent-library-tx remains unchanged

## Success Criteria
- [ ] After init-game-state, opponent has exactly 4 objects in :battlefield zone
- [ ] Those 4 objects are 2 Island and 2 Swamp (verified by card name)
- [ ] All 4 objects have :object/tapped false
- [ ] Opponent library count unchanged from before (still has its cards)
- [ ] ::opponent-battlefield subscription returns these lands in :lands group
- [ ] Player battlefield still empty after init (isolation)
- [ ] Existing goldfish flow works (no breaking changes)
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoding opponent entity ID — use opponent-eid from queries
- ❌ NO breaking existing goldfish flow — opponent lands are additive
- ❌ NO modifying opponent-library-tx — library population separate from battlefield
- ❌ NO adding setup screen config — that's deferred to hate pieces epic

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Card Entity References**
- Island and Swamp cards already exist in the db (created during init-game-state)
- Use queries to find card entities by :card/id
- Pattern: (d/q '[:find ?e . :where [?e :card/id :island]] db)
- Create objects referencing these card entities via :object/card

**Backward Compatibility: Goldfish Flow**
- Existing tests expect player battlefield to start empty
- Opponent battlefield starting populated should NOT affect player
- Integration tests may check zone counts — verify opponent zones in assertions

**Reference Implementation**
- opponent-library-tx at events/game.cljs shows pattern for creating opponent-owned objects
- Follow same pattern: get opponent-eid, create objects with that owner
- Use consistent attribute structure with library objects

**Testing Strategy**
- Unit test init-game-state directly (check opponent battlefield objects exist)
- Integration test via subscription (::opponent-battlefield returns lands)
- Isolation test (player battlefield unaffected)
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 3: 6-row mirrored battlefield layout with phase bar and life totals
description: Restructure battlefield view into mirrored 6-row layout with phase bar in center
design:
## Goal
Restructure the battlefield view into a mirrored 6-row layout: opponent battlefield (3 rows: lands/other/creatures) → phase bar with life totals → player battlefield (3 rows: creatures/other/lands). Empty rows show minimum-height placeholders with faint type labels.

## Effort Estimate
6 hours

## Layout Specification (Top to Bottom)
```
Opponent Lands          (top — farthest from center)
Opponent Other          (non-creature, non-land)
Opponent Creatures      (adjacent to phase bar)
─── Phase Bar ──── [Opp: 20]  Untap › Upkeep › Draw › Main › End  [You: 20] ───
Player Creatures        (adjacent to phase bar)
Player Other            (non-creature, non-land)
Player Lands            (bottom — farthest from center)
```

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - views/battlefield.cljs:115-134 — current battlefield-view (player only, 2 rows)
   - views/opponent.cljs — current life-view component
   - core.cljs — current layout grid structure (phase-bar + battlefield placement)
   - subs/game.cljs:176-193 — ::battlefield and ::opponent-battlefield subscriptions (already return 3 groups)

2. Write tests first (TDD)
   - This is primarily a view restructure — tests focus on data flow, not rendering
   - Test ::opponent-battlefield subscription returns lands in :lands group (already covered in task 1)
   - Test ::battlefield subscription returns creatures/other/lands groups (already covered in task 1)
   - Manual verification required for visual layout
   - Ensure make validate passes after restructure

3. Implementation checklist
   - [ ] views/battlefield.cljs — create row-view helper for rendering permanent rows
   - [ ] views/battlefield.cljs — create empty-row-placeholder helper (min-height, faint label)
   - [ ] views/battlefield.cljs — restructure battlefield-view to render both opponent and player
   - [ ] Opponent section: render lands row, other row, creatures row (top to bottom)
   - [ ] Player section: render creatures row, other row, lands row (top to bottom)
   - [ ] Move phase-bar component INTO battlefield-view (between opponent and player sections)
   - [ ] views/opponent.cljs — move life-total rendering into phase-bar (flanking style)
   - [ ] Phase bar layout: [Opp Life] ─ Phase Controls ─ [Player Life]
   - [ ] Empty rows: min-height 24px, faint label text, use Tailwind classes
   - [ ] core.cljs — adjust layout grid (battlefield now self-contained with phase bar)
   - [ ] Verify opponent permanents have NO mana/ability buttons
   - [ ] Verify player permanents retain mana/ability buttons

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Opponent battlefield renders 3 rows: lands (top), other, creatures (bottom)
- [ ] Player battlefield renders 3 rows: creatures (top), other, lands (bottom)
- [ ] Phase bar sits visually between the two battlefields
- [ ] Life totals flank the phase bar: opponent left, player right
- [ ] Empty rows show minimum-height placeholder with faint type label
- [ ] Player lands still have mana ability buttons visible and functional
- [ ] Opponent lands do NOT have clickable buttons (not player-controlled)
- [ ] Layout does not jump when rows populate/empty
- [ ] All existing tests pass (no breaking changes)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO grouping logic in view components (subscriptions already return grouped data)
- ❌ NO inline CSS color values (use Tailwind classes or CSS custom properties)
- ❌ NO removing player card interactivity (mana abilities, activated abilities must render)
- ❌ NO breaking existing goldfish flow (player side works as before)
- ❌ NO making opponent permanents clickable (opponent's cards aren't player-controlled)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Empty Row Rendering**
- Empty rows MUST reserve vertical space (min-height ~24px)
- Use faint label text: "Creatures", "Other", or "Lands"
- Prevents layout jumping when rows populate
- Pattern: `(if (seq objects) [row-view objects] [empty-row-placeholder "Creatures"])`

**Edge Case: Opponent Card Interactivity**
- Opponent permanents should render as permanent-view but WITHOUT buttons
- Check permanent-view component — does it conditionally render buttons based on controller?
- If not, create opponent-permanent-view variant or add :show-buttons? prop
- Reason: opponent's cards aren't player-controlled, clicking would be confusing

**Backward Compatibility: Player Battlefield**
- Player battlefield MUST retain all existing functionality
- Mana ability buttons on lands must still render
- Activated ability buttons on other permanents must still render
- Tapped/untapped visual distinction must be preserved
- Pattern: just reorder the rows, don't change permanent-view rendering for player

**Phase Bar Integration**
- Phase bar currently lives in core.cljs layout grid (separate from battlefield)
- Moving it INTO battlefield-view centralizes the layout
- Life totals currently in views/opponent.cljs life-view
- Refactor: move life-total rendering into phase bar flanks
- Layout: flexbox with space-between, life totals on edges, phase controls in center

**CSS Approach**
- Use Tailwind utility classes for layout (flex, gap, min-h-*, text-*)
- For faint label text: use existing text-border or text-label Tailwind classes
- If min-height needs exact px value, add utility class or inline style is acceptable for this
- Color coding (type borders, color identity tints) deferred to next task — don't implement yet

**Reference Implementation**
- Current battlefield-view at battlefield.cljs:115-134 shows pattern for rendering rows
- Use `(for [obj objects] ^{:key (:object/id obj)} [permanent-view obj])` for rendering
- opponent-battlefield subscription already working from task 1
- Just need to wire up the layout structure

**Testing Strategy**

title: Task 4: Type-based border colors and MTG color identity background tints
description: Add visual differentiation with type-based border colors and color identity background tints
design:
## Goal
Add visual differentiation to permanents with type-based border colors and MTG color identity background tints. This completes the final success criterion from the epic.

## Effort Estimate
4 hours

## Color Specifications

### Type Border Colors
- Creatures: green border
- Artifacts: gray/silver border
- Enchantments: purple border
- Lands: brown/tan border
- Multi-type: use classification priority (creature > land > other)

### MTG Color Identity Background Tints (subtle wash)
- White (W): warm cream tint
- Blue (U): cool blue tint
- Black (B): dark purple/gray tint
- Red (R): warm red tint
- Green (G): forest green tint
- Multicolor (2+ colors): gold tint
- Colorless (no colors or nil): neutral gray (current default)

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - views/battlefield.cljs:89-92 — current permanent-view border classes
   - src/css/app.css — existing color custom properties pattern
   - engine/sorting.cljs:classify-permanent-type — type classification logic
   - Card definitions — :card/types and :card/colors structure

2. Write tests first (TDD)
   - Test get-type-border-class: creature types → green, artifact → gray, enchantment → purple, land → brown
   - Test multi-type edge cases: artifact-creature → green (creature priority)
   - Test get-color-identity-bg-class: single color → corresponding tint
   - Test multicolor (2+ colors) → gold
   - Test colorless (nil or empty set) → neutral/default
   - Test tapped styling preservation (dimmed border colors)

3. Implementation checklist
   - [ ] src/css/app.css — Add CSS custom properties for type borders
   - [ ] src/css/app.css — Add CSS custom properties for color identity background tints
   - [ ] views/battlefield.cljs — Add get-type-border-class helper (uses classification priority)
   - [ ] views/battlefield.cljs — Add get-color-identity-bg-class helper (checks :card/colors)
   - [ ] views/battlefield.cljs — Update permanent-view to use dynamic border and background classes
   - [ ] Handle tapped state: use muted/grayed versions of type border colors
   - [ ] Verify all existing permanents get correct colors
   - [ ] battlefield_test.cljs — Add tests for helper functions

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Creatures have green borders
- [ ] Artifacts have gray/silver borders
- [ ] Enchantments have purple borders
- [ ] Lands have brown/tan borders
- [ ] Multi-type cards use classification priority for border (artifact-creature → green)
- [ ] Single-color cards have subtle color identity background tint
- [ ] Multicolor cards have gold tint background
- [ ] Colorless cards have neutral gray background (default)
- [ ] Tapped permanents show muted border colors (dimming preserved)
- [ ] Rotation and dimming for tapped cards still applies
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO inline CSS color values in views (use CSS custom properties or Tailwind classes)
- ❌ NO breaking tapped/untapped visual distinction (must preserve rotation + dimming)
- ❌ NO hardcoding card names for color lookups (use :card/types and :card/colors)
- ❌ NO breaking existing permanent rendering (buttons, counters must still work)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Type Classification Priority**
- Same priority as group-by-type: creature > land > other
- Artifact-creature → green border (creature wins)
- Artifact-land → brown border (land wins over artifact)
- Land-creature → green border (creature wins over land)
- Use same classify-permanent-type logic from sorting.cljs for consistency

**Edge Case: Color Identity Determination**
- :card/colors is a set like #{:black} or #{:blue :black} or nil
- Count colors: 0 → colorless, 1 → single color, 2+ → multicolor
- Multicolor gets gold regardless of which colors
- Lands typically have no :card/colors (nil) → colorless background (neutral)

**Edge Case: Tapped Styling Interaction**
- Current tapped style: "border-perm-border-tapped bg-perm-bg-tapped text-perm-text-tapped rotate-[6deg]"
- Need to preserve rotation and text dimming
- Type border color should be muted/grayed for tapped (e.g., green-tapped, brown-tapped)
- Background tint should also be dimmed for tapped

**CSS Approach: Custom Properties vs Tailwind**
- Check app.css for existing pattern (likely custom properties like --perm-border)
- Add new custom properties:
  - --type-border-creature, --type-border-creature-tapped
  - --type-border-artifact, --type-border-artifact-tapped
  - --type-border-enchantment, --type-border-enchantment-tapped
  - --type-border-land, --type-border-land-tapped
  - --color-identity-white, --color-identity-blue, etc.
- Or create Tailwind utility classes if that's the pattern
- Important: colors must work well on dark background (app uses dark theme)

**Hex Value Tuning**
- Epic says "exact hex values will be tuned during implementation"
- Pick reasonable starting values that are visible but not garish
- Creature green: something like #4ade80 (green-400) or darker for borders
- Artifact gray: #9ca3af (gray-400)
- Enchantment purple: #a78bfa (purple-400)
- Land brown: #a16207 (yellow-700 for brown/tan tone)
- Color identity tints: 10-20% opacity over background for subtle wash
- User will iterate later if colors need adjustment

**Reference Implementation**
- Current permanent border: "border-perm-border" (line 89 battlefield.cljs)
- Current background: "bg-perm-bg" (line 89 battlefield.cljs)
- These are CSS custom properties — follow same pattern
- Tapped variants: "border-perm-border-tapped bg-perm-bg-tapped"

**Testing Strategy**
- Unit test get-type-border-class with different :card/types sets
- Unit test get-color-identity-bg-class with different :card/colors sets
- Integration: start game, verify opponent lands have brown border + neutral bg
- Integration: play a creature, verify green border + color identity tint

**Deferred Considerations**
- Card images (separate backlog item) — not in this task
- Setup screen for opponent starting cards (hate pieces epic) — not here
- Keyboard shortcuts (separate epic) — not here

title: Rewrite ::yield as event-driven priority passing
description:
## Goal
Replace the synchronous yield-impl orchestrator with event-driven flow. ::yield passes priority for current player, checks if all passed, and dispatches the appropriate next event (::resolve-top or ::advance-phase). No more yield-impl calling negotiate-priority → derive-loop-state → handle-loop-state.

## What changes
1. ::yield event handler: pass priority → if all passed, dispatch resolution or phase advancement → if not all passed, transfer priority (opponent gets to act)
2. yield-impl function: deleted, logic distributed into ::yield event handler
3. negotiate-priority: pure function stays but called from ::yield, not from yield-impl
4. derive-loop-state: concept dissolves — ::yield checks stack empty vs not to decide resolve vs advance
5. handle-loop-state multimethod: deleted — each state becomes its own event path

## New event flow
- ::yield → pass priority → all passed? 
  - Stack not empty → dispatch ::resolve-top (existing event, may need adjustment)
  - Stack empty → dispatch ::advance-phase (existing event, may need adjustment) 
  - Not all passed → priority transferred, wait for opponent action
- After ::resolve-top completes → if stack has more items, dispatch ::yield again
- After ::advance-phase completes → stop at phase or dispatch ::yield again

## Key constraint
- Priority passing must be symmetric: same logic for human and bot
- Active player / non-active player ordering per MTG rules
- Auto-mode (resolving, f6) behavior preserved

## Success criteria
- make validate passes
- Human gameplay works: cast spell, yield resolves it, advance phases
- Priority passes correctly between players
- No yield-impl function
- No derive-loop-state / handle-loop-state calls
close_reason: Closed
