title: Fix Gemstone Mine sacrifice to be part of mana ability
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Gemstone Mine sacrifice must happen as part of the mana ability resolution, not as a state-based effect
- Effect system must support conditional effects via :effect/condition field
- :no-counters condition type must check if specified counter type is zero on target
- Zero-counters SBA must be removed entirely
- Sacrifice effect type must exist to move permanents to graveyard

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Gemstone Mine sacrifices itself when mana ability resolves AND no mining counters remain
- [ ] Gemstone Mine does NOT sacrifice if counters removed by other means (no SBA)
- [ ] Effect conditions are checked before effect execution
- [ ] :sacrifice effect moves target to graveyard
- [ ] All existing tests pass (updated as needed)
- [ ] New tests verify conditional effect behavior
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO state-based effect for counter depletion (rules error: sacrifice is part of ability, not SBA)
- ❌ NO custom effect types per card (maintainability: use generic conditional system)
- ❌ NO hardcoding Gemstone Mine logic in effect executor (extensibility: condition system must be generic)

## Approach
Add effect-level condition support to the effect executor. Effects with :effect/condition are only executed if the condition evaluates to true. Implement :no-counters condition type that checks if a counter type is zero. Update Gemstone Mine card definition to include conditional sacrifice as part of mana ability effects. Remove the zero-counters SBA entirely.

## Architecture
- engine/effects.cljs - Add check-condition multimethod, update execute-effect to check conditions
- engine/conditions.cljs (new) - Condition evaluation logic, or inline in effects.cljs
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Update Gemstone Mine with conditional sacrifice effect
- engine/state_based.cljs - Remove zero-counters SBA code and registration

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Gemstone Mine's sacrifice is implemented as a state-based effect, but the actual card text says 'If there are no mining counters on Gemstone Mine, sacrifice it' as part of the mana ability. This means the sacrifice should only happen when activating the mana ability, not when counters are removed by other means.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:79-100 - Current Gemstone Mine definition uses ETB for counters, mana ability for tap+remove
- engine/state_based.cljs:60-94 - Zero-counters SBA incorrectly sacrifices when counters hit 0
- engine/effects.cljs - Effect executor has no condition checking currently
- engine/costs.cljs:73-103 - Counter removal happens during cost payment

**External:**
- Scryfall rules note: 'the land won't be sacrificed if the last counter is removed through means other than activating the mana ability'

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Effect-level conditions ✓
**What it is:** Add :effect/condition field to any effect. Effect executor checks condition before running effect. Condition system is a multimethod dispatching on :condition/type.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed effects.cljs - clean place to add condition check
- Existing condition system for threshold is card-level, not effect-level
- Multimethod pattern matches existing code style

**Pros:**
- Generic, reusable for future cards
- Data-driven, keeps card definitions declarative
- Clean separation of concerns

**Cons:**
- Slightly more complex than custom effect type

**Chosen because:** Extensibility - avoids proliferation of custom effect types for each conditional card

#### 2. Custom :sacrifice-if-no-counters effect ❌
**What it is:** Create a specific effect type that combines sacrifice with counter check.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler immediate implementation

**Pros:**
- Quick to implement
- No new systems needed

**Cons:**
- Would need new effect type for each conditional pattern
- Not reusable

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** User explicitly said we need general conditional support to avoid many custom effects

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We decide effect conditions add too much complexity (unlikely)

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Effect-level condition system
- :no-counters condition type
- Gemstone Mine fix
- Remove zero-counters SBA

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Other condition types (:has-counters, :counter-equals, etc.) - add when needed
- Evaluating broader SBA system - separate future effort
- Other counter-based cards - handle if they come up

### Open Questions
- Should conditions.cljs be separate file or inline in effects.cljs? (decide during implementation, lean toward inline for now)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Fix scope? | Fix Gemstone Mine + remove zero-counters SBA | SBA system stays but zero-counters removed |
| Other cards to audit? | Only Gemstone Mine for now | No broader card audit needed |
| Condition system design? | Effect-level :effect/condition | Each effect can have optional condition |
| Condition types to support? | :no-counters only | Minimal implementation, extend later |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Gemstone Mine Rules
**Question explored:** How does the sacrifice actually work per MTG rules?
**Sources consulted:**
- Scryfall card page and rules notes
- Current implementation in state_based.cljs

**Findings:**
- Sacrifice is part of mana ability text, not a separate triggered ability
- Rules note explicitly states: won't sacrifice if counters removed by other means
- Current SBA implementation is a rules error

**Conclusion:** Must move sacrifice into ability effects with condition check

### Dead-End Paths

#### State-Based Effect Approach
**Why explored:** Was the original implementation approach
**Investigation:**
- Implemented as check-zero-counters SBA
- Fires after any action when counters <= 0

**Why abandoned:** Rules error - sacrifice should only happen as part of mana ability activation

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Is the SBA system correct in light of this?' → Deferred to future evaluation, not blocking this fix
close_reason: Closed

title: Extract opponent zone component + establish extraction pattern
design:
## Goal
Extract the opponent info view from core.cljs into views/opponent.cljs as the first zone component. Migrate its inline styles to Tailwind utility classes. This establishes the extraction + Tailwind migration pattern for all subsequent zones.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-00y4: Tailwind v4 pipeline is working. @theme tokens defined. This is the first component extraction — kept small to establish patterns before tackling larger zones like hand or battlefield.

## Implementation

### 1. Add missing @theme tokens
Edit src/css/app.css to add:
- --color-health-good: #5CB85C (green, player healthy / opponent dead)
- --color-health-danger: #F0AD4E (orange, low life warning)
- --color-health-critical: #D9534F (red, player dead / opponent healthy)
- --color-text-dim: #888 (dim labels like 'You:' / 'Opponent:')
- --color-text-label: #999 (section headers like 'LIFE TOTALS')

These are semantic health-state colors, distinct from mana colors even when hex values overlap. Mana colors are for mana display, health colors are for life totals.

### 2. Create views/common.cljs
New file: src/main/fizzle/views/common.cljs
Namespace: fizzle.views.common

Collapsible zone wrapper component:
- Takes: title (string), collapsed? (reagent atom), child content
- Renders: header bar with title + chevron/toggle, conditionally renders children
- Uses Tailwind classes for styling
- Pattern for all zone components to use

### 3. Create views/opponent.cljs
New file: src/main/fizzle/views/opponent.cljs
Namespace: fizzle.views.opponent

Move life-view from core.cljs (lines 219-243). Current code:
```clojure
(defn- life-view
  []
  (let [player-life @(rf/subscribe [::subs/player-life])
        opponent-life @(rf/subscribe [::subs/opponent-life])]
    [:div {:style {:margin-bottom "16px"}}
     [:div {:style {:color "#999" :margin-bottom "6px" :font-size "12px"}} "LIFE TOTALS"]
     [:div {:style {:display "flex" :gap "30px"}}
      [:div
       [:span {:style {:color "#888" :font-size "13px"}} "You: "]
       [:span {:style {:font-size "20px" :font-weight "bold"
                       :color (cond (<= player-life 0) "#D9534F"
                                    (<= player-life 5) "#F0AD4E"
                                    :else "#5CB85C")}}
        player-life]]
      [:div
       [:span {:style {:color "#888" :font-size "13px"}} "Opponent: "]
       [:span {:style {:font-size "20px" :font-weight "bold"
                       :color (cond (<= opponent-life 0) "#5CB85C"
                                    (<= opponent-life 5) "#F0AD4E"
                                    :else "#D9534F")}}
        opponent-life]]]]))
```

Convert to Tailwind:
- {:margin-bottom "16px"} → mb-4
- {:color "#999" :margin-bottom "6px" :font-size "12px"} → text-text-label mb-1.5 text-xs
- {:display "flex" :gap "30px"} → flex gap-8
- {:color "#888" :font-size "13px"} → text-text-dim text-sm
- {:font-size "20px" :font-weight "bold"} → text-xl font-bold
- Conditional health colors: use ClojureScript cond to select class string
  - (cond (<= life 0) "text-health-critical" (<= life 5) "text-health-danger" :else "text-health-good")

Subscriptions needed:
- fizzle.subs.game/player-life
- fizzle.subs.game/opponent-life

No events dispatched (read-only component).

### 4. Update core.cljs
- Add require: [fizzle.views.opponent :as opponent]
- Replace inline life-view call with [opponent/life-view]
- Remove the defn- life-view from core.cljs
- Note: function was defn- (private) — becomes public in new namespace, which is correct since core.cljs calls it

### 5. Verify
- make validate passes
- No changes to event/subscription contracts

## Success Criteria
- [ ] src/css/app.css has 5 new @theme tokens (health-good, health-danger, health-critical, text-dim, text-label)
- [ ] views/common.cljs exists with collapsible zone wrapper component
- [ ] views/opponent.cljs exists with life-view component
- [ ] Zero inline :style maps in opponent.cljs (all Tailwind classes)
- [ ] No hardcoded hex colors in opponent.cljs (uses @theme tokens)
- [ ] Conditional life colors use text-health-good/danger/critical classes
- [ ] core.cljs requires and uses opponent/life-view
- [ ] life-view defn removed from core.cljs
- [ ] Component is layout-agnostic (no grid/position styling)
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] No changes to event/subscription contracts

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO inline :style maps (use Tailwind utility classes)
- ❌ NO hardcoded hex colors (use @theme tokens)
- ❌ NO layout positioning in component (grid parent handles placement)
- ❌ NO game logic in view (subscribe and dispatch only)
- ❌ NO breaking existing subscriptions/events
- ❌ NO using mana color tokens for health states (semantic mismatch: use health-good/danger/critical)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Conditional Class Selection in Reagent/Hiccup:**
Tailwind classes are strings. Conditional styling in Reagent uses ClojureScript expressions to select class strings:
```clojure
[:span {:class (str "text-xl font-bold "
                    (cond (<= life 0) "text-health-critical"
                          (<= life 5) "text-health-danger"
                          :else "text-health-good"))}
 life]
```
This works because Tailwind scans compiled JS for class name strings.

**Private to Public:**
life-view is defn- (private) in core.cljs. When moved to opponent.cljs it becomes defn (public), which is correct since core.cljs imports it.

**@theme Token Naming:**
Health colors are semantically distinct from mana colors even when hex values overlap (e.g., mana-red and health-critical are both #D9534F). Separate tokens allow independent theming.

**Collapsible Zone Pattern:**
The common.cljs zone wrapper establishes the collapsible pattern. Uses a reagent atom for collapsed? state. Other zones will wrap their content in this component.
close_reason: All success criteria met. opponent.cljs extracted with Tailwind classes, common.cljs zone wrapper created, 5 @theme tokens added, 10 new tests, make validate passes (973 tests).

title: Remove core.cljs side-effect require + clean up dead app-db keys
description: Remove [fizzle.bots.interceptor] side-effect require from core.cljs. Remove dissoc of :bot/action-pending? and :bot/action-count from director.cljs entry-point cleanup (line 308). Update director_test.cljs tests that validate these keys are cleared (lines 219-220, 227-228). Run make validate.
design:
## Goal

Remove all dead references to the old event-dispatch bot coordination keys (:bot/action-pending?, :bot/action-count) and the side-effect require of bots.interceptor in core.cljs.

## Effort Estimate

1-2 hours. Pure deletion — no new code written.

## Files to Change

**src/main/fizzle/core.cljs** (line 3):
- Remove `[fizzle.bots.interceptor]` from the ns :require block
- This was a side-effect-only require to register ::bot-decide and ::bot-action-complete handlers — both are now deleted
- The require alias is bare (no :as), so removing the line is sufficient

**src/main/fizzle/events/director.cljs** (line 308):
- Remove :bot/action-pending? and :bot/action-count from the dissoc call
- Before: `(dissoc app-db :yield/epoch :yield/step-count :bot/action-pending? :bot/action-count)`
- After:  `(dissoc app-db :yield/epoch :yield/step-count)`
- Also update the docstring at line 18 that lists these keys as cleared: remove :bot/action-pending? and :bot/action-count from that line

**src/main/fizzle/events/init.cljs** (line 151):
- Remove :bot/action-pending? and :bot/action-count from the dissoc call
- Before: `(dissoc :bot/action-pending? :bot/action-count)`
- After: remove the entire `(dissoc ...)` threading step (both keys only, so the step is empty after removal)
- Verify the threading chain still compiles — if dissoc is the only step in that threading position, remove the whole step

**src/test/fizzle/events/director_test.cljs** (lines 211-228):
- Test `director-clears-coordination-state` still asserts :bot/action-pending? is cleared (line 227-228)
- Remove the :bot/action-pending? and :bot/action-count seeding from the app-db setup (lines 219-220)
- Remove the :bot/action-pending? assertion (line 227-228)
- Keep the :yield/epoch and :yield/step-count seeding/assertions — those keys ARE still cleared by director

**src/test/fizzle/bots/interceptor_test.cljs** (lines 330-342):
- Test `bot-action-count-reset-at-turn-boundary` seeds :bot/action-count 30 and asserts director clears it to nil
- Once director no longer dissocs :bot/action-count, this assertion will fail (30 persists)
- Remove the :bot/action-count 30 seeding and the `(is (nil? (:bot/action-count result-app-db)))` assertion
- If the test has no remaining assertions after removal, remove the entire test
- The test was testing director behavior, not interceptor behavior — it may be covered by updated director_test

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `grep -n "bots.interceptor" src/main/fizzle/core.cljs` returns no results
- [ ] `grep -rn "action-pending\|action-count" src/main/fizzle/` returns no results
- [ ] `make validate` passes: 0 failures, 0 errors, lint clean, fmt clean
- [ ] Test count does not drop by more than the number of assertions removed (sanity check)

## Implementation Checklist

- [ ] Read core.cljs — confirm line 3 is the only interceptor require
- [ ] Remove `[fizzle.bots.interceptor]` from core.cljs :require
- [ ] Read director.cljs lines 15-25 (docstring) and line 305-310 (dissoc)
- [ ] Remove :bot/action-pending? :bot/action-count from dissoc at line 308
- [ ] Remove those key names from the docstring at line 18
- [ ] Read init.cljs lines 148-155
- [ ] Remove the (dissoc :bot/action-pending? :bot/action-count) step from threading chain
- [ ] Read director_test.cljs lines 210-228
- [ ] Remove :bot/action-pending? true and :bot/action-count 3 from app-db setup
- [ ] Remove the :bot/action-pending? nil assertion
- [ ] Read interceptor_test.cljs bot-action-count-reset-at-turn-boundary test
- [ ] Decide: remove the test entirely OR just remove the :bot/action-count seeding + assertion
- [ ] Run `grep -rn "action-pending\|action-count" src/main/` to confirm no references remain
- [ ] Run make fmt to fix any formatting
- [ ] Run make validate — confirm 0 failures, lint clean

## Key Considerations

**init.cljs threading chain**:
The dissoc in init.cljs is inside a `->` or `->>` threading macro. If :bot/action-pending? and :bot/action-count are the ONLY keys being dissoc'd in that step, the entire `(dissoc :bot/action-pending? :bot/action-count)` call becomes a no-op and should be removed entirely (not left as `(dissoc)`). Verify by reading surrounding context.

**interceptor_test bot-action-count test**:
The test `bot-action-count-reset-at-turn-boundary` was intentionally KEPT during fizzle-dt46 because it tested `director/run-to-decision` (not the deleted handlers). However, it seeds `:bot/action-count 30` and asserts `nil` after director runs — which relies on director's dissoc. After this task removes the dissoc, the test assertion will fail. The test must be updated: either remove just the action-count-specific lines, or remove the whole test if nothing meaningful remains. The yield/epoch clearing is covered by director_test.cljs which is a better home for that test.

**No new tests needed**:
These are pure deletions. The question is whether existing tests need to be removed or updated, not whether new ones need to be written.

## Anti-patterns

- ❌ Do not leave dead `dissoc` calls with no keys (e.g., `(dissoc)` with empty args)
- ❌ Do not grep only test files — check production files too (init.cljs was missed in original description)
- ❌ Do not leave the interceptor require even if it compiles fine (stale requires mislead future readers)
close_reason: Removed core.cljs side-effect require, dead app-db key dissoc from director.cljs and init.cljs, dead test assertions. 3134 tests pass.

title: Strengthen ~15 weak contains? assertions in card tests
description: Replace (is (contains? types :instant)) with (is (= #{:instant} types)) across card definition tests. Affected files: careful_study_test, opt_test, mental_note_test, polluted_delta_test, cephalid_coliseum_test, cabal_ritual_test, dark_ritual_test, recoup_test, ray_of_revelation_test, seal_of_cleansing_test, merchant_scroll_test. These allow extra spurious types to go undetected.
design:
## Goal
Replace weak contains? assertions with exact = assertions in card type/color tests.

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours

## Pattern to Fix
Current: (is (contains? (:card/types card) :instant))
Replace: (is (= #{:instant} (:card/types card)))

Current: (is (contains? (:card/colors card) :blue))
Replace: (is (= #{:blue} (:card/colors card)))

## Files and Tests Affected
1. careful_study_test.cljs — types and colors checks
2. opt_test.cljs — types and colors checks
3. mental_note_test.cljs — types check (line ~125)
4. polluted_delta_test.cljs — types check (line ~134)
5. cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs — types check (line ~184), keywords check (line ~196)
6. cabal_ritual_test.cljs — types and colors checks (line ~77)
7. dark_ritual_test.cljs — types and colors checks (line ~65)
8. recoup_test.cljs — types check (line ~132)
9. ray_of_revelation_test.cljs — types and colors checks (line ~114)
10. seal_of_cleansing_test.cljs — types and colors checks (line ~108)
11. merchant_scroll_test.cljs — types check (line ~377)

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] For each file, grep for contains? in type/color assertions
- [ ] Look up the card definition to get the exact types/colors set
- [ ] Replace contains? with = using the exact set
- [ ] Run make validate after all changes

## Success Criteria
- [ ] No contains? assertions remain for :card/types or :card/colors in listed files
- [ ] All replacements use exact set equality (= #{:instant} ...)
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] Bug caught: extra spurious types/colors on a card would now be detected

## Anti-patterns
- Do NOT replace contains? checks on OTHER fields (e.g., :card/keywords may legitimately use contains? if card has multiple keywords)
- Do NOT change assertions for multi-type cards (e.g., artifact creature) — those should use the full set like #{:artifact :creature}
close_reason: Replaced all 24 weak contains? assertions with exact = set equality assertions across 15 card test files. All 818 tests pass.

title: Unify stack resolution: single multimethod dispatch with target threading
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Single multimethod in engine layer dispatches stack-item resolution by :stack-item/type
- All stack-item types (:spell, :storm-copy, :activated-ability, :storm) resolved through one path
- :stack-item/targets is the single source of truth for all target storage
- Target references pre-resolved before effect execution — effects never read :object/targets
- resolve-one-item becomes thin wrapper: call multimethod, handle selection or stack-item removal
- resolve-top-of-stack deleted, tests migrated to resolve-one-item
- resolve-spell-with-selection and resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection replaced by defmethods
- rules/resolve-spell removed or reduced to zone-transition helper

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Single multimethod resolve-stack-item in engine/resolution.cljs dispatching on :stack-item/type
- [ ] All existing stack-item types have defmethods (:spell, :storm-copy, :activated-ability, :storm, :default)
- [ ] No code reads :object/targets for target resolution (grep returns zero hits in resolution path)
- [ ] resolve-top-of-stack deleted from events/game.cljs
- [ ] resolve-spell-with-selection deleted from events/selection/resolution.cljs
- [ ] resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection deleted from events/selection/resolution.cljs
- [ ] resolve-one-item is <20 lines with no cond dispatch on stack-item properties
- [ ] All tests passing (make validate clean)
- [ ] No new test helpers that duplicate production logic
- [ ] Storm copies still inherit targets from original stack-item
- [ ] Cephalid Coliseum stored-player-target path still works
- [ ] Interactive effects (tutor, scry, discard selection) still pause for player choice

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO reading :object/targets in resolution path (target unification: stack-item is single source)
- ❌ NO bridge code copying targets from stack-item to object (elimination: that's the core smell)
- ❌ NO cond dispatch on stack-item properties in resolve-one-item (unification: multimethod handles dispatch)
- ❌ NO duplicating effect execution logic across defmethods (DRY: shared helpers for common patterns)
- ❌ NO test helpers that wrap resolve-top-of-stack or reimplement resolution (parity: tests use production path)
- ❌ NO changes to card definitions or effect data structures (scope: this is resolution plumbing only)
- ❌ NO new dependencies from engine/ to events/ (architecture: engine is lower layer)

## Approach
Create a multimethod resolve-stack-item in engine/resolution.cljs dispatching on :stack-item/type. Each defmethod handles its type: gets effects, reads targets from :stack-item/targets, pre-resolves target references via stack/resolve-effect-target, executes via effects/reduce-effects, and handles zone transition for spells. Returns {:db} or {:db :needs-selection effect}.

resolve-one-item in events/game.cljs becomes a thin wrapper: calls the multimethod, builds pending-selection if needed (via core/build-selection-for-effect), removes stack-item on completion.

Target unification: pre-resolve all target references before effect execution so effects receive concrete IDs. Update 3 locations in effects.cljs that read :object/targets. Remove the bridge code in resolution.cljs that copies targets to the object.

## Architecture
- engine/resolution.cljs (NEW) — multimethod + defmethods + zone-transition helper
- engine/effects.cljs — 3 target reads updated to use pre-resolved effect fields
- engine/stack.cljs — resolve-effect-target unchanged (already correct)
- events/game.cljs — delete resolve-top-of-stack, delete resolve-stack-item-effects, slim resolve-one-item
- events/selection/resolution.cljs — delete resolve-spell-with-selection, delete resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection
- engine/rules.cljs — resolve-spell removed or reduced to zone helper

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Two parallel resolution functions (resolve-top-of-stack and resolve-one-item) with overlapping cond dispatch on stack-item properties. Adding a new stack-item type requires updating both. Target storage split between :object/targets and :stack-item/targets with bridge code copying between them.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- events/game.cljs:317-340 — resolve-top-of-stack: old path, no selection awareness, used only in tests
- events/game.cljs:343-401 — resolve-one-item: production path, selection-aware, 4-branch cond
- events/selection/resolution.cljs:74-157 — resolve-spell-with-selection: 3 sub-branches for targeting
- events/selection/resolution.cljs:164-230 — resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection: parallel to spell path
- engine/effects.cljs:477,583,627 — 3 locations reading :object/targets
- events/selection/targeting.cljs:150 — spell cast writes to :stack-item/targets (canonical)
- engine/triggers.cljs:79,88 — storm copies inherit from :stack-item/targets
- engine/stack.cljs:97-124 — resolve-effect-target already works with stored-targets map

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Multimethod in engine + target threading ✓

**What it is:** New multimethod in engine/resolution.cljs dispatching on :stack-item/type. Pre-resolve targets from stack-item before execution. Events wrapper becomes thin.

**Investigation:**
- Existing execute-confirmed-selection multimethod proves pattern works in codebase
- reduce-effects already returns {:db :needs-selection} tagged results
- resolve-effect-target already pre-resolves targets for abilities
- Storm copies already use stack-item targets exclusively

**Pros:**
- Extensible: new stack-item type = new defmethod
- Matches existing multimethod pattern (execute-confirmed-selection)
- Clean layer separation (engine resolves, events wraps)
- Target pre-resolution eliminates bridge code

**Cons:**
- New file (engine/resolution.cljs)
- effects.cljs signature changes for 3 target reads

**Chosen because:** Extensible, matches codebase patterns, clean separation, eliminates all duplication

#### 2. Helper extraction without multimethod ❌

**What it is:** Keep resolve-one-item as single entry point. Extract each cond branch into named helpers. Unify target reading. No multimethod.

**Why we looked at this:** Smallest diff, no new abstractions

**Investigation:**
- Counted 4 cond branches in resolve-one-item + 3 sub-branches in resolve-spell-with-selection
- Each branch has different target handling — helpers would still need type-specific logic

**Pros:**
- Smallest change
- No new files

**Cons:**
- Not extensible — adding stack-item type means editing a growing cond
- Dispatch logic stays in events layer

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Doesn't solve the extensibility problem. Still requires editing resolve-one-item for new types.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We decide never to add new stack-item types.

#### 3. Resolution pipeline ❌

**What it is:** Model resolution as stages: validate → resolve-targets → execute-effects → zone-cleanup. Each stage is a pure function.

**Why we looked at this:** Most composable, cleanest separation

**Investigation:**
- Evaluated pipeline abstraction against current 5 stack-item types
- Pipeline overhead significant for what is essentially type dispatch + effect execution

**Pros:**
- Most composable
- Each stage independently testable

**Cons:**
- Over-engineered for current needs
- Pipeline abstraction adds complexity without proportional benefit
- YAGNI — multimethod achieves same dispatch with less machinery

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** YAGNI. Multimethod provides the extensibility we need without pipeline abstraction overhead.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Resolution stages become independently reusable across multiple subsystems.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Multimethod dispatch for all stack-item types
- Target storage unification (stack-item only)
- Deletion of duplicate functions
- Test migration to production path
- effects.cljs target read updates

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Changing stack-item types or adding new ones
- Changing effect data structures
- Changing card definitions
- Selection system changes (mechanism stays the same)

### Open Questions
- Should resolve-spell zone-transition logic become a standalone helper or inline in :spell defmethod? (decide during implementation)
- Should :spell and :storm-copy share a common helper or be separate defmethods? (decide during implementation — likely share)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Scope of refactor? | Full unification | Single path, multimethod, target unification |
| Multimethod location? | Engine layer | New engine/resolution.cljs, events wrapper stays thin |
| Test migration strategy? | Use resolve-one-item | Tests match production, extract {:db} from result |
| Include target unification? | Yes | Stack-item targets as single source, remove bridge code |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Duplicate Resolution Paths
**Question explored:** What exactly is duplicated and why?
**Sources consulted:**
- game.cljs:317-340 (resolve-top-of-stack)
- game.cljs:343-401 (resolve-one-item)
- Both have parallel cond on stack-item/object-ref

**Findings:**
- resolve-top-of-stack is dead code in production (only tests)
- resolve-one-item is the real path, selection-aware
- Both dispatch on same properties with different handling
- resolve-all-handler correctly uses resolve-one-item

**Conclusion:** resolve-top-of-stack is dead code, resolve-one-item has the right behavior but wrong structure (cond instead of multimethod)

#### Target Storage Split
**Question explored:** Where are targets stored and read?
**Sources consulted:**
- targeting.cljs:150 (writes to stack-item)
- resolution.cljs:103-104 (reads with fallback)
- resolution.cljs:145-146 (bridge to object)
- effects.cljs:477,583,627 (reads from object)
- triggers.cljs:79,88 (storm inherits from stack-item)

**Findings:**
- Cast-time writes to :stack-item/targets (canonical)
- Storm copies inherit from :stack-item/targets (correct)
- Abilities read from :stack-item/targets only (correct)
- Spells bridge to :object/targets for rules/resolve-spell (wrong)
- 3 effect implementations read :object/targets (should read pre-resolved)

**Conclusion:** Stack-item targets are already canonical, bridge code exists only because rules/resolve-spell and effects.cljs assume object targets

### Dead-End Paths

#### Keeping resolve-top-of-stack for tests
**Why explored:** Simpler test API (returns plain db)
**Investigation:** Tests using old path don't exercise selection-awareness — test/prod mismatch
**Why abandoned:** Tests must match production path to catch real bugs

### Open Concerns Raised

- Cephalid Coliseum stored-player-target path? → Pre-resolution handles this, :any-player resolved to concrete player before execution
- Storm copy target inheritance? → Already uses stack-item targets exclusively, unaffected
- effects.cljs signature changes? → Minimal — 3 locations read target from pre-resolved effect field instead of object
close_reason: All 12 success criteria met, reviewer APPROVED. Multimethod unification complete.

title: Make toggle-selection-impl pure + rewrite cast-mode-with-target
description: toggle-selection-impl returns {:app-db ... :auto-confirm? ...} instead of calling rf/dispatch. cast-mode-with-target uses build → toggle → confirm pipeline instead of d/db-with. Completes the remaining epic requirements.
design:
## Goal
toggle-selection-impl has zero rf/dispatch calls and returns a pure data map. The re-frame toggle handler reads :auto-confirm? and dispatches as an fx effect. cast-mode-with-target exercises the full production selection pipeline.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing code:
   - events/selection/core.cljs:401-453 (toggle-selection-impl — current impl with rf/dispatch at line 451)
   - events/selection.cljs (::toggle-selection handler — currently reg-event-db, calls impl)
   - events/casting.cljs:184-200 (build-spell-mode-selection — currently defn-, needs to be public)
   - test_helpers.cljs:281-309 (cast-mode-with-target — current d/db-with bypass)
   - Learnings from fizzle-dqk7: process-deferred-entry ordering, validation gate pattern, hierarchy_test exclusion set

2. Write tests first (TDD) in events/selection/core_test.cljs:
   All tests construct app-db as {:game/db db :game/pending-selection sel}.

   a. test-toggle-impl-returns-data-map-not-app-db
      - Build app-db with single-select selection (select-count 1, auto-confirm? true, candidates #{:a :b})
      - Call toggle-selection-impl with a valid candidate id
      - Assert result is a map with :app-db and :auto-confirm? keys (not raw app-db)
      - Bug caught: impl returning raw app-db (old signature)

   b. test-toggle-impl-auto-confirm-true-on-single-select
      - Build app-db with select-count 1, auto-confirm? true
      - Toggle a valid candidate
      - Assert (:auto-confirm? result) is true
      - Assert (:app-db result) has the item in :selection/selected
      - Bug caught: impl that never signals auto-confirm

   c. test-toggle-impl-auto-confirm-false-on-deselect
      - Build app-db with select-count 1, auto-confirm? true, item already selected
      - Toggle the already-selected item (deselect)
      - Assert (:auto-confirm? result) is false
      - Bug caught: impl that auto-confirms deselects

   d. test-toggle-impl-auto-confirm-false-on-multi-select
      - Build app-db with select-count 3, auto-confirm? false
      - Toggle a valid candidate
      - Assert (:auto-confirm? result) is false
      - Bug caught: impl that auto-confirms multi-select prematurely

   e. test-toggle-impl-auto-confirm-false-when-at-limit-rejected
      - Build app-db with select-count 2, two items selected
      - Toggle a NEW item (at limit, should be rejected)
      - Assert (:auto-confirm? result) is false
      - Assert (:app-db result) selection unchanged
      - Bug caught: impl that auto-confirms rejected selections

   f. test-toggle-impl-rejects-invalid-target
      - Build app-db with :selection/valid-targets set
      - Toggle an id NOT in valid-targets
      - Assert (:auto-confirm? result) is false
      - Assert (:app-db result) unchanged
      - Bug caught: impl that accepts invalid targets after signature change

3. Implementation:

   a. Make build-spell-mode-selection public:
      - events/casting.cljs:184 — change `defn-` to `defn`
      - No behavior change, just visibility

   b. Make toggle-selection-impl pure:
      - events/selection/core.cljs — change toggle-selection-impl:
      - Replace all return paths to return {:app-db new-db :auto-confirm? bool}
      - Auto-confirm case (lines 448-452): return {:app-db new-db :auto-confirm? true} instead of (do (rf/dispatch ...) new-db)
      - All other return paths: return {:app-db app-db-or-new-db :auto-confirm? false}
      - Remove the rf/dispatch call entirely from this function

   c. Update ::toggle-selection handler:
      - events/selection.cljs — change reg-event-db to reg-event-fx
      - Handler destructures result from toggle-selection-impl
      - Returns {:db (:app-db result)} when :auto-confirm? is false
      - Returns {:db (:app-db result) :fx [[:dispatch [::confirm-selection]]]} when :auto-confirm? is true
      - Pattern: (rf/reg-event-fx ::toggle-selection (fn [{:keys [db]} [_ id]] (let [result (core/toggle-selection-impl db id)] (cond-> {:db (:app-db result)} (:auto-confirm? result) (assoc :fx [[:dispatch [::confirm-selection]]])))))

   d. Rewrite cast-mode-with-target in test_helpers.cljs:
      - Replace d/db-with for :object/chosen-mode with production pipeline:
      - Call casting/build-spell-mode-selection to create selection state
      - Wrap into app-db: {:game/db db :game/pending-selection sel}
      - Set :selection/selected to #{spell-mode} on the selection
      - Call sel-core/confirm-selection-impl (which now validates + executes)
      - Extract :game/db from result, continue with targeting as before
      - NOTE: toggle-selection-impl may not be needed in helper if we can directly set :selection/selected and confirm (toggle is UI interaction; the helper can set selected and confirm like other helpers do)

4. Verify:
   - make validate passes (all existing + new tests)
   - grep -n 'rf/dispatch' src/main/fizzle/events/selection/core.cljs returns 0 matches
   - Vision Charm, Blue Elemental Blast, and other modal card tests all pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] toggle-selection-impl return type is {:app-db ... :auto-confirm? bool}
- [ ] Zero rf/dispatch calls in toggle-selection-impl (verify: grep returns 0 matches in core.cljs)
- [ ] ::toggle-selection handler is reg-event-fx, dispatches ::confirm-selection when :auto-confirm? true
- [ ] build-spell-mode-selection is public (defn, not defn-)
- [ ] cast-mode-with-target uses build-spell-mode-selection + confirm-selection-impl (no d/db-with for :object/chosen-mode)
- [ ] 6 new toggle tests pass covering return shape + auto-confirm true/false cases
- [ ] All existing modal card tests pass (Vision Charm, Blue Elemental Blast, Hydroblast)
- [ ] make validate passes: 0 failures

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO rf/dispatch inside toggle-selection-impl (pure function — return data only)
- ❌ NO reg-event-db for ::toggle-selection handler (cannot issue dispatch as side effect from reg-event-db)
- ❌ NO d/db-with for :object/chosen-mode in cast-mode-with-target (bypasses spell-mode executor)
- ❌ NO changes to confirm-selection-impl or confirm-selection test helper (done in fizzle-dqk7)
- ❌ NO changes to cast-and-resolve or cast-with-target helpers
- ❌ NO new abstraction layers

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**build-spell-mode-selection visibility:**
- Currently defn- (private) in events/casting.cljs:184
- Must become defn (public) before test_helpers.cljs can call it
- Safe change — only used within casting namespace today

**reg-event-db → reg-event-fx migration:**
- reg-event-db: fn receives db, returns db
- reg-event-fx: fn receives {:keys [db]}, returns {:db ... :fx [...]}
- The ::toggle-selection handler MUST migrate to reg-event-fx to issue dispatch as effect
- Check if other handlers in events/selection.cljs use reg-event-fx already for pattern reference

**cast-mode-with-target rewrite approach:**
- Don't need to call toggle-selection-impl — toggle is for UI interaction
- Build the selection, set :selection/selected, call confirm-selection-impl directly
- This mirrors how confirm-selection test helper works (set selected → confirm)
- The validation now runs inside confirm-selection-impl (fizzle-dqk7), so candidate membership IS checked

**Auto-confirm guard conditions (from production code):**
- Only fires when: selected? = true AND select-count = 1 AND :selection/auto-confirm? = true
- Deselect (selected? = false) → auto-confirm? false
- Multi-select (select-count > 1) → auto-confirm? false
- At-limit rejected → auto-confirm? false
- Invalid target rejected → auto-confirm? false

**From fizzle-dqk7 learnings:**
- hierarchy_test.cljs exclusion set: if adding test-only build-chain-selection defmethods, add keyword to exclusion set
- process-deferred-entry must be defined BEFORE confirm-selection-impl in core.cljs
- validate-selection returns false for nil selection automatically
close_reason: Closed

title: Strengthen ~15 weak assertion YELLOW tests
description:
Fix tests with weak or misleading assertions:
- effects_test: mill order (check actual order, not counts), restriction default turn (exercise nil path)
- triggers_test: nil controller (verify db unchanged, not just some?)
- trigger_dispatch_test: ordering (document limitation or fix)
- exile_cost_test: insufficient cards (test actual insufficient case), zero count (test exile mechanism)
- state_based_test: register-sba (verify return value used)
- peek_selection_test: remainder to bottom (verify positions, not just zone)
- turn_test: cleanup assertions (use precise assertions)
- scry_confirm_test: empty piles (verify full library state)
design:
## Goal
Strengthen ~15 YELLOW tests with weak or misleading assertions so they catch real bugs.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Tests to Strengthen (grouped by file)

### effects_test.cljs
1. test-mill-effect-preserves-order (~line 156): Currently only checks counts. Strengthen by checking actual card-ids in graveyard match expected order from top of library.
2. test-deal-damage-preserves-source (~line 575): Only checks life total. Add assertion that the damage source object is still present/unchanged in db.
3. test-add-restriction-default-turn-when-nil (~line 1350): init-game-state sets :game/turn 1 so (or nil 1) default never exercised. Create db WITHOUT :game/turn to actually test the default path.

### triggers_test.cljs
4. test-trigger-with-nil-controller-graceful (~line 11): Only (is (some? result-db)). Strengthen to (is (= db result-db)) to verify db truly unchanged.

### trigger_dispatch_test.cljs
5. test-dispatch-event-order-immediate-before-stacked (~line 215): Has duplicate assertion and can't observe order. Either: (a) add observable side-effect to immediate trigger to verify order, or (b) document limitation and remove duplicate assertion.

### exile_cost_test.cljs
6. test-can-pay-exile-cost-insufficient-cards (~line 172): Misleading name — tests minimum castable case. Add actual insufficient case (0 blue cards, X must be >= 1).
7. test-can-pay-exile-cost-zero-count (~line 191): Tests mana/can-pay? not exile cost. Rewrite to test rules/can-cast-mode? with X=0 exile cost.

### state_based_test.cljs
8. test-register-sba (~line 56): Uses atom side-effect check. Strengthen by verifying the SBA's actual effect on db (e.g., register an SBA that removes an object, verify object removed).

### peek_selection_test.cljs
9. test-execute-peek-selection-moves-remainder-to-bottom (~line 277): Only checks zone is :library. Add position assertions: remainder cards should have positions HIGHER than pre-existing library cards.
10. test-execute-peek-selection-fail-to-find (~line 301): Same weakness. Add position assertions.

### turn_test.cljs
11. test-cleanup-discard-to-max-hand-size (~line 384): (is (map? result)) is weak. Strengthen to check specific expected keys/values in the result map.
12. test-cleanup-advance-blocked-during-selection (~line 411): Never puts pending selection in app-db. Add actual pending selection to test the blocking behavior.
13. test-cleanup-repeat-does-not-trigger-at-other-phases (~line 471): Tests via grant expiry proxy. Add direct assertion that db is unchanged after maybe-continue-cleanup in non-cleanup phase.

### scry_confirm_test.cljs
14. test-confirm-scry-empty-piles-allowed (~line 240): (is (some? result-db)) is weak. Add assertions for ALL library cards unchanged (not just card A).

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] For each test, read the production code it exercises
- [ ] Write the strengthened assertion based on actual production behavior
- [ ] Verify strengthened test WOULD fail if production code broke (mentally trace)
- [ ] Run make validate after each file changed
- [ ] Total test count should remain the same

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 14 listed tests have strengthened assertions
- [ ] No (is (some? ...)) or (is (map? ...)) weak assertions remain in listed tests
- [ ] No (is (true? true)) or equivalent tautologies
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] Each strengthened test catches a specific nameable bug

## Anti-patterns
- Do NOT weaken existing assertions while strengthening — only add/replace
- Do NOT change test names unless the name is actively misleading (e.g., insufficient-cards test)
- Do NOT add new deftest — strengthen existing ones
- Do NOT test mock/fixture behavior — assert on production code output

title: Register permanent triggers on battlefield entry
description:
When a permanent spell resolves and enters the battlefield, register any card triggers in Datascript. Currently only lands do this (in events/lands.cljs). Creatures and other permanents with triggers need the same treatment.

## File to modify

### src/main/fizzle/engine/resolution.cljs — move-resolved-spell function (lines 103-127)

Add trigger-db and trigger-dispatch requires to ns:
```clojure
[fizzle.engine.trigger-db :as trigger-db]
```

Modify move-resolved-spell to register triggers when destination is :battlefield:
```clojure
(defn move-resolved-spell [db object-id obj]
  (if (:object/is-copy obj)
    (zones/remove-object db object-id)
    (let [cast-mode (:object/cast-mode obj)
          mode-destination (:mode/on-resolve cast-mode)
          card-types (:card/types (:object/card obj))
          destination (cond
                        mode-destination mode-destination
                        (rules/permanent-type? card-types) :battlefield
                        :else :graveyard)
          db-after-move (zones/move-to-zone db object-id destination)
          card (:object/card obj)]
      (if (and (= destination :battlefield) (seq (:card/triggers card)))
        (let [obj-eid (queries/get-object-eid db-after-move object-id)
              controller-ref (:object/controller obj)
              controller-eid (if (map? controller-ref) (:db/id controller-ref) controller-ref)
              tx (trigger-db/create-triggers-for-card-tx
                   db-after-move obj-eid controller-eid (:card/triggers card))]
          (d/db-with db-after-move tx))
        db-after-move))))
```

Note: queries is already imported as fizzle.db.queries in resolution.cljs. The obj parameter is a pre-fetched entity from queries/get-object, so :object/controller is a {:db/id N} ref.

## Verification
- make validate passes
- A creature with :card/triggers that resolves as a spell gets its triggers registered in Datascript
- Existing permanents without triggers still resolve normally
- Copies are unaffected (they go through remove-object path)
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature: Live Hypergeometric Draw Calculator
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Calculator shows probability of drawing target cards from the current library during active gameplay
- Users can create multiple concurrent queries, each with one or more sequential steps
- Each step specifies a draw count and one or more target groups with configurable min-count
- Sequential steps model accurate draw removal (step 2 sees library minus step 1 draws)
- Joint queries within a step compute multivariate probability (all groups must hit simultaneously)
- All probabilities update reactively as game state changes (draws, tutors, mills, etc.)
- Query definitions persist in localStorage with deck configuration
- Calculator UI positioned under the zones component
- Queries collapse to single-line summaries (label + percentage) and expand for editing
- Card selection for targets shows cards remaining in library with counts
- All math is exact (hypergeometric distribution), not simulation-based

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Single-group probability correct: P(>=k of K in n draws from N)
- [ ] Joint probability correct: P(>=k1 of K1 AND >=k2 of K2 in n draws)
- [ ] Sequential probability correct: conditional chain with intermediate state enumeration
- [ ] Probabilities update live when library contents change
- [ ] Multiple queries visible simultaneously with collapse/expand
- [ ] Queries saved to and loaded from localStorage with deck config
- [ ] Per-step and overall probability displayed with breakdown
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO Monte Carlo simulation (correctness: exact math is tractable for 60-card decks and produces deterministic, testable results)
- ❌ NO game state mutation from calculator (isolation: calculator is read-only — must not dispatch game events or modify Datascript)
- ❌ NO calculator state in Datascript (separation: calculator queries are UI state, not game state — belongs in app-db, not the immutable game db)
- ❌ NO external math dependencies (simplicity: ~30 lines of ClojureScript covers all needed combinatorics — no npm packages)
- ❌ NO card-name-specific logic in math layer (data-over-code: probability functions take counts and draw numbers, never card identities)
- ❌ NO blocking computation in event handlers (performance: all probability math happens in subscriptions, not events)

## Approach
Implement a live hypergeometric probability calculator as a read-only overlay on the game state. Pure math functions in engine/probability.cljs compute exact hypergeometric, multivariate, and sequential conditional probabilities. Calculator query state (steps, targets, min-counts) lives in app-db and is managed by simple CRUD events. Re-frame subscriptions chain library contents from Datascript through probability functions to produce reactive results. The view renders a collapsible query dashboard under the zones component. Query definitions persist in localStorage alongside deck configuration.

## Architecture
- engine/probability.cljs — Pure math functions (binomial coefficient, hypergeometric PMF/CDF, multivariate enumeration, sequential conditional probability). Zero dependencies on re-frame or Datascript.
- events/calculator.cljs — Re-frame events for calculator UI state: add/remove queries, add/remove steps, toggle cards in target groups, set draw counts, set min-counts, toggle visibility, collapse/expand queries.
- subs/calculator.cljs — Subscription chain: game-db → library-contents → library-card-counts → calculator-results. The results subscription calls probability functions with current library state + query definitions.
- views/calculator.cljs — Reagent component: query dashboard with collapse/expand, step builder, card selection grid, percentage display with per-step breakdown.

Data model (app-db):
{:calculator/visible? true
 :calculator/editing-query-id 1  ;; which query's card selector is active
 :calculator/queries
 [{:query/id 1
   :query/label "Hit a ritual"
   :query/steps [{:step/id 1
                   :step/draw-count 1
                   :step/targets [{:target/id 1
                                   :target/cards #{:dark-ritual :cabal-ritual}
                                   :target/min-count 1}]}]
   :query/collapsed? false}]}

Subscription chain:
  ::game-db → ::library-contents → ::library-card-counts
  ::calculator-queries (from app-db)
  ::calculator-results (combines card-counts + queries → probability results)

Persistence:
  Extend existing localStorage deck config to include :calculator/queries.
  On deck load, restore queries. On query change, persist.

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Storm combo practice requires understanding draw probabilities mid-game to make optimal sequencing decisions (e.g., "should I Brainstorm here or save it?"). Currently players must calculate mentally or use external tools that don't know the current game state. A live contextual calculator that knows exactly what's in the library provides much better information for practice decisions.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- No probability code exists — implement from scratch
- Decklist format: [{:card/id :dark-ritual :count 4} ...] in :setup/main-deck
- Library contents available via queries/get-objects-in-zone
- core.cljs:88-89 has reserved space for calculator panel in bottom section
- All needed subscriptions exist for library/zone data
- storage.cljs handles localStorage persistence (pure I/O, outside model)

**External:**
- Hypergeometric PMF: P(X=k) = C(K,k) * C(N-K, n-k) / C(N,n)
- Binomial coefficient: iterative implementation avoids factorial overflow (~10 lines)
- Computation is trivially fast for 60-card decks (<1ms even for complex queries)
- Existing MTG calculators use 4-input pattern (population, successes, sample, wanted)
- No ClojureScript library needed — custom implementation is ~30 lines

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Exact math everywhere ✓
**What it is:** Implement hypergeometric distribution + multivariate extension + conditional sequential calculation as pure functions. All computation exact and deterministic.

**Investigation:**
- Verified 60-card deck math completes in <1ms for all tiers
- Sequential enumeration has at most K_A × K_B terms per step pair (~16 max)
- Multivariate hypergeometric tractable via enumeration for 2-4 groups

**Pros:**
- Exact results, no approximation noise
- Deterministic — easy to test with precise expected values
- Fits re-frame subscription model (pure functions)

**Cons:**
- Sequential math implementation requires careful enumeration logic

**Chosen because:** 60-card decks make exact math trivially fast; determinism enables precise testing; pure functions fit re-frame naturally.

#### 2. Hybrid (exact + Monte Carlo for sequential) ❌
**What it is:** Use exact hypergeometric for single/joint, switch to Monte Carlo simulation (100k trials) for sequential chain calculations.

**Why we looked at this:** Sequential conditional probability is the most complex math — simulation could simplify implementation.

**Investigation:**
- 100k trials completes in ~50-100ms for 60-card decks
- Results vary by ~0.1-0.5% between runs
- Testing requires tolerance ranges, not exact values

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Non-deterministic results make testing brittle; exact math IS tractable for our input sizes; mixing two calculation approaches adds conceptual complexity.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We add support for 250+ card formats (Commander) where exact enumeration becomes costly.

#### 3. Monte Carlo everything ❌
**What it is:** All probability calculations via library shuffle simulation (100k trials per query).

**Why we looked at this:** Uniform approach for all query types — simplest implementation.

**Investigation:**
- Would need seeded PRNG for reproducible tests
- Multiple concurrent queries × 100k trials = potential performance concern
- Approximation when exact answers are available

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Introduces unnecessary approximation when exact math is fast; harder to test; performance concern with many concurrent queries.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Query shapes become so diverse that enumeration is impractical.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Live probability calculator during active gameplay
- Multi-query dashboard with collapse/expand
- Step builder for sequential conditional calculations
- Joint probability within steps (multiple target groups)
- Configurable min-count per target group
- Reactive updates as game state changes
- Query persistence in localStorage with deck

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Deckbuilding/static analysis mode (different feature — deferred)
- Pre-game/setup integration (deferred — could show opening hand odds later)
- Mulligan probability modeling (deferred — complex with London mulligan)
- Card tagging/categorization system (deferred — user wanted ad-hoc selection)
- Export/sharing of calculations (never for v1)

### Open Questions
- Exact localStorage schema extension for deck + queries (resolve during implementation)
- Whether collapsed query labels should be user-editable or auto-generated (default: user-editable)
- Color coding thresholds for percentage display (resolve during implementation)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Primary use case? | Live during practice | Calculator reads current library state from Datascript |
| What query types? | Category + combo + sequential | Need multivariate hypergeometric + conditional probability |
| Card grouping? | Pick from library (ad-hoc) | No pre-tagging system needed |
| UI placement? | Under zones component | New view component in bottom section |
| Sequential UX? | Step builder | User adds steps with draw count + target groups |
| Update mode? | Live reactive | Re-frame subscriptions recompute on library changes |
| Draw accuracy? | Model draws accurately | Each step removes drawn cards from pool for next step |
| Output detail? | Percentage + breakdown | Per-step probabilities + overall displayed |
| Query mode? | Configurable per group | Each target group has min-count selector |
| Card context? | Library only | Don't show where other copies are (hand, GY, etc.) |
| Multiple queries? | Yes, concurrent dashboard | Multiple independent calculations visible at once |
| Persistence? | Save with deck in localStorage | Queries load automatically with deck selection |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Hypergeometric Math Feasibility
**Question explored:** Can we compute exact sequential conditional probability fast enough for reactive UI?
**Sources consulted:**
- Enumeration analysis: K_A × K_B terms per step pair, typically <16 iterations
- 60-card deck constraint means all combinations are small
**Findings:**
- Single group: one formula evaluation
- Joint: multivariate enumeration with at most K₁ × K₂ × ... terms
- Sequential: nested enumeration, still fast for 60 cards and 2-3 steps
**Conclusion:** Exact math is fast for all tiers — no need for simulation

#### Existing MTG Calculator UX
**Question explored:** What patterns do existing MTG probability tools use?
**Sources consulted:**
- AetherHub, Draftsim, Flexslot, CardGameCalculator hypergeometric tools
**Findings:**
- Standard 4-input pattern (population, successes, sample, wanted)
- Most only handle single-group queries
- None offer live game-state integration
- Step builders / sequential calculations are rare
**Conclusion:** Our feature goes beyond existing tools by being contextual and supporting sequential queries

### Dead-End Paths
None — all three approaches were evaluated; Approach 1 was clearly superior for our constraints.

### Open Concerns Raised
- "Would I be able to keep multiple calculations up at once?" → Yes, redesigned to multi-query dashboard with collapse/expand
- "I take that as it would save into the local storage for that entry" → Confirmed: extend existing deck localStorage with query definitions
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement calculator query CRUD events
description: Re-frame event handlers for calculator query state management in app-db. Pure CRUD on query/step/target-group data model. No probability computation, no Datascript access.
design:
## Goal
Create \`src/main/fizzle/events/calculator.cljs\` with all re-frame event handlers for calculator query state management in app-db. This is the mutation layer — pure CRUD on the query/step/target-group data model. No probability computation. No Datascript access.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns
- Reference: \`src/main/fizzle/events/ui.cljs\` — event handler style
- Reference: \`src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs\` — namespace require pattern
- Namespace convention: \`:fizzle.events.calculator/event-name\`

### 2. App-db data model (exact shape)
\`\`\`clojure
{:calculator/visible? false
 :calculator/next-id  1      ;; incrementing counter for all IDs (query, step, target)
 :calculator/queries  []}    ;; vector of query maps (ordered)

;; Query map:
{:query/id        1
 :query/label     \"Query 1\"
 :query/collapsed? false
 :query/steps     []}

;; Step map:
{:step/id         2
 :step/draw-count 7
 :step/targets    []}

;; Target group map:
{:target/id        3
 :target/cards     #{}   ;; set of card-id keywords
 :target/min-count 1}
\`\`\`

ID generation: \`:calculator/next-id\` is incremented every time a new query, step, or target group is created. Start value: 1.

### 3. Write tests first (TDD)

Test file: \`src/test/fizzle/events/calculator_test.cljs\`

**Events to test (13 total):**
1. \`::init-calculator\` — idempotent merge of default state (no-op if \`:calculator/queries\` already present)
2. \`::toggle-calculator\` — toggle \`:calculator/visible?\`
3. \`::add-query\` — append new query with next-id, label \"Query N\", empty steps, collapsed?=false; increment next-id
4. \`::remove-query\` — filter out query by id; no-op if id not found
5. \`::set-query-label\` — update label for query by id; no-op if id not found
6. \`::toggle-query-collapsed\` — toggle \`:query/collapsed?\` for query by id; no-op if id not found
7. \`::add-step\` — append new step to query with next-id, draw-count=7, empty targets; increment next-id; no-op if query id not found
8. \`::remove-step\` — filter out step by id from its query; no-op if ids not found
9. \`::set-step-draw-count\` — set draw-count for step; clamp to minimum 0 (0 is valid — models \"no draw\"); no-op if ids not found
10. \`::add-target-group\` — append new target group to step with next-id, empty cards set, min-count=1; increment next-id; no-op if ids not found
11. \`::remove-target-group\` — filter out target group by id; no-op if ids not found
12. \`::toggle-target-card\` — if card-id in \`:target/cards\`, remove it; if not, add it; no-op if ids not found
13. \`::set-target-min-count\` — set min-count; clamp to minimum 0; no-op if ids not found

**Test scenarios (minimum 20 assertions):**
- add-query creates query with correct shape (id, label, collapsed?, steps)
- remove-query with unknown id is no-op
- add-step uses incrementing id distinct from query id
- toggle-target-card adds card when not present
- toggle-target-card removes card when already present
- set-step-draw-count clamps negative to 0
- set-target-min-count clamps negative to 0
- init-calculator is idempotent (doesn't wipe existing queries)
- toggle-calculator flips visible?

### 4. Implementation checklist

\`src/main/fizzle/events/calculator.cljs\`:
- [ ] Namespace \`fizzle.events.calculator\` with \`re-frame.core\` require
- [ ] All 13 event handlers as \`reg-event-db\`
- [ ] ID counter incrementing on every create operation
- [ ] Clamping on draw-count and min-count (minimum 0)
- [ ] No-op on missing IDs (silent, db unchanged)

\`src/test/fizzle/events/calculator_test.cljs\`:
- [ ] At least 20 test assertions covering all events and edge cases

Side-effects:
- [ ] Require \`fizzle.events.calculator\` from appropriate init point

### 5. Edge Cases (must be handled)
- Operations on non-existent IDs: all operations are no-ops (silent, no error, db unchanged)
- Remove last step from query: leaves empty steps vector — no auto-add
- Remove last target group from step: leaves empty targets vector — no auto-add
- \`::toggle-target-card\` when card in a different target group of same step: event does NOT enforce disjointness (UI layer responsibility per epic design)
- \`::set-step-draw-count\` with negative: clamp to 0
- \`::set-target-min-count\` with negative: clamp to 0
- \`::init-calculator\` when state present: no-op (idempotent)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 13 events registered and callable without crash
- [ ] 20+ test assertions pass covering all event behaviors and edge cases
- [ ] No Datascript imports in events/calculator.cljs
- [ ] No probability function calls in events/calculator.cljs
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO Datascript reads or writes (events operate on app-db only)
- ❌ NO probability computation in any event handler (belongs in subscriptions)
- ❌ NO crashing on invalid/missing IDs — all mutations are no-ops on bad input
- ❌ NO mutable atoms or vars outside re-frame db
- ❌ NO enforcement of disjoint groups in event layer (UI layer responsibility)
- ❌ NO IDs as strings — IDs are integers throughout
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement calculator subscriptions
description: Re-frame subscription chain that reads library state from Datascript, combines with calculator query definitions from app-db, and calls probability functions to produce reactive results.
design:
## Goal
Create src/main/fizzle/subs/calculator.cljs with the re-frame subscription chain that reads library state from Datascript, combines with calculator query definitions from app-db, and calls probability functions to produce reactive results.

## Effort Estimate
5-7 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns
- subs/game.cljs — ::game-db subscription, :<- dependency syntax, nil-safety pattern
- subs/game_test.cljs — sub-value helper pattern (reset! rf-db/app-db, deref subscribe)
- db/queries.cljs:get-objects-in-zone — library contents query
- engine/probability.cljs — the math functions to call

### 2. Subscription chain (3 subscriptions)

**Sub 1: ::calculator-queries**
Input: app-db
Output: (:calculator/queries app-db) or [] if absent
Pattern: simple Layer 2 extraction

**Sub 2: ::library-card-counts**
Input: :<- [::subs/game-db] (from subs.game)
Output: {card-id-keyword -> integer-count-in-library}
Default: {} when game-db is nil
Implementation:
  (when game-db
    (let [player-id (queries/get-human-player-id game-db)
          objects (queries/get-objects-in-zone game-db player-id :library)]
      (->> objects
           (group-by #(get-in % [:object/card :card/id]))
           (reduce-kv (fn [m k v] (assoc m k (count v))) {}))))

**Sub 3: ::calculator-results**
Input: :<- [::library-card-counts], :<- [::calculator-queries]
Output: vector of result maps, one per query:
  {:query/id int
   :query/label string
   :query/collapsed? bool
   :query/probability float  ;; overall query probability
   :query/steps
   [{:step/id int
     :step/draw-count int
     :step/probability float  ;; this step's probability in isolation
     :step/targets
     [{:target/id int
       :target/cards #{}
       :target/count int     ;; K = sum of library counts for target cards
       :target/min-count int
       :target/probability float}]}]}

### 3. Probability computation logic (pure helper fns, not subs)

**compute-target-count [card-counts target]**
  Sum card-counts for each card-id in :target/cards.
  K = (reduce + 0 (map #(get card-counts % 0) (:target/cards target)))

**compute-step-probability [N card-counts step]**
  For each target: K = compute-target-count
  If no targets → 1.0 (vacuously true)
  If 1 target → (probability/at-least-probability N K draw-count min-count)
  If 2+ targets → (probability/joint-probability N groups draw-count)
    where groups = [{:count K :min min-count} ...]

**compute-query-results [card-counts query]**
  N = total library size (reduce + 0 (vals card-counts))
  If no steps → {:query/probability 1.0 ...}
  If 1 step → step probability is the query probability
  If 2+ steps → use probability/sequential-probability for overall probability
    Collect all unique target groups across steps, assign indices.
    Build all-groups and steps args for sequential-probability.

  Per-step probability is computed independently (single-step isolation)
  for display purposes. Overall query probability uses sequential-probability
  which correctly handles cross-step target consumption.

### 4. Write tests first (TDD)

Test file: src/test/fizzle/subs/calculator_test.cljs

Use the sub-value helper pattern from subs/game_test.cljs:
  (defn- sub-value [db sub-vec]
    (reset! rf-db/app-db db)
    @(rf/subscribe sub-vec))

**Test scenarios (minimum 15 assertions):**

1. ::calculator-queries returns [] when absent from app-db
2. ::calculator-queries returns queries when present
3. ::library-card-counts returns {} when game-db is nil
4. ::library-card-counts returns correct counts for library with 4x Dark Ritual
5. ::calculator-results empty queries → []
6. ::calculator-results single query, single step, single target: verify probability matches at-least-probability
7. ::calculator-results single query, single step, two targets: verify matches joint-probability
8. ::calculator-results step with no targets → probability 1.0
9. ::calculator-results target with empty cards set (K=0), min-count=0 → probability 1.0
10. ::calculator-results target with empty cards set (K=0), min-count>0 → probability 0.0
11. ::calculator-results two-step query uses sequential-probability for overall (verify != product of step probabilities when cross-step consumption matters)

### 5. Implementation checklist

src/main/fizzle/subs/calculator.cljs:
- [ ] Namespace with requires: re-frame.core, fizzle.subs.game, fizzle.engine.probability, fizzle.db.queries
- [ ] compute-target-count helper (pure fn)
- [ ] compute-step-probability helper (pure fn)
- [ ] compute-query-results helper (pure fn)
- [ ] reg-sub ::calculator-queries
- [ ] reg-sub ::library-card-counts
- [ ] reg-sub ::calculator-results
- [ ] reg-sub ::calculator-visible? (simple extraction of :calculator/visible? from app-db)

src/test/fizzle/subs/calculator_test.cljs:
- [ ] 15+ assertions covering all test scenarios above

Side-effects:
- [ ] Require fizzle.subs.calculator from core.cljs

### 6. Edge Cases (must handle without crashing)
- nil game-db → {} card counts → all probabilities default correctly
- Empty queries → [] results
- Step with no targets → step probability = 1.0
- Target with empty :target/cards (K=0), min-count=0 → 1.0
- Target with empty :target/cards (K=0), min-count>0 → 0.0
- draw-count > N (library size) → clamp draw-count to min(draw-count, N)
- min-count > K → 0.0 (handled by probability functions)
- Sequential: step N's N = N_initial - sum(draw-count of steps 0..N-1)
- Card IDs in target that don't exist in library → count as 0

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 4 subscriptions registered
- [ ] ::calculator-queries returns [] when key absent
- [ ] ::library-card-counts returns {} when game-db nil
- [ ] Single-group probability matches at-least-probability
- [ ] Joint probability matches joint-probability for 2+ targets
- [ ] Sequential probability uses sequential-probability for 2+ steps
- [ ] Zero-target step returns 1.0
- [ ] 15+ test assertions pass
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO side effects in subscription functions (no dispatch, no swap!)
- ❌ NO Datascript writes
- ❌ NO crashing on nil game-db or missing keys
- ❌ NO NaN or Infinity in results (return 0.0 or 1.0 in degenerate cases)
- ❌ NO probability computation in event handlers (subscriptions only)
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement calculator view component
description: Reagent view component for the calculator dashboard: collapsible queries, step builder, card selection grid, percentage display. Mounts under the zones component in core.cljs.
design:
## Goal
Create src/main/fizzle/views/calculator.cljs — the Reagent view component for the live hypergeometric calculator dashboard. Mounts in the reserved col-span-full div at core.cljs:91.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns
- views/hand.cljs, views/graveyard.cljs — simple view component patterns
- views/mana_pool.cljs — slightly more complex view with multiple subscriptions
- core.cljs:91 — mount point: [:div {:class "col-span-full"}]
- engine/cards.cljs — card-by-id map for looking up card names from IDs

### 2. Component structure

**Top-level: calculator-panel**
- Subscribes to ::calc-subs/calculator-visible?, ::calc-subs/calculator-results, ::calc-subs/library-card-counts
- When not visible → nil (not rendered)
- When visible → renders header + query list

**Header bar:**
- Title: "Draw Calculator"
- "Add Query" button dispatches ::calc-events/add-query
- "Close" button dispatches ::calc-events/toggle-calculator

**Query row (collapsed):**
- Shows: query label + overall probability as "XX.X%"
- Click to expand (dispatches ::calc-events/toggle-query-collapsed)
- Delete button dispatches ::calc-events/remove-query

**Query row (expanded):**
- Editable label (input, on-change dispatches ::calc-events/set-query-label)
- Overall probability display
- List of steps
- "Add Step" button dispatches ::calc-events/add-step

**Step section:**
- Draw count: number input (on-change dispatches ::calc-events/set-step-draw-count)
- Step probability display as "XX.X%"
- List of target groups
- "Add Target" button dispatches ::calc-events/add-target-group
- Delete step button dispatches ::calc-events/remove-step

**Target group section:**
- Min count: number input (on-change dispatches ::calc-events/set-target-min-count)
- Target probability display
- Card count (K) display
- Card selection: clickable card names from library, toggle dispatches ::calc-events/toggle-target-card
- Delete target button dispatches ::calc-events/remove-target-group

**Card selection grid:**
- Shows all unique card IDs from ::library-card-counts
- For each card: display name (looked up via engine/cards.cljs card-by-id :card/name) + count in library
- Cards in the current target group are highlighted (checked)
- Click dispatches ::calc-events/toggle-target-card
- If card-by-id lookup fails for an ID, fall back to humanizing the keyword (replace - with space, capitalize)

**Probability display format:**
- Multiply by 100, format to 1 decimal place: "39.9%"
- 0.0 → "0.0%", 1.0 → "100.0%"
- Use (.toFixed (* p 100) 1) for formatting

### 3. Mount point integration
Replace core.cljs:91 [:div {:class "col-span-full"}] with:
  [calculator/calculator-panel]
Add require for fizzle.views.calculator in core.cljs

### 4. Toggle button
Add a calculator toggle button in the controls area or header.
Look at how other toggles work (e.g., history collapse toggle) and follow the same pattern.
The simplest approach: add a "Calculator" button in the controls-view or near the zone-counts area that dispatches ::calc-events/toggle-calculator.

### 5. Implementation checklist

src/main/fizzle/views/calculator.cljs:
- [ ] Namespace with requires: re-frame.core, fizzle.subs.calculator, fizzle.events.calculator, fizzle.engine.cards
- [ ] format-probability helper (p -> "XX.X%" string)
- [ ] card-name helper (card-id -> display name via card-by-id, fallback to humanized keyword)
- [ ] card-selection-grid component (shows library cards, toggles selection)
- [ ] target-group-view component (min-count input, card grid, delete, probability)
- [ ] step-view component (draw-count input, targets, add target, delete, step probability)
- [ ] query-row-collapsed component (label, probability, expand button, delete)
- [ ] query-row-expanded component (editable label, steps, add step, overall probability)
- [ ] query-view component (routes to collapsed/expanded)
- [ ] calculator-panel component (header, query list, conditional render on visible?)

src/main/fizzle/core.cljs:
- [ ] Require fizzle.views.calculator
- [ ] Replace [:div {:class "col-span-full"}] with [calculator/calculator-panel]
- [ ] Add toggle button somewhere accessible

### 6. Styling (Tailwind)
Follow existing conventions:
- bg-surface, bg-surface-raised for panels
- border-border for borders
- text-text for text, text-text-muted for secondary
- text-accent for highlights
- p-2, p-4 for padding
- flex, gap-2, gap-4 for layout
- text-sm, text-xs for font sizes
- cursor-pointer for clickable elements
- bg-accent/20 or similar for selected state on card grid items

### 7. Testing

Testing Reagent views is not standard in this codebase (existing views have no test files). Do NOT create a test file for this task — the view will be verified by:
- make validate passes (compilation + lint + format)
- Manual verification that the component renders (visual check in browser)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Calculator panel renders when ::calculator-visible? is true
- [ ] Calculator panel absent when ::calculator-visible? is false
- [ ] Collapsed query row shows formatted probability (e.g., "39.9%")
- [ ] Expanded query row shows steps with targets and probabilities
- [ ] Card selection grid shows library cards with counts
- [ ] All buttons dispatch correct re-frame events
- [ ] Toggle button accessible from the game view
- [ ] Probability displays as "XX.X%" format (0.0% to 100.0%)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO rf/dispatch inside subscription callbacks or during render
- ❌ NO direct access to re-frame.db/app-db in view
- ❌ NO inline probability computation in view (subscriptions provide all computed values)
- ❌ NO js/console.log or debug logging in committed code
- ❌ NO crashing when ::calculator-results is empty or nil
