title: Add missing P1 corner case tests (cards/events)
design:
## Goal
Add tests for P1 corner cases in card implementations and event handlers.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Corner Cases to Add

### cards/* (P1 - Card Implementations)

| Corner Case | Bug It Catches | Test Name | File |
|-------------|----------------|-----------|------|
| Flashback on exiled card | Playing spells from wrong zone | test-flashback-from-exile-blocked | recoup_test.cljs |
| Targeting object in wrong zone | Invalid target acceptance | test-target-wrong-zone-rejected | targeting_test.cljs |

### events/* (P1 - Event Handlers)

| Corner Case | Bug It Catches | Test Name | File |
|-------------|----------------|-----------|------|
| Event with nil coeffects | Crash on nil db | test-event-nil-cofx-handled | init_game_test.cljs |
| Selection cancel cleanup | State left in pending-selection | test-selection-cancel-cleanup | tutor_multi_select_test.cljs |
| Double-submit selection | Duplicate card movement | test-selection-double-submit | tutor_multi_select_test.cljs |

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Create test-flashback-from-exile-blocked in recoup_test.cljs
- [ ] Create test-target-wrong-zone-rejected in targeting_test.cljs
- [ ] Create test-event-nil-cofx-handled in appropriate event test file
- [ ] Create test-selection-cancel-cleanup in tutor_multi_select_test.cljs
- [ ] Create test-selection-double-submit in tutor_multi_select_test.cljs
- [ ] Run `make test` after each test addition
- [ ] Run `make validate` at the end

## Test Specifications

### test-flashback-from-exile-blocked
```clojure
(deftest test-flashback-from-exile-blocked
  (testing "Flashback only works from graveyard, not exile"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          ;; Add card with flashback to EXILE (not graveyard)
          [db' obj-id] (add-card-to-zone db :recoup :exile :player-1)
          ;; Add mana to pay flashback cost
          db'' (mana/add-mana db' :player-1 {:red 1 :black 1})]
      ;; Should NOT be castable from exile
      (is (false? (rules/can-cast-flashback? db'' :player-1 obj-id))
          "Flashback should not work from exile"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** Allowing flashback from wrong zone

### test-target-wrong-zone-rejected
```clojure
(deftest test-target-wrong-zone-rejected
  (testing "Targeting rejects objects in wrong zone"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          ;; Add card to LIBRARY (not valid target zone)
          [db' obj-id] (add-card-to-zone db :dark-ritual :library :player-1)
          targeting {:target/type :card
                     :target/zone :hand  ;; Expects hand
                     :target/criteria {}}]
      (is (false? (targeting/valid-target? db' obj-id targeting))
          "Object in wrong zone should not be valid target"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** Accepting targets from any zone

### test-selection-cancel-cleanup
```clojure
(deftest test-selection-cancel-cleanup
  (testing "Cancelling selection clears pending state"
    (let [db (create-pending-selection-state)  ;; Helper to set up pending
          db' (game/cancel-selection db)]
      (is (nil? (:pending-selection db'))
          "Pending selection should be cleared after cancel"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** UI stuck in selection mode after cancel

### test-selection-double-submit
```clojure
(deftest test-selection-double-submit
  (testing "Submitting same selection twice is idempotent"
    (let [db (create-pending-selection-state)
          selection {:selection/selected #{card-id}}
          db' (game/execute-selection db selection)
          db'' (game/execute-selection db' selection)]
      ;; Second submit should either:
      ;; - Return db' unchanged (no-op), OR
      ;; - Throw error (rejected)
      ;; Should NOT move card twice
      (is (= 1 (count (get-cards-moved-to-hand db'')))
          "Double submit should not double-move cards"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** Race condition moving cards twice

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 5 corner case tests written and passing
- [ ] Each test has clear comment stating what bug it catches
- [ ] Tests exercise actual production code paths
- [ ] No tautological assertions
- [ ] `make test` passes
- [ ] `make validate` passes

## Key Considerations

**Flashback Zone Check:**
Flashback is specifically a graveyard mechanic. The rules engine should validate zone before allowing flashback cast. If `can-cast-flashback?` doesn't exist, may need to check `can-cast?` with flashback mode.

**Selection Idempotency:**
Selection handlers may need guards against re-processing. Test documents expected behavior.

**Helper Functions Needed:**
- `create-pending-selection-state` - may need to add this helper
- `get-cards-moved-to-hand` - may need to count zone changes

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Testing mock behavior instead of production code
- ❌ Happy path only assertions
- ❌ Using `some?` as primary assertion
- ❌ Ignoring test if function doesn't exist (should note as blocked)
close_reason: Closed

title: Add effect multi-spec to card_spec.cljs with per-type validation
description: First task for Spec Phase 1 (fizzle-ngzw). Extend card_spec.cljs with s/multi-spec for effects.
design:
## Goal
Replace the single permissive ::effect spec in card_spec.cljs (line 110-122) with a multi-spec dispatching on :effect/type. Each of the 40 effect types gets its own defmethod with required/optional keys. Also add 9 missing effect keys and remove 3 unused keys. validate-cards! at init catches invalid effect definitions automatically.

## Effort Estimate
10-14 hours (40 defmethods + 6 shape groups + missing/unused key fixes + tests)

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - engine/card_spec.cljs:110-122 — current ::effect spec (single s/keys with 18 optional fields)
   - engine/card_spec.cljs:15-24 — valid-effect-types set (40 types)
   - engine/card_spec.cljs:79-98 — existing field-level specs (:effect/type, :effect/mana, :effect/amount, etc.)
   - engine/effects/*.cljs (8 files: simple, zones, life, pt_modifier, tokens, stack, grants, selection)
   - events/init.cljs:107 — validate-cards! call site (no changes needed here)
   - events/selection/library.cljs — reads :effect/target-zone, :effect/source-zone, :effect/shuffle?, :effect/pile-choice, :effect/may-shuffle?, :effect/shuffle-remainder?

2. Write tests first (TDD)
   In engine/card_spec_test.cljs (new file):

   **Shape validation tests (what specific bugs they catch):**
   - test_valid_mill_effect: {:effect/type :mill :effect/amount 3} passes — catches regression if base shape breaks
   - test_add_mana_with_mana_map: {:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:black 3}} passes — catches :effect/mana shape validation
   - test_exile_self_minimal: {:effect/type :exile-self} passes — catches over-constraining (exile-self needs ONLY :effect/type)
   - test_tutor_with_target_zone: {:effect/type :tutor :effect/target-zone :hand :effect/criteria {:card/types #{:instant}} :effect/shuffle? true} passes — catches tutor shape with required target-zone and shuffle?
   - test_create_token: {:effect/type :create-token :effect/token {:token/name "Beast" :token/power 4 :token/toughness 4}} passes — catches new :effect/token key
   - test_tap_all_with_permanent_type: {:effect/type :tap-all :effect/target :opponent :effect/permanent-type :creature} passes — catches new :effect/permanent-type key
   - test_draw_with_dynamic_amount_map: {:effect/type :draw :effect/amount {:dynamic/type :count-named-in-zone}} passes — catches dynamic amount as map value in :effect/amount (NOT a separate key)
   - test_counter_spell_with_unless_pay: {:effect/type :counter-spell :effect/target-ref :target :effect/unless-pay {:colorless 1}} passes — catches unless-pay shape

   **Rejection tests (catch invalid data):**
   - test_unknown_effect_type_fails: {:effect/type :fake-effect} fails — catches unknown types getting through
   - test_wrong_amount_type_fails: {:effect/type :mill :effect/amount "three"} fails — catches type errors on :effect/amount
   - test_missing_type_fails: {:effect/amount 3} fails — catches effect without :effect/type
   - test_mill_without_amount_fails: {:effect/type :mill} fails — catches missing required key for specific type

   **Orthogonality tests:**
   - test_condition_on_any_type: {:effect/type :exile-self :effect/condition {:condition/type :threshold}} passes — proves condition is orthogonal to effect type
   - test_extra_keys_tolerated: {:effect/type :mill :effect/amount 3 :some-future-key true} passes — s/keys allows extra keys, must not break

   **Integration tests:**
   - test_all_71_existing_cards_valid: load all-cards from registry, run s/valid? on every effect in :card/effects, :card/abilities :ability/effects, :card/etb-effects, :card/kicked-effects, :card/conditional-effects — catches any card that doesn't match the tighter spec
   - test_each_type_has_defmethod: for each keyword in valid-effect-types, verify (s/valid? ::effect {:effect/type k ...minimal-valid-map...}) — parametric test catching missing defmethods
   - test_exercise_generates_valid_effects: (s/exercise ::effect 20) returns 20 entries without error — catches broken generators

3. Implementation checklist
   - [ ] Add 9 missing field specs:
         (s/def :effect/token map?)
         (s/def :effect/counters map?)
         (s/def :effect/permanent-type keyword?)
         (s/def :effect/target-zone keyword?)
         (s/def :effect/source-zone keyword?)
         (s/def :effect/shuffle? boolean?)
         (s/def :effect/pile-choice map?)
         (s/def :effect/may-shuffle? boolean?)
         (s/def :effect/shuffle-remainder? boolean?)
   - [ ] Remove 3 unused field specs and their :opt references:
         :effect/counter-type (line 89)
         :effect/destination (line 88)
         :effect/x (line 90)
   - [ ] Define (defmulti effect-type-spec :effect/type)
   - [ ] Define 40 defmethods grouped by shape (see Per-Type Key Catalog below)
   - [ ] Replace card_spec.cljs line 110-122 (s/def ::effect) with (s/def ::effect (s/multi-spec effect-type-spec :effect/type))
   - [ ] Ensure :effect/condition stays as optional in EVERY defmethod (orthogonal to type — any effect can be conditional)
   - [ ] Run all 71 cards through validate-cards! — must pass unchanged
   - [ ] Run (s/exercise ::effect 20) in REPL — verify generators work
   - [ ] make validate passes

## Per-Type Key Catalog

### Group 1: amount+target (5 types)
| Type | Required | Optional |
|------|----------|----------|
| :deal-damage | :effect/amount | :effect/target, :effect/target-ref |
| :draw | :effect/amount | :effect/target |
| :mill | :effect/amount | :effect/target, :effect/target-ref |
| :discard | :effect/count, :effect/selection | — |
| :return-from-graveyard | :effect/count, :effect/selection | :effect/target |

### Group 2: zone-op (13 types)
| Type | Required | Optional |
|------|----------|----------|
| :exile-self | — | — |
| :bounce | :effect/target-ref | — |
| :bounce-all | :effect/criteria | :effect/target-ref |
| :destroy | :effect/target-ref | :effect/condition |
| :discard-hand | — | :effect/target |
| :sacrifice | — | :effect/target, :effect/condition |
| :exile-zone | :effect/target-ref, :effect/zone | — |
| :phase-out | :effect/target-ref | — |
| :chain-bounce | :effect/target-ref | — |
| :untap-lands | :effect/count | — |
| :tap-all | :effect/target, :effect/permanent-type | — |
| :untap-all | :effect/target, :effect/permanent-type | — |
| :welder-swap | :effect/target-ref, :effect/graveyard-ref | — |

### Group 3: mana (1 type)
| Type | Required | Optional |
|------|----------|----------|
| :add-mana | :effect/mana | — |

### Group 4: library-ops (5 types)
| Type | Required | Optional |
|------|----------|----------|
| :tutor | :effect/target-zone | :effect/criteria, :effect/shuffle?, :effect/source-zone, :effect/select-count, :effect/pile-choice |
| :scry | :effect/amount | — |
| :peek-and-select | :effect/count, :effect/selected-zone, :effect/remainder-zone, :effect/select-count | :effect/order-remainder?, :effect/shuffle-remainder? |
| :peek-and-reorder | :effect/count | :effect/target-ref, :effect/may-shuffle? |
| :peek-random-hand | :effect/target-ref | — |

### Group 5: granting (5 types)
| Type | Required | Optional |
|------|----------|----------|
| :grant-flashback | :effect/target-ref | — |
| :grant-delayed-draw | — | :effect/target |
| :grant-mana-ability | :effect/target, :effect/ability, :effect/mana | — |
| :add-restriction | — | :effect/target |
| :add-counters | :effect/target, :effect/counters | — |

### Group 6: special (11 types)
| Type | Required | Optional |
|------|----------|----------|
| :counter-spell | :effect/target-ref | :effect/condition, :effect/unless-pay |
| :counter-ability | :effect/target-ref | — |
| :apply-pt-modifier | :effect/target-ref, :effect/power, :effect/toughness | — |
| :lose-life-equal-to-toughness | :effect/target-ref | — |
| :gain-life-equal-to-cmc | :effect/target-ref | — |
| :create-token | :effect/token | — |
| :change-land-types | :effect/target-ref, :effect/amount | — |
| :discard-from-revealed-hand | :effect/target, :effect/criteria | — |
| :storm-copies | — | — |
| :lose-life | :effect/amount | :effect/target |
| :gain-life | :effect/amount | :effect/target |

NOTE: :storm-copies is runtime-generated (engine/rules.cljs:308), never in card definitions. :lose-life and :gain-life are in valid-effect-types but currently unused in cards — assumed shape mirrors :deal-damage.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] s/multi-spec for ::effect dispatches on :effect/type with 40 defmethods
- [ ] All 71 existing card definitions pass validate-cards! unchanged (check :card/effects, ability :ability/effects, :card/etb-effects, :card/kicked-effects, :card/conditional-effects)
- [ ] Unknown :effect/type keyword fails validation
- [ ] Missing required key for a type (e.g., :mill without :effect/amount) fails validation
- [ ] 9 missing keys added to spec, 3 unused keys removed
- [ ] Condition (:effect/condition) allowed on any effect type
- [ ] (s/exercise ::effect) produces valid effect maps for multiple types
- [ ] Parametric test: each of 40 types individually passes validation with its minimal valid map
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] No existing tests broken

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**:effect/mana shape**: :add-mana uses :effect/mana (a map like {:black 3}), not :effect/amount. The mana spec already exists at card_spec.cljs:66 (::mana-cost). The :add-mana defmethod should require :effect/mana, not :effect/amount.

**Dynamic amounts use :effect/amount with map values**: There is NO separate :effect/dynamic-amount key. The existing :effect/amount spec (line 81) accepts (s/or :int int? :keyword keyword? :dynamic map?). A dynamic amount like {:dynamic/type :count-named-in-zone} is stored AS the :effect/amount value. Per-type specs should use the existing :effect/amount spec which already handles all variants.

**Conditional effects**: :effect/condition is orthogonal to type. Keep it as optional in every defmethod. Verified: threshold condition on Dark Ritual, destroy condition on Pernicious Deed.

**Runtime-only types**: :storm-copies is generated at runtime by engine/rules.cljs:308 — it never appears in card definitions but IS in valid-effect-types. :lose-life and :gain-life are also in the enum but currently unused. All three still need defmethods for s/exercise and completeness.

**s/exercise generators**: Multi-spec generates values for each type proportionally. Most types should work with default generators since their required fields use simple specs (keyword?, int?, map?, boolean?). Only add custom generators (s/with-gen) if s/exercise fails — start without them.

**NO :each-player or :effect/effects**: These do NOT exist in this codebase. :each-player is not in valid-effect-types. :effect/effects is not used anywhere. Do NOT create specs for them.

**Existing field specs**: Lines 79-98 already define individual field specs (:effect/type, :effect/mana, :effect/amount, etc.). These are REUSED by the multi-spec defmethods — don't duplicate them.

**:effect/target vs :effect/target-ref**: :effect/target is a keyword like :opponent, :any-player, :controller (semantic target). :effect/target-ref is a keyword like :target, :welder-bf (reference to a targeting declaration on the card). Different keys, different purposes — some effects use one, some the other, some both.

**? suffix on boolean keys**: Several effect keys end with ?: :effect/shuffle?, :effect/may-shuffle?, :effect/shuffle-remainder?, :effect/order-remainder?. In ClojureScript, these are valid keywords. The s/def must use the exact keyword including the ?.

**Unused key removal safety**: :effect/counter-type, :effect/destination, :effect/x — confirmed NOT used in any card definition, engine code, test, or runtime-generated effect. Only appear in card_spec.cljs itself. Safe to remove.

## Anti-patterns
- NO removing fields from the spec that existing cards use (check card defs + engine runtime)
- NO changing card definitions to fit the spec
- NO validating effects at execution time (only at init via validate-cards!)
- NO separate spec file — extend existing card_spec.cljs
- NO custom generators unless s/exercise fails without them (try default generators first)
- NO referencing :each-player or :effect/effects — these do not exist in this codebase
- NO removing keys without verifying they're unused in BOTH card definitions AND runtime engine code
close_reason: Already completed as part of epic fizzle-ngzw (Spec Phase 1)

title: Implement dynamic amount resolver in effects engine
description:
## What
Add a general-purpose dynamic amount resolver to `src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs` so any effect taking `:effect/amount` or `:effect/count` can accept a computation map instead of a static integer.

## Why
Accumulated Knowledge needs draw amount computed at resolution time based on graveyard contents. This generalizes to future cards (Kindle, Flame Burst, etc.).

## Implementation

### 1. Add query helper to `src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs`

```clojure
(defn count-cards-named-in-zone
  "Count objects with the given card name in the specified zone across all players."
  [db card-name zone]
  (or (d/q '[:find (count ?e) .
             :in $ ?name ?zone
             :where
             [?e :object/zone ?zone]
             [?e :object/card ?c]
             [?c :card/name ?name]]
           db card-name zone)
      0))
```

### 2. Add resolver to `src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs`

```clojure
(defmulti resolve-dynamic-impl
  (fn [_db _player-id dynamic _object-id]
    (:dynamic/type dynamic)))

(defmethod resolve-dynamic-impl :count-named-in-zone
  [db _player-id dynamic object-id]
  (let [source-card (queries/get-card db object-id)
        card-name (:card/name source-card)
        zone (:dynamic/zone dynamic)
        plus (or (:dynamic/plus dynamic) 0)]
    (+ (queries/count-cards-named-in-zone db card-name zone) plus)))

(defn resolve-dynamic-value
  "Resolve a potentially dynamic value to an integer.
   Static integers pass through. Maps with :dynamic/type are computed from game state."
  [db player-id value object-id]
  (cond
    (integer? value) value
    (map? value) (resolve-dynamic-impl db player-id value object-id)
    :else (or value 0)))
```

### 3. Update `:draw` effect to use resolver

In the `:draw` defmethod of `execute-effect-impl`, replace:
```clojure
(get effect :effect/amount 1)
```
with:
```clojure
(resolve-dynamic-value db player-id (get effect :effect/amount 1) object-id)
```

### 4. Update card spec in `src/main/fizzle/engine/card_spec.cljs`

The `:effect/amount` spec currently allows `(s/or :int int? :keyword keyword?)`. Add `:dynamic map?` alternative.

### 5. Tests in `src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs`

- Static integer amount passes through unchanged
- Dynamic map resolves count-named-in-zone correctly
- Dynamic with :dynamic/plus adds offset
- Zero cards in zone returns :dynamic/plus value
- Cards across multiple players' graveyards are counted
- Non-integer non-map values default to 0

## Verify
```
make validate
```
close_reason: Closed

title: Add player metadata EDN encoding to snapshot encoder/decoder
description: Encode per-player metadata as EDN blobs using flag bit 2. Fix the restore-yield test.
design:
## Goal
Encode player metadata (bot-archetype, is-opponent, max-hand-size) in snapshot binary format using flag bit 2 and EDN blobs. Decoder reads and merges into player maps. Restorer already handles these keys.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing EDN blob pattern
   - encoder.cljs:76-87 — encode-edn-blob writes pr-str + 16-bit length + UTF-8
   - encoder.cljs:231-237 — write-player-grants uses this for grants
   - decoder.cljs:80-89 — read-edn-blob reads the blob back
   - decoder.cljs:229,234-235 — reads grants blobs when (pos? (bit-and flags 2))

### 2. Define player metadata keys

**Include in metadata** (non-binary player properties from extractor.cljs:109-118):
   - :player/is-opponent (boolean or absent)
   - :player/bot-archetype (keyword or absent)
   - :player/max-hand-size (integer, usually 7)

**Exclude** (already encoded or transient):
   - :player/life — in binary header (8 bits)
   - :player/mana-pool — in binary header (24 bits)
   - :player/storm-count — in binary header (6 bits)
   - :player/land-plays-left — in binary header (2 bits)
   - :player/grants — already encoded via flag bit 1
   - :player/stops — loaded from localStorage, not game state
   - :player/drew-from-empty — transient state

**Nil handling**: Use dissoc to remove nil-valued keys before encoding.
   Example: player-1 has no bot-archetype → metadata = {:player/max-hand-size 7}
   Example: player-2 goldfish → metadata = {:player/is-opponent true, :player/bot-archetype :goldfish, :player/max-hand-size 7}

### 3. Encoder changes (src/main/fizzle/sharing/encoder.cljs)

**3a. Extract metadata helper** — new private function:
```clojure
(defn- extract-player-metadata
  "Extract non-binary player properties for EDN encoding.
   Removes nil values — absent keys are omitted, not nil-encoded."
  [player-state]
  (let [m (select-keys player-state
            [:player/is-opponent :player/bot-archetype :player/max-hand-size])]
    (into {} (remove (comp nil? val)) m)))
```

**3b. Write metadata helper** — after write-player-grants (~line 237):
```clojure
(defn- write-player-metadata
  "Write per-player metadata as EDN blobs."
  [w p1-meta p2-meta]
  (-> w
      (encode-edn-blob p1-meta)
      (encode-edn-blob p2-meta)))
```

**3c. Update flags computation** in encode-snapshot (line 244-256):
- Change flags from (if has-grants? 2 0) to (bit-or for both flags)
- After the grants cond->, add another cond-> for metadata
- Metadata flag: bit 2 (value 4, i.e. 0x4)

Exact change in encode-snapshot:
```clojure
(let [[p1-id p2-id] (player-ids state)
      p1-grants    (get-in state [:players p1-id :player/grants])
      p2-grants    (get-in state [:players p2-id :player/grants])
      has-grants?  (or (seq p1-grants) (seq p2-grants))
      p1-meta      (extract-player-metadata (get (:players state) p1-id))
      p2-meta      (extract-player-metadata (get (:players state) p2-id))
      has-meta?    true  ;; Always encode metadata for forward consistency
      ...
```

**3d. Update write-header** (line 196): Change flags from `(if has-grants? 2 0)` to:
```clojure
flags (cond-> 0
        has-grants? (bit-or 2)
        has-meta?   (bit-or 4))
```
This requires passing has-meta? into write-header. Update the function signature.

**3e. Append metadata after grants** in encode-snapshot writer pipeline:
```clojure
w (-> (bits/writer)
      (write-header state p1-id p2-id has-grants? has-meta?)
      (write-player-zones ...)
      (write-player-zones ...)
      (cond-> has-grants? (as-> ww (write-player-grants ww p1-grants p2-grants)))
      (cond-> has-meta?   (as-> ww (write-player-metadata ww p1-meta p2-meta))))
```

### 4. Decoder changes (src/main/fizzle/sharing/decoder.cljs)

**4a. Check flag bit 2** — in decode-snapshot after line 229:
```clojure
has-metadata? (pos? (bit-and flags 4))
```

**4b. Read metadata blobs** — after grants (line 235), before building player maps:
```clojure
p1-meta (if has-metadata? (read-edn-blob r) {})
p2-meta (if has-metadata? (read-edn-blob r) {})
```

**4c. Merge metadata into player maps** — in the players map (lines 246-258):
```clojure
{p1-id (merge p1-zones
              {:player/life ...
               :player/max-hand-size 7
               :player/grants p1-grants}
              p1-meta)  ;; <-- merge last so metadata overrides defaults
 p2-id (merge p2-zones
              {:player/life ...
               :player/max-hand-size 7
               :player/grants p2-grants}
              p2-meta)}
```
Note: p1-meta may contain :player/max-hand-size which overrides the hardcoded 7.

### 5. Tests

**5a. Fix existing restore-yield tests** (src/test/fizzle/snapshot/restore_yield_test.cljs):
   - restored-game-yields-twice-test should now pass (bot-archetype preserved)
   - restored-game-no-phantom-pending-selection-test should pass
   - director-double-yield-without-restore-test should already pass (no restore involved)
   - Remove diagnostic console.log lines from the test after confirming pass

**5b. Add encoder/decoder round-trip tests** (in snapshot/events_test.cljs or new file):
   - test: encode game with goldfish bot → decode → verify :player/bot-archetype = :goldfish on p2
   - test: encode game with burn bot → decode → verify :player/bot-archetype = :burn on p2
   - test: encode game → decode → verify :player/is-opponent = true on p2, absent on p1
   - test: encode game → decode → verify :player/max-hand-size on both players

**5c. Add backward-compat test**:
   - Create a snapshot binary WITHOUT flag bit 2 (encode with old code, or manually craft)
   - Simplest approach: take a known encoded snapshot string from existing tests, decode it, verify no error and players have default values (no bot-archetype, max-hand-size 7)

**5d. Add edge case tests**:
   - test: encode game with both flags (grants AND metadata) → decode → verify both present
   - test: encode game where player-1 has no bot properties → decode → verify empty metadata merged correctly (no :player/bot-archetype key on p1)

**5e. Run full validation**: make validate

## Success Criteria
- [ ] restored-game-yields-twice-test passes (game advances through multiple yields)
- [ ] restored-game-no-phantom-pending-selection-test passes
- [ ] director-double-yield-without-restore-test passes (baseline: non-restored games work)
- [ ] Round-trip test: :player/bot-archetype :goldfish survives encode/decode on player-2
- [ ] Round-trip test: :player/is-opponent true survives encode/decode on player-2
- [ ] Round-trip test: :player/max-hand-size survives encode/decode on both players
- [ ] Backward-compat: v1 snapshot without flag bit 2 decodes without error, players get defaults
- [ ] Edge case: both-flags (grants + metadata) encode/decode correctly
- [ ] Edge case: player-1 metadata has no bot-archetype (absent, not nil)
- [ ] All existing snapshot tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format-check + all tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**URL Length Budget**:
   - 2 EDN blobs add ~100 bytes raw → ~134 base64 chars
   - Current limit: 2000 chars. Typical snapshot: 400-800 chars
   - Budget is fine, but encode-snapshot already returns nil when over limit
   - No action needed — existing guard at encoder.cljs:255 handles overflow

**Nil Value Handling**:
   - Use (into {} (remove (comp nil? val)) m) to strip nil values from metadata
   - EDN of {:player/bot-archetype nil} would work but wastes bytes and is ambiguous
   - Absence of key = not a bot. Presence with keyword = bot archetype.

**Malformed EDN in Metadata**:
   - read-edn-blob uses cljs.reader/read-string which can throw
   - decoder.cljs already wraps everything in try-catch → returns {:error "Invalid encoding"}
   - No additional error handling needed

**Both-Flags Ordering**:
   - Binary layout must be: header → zones → grants (if flag 1) → metadata (if flag 2)
   - Decoder MUST read in same order. Flags are independent — any combination valid.
   - Test the 4 combinations: neither, grants-only, metadata-only, both

**has-meta? Always True Decision**:
   - Always set flag bit 2 and write metadata blobs (even if both maps are {})
   - Simpler: no conditional logic for "does any player have metadata?"
   - Cost: 2 × (16-bit length + "{}" = 2 bytes) = ~6 bytes. Negligible.
   - Alternative: only set when metadata non-empty. Saves 6 bytes but adds conditional.
   - Recommendation: always encode for simplicity. Can optimize later if URL length becomes tight.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO bot-specific encoding (use generic select-keys, no case on archetype)
- ❌ NO nil values in metadata maps (strip with remove before encoding)
- ❌ NO changes to restorer.cljs (it already reads these keys)
- ❌ NO new try-catch blocks in decoder (existing catch handles EDN read failures)
- ❌ NO encoding of :player/stops or :player/drew-from-empty (transient, not game state)
close_reason: Encoder writes EDN metadata blobs via flag bit 2; decoder reads and merges; all tests pass

title: Replace synchronous yield-loop/yield-all with event cascade
description:
## Goal
Replace the synchronous yield-loop (used by ::yield-all / F6) with an event cascade. Instead of a tight loop calling yield-impl up to 100 times, F6 sets auto-mode and dispatches ::yield. The event chain handles the rest, with animated transitions.

## What changes
1. yield-loop function: deleted
2. ::yield-all event: sets auto-mode (:f6 or :resolving), dispatches ::yield. Event chain continues.
3. Animated transitions: ~100ms delay between yield steps for legible animation
4. History entries: created by event handlers naturally (no manual should-create-history-entry? filtering)

## Animation mechanism
- After each yield step, if continuing, use :dispatch-later with ~100ms delay
- Or: use requestAnimationFrame-based timing
- User sees phases advancing, stack resolving in fast but legible sequence

## Key constraint  
- F6 still resolves entire opponent turn + advances to next stop
- Auto-mode (:resolving, :f6) still controls when to stop
- Stops still fire correctly (phase stops, stack stops)

## Success criteria
- make validate passes
- F6 advances through opponent turn with visible animation
- F6 stops at correct phase stop on human turn
- No yield-loop function
- No synchronous loop for yield-all
close_reason: Replaced yield-loop with event cascade. yield-all sets auto-mode and dispatches yield; yield uses dispatch-later for animated transitions. History interceptor handles entries. 200-step safety limit.

title: Bring 7 card test files to minimum test counts
description:
Add tests to files that fall below the minimum test count requirements.

## Files Below Minimum
| File | Actual | Required | Card Type |
|------|--------|----------|-----------|
| flash_of_insight_test | 3 | 12 | Flashback+X |
| dark_ritual_test | 4 | 5 | Simple |
| lotus_petal_test | 4 | 8 | Artifact/ability |
| intuition_test | 5 | 8 | Selection |
| opt_test | 5 | 8 | Selection |
| LED_test | 6 | 8 | Artifact/ability |
| ill_gotten_gains_test | 7 | 8 | Selection |

## Rules
- New tests should fill gaps in mandatory categories first (A-D)
- Then add edge cases (Category G: 2+ per card)
- Use th/ helpers for all new tests
- Follow exemplar patterns (counterspell_test, sphere_of_resistance_test)
- flash_of_insight_test needs 9 more tests — cover flashback, exile cost, X cost

title: Snapshot decoder (base64url → binary → portable map)
description:
Create snapshot/decoder.cljs that decodes a base64url string back into a portable game state map.

## What to build
Pure function: decode-snapshot(base64url-string) → game-state-map (same shape as extractor output)

- Exact inverse of encoder: reads version byte first to determine format
- Version byte selects bit widths (v1 = 6-bit card indices)
- Reads header, zones, stack in the same order encoder writes them
- EDN fallback segments decoded via cljs.reader/read-string
- Returns {:error "message"} for malformed/truncated input instead of throwing

## Error handling
- Invalid base64url → {:error "Invalid encoding"}
- Unknown version → {:error "Unsupported snapshot version N"}
- Truncated data (reader runs out of bits) → {:error "Truncated snapshot"}
- Unknown card index → {:error "Unknown card at index N"}

## Key files
- NEW: src/main/fizzle/snapshot/decoder.cljs
- USE: snapshot/card_index.cljs (index->card-id)
- USE: snapshot/encoding.cljs (BitReader, base64url-decode)

## Verification
- Round-trip with encoder: encode-snapshot(extract(db)) |> decode-snapshot == extract(db)
- Handles all error cases without throwing
- Decodes every field type: integers, booleans, keywords, EDN strings
- Version byte correctly selects format
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature: Alternate Casting System with Flashback
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Cards can define alternate casting modes via `:card/alternate-costs`
- Alternate modes specify: zone restriction, mana cost, additional costs, on-resolve behavior
- Casting system finds all valid modes for a card based on current zone
- Mode selection UI appears when multiple valid modes exist
- Flashback spells exile when leaving stack (resolve, counter, or fizzle)
- Storm count increments on flashback casts (flashback is still casting)
- Deep Analysis card implemented with correct Oracle text

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Deep Analysis castable from hand for {3}{U}, draws 2 cards
- [ ] Deep Analysis castable from graveyard for {1}{U} + 3 life, draws 2 cards
- [ ] Flashback Deep Analysis exiles after resolution
- [ ] Flashback Deep Analysis exiles if countered
- [ ] Storm count increments when casting via flashback
- [ ] Mode selection UI appears for cards with multiple valid modes from same zone
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoding Deep Analysis logic in engine (must use generic alternate cost system)
- ❌ NO separate 'flashback zone' concept (flashback cards live in graveyard, casting checks zone)
- ❌ NO flashback spells going to graveyard on counter (must exile per MTG rules)
- ❌ NO breaking existing card definitions (alternate-costs is additive)
- ❌ NO skipping storm increment for flashback casts (flashback is casting a spell)

## Approach
Implement a generic Alternate Casting System that models flashback as one instance of alternate costs. Cards define `:card/alternate-costs` with zone restrictions, costs, and resolution behavior. The casting system is extended to find all valid modes (primary + alternates) and present choices when multiple modes are available. This approach enables future cards like Gush (return Islands), Foil (discard), Fireblast (sacrifice lands) without engine changes—only new cost types.

## Architecture
### Data Model
```clojure
{:card/alternate-costs 
 [{:alternate/id :flashback
   :alternate/zone :graveyard
   :alternate/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :blue 1}
   :alternate/additional-costs [{:cost/type :pay-life :cost/amount 3}]
   :alternate/on-resolve :exile}]}
```

### Key Components
- `src/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs` - Add `get-casting-modes`, `can-cast-mode?`
- `src/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs` - Already has `:pay-life`, add cost selection flow
- `src/fizzle/engine/stack.cljs` - Track `:stack/cast-mode`, use for resolution destination
- `src/fizzle/events/` - Cast event handles mode selection, storm increment
- `src/fizzle/views/` - Mode selection UI component
- `src/fizzle/cards/` - Deep Analysis definition

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Deep Analysis requires flashback (cast from graveyard for alternate cost). The engine currently only supports casting from hand. Need a system that handles flashback AND future alternate cost cards (Gush, Foil, Fireblast, Daze) without per-card special cases.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- `rules.cljs:28` - `can-cast?` hardcodes `(= :hand (:object/zone obj))`
- `costs.cljs:167-206` - `:pay-life` cost type already implemented
- `effects.cljs:251-282` - `:draw` effect type already implemented
- `iggy_pop.cljs` - Card definition patterns established

**External:**
- Scryfall Oracle: Deep Analysis = {3}{U} sorcery, target draws 2, flashback {1}{U} pay 3 life
- MTG Rules: Flashback exiles when leaving stack for ANY reason (resolve, counter, fizzle)
- MTG Rules: Flashback casts count toward storm

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Alternate Casting Abilities ✓

**What it is:** Generic system where cards define alternate casting modes with zone restrictions, costs, and resolution effects. Casting system finds all valid modes and presents choice when multiple exist.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed card definition patterns in iggy_pop.cljs - data-driven approach
- Verified :pay-life cost already exists in costs.cljs
- Checked stack resolution flow - can add cast-mode tracking

**Pros:**
- Enables Gush, Foil, Fireblast, Daze without engine changes
- Consistent with codebase's data-driven philosophy
- Flashback is just one alternate mode type

**Cons:**
- More upfront work than minimal flashback

**Chosen because:** User confirmed future decks need Gush, Foil, etc. Investment in generic system pays off.

#### 2. Zone-Aware Casting ❌

**What it is:** Minimal extension - just make can-cast? check graveyard for flashback keyword.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler, faster for just Deep Analysis

**Investigation:**
- Would require special-casing flashback in can-cast?
- Wouldn't generalize to Gush (alternate cost from hand)

**Pros:**
- Less code initially

**Cons:**
- Doesn't handle non-flashback alternates
- Would need rewriting for Gush

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** User plans Premodern/Pauper support - Gush, Foil, Daze all need alternates from hand.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Scope permanently limited to flashback-only cards.

#### 3. Graveyard Action System ❌

**What it is:** Separate system for graveyard-castable cards.

**Why we looked at this:** Clean separation of concerns

**Investigation:**
- Would duplicate casting logic
- Storm interaction unclear

**Pros:**
- Clear separation

**Cons:**
- Code duplication
- Storm might not trigger correctly

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Duplicates casting logic, storm interaction risky.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Complete engine rewrite.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Alternate cost framework
- Deep Analysis card
- Flashback mode (zone=graveyard, on-resolve=exile)
- Mode selection UI
- Storm interaction

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Other cost types (:return-lands, :sacrifice-lands, :sacrifice-creature, :exile-from-gy)
- Recoup (grants flashback to other cards)
- Other flashback cards (Ray of Revelation, Cabal Therapy)

### Open Questions
- Exact UI styling for mode selector (decide during implementation)
- Whether to show mode picker for single-mode cards (decided: no, auto-cast)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| What is 'deep analysis'? | MTG card Deep Analysis | Implement flashback card |
| How handle flashback? | Full flashback mechanic | Need alternate casting system |
| Target player targeting? | Any player | Full rules accuracy |
| Other flashback cards? | Ray of Revelation, Recoup, Flash of Insight | System must be extensible |
| Storm interaction? | Yes, flashback counts | Ensure cast event increments storm |
| Which approach? | Approach 2 (Alternate Casting) | Generic system for Gush, Foil, etc. |
| Data model OK? | Yes | Proceed with :card/alternate-costs |
| Mode selection UX? | Yes, popover when multiple modes | Auto-cast when single mode |
| Build all cost types now? | No, framework + Deep Analysis | Incremental cost types later |
| Phase 1 scope? | Framework + Deep Analysis | Cost types in future epics |
| Edge cases (counter)? | Flashback still exiles | Handle in move-spell-off-stack |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Flashback Implementation Status
**Question explored:** Does flashback exist in codebase?
**Sources consulted:**
- rules.cljs - can-cast? implementation
- fizzle-design.md - flashback design notes
- cards/ - existing card definitions

**Findings:**
- Flashback mentioned in design doc but not implemented
- can-cast? hardcodes hand zone check
- :pay-life cost already exists
- :draw effect already exists

**Conclusion:** Need to extend casting rules, not start from scratch

#### Alternative Cost Cards in Premodern/Pauper
**Question explored:** What other cards need alternate costs?
**Sources consulted:**
- User input on planned decks
- MTG card database knowledge

**Findings:**
- Gush: return 2 Islands instead of {4}{U}
- Foil: discard Island + card instead of {2}{U}{U}
- Daze: return Island instead of {1}{U}
- Fireblast: sacrifice 2 Mountains instead of {4}{R}{R}
- Cabal Therapy: flashback by sacrificing creature

**Conclusion:** Alternate casting system must be generic, not flashback-specific

### Dead-End Paths

#### Zone-Aware Casting (Approach 1)
**Why explored:** Simpler initial implementation
**Investigation:**
- Would require special-casing flashback
- Wouldn't handle Gush (alternate from hand)

**Why abandoned:** User confirmed Gush/Foil needed - can't special-case

#### Graveyard Action System (Approach 3)
**Why explored:** Clean separation of concerns
**Investigation:**
- Would duplicate casting logic
- Storm interaction unclear

**Why abandoned:** Duplication risk, storm might break

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Build all cost types now?' → No, framework + Deep Analysis only. Cost types added when cards need them.
- 'Countered flashback spells?' → Still exile per MTG rules. Implemented in move-spell-off-stack.
- 'Remand-style effects?' → Deferred, not in Premodern. Can add full replacement effect later if needed.
close_reason: Closed

title: Extend sacrifice-permanent with power tracking and ability activation path
description: Build the shared infrastructure both Fling and Altar of Dementia need: a sacrifice cost that presents a selection of creatures you control, sacrifices the chosen one, stores its power on the stack item, and provides a dynamic value type to read it.
design:
## Goal
Extend the EXISTING sacrifice-permanent cost infrastructure (built for Tinker) with two missing capabilities:
1. Store sacrificed creature's power on the stack item for use by effects
2. Add ability activation code path for interactive sacrifice costs (Altar of Dementia needs this)

## Existing Infrastructure (DO NOT REBUILD)
These already work — study them, don't recreate:
- `can-pay? :sacrifice-permanent` — engine/costs.cljs:436
- `pay-cost :sacrifice-permanent` — engine/costs.cljs:448 (returns db unchanged, defers to selection)
- `build-sacrifice-permanent-selection` — events/selection/costs.cljs:232
- `execute-confirmed-selection :sacrifice-permanent-cost` — events/selection/costs.cljs:463
- `evaluate-pre-cast-step :sacrifice-permanent-cost` — events/casting.cljs:72
- Pre-cast pipeline includes `:sacrifice-permanent-cost` — events/casting.cljs:41
- Tests for generic criteria (including creature): tinker_test.cljs:244-261

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns (DO FIRST)
Read these files to understand the flows:
- events/selection/costs.cljs:232-266 — sacrifice-permanent builder
- events/selection/costs.cljs:463-475 — sacrifice-permanent executor
- events/abilities.cljs:168-189 — activate-validated-ability (3 paths: targeting, generic mana, direct)
- events/abilities.cljs:129-140 — pay-costs-and-create-stack-item
- engine/effects.cljs:25-38 — resolve-dynamic-value + :count-named-in-zone
- engine/creatures.cljs:21-37 — effective-power (base + counters + grants + static)
- db/schema.cljs:84-97 — existing stack-item attributes

### 2. Add schema attribute (db/schema.cljs)
Add `:stack-item/sacrifice-info` attribute to schema. This stores a map like `{:power 3}`.
Follow pattern of existing stack-item attributes (line 84-97).
Type: plain value (not ref), stores EDN map.

### 3. Modify sacrifice executor to capture power (events/selection/costs.cljs:463-475)
The executor currently just moves to graveyard. Modify to:
1. Read `creatures/effective-power` of the selected permanent BEFORE moving to graveyard
2. Move permanent to graveyard (existing behavior)
3. Find the stack item for `:selection/spell-id` and store sacrifice info on it:
   `[:db/add stack-item-eid :stack-item/sacrifice-info {:power captured-power}]`
4. Continue with existing chaining/finalized logic

**CRITICAL TIMING**: Must read effective-power BEFORE the move-to-zone call, because after graveyard move the creature's battlefield grants/static abilities may no longer apply.

**Edge case**: If sacrificed permanent is not a creature (future extensibility), store `:power nil`.

### 4. Disable auto-confirm on sacrifice selection (events/selection/costs.cljs:263)
Change `:selection/auto-confirm? true` to `:selection/auto-confirm? false`.
Design decision: player always picks creature, even with only one option.

### 5. Add :sacrificed-power dynamic value (engine/effects.cljs)
Add new `resolve-dynamic-impl` defmethod for `:sacrificed-power`:
```clojure
(defmethod resolve-dynamic-impl :sacrificed-power
  [db _player-id _dynamic object-id]
  ;; object-id is the stack-item source. Find the stack item that has this source.
  ;; Read :stack-item/sacrifice-info -> :power
  ...)
```
**IMPORTANT**: `object-id` in resolve-dynamic-value is the SOURCE object's UUID (from `:stack-item/source`). Need to find the stack item that references this source to read sacrifice-info. Study how `:stack-item/chosen-x` is accessed for the pattern.

### 6. Add ability activation path for interactive sacrifice (events/abilities.cljs)
`activate-validated-ability` (line 168-189) has 3 paths: targeting, generic mana, direct.
None handles interactive costs like sacrifice-permanent.

Add a 4th path BEFORE the direct cost payment path:
1. Check if ability cost contains `:sacrifice-permanent` key
2. If yes: pay non-sacrifice costs first (tap, pay-life, etc. via `pay-all-costs` on remaining costs)
3. Build sacrifice-permanent selection (reuse `build-sacrifice-permanent-selection` from costs.cljs)
4. Return `{:db db-after-non-sac-costs :pending-selection sacrifice-selection}`
5. The selection must carry ability context so the executor can create the stack item after confirmation

**INTEGRATION GAP**: The sacrifice-permanent-cost executor (costs.cljs:463) currently calls `rules/cast-spell-mode` for the finalized path. For ability activation, it needs to create a stack item instead. Two approaches:
- A) Store `:selection/source-type :ability` on selection, branch in executor
- B) Create a separate `:sacrifice-permanent-ability-cost` selection type

Study how mana-allocation handles this: `activate-ability-with-generic-mana` (line 143-165) sets `:selection/source-type :ability` and `:selection/ability` on the selection. The mana-allocation executor then checks source-type to determine whether to cast a spell or create an ability stack item. Follow this same pattern.

### 7. Tests (TDD — write failing tests first)

**Power tracking tests (NEW — these don't exist):**
- test: sacrifice creature with base power 3 → sacrifice-info has {:power 3}
- test: sacrifice creature with +1/+1 counter → sacrifice-info has effective power (base + counter)
- test: sacrifice creature with threshold modifier (Nimble Mongoose 3/3) → sacrifice-info has 3
- test: sacrifice 0-power creature → sacrifice-info has {:power 0}

**Dynamic value tests (NEW):**
- test: :sacrificed-power resolves to stored power value from sacrifice-info
- test: :sacrificed-power resolves to 0 when sacrifice-info has {:power 0}

**Ability activation path tests (NEW):**
- test: ability with sacrifice-permanent cost shows selection modal
- test: confirming sacrifice in ability context creates stack item (not casts spell)
- test: ability sacrifice stores power on stack item
- test: cannot activate ability with sacrifice cost when no matching creatures

**Auto-confirm tests (MODIFY):**
- test: sacrifice selection shows modal even with exactly one creature (auto-confirm disabled)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Sacrifice executor captures effective-power BEFORE graveyard move and stores on stack item as :stack-item/sacrifice-info
- [ ] :sacrificed-power dynamic value correctly reads stored power from stack item
- [ ] Ability activation with sacrifice-permanent cost shows selection, then creates stack item after confirmation
- [ ] Auto-confirm disabled: sacrifice selection always shows modal
- [ ] Power tracking works with modifiers (counters, grants, static abilities)
- [ ] Edge case: 0-power creature stores {:power 0}, not nil
- [ ] All existing Tinker tests still pass (no regression)
- [ ] All tests passing (make validate)

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ DO NOT rebuild existing sacrifice-permanent infrastructure — extend it
- ❌ DO NOT store sacrifice info outside immutable game state (no atoms)
- ❌ DO NOT read effective-power AFTER graveyard move — grants/statics may not apply
- ❌ DO NOT create separate executor for ability sacrifice — reuse existing executor with source-type branching
- ❌ DO NOT break existing Tinker sacrifice flow — it must continue to work

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Timing of power capture**
effective-power uses grants and static abilities which may be zone-dependent. Power MUST be captured while creature is still on battlefield, before move-to-zone.

**Edge Case: 0 or negative power**
Creatures can have 0 power (tokens, base 0/X creatures). Negative power from -1/-1 counters is possible. Store the actual computed value — effects may clamp to 0 themselves.

**Edge Case: Non-creature sacrifice (future)**
Infrastructure supports sacrificing any permanent type. If sacrificed permanent isn't a creature, effective-power returns nil. Store {:power nil} — effects that use :sacrificed-power should handle nil gracefully.

**Integration: Ability vs Spell executor path**
The executor must check :selection/source-type to determine post-confirmation behavior:
- nil or :spell → existing path (cast-spell-mode or chaining)
- :ability → create stack item with stored effects, NOT call cast-spell-mode

Follow the mana-allocation pattern at abilities.cljs:143-165 which already handles this source-type distinction.

**Regression Risk: Tinker**
Tinker uses sacrifice-permanent but does NOT need power tracking (it tutors, doesn't use power). Adding sacrifice-info to the executor must not break Tinker's flow. Tinker doesn't read sacrifice-info, so storing it is safe.

## Effort Estimate
~6 hours: 1h study + 1h schema/executor + 1h dynamic value + 2h ability path + 1h tests
close_reason: Closed

title: Add HIGH priority missing corner case tests
design:
## Goal
Add tests for the highest-risk missing corner cases identified during analysis.

## HIGH Priority Corner Cases

### 1. Kicked spell resolution (engine/rules)
Test resolve-spell with a kicked mode to verify kicked effects execute end-to-end.
Currently untested despite kicker being a core mechanic (Orim's Chant).
File: engine/rules_test.cljs

### 2. Brain Freeze mill opponent with empty library (cards/)
Test that milling an opponent whose library is empty triggers the loss condition.
Brain Freeze is a win condition - this edge case MUST be covered.
File: cards/brain_freeze_test.cljs

### 3. resolve-all when first item needs selection (events/)
Test that resolve-all correctly stops when the very first item requires player selection.
File: events/resolve_all_test.cljs

### 4. grant-expired? with nil :grant/expires (engine/grants)
Likely NPE in production. Test and fix if needed.
File: engine/grants_test.cljs

### 5. History interceptor with real Datascript db (history/)
Replace keyword mock dbs with real Datascript for description generation tests.
File: history/interceptor_test.cljs

### 6. Ill-Gotten Gains opponent empty graveyard (cards/)
Test behavior when opponent has nothing to return.
File: cards/ill_gotten_gains_test.cljs

### 7. Replace history integration test
Replace the tautological test with a real integration test: init -> first entry -> step-back -> step-forward -> fork -> switch-branch.
File: history/integration_test.cljs

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 7 corner cases have tests
- [ ] Tests follow TDD (write failing test first)
- [ ] Each test documents the specific bug it catches
- [ ] make validate passes
close_reason: Closed

title: Update architecture model (.c4) and documentation
description: Update bots.c4: Turn Driver description to remove stale event-handler references. Update bots.md to reflect director inline architecture (not handler-based). Verify db_effect.cljs register! docstring (investigation says already correct). Verify model.c4 edge direction (investigation says already correct). Run make validate.
design:
## Goal

Remove all references to the deleted event-dispatch architecture from the bot architecture documentation and model files. The dead handlers (::bot-decide, ::bot-action-complete) were removed in fizzle-dt46 and the module was renamed in fizzle-0m8h — the docs still describe the old architecture.

## Effort Estimate

1-2 hours. String edits in two documentation files + two verification checks.

## Files to Change

### 1. docs/arch/components/bots.c4 — Turn Driver description (line 39)

**Before:**
```
bot_interceptor = component 'Turn Driver' {
  description 'Registers ::bot-decide and ::bot-action-complete re-frame event handlers. Provides bot-decide-action as a pure function called by the director inline during run-to-decision. The event handlers also fire via the db_effect chokepoint for compound actions (tap+cast sequences requiring re-frame dispatch).'
```

**After:**
```
bot_interceptor = component 'Turn Driver' {
  description 'Pure computation functions called inline by the director. Provides bot-should-act? (checks if priority holder is a bot), bot-decide-action (asks IBot protocol for an action plan), and find-tap-sequence (allocates mana-producing lands to pay a cost). No re-frame event handlers — the director applies the resulting actions through the standard event pipeline.'
```

Also update the top-level bots component description (line 6):

**Before:**
```
description 'Bot system for opponent simulation — IBot multimethod protocol dispatching on archetype, bot definitions (goldfish, burn), behavior rules, global re-frame interceptor driving bot turns. Bots dispatch the same events as human players.'
```

**After:**
```
description 'Bot system for opponent simulation — IBot multimethod protocol dispatching on archetype, bot definitions (goldfish, burn), behavior rules, pure decision functions called inline by the director. Bots dispatch the same events as human players through the standard event pipeline.'
```

### 2. docs/arch/components/bots.md — Full rewrite of stale sections

**Before (line 5, Responsibility section):**
```
Bot system that simulates opponent behavior for practice scenarios. The system is built around an `IBot` multimethod protocol dispatching on bot archetype (goldfish, burn). Bot definitions declare deck composition, starting hand, and behavior rules. `bots/interceptor.cljs` registers `::bot-decide` and `::bot-action-complete` re-frame event handlers that fire via the `db_effect` chokepoint for compound actions (tap+cast sequences). It also exposes `bot-decide-action` as a pure function called inline by `events/director.cljs` during `run-to-decision`. Bots dispatch the same re-frame events a human player would dispatch — no parallel engine paths.
```

**After:**
```
Bot system that simulates opponent behavior for practice scenarios. The system is built around an `IBot` multimethod protocol dispatching on bot archetype (goldfish, burn). Bot definitions declare deck composition, starting hand, and behavior rules. `bots/decisions.cljs` exposes pure decision functions (`bot-should-act?`, `bot-decide-action`, `find-tap-sequence`) called inline by `events/director.cljs` during `run-to-decision`. Bots dispatch the same re-frame events a human player would dispatch — no parallel engine paths. No re-frame event handlers are registered by the bot system; the director applies all bot actions through the standard event pipeline.
```

**Before (IN section, lines 12-13):**
```
- Calls to `bot-decide-action` from `events/director` during `run-to-decision` (inline, synchronous)
- `::bot-decide` re-frame events queued by `db_effect` after game-db mutations where a bot holds priority
```

**After:**
```
- Calls to `bot-should-act?`, `bot-decide-action`, `find-tap-sequence` from `events/director` during `run-to-decision` (inline, synchronous, pure)
```

**Before (OUT section, lines 18-20):**
```
- `bot-decide-action` return: `{:action :cast-spell :object-id :target :tap-sequence}` or `{:action :pass}`
- re-frame event dispatches for compound actions: `::activate-mana-ability`, `::cast-spell`, `::bot-action-complete` sentinel
- No direct db writes — all state changes go through the event system
```

**After:**
```
- `bot-decide-action` return: `{:action :cast-spell :object-id :target :tap-sequence}` or `{:action :pass}`
- No direct db writes — the director applies action results through the standard event pipeline
```

### 3. docs/arch/model.c4 — Verify only (no change needed)

Line 24: `bots -> events_game 'dispatches same casting, land-play, and priority events as human players; director calls bot-decide-action inline'`

This is accurate. The director (in events_game module) does call bot-decide-action inline. Edge direction bots→events_game is correct (bots is a dependency of events_game/director). **No change needed.**

### 4. src/main/fizzle/events/db_effect.cljs — Verify only (no change needed)

The `register!` docstring says "Register the custom :db effect handler... Overrides re-frame's default :db effect with the SBA+bot chokepoint." The "bot" reference is in "SBA+bot chokepoint" — this is still technically accurate since the db effect handler no longer queues ::bot-decide (that was removed in fizzle-dt46). The docstring should be updated to remove the misleading "bot" reference.

**Before (line 47):**
```
   Overrides re-frame's default :db effect with the SBA+bot chokepoint."
```

**After:**
```
   Overrides re-frame's default :db effect with game-db SBA processing."
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] grep -n 'bot-decide\|bot-action-complete\|interceptor.cljs' docs/arch/components/bots.c4 returns 0 results
- [ ] grep -n 'interceptor.cljs\|::bot-decide\|::bot-action-complete' docs/arch/components/bots.md returns 0 results
- [ ] grep -n 'SBA+bot' src/main/fizzle/events/db_effect.cljs returns 0 results
- [ ] make validate passes: 0 failures, 0 errors, lint clean, fmt clean
- [ ] Test count unchanged (3134 — doc changes don't affect tests)

## Implementation Checklist

- [ ] Read docs/arch/components/bots.c4 lines 1-52 (already done in review)
- [ ] Edit bots.c4 top-level description: remove 'global re-frame interceptor driving bot turns'
- [ ] Edit bots.c4 bot_interceptor Turn Driver description: replace full description with pure-functions text
- [ ] Read docs/arch/components/bots.md lines 1-25 (already done in review)
- [ ] Edit bots.md Responsibility section: replace interceptor.cljs + ::bot-decide references
- [ ] Edit bots.md IN section: remove ::bot-decide line, update function list
- [ ] Edit bots.md OUT section: remove ::bot-action-complete line
- [ ] Edit src/main/fizzle/events/db_effect.cljs line 47: remove 'bot' from docstring
- [ ] grep -n 'bot-decide\|bot-action-complete\|interceptor.cljs' docs/arch/ — confirm 0 results
- [ ] grep -n 'SBA+bot' src/ — confirm 0 results
- [ ] make fmt (run to ensure fmt-check passes)
- [ ] make validate — confirm 0 failures, lint clean, test count 3134

## Key Considerations

**model.c4 edge direction is already correct**:
Line 24 reads `bots -> events_game` (bots depends on events_game). The investigation already confirmed this. Do NOT change model.c4.

**db_effect.cljs 'SBA+bot chokepoint' is subtly misleading**:
The 'bot' in 'SBA+bot chokepoint' originally meant the handler queued ::bot-decide after each game-db mutation. That dispatch was removed in fizzle-dt46. The handler now only runs SBAs. Update the docstring to remove the stale 'bot' reference.

**bots.c4 has two stale descriptions**:
Both the top-level `bots` component description AND the `bot_interceptor` (Turn Driver) subcomponent description reference the old event-handler architecture. Both need updating.

**bots.md IN/OUT sections both reference dead handlers**:
The IN section lists `::bot-decide re-frame events queued by db_effect` — dead since fizzle-dt46.
The OUT section lists `::bot-action-complete sentinel` dispatches — also dead.
Both must be removed.

**No .c4 syntax errors**:
.c4 files use LikeC4 syntax. The description field is a quoted string. When editing, keep the single-quote delimiters intact and don't introduce unescaped quotes inside the description.

## Anti-patterns

- Do not change model.c4 (edge direction is already correct — verify only)
- Do not add new architecture decisions to .c4 files (this is cleanup only)
- Do not rename bot_interceptor component in bots.c4 to bot_decisions — the component ID is internal to the model and changing IDs breaks references in view files; only the description text changes
- Do not leave any reference to ::bot-decide, ::bot-action-complete, or bots/interceptor.cljs in the documentation files

title: Implement Tinker with sacrifice-permanent cost and tutor-to-battlefield
description: First card: introduces the two most reusable pieces of new infrastructure (sacrifice-permanent cost type + tutor-to-battlefield effect extension), then wires Tinker as a pure data card definition.
design:
## Goal
Implement Tinker (Sorcery {2}{U}) and the generic sacrifice-permanent cost infrastructure it requires. Tutor-to-battlefield already works (execute-tutor-selection supports :selection/target-zone :battlefield).

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns (30 min)
- Cost multimethods: engine/costs.cljs — can-pay? and pay-cost dispatch on (first (keys cost))
- Selection-based costs: events/selection/costs.cljs — builders (build-exile-cards-selection, build-return-land-selection, build-discard-specific-selection) + executors
- Pre-cast pipeline: events/game.cljs line 68-72 — ordered vector of step keywords
- evaluate-pre-cast-step: events/game.cljs — multimethod, returns nil (skip), {:selection sel} (pause), or {:db db} (done)
- Existing cost detection: has-exile-cards-x-cost?, has-return-land-cost?, has-discard-specific-cost?
- Tutor system: events/selection/library.cljs — build-tutor-selection already passes :selection/target-zone from effect; execute-tutor-selection already handles :battlefield zone

### 2. Implement sacrifice-permanent cost type (generic) — engine/costs.cljs
Add two defmethods:
- (defmethod can-pay? :sacrifice-permanent [db object-id cost]) — check player controls at least one permanent matching criteria in (:sacrifice-permanent cost). Use db/query to find battlefield permanents matching {:match/types #{:artifact}} or any criteria map. Return boolean.
- (defmethod pay-cost :sacrifice-permanent [db object-id cost]) — return db unchanged (selection-based cost, actual movement deferred to executor)

### 3. Implement sacrifice-permanent-cost selection — events/selection/costs.cljs
Add detection function:
- (has-sacrifice-permanent-cost? mode) — check if mode's cost map contains :sacrifice-permanent key

Add builder:
- (build-sacrifice-permanent-selection game-db player-id object-id mode sac-cost) — returns selection state:
  - :selection/type :sacrifice-permanent-cost
  - :selection/lifecycle :chaining (must chain to subsequent pipeline steps like :mana-allocation)
  - :selection/candidates — set of object-ids of battlefield permanents matching criteria
  - :selection/select-count 1
  - :selection/exact? true
  - :selection/validation :exact
  - :selection/auto-confirm? false
  - :selection/player-id, :selection/spell-id, :selection/mode (carry forward)
  
Add executor:
- (defmethod core/execute-confirmed-selection :sacrifice-permanent-cost [game-db selection]) — sacrifice selected permanent (zones/move-to-zone to :graveyard), return {:db updated-db} for chaining

Add chain builder:
- Register build-chain-selection for :sacrifice-permanent-cost — use derive hierarchy to chain to next pipeline step (targeting or mana-allocation). Follow pattern from return-land-cost chaining (:pre-cast-cost-to-targeting hierarchy).

### 4. Wire into pre-cast pipeline — events/game.cljs
- Add :sacrifice-permanent-cost to pipeline vector (before :targeting): [:exile-cards-cost :return-land-cost :discard-specific-cost :sacrifice-permanent-cost :pay-x-life :x-mana-cost :targeting :mana-allocation]
- Add evaluate-pre-cast-step defmethod for :sacrifice-permanent-cost:
  (defmethod evaluate-pre-cast-step :sacrifice-permanent-cost [_ ctx]
    (when (sel-costs/has-sacrifice-permanent-cost? (:mode ctx))
      (let [sac-cost (:sacrifice-permanent (:cost mode))
            sel (sel-costs/build-sacrifice-permanent-selection ...)]
        (when sel {:selection sel}))))

### 5. Define Tinker card — cards/blue/tinker.cljs
{:card/id :tinker
 :card/name "Tinker"
 :card/cmc 3
 :card/mana-cost {:blue 1 :colorless 2}
 :card/colors #{:blue}
 :card/types #{:sorcery}
 :card/text "As an additional cost to cast this spell, sacrifice an artifact.\nSearch your library for an artifact card, put that card onto the battlefield, then shuffle."
 :card/additional-cost {:sacrifice-permanent {:match/types #{:artifact}}}
 :card/effects [{:effect/type :tutor
                 :effect/criteria {:match/types #{:artifact}}
                 :effect/target-zone :battlefield}]}
Register in cards/registry.cljs (add require + concat).

### 6. Write tests (TDD) — test/fizzle/cards/blue/tinker_test.cljs

## Success Criteria
- [ ] sacrifice-permanent cost generic: can-pay? and pay-cost work with ANY criteria map (not just #{:artifact})
- [ ] sacrifice-permanent-cost selection presents only permanents matching criteria
- [ ] Pre-cast pipeline includes :sacrifice-permanent-cost step
- [ ] Chaining works: sacrifice cost -> mana allocation -> cast
- [ ] Tinker card definition matches Scryfall oracle text exactly (all fields verified with exact values)
- [ ] Tinker happy path: cast with artifact sacrifice, tutor artifact to battlefield, library shuffled
- [ ] Tinker cannot-cast: no artifacts on battlefield -> can-cast? false
- [ ] Tinker cannot-cast: insufficient mana -> can-cast? false
- [ ] Tinker storm count increments on cast
- [ ] Tinker edge: fail-to-find (no artifacts in library) -> shuffles without moving card
- [ ] Tinker edge: sacrificed artifact is the only one (no self-target since it's gone during resolution)
- [ ] Tinker edge: token artifact can be sacrificed as cost (token ceases to exist in graveyard)
- [ ] Min 8 tests total (selection spell per CLAUDE.md)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Specific Test Specifications
1. test-card-definition — verify ALL fields exact: :card/id :tinker, :card/name "Tinker", :card/cmc 3, :card/mana-cost {:blue 1 :colorless 2}, etc.
2. test-cast-and-resolve-happy-path — add Lotus Petal to battlefield + artifact to library, cast Tinker sacrificing Petal, resolve, verify artifact moved from library to battlefield, library shuffled
3. test-cannot-cast-no-artifacts — no artifacts on battlefield, verify can-cast? returns false
4. test-cannot-cast-insufficient-mana — has artifact but not {2}{U}, verify can-cast? returns false
5. test-cannot-cast-wrong-zone — Tinker in graveyard/library, verify can-cast? returns false
6. test-storm-count-increments — cast Tinker, verify storm count increased by 1
7. test-sacrifice-cost-selection — multiple artifacts on battlefield, verify selection presents all of them
8. test-fail-to-find — empty library or no artifacts in library, player finds nothing, library shuffled
9. test-sacrifice-permanent-generic-criteria — test cost with different criteria (e.g., {:match/types #{:creature}}) to verify genericity
10. test-chaining-sacrifice-to-mana-allocation — verify sacrifice cost chains properly to mana allocation step for Tinker's {2}{U} cost

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN for this task)
- ❌ NO card-name checks in engine/costs.cljs or selection code (generic by criteria, not by card)
- ❌ NO reimplementing tutor-to-battlefield (already works — just use :effect/target-zone :battlefield in card def)
- ❌ NO tautological test assertions (exact values only, no some?/string?/true?)
- ❌ NO manual selection construction in tests (use th/cast-and-resolve, th/confirm-selection, production path helpers)
- ❌ NO modifying execute-tutor-selection (already supports :battlefield)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Token Artifacts**
- Tokens can be sacrificed as additional costs (valid MTG rule)
- Token ceases to exist when moved to graveyard (zone-move handles this — move-to-zone works for tokens)
- Verify no side effects from token disappearing

**Edge Case: Fail-to-Find**
- Player may choose not to find an artifact (legal in MTG for 'search' effects)
- Existing tutor handles empty selection: shuffles without moving
- Verify Tinker's cost (sacrifice) is still paid even on fail-to-find (cost is paid at cast time, effect at resolution)

**Edge Case: Mana-then-Sacrifice Ordering**
- Player might tap an artifact for mana, then sacrifice a different artifact as cost
- Or sacrifice the artifact they would have tapped for mana
- can-pay? must check mana availability AND artifact availability independently
- The mana payment and sacrifice are separate cost components

**Pipeline Chaining**
- After sacrifice-permanent-cost selection is confirmed, pipeline must continue to subsequent steps
- Tinker has {2}{U} which may need mana-allocation (for the 2 colorless)
- Selection lifecycle must be :chaining, not :finalized
- Study return-land-cost chaining pattern for reference

**Reference Implementation**
- Closest analog: return-land-cost (selection-based pre-cast cost with chaining)
- Study events/selection/costs.cljs build-return-land-selection for builder pattern
- Study events/game.cljs evaluate-pre-cast-step :return-land-cost for pipeline wiring
close_reason: Closed
