title: Phase 4 Epic 3: Deck Import (Moxfield/MTGGoldfish)
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Import modal with name field + textarea for pasting/editing decklists
- Parser handles Moxfield and MTGGoldfish text export formats
- Case-insensitive exact card name matching against implemented card registry
- Import fails if ANY cards are unrecognized — clear list of missing card names shown
- Successful import populates active setup state AND saves as named deck in dropdown
- Edit flow: select imported deck → click Edit → modal reopens with regenerated formatted text
- Delete imported decks from dropdown
- // comment notation supported in parser (stripped during parse, regenerated for display)
- Imported decks persisted to localStorage
- Canonical text regeneration with type grouping, section headers, and card counts
- Import button disabled until both name and text provided

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Can paste a Moxfield text export and import a valid deck
- [ ] Can paste an MTGGoldfish text export and import a valid deck
- [ ] Unimplemented cards block import with clear list of missing card names
- [ ] Sideboard section parsed correctly (Sideboard / Sideboard: / SB: prefix)
- [ ] Set codes and collector numbers stripped (Moxfield format)
- [ ] Imported deck appears in deck dropdown and is selectable
- [ ] Imported deck populates main/side panels on import
- [ ] Can edit an imported deck (modal reopens with formatted text)
- [ ] Can delete an imported deck from dropdown
- [ ] Regenerated text has // comments with type grouping and card counts
- [ ] Blank lines and // comments ignored during parsing
- [ ] Case-insensitive card name matching works
- [ ] Imported decks persist across browser refreshes via localStorage
- [ ] Import modal has name field + textarea + validation results + import/cancel
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] make validate passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO silent card dropping (import MUST fail if any card unrecognized)
- NO fuzzy matching (exact case-insensitive only, no ambiguity)
- NO API calls to Moxfield/MTGGoldfish (text paste only, URL import is future epic)
- NO card-level deck builder UI (textarea IS the editor)
- NO editing built-in Iggy Pop deck (read-only, only imported decks editable)
- NO storing raw paste text (store parsed data, regenerate canonical text)

## Approach

### Parser Design
Core parser handles the universal MTG decklist text format:
1. Split text into lines
2. Strip // comments and blank lines
3. Detect sideboard boundary (case-insensitive: "Sideboard", "Sideboard:", or "SB:" prefix on individual lines)
4. For each card line: extract quantity + card name, strip optional (SET) COLLECTOR# suffix
5. Case-insensitive exact match card name against card registry (cards/all-cards)
6. If ANY cards unrecognized → fail with list of missing card names
7. If all valid → return {:deck/main [...] :deck/side [...]} in standard format

### Text Regeneration
When editing an imported deck, regenerate clean formatted text:
```
// Deck Name - Main Deck (60)

// Lands (15)
4 Polluted Delta
3 Island

// Instants (20)
4 Dark Ritual
4 Cabal Ritual

// Sorceries (16)
4 Careful Study

// Artifacts (9)
4 Lotus Petal

Sideboard
// 15 cards
2 Merchant Scroll
```

### Import Modal Flow
1. User clicks "Import" button next to deck dropdown on setup screen
2. Modal opens with: deck name text field, textarea for paste/edit, import/cancel buttons
3. On import attempt: parse text → validate cards → show errors OR succeed
4. On success: save deck to localStorage, add to dropdown, populate setup state, close modal
5. Edit flow: select imported deck → click Edit → modal reopens with regenerated text, name pre-filled

### Storage
Imported decks stored in localStorage as EDN alongside presets. Each imported deck stored as:
```clojure
{:deck/id :imported-my-deck-name  ; keyword from slugified name
 :deck/name "My Deck Name"       ; user-provided name
 :deck/main [{:card/id :dark-ritual :count 4} ...]
 :deck/side [{:card/id :merchant-scroll :count 2} ...]
 :deck/source :imported}          ; distinguishes from built-in decks
```

### Integration Points
- views/setup.cljs: Add "Import" and "Edit" buttons near deck dropdown
- New views/import_modal.cljs: Import/edit modal component
- New engine/deck_parser.cljs: Pure parser functions (no side effects)
- events/setup.cljs: New handlers for import, edit, delete imported decks
- subs/setup.cljs: Updated ::available-decks to include imported decks
- storage.cljs: New functions for imported deck persistence

## Dependencies
- Depends on Epic 2 (Setup Screen) for UI integration point

## Scope Boundaries
In scope: Text parsing, card validation, import modal, edit flow, delete, localStorage persistence, setup screen integration, canonical text regeneration
Out of scope: URL import from Moxfield/MTGGoldfish (future epic), card-level deck builder UI, Scryfall API, implementing new cards, fuzzy matching, editing built-in decks

## Design Discovery

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | Decision | Rationale |
|----------|----------|-----------|
| Unimplemented cards | Import fails, shows missing list | Surfaces what cards to build next |
| Import destination | Populates setup + saves as named deck | Best of both worlds |
| Naming | Required name field before import | Explicit, no auto-detection complexity |
| UI location | Modal dialog | Clean separation, good for paste-validate-fix cycle |
| Deck editability | Re-open modal with regenerated text | Textarea IS the editor, no deck builder needed |
| Card matching | Exact, case-insensitive | No ambiguity from fuzzy matching |
| Text storage | Store parsed data, regenerate text | Canonical format, no drift between text and data |
| Comments | // notation, stripped on parse, regenerated for display | Helps user orient when editing |
| Formats supported | Moxfield + MTGGoldfish text export | Covers primary sources, URL import deferred |
| Built-in deck editing | Read-only | Only imported decks are editable |

### Format Support
Both Moxfield and MTGGoldfish use near-identical text format:
- QTY CARDNAME (both)
- Optional (SET) COLLECTOR# suffix (Moxfield only, strip and ignore)
- Sideboard header: "Sideboard" or "Sideboard:" (case-insensitive)
- SB: prefix on individual lines (Moxfield alternative)
- Blank lines ignored
close_reason: All 3 child tasks complete. Deck import with Moxfield/MTGGoldfish text parsing, import modal, localStorage persistence, edit/delete flows all implemented and working.

title: Build deck text parser with card name validation
description:
Pure parser module at engine/deck_parser.cljs. No side effects, no re-frame dependency.

## What to Build

### Card Name Registry
Build a name-lookup map from cards/all-cards: lowercase card name → card-id keyword.
Example: {"dark ritual" :dark-ritual, "lions eye diamond" :lions-eye-diamond, ...}

### Line Parser
Parse a single line into {:card/id :count} or nil (comment/blank/header):
- Strip // comments (everything after // on a line)
- Skip blank lines
- Detect sideboard headers: case-insensitive match on "sideboard" or "sideboard:" (entire line)
- Detect SB: prefix on individual lines (Moxfield alternative)
- Extract quantity + card name from format: `QTY CARDNAME` where QTY is digits
- Strip optional (SET) COLLECTOR# suffix from card name (Moxfield format)
- Trim whitespace from card name
- Case-insensitive exact match against name-lookup

### Full Parser
parse-decklist function: text string → result map
- Returns {:ok {:deck/main [...] :deck/side [...]}} on success
- Returns {:error {:unrecognized [list of card name strings]}} on any unrecognized cards
- Lines before first sideboard header go to main, lines after go to side
- Consolidate duplicate card names (sum counts)

### Text Regenerator
deck->text function: deck map + card-lookup → formatted string
- Groups cards by type using card/types from card definitions
- Type order: Lands, Instants, Sorceries, Artifacts, Creatures, Enchantments, Other
- Format:
```
// Deck Name - Main Deck (60)
//
// Lands (15)
4 Polluted Delta
3 Island
//
// Instants (20)
4 Dark Ritual

Sideboard
// 15 cards
2 Merchant Scroll
```

## Files
- NEW: src/main/fizzle/engine/deck_parser.cljs
- NEW: src/test/fizzle/engine/deck_parser_test.cljs

## Verification
- make validate passes
- Parser handles both Moxfield and MTGGoldfish formats
- Unrecognized cards collected and returned in error
- Text regeneration round-trips: parse(generate(deck)) == deck
close_reason: Closed

title: Add imported deck storage and setup event handlers
description:
Extend storage.cljs and events/setup.cljs to support imported decks.

## Storage (storage.cljs)

Add two new functions following existing patterns:

```clojure
(defn save-imported-decks! [decks]
  ;; Persist map to localStorage["fizzle-imported-decks"] as EDN
  ;; decks is {deck-id-keyword deck-map}
  )

(defn load-imported-decks []
  ;; Load map from localStorage. Returns {} on missing/corrupt.
  )
```

Each deck stored as:
```clojure
{:deck/id :my-storm-deck      ;; slugified from user name
 :deck/name "My Storm Deck"   ;; original user-provided name
 :deck/main [{:card/id :dark-ritual :count 4} ...]
 :deck/side [{:card/id :merchant-scroll :count 2} ...]
 :deck/source :imported}       ;; distinguishes from built-in
```

## Setup Events (events/setup.cljs)

### New state keys
- :setup/imported-decks — map of {deck-id deck-map}, loaded from localStorage on init
- :setup/import-modal — nil (closed) or {:name "" :text "" :errors nil :editing-deck-id nil}

### New handlers (pure functions, testable)

1. init-setup-handler — MODIFY: load imported-decks from storage on init, merge into state

2. select-deck-handler — MODIFY: extend beyond case :iggy-pop to also look up deck-id in :setup/imported-decks map. If found, load its main/side.

3. open-import-modal-handler [db] — set :setup/import-modal to {:name "" :text "" :errors nil :editing-deck-id nil}

4. open-edit-modal-handler [db deck-id] — set :setup/import-modal with name/text pre-populated from existing imported deck. Uses deck_parser/deck->text to regenerate text. Sets :editing-deck-id to deck-id.

5. close-import-modal-handler [db] — set :setup/import-modal to nil

6. set-import-name-handler [db name] — update :setup/import-modal :name

7. set-import-text-handler [db text] — update :setup/import-modal :text, clear :errors

8. confirm-import-handler [db] — parse text via deck_parser/parse-decklist. On error: set :errors to unrecognized card list. On success:
   - Slugify name to keyword (lowercase, spaces→hyphens, strip non-alphanumeric)
   - Create deck map with :deck/id, :deck/name, :deck/main, :deck/side, :deck/source :imported
   - Add to :setup/imported-decks
   - Persist via save-imported-decks!
   - Populate active setup state (selected-deck, main-deck, sideboard)
   - Clear must-contain
   - Close modal

9. delete-imported-deck-handler [db deck-id] — remove from :setup/imported-decks, persist, if currently selected switch to :iggy-pop

### New re-frame event registrations
Register all handlers above as ::open-import-modal, ::open-edit-modal, ::close-import-modal, ::set-import-name, ::set-import-text, ::confirm-import, ::delete-imported-deck

### stash-setup-config / restore-setup-handler
MODIFY: include :setup/imported-decks in stash so imported decks survive game→setup transitions

## Subscriptions (subs/setup.cljs)

1. ::available-decks — MODIFY: read :setup/imported-decks from db, append to built-in list
2. ::import-modal — new sub, returns :setup/import-modal from db
3. ::import-valid? — derived sub, true when import-modal has non-blank name AND non-blank text

## Files Modified
- src/main/fizzle/storage.cljs
- src/main/fizzle/events/setup.cljs  
- src/main/fizzle/subs/setup.cljs
- src/test/fizzle/events/setup_test.cljs (add import handler tests)

## Dependencies
- Depends on Task 1 (parser) for parse-decklist and deck->text functions

## Verification
- make validate passes
- Import handler: valid text → deck saved + state populated + modal closed
- Import handler: invalid text → errors set, modal stays open
- select-deck works for both built-in and imported deck ids
- delete removes deck and falls back to iggy-pop
- Imported decks survive init-setup (loaded from localStorage)
- stash/restore preserves imported decks
close_reason: Closed

title: Enhance set-loss-condition to record winner atomically
design:
## Goal
Modify set-loss-condition in engine/effects.cljs to accept a losing-player-id parameter and atomically set both :game/loss-condition and :game/winner in a single Datascript transaction. Update all 3 call sites to pass the losing player.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours (small, focused change to existing helper + 3 call sites + tests)

## Implementation

1. Study existing code:
   - engine/effects.cljs:135-141 — current set-loss-condition helper (private fn, takes [db condition])
   - engine/effects.cljs:156-171 — lose-life effect (call site 1, target is the losing player)
   - engine/effects.cljs:199-231 — deal-damage effect (call site 2, target is the losing player)
   - engine/effects.cljs:263-294 — draw effect (call site 3, target is the losing player)
   - db/queries.cljs:135-145 — get-opponent-id query (finds player with :player/is-opponent true)
   - db/queries.cljs:10 — get-player-eid (converts player-id keyword to entity ID)
   - test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs:63-74 — add-opponent test helper (creates :player-2 with :player/is-opponent true)
   - test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs:256-259 — get-loss-condition test helper (reads from game state)

2. Write tests first (TDD) — all in effects_test.cljs
   Add a get-winner helper (analogous to get-loss-condition):
   ```clojure
   (defn get-winner [db]
     (let [game (q/get-game-state db)
           winner-ref (:game/winner game)]
       ;; :game/winner is :db.type/ref, need to pull :player/id from it
       (when winner-ref
         (d/q '[:find ?pid . :in $ ?eid :where [?eid :player/id ?pid]]
              db (:db/id winner-ref)))))
   ```

   Test specifications (RED phase — all must fail before implementation):
   - test-lose-life-lethal-sets-winner: lose-life reduces opponent to 0 → :game/winner is :player-1. Catches: winner not being set at all.
   - test-deal-damage-lethal-sets-winner: deal-damage reduces opponent to 0 → :game/winner is :player-1. Catches: deal-damage call site not passing player.
   - test-draw-empty-library-sets-winner: draw from empty library → :game/winner is opponent. Catches: draw call site not passing player.
   - test-player-self-loss-sets-opponent-as-winner: player-1 loses own life to 0 → :game/winner is :player-2 (opponent wins). Catches: winner determination direction (loser vs winner).
   - test-loss-condition-without-opponent-no-crash: lose-life to 0 without add-opponent → :game/loss-condition set, :game/winner is nil, no crash. Catches: NPE when get-opponent-id returns nil.
   - test-loss-condition-idempotent: opponent life reduced to 0, then reduced again to -5 → :game/winner still :player-1, :game/loss-condition still :life-zero. Catches: double-trigger corruption.

3. Implementation checklist
   - [ ] engine/effects.cljs:135-141 — Modify set-loss-condition signature from [db condition] to [db condition losing-player-id]. Look up opponent via (q/get-opponent-id db losing-player-id). If opponent exists, get their eid via (q/get-player-eid db opponent-id) and set :game/winner. Guard: if get-opponent-id returns nil, only set :game/loss-condition (no :game/winner).
   - [ ] engine/effects.cljs:169 — Change (set-loss-condition db-after-life :life-zero) to (set-loss-condition db-after-life :life-zero target)
   - [ ] engine/effects.cljs:229 — Change (set-loss-condition db-after-damage :life-zero) to (set-loss-condition db-after-damage :life-zero target)
   - [ ] engine/effects.cljs:291 — Change (set-loss-condition db-after-draw :empty-library) to (set-loss-condition db-after-draw :empty-library target)
   - [ ] effects_test.cljs — Add get-winner helper near get-loss-condition (line ~259)
   - [ ] effects_test.cljs — Add 6 new test functions (see test specifications above)
   - [ ] Verify existing loss-condition tests still pass unchanged (they don't assert :game/winner so should be unaffected)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] set-loss-condition sets :game/winner atomically with :game/loss-condition when opponent exists
- [ ] set-loss-condition gracefully handles nil opponent (sets loss-condition only, no :game/winner, no crash)
- [ ] All 3 call sites (lose-life, deal-damage, draw) pass losing-player-id to set-loss-condition
- [ ] Winner is the opponent of the losing player (verified by tests with both directions: player wins, opponent wins)
- [ ] Double-trigger is safe (calling set-loss-condition twice doesn't corrupt state)
- [ ] All existing effects tests pass unchanged (no regressions — they don't need opponent for loss-condition-only assertions)
- [ ] 6 new tests pass covering: lethal via lose-life, lethal via deal-damage, mill via draw, self-loss, nil-opponent guard, idempotent double-trigger
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: No opponent in DB (nil guard)**
- Test init-game-state only creates :player-1, no opponent
- get-opponent-id returns nil → get-player-eid with nil crashes
- MUST guard: only set :game/winner when opponent exists
- This preserves backward compatibility with all existing tests

**Edge Case: Double loss condition trigger**
- Player at -5 life takes 10 more damage → set-loss-condition called again
- Must be idempotent: second call should not corrupt :game/winner or :game/loss-condition
- Datascript :db/add on same attribute is naturally idempotent (overwrites with same value)

**Edge Case: Test opponent ID mismatch**
- Production uses :opponent as player-id, tests use :player-2
- get-opponent-id queries by :player/is-opponent true, not by ID — works for both
- New tests must use add-opponent helper (creates :player-2) and assert winner is :player-2 or :player-1 accordingly

**Edge Case: :game/winner is :db.type/ref**
- get-game-state pulls [*] which returns refs as {:db/id N} maps
- get-winner helper must extract :player/id from the ref entity
- Alternative: enhance get-game-state pull pattern (but that's next task scope)

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO scanning all players to determine winner (effects know who lost — pass it through)
- ❌ NO separate check-game-over event (atomic in set-loss-condition)
- ❌ NO modifying existing test assertions (they test loss-condition, still valid; add new tests for winner)
- ❌ NO crashing on nil opponent (guard with when-let)
- ❌ NO changing :player-2 to :opponent in test helpers (preserve existing test conventions)
close_reason: Implemented: set-loss-condition now accepts losing-player-id, atomically sets :game/winner. All 3 call sites updated. 6 new tests added. Key learning: get-opponent-id doesn't work bidirectionally (queries by :player/is-opponent), so used direct 'find other player' query instead.

title: Add integration tests: selection confirm through full event chain + SBAs
description: HIGHEST VALUE FIX. Currently 0 selection tests exercise the full production path: event dispatch → handler → confirm-selection-impl → executor → :db effect → SBAs. Add integration tests for key selection types (discard, mana-allocation, storm-split, scry) that go through rf/dispatch-sync and verify SBAs run afterward. This catches the class of bugs where selection resolution leaves game state SBA-violating.

title: Epic: Unify spell/trigger stack systems via stack-item entity
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
1. All stack entries (spells, triggers, activated abilities, storm copies) represented as `:stack-item/*` Datascript entities
2. Single `resolve-effect-target` function replaces all 10 target resolution implementations
3. Unified resolution path for all stack items (one code path, not 4 branches)
4. Selection builders parameterized by stack-item context (not spell-id vs trigger-id)
5. Game objects track physical card location (`:stack` zone per MTG rules), stack-items track resolution metadata (ordering, effects, targets)
6. Fizzle check applies to all stack items with stored targets
7. `:trigger/*` schema attributes removed entirely after migration
8. Mana abilities and land ETB effects use shared `resolve-effect-target`
9. Storm copies become stack-items with `:stack-item/is-copy true` and inline spell effects (no separate storm trigger entity)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] All existing tests pass (826+ tests, 1826+ assertions)
- [ ] Single Datalog query retrieves entire stack in LIFO order
- [ ] No `:trigger/*` entities remain in Datascript schema
- [ ] No duplicate selection builder functions (0 `-for-trigger` suffixed functions)
- [ ] Target resolution (`:self`, `:controller`, `:any-player`, `:target-ref`) exists in exactly one function
- [ ] Effect before/after splitting exists in exactly one function
- [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] Net reduction of 300+ LOC

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO creating game objects for triggers (triggers are virtual — only stack-items, no game object; reason: triggers aren't physical cards)
- ❌ NO keeping parallel resolution paths (reason: the entire point is unification — one path, not 2+)
- ❌ NO `:selection/spell-id` or `:selection/trigger-id` in selection state (reason: use `:selection/stack-item-id` — the dual-ID pattern IS the duplication)
- ❌ NO `:object/position` for stack ordering (reason: `:stack-item/position` is the sole source of truth for LIFO ordering)
- ❌ NO dual-entity stack queries that check both `:trigger/*` and `:object/*` (reason: single entity type = single query)
- ❌ NO multimethod for target resolution (reason: closed set of ~5 strategies, cond is simpler and matches codebase style)
- ❌ NO storm trigger entity that later spawns copies (reason: storm creates N stack-items directly with spell effects)

## Approach
Introduce a new `:stack-item/*` Datascript entity type as the unified stack representation. When a spell is cast, a stack-item is created referencing the game object via `:stack-item/object-ref`. When a trigger fires, a stack-item is created with inline effects (no game object). Resolution code only operates on stack-items.

A single `resolve-effect-target` function (cond-based) replaces 10 copy-pasted target resolution implementations across selection.cljs, triggers.cljs, abilities.cljs, and game.cljs. Selection builders are parameterized by stack-item context, eliminating the 3 duplicated builder pairs. Effect before/after splitting is consolidated into one function.

New `engine/stack.cljs` namespace houses all stack-item operations: creation, removal, querying, resolution, and target resolution.

## Architecture

### New namespace: engine/stack.cljs
- `create-stack-item` — create stack-item entity in Datascript
- `remove-stack-item` — remove after resolution
- `get-top-stack-item` — query highest position
- `get-next-stack-order` — moved from queries.cljs
- `resolve-effect-target` — single target resolver
- `resolve-stack-item` — unified resolution (no selection)
- `resolve-stack-item-with-selection` — unified resolution checking for selection pause
- `execute-resolved-effects` — run effects with target resolution

### Schema additions (db/schema.cljs)
`:stack-item/position`, `:stack-item/type`, `:stack-item/controller`, `:stack-item/source`, `:stack-item/effects`, `:stack-item/targets`, `:stack-item/description`, `:stack-item/is-copy`, `:stack-item/cast-mode`, `:stack-item/object-ref`

### Schema removals
All `:trigger/*` attributes after migration complete.

### Data flow
1. Cast spell → `zones/move-to-zone obj :stack` + `stack/create-stack-item {:type :spell :object-ref obj-eid ...}`
2. Fire trigger → `stack/create-stack-item {:type :etb :effects [...] ...}`
3. Resolve → `stack/get-top-stack-item` → `stack/resolve-stack-item-with-selection`
4. Cleanup → remove stack-item; for spells move object to destination zone

### Files modified
- `db/schema.cljs` — add stack-item attributes, eventually remove trigger attributes
- `db/queries.cljs` — simplify stack queries
- `engine/stack.cljs` — NEW: all stack-item operations
- `engine/rules.cljs` — cast-spell creates stack-item, resolve-spell delegates to stack
- `engine/triggers.cljs` — create stack-items instead of trigger entities
- `events/game.cljs` — `::resolve-top` simplified to one path
- `events/selection.cljs` — unified builders, remove `-for-trigger` functions
- `events/abilities.cljs` — mana abilities use `resolve-effect-target`

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Spells and triggers are conceptually the same thing (resolvable stack items) but implemented as two parallel systems, creating ~1000+ lines of duplicated logic. Fork/replay (Phase 3) needs a uniform stack model. The current dual-entity model (`:object/position` + `:trigger/stack-order`) requires comparing two entity types for LIFO resolution.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- `db/queries.cljs:113-127` — `get-next-stack-order` already shares counter between both entity types
- `events/selection.cljs:512-639` — `resolve-spell-with-selection` (5 code paths, ~130 lines)
- `events/selection.cljs:683-845` — `resolve-activated-ability-with-selection` (3 code paths, ~160 lines)
- `events/game.cljs:364-410` — `::resolve-top` handler with 4 branches comparing both entity types
- 10 distinct target resolution implementations across 4 files
- 3 duplicated selection builder pairs (tutor, player-target, discard)
- 5 effect before/after splitting implementations

**MTG Rules:**
- Stack is a real zone (Rule 601.2a) — cards move hand → stack → graveyard
- Fizzle applies to all stack items with illegal targets (not just spells)
- Storm creates copies of the spell on the stack

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Stack-item wrapper entity ✓

**What it is:** New `:stack-item/*` Datascript entity type as resolution metadata. Game objects still track physical card location. Stack-items are the sole source of stack ordering and resolution context.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed current dual-entity model — both types already share a counter via `get-next-stack-order`
- Checked that `:object/zone :stack` is needed for MTG zone rules (cards physically on stack)
- Verified storm copies need `:object/is-copy` behavior (removed from DB, not sent to graveyard)

**Pros:**
- Clean separation: physical card vs resolution metadata
- Single entity type for all stack queries
- Game object zone tracking unchanged (MTG-correct)

**Cons:**
- Adds indirection (stack-item references game object)
- Schema additions before removals during migration

**Chosen because:** Cleanest unified model without redundant naming on game objects.

#### 2. Triggers become game objects ❌

**What it is:** Give triggers `:object/*` attributes and put them in `:stack` zone like spells.

**Why we looked at this:** Simplest migration — triggers just become objects.

**Investigation:**
- Would require `:object/stack-source`, `:object/stack-controller`, `:object/stack-data`, `:object/stack-description` — duplicative of existing object attributes
- Triggers aren't physical cards — they shouldn't have card-like attributes

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Adding `:object/stack-*` attributes is redundant with existing object schema. Muddies the distinction between physical cards and virtual stack entries.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We decide triggers should be physical objects (unlikely per MTG rules).

#### 3. Normalize at resolution time only ❌

**What it is:** Keep spells and triggers stored separately, create a unified view/map only during resolution. Storage unchanged, resolution code unified.

**Why we looked at this:** Least invasive — no schema changes.

**Investigation:**
- Would still require dual-entity queries for stack ordering
- Fork/replay would still need to capture two entity types
- Doesn't address the root cause (dual representation)

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Doesn't unify storage. Dual-entity queries remain. Fork/replay complexity unchanged.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Storage unification proves too risky (only if migration can't be done safely).

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- All stack item types: spells, storm copies, activated abilities, ETB triggers, permanent-tapped triggers, land-entered triggers
- Target resolution unification across ALL callers (including mana abilities and land ETB)
- Selection builder unification
- Effect before/after splitting unification
- Schema migration (add stack-item, remove trigger)

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Fork/replay implementation (uses the unified model but is a separate epic)
- Moving pending-selection into Datascript (fizzle-4jc, separate epic)
- New trigger types not yet implemented
- UI changes beyond what's needed for stack display

### Open Questions
- Exact migration ordering: can we add stack-items and run both systems in parallel during transition, or must it be atomic?
- Should `engine/triggers.cljs` be renamed/reorganized after the trigger entity type is removed?
- Should `:object/position` be removed from schema or left for potential other uses?

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| How to unify stack items? | Triggers become objects → revised to stack-item wrapper | New `:stack-item/*` entity type, not bolting onto `:object/*` |
| Target resolution: single function or multimethod? | Single cond function | ~15 lines, matches codebase style, closed set of strategies |
| Include mana/ETB in scope? | Yes, include all | `resolve-effect-target` called from abilities.cljs and game.cljs land ETB too |
| Fizzle scope? | All items with stored targets | Not just spells — any stack item with targets gets fizzle-checked |
| Storm copies? | Become stack-items with is-copy | No separate storm trigger entity → N stack-items created directly |
| Does card move to stack zone? | Yes, per MTG rules (601.2a) | Game objects in `:stack` zone for physical location, stack-items for metadata |
| Wrapper vs objects for triggers? | Wrapper (stack-item entity type) | Avoids redundant `:object/stack-*` attributes, clean separation |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Current Stack Representation
**Question explored:** How are spells vs triggers stored and ordered?
**Sources consulted:**
- `db/schema.cljs:35-45` — object schema with `:object/position`
- `db/schema.cljs:68-77` — trigger schema with `:trigger/stack-order`
- `db/queries.cljs:113-127` — shared counter in `get-next-stack-order`

**Findings:**
- Both use shared counter space (max of both types + 1)
- Spells: game objects in `:stack` zone
- Triggers: separate entities with `:trigger/*` attributes
- LIFO comparison at resolution compares both types

**Conclusion:** Shared counter proves unification is natural. Stack-item entity replaces both ordering mechanisms.

#### Target Resolution Duplication
**Question explored:** How many times is :self/:controller resolution reimplemented?
**Sources consulted:**
- 10 distinct locations across selection.cljs, triggers.cljs, abilities.cljs, game.cljs

**Findings:**
- 4 variants: controller-only, self+controller, self+controller+target-ref, any-player
- Every variant is a cond doing the same assoc pattern
- Varying completeness (some handle :self, some don't)

**Conclusion:** Single function with all branches handles every case. ~15 lines replaces ~60 lines of duplication.

#### Resolution Path Duplication
**Question explored:** How similar are spell vs trigger resolution?
**Sources consulted:**
- `selection.cljs:512-639` — resolve-spell-with-selection
- `selection.cljs:683-845` — resolve-activated-ability-with-selection

**Findings:**
- Both: get effects → find selection → split before/after → execute before → build selection → pause
- Spell version: 5 code paths, ~130 lines
- Trigger version: 3 code paths, ~160 lines
- Key difference: trigger keeps stack-item until confirmation, spell removes on resolve

**Conclusion:** Unified with stack-item: both remove stack-item at same point (after effects + cleanup).

### Dead-End Paths

#### Triggers as Game Objects
**Why explored:** Simplest migration — just add :object/stack-* attributes to triggers
**Investigation:** Would add 4+ new :object/ attributes that duplicate existing concepts
**Why abandoned:** User correctly identified this as redundant naming. Triggers aren't physical cards.

#### Multimethod for Target Resolution
**Why explored:** Open dispatch pattern for extensibility
**Investigation:** Counted target types (~5), checked if new types expected (no — MTG targets are closed set via :target-ref)
**Why abandoned:** Too much ceremony for 5 branches. Cond matches codebase style.

### Open Concerns Raised
- 'How will targeting extend for creatures?' → :target-ref mechanism handles all future targeting. Cast-time selection stores chosen target, resolution-time :target-ref resolves it.
- 'Does the card move to the stack zone?' → Yes, per MTG Rule 601.2a. Stack is a real zone. Game objects track physical location, stack-items track resolution metadata.
close_reason: All 4 tasks complete. Stack unified via stack-items. 831 tests pass, make validate clean.

title: Schema + advance-with-stops: add opponent-stops checking
description: Core engine change: add :player/opponent-stops to schema and update advance-with-stops to check non-active player's opponent-stops. This is the foundation that all other changes build on.
design:
## Goal
Add `:player/opponent-stops` attribute and update the priority engine so advance-with-stops checks both the active player's stops and the non-active player's opponent-stops. This is the foundation all other changes build on.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - `db/schema.cljs:68` — current schema for `:player/stops {}`
   - `engine/priority.cljs:90-102` — `set-player-stops`, `check-stop` (pure helpers)
   - `events/priority_flow.cljs:13-62` — `advance-with-stops` (the loop that checks stops)
   - `events/priority_flow.cljs:95-142` — `yield-advance-phase` (calls advance-with-stops, computes ignore-stops?)
   - `db/queries.cljs` — `get-other-player-id`, `get-player-eid` (player lookups)

2. Write tests first (TDD)
   Test file: `src/test/fizzle/engine/priority_test.cljs` (extend existing) and `src/test/fizzle/events/priority_test.cljs`

   **Core behavior tests:**
   - test_advance_pauses_at_bot_phase_with_human_opponent_stop: Set human's :player/opponent-stops to #{:upkeep}. During bot's turn, advance-with-stops should pause at :upkeep. Bug caught: opponent-stops not checked during advancement.
   - test_advance_does_not_pause_when_no_opponent_stop: Human's :player/opponent-stops is #{} (empty). During bot's turn, advance-with-stops should NOT pause at phases without stops. Bug caught: false positive pauses.
   - test_advance_pauses_for_own_stops_AND_opponent_stops: Active player has stop at :main1, non-active has opponent-stop at :main1 — game pauses once (not twice). Bug caught: double-pause logic error.

   **F6 tests:**
   - test_f6_skips_opponent_stops: Human has F6 active and opponent-stops at :upkeep. During bot's turn, advance-with-stops should skip the opponent-stop. Bug caught: F6 doesn't affect opponent-stops.
   - test_f6_does_not_skip_active_player_bot_stops: Human has F6, bot has :player/stops at :main1. advance-with-stops should STILL pause at bot's main1 (bot needs to act). Bug caught: F6 incorrectly skips bot stops.

   **Edge case tests:**
   - test_nil_opponent_stops_handled_gracefully: Bot entity has no :player/opponent-stops attribute. check-opponent-stop returns false. Bug caught: NPE on nil stops.
   - test_opponent_stop_on_non_priority_phase_ignored: Human sets opponent-stop on :untap — should NOT pause (untap doesn't grant priority). Bug caught: pausing at non-priority phases.
   - test_stack_trigger_takes_precedence_over_opponent_stop: Phase has both stack trigger and opponent-stop. Stack trigger path fires (existing behavior). Bug caught: opponent-stop interfering with stack resolution.

   **Pure function tests (in engine/priority_test.cljs):**
   - test_check_opponent_stop_returns_true_when_set: `(check-opponent-stop db eid :upkeep)` returns true when :player/opponent-stops contains :upkeep.
   - test_check_opponent_stop_returns_false_when_not_set: Returns false for phase not in set.
   - test_set_opponent_stops_persists_to_entity: `set-opponent-stops` writes :player/opponent-stops attribute correctly.

3. Implementation checklist
   - [ ] `db/schema.cljs` — Add `:player/opponent-stops {}` to schema (after line 68, next to :player/stops)
   - [ ] `engine/priority.cljs` — Add `set-opponent-stops` fn: `(defn set-opponent-stops [db player-eid stops] (d/db-with db [[:db/add player-eid :player/opponent-stops stops]]))`
   - [ ] `engine/priority.cljs` — Add `check-opponent-stop` fn: query :player/opponent-stops, return false on nil (handles bot entity without attribute)
   - [ ] `events/priority_flow.cljs:advance-with-stops` — Add `ignore-opponent-stops?` parameter (boolean). Before the loop, compute `non-active-eid` from `(queries/get-other-player-id game-db active-player-id)`. In the stop-check section (lines 56-61), add second check: `(and (not ignore-opponent-stops?) (priority/check-opponent-stop advanced-db non-active-eid actual-phase))`. Pause if EITHER active player's stop OR non-active player's opponent-stop fires.
   - [ ] `events/priority_flow.cljs:yield-advance-phase` — Compute `ignore-opponent-stops?` = `f6?` (when human hit F6, skip their opponent-stops regardless of who is active). Pass to `advance-with-stops`.
   - [ ] Verify: opponent-stop check only fires for priority phases (advance-phase skips :untap/:cleanup already — confirm this by checking phases/advance-phase behavior)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] :player/opponent-stops exists in schema
- [ ] advance-with-stops pauses when non-active player has opponent-stop at a priority phase
- [ ] advance-with-stops does NOT pause for opponent-stops on non-priority phases
- [ ] advance-with-stops does NOT pause for opponent-stops when ignore-opponent-stops? is true
- [ ] F6 causes ignore-opponent-stops? = true (human's opponent-stops skipped during F6)
- [ ] F6 does NOT skip bot's own :player/stops (bot stops always respected)
- [ ] check-opponent-stop returns false when :player/opponent-stops is nil (bot entity safety)
- [ ] Stack triggers take precedence over opponent-stop checks (existing behavior unchanged)
- [ ] All existing priority/phase tests pass unchanged (no regressions)
- [ ] 11 new tests covering all scenarios listed above
- [ ] Tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Nil opponent-stops on bot entity**
Bot entities will NOT have :player/opponent-stops set. `check-opponent-stop` MUST return false when the attribute is nil/missing. Pattern: `(boolean (and stops (contains? stops phase)))` — same nil-safe pattern as existing `check-stop`.

**Edge Case: Non-priority phases**
advance-phase already skips :untap and :cleanup (they're not in the phase sequence traversed by advance-with-stops). Verify this during implementation — if advance-with-stops can never land on :untap/:cleanup, then opponent-stops on those phases are naturally ignored. Document this in a test.

**Edge Case: Combat phase skipping**
advance-phase may skip :combat when no creatures exist (see priority_flow.cljs:49-51 comment about actual-phase vs nxt). The opponent-stop check uses `actual-phase` (line 51), so this is safe. But test to confirm.

**F6 handling architecture**
Current: `advance-with-stops(app-db, ignore-stops?)`
New: `advance-with-stops(app-db, ignore-stops?, ignore-opponent-stops?)`
The two booleans are independent:
- `ignore-stops?` = `(and f6? (= active-player-id human-pid))` — only skip human's own-turn stops
- `ignore-opponent-stops?` = `f6?` — skip opponent-stops whenever F6 is active (human is always the non-active player who has opponent-stops; bot has none)

**Reference: check-stop pattern (engine/priority.cljs:97-102)**
```clojure
(defn check-stop [db player-eid phase]
  (let [stops (:player/stops (d/pull db [:player/stops] player-eid))]
    (boolean (and stops (contains? stops phase)))))
```
Follow this exact pattern for `check-opponent-stop`, substituting `:player/opponent-stops`.

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO bot-specific code in advance-with-stops (uniformity: check both players' attributes the same way)
- ❌ NO modifying the advance-with-stops signature to accept player type (mechanism stays player-agnostic)
- ❌ NO checking opponent-stops inside check-stop (keep functions separate — different attributes, different semantics)
- ❌ NO hardcoding player IDs in advance-with-stops (use queries to look up non-active player dynamically)
close_reason: Schema + advance-with-stops done. Commit 8cc5104. 9 new tests, all 3040 passing.

These duplicate th/ functions and risk divergence. Migrate to use th/ equivalents. Low priority but reduces maintenance burden for contributors.

title: Task 4: Implement Careful Study with selection modal
design:
## Goal
Add Careful Study spell that draws 2 cards, then requires player to select and discard 2 cards via a modal UI.

## Effort Estimate
3-5 hours (includes selection modal system)

## Context
Completed Task 3 (LED with discard-hand cost). Careful Study requires:
1. A :discard effect with :selection :player that pauses for player input
2. A modal UI component for selecting cards from hand
3. State management for pending selections

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing effect patterns
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs

Look for:
- How execute-effect multimethod dispatches on :effect/type
- How :draw effect adds cards to hand
- How effects return modified db
- Pattern for effects that need player input (may not exist yet)

### 2. Add pending-selection to app-db
Note: This is app-level state (UI), NOT Datascript game state.
Selection state lives in re-frame app-db, not game/db.

File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs (or new selection.cljs)

Selection state structure in app-db:
```clojure
:game/pending-selection
{:selection/zone :hand           ;; Zone to select from
 :selection/count 2              ;; Exact number to select
 :selection/player-id :player-1  ;; Who is selecting
 :selection/selected #{}         ;; Set of object-ids currently selected
 :selection/effect {:effect/type :discard ...}} ;; Effect to resume after selection
```

### 3. Write tests first (TDD)
Add tests to: src/test/fizzle/cards/careful_study_test.cljs (new file)

Test cases with bug-catching rationale:

```clojure
;; test_careful_study_is_sorcery_type
;; Verify card definition has correct type
;; Bug caught: Wrong card type would affect timing rules

;; test_careful_study_draws_two_cards
;; Setup: Player has 3 cards in hand, cast Careful Study
;; Verify: Hand size increased by 2 (minus the spell = net +1)
;; Bug caught: Missing or broken :draw effect

;; test_careful_study_creates_pending_selection
;; Setup: Player casts Careful Study from hand
;; Action: Resolve spell
;; Verify: :game/pending-selection exists with :count 2, :zone :hand
;; Bug caught: Missing selection state creation

;; test_toggle_selection_adds_and_removes_card
;; Setup: Pending selection exists
;; Action: Toggle card A (add), toggle card A again (remove)
;; Verify: Selected set correctly reflects toggles
;; Bug caught: Broken toggle logic

;; test_confirm_selection_requires_exact_count
;; Setup: Pending selection with count=2, only 1 selected
;; Action: Attempt confirm
;; Verify: Confirm fails or is disabled, selection persists
;; Bug caught: Allowing wrong count to be confirmed

;; test_confirm_selection_discards_selected_cards
;; Setup: Pending selection with 2 cards selected
;; Action: Confirm selection
;; Verify: Those 2 cards move to graveyard, selection cleared
;; Bug caught: Cards not actually discarded

;; test_careful_study_with_exactly_two_cards_after_draw
;; Setup: Player has 0 cards, draws 2 from Careful Study
;; Verify: Must discard both cards (selection required even when obvious)
;; Bug caught: Auto-selecting when hand equals count

;; test_careful_study_with_more_than_two_cards_after_draw
;; Setup: Player has 3 cards, draws 2 (now 5 in hand)
;; Verify: Must select exactly 2 of the 5
;; Bug caught: Not offering full hand for selection
```

### 4. Add :discard effect type with selection support
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs

```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect :discard [db player-id effect]
  (let [selection (:effect/selection effect)
        count-to-discard (:effect/count effect)]
    (if (= :player selection)
      ;; Player selection required - return db unchanged
      ;; The selection state is handled at app-db level, not game-db
      ;; Spell resolution pauses until selection confirmed
      db
      ;; Programmatic discard (random or specified) - not needed for Careful Study
      db)))
```

Note: The actual selection handling happens in re-frame events, not in execute-effect.
The effect returns db unchanged; the calling code checks for :selection :player
and sets up the pending selection.

### 5. Modify spell resolution to handle selection effects
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

Modify resolve-spell or add wrapper:
```clojure
(defn resolve-spell-with-selection [db player-id object-id]
  (let [spell-obj (queries/get-object db object-id)
        card (:object/card spell-obj)
        effects (:card/effects card)]
    ;; Execute effects until we hit a selection effect
    (loop [db' db
           remaining-effects effects]
      (if (empty? remaining-effects)
        ;; All effects done, move spell to graveyard
        (zones/move-to-zone db' object-id :graveyard)
        (let [effect (first remaining-effects)]
          (if (= :player (:effect/selection effect))
            ;; Pause for selection - return db with spell still on stack
            ;; Pending selection will be set up at app-db level
            {:db db'
             :pending {:selection/zone :hand
                       :selection/count (:effect/count effect)
                       :selection/player-id player-id
                       :selection/remaining-effects (rest remaining-effects)
                       :selection/spell-id object-id}}
            ;; Execute effect and continue
            (recur (effects/execute-effect db' player-id effect)
                   (rest remaining-effects))))))))
```

### 6. Add selection state management events
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::toggle-selection
  (fn [db [_ object-id]]
    (let [selected (get-in db [:game/pending-selection :selection/selected] #{})
          new-selected (if (contains? selected object-id)
                         (disj selected object-id)
                         (conj selected object-id))]
      (assoc-in db [:game/pending-selection :selection/selected] new-selected))))

(rf/reg-event-db
  ::confirm-selection
  (fn [db _]
    (let [selection (:game/pending-selection db)
          selected (:selection/selected selection)
          count-required (:selection/count selection)]
      (if (= (count selected) count-required)
        ;; Valid selection - discard selected cards and clear selection
        (let [game-db (:game/db db)
              db-after-discard (reduce (fn [gdb obj-id]
                                         (zones/move-to-zone gdb obj-id :graveyard))
                                       game-db
                                       selected)]
          (-> db
              (assoc :game/db db-after-discard)
              (dissoc :game/pending-selection)))
        ;; Invalid count - do nothing
        db))))

(rf/reg-event-db
  ::cancel-selection
  (fn [db _]
    ;; Note: Cancel behavior for Careful Study is complex
    ;; Cards already drawn can't be "undrawn"
    ;; For MVP: Cancel clears selection but keeps drawn cards
    ;; Player must still discard 2 (selection re-opens)
    (assoc-in db [:game/pending-selection :selection/selected] #{})))
```

### 7. Add selection modal component
File: src/main/fizzle/core.cljs

```clojure
(defn selection-modal []
  (let [selection @(rf/subscribe [::subs/pending-selection])
        hand @(rf/subscribe [::subs/hand :player-1])]
    (when selection
      [:div.selection-modal
       {:class "fixed inset-0 bg-black bg-opacity-50 flex items-center justify-center"}
       [:div.modal-content
        {:class "bg-gray-800 p-4 rounded-lg max-w-md"}
        [:h2.text-white.text-lg.mb-2
         (str "Select " (:selection/count selection) " cards to discard")]
        [:p.text-gray-400.mb-4
         (str (count (:selection/selected selection)) " selected")]
        
        ;; Card grid
        [:div.grid.grid-cols-3.gap-2.mb-4
         (for [card hand]
           (let [obj-id (:object/id card)
                 selected? (contains? (:selection/selected selection) obj-id)]
             ^{:key obj-id}
             [:div.card
              {:class (if selected? "ring-2 ring-blue-500" "")
               :on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/toggle-selection obj-id])}
              (:card/name (:object/card card))]))]
        
        ;; Buttons
        [:div.flex.justify-end.gap-2
         [:button.px-4.py-2.bg-gray-600.rounded
          {:on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/cancel-selection])}
          "Cancel"]
         [:button.px-4.py-2.bg-blue-600.rounded
          {:disabled (not= (count (:selection/selected selection))
                           (:selection/count selection))
           :on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/confirm-selection])}
          "Confirm"]]]])))
```

### 8. Add Careful Study card definition
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

```clojure
;; Careful Study - Card filtering cantrip
;; Draw two cards, then discard two cards.
(def careful-study
  {:card/id :careful-study
   :card/name "Careful Study"
   :card/cmc 1
   :card/mana-cost {:blue 1}
   :card/colors #{:blue}
   :card/types #{:sorcery}
   :card/text "Draw two cards, then discard two cards."
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :draw :effect/amount 2}
                  {:effect/type :discard
                   :effect/count 2
                   :effect/selection :player}]})
```

### 9. Add subscription for pending selection
File: src/main/fizzle/subs.cljs (or core.cljs if subs are there)

```clojure
(rf/reg-sub
  ::pending-selection
  (fn [db _]
    (:game/pending-selection db)))
```

### 10. Update all-cards vector and run validation

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Careful Study definition exists with :card/mana-cost {:blue 1}
- [ ] Careful Study has :card/types #{:sorcery}
- [ ] Casting Careful Study draws 2 cards to hand
- [ ] After draw, pending selection created with :count 2, :zone :hand
- [ ] Selection modal appears when pending-selection exists
- [ ] Clicking card toggles it in/out of :selection/selected set
- [ ] Confirm button disabled when count != required count
- [ ] Confirming valid selection moves selected cards to graveyard
- [ ] Cancel button clears selection (re-prompts)
- [ ] 8+ tests pass in careful_study_test.cljs
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO auto-selecting discard targets (user agency: player must choose)
- ❌ NO blocking UI during selection (must be dismissable)
- ❌ NO skipping selection even if hand has exactly 2 cards (consistency)
- ❌ NO hardcoded card IDs in selection logic (generic system)
- ❌ NO storing selection state in Datascript game-db (use app-db)
- ❌ NO modifying spell resolution to auto-discard on timeout

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Selection State Location**
- Selection state is UI state, NOT game state
- Lives in re-frame app-db at :game/pending-selection
- Do NOT add to Datascript schema - that's for game state persistence
- This allows selection to be cleared on UI reset without affecting game

**Cancel Behavior Complexity**
- Careful Study's cancel is tricky: cards already drawn can't be "undrawn"
- For MVP: Cancel clears selection but player must still discard 2
- Alternative: Cancel reverts entire spell (complex - need state snapshot)
- Decision: MVP approach - cancel just re-prompts selection

**Effect Execution Pause Pattern**
- Effects execute synchronously in engine
- Selection breaks this - need async pattern
- Solution: resolve-spell returns partial state when hitting selection effect
- Remaining effects stored in pending-selection
- On confirm, remaining effects continue

**Hand Size Edge Cases**
- After drawing 2, player might have exactly 2 cards → must still select
- After drawing 2, player might have only 1 card → ERROR CASE
  - This shouldn't happen (Careful Study draws 2, discard 2)
  - But if library was depleted mid-draw, could have <2 cards
  - Decision: If hand < count required, force discard all available
  - Add test for this edge case

**Reference Implementation**
- Study src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs for re-frame event patterns
- Study src/main/fizzle/core.cljs for Reagent component patterns
- Study how :game/selected-card already works for card selection
close_reason: Closed

title: Design card library/repository system for expansion
description:
As Fizzle grows from 1 deck (Iggy Pop) to 100-200 cards across broader Premodern combo, the card library needs a proper design:

- How are decks registered and discovered (currently hard-coded iggy-pop references in events/game.cljs and events/setup.cljs)
- How does Card Pool data flow to consumers (ADR-010 says through Interpretation Core, but the design of that interface depends on how the library is structured)
- How are new decks/cards added (currently: add a .cljs file in cards/, add to cards/iggy-pop.cljs aggregator)
- How does the setup screen present available decks (currently: hard-coded :iggy-pop case statement)

This epic should produce a design for the card library that naturally resolves ADR-010's routing requirement.

**Context:** Share-ready path item #3, ADR-010 drift detected during architecture audit.

title: Migrate modals.cljs and history.cljs inline styles to Tailwind utility classes
description: Convert all remaining inline :style maps in modals.cljs (56 instances) and history.cljs (23 instances) to Tailwind v4 utility classes using existing @theme tokens. Zero inline :style maps should remain in any view file after this task. Use semantic theme tokens (bg-surface, text-text, etc.) instead of hardcoded hex values.
design:
# Plan: Migrate modals.cljs and history.cljs inline styles to Tailwind

**Task:** `fizzle-vc9t` — Convert all remaining inline `:style` maps in `modals.cljs` (56 instances) and `history.cljs` (23 instances) to Tailwind v4 utility classes using existing `@theme` tokens.

**Verification:** `make validate` passes, zero `:style` maps remain in any view file.

---

## Step 1: Add missing @theme tokens to app.css

**File:** `src/css/app.css`

Several hex values in modals/history don't have theme tokens yet. Add these to the `@theme` block:

```css
/* Modal colors */
--color-modal-selected-bg: #1a3a5a;
--color-modal-overlay-bg: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8);

/* Scry colors */
--color-scry-top-bg: #2a4a2a;
--color-scry-bottom-bg: #4a2a2a;
--color-scry-unassigned-bg: #1a2a3a;

/* Mode button */
--color-mode-btn-bg: #1a2a3a;
--color-mode-btn-hover: #2a3a5a;

/* Additional cost text */
--color-cost-text: #cc88aa;

/* Stepper/control button colors */
--color-surface-elevated: #444;
```

**Why:** The anti-pattern forbids hardcoded hex in components. These colors are used across multiple modal components and need semantic names.

---

## Step 2: Migrate shared modal components (confirm-button, cancel-button, modal-wrapper)

**File:** `src/main/fizzle/views/modals.cljs` lines 11-82

Remove the `overlay-style`, `container-style`, and `header-style` defs. Convert to class-based helpers:

- `overlay-style` → `"fixed inset-0 bg-black/80 flex items-center justify-center z-[1000]"`
- `container-style` → `"bg-surface-raised border-2 border-border-accent rounded-xl p-6 w-[90%] max-w-[600px]"` (with parameterized border-color and max-width)
- `header-style` → `"text-text text-lg m-0 mb-4"`
- `confirm-button` → Tailwind classes with conditional `bg-btn-enabled-bg`/`bg-btn-disabled-bg`
- `cancel-button` → Tailwind classes

Confirm/cancel buttons use dynamic colors via conditional class strings (same pattern as `btn-class` in controls.cljs).

**Note:** `modal-wrapper` accepts `border-color` and `max-width` params. Since Tailwind doesn't support fully dynamic values, use Tailwind's arbitrary value syntax: `max-w-[400px]`, `border-[#6a4a8a]` → `border-gy-flashback-border`.

---

## Step 3: Migrate player-target-button and object-target-card-view

**File:** `src/main/fizzle/views/modals.cljs` lines 86-172

- `player-target-button` (line 86-101): Replace inline style with conditional class string using `border-border-accent`/`border-border`, `bg-modal-selected-bg`/`bg-surface-raised`
- `object-target-card-view` (line 106-126): Same pattern as `selection-card-view` — they share the same styling. Extract a shared card-class helper.
- `object-target-modal` (line 129-172): Convert overlay, container, header, instruction text, card grid flex layout, and confirm button area

---

## Step 4: Migrate selection-card-view and card-selection-modal

**File:** `src/main/fizzle/views/modals.cljs` lines 204-314

- `selection-card-view` (204-225): Same card styling as object-target-card-view — use shared helper
- `card-selection-modal` (228-314): Convert all inline styles to Tailwind classes

---

## Step 5: Migrate pile-choice, graveyard-selection, exile-cards, peek-selection modals

**File:** `src/main/fizzle/views/modals.cljs` lines 317-493

These 4 modals share identical structural patterns (overlay → container → header → instructions → card grid → buttons). Convert each following the same pattern established in steps 3-4.

For `border-color "#6a4a8a"` modals, use `border-gy-flashback-border` (existing token).

---

## Step 6: Migrate scry-modal

**File:** `src/main/fizzle/views/modals.cljs` lines 498-579

Most complex modal. Key conversions:
- `scry-card-view` conditional backgrounds: use `bg-scry-top-bg`/`bg-scry-bottom-bg`/`bg-scry-unassigned-bg`
- Section headers with semantic colors: `text-health-good` (green), `text-text-muted` (gray), `text-health-critical` (red)
- Top/Bottom assign buttons: small buttons with `bg-scry-top-bg border-health-good` and `bg-scry-bottom-bg border-health-critical`
- Full-width confirm button: `w-full p-3 bg-accent border-none rounded-lg text-white text-base cursor-pointer`

---

## Step 7: Migrate x-mana-selection-modal

**File:** `src/main/fizzle/views/modals.cljs` lines 584-650

- Stepper buttons (`-`/`+`): conditional classes with `bg-surface-elevated`/`bg-btn-disabled-bg`
- X display: `text-[32px] text-white font-bold min-w-[60px] text-center`
- Container border: `border-gy-flashback-border`

---

## Step 8: Migrate mode-selector-modal and mode-button

**File:** `src/main/fizzle/views/modals.cljs` lines 655-759

- `mode-button` (689-723): Replace `on-mouse-over`/`on-mouse-out` JS style manipulation with Tailwind `hover:bg-mode-btn-hover`. Use `bg-mode-btn-bg` base.
- Cancel button in mode-selector: same cancel-button pattern
- `mode-selector-modal` (726-759): overlay + container pattern

---

## Step 9: Migrate history.cljs — step-controls

**File:** `src/main/fizzle/views/history.cljs` lines 9-37

Back/Forward buttons: same conditional button pattern as controls.cljs. Use `btn-enabled-bg`/`btn-disabled-bg` tokens.

---

## Step 10: Migrate history.cljs — action-log

**File:** `src/main/fizzle/views/history.cljs` lines 40-96

- Turn headers: `px-2 py-1 text-[11px] text-text-label cursor-pointer select-none uppercase tracking-wider`
- Entry items: conditional classes for active/future/past states. Border-left for active position: `border-l-[3px] border-l-accent` vs `border-l-[3px] border-l-transparent`
- Background for active: `bg-mode-btn-bg` (`#1a2a3a`)
- Opacity for future entries: `opacity-40`
- Empty state: `text-border text-xs px-2 py-1`

---

## Step 11: Migrate history.cljs — fork-controls, fork-tree, fork-list, history-sidebar

**File:** `src/main/fizzle/views/history.cljs` lines 99-276

- `fork-controls` (99-173): New Fork button, inline edit input, fork name spans, delete buttons
- `render-tree-node` (176-196): Dynamic padding-left via arbitrary value `pl-[Npx]` — keep as `:style {:padding-left ...}` ONLY for truly dynamic calculated values (depth × 16 + 8). This is the one acceptable inline style since it's a computed value.
- `fork-tree` (199-216): Same branch styling pattern
- `fork-list` (219-251): Branch list with border-top separator
- `history-sidebar` (254-275): Fixed-width sidebar → `w-[250px] min-w-[250px] h-screen bg-surface-raised border-l border-[#444] p-3 flex flex-col font-mono text-text overflow-hidden`

---

## Step 12: Run `make validate`

Verify:
1. `make lint` — no linter errors
2. `make fmt-check` — formatting clean (run `make fmt` if needed)
3. `make test` — all 872+ tests pass
4. Zero `:style` maps in any view file (except the one dynamic padding-left in render-tree-node)

---

## Summary

| Step | File | Scope |
|------|------|-------|
| 1 | app.css | Add ~10 new theme tokens |
| 2 | modals.cljs | Shared components (lines 11-82) |
| 3 | modals.cljs | Player/object target (lines 86-172) |
| 4 | modals.cljs | Card selection (lines 204-314) |
| 5 | modals.cljs | Pile/graveyard/exile/peek (lines 317-493) |
| 6 | modals.cljs | Scry modal (lines 498-579) |
| 7 | modals.cljs | X mana modal (lines 584-650) |
| 8 | modals.cljs | Mode selector (lines 655-759) |
| 9 | history.cljs | Step controls (lines 9-37) |
| 10 | history.cljs | Action log (lines 40-96) |
| 11 | history.cljs | Fork controls + sidebar (lines 99-276) |
| 12 | — | `make validate` |
close_reason: All inline :style maps in modals.cljs (56) and history.cljs (23) converted to Tailwind utility classes. Only 1 acceptable inline style remains (computed padding-left for tree depth). Added 10 new @theme tokens. make validate passes: 996 tests, 2262 assertions, 0 failures.

title: Task 4: Remove atom registry and clean up all call sites
design:
## Goal
Remove the atom-based trigger registry entirely. After this task, ALL trigger state lives in Datascript. The atom, its registration functions, and clear-registry! test fixtures are deleted.

## Context
Completed Task 1: Datascript schema and pure query/tx functions exist in trigger_db.cljs
Completed Task 2: Dual-write creates Datascript trigger entities at play-land and game init
Completed Task 3: dispatch-event reads Datascript first, atom fallback for tests
Completed Task 3 bonus: zones.cljs already retracts Datascript trigger entities on zone leave

The atom is now write-only in production (dispatch reads Datascript). Tests still use atom via fallback. This task removes ALL atom usage.

## Implementation

### 1. Remove atom fallback from dispatch-event
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger_dispatch.cljs

Remove the if/else fallback logic. dispatch-event should ONLY query Datascript:
- Remove: `(if (seq ds-triggers) ... (filter #(matches-filter? % event) (registry/get-triggers-for-event event)))`
- Keep: `(mapv #(datascript-trigger->dispatch-format db %) (trigger-db/get-triggers-for-event db enriched-event))`
- Remove: `[fizzle.engine.trigger-registry :as registry]` require

### 2. Remove register-trigger! from turn_based.cljs
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/turn_based.cljs

- Delete `register-turn-based-actions!` function entirely
- Remove `[fizzle.engine.trigger-registry :as registry]` require
- Keep `create-turn-based-triggers-tx` (pure Datascript path)

### 3. Remove register-card-triggers! from game.cljs
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

- Delete `register-card-triggers!` function (if it exists separately)
- Remove any `registry/register-trigger!` calls
- Keep Datascript tx-data creation path (trigger-db/create-triggers-for-card-tx)
- Remove `[fizzle.engine.trigger-registry :as registry]` if no longer needed

### 4. Remove atom unregister from zones.cljs
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs

- Remove: `(registry/unregister-by-source! object-id)` side effect
- Remove: `[fizzle.engine.trigger-registry :as registry]` require
- Keep: Datascript trigger entity retraction (added in Task 3)
- The `do` block can be simplified since there's no side effect anymore

### 5. Delete trigger_registry.cljs atom
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger_registry.cljs

Either delete the file entirely or gut it down to nothing. All callers should be removed first.

### 6. Update test files to remove clear-registry! fixtures
Find all test files that call clear-registry! and update:
- Remove `clear-registry!` from fixtures
- Remove `register-turn-based-actions!` calls — tests that need game-rule triggers should create them via Datascript tx-data in their test DB setup
- Remove `[fizzle.engine.trigger-registry :as registry]` require where no longer needed
- Tests that directly registered atom triggers need rewriting to create Datascript trigger entities instead

Key test files to update (10 files use clear-registry!):
- trigger_dispatch_test.cljs — tests register atom triggers, need Datascript entities
- trigger_db_integration_test.cljs — fixture uses clear-registry!
- trigger_db_test.cljs — may use clear-registry!
- cob_event_trigger_test.cljs — fixture + helper register atom triggers
- city_of_traitors_test.cljs — fixture
- turn_based_test.cljs — fixture + register-turn-based-actions! calls
- stack_test.cljs — may use clear-registry!
- Plus any others found via grep

### 7. Remove unused imports
After all changes, grep for any remaining references to trigger-registry and remove them.

## Key Considerations

### Test rewriting strategy
Tests that currently do:
```clojure
(registry/register-trigger! {:trigger/id :t1 ...})
(dispatch/dispatch-event db event)
```

Need to become:
```clojure
(let [db-with-trigger (d/db-with db (trigger-db/create-trigger-tx {...}))]
  (dispatch/dispatch-event db-with-trigger event))
```

### Game-rule triggers in tests
Tests that call `register-turn-based-actions!` need to transact game-rule triggers into their test DB:
```clojure
(let [player-eid (q/get-player-eid db :player-1)
      db-with-rules (d/db-with db (turn-based/create-turn-based-triggers-tx player-eid))]
  ...)
```

### zones.cljs purity
After removing the atom side effect, zones.cljs becomes truly pure: (db, args) -> db. The Datascript trigger retraction is part of the transaction data (pure).

## Success Criteria
- [ ] trigger_registry.cljs atom (defonce registry (atom {})) is deleted
- [ ] No calls to register-trigger!, unregister-by-source!, or clear-registry! anywhere in codebase
- [ ] zones.cljs move-to-zone has no side effects — pure (db, args) -> db
- [ ] dispatch-event has no atom fallback — Datascript only
- [ ] All test files updated (no clear-registry! fixtures)
- [ ] All existing tests pass (no regressions)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns
- NO leaving dead atom code "for later" — remove it all now
- NO breaking test isolation — each test must set up its own trigger state in Datascript
- NO leaving register-turn-based-actions! calls — all tests use create-turn-based-triggers-tx
close_reason: All atom registry code removed. trigger_registry.cljs deleted. No register-trigger!, unregister-by-source!, or clear-registry! in codebase. zones.cljs is pure. dispatch-event queries Datascript only. Fixed nil source in stack-item creation and added game-rule triggers to tap_land_test. make validate passes: 1248 tests, 2816 assertions, 0 failures.

title: Fix reviewer gaps: complete audit, migrate bypass helpers, fix history test errors
design:
## Goal
Fix all 4 gaps identified by the reviewer before closing epic fizzle-0ewp.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Gap 1: make validate exits non-zero (7 pre-existing history test errors)

The 7 failing history tests have ':db-N is not ISeqable' errors. These predate this epic but SC11 requires 'make validate passes.' Fix these tests.

**Failing tests (all in history/):**
- test-step-back-decrements-position
- test-step-back-restores-snapshot
- test-step-forward-increments-position
- test-switch-branch-changes-branch-and-restores
- test-switch-branch-to-main
- test-jump-to-event
- test-pop-entry-on-main

**Root cause:** These tests store keyword snapshots instead of real Datascript db values. The history system tries to call Datascript operations on keywords, which fails.

**Fix approach:** Read the failing tests, understand what they're asserting, and fix them to use real Datascript db values as snapshots instead of keywords. Study passing history tests in the same file for the correct pattern.

## Gap 2: Audit report incomplete — undocumented manual construction sites

The audit docstring in objects_test.cljs documents some KEPT helpers but misses these bypass sites:
- src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs:83-102 — add-library-cards helper
- src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs:529-551 — add-permanent helper
- src/test/fizzle/subs/calculator_test.cljs:58-64 — inline manual construction
- src/test/fizzle/subs/game_test.cljs:70-73, 181-184, 340-344, 378-379 — inline manual constructions

**Fix:** Either migrate these to use build-object-tx (preferred) or document them as KEPT with explicit justification in the audit docstring.

## Gap 3: add-permanent missing :object/position (structural divergence)

effects_test.cljs:add-permanent creates battlefield objects without :object/position. This structural divergence is not caught by any canary test.

**Fix:** Migrate add-permanent to use build-object-tx, or at minimum add :object/position 0.

## Gap 4: game_test.cljs add-battlefield-creature missing :object/position

subs/game_test.cljs:add-battlefield-creature creates creatures missing :object/position, :object/summoning-sick, :object/damage-marked.

**Fix:** These are subscription presentation tests that intentionally control field sets. If migrating to build-object-tx is feasible without changing test semantics, do it. If the tests specifically need to control which fields are present (e.g., testing what happens when summoning-sick is absent), document as KEPT with justification.

## Implementation order
1. Fix Gap 1 first (history test errors) — unblocks make validate
2. Fix Gaps 2-4 (audit completeness + bypass sites)
3. Run make validate — must pass clean (0 failures, 0 errors)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 7 history test errors fixed (0 errors in make validate)
- [ ] make validate exits with 0 (passes completely)
- [ ] All manual construction bypass sites either migrated to build-object-tx or documented as KEPT with justification
- [ ] Audit docstring in objects_test.cljs updated to account for ALL sites
- [ ] No regression in test count (>= 3387)

## Anti-patterns (from epic)
- ❌ NO deleting failing tests to make validate pass
- ❌ NO manual object field construction outside build-object-tx without explicit KEPT justification
- ❌ NO reducing test coverage
close_reason: Complete: commit 67c510f. All 4 reviewer gaps fixed. 7 history test errors fixed (keyword snapshots → real Datascript dbs). 6 bypass sites migrated to build-object-tx. 2 phantom-object sites documented as KEPT. make validate exits 0: 3387 tests, 0 failures, 0 errors.

title: Gold-standard templates, CLAUDE.md references, and lions_eye_diamond test
description: Final task for epic fizzle-hg1n. Three deliverables: (1) designate gold-standard reference tests in CLAUDE.md, (2) add template skeleton to docs/testing-strategy.md, (3) write lions_eye_diamond_test.cljs.
design:
## Goal
Complete the three remaining epic success criteria: gold-standard references, template skeleton, LED test.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Deliverables
