title: Wave 1: Selection System Boilerplate Reduction
design:
## Goal
Reduce the selection system's per-type boilerplate for CARD-SELECTING types by:
1. Making the ::selection-cards subscription data-driven via :selection/card-source metadata
2. Replacing the card-selection modal cond branches with a default renderer that reads selection metadata

After this task, adding a new CARD-SELECTING selection type (tutor, discard, graveyard-return, exile-cards-cost, peek-and-select) requires only: (1) selection builder and (2) execute-confirmed-selection multimethod. Non-card types (scry, storm-split, order-bottom, x-mana, mana-allocation, player-target) retain their specialized subscriptions and modals.

## Epic Context
**Epic:** fizzle-8tl0 — Test Infrastructure & Card Testing Strategy

**Requirements (IMMUTABLE):**
- Selection system subscription and modal boilerplate reduced from 4 touch points to 2 per new card-selecting type

**Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN):**
- ❌ NO breaking existing test behavior (all selection tests must pass identically)
- ❌ NO test-only methods on production code

## File Ownership
**This task OWNS (exclusive):**
- src/main/fizzle/subs/game.cljs — specifically the ::selection-cards subscription (lines 316-382)
- src/main/fizzle/views/modals.cljs — specifically the selection-modal router and card-selection-modal

**This task MUST NOT modify:**
- Any test files in src/test/ (owned by Stream 1: Test Helpers)
- CLAUDE.md or docs/ (owned by Stream 2: Strategy Docs)
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs — execute-confirmed-selection multimethod stays as-is
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection/ builders — only ADDITIVE changes (add metadata, don't change behavior)

## Effort Estimate
6-10 hours (3-4 hours investigation + design, 3-4 hours implementation, 1-2 hours testing)

## Implementation

### 1. Understand current architecture (read-only)

**::selection-cards subscription (subs/game.cljs:316-382) has 9 branches:**

| Selection Type | Card Source Logic |
|---|---|
| :pile-choice | Maps candidates IDs to card objects, sorts |
| :cast-time-targeting (objects) | Filters valid-targets, maps to objects |
| :ability-targeting (objects) | Filters valid-targets, maps to objects |
| :tutor | Intersects candidates with library, sorts |
| :discard | Queries hand directly |
| :exile-cards-cost | Maps candidates to objects |
| :peek-and-select | Maps candidates to objects |
| Default/else | Falls back to :selection/zone |

**Specialized subscriptions that BYPASS selection-cards (DO NOT TOUCH):**
- ::scry-cards (lines 400-412) — dual-pile structure
- ::order-bottom-cards (lines 418-430) — ordered sequencing
- ::storm-split-source-name (lines 387-394) — spell name string
- ::mana-allocation-state (lines 286-290) — conditional extract

**Modal router (views/modals.cljs:741-822) has 14 branches:**
- 6 non-card modals: scry, x-mana, order-bottom, storm-split, player-target (×3 via targeting), mana-allocation (nil)
- 8 card-based modals: pile-choice, object-target (×2 via targeting zone), graveyard-return, exile-cards-cost, peek-and-select, card-selection (tutor/discard default)

### 2. Add :selection/card-source metadata to builders (ADDITIVE ONLY)

For each card-selecting builder in events/selection.cljs and events/selection/ subdirectories, add:
- :selection/card-source — keyword indicating how to derive cards

Card source keywords:
- :candidates — use :selection/candidates set, map to card objects (pile-choice, exile-cards-cost, peek-and-select)
- :valid-targets — use :selection/valid-targets, filter to objects (cast-time-targeting, ability-targeting)
- :library — intersect candidates with library zone, sort (tutor)
- :hand — query hand cards directly (discard)
- :zone — fall back to :selection/zone (default/else)

This is PURELY ADDITIVE — existing subscription still works via old cond. Card-source metadata is ignored until step 3.

### 3. Replace subscription cond with data-driven dispatch

Replace the 9-branch cond in ::selection-cards with a dispatch on :selection/card-source:

(case card-source
  :candidates  (map-candidates-to-objects ...)
  :valid-targets (filter-valid-targets-to-objects ...)
  :library     (intersect-candidates-library-sort ...)
  :hand        (query-hand ...)
  :zone        (query-zone ...)
  ;; fallback for types without card-source (shouldn't happen after migration)
  nil          (legacy-cond-dispatch ...))

Extract the common query patterns into helper functions within subs/game.cljs.

### 4. Simplify modal router for card-selecting types

The card-selection-modal (lines 174-249) already handles tutor and discard generically with an is-tutor? flag. Extend this approach:
- Add :selection/modal-title, :selection/modal-instruction metadata to builders
- Default card-selection-modal reads these from selection state
- Specialized modals (graveyard-return, exile-cards-cost, peek-and-select) may still need their own renderers if UI differs significantly

**DO NOT ATTEMPT to unify fundamentally different modals:**
- scry (dual-pile drag UI) — keep as-is
- storm-split (stepper allocation) — keep as-is
- order-bottom (indexed reordering) — keep as-is
- x-mana (value stepper) — keep as-is
- player-target (two-button choice) — keep as-is
- mana-allocation (nil, inline in mana pool) — keep as-is

### 5. Verify all selection tests pass

Run make validate. Specifically check:
- src/test/fizzle/events/selection/ — all selection event tests
- src/test/fizzle/subs/ — subscription tests
- src/test/fizzle/views/ — view tests

## Success Criteria
- [ ] ::selection-cards subscription dispatches on :selection/card-source instead of per-type cond
- [ ] Selection builders for card-selecting types include :selection/card-source metadata
- [ ] Modal router simplified: fewer cond branches for card-selecting types
- [ ] All existing selection tests pass (make validate green)
- [ ] All existing subscription tests pass
- [ ] Non-card types (scry, storm-split, order-bottom, x-mana, mana-allocation, player-target) UNCHANGED
- [ ] No lint or format warnings
- [ ] No modifications to test files or CLAUDE.md/docs
- [ ] Adding a new card-selecting type requires only builder + multimethod (2 touch points, not 4)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: 8 non-card selection types must be untouched**
- scry, storm-split, order-bottom, x-mana, mana-allocation, player-target, and targeting-as-player variants
- These have fundamentally different UIs (steppers, dual-piles, reordering) and specialized subscriptions
- DO NOT attempt to make these data-driven — they are correctly specialized
- Verify all specialized subscriptions (::scry-cards, ::order-bottom-cards, ::storm-split-source-name, ::mana-allocation-state) remain unchanged

**Edge Case: Tutor vs discard sharing a modal**
- Current card-selection-modal distinguishes tutor/discard via is-tutor? flag (for "Find Nothing" button)
- Card-source metadata should include :allow-fail-to-find? so the generic modal can show/hide this button
- Test: tutor still shows "Find Nothing", discard does not

**Edge Case: Targeting split (player vs object)**
- cast-time-targeting and ability-targeting branch on targets-player? 
- If targets player → player-target-modal (non-card, keep as-is)
- If targets objects → object-target-modal (card-based, can be data-driven)
- Card-source should only be set for object-targeting variants

**Edge Case: pile-choice candidates come from outside current zones**
- pile-choice uses :selection/candidates which are specific card IDs, not a zone query
- :candidates card-source must map candidate IDs to full card objects
- Verify pile-choice still works with the generic candidates mapper

**Risk: Incremental vs big-bang migration**
- Recommend incremental: add card-source metadata to all builders first (step 2)
- Then replace subscription cond (step 3) — can keep legacy fallback for unmigrated types
- Then simplify modals (step 4) — smallest scope change
- Run make validate between each step

**Risk: Subscription performance**
- Current cond evaluates one branch. Case dispatch is equally fast.
- Helper functions should NOT introduce additional queries — just reorganize existing logic
- Verify no N+1 query patterns introduced

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO modifying events/selection.cljs execute-confirmed-selection multimethod (out of scope)
- ❌ NO modifying specialized subscriptions (scry-cards, order-bottom-cards, storm-split-source-name, mana-allocation-state)
- ❌ NO attempting to unify non-card modals (scry, storm-split, order-bottom, x-mana are fundamentally different UIs)
- ❌ NO breaking existing selection behavior (pure refactoring, same outputs for same inputs)
- ❌ NO adding test-only code to production namespaces
- ❌ NO removing existing selection types (refactor, don't reduce functionality)
- ❌ NO creating new test files (this task only modifies production subs and views)
- ❌ NO introducing new subscription queries — only reorganize existing query logic into helper functions
close_reason: Superseded: epic restructured from wave-based to linear execution model

It falls through to :default which renders zone-pick-modal, showing the player's hand instead of battlefield creatures. Same issue affects :assign-blockers. Need to add defmethods for both combat selection types.

title: Task 2: Cleanup repeat when triggered abilities fire
design:
## Goal
Implement cleanup repeat: after grant expiration, if triggered abilities fire during cleanup (e.g., discard triggers), the cleanup cycle restarts after they resolve (MTG Rule 514.3).

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-8rj: Core cleanup discard is working. begin-cleanup and complete-cleanup-discard handle hand size check and grant expiration. Now need the repeat cycle.

## Implementation

### 1. Add maybe-continue-cleanup helper (events/game.cljs)
Pure function that wraps resolution results to check for cleanup continuation:
```clojure
(defn- maybe-continue-cleanup
  "After stack resolution during cleanup, check if cleanup should restart.
   If stack is now empty and phase is :cleanup, apply begin-cleanup.
   Returns updated app-db."
  [app-db]
  (let [game-db (:game/db app-db)]
    (if (and (= :cleanup (:game/phase (queries/get-game-state game-db)))
             (queries/stack-empty? game-db :player-1)
             (not (:game/pending-selection app-db)))  ;; not already prompting
      (let [result (begin-cleanup game-db :player-1)]
        (if (:pending-selection result)
          (-> app-db
              (assoc :game/db (:db result))
              (assoc :game/pending-selection (:pending-selection result)))
          (assoc app-db :game/db (:db result))))
      app-db)))
```
This avoids duplicating the check in each ::resolve-top branch.

### 2. Modify ::resolve-top handler (events/game.cljs, line ~306)
After each resolution branch that resolves a stack item, wrap the result with maybe-continue-cleanup:
- Spell resolution branch (line ~327): wrap result in maybe-continue-cleanup
- Activated ability branch (line ~336): wrap result in maybe-continue-cleanup
- Normal trigger branch (line ~345): wrap result in maybe-continue-cleanup
- Empty stack branch: no change (nothing resolved)

Example for the normal trigger branch:
```clojure
;; Before:
resolve-trigger?
(assoc db :game/db (resolve-top-of-stack game-db :player-1))

;; After:
resolve-trigger?
(maybe-continue-cleanup
  (assoc db :game/db (resolve-top-of-stack game-db :player-1)))
```

### 3. Also wrap ::confirm-selection for :cleanup-discard
The existing :cleanup-discard case in ::confirm-selection calls complete-cleanup-discard which expires grants. After grant expiration, triggers might fire. Wrap the result:
```clojure
;; In the :cleanup-discard branch of ::confirm-selection:
(maybe-continue-cleanup
  (-> db
      (assoc :game/db (:db result))
      (dissoc :game/pending-selection)))
```
Wait — actually, after complete-cleanup-discard, triggers aren't on the stack yet. Triggers get placed on the stack by trigger dispatch, which happens during phase advancement (not during grant expiration). So the stack won't have new triggers after confirm-selection. The repeat only applies when existing triggers resolve and empty the stack. Skip this for now.

### 4. TDD Test Cases (events/turn_test.cljs)
1. `test-cleanup-repeat-after-trigger-resolves` - Set up: phase :cleanup, add trigger to stack. Resolve trigger via resolve-top-of-stack. After stack empties, begin-cleanup should re-run (re-expire grants). Bug caught: cleanup doesn't restart after triggers resolve.
2. `test-cleanup-no-repeat-when-stack-not-empty` - Set up: phase :cleanup, add 2 triggers. Resolve one. Stack still has items. begin-cleanup should NOT re-run. Bug caught: false positive - cleanup restarts with items still on stack.
3. `test-cleanup-repeat-does-not-trigger-at-other-phases` - Set up: phase :main1, add trigger. Resolve trigger. Stack empties. begin-cleanup should NOT run. Bug caught: cleanup logic fires during non-cleanup phases.
4. `test-cleanup-repeat-rechecks-hand-size` - Set up: phase :cleanup, 9 cards in hand. Complete cleanup discard (hand goes to 7). Add trigger that draws a card (would need to simulate). After trigger resolves and stack empties, begin-cleanup re-checks hand size. If still over max, new selection appears. Bug caught: hand size not rechecked after cleanup repeat.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Resolving last stack item during cleanup phase re-runs begin-cleanup
- [ ] Stack items remaining during cleanup do NOT trigger cleanup restart
- [ ] Cleanup restart does NOT fire during non-cleanup phases
- [ ] After cleanup restart, hand size is rechecked against max
- [ ] Normal cleanup (no triggers) still works correctly (existing tests pass)
- [ ] All 737+ tests pass (make test)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Infinite Loop**
- If a trigger during cleanup causes another trigger, cleanup could loop forever
- In practice this is bounded: triggers resolve finite effects, hand size eventually <= max
- No explicit loop guard needed — game state converges naturally
- If needed later, could add a max-cleanup-repeat counter

**Edge Case: Pending Selection During Cleanup**
- If resolve-top returns a pending-selection (spell with selection effect resolving during cleanup), maybe-continue-cleanup must NOT run begin-cleanup
- The guard `(not (:game/pending-selection app-db))` prevents this

**Edge Case: Stack Has Both Triggers and Spells**
- resolve-top already handles this via LIFO ordering
- maybe-continue-cleanup only checks after resolution, regardless of what was resolved

**Edge Case: resolve-top-of-stack Returns db Unchanged**
- If the trigger type has no matching resolve-trigger method (uses :default), db is unchanged
- Stack item is still removed (triggers/remove-trigger called), so stack DOES shrink
- maybe-continue-cleanup will still fire correctly when stack empties

**Reference Implementation**
- maybe-continue-cleanup follows same pattern as begin-cleanup check in ::advance-phase handler
- Reuses begin-cleanup pure function — no duplication of logic

## Anti-patterns (from epic)
- ❌ NO hardcoding 7 (uses begin-cleanup which reads get-max-hand-size)
- ❌ NO skipping cleanup repeat check (the whole point of this task)
- ❌ NO expiring grants before discard (begin-cleanup handles ordering)
- ❌ NO infinite loop without convergence guarantee
close_reason: Implemented maybe-continue-cleanup, wired into ::resolve-top handler. All 4 tests pass, make validate clean.

title: Task 1: Add Underground River card definition and deck entry

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours

## Implementation Checklist

### Step 1: Add card definition to iggy_pop.cljs
Location: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs after line 167 (after Swamp)

```clojure
;; Underground River - Pain land producing blue or black mana

(def underground-river

   :card/name "Underground River"
```

title: Extract engine/objects.cljs with build-object-tx chokepoint
design:
## Goal
Create src/main/fizzle/engine/objects.cljs with a single build-object-tx function and refactor events/init.cljs to call it. This establishes the chokepoint pattern that subsequent tasks extend to restorer, tokens, and test helpers.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code (read these files first)
- src/main/fizzle/events/init.cljs:56-78 (objects-tx) — current manual object construction for player deck
- src/main/fizzle/events/init.cljs:81-110 (opponent-deck-tx) — current manual object construction for opponent, note: generates its own UUIDs via (random-uuid)
- src/main/fizzle/events/init.cljs:139-143 (init-game-state) — note that hand-uuids are pre-generated and used to build sculpted-id-set BEFORE transacting. This means build-object-tx must accept optional UUID parameter.
- src/main/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs:97-109 — battlefield field addition (summoning-sick, damage-marked) — DO NOT duplicate this in build-object-tx
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects/tokens.cljs:13-44 — token creation (for reference, migrated in later task). Note: tokens add :object/is-token true, :object/summoning-sick, :object/damage-marked. build-object-tx API must not preclude this extension.
- src/main/fizzle/sharing/restorer.cljs:78-116 — restorer creation (for reference, migrated in later task). Note: restorer overlays :object/counters, :object/grants, :object/tapped from snapshot data.

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Create src/test/fizzle/engine/objects_test.cljs:

**build-object-tx unit tests (what bugs they catch):**
- Non-creature card (e.g. :dark-ritual) → tx has NO :object/power or :object/toughness
  - Bug caught: incorrectly adding P/T to non-creatures
- Creature card (e.g. :xantid-swarm) → tx has :object/power = 0, :object/toughness = 1 (exact values from card def)
  - Bug caught: P/T not set or set to wrong values
- All objects get: :object/id (UUID), :object/card (entity id), :object/zone, :object/owner, :object/controller, :object/tapped (false), :object/position
  - Bug caught: missing required fields (the exact bug that started this epic)
- Position for :library zone uses provided position; non-library defaults to 0
  - Bug caught: incorrect library ordering
- Battlefield zone does NOT add :object/summoning-sick or :object/damage-marked
  - Bug caught: build-object-tx overstepping into zone-transition concerns
- Creature in :hand zone → has P/T (P/T is zone-independent after unification)
  - Bug caught: P/T only set for battlefield creatures (the original bug)
- Creature in :graveyard zone → has P/T
  - Bug caught: same as above, different zone
- Custom UUID: passing :id option uses provided UUID instead of generating one
  - Bug caught: sculpted-hand tracking breaks (caller needs to know UUID pre-transact)
- Default UUID: omitting :id generates a fresh random-uuid
  - Bug caught: nil UUID when :id not provided

**Shape canary test (structural guard — most important test in this file):**
- Create a creature card definition map with known :card/types, :card/power, :card/toughness
- Call build-object-tx with zone :hand → collect key set
- Call objects-tx from init.cljs with same card in :hand → collect key set from resulting tx map
- Assert: key sets are identical
  - Bug caught: divergence between build-object-tx and any consumer that doesn't use it yet (the exact category this epic eliminates)
- Repeat for :library zone (has :object/position)
- Note: after refactoring objects-tx to call build-object-tx, this canary becomes a tautology for init.cljs — but it will catch divergence when later tasks migrate restorer/tokens/test-helpers. Keep it.

### 3. Design Decision: UUID handling
objects-tx (line 60) accepts UUIDs as a parameter because init-game-state (line 139) pre-generates hand-uuids to build sculpted-id-set before transacting. build-object-tx MUST accept an optional :id parameter:
- If :id provided → use it as :object/id
- If :id omitted → generate (random-uuid)
This preserves the sculpted-hand tracking in init-game-state without changing its logic.

### 4. Design Decision: Position semantics
- objects-tx uses map-indexed for :library (position = index), 0 for :hand/:sideboard
- build-object-tx accepts position as required parameter (caller provides it)
- This is a REQUIRED parameter, not optional, because every consumer needs it

### 5. build-object-tx API signature
```clojure
(defn build-object-tx
  "Build a Datascript tx map for one game object from card data.
   
   Parameters:
     card-data - pulled card entity with at minimum :card/types, :card/power, :card/toughness
     zone      - keyword (:hand, :library, :graveyard, :exile, :sideboard, :battlefield, :stack)
     owner-eid - Datascript entity id of owning player
     position  - integer position (0 for non-library zones, index for library)
   
   Options (keyword args):
     :id         - UUID to use (default: generate random-uuid)
     :controller - owner eid override (default: same as owner-eid)
   
   Returns a map suitable for d/transact!/d/db-with.
   Does NOT set battlefield-specific fields (summoning-sick, damage-marked).
   Those belong to zone transitions (engine/zones.cljs:move-to-zone)."
  [card-data zone owner-eid position & {:keys [id controller]}]
  ...)
```

### 6. Implementation checklist
- [ ] src/main/fizzle/engine/objects.cljs — new file
  - [ ] ns declaration requiring only what's needed (no re-frame, no datascript queries)
  - [ ] creature? helper: (contains? (set (:card/types card-data)) :creature)
  - [ ] build-object-tx function with API described above
  - [ ] Returns: {:object/id (or id (random-uuid)) :object/card card-eid :object/zone zone :object/owner owner-eid :object/controller (or controller owner-eid) :object/tapped false :object/position position}
  - [ ] If creature?: also (assoc :object/power (:card/power card-data) :object/toughness (:card/toughness card-data))
- [ ] src/main/fizzle/events/init.cljs — refactor objects-tx (line 56-78)
  - [ ] Require fizzle.engine.objects
  - [ ] Replace manual map construction with call to build-object-tx
  - [ ] objects-tx still does the d/pull for card-data and the map-indexed for position — build-object-tx just replaces the map literal construction
  - [ ] Pass UUID from uuids vector as :id option
- [ ] src/main/fizzle/events/init.cljs — refactor opponent-deck-tx (line 81-110)
  - [ ] Replace make-obj inline fn with calls to build-object-tx
  - [ ] opponent-deck-tx still does d/pull for card-data — build-object-tx receives the pulled data
- [ ] src/test/fizzle/engine/objects_test.cljs — new test file
  - [ ] All unit tests from section 2 above
  - [ ] Shape canary test from section 2 above
- [ ] Verify: make validate passes (all ~3357 tests green + lint + format)

### 7. What NOT to do
- Do NOT touch zones.cljs (battlefield fields stay there)
- Do NOT touch test_helpers.cljs yet (that's a separate task)
- Do NOT touch restorer.cljs or tokens.cljs yet (separate tasks)
- Do NOT add battlefield-specific fields (summoning-sick, damage-marked) to build-object-tx
- Do NOT change how init-game-state works (sculpted-hand logic stays the same)
- Do NOT have build-object-tx do Datascript queries (it receives pre-pulled card-data, not a db)
- Do NOT remove the token test spec from section 2 (tokens come in a later task, but testing build-object-tx with a creature card is sufficient for now)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: card-data with nil P/T**
- Some MTG creatures have \*/* power/toughness (characteristic-defining abilities). Check if any cards in src/main/fizzle/cards/ have nil :card/power or :card/toughness.
- If yes: build-object-tx should still assoc nil values (Datascript handles this). If no: add a defensive note but no special handling needed.
- Resolution: grep for :card/power in card definitions during implementation to verify.

**Edge Case: card-data with no :card/types**
- If card-data has nil :card/types, (set nil) returns #{}, and (contains? #{} :creature) returns false — safe. No defensive code needed.

**UUID Pre-generation Contract**
- init-game-state (line 139-140) generates hand-uuids BEFORE calling objects-tx to build sculpted-id-set. build-object-tx's :id option preserves this. After refactor, init-game-state still pre-generates UUIDs and passes them via :id.
- This is the trickiest part of the refactor — verify sculpted-hand tests still pass.

**card-data Shape Contract**
- build-object-tx receives pre-pulled card data, NOT a db. This means the caller is responsible for d/pull with at minimum [:card/types :card/power :card/toughness].
- Both objects-tx and opponent-deck-tx already do this pull (lines 64 and 95). After refactor, they continue pulling and pass the result to build-object-tx.
- Future tasks (restorer, tokens) will need to pull or synthesize equivalent card-data.

**card-eid vs card-data**
- objects-tx currently sets :object/card to card-eid (the Datascript entity id from d/pull). build-object-tx receives card-data (the pulled map) but needs card-eid for :object/card.
- Solution: build-object-tx takes card-eid as a separate parameter, OR the caller includes :db/id in the pull pattern and build-object-tx extracts it.
- Recommended: add card-eid as explicit parameter. Cleaner than relying on :db/id in pulled data.
- Updated signature: (build-object-tx card-eid card-data zone owner-eid position & {:keys [id controller]})

## Success Criteria
- [ ] src/main/fizzle/engine/objects.cljs exists with build-object-tx
- [ ] build-object-tx accepts card-eid, card-data, zone, owner-eid, position, and optional :id/:controller
- [ ] events/init.cljs objects-tx calls build-object-tx (no manual field construction for the object map)
- [ ] events/init.cljs opponent-deck-tx calls build-object-tx (no manual field construction)
- [ ] No creature detection logic (contains? card-types :creature + assoc P/T) remains in init.cljs
- [ ] Shape canary test exists and passes (compares key sets between build-object-tx output and init.cljs output)
- [ ] 9+ unit tests in objects_test.cljs covering creature/non-creature/zones/UUID/position
- [ ] All existing tests pass (make validate green)
- [ ] build-object-tx is a pure function (no d/q, no d/transact!, no re-frame, no side effects)

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO manual object field construction remaining in init.cljs after refactor (reason: any manual construction recreates divergence)
- ❌ NO setting summoning-sick or damage-marked in build-object-tx (reason: zone-transition concern, owned by zones.cljs)
- ❌ NO Datascript queries inside build-object-tx (reason: must be pure — receives data, returns data)
- ❌ NO changing sculpted-hand UUID pre-generation logic in init-game-state (reason: out of scope, working correctly)
close_reason: Complete: commit 7c36ff3. engine/objects.cljs created, init.cljs refactored, 13 tests (10 unit + 3 canary), 3380 tests passing.

title: Extract events/casting.cljs from game.cljs
description: First extraction: move the casting pipeline from game.cljs into events/casting.cljs. This has zero deps on resolution or priority, making it the safest starting point. Includes the pre-cast pipeline multimethod + all 8 defmethods, cast-spell-handler, spell-mode selection, and 3 event registrations. Register events with :: (native namespace). Do NOT update downstream imports yet — that happens in a later task.
design:
## Goal
Create events/casting.cljs with the casting pipeline extracted from game.cljs lines 49-301 + 822-855. Register events with :: (native namespace :fizzle.events.casting/). Tests green after this task.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing extraction patterns
- events/phases.cljs:122-155 — re-frame event handlers registered with :fizzle.events.game/ prefix
- events/lands.cljs:70-97 — same pattern: pure fns + rf/reg-event-db
- game.cljs:32-46 — current re-exports (the pattern to follow temporarily)

### 2. Create src/main/fizzle/events/casting.cljs

Namespace declaration:
```clojure
(ns fizzle.events.casting
  (:require
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as queries]
    [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]
    [fizzle.engine.static-abilities :as static-abilities]
    [fizzle.engine.targeting :as targeting]
    [fizzle.events.selection.core :as sel-core]
    [fizzle.events.selection.costs :as sel-costs]
    [fizzle.events.selection.targeting :as sel-targeting]
    [re-frame.core :as rf]))
```

Move these items from game.cljs (CUT, not copy):

**Multimethod + pipeline (game.cljs lines 56-76):**
- evaluate-pre-cast-step multimethod def
- pre-cast-pipeline vector

**8 defmethods (game.cljs lines 78-170):**
- :exile-cards-cost, :return-land-cost, :discard-specific-cost, :sacrifice-permanent-cost
- :pay-x-life, :x-mana-cost, :targeting, :mana-allocation

**Private functions (game.cljs lines 173-232):**
- initiate-cast-with-mode (defn-)
- get-valid-spell-modes (defn-)
- build-spell-mode-selection (defn-)

**Selection multimethods (game.cljs lines 235-253):**
- sel-core/execute-confirmed-selection :spell-mode
- sel-core/apply-continuation :cast-after-spell-mode

**Public handlers (game.cljs lines 256-301):**
- cast-spell-handler (defn, public — called by priority_flow later)
- rf/reg-event-db ::cast-spell (registers :fizzle.events.casting/cast-spell)

**Mode selection (game.cljs lines 822-855):**
- select-casting-mode-handler (defn, public)
- cancel-mode-selection-handler (defn, public)
- rf/reg-event-db ::select-casting-mode
- rf/reg-event-db ::cancel-mode-selection

### 3. Update game.cljs with re-exports + old keyword forwarding

Add require for casting ns:
```clojure
[fizzle.events.casting :as casting]
```

Add these 5 re-exports (these are all downstream-referenced public symbols):
```clojure
(def cast-spell-handler casting/cast-spell-handler)
(def evaluate-pre-cast-step casting/evaluate-pre-cast-step)
(def pre-cast-pipeline casting/pre-cast-pipeline)
(def select-casting-mode-handler casting/select-casting-mode-handler)
(def cancel-mode-selection-handler casting/cancel-mode-selection-handler)
```

Add old keyword forwarding (3 events that tests/history dispatch on):
```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  :fizzle.events.game/cast-spell
  (fn [db [_ opts]] (casting/cast-spell-handler db opts)))

(rf/reg-event-db
  :fizzle.events.game/select-casting-mode
  (fn [db [_ mode]] (casting/select-casting-mode-handler db mode)))

(rf/reg-event-db
  :fizzle.events.game/cancel-mode-selection
  (fn [db _] (casting/cancel-mode-selection-handler db)))
```

### 4. Verification (NOT TDD — extraction tasks use existing tests as safety net)

The existing 2100+ tests are the verification. No new test files needed.

Run in order:
```bash
# 1. Compile check — catches require errors and missing symbols
npx shadow-cljs compile test

# 2. Full validation
make validate

# 3. Completeness grep — verify nothing was left behind
# Should find ZERO matches (all casting code moved out):
grep -n 'evaluate-pre-cast-step\|pre-cast-pipeline\|initiate-cast-with-mode\|get-valid-spell-modes\|build-spell-mode-selection' src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs | grep -v 'def \|require\|re-export\|casting/'

# 4. Verify re-exports work — these should all resolve:
grep -r 'game/cast-spell-handler\|game/evaluate-pre-cast-step\|game/pre-cast-pipeline\|game/select-casting-mode-handler\|game/cancel-mode-selection-handler' src/test/ | head -5
```

### 5. Namespace loading order
casting.cljs registers defmethods on sel-core/execute-confirmed-selection and sel-core/apply-continuation. These multimethods are defined in selection/core.cljs. The defmethods register at load time. As long as game.cljs (or core.cljs) requires casting.cljs, the defmethods will be registered before any code dispatches on them.

Verify: game.cljs already requires selection modules explicitly (line 19-27). After adding casting to game.cljs requires, the load chain is: core.cljs → game.cljs → casting.cljs → sel-core (transitively loaded).

## Success Criteria
- [ ] events/casting.cljs exists at src/main/fizzle/events/casting.cljs
- [ ] Contains: evaluate-pre-cast-step multimethod + 8 defmethods
- [ ] Contains: pre-cast-pipeline vector
- [ ] Contains: cast-spell-handler, select-casting-mode-handler, cancel-mode-selection-handler
- [ ] Contains: sel-core/execute-confirmed-selection :spell-mode defmethod
- [ ] Contains: sel-core/apply-continuation :cast-after-spell-mode defmethod
- [ ] Events registered with :: (native namespace :fizzle.events.casting/)
- [ ] game.cljs re-exports 5 symbols: cast-spell-handler, evaluate-pre-cast-step, pre-cast-pipeline, select-casting-mode-handler, cancel-mode-selection-handler
- [ ] game.cljs forwards 3 old event keywords to casting handlers
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all 2100+ tests)
- [ ] No casting pipeline code remains in game.cljs (verified by grep)

## Anti-Patterns (task-specific)
- ❌ NO writing new test files for this task (existing 2100+ tests verify behavior; new 'does it load' tests are tautological)
- ❌ NO changing function signatures, parameter orders, or return types during extraction
- ❌ NO forgetting re-exports (5 symbols + 3 event keyword forwarders — test files depend on game/ prefix until normalization task)
- ❌ NO removing the selection module requires from game.cljs (casting.cljs needs them, but game.cljs also requires them for other defmethod registrations like selection/library, selection/zone-ops)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Re-export completeness is critical:**
5 test files call game/cast-spell-handler, 4 call game/evaluate-pre-cast-step, 1 calls game/pre-cast-pipeline, 1 calls game/select-casting-mode-handler, 1 calls game/cancel-mode-selection-handler. Missing any re-export breaks tests silently (ClojureScript will get nil, not an error).

**Old keyword forwarding must delegate to casting handlers:**
history/descriptions_test.cljs dispatches :fizzle.events.game/select-casting-mode and :fizzle.events.game/cancel-mode-selection. bots/interceptor_test.cljs calls game/cast-spell-handler directly. All must keep working.

**Namespace load order is safe but verify:**
game.cljs requiring casting.cljs ensures defmethods register. If shadow-cljs tree-shaking eliminates the require (it won't, because of side effects), defmethods won't register. Verify by running tests that exercise spell-mode selection (spell_mode_selection_test.cljs, mode_selection_test.cljs).
close_reason: Extracted casting pipeline into events/casting.cljs. Commit 58b10e0. 2939 tests pass.

title: Task 3: Brain Freeze integration tests + storm copy regression tests
design:
## Goal
Create integration tests for Brain Freeze (the deck's win condition - storm mill spell) and regression tests for storm copy behavior (copies cease to exist when leaving stack). These are closely related: Brain Freeze exercises the full storm pipeline end-to-end.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Pre-existing Coverage
- rules_test.cljs has cast-spell-increments-storm-test (storm count only, not trigger/copies)
- queries_test.cljs has storm count query tests
- turn_test.cljs has storm count reset test
- NO tests exist for: storm trigger resolution, storm copy creation, copies ceasing to exist, mill effect

## Implementation

### Part A: Brain Freeze card integration tests

**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/brain_freeze_test.cljs (NEW)

**Card definition** (iggy_pop.cljs):
\`\`\`clojure
{:card/id :brain-freeze
 :card/name "Brain Freeze"
 :card/cmc 2
 :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :blue 1}
 :card/colors #{:blue}
 :card/types #{:instant}
 :card/keywords #{:storm}
 :card/effects [{:effect/type :mill :effect/amount 3 :effect/target :opponent}]}
\`\`\`

**Required imports:**
\`\`\`clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.brain-freeze-test
  (:require
    [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.cards.iggy-pop :as cards]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
    [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
    [fizzle.engine.mana :as mana]
    [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]
    [fizzle.engine.triggers :as triggers]
    [fizzle.engine.zones :as zones]))
\`\`\`

**Helpers:** Use same create-test-db + add-card-to-zone pattern from dark_ritual_test.cljs. Also need:
- add-opponent helper: transact {:player/id :opponent :player/name "Opponent" :player/life 20 ...}
- add-cards-to-library helper: takes card-ids vector and adds to library with sequential positions for player

**Tests:**

1. `brain-freeze-card-definition-test`
   - Assert: card exists in all-cards with :card/id :brain-freeze
   - Assert: :card/keywords contains :storm
   - Assert: :card/mana-cost is {:colorless 1 :blue 1}
   - Assert: single effect of type :mill with :effect/amount 3 and :effect/target :opponent
   - Bug caught: Card data integrity, ensures storm keyword present

2. `brain-freeze-mills-opponent-3-cards-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add opponent, add brain-freeze to hand, add 5 cards to opponent library, add {:blue 1 :colorless 1} mana
   - Act: cast-spell, resolve-spell
   - Assert: opponent library size decreased by 3
   - Assert: opponent graveyard size increased by 3
   - Assert: brain-freeze in player graveyard (instant)
   - Assert: storm count = 1
   - Bug caught: Verifies mill effect targets opponent correctly

3. `brain-freeze-cannot-cast-without-mana-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add brain-freeze to hand, NO mana
   - Assert: (rules/can-cast? db :player-1 obj-id) returns false
   - Bug caught: Mana cost enforcement for {1}{U}

4. `brain-freeze-creates-storm-trigger-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, cast 2 Dark Rituals first (storm count = 2), add brain-freeze to hand, add mana
   - Act: cast brain-freeze (storm count becomes 3)
   - Assert: storm trigger exists on stack
   - Assert: storm trigger has :count = 2 (spells cast BEFORE brain freeze, i.e. dec(3) = 2)
   - Bug caught: Storm trigger creation with correct copy count

5. `brain-freeze-storm-copies-mill-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add opponent, add 15+ cards to opponent library
   - Cast 2 Dark Rituals first (storm count = 2)
   - Cast brain-freeze (storm count = 3, trigger with count=2)
   - Act: resolve storm trigger (creates 2 copies on stack), resolve each copy + original
   - Assert: opponent milled 9 total cards (3 per copy * 3 instances = original + 2 copies)
   - Assert: copies no longer exist in db (removed via remove-object)
   - Assert: original brain-freeze in graveyard
   - Bug caught: Full storm pipeline end-to-end, copies cease to exist

6. `brain-freeze-storm-zero-previous-spells-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add opponent, add 5 cards to opponent library
   - Cast brain-freeze as FIRST spell (storm count = 1)
   - Assert: storm trigger has count = 0 (dec(1) = 0, no copies)
   - Act: resolve trigger (no copies created)
   - Assert: only 3 cards milled (original only, no copies)
   - Bug caught: Storm edge case - first spell of game

### Part B: Storm copy regression tests

**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/brain_freeze_test.cljs (same file, separate testing section)

These regression tests specifically verify the bug fix from commit 46a281f where storm copies were going to graveyard instead of ceasing to exist.

7. `storm-copy-ceases-to-exist-on-resolve-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add a spell to stack
   - Create a copy via triggers/create-spell-copy
   - Assert: copy has :object/is-copy true
   - Act: rules/resolve-spell on the copy
   - Assert: (q/get-object db' copy-id) returns nil (entity removed from db entirely)
   - Assert: copy is NOT in graveyard zone
   - Bug caught: Regression for storm copies going to graveyard instead of ceasing to exist

8. `storm-copy-ceases-to-exist-when-countered-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add a spell, create copy
   - Act: rules/move-spell-off-stack on the copy
   - Assert: (q/get-object db' copy-id) returns nil
   - Bug caught: Copies also cease to exist when countered/fizzled, not just resolved

9. `storm-copies-dont-trigger-storm-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add brain-freeze to hand, cast it (storm=1)
   - Resolve storm trigger to create copy
   - Manually cast the copy (should not happen in game, but defensive test)
   - Assert: no new storm trigger created from the copy
   - Bug caught: Verifies :object/is-copy prevents recursive storm triggers

### Part C: Mill effect edge cases

10. `mill-with-fewer-cards-than-amount-test`
    - Setup: create-test-db, add opponent with only 1 card in library
    - Execute mill effect with amount=3
    - Assert: mills only 1 card (all available), doesn't crash
    - Bug caught: Mill graceful degradation with insufficient library

## Success Criteria
- [ ] brain_freeze_test.cljs exists with 10 tests
- [ ] Brain Freeze mills 3 cards from opponent's library when resolved
- [ ] Storm trigger created with correct copy count (dec of storm count)
- [ ] Storm copies resolve correctly and each mills 3 additional cards
- [ ] Storm copies cease to exist (removed from db) after resolution - regression for 46a281f
- [ ] Storm copies cease to exist when countered/moved off stack
- [ ] Storm copies do not trigger storm themselves
- [ ] First spell of game creates storm trigger with count=0 (no copies)
- [ ] Mill with fewer cards than amount handles gracefully
- [ ] All 806+ existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Resolving storm copies in correct order**
- Stack is LIFO. After storm trigger creates copies, they sit on top of stack above original.
- Copies resolve first (mill 3 each), then original resolves (mill 3).
- Test must resolve in stack order to be realistic.
- Use rules/resolve-spell directly on each copy ID, not resolve-top-of-stack (which involves app-db).

**Edge Case: Opponent library too small for full mill**
- get-top-n-library returns available cards when n > library size
- Mill effect uses (or (q/get-top-n-library ...) [])
- With 0 library cards, returns [], reduce over empty vec = no-op
- Test: opponent with 1 library card, mill 3 → should mill 1, not crash

**Edge Case: Storm count timing**
- cast-spell-mode calls increment-storm BEFORE maybe-create-storm-trigger
- So storm trigger gets copy-count = dec(storm-count) which equals spells before this one
- If 2 previous spells, storm count = 3 after casting brain freeze, trigger count = dec(3) = 2
- Test must verify this exact arithmetic

**Edge Case: Storm trigger with source removed**
- If source spell no longer exists when storm trigger resolves, resolve-trigger :storm returns db unchanged
- This is defensive code, but worth noting - not testing it here (unlikely scenario)

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow dark_ritual_test.cljs for create-test-db + add-card-to-zone helpers
- Follow rules_test.cljs for cast/resolve assertions
- See triggers.cljs:182-203 for storm trigger resolution
- See rules.cljs:430-454 for resolve-spell copy handling

## Anti-patterns
- NO mocking Datascript (pure function tests against real DB)
- NO test-only methods in production code
- NO testing through re-frame events (test pure functions directly)
- NO using (is (some? result)) as sole assertion - always assert specific values
- NO duplicating the exact same helper functions verbatim from other files
close_reason: All 10 tests implemented: card definition, mill effect, storm trigger creation, full storm pipeline (2 copies + original = 9 milled), storm-zero edge case, copy ceases-to-exist on resolve, copy ceases-to-exist when countered, copies don't trigger storm, mill with insufficient library. make validate passes: 816 tests, 1764 assertions.

title: Add order-bottom-modal UI component and subscription
design:
## Goal
Create the order-bottom-modal Reagent component and wiring so the player can see and interact with the order-bottom selection. This completes the user-facing feature.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-2qpv: all selection logic exists (builder, order/unorder helpers, execute, chain, multimethod, events). Events are registered:
- :fizzle.events.selection/order-card (appends to ordered)
- :fizzle.events.selection/unorder-card (removes from ordered)
- :fizzle.events.selection/any-order (shuffles remaining + fills ordered)
- :fizzle.events.selection/confirm-selection (standard confirm)

Selection state shape:
  {:selection/type :order-bottom
   :selection/candidates #{...}
   :selection/ordered [...]
   :selection/player-id :player-1
   :selection/spell-id <uuid>}

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns
- views/modals.cljs — scry-modal (3-section layout: top pile, revealed, bottom pile)
- views/modals.cljs — peek-selection-modal (card grid with click toggles)
- subs/ — existing subscriptions for pending-selection state

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Test file: src/test/fizzle/views/order_bottom_modal_test.cljs (or extend existing modal tests if pattern exists)
- Subscription test: order-bottom-selection sub returns candidates, ordered, remaining count
- Component rendering test (if existing pattern): modal renders with correct sections

Note: Views may be tested via integration tests (cast Flash of Insight, verify modal state). Check existing view test patterns.

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] subs/ — Add subscription for :order-bottom selection state (candidates, ordered, unordered computed)
- [ ] views/modals.cljs — order-bottom-modal component with:
  - Header: "Choose order for bottom of library"
  - Instructions: "Click cards to order them (top of pile first). Click ordered cards to unsequence."
  - "Unsequenced" section: clickable card grid dispatching :fizzle.events.selection/order-card
  - "Ordered" section: numbered cards, clickable to dispatch :fizzle.events.selection/unorder-card
  - "Any Order" button: dispatches :fizzle.events.selection/any-order (always visible)
  - "Confirm" button: dispatches :fizzle.events.selection/confirm-selection (visible when all cards ordered)
- [ ] views/modals.cljs or core.cljs — Wire modal display: when pending-selection type is :order-bottom, render order-bottom-modal
- [ ] Verify card names display correctly (need card-name lookup from object-id)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Order-bottom modal renders when :order-bottom selection is pending
- [ ] Clicking unsequenced card dispatches order-card and card moves to ordered section
- [ ] Clicking ordered card dispatches unorder-card and card returns to unsequenced
- [ ] "Any Order" button dispatches any-order event
- [ ] Modal disappears after any-order or confirm completes
- [ ] Card names displayed (not just UUIDs)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO modifying generic toggle-selection handler (order-bottom uses its own events)
- ❌ NO displaying ordering UI for 0-1 cards (chain logic already prevents this)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Card name display:**
- Objects in selection are UUIDs. Need to resolve to card name for display.
- Check how peek-selection-modal and scry-modal resolve object-ids to card data.

**Modal wiring:**
- Check core.cljs or views/ for the conditional that dispatches to different modals based on :selection/type.
- Add :order-bottom case to that conditional.

**Button state:**
- "Any Order" always enabled (even with 0 ordered — equivalent to full shuffle)
- "Confirm" only enabled when all candidates are in ordered vector
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 1: Storm Keyword + Card Definitions
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- Storm triggers on CAST, not resolution (rules-accurate timing)
- Storm creates a separate trigger object on the stack
- Storm trigger resolves BEFORE original spell (trigger goes on top)
- Storm copies are NOT cast (do not increment storm count)
- Storm copies do not have active storm keyword (no cascade)
- Storm count = spells cast BEFORE the storm spell this turn
- Threshold activates at exactly 7+ cards in graveyard
- Conditional effects use card-level modes (not per-effect condition checks)
- Mill handles partial library gracefully (mill N or remaining, whichever is less)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] Brain Freeze, Cabal Ritual, LED, Dark Ritual, basic lands implemented
- [ ] Storm trigger creates correct number of copies
- [ ] Copies resolve before original spell
- [ ] Threshold condition affects Cabal Ritual output
- [ ] Mill effect moves cards from library to graveyard
- [ ] Integration test: Cast 2 rituals + Brain Freeze = 3 mill resolutions (9 cards)
- [ ] All edge case tests pass (zero storm, empty library, threshold boundary)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass (lint, format)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO storm copies incrementing storm count (breaks rules: copies are not cast)
- ❌ NO condition checks inside individual effect methods (duplication: use card-level modes)
- ❌ NO storm triggering on resolution (timing: MTG rules say cast-time trigger)
- ❌ NO hardcoded card logic in engine (architecture: cards are data, engine interprets)
- ❌ NO skipping edge case tests (quality: edge cases reveal design flaws)

## Approach

Implement storm as a rules-accurate triggered ability system:

1. Extend stack to hold trigger objects alongside spells
2. When casting a spell with :storm keyword, create trigger on top of spell
3. Storm trigger resolution creates N copies (N = spells cast before this turn)
4. Copies are new objects with :object/is-copy true
5. Conditional effects use card-level :card/conditional-effects for threshold

This approach matches MTG rules precisely while building infrastructure for future triggered abilities (Prowess, ETB effects, etc).

## Architecture

### New Schema
- :trigger/id, :trigger/type, :trigger/source, :trigger/controller, :trigger/data
- :object/is-copy (boolean, default false)

### Modified Functions
- rules/cast-spell: Check for :storm keyword, create trigger if present
- rules/resolve-spell: Handle trigger resolution separately from spell resolution

### New Functions
- rules/create-storm-trigger: Creates trigger object
- rules/resolve-trigger: Multimethod dispatching on :trigger/type
- rules/create-spell-copy: Creates copy of spell on stack
- effects/execute-effect :mill: Move cards from library to graveyard
- conditions/threshold?: Check graveyard count >= 7
- rules/get-active-effects: Select effects based on conditions

### Card Definitions
- brain-freeze: {instant, :storm, mill 3}
- cabal-ritual: {instant, :threshold, add-mana BBB/BBBBB}
- lions-eye-diamond: {artifact, activated ability}
- Basic lands (swamp, island, etc)

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Phase 1 requires storm combo to work. Current engine has no triggered ability system and only one card (Dark Ritual). Cannot test storm combos.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- engine/effects.cljs:1-20 - Multimethod dispatch on :effect/type, easy to extend
- engine/rules.cljs:30-35 - Storm count tracked, incremented on cast-spell
- db/queries.cljs - get-storm-count, get-objects-in-zone available
- No infrastructure for triggers or copies

**External:**
- MTG rules: Storm triggers on cast, creates copies equal to prior spell count
- Copies are NOT cast (critical for storm count accuracy)
- Trigger resolves before spell (copies first, then original)

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Strict Rules-Accurate (Full Trigger Model) ✓

**What it is:** Model storm as a triggered ability with explicit trigger objects on stack. Trigger resolves first, creates copies, then original spell resolves.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed MTG comprehensive rules for storm timing
- Examined codebase for extension points (multimethod pattern)
- Confirmed stack can hold different object types

**Pros:**
- Accurate MTG timing
- Builds trigger infrastructure for future abilities (ETB, Prowess)
- Supports future counter-spell interactions

**Cons:**
- More complex than simplified approaches

**Chosen because:** User explicitly chose rules-accurate approach; infrastructure investment pays off for future phases

#### 2. Simplified Cast-Time Copies ❌

**What it is:** Create copies during cast-spell without explicit trigger objects. Correct resolution order but no separate trigger.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler implementation for goldfish practice

**Investigation:**
- Would work for Phase 1 but limits future interactions
- Cannot model 'counter the trigger' scenarios

**Pros:**
- Simpler implementation
- Correct resolution order

**Cons:**
- No trigger interaction support
- Technical debt for Phase 3+ (fork/replay of trigger decisions)

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** User chose rules-accurate; future phases need trigger model

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Requirements change to goldfish-only permanently

#### 3. Ultra-Simplified Resolution Multiplier ❌

**What it is:** Execute effects N+1 times during resolve-spell. No copies on stack.

**Why we looked at this:** Minimal code change

**Investigation:**
- Breaks MTG timing model entirely
- Cannot visualize copies on stack for practice

**Pros:**
- Simplest implementation

**Cons:**
- Rules-inaccurate timing
- No stack visualization for learning
- Breaks mental model for combo practice

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Defeats purpose of practice tool (learning real timing)

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Requirements pivot to non-educational tool

### Conditional Effects Design Decision

**Decision:** Card-level effect modes (Option B) over per-effect condition checks

**Rationale:**
- Avoids duplicating condition checks in every effect method
- Effects stay pure and simple
- Rules layer handles condition selection once
- Matches MTG card text structure ('Add BBB. Threshold - Add BBBBB instead.')

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Storm keyword with full trigger model
- Brain Freeze, Cabal Ritual, LED, basic lands
- Mill effect, threshold condition
- Edge case tests

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- UI (Phase 1 completion, separate task)
- Other keywords (flashback, madness)
- Sideboard cards
- Deck import/export

### Open Questions
- LED mana color choice: UI required, may stub initially
- Threshold during resolution: Should graveyard count update mid-stack? (Default: yes, recalculate per effect)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Which Phase 1 area to tackle? | Cards + Storm together | Implement both in single epic |
| Storm implementation approach? | Strict Rules-Accurate | Full trigger model, not simplified |
| Triggered ability architecture? | Yes, proceed | Explicit trigger objects on stack |
| Card definitions correct? | Yes for Phase 1 | Brain Freeze, Cabal Ritual, LED, lands |
| Conditional effects approach? | Option B: Card-level modes | No condition checks inside effects |
| Testing strategy? | Add edge cases | Comprehensive tests including boundaries |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Codebase Architecture
**Question explored:** How do effects and rules work?
**Sources consulted:**
- engine/effects.cljs - multimethod dispatch pattern
- engine/rules.cljs - cast/resolve flow, storm count tracking
- db/queries.cljs - available queries for graveyard, storm count

**Findings:**
- Clean extension points via multimethod
- Storm count already tracked but copies not implemented
- No trigger infrastructure exists

**Conclusion:** Extend existing multimethod pattern, add trigger resolution

#### MTG Storm Rules
**Question explored:** Exact timing and mechanics of storm
**Sources consulted:**
- MTG Comprehensive Rules
- Judge blogs on storm mechanics
- Gatherer rulings for Brain Freeze

**Findings:**
- Storm triggers on cast, not resolution
- Copies NOT cast (critical)
- Trigger resolves first, then spell

**Conclusion:** Need explicit trigger model for accuracy

### Dead-End Paths

#### Per-Effect Condition Checks
**Why explored:** Initial design had conditions inside each effect method
**Investigation:** User pointed out duplication concern
**Why abandoned:** Would duplicate condition logic in every effect type

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Condition checks duplicated?' → Moved to card-level effect modes (Option B)
- 'LED needs color choice UI' → May stub initially, full UI in later task
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: Storm keyword with full trigger model, Brain Freeze/Cabal Ritual/Dark Ritual cards, mill effect, threshold condition. All 75 tests pass. Integration test verifies 2 rituals + Brain Freeze = 9 cards milled.

title: Implement Urza's Bauble
description:
Implement Urza's Bauble (0-cost artifact) with oracle text: '{T}, Sacrifice this artifact: Look at a card at random in target player'\''s hand. You draw a card at the beginning of the next turn'\''s upkeep.'

Design decisions:
- Peek effect: log-only (game log message showing revealed card name, no modal/UI)
- Targeting: full player targeting using existing :player-target selection system
- Delayed draw: reuse existing :grant-delayed-draw pattern (same as Portent)

Components:
1. NEW effect type :peek-random-hand — pick random card from target player hand, log it, no state change
2. Card definition: 0-cost artifact, activated ability with tap+sacrifice-self cost, player targeting
3. Effects: [:peek-random-hand, :grant-delayed-draw] in sequence
4. Tests per CLAUDE.md requirements (min 8 for targeted ability artifact)

Edge cases:
- Empty hand: peek does nothing, delayed draw still granted
- Sacrifice feeds threshold (artifact in graveyard when effects resolve)
- Next turn upkeep = current turn + 1 regardless of active player
close_reason: Implementation complete. Reviewer approved. Commit 415b7a3.

title: Extend card color styling to hand, modals, graveyard, and opening hand
design:
## Goal
Extract the type border and color identity background helpers from views/battlefield.cljs to a shared namespace, then apply them to all card rendering views: hand, modals, graveyard, and opening hand.

## Requirements
- Type border colors (creature green, artifact gray, enchantment purple, land brown) apply to cards everywhere they render
- MTG color identity background tints apply to cards everywhere they render
- Selected/highlighted states layer ON TOP of the color styling (selection border overrides type border when selected)
- Tapped styling still applies where relevant
- Existing selection UX preserved — color styling is additive, not replacing selection indicators

## Implementation

1. Extract helpers to shared namespace
   - Create src/main/fizzle/views/card_styles.cljs
   - Move get-type-border-class and get-color-identity-bg-class from battlefield.cljs
   - Update battlefield.cljs to require from card_styles
   - Helpers are pure functions: card data → CSS class string

2. Apply to hand view (views/hand.cljs)
   - card-view (lines 8-18): replace hardcoded border-border/bg-surface-raised with type border + color identity bg
   - Selected state: keep border-border-selected but use color identity bg
   - Cards in hand are not tapped, so tapped?=false always

3. Apply to selection modals (views/modals.cljs)
   - selection-card-view (lines 161-171): add type border + color identity bg as base, selection styling overlays on top
   - object-target-card-view (lines 86-95): same approach
   - scry-card-view (lines 394-405): add color identity bg tint, but scry pile colors (green/red/blue) take priority for border
   - order-bottom-card-view (lines 470-487): add type border + color identity bg

4. Apply to graveyard view (views/graveyard.cljs)
   - graveyard-card-view (lines 8-25): add type border + color identity bg as base
   - Selected/castable/flashback states overlay on top of color styling

5. Apply to opening hand view (views/opening_hand.cljs)
   - card-view (lines 10-23): add type border + color identity bg
   - Selected/sculpted states overlay on top

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Helpers extracted to views/card_styles.cljs and used by all 5 view files
- [ ] Hand cards show type borders and color identity tints
- [ ] Modal selection cards show type borders and color identity tints
- [ ] Graveyard cards show type borders and color identity tints
- [ ] Opening hand cards show type borders and color identity tints
- [ ] Selected/highlighted states still visually distinct on top of color styling
- [ ] Battlefield still works (regression check after helper extraction)
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO duplicating helper functions across view files (extract once, import everywhere)
- ❌ NO removing existing selection/state indicators (color styling is additive)
- ❌ NO inline CSS color values (use existing CSS custom properties)
- ❌ NO changing the classification logic (creature > land > other priority unchanged)

## Key Considerations

**Edge Case: Selection State Layering**
- When a card is selected (e.g., in a discard modal), the selection border (border-border-selected or border-border-accent) should override the type border
- But the color identity background tint should remain visible even when selected
- Implementation: use type border as default, selection border as override via conditional class

**Edge Case: Graveyard Special States**
- Graveyard has 3 states: selected, castable (flashback), non-castable
- Selected: selection border overrides type border, color identity bg remains
- Castable: flashback border may override type border, color identity bg remains
- Non-castable: type border + color identity bg both show

**Edge Case: Scry Pile Colors**
- Scry modal uses green/red/blue borders for top/bottom/unassigned piles
- These functional borders should take priority over type borders
- Color identity background tint can still apply underneath

**Backward Compatibility**
- battlefield.cljs must still work after extraction (require from new namespace)
- All existing tests must pass unchanged

title: Implement history data model and core operations
design:
## Goal

Build the pure-function foundation for the history system: data structures, initialization, entry management, stepping, forking, branch switching, and effective-entry-list computation. All operations are pure functions on app-db — no re-frame integration yet.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Create src/main/fizzle/history/core.cljs

Define data structures and pure operations:

#### Data initialization
- `init-history` — Returns initial history state map to merge into app-db:
  ```clojure
  {:history/main []
   :history/forks {}
   :history/current-branch nil
   :history/position -1}
  ```
  Position -1 means "no entries recorded yet." After the first entry is appended, position becomes 0.

#### Entry operations
- `make-entry [game-db event-type description turn]` — Creates an entry map:
  ```clojure
  {:entry/snapshot game-db
   :entry/event-type event-type
   :entry/description description
   :entry/turn turn}
  ```
- `append-entry [db entry]` — Appends entry to current branch tip, sets position to new tip index. PRECONDITION: must be at tip (at-tip? returns true) or position is -1 (empty history). If not at tip, returns db unchanged (caller should use auto-fork instead).

#### Stepping
- `step-to [db position]` — Sets :history/position, swaps :game/db to the :entry/snapshot at that position in effective entries. Returns updated db. If position < 0 or position >= entry-count, returns db unchanged (no-op on invalid bounds).
- `at-tip? [db]` — Returns true if position equals (dec (entry-count db)), or if position is -1 and history is empty.
- `can-step-back? [db]` — Returns true if position > 0.
- `can-step-forward? [db]` — Returns true if position < (dec (entry-count db)).

#### Forking
- `auto-fork [db entry]` — Creates new fork at current position with entry as first fork entry. Fork-id generated via (random-uuid). Fork name auto-generated as "Fork N" where N is (inc (count forks)). Sets :history/current-branch to new fork-id. Sets position to (branch-point + 1). Returns updated db.
  ```clojure
  ;; Fork structure:
  {:fork/id <uuid>
   :fork/name "Fork 1"
   :fork/branch-point 5       ;; INCLUSIVE — last index from parent included
   :fork/parent nil            ;; nil = parent is main, or fork-id for nested
   :fork/entries [entry]}      ;; First entry of the new branch
  ```
- `create-named-fork [db name]` — Creates empty fork at current position with given name. Does NOT switch to it. Does NOT add any entries. Returns updated db with new fork in :history/forks.

#### Branch-point semantics (CRITICAL)
Branch-point is INCLUSIVE — it is the last index from the parent's effective entries that is included in this fork's effective entries. Example:
```
main: [e0, e1, e2, e3, e4, e5]
Player at position 3, takes new action → auto-fork
Fork: {:branch-point 3, :entries [new_entry]}
Effective entries: [e0, e1, e2, e3, new_entry]  (indices 0-4)
Main is unchanged: [e0, e1, e2, e3, e4, e5]
```

For nested forks, branch-point refers to an index in the PARENT's effective entry list (not the parent's local entries):
```
Fork A effective: [e0, e1, e2, e3, fa0, fa1]  (6 entries)
Player at position 4 in Fork A, takes new action → auto-fork from Fork A
Fork B: {:branch-point 4, :parent fork-a-id, :entries [new_entry]}
Fork B effective: [e0, e1, e2, e3, fa0, new_entry]  (6 entries)
```

#### Branch operations
- `switch-branch [db fork-id]` — Sets :history/current-branch to fork-id (or nil for main). Sets position to tip of that branch. Swaps :game/db to snapshot at tip. If fork-id is not nil and not found in :history/forks, returns db unchanged (no-op).
- `get-fork [db fork-id]` — Returns fork map from :history/forks, or nil if not found.
- `list-forks [db]` — Returns vector of all fork maps (vals of :history/forks).

#### Effective entries
- `effective-entries [db]` — Computes full entry list for current branch by walking parent chain:
  - If on main (current-branch nil): returns :history/main
  - If on a fork: recursively get parent's effective entries, take first (inc branch-point) entries, concat with fork's entries
  ```clojure
  (defn effective-entries-for-branch [db fork-id]
    (if (nil? fork-id)
      (:history/main db)
      (let [fork (get-fork db fork-id)
            parent-entries (effective-entries-for-branch db (:fork/parent fork))]
        (into (vec (take (inc (:fork/branch-point fork)) parent-entries))
              (:fork/entries fork)))))
  ```
- `current-entry [db]` — Returns entry at current position, or nil if position is -1 (no entries).
- `entry-count [db]` — Returns (count (effective-entries db)).

### 2. Create src/test/fizzle/history/core_test.cljs

Use mock game-db values (distinct keywords like :db-0, :db-1 etc.) to verify snapshot swapping without needing real Datascript.

#### Test cases (22 tests):

**Initialization:**
- init-history returns map with all four keys, empty main, empty forks, nil branch, position -1

**Entry creation and appending:**
- make-entry creates entry map with all four fields (:entry/snapshot, :entry/event-type, :entry/description, :entry/turn)
- append-entry on empty history (position -1) adds to main, sets position to 0
- append-entry at tip of main adds entry, increments position
- append-entry at tip of fork adds to fork's entries, increments position
- append-entry when NOT at tip returns db unchanged (precondition violation)

**Stepping:**
- step-to valid position updates :history/position and swaps :game/db to snapshot at that position
- step-to position 0 from later position (rewind to start)
- step-to out of bounds (negative) returns db unchanged
- step-to out of bounds (>= count) returns db unchanged
- can-step-back? returns false at position 0, true at position > 0
- can-step-forward? returns false at tip, true before tip
- at-tip? returns true when position equals last index, true when empty history

**Forking:**
- auto-fork at position 3 of 6-entry main creates fork with branch-point 3
- auto-fork preserves original main entries (all 6 still present)
- auto-fork sets current-branch to new fork-id
- auto-fork entry appears as last in effective entries
- auto-fork from a fork creates nested fork with correct parent

**Branch switching:**
- switch-branch to fork updates current-branch, position to fork tip, game-db to fork tip snapshot
- switch-branch to nil returns to main tip
- switch-branch to non-existent fork-id returns db unchanged

**Effective entries:**
- effective-entries on main returns :history/main
- effective-entries on fork returns parent[0..branch-point] ++ fork entries
- effective-entries on nested fork (3 levels: main -> fork A -> fork B) correctly walks full parent chain

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All 22+ tests pass: make test
- [ ] All operations are pure functions — no require of re-frame, no side effects, no atoms
- [ ] effective-entries on 3-level nested fork returns correct combined list
- [ ] step-to at any valid position swaps :game/db to that entry's snapshot
- [ ] auto-fork from rewound position preserves original timeline unchanged
- [ ] append-entry at non-tip returns db unchanged (does not corrupt history)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty History**
- Position -1 means no entries. current-entry returns nil. at-tip? returns true (nothing to step forward to). can-step-back? returns false. can-step-forward? returns false. append-entry works (transitions from -1 to 0).

**Edge Case: Fork at Position 0**
- Branch-point 0 means effective entries = [parent-entry-0] ++ fork-entries. This is valid — the fork diverges right after the initial state.

**Edge Case: Deeply Nested Forks (3+ levels)**
- effective-entries must recurse through parent chain correctly. Test with 3 levels: main -> fork-A -> fork-B. Each level takes a prefix of its parent and appends local entries.

**Edge Case: Switch to Non-existent Fork**
- Return db unchanged. Do NOT throw — callers should handle gracefully.

**Edge Case: Append When Not at Tip**
- Return db unchanged. The caller (interceptor in future task) must check at-tip? and call auto-fork instead. This separation keeps core operations simple.

**Branch-Point Semantics**
- Branch-point is INCLUSIVE (last parent index included). This is the most critical invariant. Off-by-one here corrupts all effective entry computations. Test thoroughly with concrete index assertions.

**Fork-ID Generation**
- Use (random-uuid) for fork IDs. Fork names use sequential counter: "Fork N" where N = (inc (count (:history/forks db))). Names are display-only; IDs are for lookup.

## Anti-Patterns (Task-Level)

- NO re-frame dependency in history/core.cljs (pure functions only — re-frame integration is a separate task)
- NO atoms or mutable state (all functions take and return immutable maps)
- NO throwing exceptions for invalid input (return db unchanged for out-of-bounds, non-existent forks, non-tip append)
- NO string-based fork IDs (use UUIDs for uniqueness)
- NO flattened fork storage (maintain parent chain for correct nested fork computation)
close_reason: All 27 tests pass (899 total). Pure functions only, no re-frame. 3-level nested fork effective-entries verified. make validate clean.

title: Bug: Bot casts bypass priority — human never sees bolt on stack
description: When burn bot casts Lightning Bolt, yield-impl returns continue-yield? true without calling negotiate-priority. The bolt resolves immediately without giving the human player a priority window, even with phase stops set.
design:
## Goal
Fix the bug where the human player never gets priority to respond to bot-cast spells. Currently, Lightning Bolt resolves immediately without the human seeing it on the stack.

## Root Cause
In engine/game_loop.cljs:58, negotiate-priority auto-passes the human opponent whenever is-bot-turn is true:

    (if (or auto-mode is-bot-turn)
      (priority/yield-priority gdb opp-eid)
      ...)

During the bot's turn, the human is ALWAYS auto-passed, even when there are items on the stack that they should respond to. The bot casts a bolt (adds to stack), yield-impl returns continue-yield, next yield-impl calls negotiate-priority which auto-passes the human, both-passed? becomes true, stack resolves — human never gets a priority window.

## Fix
Change the condition in negotiate-priority (engine/game_loop.cljs line 58) from:
    (or auto-mode is-bot-turn)
to:
    (or auto-mode (and is-bot-turn (queries/stack-empty? gdb)))

This way:
- Bot's turn, stack EMPTY (phase advancement): human auto-passed (no need to respond to phase changes)
- Bot's turn, stack NON-EMPTY (bolt on stack): human NOT auto-passed (gets priority to respond)
- F6/auto-mode active: human auto-passed regardless (F6 means resolve everything)

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] negotiate-priority returns all-passed? false when it's bot's turn and stack is non-empty (unit test on negotiate-priority directly)
- [ ] negotiate-priority returns all-passed? true when it's bot's turn and stack is empty (regression test — existing behavior preserved)
- [ ] negotiate-priority returns all-passed? true when auto-mode is set even with stack non-empty (F6 override)
- [ ] Existing test negotiate-priority-bot-turn-both-pass (game_loop_test.cljs:83) still passes unchanged
- [ ] Integration: full ::yield dispatch with bolt on stack during bot turn transfers priority to human (continue-yield? is false, human has priority)
- [ ] Integration: after human yields through bolt resolution, bot can cast another bolt — priority cycle repeats
- [ ] All existing tests pass (make validate clean)
- [ ] 5+ new tests total (3 unit on negotiate-priority, 2 integration via yield-impl or ::yield)

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] In engine/game_loop.cljs line 58, change condition:
  Before: (if (or auto-mode is-bot-turn)
  After:  (if (or auto-mode (and is-bot-turn (queries/stack-empty? gdb)))
- [ ] In test/fizzle/engine/game_loop_test.cljs, add negotiate-priority unit tests:
  - negotiate-priority-human-not-auto-passed-when-stack-non-empty-on-bot-turn:
    Setup: create-test-db with opponent :burn, switch active to bot, cast a spell onto stack (e.g., dark-ritual from bot's hand)
    Assert: (is (false? (:all-passed? result))) — catches the auto-pass bug
  - negotiate-priority-human-auto-passed-when-stack-empty-on-bot-turn:
    Setup: create-test-db with opponent :burn, switch active to bot, stack empty
    Assert: (is (true? (:all-passed? result))) — regression guard
  - negotiate-priority-auto-mode-overrides-stack-check:
    Setup: create-test-db with opponent :burn, switch active to bot, cast spell onto stack, set auto-mode :resolving
    Assert: (is (true? (:all-passed? result))) — F6 still works
- [ ] In test/fizzle/bots/burn_integration_test.cljs (or new file), add integration tests:
  - yield-impl-stops-when-bot-casts-bolt:
    Setup: setup-burn-bot-app-db with 1 mountain + 1 bolt, call yield-impl
    Assert: (is (not (:continue-yield? result))) after the second yield-impl call (first casts, second should stop for human priority)
    Assert: stack is non-empty in result db (bolt still on stack)
  - yield-impl-full-priority-cycle-with-bolt:
    Setup: setup-burn-bot-app-db, call yield-impl (bot casts), verify stops
    Then simulate human yield: call yield-impl again (both should pass, bolt resolves)
    Assert: stack empty, human life = 17
- [ ] Run make validate

## Key Considerations

**Priority Flow After Fix (traced step by step):**
1. Bot has priority during main1. yield-impl checks bot-priority-decision → returns :cast-spell
2. execute-bot-priority-action runs: auto-tap Mountain, cast-spell-mode, store target on stack-item
3. yield-impl returns {continue-yield? true} (bolt now on stack)
4. Next yield-impl: bot-priority-decision returns :pass (stack-empty? is false)
5. Falls through to negotiate-priority
6. negotiate-priority: yield bot's priority. Check human: auto-mode? No. is-bot-turn && stack-empty? No (stack has bolt). Human NOT auto-passed.
7. both-passed? false → transfer priority to human
8. yield-impl returns {continue-yield? false} — human sees bolt on stack

**Edge Case: Multiple Bolts Per Turn**
After bolt resolves, stack is empty. Bot gets priority again. bot-priority-decision checks has-untapped-red-source? + bolt in hand → may return another :cast-spell. Cycle repeats. Each bolt gives human a priority window.

**Edge Case: F6 During Bot's Turn**
When auto-mode is active (set by ::yield-all), the (or auto-mode ...) check passes immediately regardless of stack state. Human is auto-passed. Bolt resolves automatically. This is correct — F6 means resolve everything without stopping.

**Edge Case: Human Opponent Stops During Bot's Turn**
Stops are checked in handle-loop-state :bot-phase (game_loop.cljs:151-153), not in negotiate-priority. The fix doesn't affect stop behavior. Stops only trigger during phase advancement, not during stack resolution.

**Existing Test Compatibility:**
The existing test negotiate-priority-bot-turn-both-pass (line 83) sets up a bot turn with empty stack. After the fix, (and is-bot-turn (queries/stack-empty? gdb)) is true, so human IS auto-passed. Test should pass unchanged.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Do NOT add a new flag/state for "bot just cast" — use stack-empty? which is already the natural indicator
- ❌ Do NOT modify yield-impl for this fix — the issue is in negotiate-priority's auto-pass logic
- ❌ Do NOT change bot-priority-decision behavior — it correctly returns :pass when stack is non-empty
- ❌ Do NOT use hardcoded player-ids in tests — use :player-2 via add-opponent helper
