These are tested INDIRECTLY through card tests (cast-with-target, confirm-selection), but have no dedicated integration tests. Discovered during fizzle-87pw investigation. Design TBD — brainstorm before starting.

title: Create engine/resolution.cljs multimethod and migrate :spell/:storm-copy defmethods
design:
## Goal
Create the resolve-stack-item multimethod in engine/resolution.cljs with :spell and :storm-copy defmethods. These two types share the most logic and are the core of the resolution unification. This task proves the multimethod pattern works end-to-end.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - events/selection/resolution.cljs:74-157 — resolve-spell-with-selection (being replaced)
   - engine/rules.cljs:418-448 — resolve-spell (being absorbed)
   - engine/rules.cljs:411-415 — permanent-type? helper (reuse in zone transition)
   - engine/effects.cljs — reduce-effects returns {:db} or {:db :needs-selection effect}
   - engine/stack.cljs:97-124 — resolve-effect-target sets :effect/target with concrete value

2. Create engine/resolution.cljs
   - Requires: engine/effects, engine/stack, engine/rules (for get-active-effects, permanent-type?), engine/zones, engine/queries, datascript.core
   - Multimethod: (defmulti resolve-stack-item (fn [_db _player-id stack-item] (:stack-item/type stack-item)))
   - Shared helper resolve-spell-effects [db player-id stack-item object-id]:
     a. Get card via queries/get-object
     b. Get effects via rules/get-active-effects using card and object-id
     c. Read targets from (:stack-item/targets stack-item)
     d. Pre-resolve ALL target refs: (mapv #(stack/resolve-effect-target % source-id controller stored-targets) effects-list)
     e. Execute via (effects/reduce-effects db player-id resolved-effects source-id)
     f. Return {:db db'} or {:db db' :needs-selection effect}
   - Zone-transition helper move-resolved-spell [db object-id]:
     a. Get object, check :object/is-copy — if copy, call zones/remove-object (copies cease to exist per MTG rules)
     b. Check :object/cast-mode for :mode/on-resolve (flashback → :exile)
     c. Check (rules/permanent-type? card-types) → :battlefield
     d. Default → :graveyard
     e. Call zones/move-to-zone
     (Logic extracted from rules.cljs:438-448)
   - :spell defmethod:
     a. Get object-ref from stack-item, resolve to object-id (same pattern as resolve-one-item:360-363)
     b. Verify spell is on stack (if not, return {:db db} — no-op)
     c. Call resolve-spell-effects
     d. If :needs-selection, return as-is (stack-item stays, selection built by caller)
     e. If done, call move-resolved-spell, return {:db db'}
   - :storm-copy defmethod:
     a. Same as :spell — delegates to resolve-spell-effects + move-resolved-spell
     b. Identical logic because storm copies have :stack-item/object-ref and resolve like spells
     (Could be (defmethod resolve-stack-item :storm-copy [db pid si] (resolve-stack-item-spell db pid si)) sharing impl)

3. Update effects.cljs target reads (3 locations)
   After pre-resolution, resolve-effect-target has already set :effect/target with concrete IDs.

   a. :destroy (effects.cljs:470-483):
      - Already checks (:effect/target effect) first (line 472) ✅
      - Fallback to :object/targets (lines 475-478) becomes dead code
      - REMOVE the fallback: simplify to just use (:effect/target effect)
      - If nil, no-op (unchanged behavior)

   b. :grant-flashback (effects.cljs:578-594):
      - Currently reads (:effect/target-ref effect) then looks up via (:object/targets source-obj)
      - CHANGE to: read target-id from (:effect/target effect) directly
      - If nil, no-op (unchanged behavior)

   c. :add-restriction (effects.cljs:622-639):
      - Currently: :targeted-player case reads (get-in source-obj [:object/targets :player] player-id)
      - After pre-resolution, :any-player effects have :effect/target set to concrete player-id
      - CHANGE :targeted-player case to: read (:effect/target effect) with fallback to player-id
      - Other cases (:self, :opponent) unchanged — they don't use :object/targets

4. Wire into resolve-one-item (partial migration)
   - For :spell and :storm-copy branches in resolve-one-item (game.cljs:358-368):
     a. Replace call to resolution/resolve-spell-with-selection with resolution/resolve-stack-item
     b. If result has :needs-selection, build pending-selection via core/build-selection-for-effect
     c. If done, remove stack-item from stack
   - Keep existing paths for :activated-ability and :storm (migrated in next task)
   - Keep resolve-stack-item-effects for now (used by :storm and default branches)

5. Write tests (TDD — RED first)
   Test file: src/test/fizzle/engine/resolution_test.cljs (new)

   a. test-spell-no-targets-resolves-and-moves-to-graveyard
      - Create spell with no targeting, cast it, resolve via multimethod
      - Assert: effects executed (e.g., draw effect → hand size increased)
      - Assert: spell moved to graveyard
      - Bug caught: zone transition regression

   b. test-spell-with-targets-uses-stack-item-targets
      - Create targeted spell (e.g., Orim's Chant), set :stack-item/targets
      - Do NOT set :object/targets
      - Resolve via multimethod
      - Assert: effect applied to correct target
      - Bug caught: target unification regression (would fail if code reads :object/targets)

   c. test-spell-interactive-effect-returns-needs-selection
      - Create spell with tutor/discard effect
      - Resolve via multimethod
      - Assert: result contains :needs-selection with the interactive effect
      - Assert: spell NOT moved to graveyard yet (stays on stack)
      - Bug caught: selection pausing regression

   d. test-storm-copy-resolves-and-ceases-to-exist
      - Create storm copy (is-copy: true), resolve via multimethod
      - Assert: effects executed
      - Assert: object removed from db (copies cease to exist)
      - Bug caught: copy cleanup regression

   e. test-spell-flashback-exiles-after-resolution
      - Create spell with :object/cast-mode {:mode/on-resolve :exile}
      - Resolve via multimethod
      - Assert: spell moved to exile (not graveyard)
      - Bug caught: cast-mode destination regression

   f. test-spell-empty-effects-still-moves-to-graveyard
      - Create spell with empty effects list (or no matching conditional effects)
      - Resolve via multimethod
      - Assert: spell moved to graveyard despite no effects
      - Bug caught: premature return on empty effects

   g. test-spell-not-on-stack-is-noop
      - Create spell in hand (not on stack)
      - Resolve via multimethod
      - Assert: db unchanged
      - Bug caught: missing zone guard

## Success Criteria
- [ ] engine/resolution.cljs exists with multimethod and :spell/:storm-copy defmethods
- [ ] Spell resolution reads targets from :stack-item/targets only (verify: grep -r 'object/targets' engine/resolution.cljs returns 0 hits)
- [ ] Zone transition helper handles: copy removal, cast-mode destination, permanent-type check, default graveyard
- [ ] effects.cljs :destroy fallback to :object/targets removed (line 475-478 gone)
- [ ] effects.cljs :grant-flashback reads :effect/target instead of :object/targets
- [ ] effects.cljs :add-restriction :targeted-player reads :effect/target instead of :object/targets
- [ ] resolve-one-item delegates :spell/:storm-copy to new multimethod
- [ ] 7+ new tests in engine/resolution_test.cljs covering scenarios above
- [ ] All existing tests pass: make validate clean
- [ ] Cephalid Coliseum discard-with-player-target still works (run its test file)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Empty Effects List**
- Spell with no effects (or conditional effects where none match) must still move to destination zone
- resolve-spell-effects should return {:db db} (unchanged), then move-resolved-spell still runs
- Test: test-spell-empty-effects-still-moves-to-graveyard

**Edge Case: Spell Not On Stack**
- Data integrity guard: if spell object zone != :stack, return {:db db} no-op
- Current rules/resolve-spell has this check (line 429)
- Must preserve in :spell defmethod
- Test: test-spell-not-on-stack-is-noop

**Edge Case: Copy Zone Handling**
- :object/is-copy true → zones/remove-object (copies cease to exist per MTG rules 707.2)
- :object/is-copy false → move to destination zone
- This distinction is in move-resolved-spell helper
- Test: test-storm-copy-resolves-and-ceases-to-exist

**Edge Case: Cast Mode Destination**
- Flashback sets :object/cast-mode with :mode/on-resolve :exile
- Must check cast-mode BEFORE permanent-type check
- Priority: cast-mode > permanent-type > default(:graveyard)
- Test: test-spell-flashback-exiles-after-resolution

**Target Pre-Resolution Completeness**
- resolve-effect-target handles: :effect/target-ref, :self, :controller, :any-player, passthrough
- After mapv pre-resolution, ALL effects have :effect/target set to concrete values
- Effects that previously reached back to :object/targets will find :effect/target populated
- Critical: if an effect has BOTH :effect/target and :effect/target-ref, resolve-effect-target correctly uses target-ref (line 110-111)

**Partial Migration Safety**
- Only :spell/:storm-copy move to new path in this task
- :activated-ability and :storm keep old paths (resolve-stack-item-effects, sel-storm)
- resolve-one-item will have mixed dispatch: new multimethod for spell/storm-copy, old cond branches for others
- This is intentional — full migration in next task
- Verify: resolve-stack-item-effects still called for :storm and default branches

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO reading :object/targets in engine/resolution.cljs (target unification: stack-item is single source)
- ❌ NO duplicating zone transition logic (extract move-resolved-spell, use in both :spell and :storm-copy)
- ❌ NO calling execute-effect directly — must use reduce-effects (selection awareness: only reduce-effects returns :needs-selection)
- ❌ NO modifying resolve-stack-item-effects in this task (partial migration: keep working for ability/storm paths)
- ❌ NO test helpers that reimplement resolution logic (test through production multimethod)
close_reason: All implementation complete: multimethod in engine/resolution.cljs, spell/storm-copy defmethods, effects.cljs target reads updated, resolve-one-item wired, 8 new tests, make validate clean (1472 tests, 3798 assertions, 0 failures)

title: Add q-safe wrapper and replace all d/q calls
description: Add q-safe and pull-safe to db/queries.cljs, replace every d/q call in the codebase, add lint rule, restore :advanced optimizations.
design:
## Goal
Add q-safe and pull-safe wrappers, replace ALL d/q and d/pull calls in src/main/, add lint rules for both, restore :advanced optimizations. Single task — atomic change.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Implementation

1. Add to db/queries.cljs:
   - reintern-kw: (defn- reintern-kw [arg] (if (keyword? arg) (keyword (namespace arg) (name arg)) arg))
   - q-safe: wraps d/q, re-interns keyword args passed as :in params
   - pull-safe: wraps d/pull, re-interns keywords in lookup ref (if 2nd arg is vector)

2. Replace ALL d/q calls with q-safe across src/main/ (~30 calls in ~10 files)
   - db/queries.cljs (internal calls use q-safe too, d/q only inside q-safe impl)
   - engine/effects.cljs, engine/grants.cljs
   - sharing/restorer.cljs, sharing/extractor.cljs
   - subs/game.cljs
   - bots/protocol.cljs, bots/rules.cljs
   - events/init.cljs
   - Any other files found via grep

3. Replace ALL d/pull calls with pull-safe across src/main/

4. Add lint-datascript target to Makefile:
   - grep for (d/q and (d/pull outside db/queries.cljs in src/main/ — fail if found
   - Wire into make validate

5. Switch shadow-cljs.edn: remove :release {:compiler-options {:optimizations :simple}}

6. Verify:
   - make validate passes (tests + lint + format)
   - make release produces ~1.1MB main.js
   - Release build works in browser (Start Game, play a few actions)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] q-safe and pull-safe exist in db/queries.cljs
- [ ] Zero (d/q calls outside db/queries.cljs in src/main/
- [ ] Zero (d/pull calls outside db/queries.cljs in src/main/
- [ ] make lint-datascript passes
- [ ] shadow-cljs.edn uses default :advanced for release (no :simple override)
- [ ] make release produces ~1.1MB main.js
- [ ] Release build Start Game works in browser
- [ ] make validate passes (3051+ tests)

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO leaving any d/q or d/pull call unreplaced — blanket replacement, zero classification
- ❌ NO adding wrappers to a new file — keep in db/queries.cljs
- ❌ NO skipping lint rule for either d/q or d/pull
- ❌ NO lint checking test files — only src/main/ (tests use node compilation, not release)
close_reason: q-safe wrapper approach doesn't work — Datascript corruption is in stored datoms (hash numbers instead of keywords), not just query parameter binding. Re-interning keywords at query time doesn't help when stored values are already corrupted. Reverted.

title: Task 2: Implement pile choice selection phase
design:
## Goal
Implement the pile choice selection phase that triggers after multi-select tutor confirmation when :selection/pile-choice is present. Player chooses which card(s) go to hand, rest go to graveyard.

## Context
Completed fizzle-kd6: Multi-select tutor selection now works. Selection state includes :selection/pile-choice from the tutor effect. Need to chain pile choice after tutor confirmation.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study current tutor confirmation flow
- events/game.cljs ::confirm-tutor-selection - currently executes tutor then clears selection
- CRITICAL CHANGE: When pile-choice present, skip execute-tutor-selection (don't move cards yet)
- Instead: Build pile-choice selection with the selected card IDs
- Cards remain in library until pile choice confirms

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Create tests in src/test/fizzle/events/pile_choice_test.cljs:

```clojure
;; Test: build-pile-choice-selection creates correct state
;; Setup: 3 card IDs, pile-choice config {:hand 1 :graveyard :rest}
;; Action: (build-pile-choice-selection card-ids pile-choice player-id spell-id)
;; Assert:
;;   - (:selection/type result) = :pile-choice
;;   - (:selection/candidates result) = set of 3 card IDs
;;   - (:selection/hand-count result) = 1
;;   - (:selection/player-id result) = player-id
;;   - (:selection/allow-random result) = true
;;   - (:selection/selected result) = #{}

;; Test: execute-pile-choice moves 1 to hand, 2 to graveyard
;; Setup: db with 3 cards in library, selection with hand-selected = #{card-1}
;; Action: (execute-pile-choice db selection)
;; Assert:
;;   - card-1 zone = :hand
;;   - card-2 zone = :graveyard
;;   - card-3 zone = :graveyard
;;   - library count unchanged (cards came from library)
;;   - NO shuffle (verify library order preserved)

;; Test: confirm-tutor-selection chains to pile-choice when present
;; Setup: app-db with pending tutor selection, 3 cards selected, pile-choice in selection
;; Action: dispatch ::confirm-tutor-selection
;; Assert:
;;   - :game/pending-selection now has :selection/type :pile-choice
;;   - Cards still in library (not moved to hand yet)
;;   - Spell NOT moved to graveyard yet (still on stack)

;; Test: confirm-tutor-selection without pile-choice works normally
;; Setup: app-db with pending tutor selection, 1 card selected, NO pile-choice
;; Action: dispatch ::confirm-tutor-selection
;; Assert:
;;   - Card moved to hand
;;   - Library shuffled
;;   - :game/pending-selection cleared
;;   - Spell moved to graveyard

;; Test: select-random picks exactly hand-count cards
;; Setup: pile-choice selection with 3 candidates, hand-count 1
;; Action: dispatch ::select-random-pile-choice
;; Assert:
;;   - (:selection/selected) has exactly 1 element
;;   - Selected element is in candidates
;;   - Selection NOT auto-confirmed (can still change)

;; Test: single-card pile-choice auto-selects
;; Setup: 1 card ID, pile-choice {:hand 1 :graveyard :rest}
;; Action: build-pile-choice-selection
;; Assert:
;;   - (:selection/selected result) = #{single-card-id}
;;   - Still requires confirm (UI shows pre-selected state)
```

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] events/game.cljs - Add build-pile-choice-selection function:
      ```clojure
      (defn build-pile-choice-selection
        [card-ids pile-choice player-id spell-id remaining-effects]
        (let [hand-count (:hand pile-choice)
              candidates (set card-ids)
              ;; Auto-select if only 1 card (per epic open question)
              auto-selected (if (= 1 (count candidates))
                              candidates
                              #{})]
          {:selection/type :pile-choice
           :selection/candidates candidates
           :selection/hand-count hand-count
           :selection/selected auto-selected
           :selection/player-id player-id
           :selection/spell-id spell-id
           :selection/remaining-effects remaining-effects
           :selection/allow-random true}))
      ```
- [ ] events/game.cljs - Add execute-pile-choice function:
      ```clojure
      (defn execute-pile-choice
        [db selection]
        (let [hand-selected (:selection/selected selection)
              all-candidates (:selection/candidates selection)
              graveyard-cards (clojure.set/difference all-candidates hand-selected)]
          ;; Move hand cards from library to hand
          (let [db-after-hand (reduce #(zones/move-to-zone %1 %2 :hand)
                                      db hand-selected)
                ;; Move remaining to graveyard
                db-after-graveyard (reduce #(zones/move-to-zone %1 %2 :graveyard)
                                           db-after-hand graveyard-cards)]
            db-after-graveyard)))
      ```
- [ ] events/game.cljs - Modify ::confirm-tutor-selection:
      - Check if :selection/pile-choice present AND cards were selected
      - If pile-choice: build-pile-choice-selection, DON'T execute-tutor-selection
      - Keep spell on stack (don't move to graveyard yet)
      - Store new selection in :game/pending-selection
      - If no pile-choice: existing behavior (execute-tutor, shuffle, clear)
- [ ] events/game.cljs - Add ::confirm-pile-choice-selection event:
      ```clojure
      (rf/reg-event-db
        ::confirm-pile-choice-selection
        (fn [db _]
          (let [selection (:game/pending-selection db)
                hand-count (:selection/hand-count selection)
                selected (:selection/selected selection)
                candidates (:selection/candidates selection)
                player-id (:selection/player-id selection)
                spell-id (:selection/spell-id selection)
                remaining-effects (:selection/remaining-effects selection)
                game-db (:game/db db)]
            ;; Validate: correct number selected and all are candidates
            (if (and (= hand-count (count selected))
                     (every? #(contains? candidates %) selected))
              (let [db-after-pile (execute-pile-choice game-db selection)
                    ;; Shuffle library AFTER pile choice (tutor requirement)
                    db-after-shuffle (zones/shuffle-library db-after-pile player-id)
                    ;; Execute remaining effects
                    db-after-effects (reduce (fn [d e] (effects/execute-effect d player-id e))
                                             db-after-shuffle
                                             (or remaining-effects []))
                    ;; Move spell to graveyard (or exile for flashback)
                    spell-obj (queries/get-object db-after-effects spell-id)
                    cast-mode (:object/cast-mode spell-obj)
                    destination (or (:mode/on-resolve cast-mode) :graveyard)
                    db-final (zones/move-to-zone db-after-effects spell-id destination)]
                (-> db
                    (assoc :game/db db-final)
                    (dissoc :game/pending-selection)))
              ;; Invalid - do nothing
              db))))
      ```
- [ ] events/game.cljs - Add ::select-random-pile-choice event:
      ```clojure
      (rf/reg-event-db
        ::select-random-pile-choice
        (fn [db _]
          (let [selection (:game/pending-selection db)
                candidates (vec (:selection/candidates selection))
                hand-count (:selection/hand-count selection)
                random-selected (set (take hand-count (shuffle candidates)))]
            (assoc-in db [:game/pending-selection :selection/selected] random-selected))))
      ```

### 4. Verification
- Run: make test (all tests pass)
- Run: make validate (lint, format, tests)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] build-pile-choice-selection returns map with :selection/type :pile-choice
- [ ] :selection/candidates contains exactly the tutored card IDs
- [ ] :selection/hand-count matches pile-choice config (:hand value)
- [ ] :selection/allow-random = true for Random button
- [ ] Single-card selection auto-populates :selection/selected
- [ ] execute-pile-choice moves hand-selected to :hand zone
- [ ] execute-pile-choice moves remaining to :graveyard zone
- [ ] ::confirm-tutor-selection chains to pile-choice when :selection/pile-choice present
- [ ] ::confirm-tutor-selection preserves backwards compat (no pile-choice = normal flow)
- [ ] ::confirm-pile-choice-selection validates count and shuffles library
- [ ] ::select-random-pile-choice selects exactly hand-count cards randomly
- [ ] 6+ unit tests pass with specific assertions
- [ ] make validate passes (lint, format, tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**CRITICAL: Card location during pile choice**
- Cards stay in library during tutor selection phase
- Cards stay in library during pile choice selection phase
- Only on pile-choice confirm do cards move to hand/graveyard
- Shuffle happens AFTER pile choice confirm, not during tutor confirm

**Edge Case: Empty selection after tutor (fail-to-find)**
- If fail-to-find (0 cards selected), no pile choice phase
- ::confirm-tutor-selection should use existing flow (just shuffle)

**Edge Case: Single card selected**
- If only 1 card from tutor, pile choice triggers with auto-selected card
- User sees confirmation UI but choice is predetermined
- Still requires explicit confirm click

**Edge Case: hand-count > selected cards**
- If pile-choice {:hand 2} but only 1 card selected
- All cards go to hand, none to graveyard
- Should work but log warning (unexpected state)

**Edge Case: Spell lifecycle**
- Spell stays on stack until pile-choice confirms
- Only after confirm: spell moves to graveyard/exile
- This is correct MTG rules (spell resolves after all effects)

**Integration: Shuffle timing**
- Tutor confirm does NOT shuffle when pile-choice present
- Pile-choice confirm shuffles library
- This maintains single shuffle per Intuition resolution

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO shuffling during pile-choice (shuffle only on final confirm)
- ❌ NO skipping pile-choice UI when 1 card (show UI with auto-selected)
- ❌ NO auto-confirming random selection (let user review before confirm)
- ❌ NO moving cards to hand during tutor confirm when pile-choice present
- ❌ NO moving spell to graveyard until pile-choice complete
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 3: Targeting System Foundation
description:
## Goal
Create the targeting system infrastructure for cast-time target declaration and validation.

## Context
Grant System (Phase 1-2) is complete. Now we need targeting for:
- Recoup targeting sorceries in graveyard
- Deep Analysis targeting players
- Cephalid Coliseum ability targeting players

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Update schema for target storage
- Change :object/targets from ref collection to map
- Map format: target-ref keyword → target-id (UUID or player-id)

### Step 2: Create engine/targeting.cljs with core functions
- get-targeting-requirements: Extract targeting from card/ability
- find-valid-targets: Find objects/players matching criteria
- has-valid-targets?: Check if any valid target exists
- target-still-legal?: Check if chosen target is still valid
- all-targets-legal?: Check all targets on resolution

### Step 3: Write tests for each function (TDD)
- Test target finding in different zones
- Test player targeting
- Test legality checking when target moves zones

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Schema updated for map-based target storage
- [ ] All 5 targeting functions implemented
- [ ] Tests written and passing for each function
- [ ] Functions handle both object and player targets

## Anti-Patterns
- Do NOT modify casting flow yet (Phase 4 will do that)
- Do NOT implement fizzle logic yet (Phase 4 will do that)
design:
## Goal
Create the targeting system infrastructure for cast-time target declaration and validation.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Context
Grant System (Phase 1-2) is complete. Now we need targeting for:
- Recoup targeting sorceries in graveyard
- Deep Analysis targeting players
- Cephalid Coliseum ability targeting players

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Update schema for target storage (TDD)
**File:** src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs

Change :object/targets from:
\`\`\`clojure
:object/targets {:db/cardinality :db.cardinality/many
                 :db/valueType :db.type/ref}
\`\`\`

To:
\`\`\`clojure
:object/targets {}  ; Map of target-ref keyword → target-id
\`\`\`

**Example stored value:**
\`\`\`clojure
{:graveyard-sorcery #uuid "abc-123"
 :player :player-1}
\`\`\`

### Step 2: Create engine/targeting.cljs with functions (TDD)
**File:** src/main/fizzle/engine/targeting.cljs
**Test file:** src/test/fizzle/engine/targeting_test.cljs

**Function 1: get-targeting-requirements**
\`\`\`clojure
(defn get-targeting-requirements
  "Extract targeting requirements from card or ability.
   Returns vector of target requirement maps, or empty vector if no targeting."
  [card-or-ability]
  ;; Check :card/targeting or :ability/targeting
  ;; Return vector of {:target/id :target/type :target/zone ...} maps
  )
\`\`\`

**Function 2: find-valid-targets**
\`\`\`clojure
(defn find-valid-targets
  "Find all objects/players that are valid targets for a requirement.
   Returns vector of valid target IDs (object UUIDs or player keywords)."
  [db player-id target-requirement]
  ;; For :target/type :object - query zone for matching objects
  ;; For :target/type :player - return valid player options
  )
\`\`\`

**Function 3: has-valid-targets?**
\`\`\`clojure
(defn has-valid-targets?
  "Check if at least one valid target exists for EACH required target.
   Returns true only if ALL required targets have valid options."
  [db player-id card-or-ability]
  ;; Get requirements, check each has at least one valid target
  )
\`\`\`

**Function 4: target-still-legal?**
\`\`\`clojure
(defn target-still-legal?
  "Check if a chosen target is still legal (zone, attributes match).
   Used on resolution to check if target moved/changed."
  [db target-id target-requirement]
  ;; Check target still in correct zone
  ;; Check target still matches criteria
  )
\`\`\`

**Function 5: all-targets-legal?**
\`\`\`clojure
(defn all-targets-legal?
  "Check if ALL chosen targets on an object are still legal.
   Used on resolution to determine if spell fizzles."
  [db object-id]
  ;; Get object's stored targets and targeting requirements
  ;; Check each target against its requirement
  )
\`\`\`

### Step 3: Target requirement structure
Define the target requirement map format:
\`\`\`clojure
{:target/id       :graveyard-sorcery  ; Identifier for this target slot
 :target/type     :object             ; :object or :player
 :target/zone     :graveyard          ; For objects: required zone
 :target/controller :self             ; :self, :opponent, :any
 :target/criteria {:card/types #{:sorcery}}  ; Card attributes to match
 :target/required true}               ; Must have valid target to cast
\`\`\`

For player targeting:
\`\`\`clojure
{:target/id       :player
 :target/type     :player
 :target/options  [:self :opponent :any-player]
 :target/required true}
\`\`\`

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Schema :object/targets changed to map format (not ref collection)
- [ ] get-targeting-requirements extracts targeting from cards and abilities
- [ ] find-valid-targets returns correct objects for zone/criteria queries
- [ ] find-valid-targets returns correct players for player targeting
- [ ] has-valid-targets? returns false when no valid targets exist
- [ ] target-still-legal? detects when target moved to wrong zone
- [ ] target-still-legal? detects when target no longer matches criteria
- [ ] all-targets-legal? correctly checks all targets on object
- [ ] 15+ unit tests pass covering all functions and edge cases
- [ ] make test passes with no failures

## Specific Test Cases (must implement)
1. test_get_targeting_requirements_from_card - extract :card/targeting
2. test_get_targeting_requirements_from_ability - extract :ability/targeting
3. test_get_targeting_requirements_no_targeting - returns empty vector
4. test_find_valid_targets_sorcery_in_graveyard - finds matching sorcery
5. test_find_valid_targets_empty_graveyard - returns empty vector
6. test_find_valid_targets_no_matching_type - sorcery criteria, only instants in gy
7. test_find_valid_targets_player_any - returns both players
8. test_find_valid_targets_player_self - returns only caster
9. test_has_valid_targets_true - at least one valid target exists
10. test_has_valid_targets_false_empty_zone - no cards in zone
11. test_has_valid_targets_false_no_match - cards exist but don't match
12. test_target_still_legal_in_zone - target still in graveyard
13. test_target_still_legal_moved_zone - target moved to exile (illegal)
14. test_target_still_legal_type_changed - (edge: type-changing effects, defer)
15. test_all_targets_legal_all_valid - all targets still legal
16. test_all_targets_legal_one_invalid - one target moved, returns false

## Key Considerations

**Edge Case: Empty Zone**
- find-valid-targets with empty graveyard MUST return empty vector
- has-valid-targets? MUST return false if zone is empty
- Test: Call with player who has no cards in graveyard

**Edge Case: No Matching Cards**
- Graveyard has cards but none are sorceries
- find-valid-targets MUST return empty (not all cards in zone)
- Test: Graveyard with only instants, targeting sorcery

**Edge Case: Target Moves Zone Before Resolution**
- Target was in graveyard when chosen
- Opponent exiles it with Tormod's Crypt
- target-still-legal? MUST return false
- Test: Move target to exile, verify detection

**Edge Case: Multiple Targets**
- Some spells target multiple things
- all-targets-legal? must check ALL targets
- If ANY target is illegal, return false
- Test: Two targets, move one to exile

**Edge Case: Player Targeting**
- Player targets don't move zones
- Player targeting is always legal on resolution (can't fizzle)
- Test: Verify player targets always return legal

**Edge Case: Object Not Found**
- Target ID doesn't exist in DB (deleted somehow)
- target-still-legal? MUST return false (not throw)
- Test: Query with non-existent UUID

**Reference Implementation**
- Study existing zone queries in src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs
- Study existing object lookups in q/get-object

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ Do NOT modify casting flow yet (Phase 4 will do that)
- ❌ Do NOT implement fizzle logic yet (Phase 4 will do that)
- ❌ Do NOT throw exceptions for missing targets - return false/empty
- ❌ Do NOT hardcode player IDs - use :self/:opponent/:any
- ❌ Do NOT use unwrap/expect patterns - handle nil gracefully

title: Restore Closure advanced optimizations via q-safe wrapper
description: Datascript d/q with :in keyword params breaks under Closure advanced compilation (keyword identity mismatch across compilation units). Add q-safe wrapper that re-interns keywords, replace all d/q calls, add lint rule to prevent regression, restore :advanced optimizations (4.1MB -> ~1.1MB).
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- All d/q calls go through q-safe wrapper that re-interns keyword args
- No direct d/q usage outside db/queries.cljs
- Lint rule prevents regression (flags direct d/q usage)
- Release build uses :advanced optimizations
- App works correctly in release build (local verification)
- All existing tests pass
- Bundle size returns to ~1.1MB from current 4.1MB

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] q-safe wrapper exists in db/queries.cljs
- [ ] Zero direct d/q calls outside db/queries.cljs (lint check passes)
- [ ] d/pull with lookup refs also handled (reintern keyword in lookup ref)
- [ ] shadow-cljs.edn uses :advanced optimizations for release
- [ ] Release build loads and Start Game works in browser
- [ ] All 3051+ tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] Bundle size ~1.1MB (not 4.1MB)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO direct d/q calls outside db/queries.cljs (regression: would break on next keyword param addition)
- ❌ NO selective q-safe replacement (only broken calls) — replace ALL d/q calls, no classification needed
- ❌ NO :simple optimizations fallback (the whole point is restoring :advanced)
- ❌ NO transaction-side normalization (over-engineering: read-side wrapper is sufficient)

## Approach
1. Add q-safe to db/queries.cljs — wraps d/q, re-interns keyword args via (keyword (namespace k) (name k))
2. Add pull-safe for d/pull calls with lookup refs — re-interns keyword in lookup ref vector
3. Replace every d/q call across all 10+ files with q-safe (require db/queries as q or queries)
4. Replace d/pull calls with keyword lookup refs with pull-safe
5. Add lint rule to Makefile: grep for (d/q outside db/queries.cljs
6. Switch shadow-cljs.edn release from :simple back to :advanced
7. Build release, serve locally, verify Start Game works

## Architecture
Files changed:
- db/queries.cljs — add q-safe, pull-safe helpers
- All files with d/q calls (~10 files) — switch to q-safe
- Makefile — add lint-datascript target, wire into validate
- shadow-cljs.edn — remove :release {:compiler-options {:optimizations :simple}}

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Datascript d/q :in parameter binding fails under Closure advanced compilation. Keywords passed as runtime params don't match keywords stored in indexes due to identity mismatch across compilation units. Current workaround (:simple optimizations) bloats bundle from 1.1MB to 4.1MB.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- 30+ d/q calls total, ~18 with keyword :in params (all broken under :advanced)
- 1+ d/pull with keyword lookup refs (also broken)
- All calls spread across ~10 files

**Debugging evidence:**
- d/q with inline keyword literal works (same compilation unit)
- d/q with :in keyword param fails (cross-unit identity mismatch)
- d/entid with lookup ref fails (same issue)
- d/datoms raw value access also returns hashes not keywords

### Approaches Considered
#### 1. q-safe wrapper — replace ALL d/q calls + lint rule ✓
**Chosen because:** One chokepoint, zero classification overhead, lint prevents regression. Re-interning keywords forces them through same constructor in same compilation unit.

#### 2. Safe Datascript module (re-export all of d/) ❌
**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Over-scoped. Only d/q and d/pull with lookup refs are affected. d/transact!, d/create-conn, d/datoms, etc. don't have the keyword comparison issue.
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Other Datascript functions also break under :advanced.

#### 3. :simple optimizations (current) ❌
**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** 4x bundle size (4.1MB vs 1.1MB). Blunt instrument that hides the real problem.
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** q-safe wrapper somehow insufficient.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** q-safe wrapper, all d/q replacement, d/pull lookup ref handling, lint rule, :advanced restore
**Out of scope:** Transaction-side normalization, other Datascript function wrappers, externs investigation
close_reason: Closed — q-safe approach insufficient. Datascript keyword corruption under Closure :advanced is in stored datoms, not just query params. Keeping :simple optimizations (4.1MB, works correctly).

title: Add Phyrexian Devourer, Tinker, and Goblin Welder with data-driven infrastructure
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Tinker: Sorcery {2}{U}. Additional cost: sacrifice an artifact. Search library for artifact, put onto battlefield, shuffle.
- Phyrexian Devourer: Artifact Creature {6} 1/1. Activated ability (exile top card of library as cost): put X +1/+1 counters where X = exiled card's mana value. State trigger: when power >= 7, sacrifice (goes on stack, can respond).
- Goblin Welder: Creature {R} 1/1 Goblin Artificer. {T}: Choose target artifact on battlefield and target artifact in graveyard (same controller). Simultaneously sacrifice battlefield artifact and return graveyard artifact to battlefield. Both targets must be legal at resolution.
- All three cards legal in Premodern format with oracle-accurate implementations
- New generic cost type: sacrifice-permanent with criteria matching (data-driven, not card-specific)
- New generic cost type: exile-library-top (exile top N cards of library as cost, store on stack item)
- New dynamic value type: cost-exiled-card-mana-value (reads exiled card's mana value from stack item)
- New generic state-check trigger type (condition-based, goes on stack, respondable)
- Extended tutor effect: support :battlefield as target-zone (put onto battlefield)
- Extended targeting: dual-zone targeting with same-controller constraint
- Every card has dedicated test file with mandatory categories (A-D minimum + applicable conditionals)
- All new infrastructure has unit tests

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Tinker: can cast with artifact sacrifice, tutors artifact to battlefield, shuffle after
- [ ] Tinker: cannot cast without artifacts to sacrifice or without {2}{U}
- [ ] Phyrexian Devourer: activated ability exiles top card, adds correct counters
- [ ] Phyrexian Devourer: state trigger fires when power >= 7, goes on stack
- [ ] Phyrexian Devourer: player can activate ability in response to sacrifice trigger (combo line)
- [ ] Goblin Welder: targets artifact on battlefield + artifact in graveyard (same controller)
- [ ] Goblin Welder: simultaneously sacrifices and returns at resolution
- [ ] Goblin Welder: fails if either target illegal at resolution
- [ ] sacrifice-permanent cost is generic (works with any criteria, not Tinker-specific)
- [ ] exile-library-top cost is generic (works with any count)
- [ ] state-check trigger is generic (works with any condition, not Devourer-specific)
- [ ] All tests passing (make validate)
- [ ] Each card has min required tests per CLAUDE.md

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO card-specific cost handlers (sacrifice-permanent must be generic with criteria — future cards like Goblin Bombardment, Arcbound Ravager need it)
- ❌ NO hardcoded power threshold in trigger (state-check trigger must be condition-driven data, not 'if devourer then 7')
- ❌ NO bypassing the stack for Devourer's ability or trigger (both must go on stack for proper combo interaction)
- ❌ NO separate engine paths for put-onto-battlefield (extend existing tutor/return-from-graveyard, don't duplicate zone-move logic)
- ❌ NO reimplementing production handlers in tests (use th/cast-and-resolve, th/cast-with-target, th/resolve-top, th/confirm-selection)
- ❌ NO tautological assertions in tests (exact values, not some?/string?)
- ❌ NO card-name case statements in engine code (data over code — engine interprets effect data)

## Approach
Data-driven infrastructure: build generic, reusable mechanisms for each new mechanic, then wire cards as pure data definitions. Each card's definition is EDN data the engine interprets — no card-specific logic in the engine.

Implementation order: Tinker first (introduces sacrifice-permanent cost + tutor-to-battlefield — most broadly reusable), then Phyrexian Devourer (exile-library-top cost + dynamic counters + state trigger — self-contained), then Goblin Welder (dual-zone targeting + swap — most complex, builds on patterns from 1 and 2).

## Architecture
New infrastructure:
1. engine/costs.cljs — add sacrifice-permanent cost type with criteria matching + exile-library-top cost type
2. events/selection/ — add sacrifice-permanent-cost selection builder/executor (follows zone-pick pattern)
3. engine/effects.cljs — extend tutor to support :battlefield target-zone, extend return-from-graveyard similarly
4. engine/effects.cljs — add :cost-exiled-card-mana-value to resolve-dynamic-value multimethod
5. engine/trigger_db.cljs or new module — add state-check trigger type with condition evaluation
6. engine/targeting or events/selection — extend for dual-zone targeting with same-controller constraint
7. cards/blue/tinker.cljs, cards/artifacts/phyrexian_devourer.cljs, cards/red/goblin_welder.cljs — pure data card definitions

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Adding Phyrexian Devourer, Tinker, and Goblin Welder — classic Premodern artifact combo cards. Each requires new game mechanics not yet in the engine.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- Creature infrastructure complete: P/T computation, combat, summoning sickness
- Artifact cards exist (Lotus Petal pattern)
- Activated abilities system complete (mana, activated, triggered types)
- +1/+1 counter system exists (add-counters effect, counter-mod in P/T calc)
- Tutor effect exists but only to hand (not battlefield)
- Sacrifice-self cost exists but not sacrifice-a-chosen-permanent
- Selection cost pattern established (discard-specific-cost, exile-cards-cost, return-land-cost)
- resolve-dynamic-value multimethod exists (used by Accumulated Knowledge)
- Targeting supports vector of requirements but no cross-target constraints

**External (Scryfall):**
- Phyrexian Devourer: Artifact Creature — Phyrexian Construct, 1/1, exile-top-of-library is the COST (before colon), counters are the EFFECT
- Tinker: sacrifice artifact is additional cost (paid during casting), search+put is the effect
- Goblin Welder: both targets must be legal at resolution for anything to happen

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Data-driven infrastructure ✓
Build generic cost types, effect extensions, trigger system, and targeting extensions. Cards are pure data.

**Chosen because:** User preference. Maximizes reuse — sacrifice-permanent, exile-library-top, state-check triggers, and dual-zone targeting are all patterns that many future cards need. Matches codebase philosophy of data over code.

#### 2. Hybrid (reusable where common, specific where unique) ❌
**Why explored:** Lower upfront cost for unique mechanics
**Rejected because:** User preferred full data-driven approach for maximum reuse.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Infrastructure proves impossible to generalize for a specific mechanic.

#### 3. Card-specific handlers ❌
**Why explored:** Fastest implementation
**Rejected because:** Creates technical debt; sacrifice-permanent alone is used by dozens of MTG cards.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** N/A — fundamentally wrong approach for this codebase.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** Three card implementations + all supporting generic infrastructure + tests
**Out of scope:** Decklists using these cards, creature death state-based actions (0 toughness), keyword ability effects (haste etc.), combat improvements

### Open Questions
- Exact location for state-check trigger evaluation (after effect resolution? after counter changes? both?)
- Whether exile-library-top cost stores full card entity or just mana-value on stack item
- Whether Welder's 'simultaneously' needs special handling or can be sequential (sacrifice then return)
close_reason: All three cards implemented with data-driven infrastructure. Tinker, Phyrexian Devourer, Goblin Welder. Reviewer approved. Bug fix for multi-target chaining applied. 2687 tests passing.

title: Implement Phyrexian Devourer with exile-library-top cost and state-check trigger infrastructure
description:
Second card in fizzle-wxm9 epic. Introduces exile-library-top cost type, cost-exiled-card-mana-value dynamic value, and generic state-check trigger infrastructure.

## Card
Phyrexian Devourer: Artifact Creature {6} 1/1 Phyrexian Construct
- Activated ability: [Exile top card of library]: Put X +1/+1 counters on Phyrexian Devourer, where X is the exiled card's mana value.
- State trigger: When Phyrexian Devourer's power is 7 or greater, sacrifice it.

## Infrastructure
1. engine/costs.cljs — exile-library-top cost type (generic, works with any count)
2. engine/effects.cljs — cost-exiled-card-mana-value dynamic value (reads from stack item)
3. engine/trigger_db.cljs or state-based — generic state-check trigger type (condition-driven data)

## Anti-patterns
- State-check trigger MUST be generic with condition data, not card-specific
- Both ability and trigger MUST go on the stack
- exile-library-top cost must store exiled card info on stack item
close_reason: Closed

title: Bug: Goblin Welder ability stops after first target selection
