close_reason: Fixed: execute-confirmed-selection :ability-targeting was dropping the pending-selection from confirm-ability-target. Added build-chain-selection :ability-targeting to properly chain multi-target ability selections through the selection system.

title: Implement inline stack item preview in Yield buttons
design:
## Goal
Modify Yield and Yield All button labels in views/controls.cljs to show top stack item name and stack count.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours (single-file change + tests)

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - views/controls.cljs:40-47 — current Yield/Yield All buttons, already subscribes to ::stack
   - views/stack.cljs:9-17 — label priority logic to replicate
   - subs/game.cljs:42-66 — ::stack sub returns mixed types: spell objects (have :object/card :card/name) AND enriched stack-items (have :stack-item/card-name)

2. Extract pure helper function (testable independently)
   Create `top-stack-item-name` in controls.cljs:
   ```clojure
   (defn- top-stack-item-name
     [stack]
     (when-let [item (first stack)]
       (let [card-name (or (get-in item [:object/card :card/name])
                           (:stack-item/card-name item))
             desc (:stack-item/description item)]
         (cond
           card-name card-name
           desc desc
           :else (when-let [t (:stack-item/type item)]
                   (name t))))))
   ```
   Note: Unlike stack-item-view, we only want the name (not 'Name: desc'), since the button already has the 'Yield:' prefix.

3. Write tests first (TDD) — new file src/test/fizzle/views/controls_test.cljs
   Test the pure helper function directly. No view rendering needed.
   ```clojure
   ;; Spell on stack (object with :object/card)
   (is (= "Dark Ritual" (top-stack-item-name [{:object/card {:card/name "Dark Ritual"} :object/position 1}])))

   ;; Enriched non-spell (stack-item with :stack-item/card-name)
   (is (= "Lion's Eye Diamond" (top-stack-item-name [{:stack-item/card-name "Lion's Eye Diamond" :stack-item/position 1}])))

   ;; Stack item with description only (no card-name)
   (is (= "Draw 3 cards" (top-stack-item-name [{:stack-item/description "Draw 3 cards" :stack-item/position 1}])))

   ;; Stack item with only type
   (is (= "etb" (top-stack-item-name [{:stack-item/type :etb :stack-item/position 1}])))

   ;; Empty stack
   (is (nil? (top-stack-item-name [])))

   ;; Nil stack
   (is (nil? (top-stack-item-name nil)))

   ;; Card-name takes priority over description
   (is (= "Brain Freeze" (top-stack-item-name [{:stack-item/card-name "Brain Freeze" :stack-item/description "Mill 3" :stack-item/position 1}])))
   ```

4. Implementation checklist
   - [ ] Add `top-stack-item-name` helper fn in controls.cljs (handles both spell objects and enriched stack-items)
   - [ ] Update Yield button label: `(if-let [n (top-stack-item-name stack)] (str "Yield: " n) "Yield")`
   - [ ] Update Yield All button label: `(if (> (count stack) 1) (str "Yield All (" (count stack) ")") "Yield All")`
   - [ ] Create controls_test.cljs with 7+ test cases for pure helper
   - [ ] Run make validate (lint + format + tests)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `(top-stack-item-name [{:object/card {:card/name "Dark Ritual"}}])` returns "Dark Ritual" (spell path)
- [ ] `(top-stack-item-name [{:stack-item/card-name "LED"}])` returns "LED" (enriched stack-item path)
- [ ] `(top-stack-item-name [])` returns nil → button shows plain "Yield"
- [ ] Yield All shows "Yield All (5)" when stack has 5 items
- [ ] Yield All shows "Yield All" (no count) when stack has <=1 items
- [ ] Fallback chain: card-name > description > type name > nil
- [ ] 7+ unit tests pass in controls_test.cljs
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Two item types in ::stack subscription:**
The ::stack subscription (subs/game.cljs:42-66) returns a mixed vector: spell objects have `:object/card {:card/name ...}`, while non-spell stack-items have `:stack-item/card-name`. The helper MUST check both paths with `(or (get-in item [:object/card :card/name]) (:stack-item/card-name item))`. Reference: views/stack.cljs:9-10 does exactly this.

**Edge case — item with no displayable name:**
If a stack item somehow has nil for card-name, description, AND type, `top-stack-item-name` returns nil and the button shows plain "Yield". This is safe — no crash, no blank label.

**Edge case — Yield All with exactly 1 item:**
`(> (count stack) 1)` is false, so button shows "Yield All" without count and is disabled. Matches existing behavior at controls.cljs:44-46.

**Testing approach:**
No view/component test infrastructure exists. Extract label logic as a pure function and test that directly. The helper must be `defn-` (private) but tests can access via `#'fizzle.views.controls/top-stack-item-name`.

## Anti-Patterns (from epic)
- ❌ NO new subscriptions (reuse existing ::stack already in controls view)
- ❌ NO new UI elements/components (modify button labels only)
- ❌ NO card name truncation
- ❌ NO changes to stack-item-view or stack panel
- ❌ NO changes to the ::stack subscription
close_reason: Closed

title: Create sort-cards utility and apply to hand + selection subscriptions
design:
## Goal
Create the foundational sort-cards function and apply it to the simplest consumers first: hand, selection dialogs, opening hand, and scry.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

1. Create src/main/fizzle/engine/sorting.cljs with sort-cards function
   - Sort by CMC ascending (default 0 for nil/missing), then name alphabetical (default empty string for nil/missing)
   - Function signature: (sort-cards objects) -> sorted vector of objects
   - Uses (sort-by (juxt cmc-key name-key) objects) where:
     - cmc-key: (fn [obj] (get-in obj [:object/card :card/cmc] 0))
     - name-key: (fn [obj] (get-in obj [:object/card :card/name] ""))
   - Reference: views/opening_hand.cljs:37 for existing name-only sort pattern

2. Write tests first (TDD) in src/test/fizzle/engine/sorting_test.cljs
   - test-sort-by-cmc-ascending: Dark Ritual (CMC 1), Cabal Ritual (CMC 2), Lion's Eye Diamond (CMC 0) -> LED, DR, CR. Catches: wrong sort direction.
   - test-same-cmc-sorts-by-name: Cabal Ritual (CMC 2) vs Burning Wish (CMC 2) -> Burning Wish first. Catches: missing secondary sort key.
   - test-lands-sort-with-zero-cmc: Swamp (land, CMC 0) vs Lion's Eye Diamond (CMC 0) -> LED before Swamp. Catches: lands treated differently from spells.
   - test-empty-input-returns-empty: (sort-cards []) -> []. Catches: nil/empty NPE.
   - test-nil-cmc-defaults-to-zero: Card with no :card/cmc sorts as CMC 0. Catches: NPE on nil CMC.
   - test-sort-stability-duplicates: 3x Dark Ritual all appear (no cards lost in sort). Catches: duplicate-dropping bug.
   - test-mixed-deck-realistic: Full hand of Iggy Pop cards sorts correctly (LED 0, DR 1, CR 2, IGG 4, etc). Catches: integration-level regression.

3. Apply sort-cards in subs/game.cljs ::hand subscription (line 17-21)
   - Change: (when game-db (queries/get-hand game-db :player-1))
   - To: (when game-db (sorting/sort-cards (queries/get-hand game-db :player-1)))
   - Add require: [fizzle.engine.sorting :as sorting]

4. Apply sort-cards in subs/game.cljs ::selection-cards subscription (line 190-249)
   - Wrap every return value in sorting/sort-cards:
     - :pile-choice branch (line 205): wrap filterv result
     - :cast-time-targeting branch (line 213): wrap filterv result
     - :ability-targeting branch (line 221): wrap filterv result
     - :tutor branch (line 227): wrap filterv result
     - :discard branch (line 231): wrap get-hand result
     - :exile-cards-cost branch (line 238): wrap filterv result
     - :peek-and-select branch (line 245): wrap filterv result
     - :else default branch (line 249): wrap get-objects-in-zone result

5. Apply sort-cards in subs/game.cljs ::scry-cards subscription (line 254-267)
   - Sort the :cards vector: {:cards (sorting/sort-cards (mapv get-obj card-ids)) ...}
   - Do NOT sort :top-pile or :bottom-pile (user-assigned order matters)

6. Update views/opening_hand.cljs
   - Lines 37, 66: replace (sort-by #(get-in % [:object/card :card/name]) hand) with (sorting/sort-cards hand)
   - Add require: [fizzle.engine.sorting :as sorting]

7. Verify
   - make validate passes (lint + format + 1148+ tests)
   - Visually confirm hand sorted in browser dev mode

## Success Criteria
- [ ] src/main/fizzle/engine/sorting.cljs exists with sort-cards function
- [ ] 7 unit tests in engine/sorting_test.cljs all pass (names listed above)
- [ ] ::hand subscription wraps result in sort-cards (verified by reading subs/game.cljs)
- [ ] All 8 branches of ::selection-cards subscription return sorted results
- [ ] ::scry-cards :cards vector sorted (:top-pile/:bottom-pile unchanged)
- [ ] Opening hand views use sort-cards (views/opening_hand.cljs no longer has inline sort-by)
- [ ] make validate passes with zero failures

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: nil CMC field**
- Some card definitions may omit :card/cmc (e.g., lands historically had no CMC in some systems)
- MUST default to 0 via (get-in obj [:object/card :card/cmc] 0)
- Test: test-nil-cmc-defaults-to-zero covers this

**Edge Case: Duplicate cards**
- Decks have 4x copies of cards (4x Dark Ritual, 4x Lion's Eye Diamond)
- Sort must not drop duplicates - all cards must appear in output
- Test: test-sort-stability-duplicates covers this

**Edge Case: Empty/nil input**
- sort-by on nil returns nil, on empty returns empty - both safe in Clojure
- Still worth explicit test for documentation clarity

**Performance: Subscription memoization**
- re-frame subscriptions are memoized - sort only runs when underlying data changes
- Hand is max ~7 cards, selection lists are small - no performance concern
- No need for caching beyond re-frame's built-in memoization

## Anti-Patterns (task-level)
- ❌ NO sorting in view layer (epic rule: sort in subscriptions only)
- ❌ NO sorting :top-pile or :bottom-pile in scry (user-assigned order)
- ❌ NO custom sort logic per subscription (use single sort-cards everywhere)
- ❌ NO (sort ...) or (sort-by ...) calls — use sort-cards wrapper for consistency
close_reason: Sort utility created, applied to hand/selection/scry/opening-hand subscriptions. 7 unit tests, make validate passes (1196 tests, 0 failures).

title: Prepare release build: fix asset paths + add make release
description: Fix absolute asset paths in index.html to relative and add make release Makefile target so the build works for GitHub Pages project site deployment.
design:
## Goal
Make the release build work for GitHub Pages project site deployment. Two changes: relative asset paths in index.html, and a make release target.

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours

## Implementation

1. Fix asset paths in resources/public/index.html
   - Line 6: href="/css/app.css" → href="css/app.css"
   - Line 10: src="/js/main.js" → src="js/main.js"
   - Relative paths work for both dev server (root) and project site (subdirectory)

2. Add release target to Makefile (after build-css target, before clean target)
   ```makefile
   release: clean build-css
   	npx shadow-cljs release app
   ```
   - Also add 'release' to the .PHONY line (line 1)

3. Verify release build: make release
4. Verify dev still works: make dev, load localhost:8080

## Success Criteria
- [ ] index.html uses relative paths — verify: grep -c '^/' resources/public/index.html returns 0 (no absolute paths to css/js)
- [ ] make release exits 0 and produces: resources/public/index.html, resources/public/js/main.js, resources/public/css/app.css
- [ ] make dev still works — shadow-cljs watch starts, app loads at localhost:8080 (manual check)
- [ ] All tests pass: make test exits 0
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass: make validate exits 0

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO absolute paths in index.html (breaks on project site subdirectory)
- ❌ NO protocol-relative paths (//js/main.js) — use plain relative (js/main.js)
- ❌ NO base tag in index.html (fragile, breaks differently per deployment context)
- ❌ NO changes to shadow-cljs.edn asset-path (irrelevant for single-module release build, affects dev hot-reload)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Dev server hot-reload with relative paths**
- shadow-cljs dev server serves resources/public/ at localhost:8080 root
- Relative path js/main.js from page at localhost:8080/ resolves to localhost:8080/js/main.js — same as absolute /js/main.js
- shadow-cljs.edn asset-path /js is used internally by shadow-cljs runtime for hot-reload module loading, NOT by the script tag in index.html — do NOT change it
- Verification: start make dev, confirm hot-reload still works after saving a .cljs file

**Edge Case: make clean followed by make dev**
- make clean removes resources/public/js/ and resources/public/css/
- make dev rebuilds both (shadow-cljs watch + postcss watch)
- Relative paths in index.html are unaffected by clean since index.html is source-controlled, not generated

**Edge Case: Release build output structure**
- shadow-cljs release with :advanced optimizations produces single main.js (no cljs-runtime/ directory)
- Total deployable: index.html (~200B) + main.js (~1.1MB) + app.css (~35KB)
- All in resources/public/ — this is the upload-pages-artifact path for the next task
close_reason: Complete — commit f5bc6c6. Relative paths in index.html, make release target added. 3051 tests pass.

title: ADR-017: Shared game state invariants + scenario test helper
description: Extract shared invariants from the three game state construction sites (production init, test helpers, snapshot restoration) into a shared module. Add a scenario test helper for tests that run the game loop. Prerequisite for the game director epic (fizzle-9pnl).
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- R1: A shared module defines player entity construction — required attributes, player-id constants, turn-based trigger registration. All three consumers (init.cljs, test_helpers.cljs, restorer.cljs) use it.
- R2: Player-id constants (:player-1, :player-2) defined in ONE place. No consumer hardcodes player-ids independently.
- R3: create-game-scenario test helper produces valid game state that survives the game director loop (libraries, stops, turn-based triggers for both players).
- R4: create-test-db and add-opponent continue to work for narrow/surgical tests. No change to existing narrow test patterns.
- R5: No behavior changes to production game initialization or snapshot restoration.
- R6: The class of bugs identified in ADR-017 (3-week silent regression from player-id divergence) becomes structurally impossible.

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] Shared invariant module exists (e.g., db/game_state.cljs or new module)
- [ ] events/init.cljs uses shared module for player entity construction
- [ ] test_helpers.cljs uses shared module for player entity construction
- [ ] sharing/restorer.cljs uses shared module for player entity construction
- [ ] Player-id constants come from ONE source (no independent :opponent or :player-2 literals)
- [ ] create-game-scenario helper exists and produces state that survives 3+ turn cycles via director
- [ ] create-test-db and add-opponent are unchanged or minimally adapted (narrow tests still work)
- [ ] All existing tests pass (make validate)
- [ ] No production behavior changes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO replacing create-test-db with create-game-scenario (narrow tests need minimal state — different purpose)
- ❌ NO requiring library cards for narrow tests (only scenario tests need them)
- ❌ NO changing production init behavior (shared module extracts, doesn't modify)
- ❌ NO hardcoding player-ids in any consumer (must come from shared constants)
- ❌ NO adding create-game-scenario as a dependency for narrow tests (it's opt-in for loop/scenario tests only)

## Approach

1. Extract shared invariants from events/init.cljs into a module (possibly extending db/game_state.cljs which already has player-id constants). Invariants: player entity construction, game entity construction, turn-based trigger registration.

2. Refactor the three consumers to call the shared module:
   - events/init.cljs: calls shared module, adds policy (deck construction, opening hand, storage)
   - test_helpers.cljs: calls shared module, adds policy (configurable mana, life, stops)
   - sharing/restorer.cljs: calls shared module, adds policy (snapshot-specific restoration)

3. Create create-game-scenario test helper built on the shared module with scenario defaults: 10+ library cards per player, default stops #{:main1 :main2}, both players with turn-based triggers. Used by tests that run the game director loop.

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Game state construction diverges silently between 3 consumers. A player-id mismatch caused a 3-week undetected regression (2939 tests passed while opponent draw was broken). The game director makes this worse — it actually runs the full game loop, so invalid test state (no library) causes instant game-over.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Shared invariant module + scenario helper ✓
Chosen. Addresses root cause (divergent construction) and immediate need (director tests).

#### 2. Just fix add-opponent to add library cards ❌
Patches symptom. Doesn't prevent future divergence. Broke 43 tests when tried (library cards changed zone counts that narrow tests asserted on).

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** Shared invariant extraction, scenario helper, consumer refactoring
**Out of scope:** Changing production init behavior, rewriting existing narrow tests, game director implementation (separate epic)
close_reason: Complete — shared invariants (d42b200) + scenario helper (becd4b3).

title: Audit+fix blue/ card tests batch 1 (10 files: accumulated_knowledge through daze)
description: Third batch of card test audit. Blue/ batch 1: accumulated_knowledge, annul, blue_elemental_blast, brain_freeze, careful_study, chain_of_vapor, chill, cloud_of_faeries, counterspell, cunning_wish.
design:
## Goal
Audit 10 blue card test files with analyze-tests, fix all issues found.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Files to Audit
1. src/test/fizzle/cards/blue/accumulated_knowledge_test.cljs
2. src/test/fizzle/cards/blue/annul_test.cljs
3. src/test/fizzle/cards/blue/blue_elemental_blast_test.cljs
4. src/test/fizzle/cards/blue/brain_freeze_test.cljs
5. src/test/fizzle/cards/blue/careful_study_test.cljs
6. src/test/fizzle/cards/blue/chain_of_vapor_test.cljs
7. src/test/fizzle/cards/blue/chill_test.cljs
8. src/test/fizzle/cards/blue/cloud_of_faeries_test.cljs
9. src/test/fizzle/cards/blue/counterspell_test.cljs
10. src/test/fizzle/cards/blue/cunning_wish_test.cljs

## Implementation
Same pattern as black/ and artifacts/ batches:
1. Run analyze-tests on all 10 files
2. Check CLAUDE.md mandatory categories A-D per file
3. Fix: legacy API migration, tautological/weak assertions, when-let anti-pattern, missing categories
4. EXCEPTION: Interactive spells using rules/cast-spell + resolution/resolve-one-item are correct
5. 0-cost spells (like Daze with alternate cost) need explicit Cat B
6. Run make validate

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 10 files reviewed by analyze-tests
- [ ] Zero rules/resolve-spell calls
- [ ] Every test has categories A, B, C, D
- [ ] No tautological assertions, no vacuous when-let
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] Test count >= 3321

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO removing existing passing tests
- NO using rules/resolve-spell in new test code
- NO tautological assertions (some?, string?, true? true)
- NO hand-built selection maps
- NO changing card definitions
- NO skipping make validate
close_reason: Audited+fixed 10 blue batch 1 tests. Commit 7d3671e. 3321 tests.

title: Epic: Extract modal components from core.cljs into views/modals.cljs
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- All 13 modal components extracted from core.cljs into src/main/fizzle/views/modals.cljs
- 3 player-target modals merged into 1 parameterized function accepting confirm-event
- 2 object-target modals merged into 1 parameterized function accepting confirm-event and select-event
- Shared style constants extracted: overlay-style, container-style, header-style
- Shared components created: modal-wrapper, confirm-button, cancel-button, card-selection-grid
- selection-modal router and mode-selector-modal moved to modals.cljs
- core.cljs requires views/modals and delegates to it from app component
- All existing modal behavior preserved exactly (no visual or functional changes)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] All 13 modals render identically to before extraction
- [ ] core.cljs contains zero modal component definitions
- [ ] Modal overlay style defined exactly once (not 13x)
- [ ] Modal container style defined exactly once (not 13x)
- [ ] Confirm/cancel button patterns defined once each
- [ ] 3 player-target modals consolidated to 1 parameterized function
- [ ] 2 object-target modals consolidated to 1 parameterized function
- [ ] core.cljs reduced from ~1595 to ~450 lines
- [ ] Net LOC reduction of 200-300 lines through deduplication
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)
- [ ] No new namespaces beyond views/modals.cljs

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO behavioral changes to any modal (refactor only, not feature work)
- ❌ NO extracting zone views (hand, battlefield, graveyard, stack) — modals only scope
- ❌ NO splitting modals across multiple files (single views/modals.cljs)
- ❌ NO data-driven modal registry or multimethod dispatch (over-engineering)
- ❌ NO adding new dependencies or libraries
- ❌ NO changing event keyword namespaces (keep all ::selection-events/*, ::ability-events/*, ::events/* as-is)
- ❌ NO modifying any re-frame event handlers or subscriptions

## Approach
Extract + Parameterize pattern. Create shared style constants and reusable Reagent components (modal-wrapper, confirm-button, cancel-button, card-selection-grid). Merge the 3 near-identical player-target modals into 1 parameterized function and the 2 object-target modals into 1 parameterized function. Move all modal code to views/modals.cljs. Keep zone views in core.cljs.

## Architecture
### New file: src/main/fizzle/views/modals.cljs
Requires: re-frame.core, fizzle.events.selection, fizzle.events.abilities, fizzle.events.game, fizzle.subs.game

Shared constants: overlay-style, container-style (fn), header-style
Shared components: modal-wrapper, confirm-button, cancel-button, card-selection-grid
Merged modals: player-target-modal (1 fn, was 3), object-target-modal (1 fn, was 2)
Individual modals: card-selection-modal, scry-modal, pile-choice-modal, graveyard-selection-modal, exile-cards-selection-modal, peek-selection-modal, x-mana-selection-modal, mode-selector-modal
Routers: selection-modal, mode-selector-modal entry points

### Changes to core.cljs
- Remove lines ~420-1559
- Add require [fizzle.views.modals :as modals]
- app component calls [modals/selection-modal] and [modals/mode-selector-modal]

## Design Rationale
### Problem
core.cljs is 1,595 lines with 13 modal components sharing 80-95% identical structure. The modal overlay style (position fixed, rgba backdrop, z-index 1000) is copy-pasted 13 times. The container style (#1a1a2a, border, border-radius) is copy-pasted 13 times. Confirm button styling is duplicated 12 times. 3 player-target modals differ by a single event dispatch line. This makes maintenance error-prone and the file unwieldy.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- core.cljs:462-508 — player-target-modal, first of 3 identical variants
- core.cljs:511-558 — ability-player-target-modal, differs only at line 557 (confirm event)
- core.cljs:641-688 — cast-time-player-target-modal, differs only at line 687 (confirm event)
- core.cljs:561-638 — ability-object-target-modal
- core.cljs:691-768 — cast-time-object-target-modal, near-identical to above
- core.cljs:420-441 — selection-card-view helper, already shared by 5 modals
- core.cljs:444-459 — player-target-button helper, shared by 3 modals
- core.cljs:1375-1435 — selection-modal router dispatches by selection type
- No existing views/ directory

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Extract + Parameterize ✓
**What it is:** Extract shared style constants and create reusable Reagent components. Merge near-identical modals by parameterizing the differing parts (event dispatches).

**Investigation:**
- Compared all 13 modal overlay sections — identical across all
- Compared 3 player-target modals — differ only in confirm event dispatch
- Compared 2 object-target modals — differ in events and default zone
- Identified 4 existing helper functions that already share code

**Pros:**
- Straightforward, idiomatic Reagent
- Clear deduplication of 13x overlay and container styles
- 3→1 and 2→1 merges eliminate obvious redundancy
- Easy to understand and maintain

**Cons:**
- Still ~10 modal functions (just shorter)

**Chosen because:** Best balance of deduplication without over-engineering. Natural Reagent component composition.

#### 2. Data-Driven Modal Registry ❌
**What it is:** Define modals as data maps with title, events, render function. Single dispatcher renders based on type.

**Why we looked at this:** Maximum DRY — could eliminate most modal function definitions

**Investigation:**
- Scry modal has unique 3-section layout (top/unassigned/bottom piles)
- X-mana modal has unique +/- button layout
- Card-selection-modal has complex validation logic (multi-select vs single-select vs discard)
- Mode-selector has full-height buttons with hover effects

**Pros:** Maximum DRY for simple modals

**Cons:** Indirection makes debugging harder, complex modals still need custom render

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Too many modals have genuinely different structures. Forcing them into a data-driven pattern would add complexity without proportional benefit.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We add 10+ more simple modals that follow an identical pattern.

#### 3. HOC Only (No Merging) ❌
**What it is:** Create modal-base wrapper but keep all 13 modal functions separate.

**Why we looked at this:** Lowest risk, minimal behavioral change

**Investigation:**
- 3 player-target modals literally differ by one line — not merging wastes obvious opportunity
- Overlay/container extraction alone gives ~50% of the benefit

**Pros:** Safe, minimal changes

**Cons:** Leaves obvious 3→1 and 2→1 merges on the table

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Under-delivers on deduplication goal when easy wins are available.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Merging player-target modals causes unexpected issues.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- All 13 modal components
- Shared style constants and helper components
- Modal router (selection-modal) and mode-selector entry point
- Helper functions used only by modals (selection-card-view, player-target-button, scry-card-view, mode-button, format-mana-cost, format-additional-costs)

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Zone view extraction (hand, battlefield, graveyard, stack) — separate task
- Any behavioral or visual changes to modals
- New modal features or styling updates

### Open Questions
- Exact core.cljs line count after extraction (estimated ~450, verify during implementation)
- Whether modal-wrapper should use Reagent Form-2 or Form-1 component (decide during first task)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Extract zone views too? | Modals only | core.cljs stays ~450 lines, not ~200 |
| File organization? | Single views/modals.cljs | All modal code in one file, simpler requires |
| Approach? | Extract + Parameterize | Shared components + merge near-identical modals |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Modal Duplication Analysis
**Question explored:** How much code is actually duplicated across the 13 modals?
**Sources consulted:**
- core.cljs lines 462-1559 — all 13 modal implementations
- Compared overlay styles across all 13 — byte-identical
- Compared confirm button patterns across 12 modals — structurally identical

**Findings:**
- Overlay style: 13 copies, 0 variation
- Container style: 13 copies, 2 variations (max-width 400px vs 600px, blue vs purple border)
- Confirm button: 12 copies, minor padding variation (10px 32px vs 8px 20px)
- Player-target modals: 3 copies, differ only in confirm event dispatch
- Object-target modals: 2 copies, differ in events and default zone
- Card grid rendering: 6+ copies with minor variation in card render function

**Conclusion:** High-confidence extraction. Variations are parameterizable.

### Dead-End Paths
None — straightforward refactoring with clear patterns.

### Open Concerns Raised
- Core.cljs won't reach 300 lines (original task target) without extracting zone views → Accepted, 450 lines is the realistic target for modals-only scope
close_reason: All 4 subtasks complete. 13 modals extracted from core.cljs to views/modals.cljs. core.cljs reduced from 1595 to 449 lines. 3 player-target modals merged to 1, 2 object-target modals merged to 1. Shared style constants extracted. All 741 tests pass, lint + format clean.

title: Implement land tapping for mana production
design:
## Goal
Enable lands to be tapped to produce mana so player can generate resources for spells.

## Context
Epic success criterion: 'Lands can be played (1 per turn) and tapped for mana'
Epic anti-pattern: 'NO auto-tapping lands (player clicks to tap, explicit mana decisions)'
Currently no tap mechanic exists - :object/tapped field in schema but no events/UI.

## Investigation Needed
- Review schema for :object/tapped field
- Check how mana pool add-mana works
- Review City of Brass and Gemstone Mine card definitions
- Check existing UI patterns for click handling

## Implementation Steps
1. RED: Write test that tapping untapped land sets :object/tapped true
2. GREEN: Implement ::tap-permanent event
3. RED: Write test that tapping land adds correct mana to pool
4. GREEN: Implement ::activate-mana-ability event
5. RED: Write test that tapping tapped land fails
6. GREEN: Add validation to prevent double-tap
7. RED: Write test that lands untap on untap phase
8. GREEN: Verify ::start-turn untaps all lands
9. VERIFY: All tests pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Lands can be tapped (sets :object/tapped true)
- [ ] Tapping land adds mana to pool
- [ ] Cannot tap already-tapped land
- [ ] Lands untap during untap step
- [ ] Manual tap only (player clicks to tap)
close_reason: All success criteria met: lands can be tapped, tapping adds mana to pool, double-tap prevented, lands untap on start-turn. Manual tap via activate-mana-ability with explicit color choice.

title: Add test.check + full custom generators for all multi-spec types
description: Add test.check dependency, create spec_generators.cljs with per-type custom generators for all 92 defmethod types across effects, selections, stack items, and bot actions. Verify s/exercise produces valid data for every multi-spec.
design:
## Goal
Add test.check as a dependency and create full custom generators for all 92 multi-spec defmethod types so that s/exercise produces valid random data for every spec.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours (92 generators, ~500-800 lines)

## Implementation

### 1. Add test.check dependency
In shadow-cljs.edn, add to the MAIN :dependencies vector (shadow-cljs has no :dev-deps):
```clojure
:dependencies
[[datascript "1.7.3"]
 [reagent "1.2.0"]
 [re-frame "1.4.3"]
 [org.clojure/test.check "1.1.1"]]
```
Verify: `npx shadow-cljs compile test` succeeds with test.check available.

### 2. Study existing spec structure
Read each spec file to discover all defmethod types and their required fields:

**Actual multi-spec names (MUST use these exact names):**
- `:fizzle.engine.card-spec/effect` — dispatches via `effect-type-spec` on `:effect/type` (~40 types)
- `:fizzle.engine.stack-spec/stack-item` — dispatches via `stack-item-type-spec` on `:stack-item/type` (~16 types)
- `:fizzle.events.selection.spec/selection` — dispatches via `selection-type-spec` on `:selection/type` (~31 types)
- `:fizzle.bots.action-spec/bot-action` — dispatches via `action-type-spec` on `:action` (3 types)

Read existing minimal-valid-* example maps at the bottom of each spec file for reference on valid shapes.

### 3. Write tests first (TDD)
Create src/test/fizzle/engine/spec_generative_test.cljs with:

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.spec-generative-test
  (:require [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
            [cljs.spec.alpha :as s]
            [cljs.spec.gen.alpha :as gen]
            [fizzle.engine.card-spec]
            [fizzle.engine.stack-spec]
            [fizzle.events.selection.spec]
            [fizzle.bots.action-spec]
            [fizzle.engine.spec-generators])) ;; loads generator registrations

(deftest test-effect-exercise-roundtrip
  (testing "s/exercise produces valid effects"
    (doseq [[val _] (s/exercise :fizzle.engine.card-spec/effect 10)]
      (is (s/valid? :fizzle.engine.card-spec/effect val)
          (str "Generated effect failed validation: " (pr-str val))))))

;; Same pattern for ::stack-item, ::selection, ::bot-action
```

These tests will FAIL initially (RED) because no generators exist yet.

### 4. Create spec_generators.cljs
src/test/fizzle/engine/spec_generators.cljs

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.spec-generators
  (:require [cljs.spec.alpha :as s]
            [cljs.spec.gen.alpha :as gen]
            [clojure.test.check.generators :as tgen]
            [fizzle.engine.card-spec :as card-spec]
            [fizzle.engine.stack-spec :as stack-spec]
            [fizzle.events.selection.spec :as sel-spec]
            [fizzle.bots.action-spec :as action-spec]
            [fizzle.engine.spec-common]))
```

**UUID generator (no built-in in CLJS):**
```clojure
(def gen-uuid
  (tgen/fmap (fn [_] (random-uuid)) tgen/nat))
```

**Per-type generator pattern:**
```clojure
(defn gen-effect-add-mana []
  (tgen/hash-map
    :effect/type (tgen/return :add-mana)
    :effect/mana (tgen/fmap
                   (fn [colors]
                     (into {} (map (fn [c] [c (rand-int 5)]) colors)))
                   (tgen/not-empty
                     (tgen/vector
                       (tgen/elements [:white :blue :black :red :green :colorless]))))))
```

**CRITICAL: Optional field handling:**
- s/keys :opt fields should be ABSENT from the map, not nil
- Use: `(tgen/one-of [(tgen/return {}) (tgen/hash-map :opt-field valid-gen)])` then merge with required fields
- Exception: s/nilable fields (e.g., :stack-item/targets is s/nilable map?) — for these, nil IS a valid value

**Top-level multi-spec generator:**
```clojure
(s/def :fizzle.engine.card-spec/effect
  (s/with-gen
    (s/get-spec :fizzle.engine.card-spec/effect)
    #(tgen/one-of [(gen-effect-add-mana)
                    (gen-effect-draw)
                    ;; ... all 40 effect types
                    ])))
```

NOTE: s/with-gen returns a new spec. To override an existing spec's generator, use s/def with the same name + s/with-gen wrapping the original spec.

**Generator groups to implement:**

**Effect generators (~40 types) — read card_spec.cljs defmethods starting at line 125:**
- Simple (no extra fields): :exile-self, :sacrifice, etc. — gen/return {:effect/type :the-type}
- Mana: :add-mana — needs :effect/mana (partial mana-map)
- Amount: :draw, :mill, :scry-n, :lose-life, :gain-life — needs :effect/amount nat-int
- Zone ops: :return-from-graveyard, :tutor — needs :effect/zone keyword, :effect/count
- Complex: :drain, :each-player — may have nested effects
- Conditional: effects with :effect/condition — generate valid condition maps

**Selection generators (~31 types) — read selection/spec.cljs defmethods starting at line 60:**
- All need: :selection/type, :selection/player-id (keyword), :selection/spell-id (uuid or keyword — use :selection/object-id pattern)
- Zone-pick: :discard, :graveyard-return — needs :selection/candidates (set of uuids)
- Accumulator: :x-mana-cost, :storm-split — needs :selection/selected (number or map)
- Reorder: :scry, :peek-and-reorder — needs card lists as vectors
- Targeting: :cast-time-targeting — needs :selection/valid-targets

**Stack-item generators (~16 types) — read stack_spec.cljs defmethods starting at line 41:**
- All need: :stack-item/type, :stack-item/controller (keyword — :player-1 or :player-2)
- Spells: + :stack-item/source (uuid), :stack-item/object-ref (int), :stack-item/effects (vector)
- Triggers: + :stack-item/source (uuid)
- Combat markers: minimal (type + controller only)
- NOTE: :stack-item/targets is (s/nilable map?) — generate nil OR valid map

**Bot action generators (3 types) — read action_spec.cljs defmethods starting at line 49:**
- Uses UNNAMESPACED keys (:req-un/:opt-un)
- :pass — {:action :pass, :player-id keyword}
- :cast-spell — {:action :cast-spell, :object-id uuid, :player-id keyword, :tap-sequence vector}
- :play-land — {:action :play-land, :object-id uuid, :player-id keyword}

### 5. Register generators with specs
For each multi-spec, use s/def + s/with-gen to replace the default generator:
```clojure
(s/def :fizzle.engine.card-spec/effect
  (s/with-gen
    (s/multi-spec card-spec/effect-type-spec :effect/type)
    #(tgen/one-of [all-effect-generators...])))
```

This RE-DEFINES the spec with the same multi-spec but adds a custom generator. The spec_generators.cljs namespace must be required by the test file to trigger this registration.

### 6. GREEN — verify s/exercise works
Run tests. Fix generator/spec mismatches iteratively.
Common failures:
- Generator produces key that spec doesn't expect → remove from generator
- Generator misses required key → add to generator
- Type mismatch (string vs keyword) → fix generator value type

### 7. Verify
- make validate passes
- s/exercise produces valid data for all 4 multi-specs (test assertions)
- All 3239+ existing tests still pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] test.check added to shadow-cljs.edn :dependencies
- [ ] spec_generators.cljs exists with generators for all 92 defmethod types
- [ ] s/exercise roundtrip test passes for each of the 4 multi-specs
- [ ] Every generated sample passes s/valid? (no generator/spec divergence)
- [ ] All generators use proper custom gen/hash-map (not gen/elements over manual examples)
- [ ] Optional fields handled correctly (absent, not nil — except s/nilable fields)
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] All existing tests pass (no regressions)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Optional fields — absent vs nil**
s/keys :opt means the key may be ABSENT. If present, it must match the spec. Generating nil for optional fields will FAIL unless the field spec uses s/nilable. Pattern: merge required fields with conditionally-included optional fields. Do NOT use gen/return nil for optional fields.

**Edge Case: s/nilable fields**
:stack-item/targets is (s/nilable map?). For nilable specs, generators must produce EITHER nil OR a valid value. Use (tgen/one-of [(tgen/return nil) (tgen/hash-map ...)]).

**Edge Case: UUID generation in ClojureScript**
ClojureScript has no gen/uuid built-in. Use: (tgen/fmap (fn [_] (random-uuid)) tgen/nat) to create a UUID generator.

**Edge Case: Re-defining specs with generators**
s/with-gen on a multi-spec requires re-defining the spec via s/def. This REPLACES the spec globally in the spec registry. The spec_generators.cljs namespace should ONLY be required by test code, never production code, to avoid polluting the registry.

**Edge Case: Unnamespaced keys for bot actions**
Bot action specs use :req-un/:opt-un. Generators must produce maps with unnamespaced keys (:action, :player-id, :object-id) not namespace-qualified ones.

**Edge Case: :selection/object-id is (s/or :kw keyword? :uuid uuid?)**
Generators for selection types that include :selection/object-id must produce EITHER a keyword OR a uuid, not always one type. Use (tgen/one-of [(tgen/return :test-spell) gen-uuid]).

**Reference: shadow-cljs dependency format**
shadow-cljs uses Clojure vector format in :dependencies, not npm. test.check is a Clojure/ClojureScript library, added as [org.clojure/test.check "1.1.1"].

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO gen/elements over manual examples (epic anti-pattern: must use proper custom generators)
- ❌ NO skipping types (every defmethod gets a generator — no exceptions)
- ❌ NO generators that ignore required fields (must include all :req keys)
- ❌ NO modifying existing spec definitions to make generators easier (descriptive specs are immutable)
- ❌ NO nil for optional fields unless spec is s/nilable (use absent keys instead)
- ❌ NO requiring spec_generators from production code (test-only namespace)
close_reason: test.check added, 91 custom generators, s/exercise roundtrip tests pass, 3243 tests

title: Implement counter-spell effect and Counterspell + Annul cards
description: First task for the Counter Foundation epic. Implement the :counter-spell effect type and both card definitions with full test suites.
design:
## Goal
Implement the :counter-spell effect type in engine/effects.cljs and define both Counterspell and Annul cards with stack-zone targeting. Full test suites for both cards.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing patterns
   - engine/effects.cljs:442 — :destroy effect handler (model for :counter-spell)
   - engine/rules.cljs:457 — move-spell-off-stack (handles graveyard/exile/copy removal)
   - engine/targeting.cljs:25 — find-valid-targets with :target/zone (stack targeting already works)
   - db/queries.cljs:247 — matches-criteria? (supports :match/types for Annul type restriction)
   - cards/green/crumble.cljs — targeted spell pattern (destroy target artifact)

2. Write tests first (TDD)
   See detailed test specifications below.

3. Implementation checklist
   - [ ] engine/effects.cljs — Add :counter-spell defmethod on execute-effect-impl
         Guard: check target-id exists, target object exists, target is still in :stack zone
         Action: call (rules/move-spell-off-stack db nil target-id)
         Return: updated db (or unchanged db if any guard fails)
   - [ ] cards/blue/counterspell.cljs — Card definition:
         :card/id :counterspell, :card/name "Counterspell", :card/cmc 2
         :card/mana-cost {:blue 2}, :card/colors #{:blue}, :card/types #{:instant}
         :card/text "Counter target spell."
         :card/targeting [{:target/id :target-spell :target/type :object :target/zone :stack :target/controller :any :target/required true}]
         :card/effects [{:effect/type :counter-spell :effect/target-ref :target-spell}]
   - [ ] cards/blue/annul.cljs — Card definition:
         Same pattern but :card/id :annul, :card/name "Annul", :card/cmc 1
         :card/mana-cost {:blue 1}
         :card/text "Counter target artifact or enchantment spell."
         :card/targeting adds :target/criteria {:match/types #{:artifact :enchantment}}
   - [ ] cards/registry.cljs — Require both namespaces, concat both cards into all-cards
   - [ ] test/fizzle/cards/blue/counterspell_test.cljs — 10+ tests
   - [ ] test/fizzle/cards/blue/annul_test.cljs — 10+ tests

## Test Specifications

### Counterspell Tests (counterspell_test.cljs)

**A. Card Definition (1 test)**
- test-card-definition: Verify ALL fields with exact values — :card/id :counterspell, :card/name "Counterspell", :card/cmc 2, :card/mana-cost {:blue 2}, :card/colors #{:blue}, :card/types #{:instant}, :card/text exact oracle text, targeting vector has 1 entry with :target/zone :stack, effects vector has 1 entry with :effect/type :counter-spell

**B. Cast-Resolve Happy Path (2 tests)**
- test-counter-spell-on-stack: Setup: add opponent, put Dark Ritual in opponent's hand, opponent casts Dark Ritual (now on stack), add Counterspell to player's hand with {U}{U} mana. Build targeting selection with Dark Ritual as target. Confirm target, resolve Counterspell. Assert: Dark Ritual is in graveyard (not on stack), Counterspell is in graveyard, stack is empty.
- test-counter-instant-on-stack: Same setup but with a different spell (e.g., Opt) to verify not card-specific. Assert: Opt in graveyard, stack empty.

**C. Cannot-Cast Guards (3 tests)**
- test-cannot-cast-without-mana: Add Counterspell to hand, no blue mana. Assert: get-casting-modes returns empty.
- test-cannot-cast-with-empty-stack: Add Counterspell to hand with {U}{U} mana, but NO spells on stack. Assert: has-valid-targets? returns false for Counterspell.
- test-cannot-cast-from-graveyard: Add Counterspell to graveyard with mana. Assert: not castable (wrong zone).

**D. Storm Count (1 test)**
- test-casting-increments-storm: Cast Counterspell (needs a spell to target). Assert storm count incremented by 1.

**E. Targeting Tests (2 tests)**
- test-targets-any-spell-type: Put sorcery + instant on stack. Assert: both appear as valid targets.
- test-does-not-target-abilities: Verify that triggered ability stack-items are NOT valid targets (they're stack-items, not objects in :stack zone). This should work automatically since find-valid-targets queries objects.

**G. Edge Cases (2+ tests)**
- test-counter-fizzles-when-target-leaves-stack: Put spell on stack, target it with Counterspell, resolve the targeted spell first (it leaves stack), then resolve Counterspell. Assert: Counterspell fizzles (returns {:fizzled? true}).
- test-counter-copy-removes-from-db: Put a storm copy on stack (object with :object/is-copy true). Counter it. Assert: copy object is removed from db entirely (not in graveyard).
- test-counter-flashback-spell-exiles: Cast a spell via flashback mode (e.g., Cabal Therapy if available, or manually set :object/cast-mode with :mode/on-resolve :exile). Counter it. Assert: spell goes to exile (not graveyard).

### Annul Tests (annul_test.cljs)

**A. Card Definition (1 test)**
- test-card-definition: Verify ALL fields with exact values — :card/id :annul, :card/cmc 1, :card/mana-cost {:blue 1}, targeting criteria {:match/types #{:artifact :enchantment}}, etc.

**B. Cast-Resolve Happy Path (2 tests)**
- test-counter-artifact-spell: Put Lotus Petal on stack, counter with Annul. Assert: Lotus Petal in graveyard.
- test-counter-enchantment-spell: Put an enchantment spell on stack (may need to add one to test db or use manual setup). Assert: spell in graveyard.

**C. Cannot-Cast Guards (3 tests)**
- test-cannot-cast-without-mana: No blue mana. Assert: not castable.
- test-cannot-target-instant-on-stack: Put an instant (non-artifact, non-enchantment) on stack. Assert: has-valid-targets? returns false for Annul.
- test-cannot-target-sorcery-on-stack: Put a sorcery on stack. Assert: not a valid target.

**D. Storm Count (1 test)**
- test-casting-increments-storm: Cast Annul targeting artifact spell. Assert storm +1.

**G. Edge Cases (2+ tests)**
- test-targets-only-artifact-enchantment: Put artifact + instant on stack. Assert: only artifact is valid target.
- test-counter-fizzles-when-target-leaves: Same fizzle pattern as Counterspell.
- test-annul-cannot-target-creature-spell: If creature spells exist in future, ensure they're excluded. For now, verify type filtering works with instants/sorceries.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] :counter-spell effect handler added to engine/effects.cljs
- [ ] Effect calls rules/move-spell-off-stack (not reimplemented)
- [ ] Effect guards: no target, target gone, target not on stack = no-op
- [ ] Countered normal spell goes to graveyard
- [ ] Countered flashback spell goes to exile
- [ ] Countered copy is removed from db (not moved to graveyard)
- [ ] Counterspell correctly targets any spell (object) on stack
- [ ] Counterspell does NOT target triggered abilities (stack-items without objects)
- [ ] Annul only targets artifact/enchantment spells on stack via :match/types criteria
- [ ] Fizzling works when counter target leaves stack before resolution
- [ ] Both cards registered in registry.cljs
- [ ] 10+ tests per card, all passing
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Self-targeting**
Counterspell on the stack can technically target itself (legal in MTG but pointless). The targeting system will show it as a valid target since it's an object in :stack zone. This is correct behavior — don't add special-case exclusion.

**Edge Case: Abilities vs Spells**
Triggered abilities (e.g., storm trigger) are stack-items in Datascript but NOT objects with :object/zone :stack. find-valid-targets queries objects by zone, so abilities are automatically excluded. Verify this with a test.

**Edge Case: Counter resolves but target already gone**
If spell A and Counterspell both target spell B, and spell A resolves first removing B from stack, Counterspell should fizzle. The existing fizzle mechanism (target-still-legal? checks zone) handles this.

**Edge Case: matches-criteria? on stack objects**
Annul's type filter uses :match/types #{:artifact :enchantment}. matches-criteria? reads :card/types from the object's card. Objects on the stack have their card data available via :object/card. Verify this works in tests.

**Parameter mapping for move-spell-off-stack**
Function signature: (move-spell-off-stack db _player-id object-id). The _player-id is unused. The effect handler receives target-id from (:effect/target effect). Pass nil for player-id.

**Reference implementations**
- :destroy effect at engine/effects.cljs:442 — exact same pattern (get target, guard, act)
- Crumble card at cards/green/crumble.cljs — targeted spell definition pattern
- Crumble test at test/fizzle/cards/green/crumble_test.cljs — targeted spell test setup pattern

## Anti-Patterns (from epic + task-specific)
- ❌ NO hardcoded graveyard destination (use move-spell-off-stack)
- ❌ NO special-casing card names in engine code
- ❌ NO reimplementing spell removal logic

title: Dispatch creature-attacked events from combat
description:
After attackers are marked in combat, dispatch :creature-attacked events for each attacker so the trigger system can fire attack triggers.

## File to modify

### src/main/fizzle/events/selection/combat.cljs

Add requires:
```clojure
[fizzle.engine.trigger-dispatch :as dispatch]
[fizzle.engine.events :as game-events]
```

Modify execute-confirmed-selection :select-attackers (lines 24-42):
```clojure
(defmethod sel-core/execute-confirmed-selection :select-attackers
  [game-db selection]
  (let [selected (:selection/selected selection)
        attacker-ids (vec selected)
        controller (:selection/player-id selection)
        si-eid (:selection/stack-item-eid selection)
        db (stack/remove-stack-item game-db si-eid)]
    (if (empty? attacker-ids)
      {:db db}
      (let [db (combat/tap-and-mark-attackers db attacker-ids)
            db (stack/create-stack-item db
                                        {:stack-item/type :combat-damage
                                         :stack-item/controller controller
                                         :stack-item/description "Combat Damage"})
            db (stack/create-stack-item db
                                        {:stack-item/type :declare-blockers
                                         :stack-item/controller controller
                                         :stack-item/description "Declare Blockers"})
            ;; Dispatch attack triggers AFTER creating combat stack items
            ;; so triggers go on top of stack (resolve before blockers)
            db (reduce (fn [d atk-id]
                         (dispatch/dispatch-event d
                           (game-events/creature-attacked-event atk-id controller)))
                       db attacker-ids)]
        {:db db}))))
```

## Stack ordering after dispatch
1. Attack trigger(s) — top, resolve first
2. declare-blockers
3. combat-damage

This matches MTG rules: attack triggers resolve before blockers are declared.

## Verification
- make validate passes
- When a creature with :creature-attacks trigger attacks, a trigger stack item is created
- Stack order is correct (trigger on top of declare-blockers)
- Creatures without attack triggers still work normally
close_reason: Closed
