title: Implement Frantic Search with untap-lands infrastructure
description: Implement Frantic Search ({2}{U} Instant: Draw 2, discard 2, untap up to 3 lands). This is the first card in the blue combo epic and builds the shared untap infrastructure.
design:
## Goal
Implement Frantic Search and the untap-lands infrastructure it requires. This card draws 2, then the player discards 2 (existing selection), then the player selects up to 3 tapped lands to untap (new infrastructure).

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns (reference code)
- Zone-pick builder: events/selection/core.cljs:113-172 (generic zone-pick builder with config)
- Zone-pick config map: events/selection/core.cljs:121-132 (per-effect-type overrides)
- Zone-pick executor: events/selection/core.cljs:179-186 (generic — moves cards between zones)
- Selection hierarchy derives: events/selection/core.cljs:30-63 (route effect types to patterns)
- build-selection-for-effect multimethod: events/selection/core.cljs:85-106 (dispatches on effect type)
- execute-confirmed-selection multimethod: events/selection/core.cljs:70-82 (dispatches on selection type)
- Careful Study card definition: cards/blue/careful_study.cljs:8-20 (draw + discard chaining pattern)
- tap-permanent function: events/lands.cljs:78-89 (pattern for untap-permanent)
- Effect registration: engine/effects.cljs:79 (execute-effect-impl multimethod)
- Validation types: engine/validation.cljs:74-82 (:at-most already exists)

### 2. Add untap-permanent function
- File: src/main/fizzle/events/lands.cljs (alongside tap-permanent at line 78)
- Pattern: inverse of tap-permanent — set :object/tapped to false
- Signature: (untap-permanent [db object-id]) -> db
- Uses Datascript: [:db/add obj-eid :object/tapped false]
- Guard: check object exists, return db unchanged if not

### 3. Add :untap-lands effect type
- File: src/main/fizzle/engine/effects/zones.cljs
- Register: (defmethod execute-effect-impl :untap-lands ...)
- This effect is INTERACTIVE — it needs player selection
- Return: {:db db :needs-selection effect} to pause and trigger selection
- The effect data shape: {:effect/type :untap-lands :effect/count 3} (up to 3)

### 4. Add untap-lands selection (zone-pick pattern with custom executor)
- **Hierarchy derive**: In events/selection/core.cljs, add (derive :untap-lands :zone-pick) to route to zone-pick builder
- **Zone-pick config**: Add to zone-pick-config map in core.cljs:121:
  {:untap-lands {:selection-type :untap-lands
                 :source-zone :battlefield
                 :target-zone :battlefield
                 :card-source :zone
                 :validation :at-most
                 :min-count 0}}
- **Candidate filtering**: The zone-pick builder must find tapped lands controlled by the casting player:
  - Query: objects in :battlefield zone, :object/tapped true, :object/controller = player, has land type
  - Populate :selection/candidate-ids with these entity IDs
- **CRITICAL: Custom executor required** — the generic zone-pick executor calls zones/move-to-zone (moves cards between zones), but untap-lands needs to change :object/tapped state WITHOUT moving. Create custom executor:
  (defmethod execute-confirmed-selection :untap-lands [game-db selection]
    (let [selected (:selection/selected selection)]
      {:db (reduce untap-permanent game-db selected)}))
  File: src/main/fizzle/events/selection/zone_ops.cljs

### 5. Define Frantic Search card
- File: src/main/fizzle/cards/blue/frantic_search.cljs
- Card data (using actual EDN format matching Careful Study pattern):
  {:card/id :frantic-search
   :card/name "Frantic Search"
   :card/cmc 3
   :card/mana-cost {:generic 2 :blue 1}
   :card/colors #{:blue}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/text "Draw two cards, then discard two cards. Untap up to three lands."
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :draw :effect/amount 2}
                  {:effect/type :discard :effect/count 2 :effect/selection :player}
                  {:effect/type :untap-lands :effect/count 3}]}

### 6. Register in registry
- File: src/main/fizzle/cards/registry.cljs
- Add require for fizzle.cards.blue.frantic-search
- Add card to all-cards vector

### 7. Write tests (TDD - tests first)
- File: src/test/fizzle/cards/blue/frantic_search_test.cljs
- Use test helpers: th/cast-and-resolve, th/resolve-top, th/confirm-selection
- See Category A-G test specifications in Success Criteria below

## Success Criteria
- [ ] untap-permanent function exists and sets :object/tapped to false on a battlefield permanent
- [ ] :untap-lands effect returns {:needs-selection effect} to trigger player selection
- [ ] Untap-lands selection candidate list contains ONLY: tapped lands, controlled by casting player, on battlefield
- [ ] Untap-lands selection uses :at-most validation (player can select 0 to N lands)
- [ ] Untap-lands custom executor sets :object/tapped false on selected lands (does NOT move zones)
- [ ] Frantic Search: cast resolves draw 2 → discard 2 selection → untap lands selection in sequence
- [ ] Frantic Search: after full resolution, 2 new cards drawn, 2 cards discarded, selected lands untapped
- [ ] Card definition test: all fields match exact values (id, name, cmc, mana-cost, colors, types, text, effects)
- [ ] Cannot-cast: insufficient mana returns false from can-cast?
- [ ] Cannot-cast: card in graveyard returns false from can-cast?
- [ ] Storm count increments by 1 after casting
- [ ] Edge: 0 tapped lands → selection auto-confirms or shows empty selection
- [ ] Edge: fewer than 3 tapped lands → player can select all available (at-most validation)
- [ ] Edge: player selects 0 lands → valid (at-most allows 0)
- [ ] Edge: opponent's tapped lands do NOT appear in selection candidates
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Custom executor is required (not generic zone-pick)**
The generic zone-pick executor in core.cljs:179-186 calls zones/move-to-zone to move cards between zones. Untap-lands does NOT move cards — it changes :object/tapped state on permanents already on the battlefield. You MUST write a custom execute-confirmed-selection :untap-lands defmethod that calls untap-permanent instead.

**Candidate filtering must be precise**
The selection must show ONLY tapped lands controlled by the casting player. Query must filter on:
- :object/zone = :battlefield
- :object/tapped = true
- :object/controller = casting player
- card has land type (check :card/types for :land)
If ANY of these filters is missing, wrong cards will appear in selection.

**Effect chaining with multiple selections**
Frantic Search has TWO interactive effects (discard + untap-lands). The engine's reduce-effects pauses at the first interactive effect, stores remaining-effects, and resumes after selection. Verify that after discard selection completes, the :untap-lands effect is correctly stored in remaining-effects and triggers its own selection.

**Empty tapped lands (0 candidates)**
When there are 0 tapped lands, the selection system must handle gracefully. With :at-most validation and 0 candidates, either:
- Auto-confirm empty selection (if :selection/auto-confirm? handles this), or
- Show selection with 0 candidates and let player confirm empty
Check how :return-from-graveyard handles 0 candidates in the graveyard for the precedent.

**Edge: fewer cards in library than draw amount**
If library has < 2 cards when Frantic Search resolves, the draw effect draws what's available (existing behavior). The discard selection still requires 2 — verify behavior when hand size after draw is < 2. This is existing engine behavior, not new, but should have a test.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO using generic zone-pick executor for untap — it moves cards between zones; untap must change :object/tapped in place
- ❌ NO case statements on card names in engine code — the :untap-lands effect and selection must be generic, driven by effect data
- ❌ NO hardcoding Frantic Search-specific logic in the selection system — must work for any card with :untap-lands effect
- ❌ NO reimplementing production handlers in tests — use th/cast-and-resolve, th/resolve-top, th/confirm-selection
- ❌ NO tautological assertions — assert exact values, not some?/string?
- ❌ NO skipping player selection — player MUST choose which lands to untap
- ❌ NO including opponent's permanents in untap selection candidates
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 2: Extend cast-spell and resolve-spell for alternate modes
design:
## Goal
Extend cast-spell to accept an optional mode parameter, pay additional costs, and track the cast mode on the stack. Extend resolve-spell to use the mode's on-resolve destination (e.g., exile for flashback).

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Task 1 completed: get-casting-modes, can-cast-mode?, get-castable-cards are implemented.
Now we need to actually cast spells using a specific mode.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- `rules.cljs:171-189` - Current `cast-spell` implementation
- `rules.cljs:222-244` - Current `resolve-spell` implementation
- `zones.cljs` - How zones work
- `db/schema.cljs` - Datascript schema for adding :object/cast-mode

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

```clojure
;; cast-spell-mode tests
(deftest cast-spell-mode-primary-test
  (testing "cast-spell-mode casts with primary mode from hand"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          obj-id (:object/id ritual)
          modes (rules/get-casting-modes db :player-1 obj-id)
          primary-mode (first modes)
          db' (rules/cast-spell-mode db :player-1 obj-id primary-mode)]
      (is (= :stack (:object/zone (q/get-object db' obj-id)))
          "Card should be on stack")
      (is (= 0 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))
          "Mana should be paid")
      (is (= 1 (q/get-storm-count db' :player-1))
          "Storm should increment"))))

(deftest cast-spell-mode-flashback-test
  (testing "cast-spell-mode casts flashback from graveyard"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          [obj-id db] (add-card-to-zone db :player-1 deep-analysis-card :graveyard)
          db (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {:colorless 1 :blue 1})
          initial-life (q/get-life-total db :player-1)
          modes (rules/get-casting-modes db :player-1 obj-id)
          flashback-mode (first modes)
          db' (rules/cast-spell-mode db :player-1 obj-id flashback-mode)]
      (is (= :stack (:object/zone (q/get-object db' obj-id)))
          "Card should be on stack")
      (is (= (- initial-life 3) (q/get-life-total db' :player-1))
          "Life should be paid (3 life for flashback)")
      (is (= 1 (q/get-storm-count db' :player-1))
          "Storm should increment for flashback cast"))))

(deftest cast-spell-mode-tracks-mode-on-stack-test
  (testing "cast-spell-mode stores :object/cast-mode on stack"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          [obj-id db] (add-card-to-zone db :player-1 deep-analysis-card :graveyard)
          db (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {:colorless 1 :blue 1})
          modes (rules/get-casting-modes db :player-1 obj-id)
          flashback-mode (first modes)
          db' (rules/cast-spell-mode db :player-1 obj-id flashback-mode)
          obj' (q/get-object db' obj-id)]
      (is (some? (:object/cast-mode obj'))
          "Cast mode should be stored on object")
      (is (= :exile (get-in obj' [:object/cast-mode :mode/on-resolve]))
          "Flashback mode should have :exile on-resolve"))))

;; resolve-spell with flashback mode tests
(deftest resolve-flashback-exiles-test
  (testing "resolve-spell sends flashback spell to exile"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          [obj-id db] (add-card-to-zone db :player-1 deep-analysis-card :graveyard)
          db (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {:colorless 1 :blue 1})
          modes (rules/get-casting-modes db :player-1 obj-id)
          flashback-mode (first modes)
          db-cast (rules/cast-spell-mode db :player-1 obj-id flashback-mode)
          db-resolved (rules/resolve-spell db-cast :player-1 obj-id)]
      (is (= :exile (:object/zone (q/get-object db-resolved obj-id)))
          "Flashback spell should go to exile, not graveyard"))))

(deftest resolve-normal-goes-to-graveyard-test
  (testing "resolve-spell sends normal spell to graveyard"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          [obj-id db] (add-card-to-zone db :player-1 deep-analysis-card :hand)
          db (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {:colorless 3 :blue 1})
          modes (rules/get-casting-modes db :player-1 obj-id)
          primary-mode (first modes)
          db-cast (rules/cast-spell-mode db :player-1 obj-id primary-mode)
          db-resolved (rules/resolve-spell db-cast :player-1 obj-id)]
      (is (= :graveyard (:object/zone (q/get-object db-resolved obj-id)))
          "Normal spell should go to graveyard"))))

;; Backwards compatibility
(deftest cast-spell-without-mode-works-test
  (testing "cast-spell without mode argument still works (backwards compatible)"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          obj-id (:object/id ritual)
          db' (rules/cast-spell db :player-1 obj-id)]
      (is (= :stack (:object/zone (q/get-object db' obj-id)))
          "Existing cast-spell API should still work")
      (is (= 1 (q/get-storm-count db' :player-1))
          "Storm should increment"))))
```

### 3. Implementation checklist

- [ ] Study current cast-spell implementation in rules.cljs
- [ ] Create `pay-additional-cost` helper (reuse can-pay-additional-cost? logic):
  ```clojure
  (defn- pay-additional-cost
    "Pay a single additional cost. Returns updated db."
    [db player-id object-id cost]
    (case (:cost/type cost)
      :pay-life (let [obj (q/get-object db object-id)
                      controller-eid (:db/id (:object/controller obj))
                      current-life (d/q '[:find ?life . :in $ ?p :where [?p :player/life ?life]] db controller-eid)
                      new-life (- current-life (:cost/amount cost))]
                  (d/db-with db [[:db/add controller-eid :player/life new-life]]))
      ;; Future cost types added here
      db))
  ```
- [ ] Create `pay-additional-costs` to pay all costs:
  ```clojure
  (defn- pay-additional-costs [db player-id object-id costs]
    (reduce (fn [d cost] (pay-additional-cost d player-id object-id cost)) db costs))
  ```
- [ ] Create `set-cast-mode` helper to store mode on object:
  ```clojure
  (defn- set-cast-mode [db object-id mode]
    (let [obj-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?oid :where [?e :object/id ?oid]] db object-id)]
      (d/db-with db [[:db/add obj-eid :object/cast-mode mode]])))
  ```
- [ ] Create `cast-spell-mode` function:
  ```clojure
  (defn cast-spell-mode
    "Cast a spell using a specific mode.
     Pays mana cost and additional costs, moves to stack, tracks mode.
     Increments storm count. Creates storm trigger if applicable."
    [db player-id object-id mode]
    (-> db
        (mana/pay-mana player-id (:mode/mana-cost mode))
        (pay-additional-costs player-id object-id (:mode/additional-costs mode))
        (zones/move-to-zone object-id :stack)
        (set-stack-order object-id)
        (set-cast-mode object-id mode)
        (increment-storm player-id)
        (maybe-create-storm-trigger player-id object-id)))
  ```
- [ ] Update existing `cast-spell` to use cast-spell-mode with primary mode:
  ```clojure
  (defn cast-spell [db player-id object-id]
    (let [modes (get-casting-modes db player-id object-id)
          primary (first (filter #(= :primary (:mode/id %)) modes))]
      (cast-spell-mode db player-id object-id primary)))
  ```
- [ ] Modify `resolve-spell` to check :object/cast-mode for destination:
  ```clojure
  (let [cast-mode (:object/cast-mode obj)
        mode-destination (:mode/on-resolve cast-mode)
        destination (cond
                      mode-destination mode-destination
                      (permanent-type? card-types) :battlefield
                      :else :graveyard)]
    ...)
  ```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] cast-spell-mode casts from hand with primary mode (tests verify stack placement, mana paid)
- [ ] cast-spell-mode casts from graveyard with flashback mode (tests verify stack placement)
- [ ] cast-spell-mode pays additional costs - life reduced by 3 for flashback
- [ ] cast-spell-mode tracks :object/cast-mode on stack object (test verifies mode stored)
- [ ] resolve-spell uses mode's :mode/on-resolve destination
- [ ] Flashback spells go to :exile on resolution (not graveyard)
- [ ] Normal spells still go to :graveyard on resolution
- [ ] Existing cast-spell behavior unchanged (backwards compatible) - existing tests pass
- [ ] Storm count increments for flashback casts (test verifies storm=1)
- [ ] All tests pass: `make test`
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass: `make validate`

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Invalid/nil mode**
- cast-spell-mode should handle nil mode gracefully
- If called with nil, could throw or return db unchanged
- Decision: Require valid mode (caller must use get-casting-modes first)
- Document precondition in docstring

**Edge Case: Object in wrong zone for mode**
- What if someone calls cast-spell-mode with flashback mode but card is in hand?
- Mana/costs would be paid but semantically wrong
- Decision: Caller responsibility - can-cast-mode? should be checked first
- Same pattern as existing cast-spell (caller checks can-cast? first)

**Edge Case: Unknown :mode/on-resolve value**
- If mode has :mode/on-resolve :unknown, zones/move-to-zone would fail or create :unknown zone
- Decision: Only support :graveyard, :exile, :battlefield
- Default to :graveyard if nil (matches current behavior)

**Edge Case: Partial cost payment failure**
- If mana paid but life payment fails, state is inconsistent
- Current design: can-cast-mode? validates ALL costs first
- Implementation: pay-additional-costs assumes validation done
- No rollback needed - caller must validate first

**Datascript Schema Note**
- :object/cast-mode stores the entire mode map
- Datascript allows arbitrary nested maps without schema changes
- No schema modification needed - verify this pattern works

**Reference Implementation**
- Study how :object/tapped is set in costs.cljs:70
- Follow same d/db-with pattern for :object/cast-mode

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO hardcoding 'flashback goes to exile' - use mode's :mode/on-resolve
- ❌ NO breaking existing cast-spell callers - maintain backwards compatibility
- ❌ NO skipping storm increment for alternate modes - flashback is still casting
- ❌ NO partial state updates - always pay all costs or none (validate first)
- ❌ NO modifying zones.cljs - use existing move-to-zone with :exile keyword
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement Infernal Contract card + dynamic life loss
description: Implement the Infernal Contract card (BBB Sorcery - Draw four cards, lose half life rounded up) with a new :half-life-rounded-up dynamic resolver and update :lose-life to support dynamic amounts.
design:
## Goal
Implement Infernal Contract as a complete card with all production code, registry integration, and tests.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Add :half-life-rounded-up dynamic resolver
**File:** src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs (after existing resolve-dynamic-impl defmethods, ~line 84)
```clojure
(defmethod resolve-dynamic-impl :half-life-rounded-up
  [db player-id _dynamic _object-id]
  (let [life (q/get-life-total db player-id)]
    (js/Math.ceil (/ life 2))))
```

### 2. Update :lose-life to support dynamic amounts
**File:** src/main/fizzle/engine/effects/life.cljs (line 12)
Change: `(get effect :effect/amount 0)` to `(effects/resolve-dynamic-value db player-id (get effect :effect/amount 0) object-id)`
This mirrors how :deal-damage already works at line 48. Add `[fizzle.engine.effects :as effects]` to the require if not already present.

**Regression safety:** No production cards currently use :lose-life (it's a runtime-only type). Test files (effects_test.cljs:401, rules_test.cljs:1311) use static integers, which resolve-dynamic-value passes through unchanged via its `(integer? value) value` branch.

### 3. Create card definition
**File:** src/main/fizzle/cards/black/infernal_contract.cljs (NEW)
```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.black.infernal-contract
  "Infernal Contract card definition.

   Infernal Contract: BBB - Sorcery
   Draw four cards. You lose half your life total, rounded up.")

(def card
  {:card/id :infernal-contract
   :card/name "Infernal Contract"
   :card/cmc 3
   :card/mana-cost {:black 3}
   :card/colors #{:black}
   :card/types #{:sorcery}
   :card/text "Draw four cards. You lose half your life total, rounded up."
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :draw
                   :effect/amount 4}
                  {:effect/type :lose-life
                   :effect/amount {:dynamic/type :half-life-rounded-up}}]})
```

### 4. Register in registry
**File:** src/main/fizzle/cards/registry.cljs
- Add require: `[fizzle.cards.black.infernal-contract :as infernal-contract]`
- Add to all-cards vector: `infernal-contract/card` (in the black cards section, after `ill-gotten-gains/card`)

### 5. Update runtime-only comment in card_spec.cljs
**File:** src/main/fizzle/engine/card_spec.cljs (line 327)
Move the :lose-life defmethod OUT of the "Runtime-only types" comment section, since Infernal Contract now uses it in a card definition. Place it with other amount+target effects (Group 1, near :deal-damage). No spec field changes needed — :effect/amount already accepts dynamic maps via `(s/or :int int? :keyword keyword? :dynamic map?)`.

### 6. Write tests (TDD)
**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/black/infernal_contract_test.cljs (NEW)

**A. Card definition test:**
Verify ALL fields with exact values: :card/id :infernal-contract, :card/name "Infernal Contract", :card/cmc 3, :card/mana-cost {:black 3}, :card/colors #{:black}, :card/types #{:sorcery}, :card/text exact oracle, effects vector has 2 entries (:draw with amount 4, :lose-life with {:dynamic/type :half-life-rounded-up}).

**B. Happy path (20 life):**
Setup: create-test-db with {:mana {:black 3} :life 20}, add 10 cards to library, add infernal-contract to hand.
Cast via th/cast-and-resolve. Verify: drew 4 cards (hand count increased by 3 = 4 drawn - 1 cast), life is 10 (lost ceil(20/2) = 10).

**C. Cannot-cast guards:**
- Insufficient mana: only 2 black → can-cast? false
- Wrong zone: card in graveyard → can-cast? false

**D. Storm count:**
Casting increments storm by 1.

**G. Edge cases (use doseq where appropriate):**
- Odd life (19): loses ceil(19/2) = 10, ends at 9
- Odd life (7): loses ceil(7/2) = 4, ends at 3
- Even life (20): loses ceil(20/2) = 10, ends at 10
- 1 life: loses ceil(1/2) = 1, ends at 0
- 0 life: loses ceil(0/2) = 0, life stays at 0 (guard: amount <= 0 returns db)
- Negative life (-3): loses ceil(-3/2) = ceil(-1.5) = -1, guard catches (<= -1 0), life stays at -3
- Library has fewer than 4 cards (e.g., 2): draws 2 cards, still loses half life

Use `th/cast-and-resolve` for all tests — Infernal Contract has no selections, no additional costs, no targeting.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Card definition at cards/black/infernal_contract.cljs matches oracle text exactly
- [ ] :half-life-rounded-up dynamic resolver computes ceil(life/2) for all life totals
- [ ] :lose-life handler uses resolve-dynamic-value (one-line change at life.cljs:12)
- [ ] Card registered in registry.cljs and loadable
- [ ] Existing :lose-life tests still pass unchanged (effects_test.cljs, rules_test.cljs)
- [ ] Min 8 tests covering: definition, happy path, cannot-cast x2, storm, edge cases x3+
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Regression safety:**
No production cards use :lose-life directly. Only vendetta uses :lose-life-equal-to-toughness (different handler). The resolve-dynamic-value call passes static integers through unchanged, so effects_test.cljs and rules_test.cljs tests are unaffected.

**Edge case: negative/zero life:**
js/Math.ceil on negative or zero life produces <= 0 amounts. The existing guard in :lose-life (`if (<= amount 0) db`) handles this correctly — no life change occurs. Test this explicitly.

**Edge case: library smaller than 4:**
The :draw effect draws min(amount, library-size). If library has 2 cards, draws 2. Life loss still computed from current life total (not cards drawn). Test with small library.

**No spec changes needed:**
:effect/amount spec at card_spec.cljs:82 already accepts `(s/or :int int? :keyword keyword? :dynamic map?)`. The dynamic map {:dynamic/type :half-life-rounded-up} is already valid.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO imperative logic in card definition (cards are data, engine interprets)
- ❌ NO new effect type for life loss (use existing :lose-life with dynamic amount)
- ❌ NO hardcoded life amount (must compute from current life total at resolution)
- ❌ NO additional cost mechanism for life loss (oracle says it's an effect, not a cost)
- ❌ NO tautological test assertions (test exact values, not some?/string?)
- ❌ NO reimplementing production handlers in tests (use th/ helpers)
close_reason: All success criteria met. Commit 14bc07c. 3297 tests, 0 failures.

title: Creature UX polish: P/T display, combat indicators, blocker assignment UI
description: Follow-on from fizzle-y45z (Creatures, Tokens, and Combat). The core engine works but the UX needs polish for creature-specific information: power/toughness display on battlefield cards, visual indicators for attacking/blocking/summoning-sick state, and a clear blocker assignment flow. Currently hard to test blocking without better visual feedback.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
1. Creatures on battlefield display effective P/T below card name
2. P/T is color-coded: green when effective > base (buffed), red when effective < base (debuffed), default when equal to base
3. Damage marked shown as red badge when object/damage-marked > 0
4. Summoning sickness indicator on creatures that cannot attack (no haste, entered this turn)
5. Combat status badges: sword icon for attacking creatures, shield icon for blocking creatures
6. Blocker selection modal shows P/T on creature cards and combat math preview (e.g. '3 power vs 2 toughness')
7. Attacker selection modal shows P/T on creature cards

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] All creatures display effective P/T on battlefield
- [ ] Modified P/T visually distinguished by color (green=buffed, red=debuffed)
- [ ] Damage badge visible on creatures with damage-marked > 0
- [ ] Summoning sickness indicator shown on applicable creatures
- [ ] Attacking creatures show sword badge during combat
- [ ] Blocking creatures show shield badge during combat
- [ ] Blocker selection modal displays P/T and combat math
- [ ] Attacker selection modal displays P/T
- [ ] All existing tests pass (make validate)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO per-field subscriptions for creature data (performance: single creature-display subscription bundles all visual data per creature, not separate subs for P/T, damage, status)
- ❌ NO hardcoded P/T values in views (correctness: always use engine's creatures/effective-power and creatures/effective-toughness which account for counters, grants, static abilities)
- ❌ NO combat zone separation or card movement (scope: badge-only approach per design decision, no separate combat area)
- ❌ NO keyword badges (scope: deferred, this epic covers P/T + status + damage only)
- ❌ NO mutable state outside Datascript for creature display (architecture: all display data derived from immutable game state via subscriptions)
- ❌ NO reimplementing P/T calculation in views (DRY: engine/creatures.cljs already has effective-power/effective-toughness)

## Approach
Enhance existing permanent-view component in views/battlefield.cljs with creature-specific rendering. All creature data is already computed by the engine (effective P/T, damage-marked, attacking/blocking flags, summoning sickness). Create a single creature-display subscription that bundles all visual data per creature. Enhance combat selection modals with P/T display and combat math preview.

This is purely a presentation-layer change — no engine modifications needed.

## Architecture
- views/battlefield.cljs — Enhance permanent-view with P/T display, damage badge, combat badges, summoning sickness indicator
- subs/game.cljs — New ::creature-display-data subscription (effective P/T, base P/T, modified?, damage-marked, attacking, blocking, summoning-sick)
- views/selection/ — Enhance attacker/blocker selection modals with P/T display and combat math

Data flow:
  Engine (exists) → ::creature-display-data sub (new) → permanent-view (enhanced)
  engine/creatures/effective-power    →  single sub per creature  →  P/T with color
  engine/creatures/effective-toughness                             →  damage badge
  object/damage-marked                                             →  combat badges
  object/attacking / object/blocking                               →  sick indicator
  summoning-sick? check

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The combat engine fully supports creatures, combat, and damage — but the UI shows almost none of this data. permanent-view displays card name, tapped state, counters, and ability buttons only. Players cannot see P/T, combat status, damage, or summoning sickness, making creature-based gameplay impractical.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- views/battlefield.cljs: permanent-view component handles all battlefield cards, shows name/tapped/counters/abilities
- engine/creatures.cljs: effective-power/effective-toughness already compute P/T with counters, grants, static abilities
- engine/combat.cljs: full combat system with attacker/blocker selection, damage assignment, SBAs
- objects store: object/power, object/toughness (base), object/damage-marked, object/attacking (bool), object/blocking (ref)
- events/selection/combat.cljs: :select-attackers and :assign-blockers selection types with chaining
- views/selection/: selection-card-view shows card names only in combat modals

**Patterns:**
- Subscriptions in subs/game.cljs derive all view data from Datascript
- permanent-view already conditionally renders based on card type (lands get ability buttons)
- Selection modals use selection-card-view for card display

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Incremental enhancement of permanent-view ✓
**What it is:** Add P/T display, badges, and damage to existing permanent-view. Enhance combat selection modals with P/T. Single new subscription bundles all creature display data.

**Investigation:**
- permanent-view already has conditional rendering for card features (tapped, counters, abilities)
- Adding creature-specific sections follows existing pattern
- Single subscription avoids per-field subscription overhead

**Pros:**
- Follows existing component pattern
- Minimal structural changes
- Leverages existing subscription infrastructure

**Cons:**
- permanent-view grows larger (mitigated: creature section is self-contained)

**Chosen because:** Consistent with existing codebase patterns, minimal complexity, all data already available in engine

#### 2. Separate creature-card-view component ❌
**What it is:** Create a dedicated creature rendering component separate from permanent-view, dispatched based on card type.

**Why we looked at this:** Creatures have enough unique display needs that a specialized component might be cleaner.

**Investigation:**
- Counted creature-specific fields: P/T, damage, combat status, summoning sickness = 4 overlays
- permanent-view already handles type-specific rendering (lands, artifacts)
- Splitting would duplicate shared rendering (name, tapped, counters, abilities)

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Would duplicate 70% of permanent-view's rendering. Creatures share name/tapped/counter/ability display with all permanents. The creature-specific additions (P/T, badges) are small enough to add inline.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** permanent-view exceeds 200 lines and creatures need fundamentally different layout.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- P/T display on battlefield creatures
- Color-coded P/T modification indicator
- Damage marked badge
- Summoning sickness indicator
- Attacking/blocking combat badges
- Enhanced combat selection modals with P/T and math preview

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Keyword badges (flying, trample, etc.) — separate concern, can be added later
- Combat zone separation — decided against per design discussion
- Combat damage animation — no animation system exists yet
- Creature token display differences — tokens use same rendering as cards

### Open Questions
- Exact Tailwind classes for color-coded P/T (resolve during implementation)
- Whether summoning sickness uses icon or opacity change (resolve during implementation)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| What creature info to show? | P/T + status badges | No keyword badges in scope |
| Blocker assignment UX? | Enhanced modal with P/T | Keep existing chained modal flow, add P/T + math |
| Modified P/T display? | Color-coded (green/red) | Need base vs effective comparison in subscription |
| Damage visibility? | Always show when > 0 | Damage badge on battlefield, not just during combat |
| Combat visual style? | Badge icons only | No card movement, no border highlights, just icons |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Creature Display Data Availability
**Question explored:** What creature data does the engine already provide?
**Sources consulted:**
- engine/creatures.cljs — effective-power/effective-toughness with modifier pipeline
- db/schema — object/power, object/toughness, object/damage-marked, object/attacking, object/blocking
- engine/combat.cljs — summoning sickness check logic

**Findings:**
- ALL needed data is already computed by the engine
- effective-power/effective-toughness handle counters, grants, static abilities
- Summoning sickness check exists in combat eligibility
- No engine changes needed — purely presentation work

**Conclusion:** Create single subscription that calls existing engine functions, no new engine code

### Dead-End Paths
None — straightforward presentation enhancement with clear engine support.

### Open Concerns Raised
None raised — design is well-scoped to existing engine capabilities.
close_reason: Closed

title: Data-driven bot definitions: EDN spec for bot behavior
description:
Replace hand-coded bot defmethods with an EDN-based bot definition format and interpreter. This is a core architectural change aligned with our data-over-code principle — bots should be data the engine interprets, just like cards.

## Current State
- Bots defined via 3 multimethods dispatching on archetype keyword (protocol.cljs)
- bot-deck already returns data (EDN vectors)
- bot-phase-action is trivial logic (phase→action lookup)
- bot-priority-decision is hand-coded query logic (burn bot: 18 lines of game state checks)
- Interceptor + action execution layers already consume data ({:action :cast-spell ...})

## Vision
A bot definition is a single EDN map:
- :bot/deck — card list (already data)
- :bot/phase-actions — phase→action map (trivially data)
- :bot/priority-rules — ordered rules with conditions over game state + actions
- :bot/script — optional, for scripted bots with fixed sequences

An interpreter evaluates the spec against game state and returns the same action maps the interceptor already consumes. No changes needed downstream.

## Key Design Questions (for brainstorm)
1. What condition predicates do we need? (hand-contains, has-mana, stack-empty, turn-number, phase, life-below, etc.)
2. How do conditions compose? (implicit AND? explicit OR/NOT?)
3. How do scripted bots fit the same spec? (turn+phase keyed scripts vs rules)
4. Where does data hit limits? When is an escape hatch (:bot/priority-fn) justified?
5. Should bot defs live in files (bots/defs/*.edn) or a registry map?
6. How does this interact with the reactive priority change (fizzle-ay2q)? Stack-aware conditions?
7. Rule priority/ordering — first match wins, or weighted?

## Requires full brainstorm before implementation
All three existing bots (goldfish, burn, scripted) and the reactive bot concept should be expressible in the spec before we commit to it.
design:
## Architecture: Data-Driven Bot Definitions

### Core Concept
Bot behavior is an EDN spec interpreted by a rules engine. Three multimethods in protocol.cljs become regular functions that look up a bot spec and delegate to a rules engine.

### Bot Spec Shape
```clojure
{:bot/name "Burn"
 :bot/deck [{:card/id :mountain :count 20} {:card/id :lightning-bolt :count 40}]
 :bot/phase-actions {:main1 :play-land}  ;; default :pass for unlisted phases
 :bot/priority-rules [<ordered rules, first match wins>]}
```

### Rule Shape
```clojure
{:rule/mode :auto            ;; or :interactive (future)
 :rule/tag :counter          ;; groups interactive rules into single UI toggle (future)
 :rule/conditions [...]      ;; ALL must be true (implicit AND)
 :rule/action {:action :cast-spell :card-id :lightning-bolt :target :opponent}}
```

### Condition Predicates (query any zone, any player)
```clojure
{:check :zone-contains :zone :hand :player :self :card-id :lightning-bolt}
{:check :has-mana-for :card-id :lightning-bolt}
{:check :stack-empty}
{:check :has-untapped-source :color :red}
{:check :zone-count :zone :graveyard :player :self :gte 7}
{:check :stack-has :owner :self :type :trigger}
{:check :stack-has :owner :opponent :type :spell}
```

### Action Resolution
Actions use symbolic references resolved at evaluation time:
- :card-id :lightning-bolt → finds actual object-id in hand
- :target :opponent → resolves to opponent player-id
- :target :top-of-stack → resolves to stack item (future)

### Two Rule Modes
- :auto — fires immediately when conditions met (this epic)
- :interactive — shows as UI toggle, player approves (future epic, enables fizzle-ay2q)

### File Layout
```
bots/
  rules.cljs        — NEW: condition evaluator, rule matcher, action resolver
  definitions.cljs  — NEW: EDN bot specs (goldfish, burn), registry
  protocol.cljs     — MIGRATED: multimethods → regular functions delegating to rules engine
  interceptor.cljs  — UPDATED: minimal changes (protocol API unchanged)
  actions.cljs      — DELETED: unused utility
```

### Key Design Decisions
1. First-match-wins rule ordering (no weighted/scoring)
2. Implicit AND for conditions within a rule
3. Condition evaluator is a multimethod on :check (extensible)
4. Bot specs stored in definitions registry, looked up by archetype keyword
5. Protocol public API unchanged — interceptor needs minimal updates
6. Interactive mode (:rule/tag, :interactive) in schema but UI deferred to future epic
7. No :bot/priority-fn escape hatch until data hits a wall
close_reason: All 5 sub-tasks complete. Bot protocol migrated from 3 hardcoded multimethods to data-driven EDN specs with rules engine. Goldfish + burn expressed as EDN. Spec validated for burn+hate, counterspell, and extended conditions.

title: Implement Xantid Swarm card
description:
Implement Xantid Swarm: {G} Creature — Insect, 0/1, Flying. 'Whenever Xantid Swarm attacks, defending player can't cast spells this turn.'

## Requirements (Immutable)
1. Card definition: {G} 0/1 Creature — Insect with :flying keyword
2. New :creature-attacks trigger type in trigger_db.cljs (defmethod trigger-type->event-type)
3. Trigger dispatch from combat attacker selection executor — fire per-creature events after attackers declared
4. Trigger effect: :add-restriction with :effect/target :opponent and :restriction/type :cannot-cast-spells
5. :opponent resolves relative to creature's controller (works for both player-controlled and bot-controlled)
6. Trigger uses stack (default behavior) — opponent gets priority to respond
7. Trigger always resolves once on stack (no fizzle check)
8. Card registered in registry.cljs
9. Full test suite per CLAUDE.md card testing requirements

## What's Reused (No Changes)
- :add-restriction effect handler (engine/effects/grants.cljs)
- :cannot-cast-spells restriction enforcement (engine/rules.cljs can-cast?)
- Flying keyword in combat blocking (engine/creatures.cljs)
- Grant auto-expiry at cleanup (engine/grants.cljs)

## Scope Boundaries
- No new restriction types needed
- No changes to combat damage, blocking, or other combat phases
- No UI changes beyond what existing creature rendering handles
close_reason: All 5 tasks complete, reviewer approved, user manually validated trigger on stack

title: Fix 7 history event test errors — keyword snapshots vs real Datascript dbs

Root cause: tests use keyword snapshots (:db-0, :db-1) but rf/dispatch-sync triggers custom :db effect handler which calls sba/check-and-execute-sbas on the keyword. Fix: replace keyword snapshots with real Datascript databases (same pattern as integration_test.cljs which passes). NOT a production bug — only test isolation. Core history algorithms (68 tests) and integration tests (2 tests) are GREEN.

title: Task 3: Add scry confirmation handler to reorder library
design:
## Goal
When player confirms scry selection, reorder library with top-pile cards on top (in click order) and bottom-pile cards on bottom (in click order). Then execute remaining effects (e.g., draw for Opt).

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-1gy: :scry effect type exists
Completed fizzle-abg: :selection/type :scry state created with cards, top-pile, bottom-pile, remaining-effects
Now need handler to process player's choices and finalize scry.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing confirmation handlers
Reference implementations:
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs:1152-1184 - confirm-tutor-selection event
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs:1197-1250 - confirm-player-target-selection event
- Pattern: Handler receives selection state from :game/pending-selection, updates db, clears selection, executes remaining effects, moves spell to graveyard

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Create tests in src/test/fizzle/events/scry_confirm_test.cljs:

```clojure
;; Test 1: test-confirm-scry-1-top-keeps-card-on-top
;; Bug caught: Top pile not placing cards at position 0
;; Setup: Library [A B C], scry 1, put A in top-pile
;; Assert: After confirm, library is [A B C] (unchanged order)

;; Test 2: test-confirm-scry-1-bottom-moves-card-to-bottom
;; Bug caught: Bottom pile not placing cards at end
;; Setup: Library [A B C], scry 1, put A in bottom-pile
;; Assert: After confirm, library is [B C A]

;; Test 3: test-confirm-scry-2-both-top-click-order-preserved
;; Bug caught: Click order ignored, cards reversed
;; Setup: Library [A B C], scry 2, top-pile [A B] (A clicked first)
;; Assert: After confirm, library is [A B C] (A at top, then B)

;; Test 4: test-confirm-scry-2-both-bottom-click-order-preserved
;; Bug caught: Click order ignored for bottom pile
;; Setup: Library [A B C], scry 2, bottom-pile [A B] (A clicked first)
;; Assert: After confirm, library is [C A B] (C stays, then A B at bottom)

;; Test 5: test-confirm-scry-2-split-top-and-bottom
;; Bug caught: Split assignment not handling both piles
;; Setup: Library [A B C], scry 2, top-pile [B], bottom-pile [A]
;; Assert: After confirm, library is [B C A]

;; Test 6: test-confirm-scry-executes-remaining-effects-opt-pattern
;; Bug caught: Remaining effects lost after scry
;; Setup: Scry selection with remaining-effects [{:effect/type :draw :effect/amount 1}]
;; Assert: After confirm, hand has 1 more card (draw executed)

;; Test 7: test-confirm-scry-clears-pending-selection
;; Bug caught: Selection state leaking after confirm
;; Setup: :game/pending-selection contains scry selection
;; Assert: After confirm, :game/pending-selection is nil

;; Test 8: test-confirm-scry-moves-spell-to-graveyard
;; Bug caught: Spell stays on stack after resolution
;; Setup: Spell object on :stack with spell-id
;; Assert: After confirm, spell is in :graveyard

;; Test 9: test-confirm-scry-empty-piles-allowed
;; Bug caught: Crash when confirming with all cards unassigned
;; Setup: Scry 1, both piles empty (card still in :selection/cards)
;; Assert: No crash, library unchanged (card back to original position)
```

### 3. Implementation

#### 3.1 Add confirm-scry-selection function (game.cljs after confirm-tutor-selection ~line 1185)

```clojure
(defn- reorder-library-for-scry
  "Reorder library after scry confirmation.
   top-pile cards go to positions 0, 1, 2... (click order)
   bottom-pile cards go to last positions (click order)
   Unassigned cards from selection stay at original relative positions.
   Cards not in scry selection are unaffected."
  [db player-id selection]
  (let [scry-cards (set (:selection/cards selection))
        top-pile (:selection/top-pile selection)
        bottom-pile (:selection/bottom-pile selection)
        player-eid (queries/get-player-eid db player-id)
        ;; Get all library objects
        library-objs (queries/get-objects-in-zone db player-id :library)
        ;; Separate scry cards from non-scry cards
        non-scry-objs (remove #(scry-cards (:object/id %)) library-objs)
        ;; Cards in selection but not assigned to either pile
        unassigned (remove #(or ((set top-pile) %)
                                 ((set bottom-pile) %))
                           (:selection/cards selection))
        ;; Build new position order:
        ;; 1. Top pile (click order)
        ;; 2. Unassigned scry cards (original order)
        ;; 3. Non-scry cards (original positions preserved)
        ;; 4. Bottom pile (click order)
        new-order (concat top-pile unassigned (map :object/id non-scry-objs) bottom-pile)]
    ;; Update positions
    (reduce-kv (fn [d idx obj-id]
                 (queries/update-object-position d obj-id idx))
               db
               (vec new-order))))
```

#### 3.2 Add re-frame event handler (after confirm-tutor-selection)

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::confirm-scry-selection
  (fn [db _]
    (let [selection (:game/pending-selection db)
          spell-id (:selection/spell-id selection)
          remaining-effects (:selection/remaining-effects selection)
          player-id (:selection/player-id selection)
          game-db (:game/db db)]
      (if (= :scry (:selection/type selection))
        (let [;; Reorder library based on pile assignments
              db-after-reorder (reorder-library-for-scry game-db player-id selection)
              ;; Execute remaining effects (e.g., draw for Opt)
              db-after-remaining (reduce (fn [d effect]
                                           (effects/execute-effect d player-id effect))
                                         db-after-reorder
                                         (or remaining-effects []))
              ;; Move spell to graveyard
              db-final (zones/move-to-zone db-after-remaining spell-id :graveyard)]
          (-> db
              (assoc :game/db db-final)
              (dissoc :game/pending-selection)))
        ;; Not a scry selection - do nothing
        db))))
```

### 4. Library reorder logic detail

New library positions after scry confirm:
1. Position 0, 1, 2... = top-pile cards (first clicked = position 0)
2. Middle positions = unassigned scry cards + non-scry cards (original relative order)
3. Last positions = bottom-pile cards (first clicked = first of bottom group)

Example: Library [A B C D E], Scry 2 (reveals A, B)
- top-pile: [B] (clicked first)
- bottom-pile: [A] (clicked second)
- Result: [B C D E A]

## Success Criteria
- [ ] reorder-library-for-scry function exists in game.cljs
- [ ] rf/reg-event-db ::confirm-scry-selection registered
- [ ] test-confirm-scry-1-top-keeps-card-on-top passes
- [ ] test-confirm-scry-1-bottom-moves-card-to-bottom passes
- [ ] test-confirm-scry-2-both-top-click-order-preserved passes
- [ ] test-confirm-scry-2-both-bottom-click-order-preserved passes
- [ ] test-confirm-scry-2-split-top-and-bottom passes
- [ ] test-confirm-scry-executes-remaining-effects-opt-pattern passes
- [ ] test-confirm-scry-clears-pending-selection passes
- [ ] test-confirm-scry-moves-spell-to-graveyard passes
- [ ] test-confirm-scry-empty-piles-allowed passes (no crash)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty piles (no cards assigned)**
- Player can confirm without assigning cards
- Unassigned cards stay at original positions
- MUST NOT crash or lose cards
- Test: test-confirm-scry-empty-piles-allowed

**Edge Case: Remaining effects is nil**
- Use (or remaining-effects []) pattern
- Already in reference implementation (confirm-tutor-selection:1173)

**Edge Case: Cards not in library (already moved)**
- Defensive: Only update position if card still in library
- Use update-object-position that handles missing objects gracefully

**Position Update Pattern**
- Reference: queries.cljs - need to check if update-object-position exists
- If not, use d/transact! with [:db/add eid :object/position new-pos]

**Trigger vs Spell source**
- Current scope: Spell-based scry only (Opt is a spell)
- Future: May need :selection/trigger-id support like confirm-tutor-selection
- For now, always use spell-id and move spell to graveyard

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO shuffling - scry never shuffles
- ❌ NO graveyard interaction during scry (only move spell after complete)
- ❌ NO modifying cards not in scry selection
- ❌ NO losing remaining effects
- ❌ NO assuming piles are non-empty (player may confirm with unassigned cards)
- ❌ NO hardcoding positions (use calculated indices)

Tests call toggle-selection-impl directly and read auto-confirm? but never dispatch confirm. The full toggle→confirm→SBA chain is untested. Pattern exists in auto_confirm_test.cljs (dispatch-sync-toggle-with-auto-confirm) but is not used consistently. Add tests that verify the complete chain for single-select auto-confirm types.

title: Fix stack-empty? to treat stack as shared (MTG rules)
description:
stack-empty? in db/queries.cljs has inconsistent semantics: spell objects are checked per-player (via get-objects-in-zone filtering by :object/owner), but stack-items are checked globally. In MTG, the stack is shared — if ANY player has a spell or ability on the stack, the stack is not empty.

Current behavior:
- Player-2 has a spell on :stack zone → stack-empty? for player-1 returns true (wrong)
- Any stack-item exists globally → stack-empty? for player-1 returns false (correct)

Fix: stack-empty? should check for ANY objects in :stack zone across all players, not just the given player. Consider whether the player-id parameter is even needed, or if it should be removed entirely to reflect the shared nature of the stack.

Discovered by test: stack-empty?-ignores-other-player-spell-on-stack-test in queries_test.cljs — this test documents the current (incorrect) behavior and should be updated when the fix lands.
notes:
## Fix Status: FIXED

**Root cause:** queries/stack-empty? used get-objects-in-zone which filters by player-id. The stack is shared in MTG (Rules 117, 405), so any player's spells should make the stack non-empty.

**Fix:** Removed player-id parameter from queries/stack-empty?. Now queries ALL objects in :stack zone globally. Updated 3 callers in events/game.cljs. Cleaned up redundant stack check in engine/rules.cljs sorcery-speed-ok?.

**Regression test:** stack-empty?-shared-stack-test in queries_test.cljs (asserts stack is non-empty when opponent has spells).

**Evidence:** 872 tests, 1982 assertions, 0 failures.
close_reason: Fixed: stack-empty? now checks all objects in :stack zone globally (shared stack per MTG rules). Regression test added.

title: Create selection multi-spec + set-pending-selection helper + Makefile lint
description:
Create events/selection/spec.cljs with:
1. defmulti selection-type-spec dispatching on :selection/type
2. One defmethod per concrete selection type (~31) — read each builder to determine required keys
3. s/def ::selection wrapping the multi-spec
4. minimal-valid-selection map (one entry per type, like Phase 1's minimal-valid-effect)
5. set-pending-selection helper fn: validates via s/valid? under goog.DEBUG, assocs :game/pending-selection
6. Makefile lint rule: grep for direct assoc :game/pending-selection outside spec.cljs, fail if found

Base keys (every selection): :selection/type, :selection/lifecycle, :selection/selected, :selection/validation, :selection/player-id

Type-specific keys: determined by reading each builder output in selection/*.cljs, casting.cljs, abilities.cljs

Test file: test/fizzle/events/selection/spec_test.cljs
- Exercise test: (s/exercise ::selection) for each type using minimal-valid-selection
- Negative tests: missing :selection/lifecycle, missing type-specific keys
- set-pending-selection validates in dev, passes through in prod

Reference pattern: card_spec.cljs:124-405 (Phase 1)
Reference for Makefile lint: existing q-safe/pull-safe grep rule

Does NOT include call-site migration (that is a separate task).
design:
## Goal
Create events/selection/spec.cljs with a multi-spec for selection maps, a set-pending-selection helper function with dev-only validation, and a Makefile lint rule preventing bypass. Does NOT include migrating the 12 call sites (separate task).

## Effort Estimate
8-12 hours (homogeneous: repeat same pattern ~31 times, then helper + lint + tests)

## Implementation Checklist

### Step 1: Create spec file with multi-spec structure
- [ ] Create src/main/fizzle/events/selection/spec.cljs
- [ ] Add ns declaration requiring [cljs.spec.alpha :as s]
- [ ] Define defmulti selection-type-spec dispatching on :selection/type
- [ ] Define s/def ::selection via (s/multi-spec selection-type-spec :selection/type)

### Step 2: Write base key specs
Base keys present on EVERY selection map:
- :selection/type — keyword
- :selection/lifecycle — one of #{:standard :finalized :chaining}
- :selection/selected — collection (set, vector, or map depending on type)
- :selection/validation — one of #{:exact :at-most :at-least-one :exact-or-zero :always}
- :selection/player-id — keyword

### Step 3: Write one defmethod per concrete selection type (~31 types)
Read each builder to determine required keys. Complete type list with builder locations:

**Zone-pick pattern types** (builder: core.cljs:148-174):
1. :discard — executor: zone_ops.cljs:66
2. :graveyard-return — executor: uses parent zone-pick core.cljs:181
3. :hand-reveal-discard — executor: zone_ops.cljs:83; builder: zone_ops.cljs:57
4. :chain-bounce — executor: zone_ops.cljs:188; builder: zone_ops.cljs:165
5. :chain-bounce-target — executor: zone_ops.cljs:204
6. :unless-pay — executor: zone_ops.cljs:258

**Accumulator pattern types:**
7. :storm-split — executor: storm.cljs:64
8. :x-mana-cost — executor: costs.cljs:635
9. :mana-allocation — executor: costs.cljs:692

**Reorder pattern types:**
10. :scry — executor: library.cljs:766
11. :peek-and-reorder — executor: library.cljs:721
12. :order-bottom — executor: library.cljs:711
13. :order-top — executor: library.cljs:716

**Library ops:**
14. :tutor — executor: library.cljs:683
15. :peek-and-select — executor: library.cljs:694
16. :pile-choice — executor: library.cljs:744

**Targeting:**
17. :player-target — executor: targeting.cljs:188
18. :cast-time-targeting — executor: targeting.cljs:217
19. :ability-cast-targeting — executor: targeting.cljs:226
20. :ability-targeting — executor: abilities.cljs:318

**Pre-cast costs:**
21. :discard-specific-cost — executor: costs.cljs:498
22. :return-land-cost — executor: costs.cljs:516
23. :sacrifice-permanent-cost — executor: costs.cljs:543
24. :exile-cards-cost — executor: costs.cljs:590
25. :pay-x-life — executor: costs.cljs:608

**Land types:**
26. :land-type-source — executor: land_types.cljs:39
27. :land-type-target — executor: land_types.cljs:89

**Combat:**
28. :select-attackers — executor: combat.cljs:26
29. :assign-blockers — executor: combat.cljs:72

**Other:**
30. :spell-mode — executor: casting.cljs:203
31. :untap-lands — executor: untap.cljs:53

For each type: read the builder that constructs it, note which keys it always sets (required) vs conditionally sets (optional). Write s/keys spec.

### Step 4: Write minimal-valid-selection map
One entry per type (like card_spec.cljs:348-395 minimal-effects map). Each entry must pass its type's spec. Used by exercise tests.

### Step 5: Write set-pending-selection helper
```clojure
(defn set-pending-selection
  [app-db selection]
  (when ^boolean goog.DEBUG
    (when-not (s/valid? ::selection selection)
      (js/console.error
        "Invalid selection map:"
        (s/explain-str ::selection selection))))
  (assoc app-db :game/pending-selection selection))
```
- Uses goog.DEBUG for dead-code elimination in release builds
- console.error (not throw) — logs violation but doesn't break game flow in dev
- Pure function: takes app-db, returns app-db

### Step 6: Add Makefile lint rule
Add lint-pending-selection target following the pattern from commit 555f66d:
```makefile
lint-pending-selection:
	@echo "Checking for direct assoc :game/pending-selection outside spec.cljs..."
	@! grep -rn '(assoc [^-].*:game/pending-selection' src/main/ --include="*.cljs" \
		| grep -v 'selection/spec.cljs' \
		| grep -q . \
		&& echo "  No direct pending-selection assocs found outside spec.cljs." \
		|| (grep -rn '(assoc [^-].*:game/pending-selection' src/main/ --include="*.cljs" | grep -v 'selection/spec.cljs'; exit 1)
```
IMPORTANT: grep pattern must match `assoc :game/pending-selection` and `assoc app-db :game/pending-selection` but NOT `assoc-in [:game/pending-selection ...]` — assoc-in updates an existing (already validated) selection, not creation.
Add to validate target: `@$(MAKE) lint && $(MAKE) fmt-check && $(MAKE) lint-pending-selection && $(MAKE) test`
Add to .PHONY line.

NOTE: This lint rule will FAIL until the call-site migration task is done (the 12 existing direct assocs still exist). Either:
- Add the rule but don't add to validate yet (add to validate in migration task), OR
- Accept that validate will fail until migration is complete

### Step 7: Write tests
Test file: src/test/fizzle/events/selection/spec_test.cljs

Exercise tests (one per type):
```clojure
(doseq [[sel-type minimal] minimal-valid-selections]
  (testing (str "minimal " sel-type " is valid")
    (is (s/valid? ::selection minimal)
        (str "Failed: " (s/explain-str ::selection minimal)))))
```

Negative tests — these catch SPECIFIC historic bug classes:
- Missing :selection/lifecycle → spec fails (historic bug: builders forgetting lifecycle)
- Missing :selection/player-id → spec fails
- Wrong :selection/lifecycle value (e.g., :bogus) → spec fails
- Missing type-specific key (e.g., zone-pick without :selection/zone) → spec fails
- Wrong :selection/type (keyword not in any defmethod) → spec fails

set-pending-selection tests:
- Valid selection → returns app-db with :game/pending-selection set
- set-pending-selection is pure (returns value, no side effects beyond console)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] spec.cljs exists with defmulti + s/multi-spec ::selection
- [ ] Exactly 31 defmethod selection-type-spec (one per concrete type)
- [ ] minimal-valid-selection map has 31 entries, all pass s/valid?
- [ ] Exercise test passes for every type via doseq
- [ ] 5+ negative tests catching missing/wrong keys
- [ ] set-pending-selection helper validates under goog.DEBUG, assocs :game/pending-selection
- [ ] Makefile has lint-pending-selection target with correct grep pattern
- [ ] make lint passes (clj-kondo clean)
- [ ] make fmt-check passes
- [ ] make test passes (all existing tests unchanged)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: :selection/selected type varies by selection type**
- Zone-pick types use #{} (set of object IDs)
- Accumulator types use maps or integers
- Reorder types use vectors
- Spec should use s/or or per-type specs for :selected — do NOT constrain to one collection type globally

**Edge Case: Enum-like fields**
- :selection/lifecycle MUST be constrained to #{:standard :finalized :chaining}
- :selection/validation MUST be constrained to #{:exact :at-most :at-least-one :exact-or-zero :always}
- If builders produce values outside these sets, the spec is wrong — investigate the builder, don't widen the spec

**Edge Case: Optional vs always-present keys**
- Some builders use cond-> which conditionally adds keys
- A key added via cond-> is OPTIONAL in the spec even if it's present 95% of the time
- Only list keys in s/keys :req that the builder ALWAYS sets (no cond->)

**Edge Case: Lint rule grep precision**
- Must NOT match assoc-in (those are updates to existing validated selections)
- Must NOT match reads like (:game/pending-selection app-db)
- Must NOT match dissoc :game/pending-selection
- Pattern `(assoc [^-].*:game/pending-selection` handles this: [^-] excludes assoc-in

**Stale reference: q-safe/pull-safe lint**
- The memory says Makefile has lint-datascript rule — this is STALE
- Commit 555f66d added it, commit a72bebc reverted it. Neither rule nor q-safe fns exist in current codebase
- The PATTERN from that commit is still valid as a template for the grep rule
- The lint-test-paths target IS in the current Makefile but is NOT in the validate target

**Lint rule timing**
- The lint rule will fail until the migration task replaces 12 direct assocs
- Recommended: add the rule to Makefile but do NOT add to validate target yet
- Migration task will add lint-pending-selection to validate after all sites are migrated

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO changing builder output shapes to fit spec (spec documents what IS, not what should be)
- ❌ NO hierarchy-aware dispatch in spec multimethod (one defmethod per type, per epic anti-pattern)
- ❌ NO constraining :selection/selected to a single collection type globally (varies per type)
- ❌ NO catching assoc-in in the lint rule (updates to existing selections are fine)
- ❌ NO adding lint-pending-selection to validate target yet (will fail until migration task)
- ❌ NO throwing on invalid selection in set-pending-selection (console.error only — don't break dev flow)
close_reason: Committed as a12a827. 31 defmethods, set-pending-selection helper, Makefile lint rule, 3184 tests pass.

Currently :selection/allow-fail-to-find lacks the ? while :selection/exact?, :selection/shuffle?, and :selection/shuffle-remainder? all have it. Rename :selection/allow-fail-to-find to :selection/allow-fail-to-find? for consistency.
